详细信息
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
第一篇　给过河者的书信
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：创十四13，出三18，九1，13，来一1～2上，十一8～16，六19，十三13。
壹　希伯来书是写给过河者的一卷书信：
一　希伯来这辞字根的意思是“过去”─创十四13：
１　这辞特别是指“过河”，也就是说，从河这边过到河那边。
２　希伯来人是过河的人，就是从河另一边过来的人。
３　过河的人乃是一班从世界分别出来的人。
二　亚伯拉罕是头一个希伯来人，头一个过河者─创十四13，来十一8～16：
１　亚伯拉罕离开迦勒底，过了大水，进入迦南地，就是蒙福的美地─书二四2～3。
２　亚伯拉罕过河进入新地，表征他进入一个拔高、全新的人类，这人类要被神用作祂的彰显。
三　我们的神是希伯来人的神─出三18，九1，13：
１　祂是过河之人的神，是分别出来之民的神。
２　希伯来人的神，乃是为着完成神定旨而过河之人的神。
四　希伯来人的神，写了希伯来书给我们─来一1～2上，十二25上：
１　希伯来书的基本观念，乃是过河的观念，从一边过到另一边的观念。
２　希伯来书是只为着过河之人写的。
３　我们若过了河，就能明白这卷奇妙的书─来五12，六1。
贰　神在希伯来书中说话的目的，是要凡凭信接触神的人，都成为过河的人─来十一1，6：
一　主耶稣受死时过了河，进入神彰显之地，就是神荣耀之地─来二9，六19～20：
１　主耶稣所经过的死，乃是一个浸─可十38，路十二50。
２　主耶稣是真约书亚，率先过约但河，进入了荣耀─神圣者之彰显的实际─路二四26，彼前一11。
３　基督是我们救恩的元帅，走在我们前头，要帮助我们经过苦难之水，而进入荣耀─来二10。
二　受浸使我们成为过河的人，现今我们是真实、真正的希伯来人，跟随开拓者“先锋耶稣”，进荣耀里去─罗六3，加三27，太二八19，来六20：
１　过河就是洗去我们一切老旧、败坏、与神的荣耀不相配的东西─启十五2～3。
２　每次我们要进入圣殿，都必须经过一道静止的河─洗濯盆─出三十18，四十30～32，王上七23，38。
３　我们过了所有的河，就被带进荣耀里，而这荣耀就是神自己彰显出来。
三　我们若要进入新耶路撒冷，作荣耀之神完满的彰显（来十一10，16，十二22，启二一2，10～11），就必须经过许多河：─引用经文
１　我们必须离开律法过到恩典─来四16，七18～19，十二28，十三9。
２　我们必须离开旧约过到新约─来八6～7，13。
３　我们必须离开旧约的仪式事奉过到新约的属灵实际─来八5，九9～14。
４　我们必须离开犹太教过到召会─来十三13，十25。
５　我们必须离开属地的过到属天的─来十二18～24。
６　我们必须离开外院子过到至圣所─来十三9～10，十19～20。
７　我们必须离开魂过到灵─来四12。
８　我们必须离开真理和生命的开端过到在真理中生命的成熟─来五11～六1。
叁　我们这些今日的希伯来人，过河者，必须看见神的经纶（提前一4），并经历使徒行传所启示时代的转移─徒一4～8：
一　时代转换的事─神永远经纶中神圣的安排─是使徒行传中很强的点：
１　说到在使徒行传中时代的转换，意思是说，我们在使徒行传中看见，有一个大转移，大转变的需要。
２　这个转换，这个转移，乃是从旧时代转到新时代。
二　我们必须在经纶一面从旧约转移到新约：
１　我们虽然是新约的人，但也许仍有旧约的观念。
２　我们必须从律法转到基督里，从守律法者转为耶稣的见证人。
三　“在希伯来书，保罗呈现一幅清楚的图画，给我们看见旧约的事物已经过去。现今留在神新约经纶里的，乃是耶稣基督，那包罗万有的一位。保罗有这样的看见，就不能容忍这样一位包罗万有的基督，与过时之旧约经纶的较低事物搀杂。”（使徒行传生命读经，五九四页。）

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message One
An Epistle to River Crossers
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Gen. 14:13; Exo. 3:18; 9:1, 13; Heb. 1:1-2a; 11:8-16; 6:19; 13:13
I.　The Epistle to the Hebrews is an epistle to river crossers:
A.　The root of the word?Hebrew?means "to pass over"—Gen. 14:13:
1.　Specifically, it can mean "to pass over a river," that is, to pass over from one side of the river to another.
2.　A Hebrew is one who crosses a river, a person from the other side of the river.
3.　River crossers are a people separated from the world.
B.　Abraham was the first Hebrew, the first river crosser—Gen. 14:13; Heb. 11:8-16:
1.　Abraham left Chaldea, crossed the flood, and came into Canaan, the good land of blessing—Josh. 24:2-3.
2.　Abraham's crossing the river and entering into the new land signified his entering into an uplifted, new mankind, which would be used by God to be His expression.
C.　Our God is the God of the Hebrews—Exo. 3:18; 9:1, 13:
1.　He is the God of the river crossers, the God of the separated people.
2.　The God of the Hebrews is the God of the people who have crossed the river for the fulfillment of God's purpose.
D.　The God of the Hebrews has written the book of Hebrews to us—Heb. 1:1-2a; 12:25a:
1.　The basic concept of the book of Hebrews is that of crossing the river, of passing over from one side to another.
2.　The Epistle to the Hebrews is only for river crossers.
3.　If we have crossed the river, we can understand this wonderful book—Heb. 5:12; 6:1.
II.　The purpose of God's speaking in the Epistle to the Hebrews is that all who contact God by faith would be river crossers—Heb. 11:1, 6:
A.　In suffering death, the Lord Jesus crossed the river and entered into the region of God's expression, the region of God's glory—Heb. 2:9; 6:19-20:
1.　The death through which the Lord Jesus passed was a baptism—Mark 10:38; Luke 12:50.
2.　As the real Joshua, the Lord Jesus took the lead to cross the Jordan River and enter into glory—the reality of the expression of the Divine Being—Luke 24:26; 1 Pet. 1:11.
3.　As the Captain of our salvation, Christ has gone ahead of us that He might help us pass through the waters of suffering and enter into glory—Heb. 2:10.
B.　Baptism has made us river crossers, and now we are true, genuine Hebrews, following the Pioneer, "the Forerunner, Jesus," into glory— Rom. 6:3; Gal. 3:27; Matt. 28:19; Heb. 6:20:
1.　To cross the river is to wash ourselves from anything old, anything ruined, and anything that does not match God's glory—Rev. 15:2-3.
2.　Every time we desire to enter into the temple we need to cross a standing river— the laver—Exo. 30:18; 40:30-32; 1 Kings 7:23, 38.
3.　The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, which is God Himself expressed.
C.　If we would enter into the New Jerusalem as the full expression of the glorious God (Heb. 11:10, 16; 12:22; Rev. 21:2, 10-11), we need to cross over many rivers:
1.　We need to leave the law and cross over to grace—Heb. 4:16; 7:18-19; 12:28; 13:9.
2.　We need to leave the old covenant and cross over to the new covenant—Heb. 8:6-7, 13.
3.　We need to leave the ritualistic service of the Old Testament and cross over to the spiritual reality of the New Testament— Heb. 8:5; 9:9-14.
4.　We need to leave Judaism and cross over to the church—Heb. 13:13; 10:25.
5.　We need to leave the earthly things and cross over to the heavenly things—Heb. 12:18-24.
6.　We need to leave the outer court and cross over to the Holiest of all—Heb. 13:9-10; 10:19-20.
7.　We need to leave the soul and cross over to the spirit—Heb. 4:12.
8.　We need to leave the beginning of truth and life and cross over to the maturity of life in the truth—Heb. 5:11—6:1.
III.　As today's Hebrews, river crossers, we need to see God's economy (1 Tim. 1:4) and experience the dispensational transfer revealed in the book of Acts—Acts 1:4-8:
A.　The matter of the change of dispensation—the divine arrangement in God's eternal economy— is a strong point in Acts:
1.　To speak of a dispensational change in Acts means that in Acts we see the need of a great transfer, a great turn.
2.　This change, this transfer, is from the old dispensation to the new.
B.　We need to be transferred economically from the Old Testament to the New Testament:
1.　Although we are New Testament people, we may still have an Old Testament concept.
2.　We need a transfer out of the law and into Christ, a transfer from being law-keepers to being Jesus-witnesses.
C.　"In the book of Hebrews Paul presents a clear picture showing us that the Old Testament things are over. What remains now in God's New Testament economy is Jesus Christ, the all-inclusive One. Having seen such a view, Paul could not tolerate a mixture of such an all-inclusive Christ with the inferior things of the out-of-date, Old Testament economy" (Life-study of Acts,?p. 518).

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第一篇　给过河者的书信
An Epistle to River Crossers
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：创十四13，出三18，九1，13，来一1～2上，十一8～16，六19，十三13。
壹　希伯来书是写给过河者的一卷书信：
I.　The Epistle to the Hebrews is an epistle to river crossers:
一　希伯来这辞字根的意思是“过去”─创十四13：
A.　The root of the word?Hebrew?means "to pass over"—Gen. 14:13:
１　这辞特别是指“过河”，也就是说，从河这边过到河那边。
1.　Specifically, it can mean "to pass over a river," that is, to pass over from one side of the river to another.
２　希伯来人是过河的人，就是从河另一边过来的人。
2.　A Hebrew is one who crosses a river, a person from the other side of the river.
３　过河的人乃是一班从世界分别出来的人。
3.　River crossers are a people separated from the world.
二　亚伯拉罕是头一个希伯来人，头一个过河者─创十四13，来十一8～16：
B.　Abraham was the first Hebrew, the first river crosser—Gen. 14:13; Heb. 11:8-16:
１　亚伯拉罕离开迦勒底，过了大水，进入迦南地，就是蒙福的美地─书二四2～3。
1.　Abraham left Chaldea, crossed the flood, and came into Canaan, the good land of blessing—Josh. 24:2-3.
２　亚伯拉罕过河进入新地，表征他进入一个拔高、全新的人类，这人类要被神用作祂的彰显。
2.　Abraham's crossing the river and entering into the new land signified his entering into an uplifted, new mankind, which would be used by God to be His expression.
三　我们的神是希伯来人的神─出三18，九1，13：
C.　Our God is the God of the Hebrews—Exo. 3:18; 9:1, 13:
１　祂是过河之人的神，是分别出来之民的神。
1.　He is the God of the river crossers, the God of the separated people.
２　希伯来人的神，乃是为着完成神定旨而过河之人的神。
2.　The God of the Hebrews is the God of the people who have crossed the river for the fulfillment of God's purpose.
四　希伯来人的神，写了希伯来书给我们─来一1～2上，十二25上：
D.　The God of the Hebrews has written the book of Hebrews to us—Heb. 1:1-2a; 12:25a:
１　希伯来书的基本观念，乃是过河的观念，从一边过到另一边的观念。
1.　The basic concept of the book of Hebrews is that of crossing the river, of passing over from one side to another.
２　希伯来书是只为着过河之人写的。
2.　The Epistle to the Hebrews is only for river crossers.
３　我们若过了河，就能明白这卷奇妙的书─来五12，六1。
3.　If we have crossed the river, we can understand this wonderful book—Heb. 5:12; 6:1.
贰　神在希伯来书中说话的目的，是要凡凭信接触神的人，都成为过河的人─来十一1，6：
II.　The purpose of God's speaking in the Epistle to the Hebrews is that all who contact God by faith would be river crossers—Heb. 11:1, 6:
一　主耶稣受死时过了河，进入神彰显之地，就是神荣耀之地─来二9，六19～20：
A.　In suffering death, the Lord Jesus crossed the river and entered into the region of God's expression, the region of God's glory—Heb. 2:9; 6:19-20:
１　主耶稣所经过的死，乃是一个浸─可十38，路十二50。
1.　The death through which the Lord Jesus passed was a baptism—Mark 10:38; Luke 12:50.
２　主耶稣是真约书亚，率先过约但河，进入了荣耀─神圣者之彰显的实际─路二四26，彼前一11。
2.　As the real Joshua, the Lord Jesus took the lead to cross the Jordan River and enter into glory—the reality of the expression of the Divine Being—Luke 24:26; 1 Pet. 1:11.
３　基督是我们救恩的元帅，走在我们前头，要帮助我们经过苦难之水，而进入荣耀─来二10。
3.　As the Captain of our salvation, Christ has gone ahead of us that He might help us pass through the waters of suffering and enter into glory—Heb. 2:10.
二　受浸使我们成为过河的人，现今我们是真实、真正的希伯来人，跟随开拓者“先锋耶稣”，进荣耀里去─罗六3，加三27，太二八19，来六20：
B.　Baptism has made us river crossers, and now we are true, genuine Hebrews, following the Pioneer, "the Forerunner, Jesus," into glory— Rom. 6:3; Gal. 3:27; Matt. 28:19; Heb. 6:20:
１　过河就是洗去我们一切老旧、败坏、与神的荣耀不相配的东西─启十五2～3。
1.　To cross the river is to wash ourselves from anything old, anything ruined, and anything that does not match God's glory—Rev. 15:2-3.
２　每次我们要进入圣殿，都必须经过一道静止的河─洗濯盆─出三十18，四十30～32，王上七23，38。
2.　Every time we desire to enter into the temple we need to cross a standing river— the laver—Exo. 30:18; 40:30-32; 1 Kings 7:23, 38.
３　我们过了所有的河，就被带进荣耀里，而这荣耀就是神自己彰显出来。
3.　The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, which is God Himself expressed.
三　我们若要进入新耶路撒冷，作荣耀之神完满的彰显（来十一10，16，十二22，启二一2，10～11），就必须经过许多河：─引用经文
C.　If we would enter into the New Jerusalem as the full expression of the glorious God (Heb. 11:10, 16; 12:22; Rev. 21:2, 10-11), we need to cross over many rivers:
１　我们必须离开律法过到恩典─来四16，七18～19，十二28，十三9。
1.　We need to leave the law and cross over to grace—Heb. 4:16; 7:18-19; 12:28; 13:9.
２　我们必须离开旧约过到新约─来八6～7，13。
2.　We need to leave the old covenant and cross over to the new covenant—Heb. 8:6-7, 13.
３　我们必须离开旧约的仪式事奉过到新约的属灵实际─来八5，九9～14。
3.　We need to leave the ritualistic service of the Old Testament and cross over to the spiritual reality of the New Testament— Heb. 8:5; 9:9-14.
４　我们必须离开犹太教过到召会─来十三13，十25。
4.　We need to leave Judaism and cross over to the church—Heb. 13:13; 10:25.
５　我们必须离开属地的过到属天的─来十二18～24。
5.　We need to leave the earthly things and cross over to the heavenly things—Heb. 12:18-24.
６　我们必须离开外院子过到至圣所─来十三9～10，十19～20。
6.　We need to leave the outer court and cross over to the Holiest of all—Heb. 13:9-10; 10:19-20.
７　我们必须离开魂过到灵─来四12。
7.　We need to leave the soul and cross over to the spirit—Heb. 4:12.
８　我们必须离开真理和生命的开端过到在真理中生命的成熟─来五11～六1。
8.　We need to leave the beginning of truth and life and cross over to the maturity of life in the truth—Heb. 5:11—6:1.
叁　我们这些今日的希伯来人，过河者，必须看见神的经纶（提前一4），并经历使徒行传所启示时代的转移─徒一4～8：
III.　As today's Hebrews, river crossers, we need to see God's economy (1 Tim. 1:4) and experience the dispensational transfer revealed in the book of Acts—Acts 1:4-8:
一　时代转换的事─神永远经纶中神圣的安排─是使徒行传中很强的点：
A.　The matter of the change of dispensation—the divine arrangement in God's eternal economy— is a strong point in Acts:
１　说到在使徒行传中时代的转换，意思是说，我们在使徒行传中看见，有一个大转移，大转变的需要。
1.　To speak of a dispensational change in Acts means that in Acts we see the need of a great transfer, a great turn.
２　这个转换，这个转移，乃是从旧时代转到新时代。
2.　This change, this transfer, is from the old dispensation to the new.
二　我们必须在经纶一面从旧约转移到新约：
B.　We need to be transferred economically from the Old Testament to the New Testament:
１　我们虽然是新约的人，但也许仍有旧约的观念。
1.　Although we are New Testament people, we may still have an Old Testament concept.
２　我们必须从律法转到基督里，从守律法者转为耶稣的见证人。
2.　We need a transfer out of the law and into Christ, a transfer from being law-keepers to being Jesus-witnesses.
三　“在希伯来书，保罗呈现一幅清楚的图画，给我们看见旧约的事物已经过去。现今留在神新约经纶里的，乃是耶稣基督，那包罗万有的一位。保罗有这样的看见，就不能容忍这样一位包罗万有的基督，与过时之旧约经纶的较低事物搀杂。”（使徒行传生命读经，五九四页。）
C.　"In the book of Hebrews Paul presents a clear picture showing us that the Old Testament things are over. What remains now in God's New Testament economy is Jesus Christ, the all-inclusive One. Having seen such a view, Paul could not tolerate a mixture of such an all-inclusive Christ with the inferior things of the out-of-date, Old Testament economy" (Life-study of Acts,?p. 518).

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE ONE
AN EPISTLE TO RIVER CROSSERS
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Prayer: Lord, we worship You as the very God, the God of the Hebrews, the God of the river crossers. We thank You for becoming a man, for becoming what we are that we may become what You are. Thank You for coming to where we are that we may cross over to where You are. Lord, we are on our way to becoming You in life and nature and expression and on our way to entering into You to be in that large, universal, divine-human incorporation. Lord, in one accord we offer You this crystallization-study. We call down Your blessing. Pour out Your blessing. Bless Your recovery. Bless all the seeking saints. Lord, now we come to the edge of a river, but in faith we step into the river Jordan. We do not fear it; we cross it. As one man we cross each river until we are in Your glory, until we become Your glory, and until we rest forever with You. Hallelujah, Amen.
The book of Hebrews in the New Testament is a vast, profound source of divine riches. There are many marvelous points and crystals in Hebrews. It is quite an exercise to identify the essence and intrinsic significance of this book. By the Lord's mercy and grace and by trusting in the supply of the Body and the intercession of the Head, we have selected twelve major crystals that we will cover in these messages. The following statements express the essence of the burden of the crystallization of the first six chapters of Hebrews:
1) The believers in Christ should be river crossers following the Lord Jesus, the Pioneer and Forerunner, into the glorious expression of the Divine Being.
2) Christ, as the Captain of salvation, is leading God's many sons into glory, the corporate expression of God, by saving them organically through sanctification.
3) Christ, our High Priest, ministers to us whatever we need, dispensing Himself into us as our supply for the fulfillment of God's eternal purpose.
4) We need to be brought on to maturity, pursuing toward the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ for the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom.
AN EPISTLE TO RIVER CROSSERS
The Epistle to the Hebrews is an epistle written to river crossers. The title given to this book under God's sovereignty is the Epistle to the Hebrews. It was written neither to the Christians nor to the Jewish Christians, but to Hebrews. This should cause us to consider what a Hebrew is. Why was such an epistle written to Hebrews? At that time, in Jerusalem and elsewhere, there were Jews who heard the gospel from the brothers; they believed into Christ, were baptized into Christ, and began to meet with the church and to practice the church life. We know from the early chapters of Acts that it was a glorious and wonderful beginning. They broke bread with gladness and met in a marvelous way (2:46-47).
However, there was still a twofold problem. On one hand, these believers were in the New Testament, the new covenant, and on the other hand, their background continued to pull and attract them. When the feasts came and the people flocked to Jerusalem, the believers in Christ were meeting in homes while the spectacular festivals were going on in the city and the temple. Many family members who were not believers would say, "Come and eat the sacrifices. Come into the temple and worship. Look at how beautiful and how glorious this is. What do you have there with the believers?" There was a temptation to compromise, to have one foot in the new covenant and one in the old, one in the church and one in Judaism. There was also outright persecution. For this reason Hebrews speaks of the believers' suffering (10:32-34). The writer says, "You accepted with joy the plundering of your possessions" (v. 34). They were being robbed. They were being defrauded, and that by their countrymen. Many were hesitating on a bridge between two realms. Some were considering going back. They had received Christ crucified, yet they were considering to go back to participate in offering animals as sin offerings with blood. They were in danger of considering the blood of Jesus the same as animal blood, making it common (v. 29). They were in danger of forsaking the meetings of the church either to appease their relatives, to have a political or diplomatic solution to the tension, or to escape persecution (v. 25).
God wrote an epistle to them through a prepared vessel, who we believe was Paul. This Epistle presents a marvelous contrast between the all-inclusive Christ and the entire system of Judaism that has now been replaced. There is much encouragement for the believers to go on, to advance, to run, to follow, and to continue. But there are also some serious warnings interspersed throughout the book These warnings concern the believers' accountability to God in His righteous administration, to not forsake the God-ordained way and, for the sake of their soul-life, go back to religion or work out a compromise. So there are a number of warnings which tell the believers, a If you forsake this way and disregard all the provisions, even all the bequests of the New Testament, you will face "God who is a consuming fire. You will give an account to Him. You were not saved for your convenience or on your terms. You were saved for God's glory, economy, and hearts desire."
The real-life situation was one of staggering, halting, hesitating, and then getting into the mind and wondering in the mind and thereby wandering in the soul. This Epistle was written by a brother who had gone on; he once had been where the recipients were, but he had gone on. He was where the Lord Jesus is. He was in the intrinsic reality of the new covenant, in the Spirit, in Christ, in the church, in the Sabbath rest, in the kingdom, and in the Holy of Holies. This brother called the brothers in one spirit with the Lord. He seemed to be saying, "Come forward. We are in God, in the Holy of Holies, in glory, in the New Jerusalem, in the church, and in the Spirit. Come forward. Look at this wonderful Christ in whom God is speaking. He is greater than Aaron because He is Melchisedec. He is greater than Moses, for this One is the Builder of the house. He is greater than Joshua, for this One is the real Joshua fighting the way. This One is a High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec, and we all are on His heart and on His shoulders." We may rest secure that the Lord's recovery is on the heart of our glorified High Priest. We not only need love; we also need strength. We are therefore on His shoulders, and nothing will happen to us that has not passed through the government of God in the glory and that has not been sustained by the Lord's priestly ministry. This book was written to those who are willing by God's grace to be river crossers.
The Meaning of Hebrew— to Pass Over
The root of the word Hebrew means "to pass over" (Gen. 14:13). The Hebrews are the passing-over people. It is no coincidence that when God's people were brought out of Egypt they experienced the Passover. Once they were brought out of Egypt, God brought them into the seemingly hopeless situation of the Red Sea in front and the Egyptian armies in back. Then they crossed a body of water, the Red Sea, and that crossing became their salvation. Every river is a veil separating you from God in the Holy of Holies. Every river that you do not cross becomes a veil to you, but every river that you cross becomes a salvation to you. The word Hebrew can specifically mean "to pass over a river,n that is, to pass over from one side of the river to another. A Hebrew is one who crosses a river, a person from the other side of the river.
There are times when we need to let God, the holy angels, the demons, and the evil spirits know that we will cross the river and enter into a new realm. Eventually, God told Moses to speak to Pharaoh: "Thus saith the Lord God of the Hebrews, Let my people g o "(Exo. 9:1). We should tell Pharaoh, the one who signifies the enemy Satan, "The God of the Hebrews has appeared to us; let God's people go." Will you be held enthralled to your job, to your career, or to your environment? You can speak to the enemy behind the scene: "My God is the God of the Hebrews. While I am an employee here I will be a faithful employee, but I am not your slave; I am a Hebrew. And my God is the God of the Hebrews." We are from the other side.
River crossers are a people separated from the world. Once you cross the river, it becomes a protection to you. It will not be that easy for something to follow you. The Egyptian forces followed the children of Israel into the sea but did not make it through. There is something about water that saves God's people and destroys God's enemy.
Abraham, the First River Grosser
Now we can fellowship about the first Hebrew, Abraham. In Genesis 14:13 he is called "Abram the Hebrew." He was the first river crosser (Heb. 11:8-16). Abraham left Chaldea, crossed the flood, and came into Canaan, the good land of blessing (Josh. 24:2-3). Abraham's crossing the river and entering into the new land signified his entering into an uplifted, new mankind, which would be used by God to be His expression.
The Bible often uses lands and cities to signify people. Egypt, for instance, signifies the worldly things, and Babylon, the idolatrous things. Abraham, who as far as we can tell was not a believer at that time, dwelt in Ur of the Chaldees; then he had a visit, an appearance,from a wonderful being, whom Stephen calls "the God of glory" (Acts 7:2). Have you ever considered why it was the God of glory who appeared to Abraham? We know from the ministry that the God of glory infuses His element into us so that we can respond to Him by believing into Him (see Life-study of Romans, Message Eight). Even the faith we need to cross the river has its source in Him.
It is crucial to see that the goal of river crossing is glory. The river crossers follow the Lord Jesus into the glorious expression of the Divine Being. You may be in a pitiful situation, but the God of glory appears, not to condemn you for being so pitiful, but to shine on you in such a way to indicate that from now on you are bound for glory.
You are glory bound, and as a son He has given you a taste of glory. The element of glory is in you. You have seen that shining and splendor, even if but for a moment. You have found out there is another realm you can be in. You now feel homeless on the earth. You feel like you do not belong here. You feel like an expatriate, a sojourner, who is looking for another place. You are looking for a city that has foundations, a city whose Architect and Builder is God (Heb. 11:10). The God of glory appeared as a way of indicating to the river crosser, am going to bring you into Myself as glory. I am going to make you a father of a multitude of people who will enter into glory with you."
Many can testify from experience that once the burden comes for migration they feel they no longer belong where they are. You belong to another place, to another state, to another city, to another country. You have to be on the move. There can be no migration without the appearing of the God of glory. I believe the God of glory is going to appear to many of us. He may wake you at night in His shining presence. You may take a walk in the morning, and He is just there shining Himself into you as a way of indicating that it is time for you to go where you have never been before. As a result of God's appearing, Abraham left Chaldea and eventually crossed the river into the good land.
The God of the Hebrews
Our God is the God of the Hebrews (Exo. 3:18; 9:1, 13). I love this expression. This title was spoken to Pharaoh. Is your God the God of the Hebrews? Are you a Hebrew? If this matter is settled, the root that keeps you colonized on the earth is cut. If you are not a Hebrew, you are not going anywhere. If you are a Hebrew, it is normal for you to go when God leads you to go. Your God is a God of the Hebrews, and He says, "I am to you the God of the Hebrews because you are one of My Hebrews." We may p ray j'0 God of the Hebrews, send some more Hebrews to Russia." What an indictment to Pharaoh! Satan does not possess, own, or control us. He does not run our lives; we are Hebrews.
It is time for the Lord's recovery to make a fresh declaration: "We are Hebrews! And our God is the God of the Hebrews."
As God, Christ is the God of the river crossers. But this wonderful God became a man, a Jew, a Nazarene, a Hebrew, a river crosser. He began His public ministry by crossing a river and burying everything under John the Baptist's river-crossing ministry (Mark 1:1, 9). River crossing brought in the age of the New Testament. Throughout His life, especially throughout His ministry, the Lord crossed river after river until He crossed the ultimate river of death. But before He did that, He prayed for His glorification (John 17:1). He prayed that through His death the element of glory in Him would be released and manifested in resurrection to produce a marvelous, divine-human incorporation. Before He died, He prayed, "Father, concerning that which You have given Me, I desire that they also may be with Me where I am, that they may behold My glory, which You have given Me" (v. 24). He prayed for us that we would enter into the glory of the expressed Triune God. Then He died and crossed the river. By crossing the river He rent the veil (Matt. 27:51). By rending the veil He opened the way into the glorious being of God. Now He is in glory. In a very real sense the book of Hebrews is part of the answer to the Lord's prayer for glorification. Now in glory He is ministering from the heavens as our glorious High Priest and the Captain of our salvation. Yet He is also the pneumatic Hebrew in our spirit, helping us to identify the rivers and enabling us to cross them all.
The God of the Hebrews is the God of the river crossers, the God of the separated people. You can testify that every time you cross a river, God is more real to you. When you cross a particular river, you will taste God as you have never tasted Him before. You may get the light to know that you can do this every day, even many times a day. There are many kinds of rivers to cross, and by crossing them you enter into God and become God in life and nature.
The God of the Hebrews is the God of the people who have crossed many rivers for the fulfillment of God's purpose. Sometimes you may not be sure why you are crossing a river. The whole church is crossing a river, and you are a simple believer, so you cross. That is good, but eventually, we should have the clear view of why we are doing something and what we;are doing. In simple words, we are here for God. We are here for the Lord and for His purpose. We are not here for ourselves, so we cross rivers to meet God's need. We cross rivers so that God's heart can rest. Have you ever prayed concerning God's rest? Do you have any burden concerning God's need to rest? The more we cross the river and fulfill God's purpose, the more He is satisfied and the more He rests. I hope that when we have the Lord's table together we will all sense we are in the Sabbath. I hope we will have a time to worship our Father, the God of the Hebrews, and enjoy with Him the sweet rest of the church life.
The Hebrews are a mysterious people; they always go on, and they are always at rest. They are at rest but not in the sense of being idle. If you do not go on, you will not have rest. We may pray, "Lord, do not give the Hebrews any rest until they move." When you begin to move, your whole being is in harmony. You are so peaceful and restful. You are not afraid anymore, and you are not striving anymore. You are on the move yet you are deeply at rest. This is the normal experience of a Hebrew enjoying the God of the Hebrews. The God of the Hebrews is the God who is always going on.
The Book of Hebrews Being Written to Us
The God of the Hebrews has written the book of Hebrews to us (1:1-2a; 12:25a). Many religious Christians neither know God as the God of the Hebrews nor call themselves Hebrews. If we admit that we are a Hebrew and that our God is the God of the Hebrews, then we will realize that there is an epistle in the New Testament written just to us.
Others can read it, but they may not get it. The God of the Hebrews would like to speak in the Son to all of the Hebrews (1:2). How precious it is to God that there are thousands on the earth who are willing to go on, willing to be Hebrews. God has something to say, and only Hebrews can understand it.
God is speaking in the Son. The God of the Hebrews is speaking to the Hebrews. The basic concept of the book of Hebrews is that of crossing the river. This concept runs throughout the book. The basic thought of this book is crossing over from where you are to a wonderful realm where Christ is everything, the Body of Christ is real to you, God is glorified, the kingdom is a reality, and we all have rest.
Our prayer is that the light will dawn on us concerning our God and also ourselves that we are Hebrews. If we are world lovers and world dwellers, this book will pass us by. There is a warning in Hebrews 2:1: ('Therefore we ought to give heed more abundantly to the things which have been heard lest perhaps we drift away." There are two kinds of Christians in the Lord's recovery: those who drift and those who cross. When you get up in the morning you cannot drift into the Spirit or into the Holy of Holies. You have to cross. If you do not cross, you will drift. You may consider that you have a quiet disposition, and that you are neither assertive nor aggressive, and therefore you cannot be a Hebrew.
However, God is the God of the Hebrews. He is able to infuse His river-crossing capacity into you. Will you exercise your God-given will and get into the spirit, or will you drift? None of us can make it alone.
Let us cross this river together. If we have crossed the river, we will have the capacity to understand this wonderful, rich, deep, and profound book. The basic concept of the book of Hebrews is that of crossing the river, of passing over from one side to another. The Epistle to the Hebrews is only for river crossers. If we have crossed the river, we can understand this wonderful book (5:12; 6:1).
GOD'S SPEAKING PRODUCING RIVER CROSSERS
The purpose of God's speaking in the Epistle to the Hebrews is that all who contact God by faith would be river crossers (11:1, 6). The God of the Hebrews became a man. As a man He lived the life of a river crosser. His whole life was a life of river crossing. When He was twelve He was in the temple. He crossed the river to be apart from His family for a while to pursue the things of His Father. When His parents found Him, He asked them, "Did you not know that I must be in the things of My Father?" (Luke 2:49). Then He crossed the river again and went back to Nazareth to be a son under Joseph and Mary. At the age of thirty He crossed another river. He laid aside His carpenter's tools. He left His homeland and He began to minister; that is, He began to pro claim the jubilee (4:17-19), He faced religious and political opposition and frustrations even from His followers. But He crossed every river until He crossed the final river. Then God resurrected Him and the glory was manifested. He entered into the glory and He is now the pattern for us. He knows that sometimes we cross rivers with tears, with broken hearts, realizing that a price is being paid to cross over. The other rivers may have been easy, but the one we may now be facing may not be easy. The rivers seem to get swifter, deeper, and colder as we go on. However, our faith is now stronger, the glory is richer, the truth is wrought more into our being, and Christ is more real to us.
Sometimes the God of the Hebrews who is the God of glory comes in and He takes a certain dear one across that ultimate river, as He took our Brother Lee. I believe our brother's crossing that river was something meant by God to open the way for us to go on. If I have to go to the Lord before He comes, I want to go the way our brother went- in glory, in triumph, in victory, as a real Hebrew.
The Lord Jesus Himself is our ultimate model of a river crosser. He is our Pioneer. He was the first man to enter into the being of the Father where He is now resting, praying, ministering, and fighting until each one of us is in fact where He is, in the glorious being of God. After all the ages, God's elected, redeemed, and regenerated people will enjoy an eternal Sabbath rest in the glorious expressed being of God. We will partake of His rest and satisfaction in seeing the desire of His heart fulfilled by a multitude of people expressing His glorious image and reigning with His authority as co-heirs with His Son in the new heaven and new earth forever and ever.
The Lord Jesus Entering into God's Glory
In suffering death, the Lord Jesus crossed the river and entered into the region of God's expression, the region of God's glory (Heb. 2:9; 6:19-20). The expression of the processed and consummated Triune God is glory. The Triune God is expressed in and through the enlarged, universal, divine-human incorporation. He is going to sanctify, transform, glorify, and bring you into maturity. He is going to make you Himself in life and nature. The death through which the Lord Jesus passed was a baptism (Mark 10:38; Luke 12:50). As the real Joshua, the Lord Jesus took the lead to cross the Jordan River and enter into glory th e reality of the expression of the Divine Being (Luke 24:26; 1 Pet. 1:11). As the Captain of our salvation, Christ has gone ahead of us that He might help us pass through the waters of suffering and enter into glory (Heb. 2:10). In His ascension the Lord not only went to a place; He went to a person. When the Lord ascended, He entered into the being of the Father and sat down there. The glorified God-man Jesus is resting in the being of the Father. He wants us to be there as well. There are many rivers between here and there. He knows how to cross rivers. He will never leave you by yourself.
As believers in the wonderful person of Christ, we have a wonderful status in the so-great salvation of God. As believers in Christ we should be river crossers. We do not merely cross rivers. We are a certain category of person, river crossers; we are persons whose being is a river-crossing being and whose life is a river-crossing life. We cannot do this by our own initiative, but by following the Lord Jesus.
Following the Forerunner, Jesus, into Glory
Baptism has made us river crossers, and now we are true, genuine Hebrews, following the Pioneer, "the Forerunner, Jesus," into glory (Rom. 6:3; Gal. 3:27; Matt. 28:19; Heb. 6:20). A believer who has not been properly baptized may not have rest until he is immersed into water, indicating that he is being put into the processed and consummated Triune God. Baptism makes us river crossers. To cross the river is to wash ourselves from anything old, anything ruined, and anything that does not match God's glory (Rev. 15:2-3). There was a laver in front of the temple for a reason. In 1 Kings the enlarged laver is called a sea (7:23-26), Everyone had to wash their hands and their feet there. Our hands may have touched things that have made us old and defiled. Our feet may have taken us to improper places. The blood is applied to our conscience, but we still need to wash our being. Some of us need a washing of our inner being. We should immerse ourselves in the laver of the Word and let the living and abiding rhema of God wash our whole inner being until we are new and crystal clear. The more we are washed, the more we cross. We touch the Lord in the morning through the Word, He washes our being, and we come out on the other side. Every time we desire to enter into the temple, we need to cross a standing river—the laver (Exo. 30:18; 40:30-32; 1 Kings 7:23, 38).
Before we come to a meeting, we need to have this consideration. We should not come to a meeting without crossing the river. We should not bring the oldness, the stain of the earth, into the tabernacle. We should wash. Many of us remember to pray for the application of the blood, but we may not yet have the thought to pray for the washing of the laver. The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, which is God Himself expressed.
Entering into the New Jerusalem by Crossing Rivers
If we would enter into the New Jerusalem as the full expression of the glorious God (Heb. 11:10, 16; 12:22; Rev. 21:2, 10-11), we need to cross over many rivers (see note 2 of Heb. 1:1). We need to leave the law and cross over to grace (4:16; 7:18-19; 12:28; 13:9). What a blessing if the elders themselves know how to practice all the things of God's economy in the way of grace. We need to be grace-husbands, not law-husbands, and grace-brothers, not law-brothers. We need to leave the old covenant, the soul, and cross over to the new covenant, the spirit (8:6-7, 13). We need to leave the ritualistic service of the Old Testament and cross over to the spiritual reality of the New Testament (v. 5; 9:9-14). Reality is on this side of the river. As soon as we cross, we touch reality. We need to leave Judaism, Christianity, and our self-made religion and cross over to the church (13:13; 10:25). We want to be the church, pure and simple, according to the God-ordained way. We need to leave the earthly things and cross over to the heavenly things (12:18-24), We need to leave the outer court and cross over to the Holiest of all (13:9-10; 10:19-20).
If you sense you are far off, that may be an inner clue that you are in the outer court. It is delightful to be in the Holy of Holies. The blood has been shed, and you may apply it with boldness to walk all the way in and enjoy the God of glory. We need to leave the soul and cross over to the spirit (Heb. 4:12). We have to cross over this river many times during the day. Deny the soul. Cross the river into the spirit. We need to leave the beginning of truth and life and cross over to the maturity of life in the truth (5:11- 6:1). It is a shame if our growth in life is stopped. It is a shame if there is any system or control in any place in the Lord's recovery that would hinder the saints from growing. We do not have any second class Hebrews. We all have a God-given capacity to understand the divine things. We want to grow normally in the divine life unto maturity and understand the deep things of God, which is our new covenant.
GOD'S ECONOMY AND A DISPENSATIONAL TRANSFER
As today s Hebrews, the river crossers, we need to see God's economy (1 Tim. 1:4) and experience the dispensational transfer revealed in the book of Acts (1:4-8). The Lord, as the pneumatic Christ, spoke many things to His disciples during the forty days of post-resurrection, pre-ascension training (v. 3). At the end of those days of training, the brothers asked, "Lord, are You at this time restoring the kingdom to Israel?" (v. 6). Their thought was still concerning the physical and outward Israel. The Lord said, "It is not for you to know times or seasons" (v. 7). He seemed to be saying that the time was not for the physical and outward Israel, but the time, the age, the dispensation, was for them to be witnesses. a You shall be My witnesses both in Jerusalem and in all Judea and Samaria and unto the uttermost part of the earth" (v. 8). The Lord was saying that He was changing the dispensation, the way of God's dealing with His people to carry out His economy.
The book of Acts is a book about dispensational change. There were some who made it and some who did not. There were also grievous problems caused by those who compromised. They came into the new dispensation but they would not leave the old. So they ended up with a mixture. That became so serious and so intolerable to God that He destroyed Jerusalem and everything in it, obliterating the temple. Any compromising ones that remained there in all likelihood perished with it. Our God is absolute. The God of the Hebrews is absolute. When He makes a change, He makes a change. And He wants to transfer us absolutely into the realm of His New Testament economy. The matter of the change of dispensation—the divine arrangement in God's eternal economy—is a strong point in Acts.
To speak of a dispensational change in Acts means that in Acts we see the need of a great transfer, a great turn. This change, this transfer, is from the old dispensation to the new. We need to be transferred economically from the Old Testament to the New Testament. Although we are New Testament people, we may still have an Old Testament concept. For example, the Pentecostals prophesy in an Old Testament way when they say, "Thus saith the Lord." The Catholics worship in an Old Testament way particularly in their ritualistic practices. The Calvinists, the Reformed theologians, and their followers practice and emphasize the law. How sad that we could be Old Testament Christians! Instead of trying to live according to self-made laws, we must let Christ live in us. Instead of worshipping in a form or ritual, we must worship genuinely in spirit and reality.
We need a transfer out of the law and into Christ, a transfer from being law-keepers to being Jesus-witnesses. In the Life-study of Acts, Brother Lee said, "In the book of Hebrews Paul presents a clear picture showing us that the Old Testament things are over. What remains now in God's New Testament economy is Jesus Christ, the all-inclusive One.
Having seen such a view, Paul could not tolerate a mixture of such an all-inclusive Christ with the inferior things of the out-of-date, Old Testament economy" (p. 518 ).-R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第二篇　神在子里说话
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来一1～2上，徒六7，十二24，十九20。
壹　我们的神是启示出来的神，因为祂已经在祂的说话中，将自己启示出来─来一1～2上：
一　今天在主的恢复中，神的话是繁增的，也是洋溢的─徒六7，十二24，十九20：
１　一天又一天，一个聚会又一个聚会，神一直在说话─利一1。
２　神向我们说话，证明我们是在祂的祝福之下─参撒上三1，10，21。
３　信徒生命的关键，完全在于主的说话─来一3，太十七5，启二7上，歌八13，来五13～14。
４　活神借着说话，将祂自己分赐并注入到我们里面─提后三16～17，结三七4～6：
a　神一说话，光就照亮，带给我们领悟、异象、知识、智慧和口才─诗一一九105，130。
b　神一说话，生命就分赐出来，而这生命包含基督一切神圣的属性和人性的美德─约六63，一1，4。
c　神一说话，能力就传输出来，而这是生命生长并繁殖的能力─可四14，26。
d　“就是主带我离开这个国家，我也确实知道，主的恢复仍然要一直往前，因为这不是人为的运动，乃是神圣生命生长的行动。种子已经撒在这里，神的话已经有了，生命也在这里了。阿利路亚！神的话在那里，生命的能力就在那里。现在我们知道神为什么要说话，祂说话是为着光照，为着点活，为着加给能力。”（希伯来书生命读经，三二页。）
二　整个宇宙是凭神说话而有的─罗四17，来十一3，诗三三9。
三　在旧约，神借着众申言者，就是借着被神的灵推动的人，多分多方向列祖说话─彼后一21。
四　在新约，神在子里，就是在子的人位里说话：
１　这个人位先是个人的，然后成了团体的。
２　神今天是在一个人位里说话，而这人位已经扩增为一个团体的人，包括众使徒和这人位之身体的众肢体─林前十四4下，31。
五　希伯来书的素质，乃是神在子里的说话：
１　神在子里说话，子作为那灵向众召会说话；至终，那灵同着召会说话─启二7上，二二17。
２　乃是完全借着这说话的故事，神被带到人里面，人也被带到神里面。
贰　使徒的教训，就是神新约经纶独一的教训，乃是神在子里的说话─神在新约中的整个说话，论到基督在三个时期中丰满的职事─徒二42，提前一3～4，六3，来一1～2上：
一　首先，神在为人的子里，在四福音里说话，启示基督在成肉体的时期─约十四10，五24，十六12，十30：
１　将无限的神带到有限的人里面。
２　将三一神与三部分人联结并调和一起。
３　借着祂芬芳的美德，在祂的人性里彰显全备之神丰富的属性。
４　完成祂包罗万有之法理的救赎。
二　第二，神在是那灵的子里，借着使徒，在使徒行传和二十一卷书信（罗马书到犹大书）里说话，启示基督在总括的时期─约十六12～15，太二八19～20，来二3～4，彼后三15～16，西一25～27：
１　生为神的长子。
２　成为赐生命的灵。
３　为着祂的身体重生众信徒。
三　第三，神在那是七灵的子里，借着使徒约翰，在启示录里说话，启示基督在加强的时期：
１　加强祂生机的救恩。
２　产生得胜者。
３　终极完成新耶路撒冷。
四　正确的职事乃是照着使徒教训的职事，而使徒的教训就是神新约经纶的教训，为着建造基督的身体，以终极完成新耶路撒冷。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Two
God's Speaking in the Son
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 1:1-2a; Acts 6:7; 12:24; 19:20
I.　Our God is the revealed God because He has revealed Himself in His speaking—Heb. 1:1-2a:
A.　In the Lord's recovery today there is the multiplication and abundance of God's word—Acts 6:7; 12:24; 19:20:
1.　God is speaking day after day and meeting after meeting—Lev. 1:1.
2.　God's speaking to us proves that we are under His blessing—cf. 1 Sam. 3:1, 10, 21.
3.　The life of the believers hinges totally upon the Lord's speaking—Heb. 1:3; Matt. 17:5; Rev. 2:7a; S. S. 8:13; Heb. 5:13-14.
4.　The living God imparts and infuses Himself into us by speaking—2 Tim. 3:16-17; Ezek. 37:4-6:
a.　When God speaks, the light shines, bringing us understanding, vision, knowledge, wisdom, and utterance—Psa. 119:105, 130.
b.　When God speaks, life is imparted, and this life includes all the divine attributes and human virtues of Christ—John 6:63; 1:1, 4.
c.　When God speaks, power is transmitted, and this is the growing and reproducing power of life—Mark 4:14, 26.
d.　"If the Lord would take me away from this country, I would have the complete assurance that the Lord's recovery in this country would still go on because it is not a movement of man's doing. It is a move of the divine life growing. The seed has been sown here, the word has come, and life is here. Hallelujah! Where the word is, there is power in life. Now we can see why God speaks. He speaks to shine, to enliven, and to empower" (Life-study of Hebrews,?p. 26).
B.　The whole universe came into being by God's speaking—Rom. 4:17; Heb. 11:3; Psa. 33:9.
C.　In the Old Testament, God spoke in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets, in men borne by His Spirit—2 Pet. 1:21.
D.　In the New Testament, God speaks in the Son, in the person of the Son:
1.　This person was firstly individual and then became corporate.
2.　God today speaks in a person, and this person has been increased to be a corporate person, including all the apostles and all the members of this person's Body—1 Cor. 14:4b, 31.
E.　The essence of the Epistle to the Hebrews is God's speaking in the Son:
1.　God speaks in the Son, the Son speaks as the Spirit to the churches, and ultimately the Spirit speaks with the church—Rev. 2:7a; 22:17.
2.　It is altogether by this speaking story that God is brought into man and man is brought into God.
II.　The apostles' teaching, the unique teaching of God's New Testament economy, is God's speaking in the Son—the entire speaking of God in the New Testament concerning the full ministry of Christ in three stages—Acts 2:42; 1 Tim. 1:3-4; 6:3; Heb. 1:1-2a:
A.　First, God spoke in the Son as a man in the four Gospels, revealing the stage of Christ in incarnation—John 14:10; 5:24; 16:12; 10:30:
1.　To bring the infinite God into the finite man.
2.　To unite and mingle the Triune God with the tripartite man.
3.　To express in His humanity the bountiful God in His rich attributes through His aromatic virtues.
4.　To accomplish His all-inclusive judicial redemption.
B.　Second, God spoke in the Son as the Spirit through the apostles in Acts and the twenty-one Epistles (Romans through Jude), revealing the stage of Christ in inclusion—John 16:12-15; Matt. 28:19-20; Heb. 2:3-4; 2 Pet. 3:15-16; Col. 1:25-27:
1.　To be begotten as God's firstborn Son.
2.　To become the life-giving Spirit.
3.　To regenerate the believers for His Body.
C.　Third, God spoke in the Son as the seven Spirits through the apostle John in Revelation, revealing the stage of Christ in intensification:
1.　To intensify His organic salvation.
2.　To produce the overcomers.
3.　To consummate the New Jerusalem.
D.　The proper ministry is the ministry according to the apostles' teaching, the teaching of God's New Testament economy, for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第二篇　神在子里说话
God's Speaking in the Son
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来一1～2上，徒六7，十二24，十九20。
壹　我们的神是启示出来的神，因为祂已经在祂的说话中，将自己启示出来─来一1～2上：
I.　Our God is the revealed God because He has revealed Himself in His speaking—Heb. 1:1-2a:
一　今天在主的恢复中，神的话是繁增的，也是洋溢的─徒六7，十二24，十九20：
A.　In the Lord's recovery today there is the multiplication and abundance of God's word—Acts 6:7; 12:24; 19:20:
１　一天又一天，一个聚会又一个聚会，神一直在说话─利一1。
1.　God is speaking day after day and meeting after meeting—Lev. 1:1.
２　神向我们说话，证明我们是在祂的祝福之下─参撒上三1，10，21。
2.　God's speaking to us proves that we are under His blessing—cf. 1 Sam. 3:1, 10, 21.
３　信徒生命的关键，完全在于主的说话─来一3，太十七5，启二7上，歌八13，来五13～14。
3.　The life of the believers hinges totally upon the Lord's speaking—Heb. 1:3; Matt. 17:5; Rev. 2:7a; S. S. 8:13; Heb. 5:13-14.
４　活神借着说话，将祂自己分赐并注入到我们里面─提后三16～17，结三七4～6：
4.　The living God imparts and infuses Himself into us by speaking—2 Tim. 3:16-17; Ezek. 37:4-6:
a　神一说话，光就照亮，带给我们领悟、异象、知识、智慧和口才─诗一一九105，130。
a.　When God speaks, the light shines, bringing us understanding, vision, knowledge, wisdom, and utterance—Psa. 119:105, 130.
b　神一说话，生命就分赐出来，而这生命包含基督一切神圣的属性和人性的美德─约六63，一1，4。
b.　When God speaks, life is imparted, and this life includes all the divine attributes and human virtues of Christ—John 6:63; 1:1, 4.
c　神一说话，能力就传输出来，而这是生命生长并繁殖的能力─可四14，26。
c.　When God speaks, power is transmitted, and this is the growing and reproducing power of life—Mark 4:14, 26.
d　“就是主带我离开这个国家，我也确实知道，主的恢复仍然要一直往前，因为这不是人为的运动，乃是神圣生命生长的行动。种子已经撒在这里，神的话已经有了，生命也在这里了。阿利路亚！神的话在那里，生命的能力就在那里。现在我们知道神为什么要说话，祂说话是为着光照，为着点活，为着加给能力。”（希伯来书生命读经，三二页。）
d.　"If the Lord would take me away from this country, I would have the complete assurance that the Lord's recovery in this country would still go on because it is not a movement of man's doing. It is a move of the divine life growing. The seed has been sown here, the word has come, and life is here. Hallelujah! Where the word is, there is power in life. Now we can see why God speaks. He speaks to shine, to enliven, and to empower" (Life-study of Hebrews,?p. 26).
二　整个宇宙是凭神说话而有的─罗四17，来十一3，诗三三9。
B.　The whole universe came into being by God's speaking—Rom. 4:17; Heb. 11:3; Psa. 33:9.
三　在旧约，神借着众申言者，就是借着被神的灵推动的人，多分多方向列祖说话─彼后一21。
C.　In the Old Testament, God spoke in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets, in men borne by His Spirit—2 Pet. 1:21.
四　在新约，神在子里，就是在子的人位里说话：
D.　In the New Testament, God speaks in the Son, in the person of the Son:
１　这个人位先是个人的，然后成了团体的。
1.　This person was firstly individual and then became corporate.
２　神今天是在一个人位里说话，而这人位已经扩增为一个团体的人，包括众使徒和这人位之身体的众肢体─林前十四4下，31。
2.　God today speaks in a person, and this person has been increased to be a corporate person, including all the apostles and all the members of this person's Body—1 Cor. 14:4b, 31.
五　希伯来书的素质，乃是神在子里的说话：
E.　The essence of the Epistle to the Hebrews is God's speaking in the Son:
１　神在子里说话，子作为那灵向众召会说话；至终，那灵同着召会说话─启二7上，二二17。
1.　God speaks in the Son, the Son speaks as the Spirit to the churches, and ultimately the Spirit speaks with the church—Rev. 2:7a; 22:17.
２　乃是完全借着这说话的故事，神被带到人里面，人也被带到神里面。
2.　It is altogether by this speaking story that God is brought into man and man is brought into God.
贰　使徒的教训，就是神新约经纶独一的教训，乃是神在子里的说话─神在新约中的整个说话，论到基督在三个时期中丰满的职事─徒二42，提前一3～4，六3，来一1～2上：
II.　The apostles' teaching, the unique teaching of God's New Testament economy, is God's speaking in the Son—the entire speaking of God in the New Testament concerning the full ministry of Christ in three stages—Acts 2:42; 1 Tim. 1:3-4; 6:3; Heb. 1:1-2a:
一　首先，神在为人的子里，在四福音里说话，启示基督在成肉体的时期─约十四10，五24，十六12，十30：
A.　First, God spoke in the Son as a man in the four Gospels, revealing the stage of Christ in incarnation—John 14:10; 5:24; 16:12; 10:30:
１　将无限的神带到有限的人里面。
1.　To bring the infinite God into the finite man.
２　将三一神与三部分人联结并调和一起。
2.　To unite and mingle the Triune God with the tripartite man.
３　借着祂芬芳的美德，在祂的人性里彰显全备之神丰富的属性。
3.　To express in His humanity the bountiful God in His rich attributes through His aromatic virtues.
４　完成祂包罗万有之法理的救赎。
4.　To accomplish His all-inclusive judicial redemption.
二　第二，神在是那灵的子里，借着使徒，在使徒行传和二十一卷书信（罗马书到犹大书）里说话，启示基督在总括的时期─约十六12～15，太二八19～20，来二3～4，彼后三15～16，西一25～27：
B.　Second, God spoke in the Son as the Spirit through the apostles in Acts and the twenty-one Epistles (Romans through Jude), revealing the stage of Christ in inclusion—John 16:12-15; Matt. 28:19-20; Heb. 2:3-4; 2 Pet. 3:15-16; Col. 1:25-27:
１　生为神的长子。
1.　To be begotten as God's firstborn Son.
２　成为赐生命的灵。
2.　To become the life-giving Spirit.
３　为着祂的身体重生众信徒。
3.　To regenerate the believers for His Body.
三　第三，神在那是七灵的子里，借着使徒约翰，在启示录里说话，启示基督在加强的时期：
C.　Third, God spoke in the Son as the seven Spirits through the apostle John in Revelation, revealing the stage of Christ in intensification:
１　加强祂生机的救恩。
1.　To intensify His organic salvation.
２　产生得胜者。
2.　To produce the overcomers.
３　终极完成新耶路撒冷。
3.　To consummate the New Jerusalem.
四　正确的职事乃是照着使徒教训的职事，而使徒的教训就是神新约经纶的教训，为着建造基督的身体，以终极完成新耶路撒冷。
D.　The proper ministry is the ministry according to the apostles' teaching, the teaching of God's New Testament economy, for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TWO
GOD'S SPEAKING IN THE SON
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Hebrews 1:1-2 says, "God, having spoken of old in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets, has at the last of these days spoken to us in the Son, whom He appointed Heir of all things, through whom also He made the universe Hebrews is unique in that it does not identify the writer of the book. Most of the Epistles in the New Testament identify the writer. Paul identifies himself in a certain way, and Peter does the same. However, in Hebrews there is no identification of the writer, because this book emphasizes God's speaking. This book is God's speaking, not mans speaking. When this book quotes passages from the Old Testament, it purposefully does not mention the names of the speakers or the writers in the Old Testament (9:8; 10:15-17). For example, when the writer quotes from Psalm 95 (Heb. 3:7), he does not refer to the writer of the psalm; instead, he says it is the Holy Spirit who is speaking, in order to emphasize that this book is God's speaking. Furthermore, God's speaking has gone through a dispensational transfer from the speaking through the prophets to the speaking in the Son.
The opening word of this book says that God has spoken (Heb. 1:1). God is identified and characterized as the One who has spoken. Some may characterize God as the One who created the universe or the One who in our experience saved us from the tyranny of sin. Yet in this book the defining characteristic of God is that He has spoken. He is the speaking God who unveils Himself by His speaking. In the Old Testament He spoke "of old in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets" (v. 1). Now at the last of these days, God's speaking is in the person of the Son (v. 2). The phrase at the last of these days is a very particular Hebrew expression indicating the end of the dispensation of the law, when the Messiah was to be introduced (Isa. 2:2; Micah 4:1). He no longer speaks in the prophets as in the days of old; today His speaking is altogether in the person of the Son. Apart from the Son, God has no speaking. Hebrews is a book of God's speaking in the person of the Son.
GOD REVEALING HIMSELF IN HIS SPEAKING
Our God is the revealed God because He has revealed Himself in His speaking (Heb. 1:1-2a). In the Lord's recovery today there is the multiplication and abundance of God's word (Acts 6:7; 12:24; 19:20). If we were void of God's speaking, our coming together would be in vain. It would be no different from a trade association or a religious convention where there is nothing but some entertainment and socialization. The Lord has kept our coming together in meetings, conferences, and trainings for His speaking. The characteristic of the Lord's recovery must be the multiplication of His speaking in our midst.
God is speaking day after day and meeting after meeting. God's speaking to us proves that we are under His blessing (cf. 1 Sam. 3:1, 10, 21). Leviticus 1:1 says that God spoke out of the tent of meeting. The tent of meeting was God's ordained place where His people were to meet together. They were not to meet just anywhere, because they would be devoid of God's speaking. They had to meet together at the tent of meeting because it was only there that God spoke. Likewise, God's speaking in our midst is a true validation that we are on the proper ground. If we were not on th e,proper ground, we would be devoid of God's speaking, or God's speaking would eventually wane and disappear. We have the assurance that our meetings are on the proper ground at the tent of meeting because God's oracle is here and God's speaking is richly with us. Intrinsically, where there is God's speaking, we are under God's blessing, and under God's blessing the entire curse disappears. The days of Eli, the priest, were a time of degradation, and in that situation God's word was rare (1 Sam. 3:1). This may be the situation among Christians today. Mostly one hears man's speaking rather than God's speaking. In the Lord's recovery we are under the Lord's blessing, and His speaking is so abundant here.
The life of the believers hinges totally upon the Lord's speaking (Heb. 1:3; Matt. 17:5; Rev. 2:7a; S. S. 8:13; Heb, 5:13-14). Our Christian life must be one that is entirely dependent on the Lord's speaking. Hebrews 1:3 says that He upholds and bears all things by the word of His power. We are included in the "all things" which He upholds. We are held together and borne by the word of His power. In Greek, word is rhema meaning the instant word. If we were devoid of the word of God, we would have many problems in the church life. How can there be an enjoyable, sweet atmosphere when there are perhaps hundreds of people with different backgrounds and dispositions coming together frequently? This is only possible because we are upheld by the word of His power. This power is not a miraculous power but a life power. Matthew 17:5 refers to the time the Lord brought three of His disciples to the Mount of Transfiguration. There the Lord taught them one lesson: that the Christian life is no longer a matter of Moses (the law) or Elijah (the prophets), but a matter of hearing Him. It is His speaking and our hearing that causes us to progress in our Christian life. Revelation 2:7 says, "He who has an ear, let him hear what the Spirit says to the churches." To be an overcoming Christian we all have to hear. The second to last verse of Song of Songs says, "My companions listen for your voice; / Let me hear it" (8:13). Here the seeker of the Lord, at the end of her pursuit and in the last stage of maturity, has learned this one thing- that she needs to hear the Lord's voice. She longs to hear the Lord's voice. The seeker learns the same lesson taught to the disciples on the Mount of Transfiguration, which was to hear Him. She has this longing, not only by herself, but also with her companions, to hear the voice of her beloved. Hebrews 5:13-14 and 6:1 tell us that our being brought on to maturity has much to do with the word, particularly the word of righteousness. The Lord's speaking to us, both corporately and individually, must progressively go from the rudimentary things to the word of righteousness that we may be brought on to maturity. Our Christian life absolutely hinges on the Lord's speaking.
The living God imparts and infuses Himself into us by speaking (2 Tim. 3:16-17; Ezek. 37:4-6), God's speaking is not mere words. When He speaks, something of God is imparted into us. When God speaks, the light shines, bringing us understanding, vision, knowledge, wisdom, and utterance (Psa. 119:105, 130). The friends of Job thought they had understanding, but it is the shining that comes from the word of God that gives us understanding. As a result of His speaking, we understand matters pertaining to God, God's economy, man, our self, and even the world. In addition to understanding, there is vision. When we see something, the vision changes us and often causes us to take another way. As a result of seeing something of God's recovery and of His move on the earth today, we will have a change. God's speaking gives knowledge and wisdom. Also, if we are under God's speaking, we have utterance. There has probably never before been such a group of Christians coming together where everyone can speak. Brother Nee was quite burdened that the meetings in the local churches would not be just one mans speaking. He said that the practice of one man speaking on the Lord's Day was according to the custom of the nations (2 Kings 17:8). Yet at that time there was no practical substitute to this kind of meeting. Thank the Lord that today the Lord's Day morning meetings of the local churches are full of many attendants prophesying, each speaking forth the word of God, one after another. This happens week after week. Many saints have such a rich utterance of the word of God. The Lord has fulfilled in our midst what our Brother Nee spoke of and perhaps even dreamed of in the 1940s. This came about by all these years of being under God's speaking. God's speaking causes the shining of the light and the shining causes us to have the utterance to also speak the word of God. If you are under God's speaking, you cannot be silent. The speaking which imparts God into you will also make you one who speaks the word of God.
When God speaks, life is imparted, and this life includes all the divine attributes and human virtues of Christ (John 6:63; 1:1, 4). First the light shines, and then life is imparted. This life is the divine life, including the divine attributes and human virtues of Christ. We must have confidence in the life of God. It is tempting, especially for the leading ones and responsible ones, to immediately exercise their human hand to adjust when they see something that is not good or proper. However, outward human adjustment is like the work of the mortician to beautify something dead without imparting any life into it. We have to trust in God's speaking, which will impart the divine life with all the divine attributes and all the human virtues of Christ.
When God speaks, power is transmitted, and this is the growing and reproducing power of life (Mark 4:14, 26). This power is not a miraculous power. Mark 4 speaks of the sowing of the seed, which is the word of life. The seed has power within it, which is not miraculous. The seed does not turn into a big tree overnight; instead, the seed has the life power that overcomes everything, day by day, to grow and reproduce life. This is an illustration of the life power that comes with God's speaking. In the Life-study of Hebrews, page 26, Brother Lee says,
In the churches in the Lord's recovery we have no mushrooming developments. If the brothers in a certain locality would claim to have gained three thousand people in a week, I would say, "Let us wait and see. After a short time, most of them will disappear." We like to see the Word being sown and grown in the church field. Year after year it will grow and reproduce. You wait and see. Nothing will last so prevailingly as what is in the churches. It will be prevailing and it will endure forever because the power here is nothing other than life. This life comes from the Word. The Word brings in life, and life remains and reproduces. If the Lord would take me away from this country, I would have the complete assurance that the Lord's recovery in this country would still go on because it is not a movement of mans doing. It is a move of the divine life growing. The seed has been sown here, the Word has come, and life is here. Hallelujah! Where the Word is, there is power in life. Now we can see why God speaks. He speaks to shine, to enliven, and to empower.
Many wondered what would happen to the recovery after our Brother Lee went to be with the Lord. We surely miss our brother; however, the Lord's recovery has not stopped, and neither has God's speaking stopped. The seed has been sown, and it is growing and reproducing. If you are a doubter, have faith and take the unique way of faith. Have faith in this life-seed which has been sown by the speaking of the word of God. This life is still reproducing, and the word of God is still multiplying. Acts refers to the word of God growing mightily, and prevailing (6:7; 12:24; 19:20). That the word of God can grow indicates that the word of God is living. It has life power in it. According to Acts 6:7, "The word of God grew, and the number of the disciples in Jerusalem multiplied greatly." We are not for an increase in numbers merely in the way of a movement. Our hearts ache for the seeking ones, for the children of God who are starving and who are devoid of the riches of God's word. In this sense we pray for these ones that they would be brought into the Lord's recovery. However, before there is the increase in number, there must be the word growing and multiplying. The word growing and multiplying precedes the increase in numbers. Many have joined in this labor for the propagation of the New Testament ministry by serving or by giving financially that the word of God would grow and multiply. We have the full confidence that when the word grows, the numbers will increase. We all have been under the shining and impartation of life and the life power. We are now together propagating the speaking of God. We have the confidence that the Lord's recovery has a very bright future.
The whole universe came into being by God's speaking (Rom. 4:17; Heb. 11:3; Psa. 33:9). In the Old Testament, God spoke in many portions and in many ways to the fathers in the prophets, in men borne by His Spirit (2 Pet. 1:21). In the Old Testament God spoke not just once in one way. He spoke in many portions, in many ways, meeting many needs. He did not just speak one way at one time, but in many portions, in many ways, to the fathers in the prophets. These were men being borne by the Spirit, just as a ship borne by the wind. Furthermore, they spoke out of the will, desire, and wish of God.
Now there has been a dispensational change. In the New Testament, God speaks in the Son, in the person of the Son. God's speaking today in the New Testament age is entirely wrapped up with the Son. If there is no Son, there is no speaking. God's speaking is in the Son. This Son is firstly individual and then corporate. Individually, the Lord Jesus is the Son of God, the Only Begotten who was incarnated to be a man. He lived on the earth, and as He was living, He spoke the word of God by words, works, and actions. Sometimes He ,spoke by words, and other times He spoke by His being or by His actions. He was God's speaking in the Son. He spoke God. "No one has ever seen God; the only begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He has declared Him"(John 1:18). He expressed, defined, and explained God. God spoke in Himself, in Himself as the Son, as the One who is God expressed. The Son is God (Heb. 1:8); therefore, God's speaking in the Son is God speaking in Himself.
As the God-man who was God incarnated to be a man, He spoke God. Then He spoke as an individual; now His speaking is through the corporate Son. God speaks in a person, and this person has been increased to be a corporate person, including all the apostles and all the members of this person's Body (1 Cor. 14:4b, 31). All the speaking of the apostles was God's speaking in the Son. Paul said that he completed the word of God (Col. 1:25). He had the confidence that what he spoke was God's speaking. God spoke through Paul, but not because he was a smart and educated man. If he had remained Saul of Tarsus, God would not have been able to speak through him. God can only speak in the Son. Saul of Tarsus became a son of God, and even became one of the top ones to be "sonized" that is, to be saturated with the divine life and nature of Christ. God can speak in the Son, not only through the apostles, but also through all the members of the Body of Christ. We are the sons of God, born of God, God's kind, God's species, becoming God in life and nature but not in the Godhead. The sons of God together constitute the Body of Christ. The sons of God are the members of the Body of Christ. Today God's speaking in the Son is in this corporate person. This person was firstly individual, and then corporate.
The essence of the Epistle to the Hebrews is God's speaking in the Son. This Son, in whom God is speaking, is superior to the angels, superior to Moses, superior to Aaron, and the One who has enacted a better covenant. God speaks in the Son, the Son speaks as the Spirit to the churches, and ultimately the Spirit speaks with the church (Rev. 2:7a; 22:17). In Revelation 2 the Spirit speaks to the churches, and consummately in Revelation 22 the Spirit speaks with the church; this shows a process. By His speaking as the Spirit to the churches, He imparts Himself into the church to the point that we become fully one with Him. Ultimately, the Spirit and the bride speak together as one. By these two verses we see what God is doing today. By His speaking Himself into us, He speaks us into a total oneness with Him. By speaking He produces the bride, the church, who speaks in total oneness with the Spirit. This is the full sonship, and it is also the way to enter into glory. By this speaking we are spoken into an absolute oneness with the Spirit. By this speaking God is brought into man and man is brought into God until there is the ultimate oneness which is the universal divine-human incorporation.
It is altogether by this speaking story that God is brought into man and man is brought into God. Never despise God's speaking in our midst. It may seem small at times, but the cumulative effect of the daily speaking of God will ultimately produce a consummate union, a mingling and incorporation of God with man. Do not miss a meeting; otherwise, you will miss some speaking, some working of God into man and man into God. Do not miss God's speaking. God's work is done by His speaking to reach the ultimate goal of His being brought into man and man being brought into God.
The apostles' teaching, the unique teaching of God's New Testament economy, is God's speaking in the Son—the entire speaking of God in the New Testament concerning the full ministry of Christ in three stages (Acts 2:42; 1 Tim. 1:3-4; 6:3; Heb. 1:1-2a). God's speaking cannot deviate from this. Some powerful speakers may arise among us, but we need to discern whether their speaking has the content of the apostles1 teaching, the unique teaching of God's New Testament economy, which is concerning the full ministry of Christ in these three stages. We need to judge all speaking by this. A person may be eloquent, powerful, and able to stir up and excite his hearers. But we must discern whether such speaking is God's New Testament economy concerning the full ministry of Christ in three stages. We should not be enticed by beautiful, eloquent, or powerful speaking. We should always come back to God's New Testament economy and the full ministry of Christ in three divine and mystical stages.
The Stage of Incarnation
First, God spoke in the Son as a man in the four Gospels, revealing the stage of Christ in incarnation (John 14:10; 5:24; 16:12; 10:30): to bring the infinite God into the finite man, to unite and mingle the Triune God with the tripartite man, to express in His humanity the bountiful God in His rich attributes through His aromatic virtues, and to accomplish His all-inclusive judicial redemption. This is God's speaking in the four Gospels. This is the stage of His incarnation wherein He brought the infinite God into the finite man. He united and mingled the Triune God with the tripartite man. He expressed in His humanity the bountiful God in His rich attributes through His rich aromatic virtues. How could we know God without this stage of God's speaking? God spoke in this stage of incarnation.
John 8 speaks of a sinful woman who was caught in the very act of adultery. The Pharisees brought this woman to the Lord, wanting to stone her (w. 4-5). They wanted to stone her because they thought that was what God wanted. According to the law this one had to be stoned. The Lord Jesus was there as the very expression of God, as the living expression of God. What would God do? When we read this story, we realize it was handled in such a wonderful way. He told the woman, "Neither do I condemn you; go, and from now on sin no more"(v. 11). Had there not been this stage of incarnation in the full ministry of Christ and had God not been expressed in His humanity, we all would have thought that God wanted us to stone such a one. But by having God expressed in His humanity, we see that God does not condemn this one. Instead He empowers her to go and sin no more. This is God being expressed in humanity, in the aromatic virtues of Christ. He also died an all-inclusive death to accomplish judicial redemption. This judicial redemption consists of forgiveness of our sins, cleansing of our sins, our justification before God, our reconciliation to God, and our positional sanctification.
The Stage of Inclusion
In the second stage, God spoke in the Son as the Spirit through the apostles in Acts and the twenty-one Epistles (Romans through Jude), revealing the stage of Christ in inclusion (John 16:12-15; Matt. 28:19-20; Heb. 2:3-4; 2 Pet. 3:15-16; Col 1:25-27), The term inclusion refers back to the holy anointing oil in Exodus 30:23-25. This compound ointment included not only olive oil but also four spices, showing the inclusion of the death of Christ, the effectiveness of His death, His resurrection, and the power of His resurrection. In this stage of inclusion He was begotten as God's firstborn Son, He became the life-giving Spirit, and He regenerated the believers for His Body. To be begotten as the firstborn Son implies that there are many sons. In eternity past He was surely the Son of God, ever-existing and self-existing. In eternity past He was the only-begotten Son possessing only divinity. After passing through death and resurrection He became the firstborn Son of God, possessing both divinity and humanity, that He may reproduce Himself in us, the many sons of God, who also possess both divinity and humanity. This One is our Forerunner to bring us, the many sons, into glory (Heb. 2:10; 6:20). To say that He was begotten as the firstborn Son of God implies that He wants to beget many sons and to bring the many sons into glory.
Christ in His resurrection became the pneumatic Christ, the life- giving Spirit. John 7:39 tells us that before Christ's glorification the Spirit was "not yet." The Spirit here refers to the life-giving Spirit. The Spirit of God in Genesis 1:2 was involved with creation, the Spirit of Jehovah contacted men in the Old Testament, and the Holy Spirit was the begetting source of the incarnation (Matt. 1:18, 20). However, the Spirit was "not yet" in the sense that He was not yet the life-giving Spirit. The Spirit did not give divine life until the resurrection of Christ. The last Adam became a life-giving Spirit (1 Cor. 15:45b) so that He could give this divine life to His chosen ones. The Spirit is the pneumatic Christ. He is the life-giving Spirit to regenerate the believers for His Body.
The intrinsic meaning in the twenty-two books from Acts to Jude is found in these three points: Christ being begotten as God's firstborn Son, becoming the life-giving Spirit, and regenerating the believers for His Body. God's speaking in the Epistles is about Christ being begotten as the Firstborn that God could have many sons, that He might impart Himself as life by becoming the life-giving Spirit, and that the believers by being regenerated become the Body of Christ. The Epistles are principally about giving life, building up the Body of Christ, and conforming the many sons to the firstborn Son.
The Stage of Intensification
God then spoke in the Son as the seven Spirits through the apostle John in Revelation, revealing the stage of Christ in intensification. In this stage, Christ intensifies His organic salvation, produces the overcomers, and consummates the New Jerusalem. The entire book of Revelation is God's speaking in this stage of Christ's full ministry. In this stage He intensifies His organic salvation. God's organic salvation comprises regeneration, shepherding, sanctification, renewing, transformation, building up, conformation, and glorification. God in His speaking in this stage of intensification is intensifying these eight aspects of His organic salvation. In the stage of intensification, Christ is also producing the overcomers. The overcomers are those who live in the organic salvation of God. By these overcoming ones, God is consummating the New Jerusalem. On page 52 of How to Be a Co-worker and an Elder and How to Fulfill Their Obligations, Brother Lee says,
To be overcomers we should take the goal of God's eternal economy, the New Jerusalem, as our unique and ultimate goal. We all need to remember this goal. Our goal is not to help people to be spiritual or to be holy. Rather, we are leading people toward the New Jerusalem to consummate the New Jerusalem. How do we do this? It is by drinking the Spirit and eating Christ for us to receive His rich and fresh supply. Thus, we adorn and consummate the New Jerusalem with God the Father as its golden base, God the Son as its pearl gates, and God the Spirit for its walls of precious stones. It is not according to your will, nor by using your, way, nor with you as the element and essence. Rather, it is with God as the essence, Christ as the element, and the Spirit as the way. We need to daily drink the flowing God, the Spirit, as our river of water of life; we need to eat the overcoming Lion-Lamb as the tree of life to be our fresh and rich supply; and we need to take the Triune God as the essence, the element, and the way to build and consummate the New Jerusalem. This is the consummation of the full ministry of Christ.
The proper ministry is the ministry according to the apostles5 teaching, the teaching of God's New Testament economy, for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem. We all need to become this kind of minister speaking these things. God's speaking has been in our midst. We have faith that by His speaking and by the story of His speaking, God will be spoken into man and man will be spoken into God, until there is the final consummation of the New Jerusalem. May this kind of speaking multiply and may we have it in abundance. This is God's speaking in His Son.—G. C.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第三篇　子─祂的神性
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来一1～13。
壹　子就是神自己说话─来一1～2上，8：
一　神在子里向我们说话，这意思乃是神在祂自己里面说话。
二　神在子里说话（2上），子就是神；这指明神在祂自己里面说话。─引用经文
三　子讲说神，表明神，并解释神─约一1，14，18。
贰　子是希伯来书的中心和重点─2上，5，8，三6，四14，五5，8，六6，七3，28，十29：
一　基督是神的儿子，含示神的彰显，神的流出、或出来─约一18：
１　基督是神的具体化身和基督是神的流出，二者之间有重大的不同：
a　基督是神的具体化身，与神的存在有关；基督是完整的神，作神的具体化身─西二9。
b　基督是神的流出，与神的彰显有关；基督是神的出来、显出，作神的彰显。
c　父是源，在子这泉里显出─约四14。
２　在犹太教里神虽然是真的，却是隐藏的，但在神的救恩里，神是显出来的；这显出来的神，乃是子神。
二　论到神的儿子，有两面的讲究：神独生子的一面，和神长子的一面：
１　从已过永远到祂复活的时候，基督只在神独生子这一面是神的儿子─约一18，三16。
２　借着复活，基督在神长子这一面成为神的儿子─来一5～6，罗一3～4，徒十三33：
a　作为神的独生子，基督是神圣生命的具体化─约一4，约壹五11～12。
b　借着复活，基督成为神的长子，作分赐生命者，使那具体化在祂里面的生命得以繁殖─罗一3～4，八10，6，11，29。
３　基督在第一次来时只是独生子；祂在第二次来时，乃是长子─来一6～8。
叁　在神格里，子是神荣耀的光辉，是神本质的印像─来一3上：
一　荣耀是外在的彰显，本质是内在的素质。
