二　说到荣耀是神外在的彰显，子是神荣耀的光辉：
１　神荣耀的光辉，如同日光的照耀。
２　子是父荣耀的照耀和光辉。
三　说到本质是神内在的素质，子是神本质的印像：
１　神本质的印像，如同图章的印记。
２　子是父本质的彰显，是父所是的彰显。
四　子是神荣耀的光辉，是神本质的印像，意思是说，子是神临到我们：
１　子是神临及我们，是神来与我们成为一。
２　在神新约的经纶里，我们有一位临及我们的神，这位神来到我们这里拯救我们，并将祂自己分赐到我们里面。
肆　在神的创造里，子是创造者，维持者，承受者：
一　已过，万有是在祂里面并借着祂而有的，也是为着归于祂而有的─来一2，约一3，林前八6，西一16。
二　现今，子用祂大能的话维持万有，万有也在祂里面得以维系─来一3，西一17。
三　将来，子是万有的承受者─来一2。
伍　在救赎里，子成就了洗罪的事，如今就坐在高处至尊至大者的右边，等候祂的仇敌被征服─来一3，13：
一　子为我们的罪，将祂自己当作祭物献上，而除去了罪，并洗净了我们的罪─约一29，来十12，九26。
二　子在诸天之上坐在神的右边，表明祂的工作已经完成，现今在那里安息了─来一13，八1，十12，十二2。
三　子在等候祂的仇敌被征服，也就是说，等候神使祂的仇敌作祂的脚凳─来一13，十13。
陆　希伯来一章陈明从已过永远到将来永远，子在祂神性里奇妙的启示：
一　子是地与诸天的创造者─10节，2节。
二　子是万有的维持者─3节。
三　子是万有的承受者─2节。
四　子成为肉体，为着成功救赎而被钉十字架，成就了洗罪的事─3节。
五　子在复活里，生为神的长子─5节。
六　子在升天里，在诸天之上坐在神的右边─3节。
七　子还要以长子的身分再来─6～8节。
八　子在国度里，要在宝座上作王掌权─8～9节。
九　子在将来的永世，要存到永永远远─12节。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Three
The Son—His Divinity
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 1:1-13
I.　The Son is God Himself speaking—Heb. 1:1-2a, 8:
A.　To say that God has spoken to us in the Son means that God speaks in Himself.
B.　God has spoken in the Son (v. 2a), and the Son is God; this indicates that God speaks in Himself.
C.　The Son speaks God, declares God, and defines God—John 1:1, 14, 18.
II.　The Son is the center, the focus, of the book of Hebrews—1:2a, 5, 8; 3:6; 4:14; 5:5, 8; 6:6; 7:3, 28; 10:29:
A.　Christ's being the Son of God implies the expression of God, the issue, or the coming out, of God—John 1:18:
1.　There is an important difference between Christ as the embodiment of God and as the issue of God:
a.　Christ as the embodiment of God is related to God's existence; Christ is the complete God as His embodiment—Col. 2:9.
b.　Christ as the issue of God is related to God's expression; Christ is the coming out, the emergence, of God as His expression.
c.　The Father as the fountain emerges in the Son as the spring—John 4:14.
2.　In Judaism God is true but hidden, whereas in God's salvation God is expressed; this expressed God is God the Son.
B.　Regarding the Son of God there are two aspects: the aspect of the only begotten Son of God and the aspect of the firstborn Son of God:
1.　From eternity until the time of His resurrection, Christ was the Son of God only in the aspect of the only begotten Son of God—John 1:18; 3:16.
2.　Through resurrection Christ became the Son of God in the aspect of the firstborn Son of God—Heb. 1:5-6; Rom. 1:3-4; Acts 13:33:
a.　As the only begotten Son of God, Christ is the embodiment of the divine life—John 1:4; 1 John 5:11-12.
b.　Through resurrection Christ became the firstborn Son of God as the life-dispenser for the propagation of the life embodied in Him—Rom. 1:3-4; 8:10, 6, 11, 29.
3.　In His first coming Christ was the only begotten Son; in His second coming He will be the firstborn Son—Heb. 1:6-8.
III.　In the Godhead the Son is the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of His substance— Heb. 1:3a:
A.　The glory is the outward expression, and the substance is the inward essence.
B.　With respect to glory as the outward expression of God, the Son is the effulgence of God's glory:
1.　The effulgence of God's glory is like the shining of the light of the sun.
2.　The Son is the shining, the brightness, of the Father's glory.
C.　With respect to substance as the inward essence of God, the Son is the impress of God's substance:
1.　The impress of God's substance is like the impress of a seal.
2.　The Son is the expression of the Father's substance, the expression of what the Father is.
D.　For the Son to be the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of God's substance means that the Son is God coming to us:
1.　The Son is God reaching us, God coming to be one with us.
2.　In God's New Testament economy, we have a God who reaches us, a God who comes to us to save us and to dispense Himself into us.
IV.　In God's creation the Son is the Creator, the Upholder, and the Heir:
A.　In the past all things came into existence in Him, through Him, and unto Him—Heb. 1:2; John 1:3; 1 Cor. 8:6; Col. 1:16.
B.　In the present the Son upholds all things by the word of His power, and all things cohere in Him—Heb. 1:3; Col. 1:17.
C.　In the future the Son will be the Heir to inherit all things—Heb. 1:2.
V.　In redemption the Son has accomplished the purification of sins and is now sitting on the right hand of the Majesty on high, waiting for the subduing of His enemies—Heb. 1:3, 13:
A.　The Son has taken away sin and has made purification of sins by offering Himself as a sacrifice for our sins—John 1:29; Heb. 10:12; 9:26.
B.　The Son's sitting at the right hand of God in the heavens signifies that His work has been accomplished and that He is resting there—Heb. 1:13; 8:1; 10:12; 12:2.
C.　The Son is waiting for the subduing of His enemies, that is, waiting for God to set His enemies as a footstool for His feet—Heb. 1:13; 10:13.
VI.　Hebrews 1 presents a marvelous revelation of the Son in His divinity from eternity past to eternity future:
A.　The Son was the Creator of the earth and the heavens—vv. 10, 2.
B.　The Son is the Upholder of all things—v. 3.
C.　The Son is the Heir of all things—v. 2.
D.　The Son, having been incarnated for redemption by crucifixion, made purification of sins—v. 3.
E.　The Son was begotten in resurrection to be the firstborn Son of God—v. 5.
F.　The Son, in His ascension, sat down on the right hand of God in the heavens—v. 3.
G.　The Son will come again as the Firstborn— vv. 6-8.
H.　The Son will be the King on the throne with the scepter in the kingdom—vv. 8-9.
I.　The Son will remain forever and ever in eternity future—v. 12.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第三篇　子─祂的神性
The Son—His Divinity
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来一1～13。
壹　子就是神自己说话─来一1～2上，8：
I.　The Son is God Himself speaking—Heb. 1:1-2a, 8:
一　神在子里向我们说话，这意思乃是神在祂自己里面说话。
A.　To say that God has spoken to us in the Son means that God speaks in Himself.
二　神在子里说话（2上），子就是神；这指明神在祂自己里面说话。─引用经文
B.　God has spoken in the Son (v. 2a), and the Son is God; this indicates that God speaks in Himself.
三　子讲说神，表明神，并解释神─约一1，14，18。
C.　The Son speaks God, declares God, and defines God—John 1:1, 14, 18.
贰　子是希伯来书的中心和重点─2上，5，8，三6，四14，五5，8，六6，七3，28，十29：
II.　The Son is the center, the focus, of the book of Hebrews—1:2a, 5, 8; 3:6; 4:14; 5:5, 8; 6:6; 7:3, 28; 10:29:
一　基督是神的儿子，含示神的彰显，神的流出、或出来─约一18：
A.　Christ's being the Son of God implies the expression of God, the issue, or the coming out, of God—John 1:18:
１　基督是神的具体化身和基督是神的流出，二者之间有重大的不同：
1.　There is an important difference between Christ as the embodiment of God and as the issue of God:
a　基督是神的具体化身，与神的存在有关；基督是完整的神，作神的具体化身─西二9。
a.　Christ as the embodiment of God is related to God's existence; Christ is the complete God as His embodiment—Col. 2:9.
b　基督是神的流出，与神的彰显有关；基督是神的出来、显出，作神的彰显。
b.　Christ as the issue of God is related to God's expression; Christ is the coming out, the emergence, of God as His expression.
c　父是源，在子这泉里显出─约四14。
c.　The Father as the fountain emerges in the Son as the spring—John 4:14.
２　在犹太教里神虽然是真的，却是隐藏的，但在神的救恩里，神是显出来的；这显出来的神，乃是子神。
2.　In Judaism God is true but hidden, whereas in God's salvation God is expressed; this expressed God is God the Son.
二　论到神的儿子，有两面的讲究：神独生子的一面，和神长子的一面：
B.　Regarding the Son of God there are two aspects: the aspect of the only begotten Son of God and the aspect of the firstborn Son of God:
１　从已过永远到祂复活的时候，基督只在神独生子这一面是神的儿子─约一18，三16。
1.　From eternity until the time of His resurrection, Christ was the Son of God only in the aspect of the only begotten Son of God—John 1:18; 3:16.
２　借着复活，基督在神长子这一面成为神的儿子─来一5～6，罗一3～4，徒十三33：
2.　Through resurrection Christ became the Son of God in the aspect of the firstborn Son of God—Heb. 1:5-6; Rom. 1:3-4; Acts 13:33:
a　作为神的独生子，基督是神圣生命的具体化─约一4，约壹五11～12。
a.　As the only begotten Son of God, Christ is the embodiment of the divine life—John 1:4; 1 John 5:11-12.
b　借着复活，基督成为神的长子，作分赐生命者，使那具体化在祂里面的生命得以繁殖─罗一3～4，八10，6，11，29。
b.　Through resurrection Christ became the firstborn Son of God as the life-dispenser for the propagation of the life embodied in Him—Rom. 1:3-4; 8:10, 6, 11, 29.
３　基督在第一次来时只是独生子；祂在第二次来时，乃是长子─来一6～8。
3.　In His first coming Christ was the only begotten Son; in His second coming He will be the firstborn Son—Heb. 1:6-8.
叁　在神格里，子是神荣耀的光辉，是神本质的印像─来一3上：
III.　In the Godhead the Son is the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of His substance— Heb. 1:3a:
一　荣耀是外在的彰显，本质是内在的素质。
A.　The glory is the outward expression, and the substance is the inward essence.
二　说到荣耀是神外在的彰显，子是神荣耀的光辉：
B.　With respect to glory as the outward expression of God, the Son is the effulgence of God's glory:
１　神荣耀的光辉，如同日光的照耀。
1.　The effulgence of God's glory is like the shining of the light of the sun.
２　子是父荣耀的照耀和光辉。
2.　The Son is the shining, the brightness, of the Father's glory.
三　说到本质是神内在的素质，子是神本质的印像：
C.　With respect to substance as the inward essence of God, the Son is the impress of God's substance:
１　神本质的印像，如同图章的印记。
1.　The impress of God's substance is like the impress of a seal.
２　子是父本质的彰显，是父所是的彰显。
2.　The Son is the expression of the Father's substance, the expression of what the Father is.
四　子是神荣耀的光辉，是神本质的印像，意思是说，子是神临到我们：
D.　For the Son to be the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of God's substance means that the Son is God coming to us:
１　子是神临及我们，是神来与我们成为一。
1.　The Son is God reaching us, God coming to be one with us.
２　在神新约的经纶里，我们有一位临及我们的神，这位神来到我们这里拯救我们，并将祂自己分赐到我们里面。
2.　In God's New Testament economy, we have a God who reaches us, a God who comes to us to save us and to dispense Himself into us.
肆　在神的创造里，子是创造者，维持者，承受者：
IV.　In God's creation the Son is the Creator, the Upholder, and the Heir:
一　已过，万有是在祂里面并借着祂而有的，也是为着归于祂而有的─来一2，约一3，林前八6，西一16。
A.　In the past all things came into existence in Him, through Him, and unto Him—Heb. 1:2; John 1:3; 1 Cor. 8:6; Col. 1:16.
二　现今，子用祂大能的话维持万有，万有也在祂里面得以维系─来一3，西一17。
B.　In the present the Son upholds all things by the word of His power, and all things cohere in Him—Heb. 1:3; Col. 1:17.
三　将来，子是万有的承受者─来一2。
C.　In the future the Son will be the Heir to inherit all things—Heb. 1:2.
伍　在救赎里，子成就了洗罪的事，如今就坐在高处至尊至大者的右边，等候祂的仇敌被征服─来一3，13：
V.　In redemption the Son has accomplished the purification of sins and is now sitting on the right hand of the Majesty on high, waiting for the subduing of His enemies—Heb. 1:3, 13:
一　子为我们的罪，将祂自己当作祭物献上，而除去了罪，并洗净了我们的罪─约一29，来十12，九26。
A.　The Son has taken away sin and has made purification of sins by offering Himself as a sacrifice for our sins—John 1:29; Heb. 10:12; 9:26.
二　子在诸天之上坐在神的右边，表明祂的工作已经完成，现今在那里安息了─来一13，八1，十12，十二2。
B.　The Son's sitting at the right hand of God in the heavens signifies that His work has been accomplished and that He is resting there—Heb. 1:13; 8:1; 10:12; 12:2.
三　子在等候祂的仇敌被征服，也就是说，等候神使祂的仇敌作祂的脚凳─来一13，十13。
C.　The Son is waiting for the subduing of His enemies, that is, waiting for God to set His enemies as a footstool for His feet—Heb. 1:13; 10:13.
陆　希伯来一章陈明从已过永远到将来永远，子在祂神性里奇妙的启示：
VI.　Hebrews 1 presents a marvelous revelation of the Son in His divinity from eternity past to eternity future:
一　子是地与诸天的创造者─10节，2节。
A.　The Son was the Creator of the earth and the heavens—vv. 10, 2.
二　子是万有的维持者─3节。
B.　The Son is the Upholder of all things—v. 3.
三　子是万有的承受者─2节。
C.　The Son is the Heir of all things—v. 2.
四　子成为肉体，为着成功救赎而被钉十字架，成就了洗罪的事─3节。
D.　The Son, having been incarnated for redemption by crucifixion, made purification of sins—v. 3.
五　子在复活里，生为神的长子─5节。
E.　The Son was begotten in resurrection to be the firstborn Son of God—v. 5.
六　子在升天里，在诸天之上坐在神的右边─3节。
F.　The Son, in His ascension, sat down on the right hand of God in the heavens—v. 3.
七　子还要以长子的身分再来─6～8节。
G.　The Son will come again as the Firstborn— vv. 6-8.
八　子在国度里，要在宝座上作王掌权─8～9节。
H.　The Son will be the King on the throne with the scepter in the kingdom—vv. 8-9.
九　子在将来的永世，要存到永永远远─12节。
I.　The Son will remain forever and ever in eternity future—v. 12.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE THREE
THE SON—HIS DIVINITY
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The book of Hebrews is a book of comparisons. It unveils the superiority of Christ in God's economy and compares God's economy in Christ to Judaism and all the items of the Old Testament. The more you examine this book and the writings of Paul, the more you are led to conclude that no other New Testament writer could have written the book of Hebrews except the apostle Paul. In writing this book, Paul did not take the way of rebuking the Hebrew believers, by chastising them or scolding them. Instead, he took the higher way, the way of comparison.
PAUL, A PURE-BLOODED HEBREW, COUNTING ALL THE JEWISH THINGS TO BE LOSS ON ACCOUNT OF THE EXCELLENCY OF THE KNOWLEDGE OF CHRIST
In chapter three of Philippians Paul declared that, according to his flesh, he was circumcised on the eighth day, of the race of Israel, of the tribe of Benjamin, and a Hebrew of Hebrews (v. 5). Many of us may think that in saying this Paul meant that he was a "super-Hebrew" Actually, by this he demonstrated that he was a pure-blooded Hebrew.
He was not a mixed Jew or a half Jew; he was a one-hundred-percent, pure-blooded Jew. No one was more advanced or more accomplished in the Jewish religion than Paul. He was the a top dog." Such a reference to dogs is used by Paul in Philippians 3:2, where he said, "Beware of the dogs, beware of the evil workers, beware of the concision," "Dog" was a derogatory term used for those of the circumcision. Paul was surely such a "top dog" in Judaism. However, one day he turned to Christ. Paul, a top Judaizer, saw a contrast by the revelation he received directly from the Father who revealed. His Son in him (Gal. 1:15-16). In Philippians, he said, "What things were gains to me, these I have counted as loss on account of Christ"(3:7). The things that were gains to him included all the Jewish things, all the religious things, and all the a good things" of that religion. These all were counted as loss on account of Christ. This is the contrast. Paul considered all the best things and all the top things of that God-ordained religion as loss on account of the excellency of the knowledge of Christ Jesus the Lord (v. 8).
THE HEBREW BELIEVERS STAGGERING BETWEEN THE THINGS IN JUDAISM AND CHRIST
One day Paul's eyes were opened to see Christ, and he counted all things to be loss on account of the excellency of the knowledge of Christ. The vision and the knowledge of the excellent Christ caused him to count everything as loss. Dear saints, our inner eyes must be opened to see Christ, the Son of God. If we see Christ as Paul saw Him, everything else will drop away. We will gladly become river crossers. Why were the Hebrew believers wavering, staggering, wondering, wandering, troubled, and bothered, not knowing what to do? They did not see the clear contrast between Christ and all the things of the Jewish religion.
On the one hand, they saw something of Christ, they believed into Him, they appreciated Him, and they got saved. But on the other hand, they were looking back at the religion out of which they had come. They may have been considering, "Was that religion from God or not? It was not pagan or heathen. It was not something out of human imagination. It was established according to God's oracle. It came through the law given to Moses on Mount Sinai." It was not easy for them to give this up. While the Hebrew believers were meeting in the homes in Jerusalem, they might have lamented, saying, " Oh, the great temple that our forefathers built according to a blueprint from the vision on the mount! Oh, where is our dear Moses? Oh, I smell the incense that is drifting out of the temple! Oh, the priests with their very dignified robes and all the feasts, with the eating and drinking, the new moon, and the Sabbaths! What about these wonderful things? Yet, here we are, just meeting from house to house." For some this might not have been so easy to understand.
Today, we might feel the same way. After a while in the Lord's recovery we may feel that every day is just about the same. Every day we only receive the apostles5 teaching. We might ask, a How about some different food? Where is psychology? How about the way to have a stress-free life? How about prosperity?" In addition, we also might be suffering some persecution. Many times we are in the same situation as the Hebrew believers. In 1949 when Brother Lee went to Taiwan, a good number of saints came together claiming to be from the Lord's recovery in mainland China. In the first meeting Brother Lee said to them, a Dear saints, this place only serves "white rice? If you want fried rice, do not come here." (Fried rice has many things added into it to make it very tasty.) Many did not return for the second meeting. Only "white rice" is served in the Lord's recovery. We do not have all the wonderful things of religion, and we even have some persecution. To be in the Lord's recovery we must cross the river every day.
The vision of the Son is what can make us cross the river day by day. We must see the Son of God! We must see His supereminence and supreme preciousness! We must see His surpassing worth. When we see the Son, everything else pales. Paul said, "On account of whom [Christ] I have suffered the loss of all things and count them as refuse that I may gain Christ(Phil. 3:8). Paul saw through everything. What others considered high, dignified, glorious, and wonderful, Paul considered as refuse, dung, and trash! It all depends on what you see.
To one person something may be worth a billion dollars; to another person it is trash!
THE SON OF GOD BEING EVERYTHING IN THE UNIVERSE
What is Christ to you? What is religion to you? If you have the proper view of Christ, all religion is refuse. Christ is the only worth-while One in the universe, the only dear and valuable One in the universe. The Son of God is everything; God will not tolerate anything else being compared with Him. On the Mount of Transfiguration, when Peter said, "If You are willing, I will make three tents here, one for You and one for Moses and one for Elijah" (Matt. 17:4), a bright cloud overshadowed them. The disciples fell on their faces and a voice came, saying, "This is My Son, the Beloved, in whom I have found My delight. Hear Him!" (v. 5).
Christ is versus religion. The Son of God is versus Judaism. There is no comparison. Dear saints, we must pray, "Father, reveal Your Son in me! There is only one thing I need to see in the universe, that is, the Son of the living God." There were many things said in answer to the question, "Who do men say that the Son of Man is?" (Matt. 16:13), but Peter said, "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God." (v. 16). The Christ refers to His office and commission, and the Son refers to His person in the Godhead.
In the book of Hebrews the writer relentlessly and persistently makes the contrast between the things in Judaism and Christ in order to show us how superior the Son of God is compared to the earthly religion of Judaism. Anything that tries to compete with this Christ, God will judge. There is no divine toleration for anything that would compete with Christ. Christ must be first, Christ must be last, and Christ must be everything. Christ must be the centrality, Christ must be the universality, and Christ must be all and in all. Christ must be everything in God's economy, in the whole universe, and in all of our lives! Christ, the Son of God, is the One we need to see, the One we need to receive, the One we need to love, and the One we need to experience day by day. God has no other purpose outside of His beloved Son. Everything He wants to do and carry out is in the Son. All that He is in all His rich divine attributes is embodied in His Son. He has no work outside of the Son of God; He has no speaking outside of the Son of God. God has nothing outside of the Son of God!
All of God, all that God is, and all that God has accomplished is in the Son of God! We have no business handling, dealing with, or being occupied with anything other than the Son of God. "God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son" (John 3:16). God has nothing else to give. When He gave His Son, He gave everything. When He gave His Son, He gave Himself, because the Son of God is the very God Himself embodied in Christ and expressed in Christ.
In the Lord's recovery we have nothing but the Son of God. There is nothing else for us to pursue. He must be revealed in us; He must make His home in us; He must live in us; He must be formed in us; and He must become us. God wants us all to become His Son. This is the only thing that will satisfy Him, and this is the only thing that will fulfill His eternal desire.
The book of Hebrews contrasts the old religion of Judaism, which is earthly and temporal, with the spiritual, heavenly, and permanent Son of God. He is the very center and focus of the book of Hebrews.
The Son Being God Himself Speaking
The Son is God Himself speaking (Heb. 8). The very essence of the Epistle to the Hebrews is God's speaking in the Son. God's speaking in the Son means that the Son is God Himself speaking. In the past ages and in many ways God spoke through the prophets, through all kinds of men, both educated and uneducated, men of high status and low status. Then a day came when what was spoken before became inadequate. God determined that He must speak directly to His chosen people. He did not speak by lightning and thunder from the heavens as at Mount Sinai, nor did He speak by a still small voice in a cave. Instead, He spoke by His coming; He spoke by incarnation; He spoke in a person. God's coming to man is His speaking to man. All the previous speaking pales in the presence of the Son of God.
The Son of God is God's speaking. God not only speaks through the Son, but He speaks the Son. The Son is God's speaking. To say that God has spoken to us in the Son means that God speaks in Himself. In referring to Christ the Son, Hebrews 1:8 says, "Your throne, O God." Christ is clearly God. For God to speak in the Son means that He is speaking in Himself. God has spoken in the Son, and the Son is God; this indicates that God speaks in Himself.
The Son speaks God, declares God, and defines God. John 1:1 and 14 say, "In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God....And the Word became flesh.n The Word refers to Christ, the Son of God. The Word indicates speaking. Speaking declares something and makes something known. Speaking explains, defines, and expresses something.
If a person were to stand up in a meeting for one hour, smiling at the audience but saying nothing, the attendants might consider that meeting as a mysterious meeting. Every attendant might be puzzled. In like manner, God is a mystery, and God's economy is a mystery. Everything in the divine and mystical realm is mysterious. Yet God has spoken! What was a mystery is no longer a mystery! One day, Someone told the story of God. Someone told "His" story. History is a matter of the story of God. The story of God is Christ's story; His story is God's story. This is real history. God's Son is God's story! God's Son is God's history; God's Son is God expressed, explained, and defined. Do you want to know God? Then you have to come to the Son. When you touch the Son, you touch God; when you know the Son, you know God; when you see the Son, you see God. Today God is altogether embodied in the Son. You cannot come to God in any other way, but through the Son. This is the speaking of God in the Son.
The more we know Christ, the more we know God. Do not try to know God outside of Christ. That is Judaism. Those in Judaism may boast that their God is a moral God, a righteous God, and an ethical God; but their God is a God we cannot know. He is still a God of mystery. If we want to really know God, we have to know His Son, Christ. Then we really know Him! If we want to know God's attributes, then we have to know Christ. If we want to know His love, His light, His righteousness, His holiness, then we must know Christ. This is the reason that we must gain Christ and pursue Christ. Let us spend our whole life knowing this Christ.
Christ, the Son of God, Being the Embodiment and the Issue of God
Christs being the Son of God implies the expression of God, the issue, or the coming out, of God (John 1:18). There is an important difference between Christ as the embodiment of God and Christ as the issue of God. He is both the embodiment and the issue. Christ as the embodiment of God is related to God's existence; Christ is the complete God as His embodiment. As the embodiment of God, Christ the Son of God is related to God's existence. In His existence the Son of God is the very embodiment of God. He embodies the whole God. The Triune God is embodied in the Son of God. All the fullness of the Godhead dwells in this One (Col. 2:9).
Christ as the issue of God is related to God's expression; Christ is the coming out, the emergence, of God as His expression. The embodiment of God is related to God's existence. The Son of God as the issue of God is related to God's expression. God did not want to stay in Himself; He wanted to come out. He emerged and that emergence is the Son of God, the very expression of God. So this Son, Christ, is both the embodiment of God related to God's existence, and the issue of God related to God's expression. Both are the Son of God.
The Father as the fountain emerges in the Son as the spring. The Son is the gushing out of that fountain as the very spring, springing into eternal life. This flowing is God the Spirit; this is the New Jerusalem. We have to know the Son of God to this extent. For us to know the Son, we must know the entire Triune God. The Son can never be separated from the Father, as the tritheists today would claim. The Son is the emergence from the Father becoming the Spirit flowing into eternal life. This is the Son of God.
In Judaism God is true but hidden. In God's salvation God is expressed; this expressed God is God the Son. Today the Jews do have the true God, the real God, Jehovah, but that God, though true, is hidden. However, our God, the God of the Hebrew believers, the God of the New Testament Christians, the God of our salvation, is the expressed God, the knowable God, and the declared God. This is the Son of God. The Jews may have God but they do not have the Son, for they killed the Son. Today, we not only have God; we also have the Son of God.
Regarding the Son of God there are two aspects: the aspect of the only begotten Son of God and the aspect of the firstborn Son of God. From eternity until the time of His resurrection, Christ was the Son of God only in the aspect of the only begotten Son of God (John 1:18; 3:16). Through resurrection Christ became the Son of God in the aspect of the firstborn Son of God (Heb. 1:5-6; Rom. 1:3-4; Acts 13:33). As the only begotten Son of God, Christ is the embodiment of the divine life. He who has the Son has the life (John 1:4; 1 John 5:11-12). Through resurrection Christ became the firstborn Son of God as the life-dispenser for the propagation of the life embodied in Him (Rom. 1:3-4; 8:10, 6, 11, 29). Not only does He embody that life, but also through resurrection He became the life-dispenser. He is not only life in Himself, but now He is life dispensed, life propagated, and life flowing out. This is Christ as the firstborn Son in resurrection. In His first coming Christ was the only begotten Son; in His second coming He will be the firstborn Son (Heb. 1:6-8).
When Christ came the first time He was the only One; the second time He comes He will come with many. We are going to come with Him as sons! The first coming was the coming of the individual Son; the second coming will be the corporate Son coming back. Hallelujah!
The Son Being the Effulgence of God's Glory and the Impress of His Substance
In the Godhead the Son is the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of His substance (Heb. 1:3a). The glory is the outward expression, and the substance is the inward essence. The Son of God has the aspect of the inner essence of God and the aspect of the outward expression of God. With respect to glory as the outward expression of God, the Son is the effulgence of God's glory. The effulgence of God's glory is like the shining of the light of the sun. The Son is the shining, the brightness, of the Father's glory.
The picture here is that of the sun which in itself is full of light, full of heat, and full of glory. When the sun comes out and shines forth, it becomes the glory, the rays of the sun, and it reaches the earth. The sun reaches us through the rays of the sun. The effulgence of God's glory, on the one hand, refers to the outward expression of God, and, on the other hand, it has the thought of God reaching us, as the very rays of the sun reach us. We should open ourselves completely to the Lord and say, "O Son of God, shine on me! Shine into me, reach me, in all that You are. Be my moment-by-moment supply.Enjoy the Son in this way. This is the Son of God reaching us.
The Son is the impress of God's substance. The impress of God's substance is like the impress of a seal. The Son is the expression of the Father's substance, the expression of what the Father is. God has His outward expression, and He also has His inward essence. The Son as the impress of God's substance is related to God's inward essence. Long ago, documents were "sealed" by pressing a signature ring into soft sealing wax. The substance of the seal was impressed on the page. When Christ as the seal is put upon us, it is God coming to us and reaching us. Every day we need the sealing. The Son of God is the impress of God's substance. He is the image of the invisible God (Col. 1:15), God cannot be seen, but He has an image. Image does not refer to a physical form. Image refers to the attributes of God in His person. These attributes are Christ, the Son of God. If you do not know God, when you see Christ, you see the image of God with all that God is.
For the Son to be the effulgence of God's glory and the impress of God's substance means that the Son is God coming to us. The Son is God reaching us, and the Son is God coming to be one with us. In God's New Testament economy, we have a God who reaches us, a God who comes to us to save us and to dispense Himself into us. This is a big contrast to Judaism. Do you want to be in the old religion? Do you want to be in a religion where you just bow down to God but He never comes to you? Or would you rather be in a place where God is always coming to you? Just as you say, "O Lord," He comes. Before you can say "Jesus," He is there. Hallelujah! We have a coming God, a reaching God, a dispensing God. We have a God who wants to be one with us. We have a God who wants not just to embrace us but to become one with us and mingled with us.
The Son Being the Creator, the Upholder, and the Heir
In God's creation the Son is the Creator, the Upholder, and the Heir. In the past all things came into existence in Him, through Him, and unto Him (Heb. 1:2; John 1:3；1 Cor. 8:6; Col. 1:16). In the present the Son upholds all things by the word of His power, and all things cohere in Him (Heb. 1:3; Col. 1:17). He did not just create; Christ was the instrument, the means of the creation, and also in Christ is the power of this creation. All creation bears the characteristic of Christ's intrinsic power. He not only created all these things, but today He is holding everything together. Without Christ the whole universe would either explode or implode. However, the whole universe is not imploding or exploding. Something is holding it together. What is that something? It does not take a Nobel-Prize physicist to understand. It only takes an open heart and an honoring of the pure, divine revelation. Christ is holding the whole universe together. What is holding the recovery together? Christ. The Son of God is holding the recovery together. Do not be afraid; do not be threatened; do not have any doubts. The Son of God is holding all things together. All things cohere in Him.
In the future the Son will be the Heir to inherit all things (Heb. 1:2). He is not only upholding all things today, but also one day He is going to inherit the whole universe. All things are out of Him. This means that He created all things. Through Him means that He upholds all things and all things are unto Him. Unto Him means that one day He is going to be the Owner. It also means that we are going to be co-owners with Him of the universe. What is your graduate degree? What is your career? One day we are going to own the universe with the Son of God.
The Son Having Accomplished the Purification of Sins
In redemption the Son has accomplished the purification of sins and is now sitting on the right hand of the Majesty on high, waiting for the subduing of His enemies (Heb. 1:3,13). The Son has taken away sin and has made purification of sins by offering Himself as a sacrifice for our sins (John 1:29; Heb. 10:12; 9:26). In the Old Testament all the priests stood, day and night, making offering after offering. This was because in the Old Testament God could only cover the sins; He could not take them away. The Old Testament priests could never sit down. There were no chairs in the tabernacle. In the New Testament, there is one Priest who made one offering for sin once and for all. By this one offering, He made purification of sins forever (Heb. 10:12, 14). Today He is no longer standing. Jesus is sitting in glory! Hallelujah! He is waiting for the enemy to become His footstool. All things will be subjected under His feet.
The Son's sitting at the right hand of God in the heavens signifies that His work has been accomplished and that He is resting (Heb. 1:13; 8:1; 10:12; 12:2). Christ is resting and waiting because everything has been accomplished. The Son is waiting for the subduing of the enemies.
He is waiting for God to set His enemies as a footstool for His feet (10:13). He has the crown for His head. He has the throne for His body, and now He just needs a little footstool that He may have complete rest.
THE SON IN HIS DIVINITY FROM ETERNITY PAST TO ETERNITY FUTURE
Hebrews 1 presents a marvelous revelation of the Son in His divinity from eternity past to eternity future. The Son was the Creator of the earth and the heavens (vv. 10, 2), The Son is the Upholder of all things (v. 3). The Son is the Heir of all things (v. 2). The Son, having been incarnated for redemption by crucifixion, made purification of sins (v. 3). The Son was begotten in resurrection to be the firstborn Son of God (v. 5). The Son, in His ascension, sat down on the right hand of God in the heavens (v. 3). The Son will come again as the Firstborn (vv. 6-8), The Son will be the King on the throne with the scepter in the kingdom (vv. 8-9), The Son will remain forever and ever in eternity future (v. 12).
By seeing the Son in such a way, how can we say that we love the angels? The angels are as nothing. They are just the servants of the Son and even the ministers to us (Heb. 1:14). We need to see this comparison. We need to see the Son in His divinity. Seeing this will cause us to cross the river away from all the worthless things of the old realm and cause us to enter into the divine and mystical realm. In the realm of the Son of His love, we enjoy the expressed, declared, and defined God.—M. C.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第四篇　子─祂的人性
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：创一26～28，诗八4～8，来二5～18。
壹　希伯来二章启示，为人的耶稣这位为着完成神定旨的人而神者：
一　神在创造中命定人要照着祂的形像彰显祂，并以祂的权柄代表祂（创一26～28），但在创造里的人，在神的定旨上失败了。─引用经文
二　在神的感动下，诗人预言，人在创世记一章所得而失去的定命，要得着恢复─诗八4～8。
三　第二个人主耶稣，恢复了人失去的定命，并完成了神原初的定旨。这一位在希伯来二章向我们陈明出来：
１　在诗篇第八篇预言的应验里，为人的耶稣因着受死的苦，成为比天使微小一点的─来二9下。
２　耶稣借着受死完成了救赎之后，就在复活里得了荣耀（路二四26），并在祂的升天里，在祂的人性里得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─来二9上：
a　祂得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕，被立为主为基督─徒二36，十36。
b　祂被高举作元首，作救主─徒五31。
贰　希伯来二章启示，基督为着完成神的定旨，而在祂人性里所经过的过程：
一　在成肉体里：
１　基督成为人，为要有分于血肉之体（来二14），在性情上与我们一样：─引用经文
a　将神带进人里。
b　使神成为人，好叫人在生命、性情上成为神，但无分于神格。
c　使神与人调和，好叫神与人成为一。
d　为人完成神的救赎─罗八3，彼前一18～20，来九26，28，12，二14。
e　完成神在人身上的救恩─提前一15。
f　将神圣的生命分赐到人里面─约壹四9。
２　基督在凡事上与祂的弟兄一样，使祂成为怜悯、忠信的大祭司，能同情我们软弱的感觉─来二17，四15。
二　在钉十字架里：
１　基督为样样尝了死味─来二9，西一20，提后一10。
２　在祂的死里，基督为百姓的罪成就了平息─来二17。
３　在祂的钉十字架里，基督废除了（使之归于无有，使之失效，废掉，消除，取消，弃绝）魔鬼─来二14，创三15，罗八3。
４　基督的死也释放我们脱离因怕死而受的奴役─来二15。
三　在复活里：
１　在祂的复活里，基督产生了许多弟兄─来二11～12，彼前一3，约十二24，罗八29。
２　在祂的复活里，基督到祂的弟兄那里，向他们宣告父的名─来二12上。
３　在祂的复活里，基督在召会中歌颂父─来二12下。
四　在得荣耀里：
１　基督作开拓者率先进入荣耀，为要领神的众子进入荣耀─来二10、六20。
２　基督得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─来二9，参约十九2。
五　在被高举里：
１　基督在升天里得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕之后，神在祂的高举里赐给祂权柄管理万有─来二7，弗一22～23，结一26。
２　在祂的高举里，基督被立为万有的头─来二8，诗一一○1，林前十五27。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Four
The Son—His Humanity
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Gen. 1:26-28; Psa. 8:4-8; Heb. 2:5-18
I.　Hebrews 2 reveals the Man Jesus, the Man-God, for the fulfillment of God's purpose:
A.　God ordained the man in creation to express Him in His image and to represent Him with His dominion (Gen. 1:26-28), but the man in creation failed God in His purpose.
B.　Under the inspiration of God, the psalmist prophesied the recovery of man's lost ordination which was given in Genesis 1—Psa. 8:4-8.
C.　The second man, the Lord Jesus, who has recovered man's lost ordination and has fulfilled God's original purpose, is presented to us in Hebrews 2:
1.　The Man Jesus, in the fulfillment of the prophecy in Psalm 8, was made a little inferior to the angels because of the suffering of death—Heb. 2:9a.
2.　After He had accomplished redemption by suffering death, Jesus was glorified in His resurrection (Luke 24:26), and in His ascension, in His humanity, He was crowned with glory and honor—Heb. 2:9b:
a.　He was crowned with glory and honor to be the Lord and Christ—Acts 2:36; 10:36b.
b.　He has been exalted to be a Leader and a Savior—Acts 5:31.
II.　Hebrews 2 reveals the process through which Christ passed in His humanity to accomplish God's purpose:
A.　In incarnation:
1.　Christ became a man in order to partake of our blood and flesh (v. 14), becoming the same in nature as we are:
a.　To bring God into man
b.　To make God man that man may become God in life and nature but not in the Godhead.
c.　To mingle God with man that God and man may be one.
d.　To accomplish God's redemption for man— Rom. 8:3; 1 Pet. 1:18-20; Heb. 9:26, 28, 12; 2:14.
e.　To carry out God's salvation in man— 1 Tim. 1:15.
f.　To impart the divine life into man— 1 John 4:9.
2.　Christ was made like His brothers in all things that He might become a merciful and faithful High Priest, who can be touched with the feeling of our weaknesses—Heb. 2:17; 4:15.
B.　In crucifixion:
1.　Christ tasted death on behalf of everything—Heb. 2:9; Col. 1:20; 2 Tim. 1:10.
2.　In His death Christ made propitiation for the sins of the people—Heb. 2:17.
3.　In His crucifixion Christ destroyed (brought to nought, made of none effect, did away with, abolished, annulled, discarded) the devil—Heb. 2:14; Gen. 3:15; Rom. 8:3.
4.　Christ's death has also released us from the slavery under the fear of death—Heb. 2:15.
C.　In resurrection:
1.　In His resurrection Christ brought forth many brothers—Heb. 2:11-12; 1 Pet. 1:3; John 12:24; Rom. 8:29.
2.　In His resurrection Christ came to His brothers and declared to them the Father's name— Heb. 2:12a.
3.　In His resurrection Christ praised the Father in the church—v. 12b.
D.　In glorification:
1.　Christ took the lead to enter into glory as the Pioneer to lead God's many sons into glory— Heb. 2:10; 6:20.
2.　Christ has been crowned with glory and honor—Heb. 2:9; cf. John 19:2.
E.　In exaltation:
1.　After Christ had been crowned with glory and honor in ascension, God gave Him the dominion over all things in His exaltation—Heb. 2:7; Eph. 1:22-23; Ezek. 1:26.
2.　In His exaltation Christ was made Head over all things—Heb. 2:8; Psa. 110:1; 1 Cor. 15:27.

第四篇　子─祂的人性
The Son—His Humanity
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：创一26～28，诗八4～8，来二5～18。
壹　希伯来二章启示，为人的耶稣这位为着完成神定旨的人而神者：
I.　Hebrews 2 reveals the Man Jesus, the Man-God, for the fulfillment of God's purpose:
一　神在创造中命定人要照着祂的形像彰显祂，并以祂的权柄代表祂（创一26～28），但在创造里的人，在神的定旨上失败了。─引用经文
A.　God ordained the man in creation to express Him in His image and to represent Him with His dominion (Gen. 1:26-28), but the man in creation failed God in His purpose.
二　在神的感动下，诗人预言，人在创世记一章所得而失去的定命，要得着恢复─诗八4～8。
B.　Under the inspiration of God, the psalmist prophesied the recovery of man's lost ordination which was given in Genesis 1—Psa. 8:4-8.
三　第二个人主耶稣，恢复了人失去的定命，并完成了神原初的定旨。这一位在希伯来二章向我们陈明出来：
C.　The second man, the Lord Jesus, who has recovered man's lost ordination and has fulfilled God's original purpose, is presented to us in Hebrews 2:
１　在诗篇第八篇预言的应验里，为人的耶稣因着受死的苦，成为比天使微小一点的─来二9下。
1.　The Man Jesus, in the fulfillment of the prophecy in Psalm 8, was made a little inferior to the angels because of the suffering of death—Heb. 2:9a.
２　耶稣借着受死完成了救赎之后，就在复活里得了荣耀（路二四26），并在祂的升天里，在祂的人性里得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─来二9上：
2.　After He had accomplished redemption by suffering death, Jesus was glorified in His resurrection (Luke 24:26), and in His ascension, in His humanity, He was crowned with glory and honor—Heb. 2:9b:
a　祂得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕，被立为主为基督─徒二36，十36。
a.　He was crowned with glory and honor to be the Lord and Christ—Acts 2:36; 10:36b.
b　祂被高举作元首，作救主─徒五31。
b.　He has been exalted to be a Leader and a Savior—Acts 5:31.
贰　希伯来二章启示，基督为着完成神的定旨，而在祂人性里所经过的过程：
II.　Hebrews 2 reveals the process through which Christ passed in His humanity to accomplish God's purpose:
一　在成肉体里：
A.　In incarnation:
１　基督成为人，为要有分于血肉之体（来二14），在性情上与我们一样：─引用经文
1.　Christ became a man in order to partake of our blood and flesh (v. 14), becoming the same in nature as we are:
a　将神带进人里。
a.　To bring God into man
b　使神成为人，好叫人在生命、性情上成为神，但无分于神格。
b.　To make God man that man may become God in life and nature but not in the Godhead.
c　使神与人调和，好叫神与人成为一。
c.　To mingle God with man that God and man may be one.
d　为人完成神的救赎─罗八3，彼前一18～20，来九26，28，12，二14。
d.　To accomplish God's redemption for man— Rom. 8:3; 1 Pet. 1:18-20; Heb. 9:26, 28, 12; 2:14.
e　完成神在人身上的救恩─提前一15。
e.　To carry out God's salvation in man— 1 Tim. 1:15.
f　将神圣的生命分赐到人里面─约壹四9。
f.　To impart the divine life into man— 1 John 4:9.
２　基督在凡事上与祂的弟兄一样，使祂成为怜悯、忠信的大祭司，能同情我们软弱的感觉─来二17，四15。
2.　Christ was made like His brothers in all things that He might become a merciful and faithful High Priest, who can be touched with the feeling of our weaknesses—Heb. 2:17; 4:15.
二　在钉十字架里：
B.　In crucifixion:
１　基督为样样尝了死味─来二9，西一20，提后一10。
1.　Christ tasted death on behalf of everything—Heb. 2:9; Col. 1:20; 2 Tim. 1:10.
２　在祂的死里，基督为百姓的罪成就了平息─来二17。
2.　In His death Christ made propitiation for the sins of the people—Heb. 2:17.
３　在祂的钉十字架里，基督废除了（使之归于无有，使之失效，废掉，消除，取消，弃绝）魔鬼─来二14，创三15，罗八3。
3.　In His crucifixion Christ destroyed (brought to nought, made of none effect, did away with, abolished, annulled, discarded) the devil—Heb. 2:14; Gen. 3:15; Rom. 8:3.
４　基督的死也释放我们脱离因怕死而受的奴役─来二15。
4.　Christ's death has also released us from the slavery under the fear of death—Heb. 2:15.
三　在复活里：
C.　In resurrection:
１　在祂的复活里，基督产生了许多弟兄─来二11～12，彼前一3，约十二24，罗八29。
1.　In His resurrection Christ brought forth many brothers—Heb. 2:11-12; 1 Pet. 1:3; John 12:24; Rom. 8:29.
２　在祂的复活里，基督到祂的弟兄那里，向他们宣告父的名─来二12上。
2.　In His resurrection Christ came to His brothers and declared to them the Father's name— Heb. 2:12a.
３　在祂的复活里，基督在召会中歌颂父─来二12下。
3.　In His resurrection Christ praised the Father in the church—v. 12b.
四　在得荣耀里：
D.　In glorification:
１　基督作开拓者率先进入荣耀，为要领神的众子进入荣耀─来二10、六20。
1.　Christ took the lead to enter into glory as the Pioneer to lead God's many sons into glory— Heb. 2:10; 6:20.
２　基督得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─来二9，参约十九2。
2.　Christ has been crowned with glory and honor—Heb. 2:9; cf. John 19:2.
五　在被高举里：
E.　In exaltation:
１　基督在升天里得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕之后，神在祂的高举里赐给祂权柄管理万有─来二7，弗一22～23，结一26。
1.　After Christ had been crowned with glory and honor in ascension, God gave Him the dominion over all things in His exaltation—Heb. 2:7; Eph. 1:22-23; Ezek. 1:26.
２　在祂的高举里，基督被立为万有的头─来二8，诗一一○1，林前十五27。
2.　In His exaltation Christ was made Head over all things—Heb. 2:8; Psa. 110:1; 1 Cor. 15:27.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE FOUR
THE SON — HIS HUMANITY
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The writer's intention in the book of Hebrews is to show us that God's salvation is superior to Judaism. If we could see the situation at that time with all the Jewish Christians, we would realize how much this book needed to be written. Yet this book was written not only for them in that day but also for us today.
THE BACKGROUND OF HEBREWS-JUDAISM WITH ITS BOASTS
The Jews boasted in many things in Judaism. They boasted in God, in the angels, in Moses, in Aaron the high priest, and in the Old Testament with all of its services. On the whole earth there was nothing that could compare with Judaism. It was something established by God, and it contained so many items that came from God. These items, however, became a great distraction to God's New Testament economy.
This is the reason that Paul wrote the book of Hebrews. He wrote it in the way of presenting marvelous comparisons.
God—True but Hidden
First, he compared the God of Judaism to God in His New Testament economy. There was God in Judaism, but He was hidden; God in His New Testament economy is God expressed.
The Angels
Second, Judaism also had the angels; but Christ is superior to all the angels. The law was given through the mediation of angels, and throughout the Old Testament the angels appeared to many of God's people, including the patriarchs and prophets. The Jews boasted in all of these angels. However, Christ is far superior to all the angels. In Hebrews 1 and 2 Paul's burden is to show that Christ as the Son of God as God is superior to the angels and that Christ as the Son of Man as man is also superior to the angels. It may be difficult for us to understand how the Jews could boast in angels to the degree in which they did. But if we see what the situation in Judaism was at that time, we can realize that the angels were something great to all the Jews. Yet there is One who is greater than all the angels: Jesus the Son of God as God and Jesus the Son of； Man as man. Today there is a kind of movement spreading throughout Christianity to revere the angels, that is, to consider that the angels are something special. But Paul, at the end of chapter one in Hebrews, says of the angels "Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth for service for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?" (v. 14). We, the believers, are those who will inherit salvation, and the angels are simply our servants. We are the masters, and they are the servants. We are far superior to the angels. However, there is One among all men who is far superior to all the angels— Jesus the Son of God as God and the Son of Man as man.
A WARNING NOT TO NEGLECT SO GREAT A SALVATION
Hebrews 2:2-3 says, "For if the word spoken through angels proved firm and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompense, how shall we escape if we have neglected so great a salvation?" This salvation that we have entered into is wonderful in its person and glorious in its work. This is the great salvation into which God has brought us. If we would neglect this wonderful person and the glorious work that has been accomplished by this person in any way, there will be a just recompense. It is very serious to neglect this great salvation. To neglect this person and to neglect this work will bring a just recompense to the believers.
The Coming Inhabited Earth—the Kingdom of God
According to Hebrews 2:5, what will the recompense be? The recompense will be that we will not participate in the coming inhabited earth. This coming inhabited earth is the kingdom of Christ during the millennium. At that time the kingdom of this world will become the kingdom of Christ (Rev. 11:15), In Daniel 2:34-35, we see a stone cut out without hands coming to strike the great image. That stone which becomes a great mountain signifies the kingdom of God that spreads throughout the whole earth (see Life-study of Daniel, pp. 16-19).
Psalm 2:8 says, 'Ask of Me, / And I will give the nations as Your inheritance / And the limits of the earth as Your possession." This will transpire in the millennial kingdom. The whole world will become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Christ. Today we are in the process of being prepared to reign in that kingdom. Anyone who neglects this great salvation will miss the enjoyment of this kingdom. Hebrews 1:9 and 3:14 say that we are partners of Christ. When the time comes, all the redeemed ones, who have been brought into the kingdom of God in this age and who have been dealt with and brought on to maturity, will be with the Lord and will reign in the kingdom with Him for a thousand years (Rev, 20:4, 6). To neglect this great salvation will cause us to receive a just recompense. The just recompense is to not be the partners with Christ during the millennium. It is very serious to neglect so great a salvation. Some of the Jews who were believers had come out of Judaism but were returning to the things of Judaism. They began to neglect this so great a salvation. Today we believers may not be going back to Judaism, but it is very easy for us to fall away from this great salvation. We have such a wonderful Christ. We have such a glorious work that He has accomplished. This is a great salvation that we must enter into and enjoy so that God can carry out His wonderful work in us.
Man in Creation, Man in Prophecy, and Man in Fulfillment
Hebrews 2:5 speaks of the coming inhabited earth. There are three strong portions of the Word concerning man in God's economy and man ruling over the earth. The first is in Genesis 1 concerning creation. The second is in Psalm 8, which is a prophecy. The third is in Hebrews 2, which is the fulfillment of the, prophecy in Psalm 8. These three portions show that God has ordained man to rule over His created earth. Man's ruling over the created earth is spoken of in three different stages: the stage of creation, the stage of prophecy, and the stage of fulfillment.
THE MAN JESUS, THE MAN-GOD, FOR THE FULFILLMENT OF GOD'S PURPOSE
Hebrews 2 reveals the man Jesus, the Man-God, for the fulfillment of God's purpose. God ordained the man in creation to express Him in His image and to represent Him with His dominion (Gen. 1:26-28), but the man in creation failed God in His purpose. He lost the ordination that had been given to him by God. Under the inspiration of God, the psalmist prophesied of the recovery of mans lost ordination which was given him in Genesis 1 (Psa. 8:4-8).
Psalm 8:4-5 says, "What is man, that You remember him, / And the son of man, that You visit him? / You have made him somewhat lower than angels / And have crowned him with glory and honor." The Son of Man was made a little lower than the angels, that is, a little inferior to the angels. That man is inferior to the angels does not mean that he is inferior to the angels in position; he is only inferior to the angels physically. The man spoken of here is the Lord Jesus Christ. As this psalm says,the Son of Man has been crowned with glory and honor. The Lord Jesus is the only person in the whole universe who has been crowned with glory and honor. As the Son of Man He has been set over all the works of God (vv. 5-9; Acts 2:36).
Psalm 8 also reveals several other items concerning the fulfillment of God's purpose, including incarnation, crucifixion, resurrection, and enthronement. Christ was incarnated to accomplish redemption; He was resurrected to impart the divine life into us; and He was crowned with glory and honor (v. 5; Heb. 2:9, 14, 17). Christ resurrected and ascended to the heavens to be crowned with glory and honor, and He was enthroned to have dominion over all things with His Body. That the Body of Christ also has dominion over all things is indicated by the phrase "under his feet" in verse 6 of Psalm 8. The feet represent the Body of Christ. Christ is the Head and we are His Body. The One reigning in Psalm 8 is not just Christ; it is Christ with His Body. Ephesians 1:22-23a says "And He subjected all things under His feet and gave Him to be Head over all things to the church, which is His Body." Everything has been subjected under His feet. We reign as the partners of Christ, and we participate as His partners until everything is subdued under His feet.
The Lord Jesus, the Second Man, Having Recovered Man's Lost Ordination and Having Fulfilled God's Original Purpose
The Lord Jesus as the second man has recovered man's lost ordination and has fulfilled God's original purpose. This is the One presented in Hebrews 2. Do not think that He recovered mans lost ordination merely as God. He recovered it as a man. Today, the One who recovered this lost ordination is seated at the right hand of God as a man. The first line of Hymns, #505 says, "There's a Man in the glory." Our natural concept is that there is only the Son of God in glory. Yes, God is in glory; but the One who is there as God is a man. This is a wonderful man who has been gained by God to recover man's ordination given to him by God in the beginning.
The Man Jesus in the Fulfillment of the Prophecy in Psalm 8
The man Jesus in the fulfillment of the prophecy in Psalm 8 was made a little inferior to the angels because of the suffering of death (Heb. 2:9a). Although He was the very God of the universe, in His incarnation He was physically made inferior to the angels. This was for the suffering of death. Without Christ becoming inferior to the angels and becoming a man like you and me, partaking of flesh and blood, He could never have accomplished redemption. The greatest accomplishment is that our God became inferior to the angels for a period of time so that He could carry out the wonderful redemption to recover man back to His original ordination. Man was commissioned with such a wonderful ordination to express God and represent God with His dominion. But man fell and lost his ordination. So God was incarnated to be a man, to become inferior to the angels, that He could suffer, die, and gain us, that the original ordination might be restored.
Jesus, Having Accomplished Redemption by Suffering Death, Being Glorified in His Resurrection
After He had accomplished redemption by suffering death, Jesus was glorified in His resurrection (Luke 24:26), and in His ascension, in His humanity, He was crowned with glory and honor (Heb. 2:9b). The man Jesus was glorified in His resurrection. In ascension, in His humanity- not in His divinity- He was crowned with glory and honor. Glory is the splendor related to Jesus' person; honor is the preciousness related to Jesus' worth, value, and dignity, which is related to His position (2 Pet. 1:17; cf. 1 Pet. 2:17; Rom. 13:7). He was not only crowned with glory and honor; He also was exalted to be a Leader and a Savior. The word leader has several meanings in the Greek language: a captain, an author, an inaugurator, an originator, and a pioneer. The English language does not convey the full meaning of this word. However, Christ has been inaugurated to the highest position. He is the Originator, the Leader, leading us into glory. He is also the Forerunner, going before us, so that we might be brought into glory.
When Jesus was resurrected, according to His divine nature He was glorified. He was not only glorified in resurrection; He also entered into the realm of glory. This man recovered God's original intention. The coming inhabited earth will be subjected to a man and not to God alone. This man is greater than the angels. This man recovered God's image for His expression and the dominion that God wanted man to exercise on this earth.
In addition to this, we are partners with Christ. We have been brought into Christ the Son, and we now share in all His attainments. We are qualified to participate because we also are men. If we were not men, we would not be qualified to participate with Him. He as a man passed through all the processes and brought us to participate as a partner with Him. One day everything that God ordained for us will be fully recovered. How great our Christ is! Our speaking that Christ is superior to the angels as a man does not diminish the fact that the Son of God as God is altogether superior to the angels. Yet even as man He is superior. This wonderful man- the man spoken of in Psalm 8 and the man spoken of in Hebrews 2- is the One who has recovered the lost ordination. He passed through human living on this earth and eventually resurrected and entered into glory. Now in glory He is the Forerunner, the Leader, going before us to bring us into the realm of glory. This man Jesus was crowned with glory and honor to be the Lord and Christ (Acts 2:36; 10:36b). He has also been exalted to be a Leader and a Savior (5:31). Praise the Lord for such a Christ! I love Him. We all love Him. We love Him because of what He is in His great salvation. We love Him because of what He has accomplished. We love Him because of all the works that He has done in this great salvation.
THE ACCOMPLISHMENT OF GOD'S PURPOSE THROUGH CHRIST'S PROCESS
Hebrews 2 reveals the process through which Christ passed in His humanity to accomplish God's purpose. In this chapter, concerning the process that Christ passed through, five marvelous items are covered: His incarnation, His crucifixion, His resurrection, His glorification, and His exaltation.
Incarnation
In incarnation Christ became a man in order to partake of our blood and flesh (Heb. 2:14), becoming the same in nature as we are. There are six reasons for Christ's incarnation. The first reason for incarnation was to bring man into God. First John 4:15 says, "Whoever confesses that Jesus is the Son of God, God abides in him and he in God." Incarnation brought God into man. When He was incarnated, He entered into man. When we are regenerated, God enters into us. Actually, our regeneration is a further carrying out of incarnation. First, God was incarnated into the man Jesus. Then when we believe in Him, He is incarnated into us. This means that God comes to indwell us. Praise the Lord for His incarnation. He brought God into man.
Second, incarnation made God man that man may become God in life and nature but not in the Godhead. We are not only men; we are God-men. This wonderful Christ regenerated us so that we might become Him in life and nature. This is a wonderful, glorious matter. We will not enter into the Godhead to be worshipped. Yet we have to boldly proclaim that we have become God in life and in nature. Men bring forth the likeness of whatever they are. If I am Caucasian and my wife is Caucasian, then we will have children who are Caucasian. Man brings forth man. Have you been born of God? Then what is being brought forth? God is being brought forth, not God in the Godhead but God in life and in nature. We have to believe and even proclaim to the whole universe that we are God, not God to be worshipped but God in life and in nature. God was incarnated as a man to produce such an entity on the earth.
Third, the purpose of incarnation was to mingle God with man that God and man may be one. We like to say that we are one with God, but we also have to say that God and we have become one. We became one by the mingling of God with man. Mingling is the best word to describe the oneness that we enjoy with the Triune God. In the third century there was a heresy related to the word mingle. As a result, many are reluctant to use the word mingle. But we believe mingle is the best and most scriptural word to use in describing our relationship with God. In Leviticus 2:4 the word mingle is used to describe how the oil and the fine flour are mixed together to produce the offering. The oil represents divinity, and the fine flour, the meal, represents humanity. The offering typifies Christ. Divinity has been mingled with humanity. The very God has been mingled with us.
The word mingle in the third century was defined and used in a heretical way. The heretical teaching said that when the two natures are mingled together, they lose their distinctiveness; they lose their coexistence, and they become a third nature. Such a teaching is heretical. The mingling of God and man never produces a third nature. God was mingled with man in the Lord Jesus Christ, and God is mingled with us when we believe in Him. In Christ and in us, divinity has been mingled with humanity; but these two natures keep their own distinctiveness. They keep their distinctiveness because they are two different entities. A third nature is never produced. To say a third nature is produced is heretical.
Fourth, the purpose of incarnation was to accomplish God's redemption for man (Rom. 8:3; 1 Pet. 1:18-20; Heb, 9:26, 28, 12; 2:14). Christ died a substitutionary death on the cross. We participate in this wonderful redemption because we have been mingled with the Triune God.
Fifth, the reason for incarnation was to carry out God's salvation in man (1 Tim. 1:15). How do we participate in this salvation? After redemption, we need to participate in God's salvation. We participate in all the things that Christ has accomplished and is working out in us because we have been mingled with Him. Because we are mingled with Him, we enjoy this glorious organic salvation. Day by day we are being saved and brought on to maturity.
The sixth reason for incarnation was to impart the divine life into man. First John 4:9 says, "In this the love of God was manifested among us, that God sent His only begotten Son into the world that we might have life and live through Him." The purpose of God's incarnation was to impart the divine life into man. Today we have the divine life because Christ has been incarnated.
Hebrews 2 also reveals that through incarnation Christ was made like His brothers in all things that He might become a merciful and faithful High Priest who can be touched with the feeling of our weaknesses (v. 17; 4:15). This One is able to sympathize with us, the weak ones. He is sympathizing with us, but to sympathize with someone, you must be on the same level and have the same situation. Christ put Himself on the same level with man. He came to the same level and into the same situation. Hence, He can sympathize with us in all our weaknesses.
Crucifixion
In crucifixion Christ tasted death on behalf of everything (Heb. 2:9; 2 Tim. 1:10). This means that He tasted death on behalf of every creature. Colossians 1:20 says that He reconciled all things to Himself. How could He reconcile all things to Himself? This is only because He died on behalf of everything. Noah's ark is a very good example of this. It was not just Noah and his family who were brought through the waters, but also all the creatures in the ark were brought through and saved.
In His death Christ made propitiation for the sins of the people (Heb. 2:17). In order for God to be reconciled to us, someone had to appease our situation. Christ was the propitiatory sacrifice to appease God that we and God could become one. He propitiated for our sins that we could be reconciled to God, that God could be appeased, and that we could be joined in oneness with Him. This was accomplished through the death of Christ.
In His crucifixion Christ destroyed (brought to nought, made of none effect, did away with, abolished, annulled, discarded) the devil (Heb. 2:14; Gen, 3:15; Rom. 8:3). Hallelujah! He not only dealt with death and sin; He also dealt with Satan. Some may ask, "How can we say that Satan has been annulled or that Satan has been dealt with, when it seems that he is so prevailing on the earth?" The Bible does not say that Satan is prevailing. You are the one saying that Satan is prevailing. Satan is not prevailing; Satan has been defeated. He has even been discarded. Satan should not bother us anymore. We take our stand with Christ and His work in crucifixion and declare that the enemy has been dealt with.
Christ's death has also released us from the slavery under the fear of death (Heb. 2:15). After Brother Lee discovered that he had cancer, he met with some of us and told us that he had dealt with the Lord and that he hoped the Lord would keep him on the earth for a longer period of time. But he said that if it was the Lord's will to take him, he was ready to go. As I listened to him, I realized that here was a man who had no fear of death. The fear of death had been taken away by the cross. When I left Brother Lee's house that night, I thought to myself, 'Am I living without any fear of death?" The fear of death causes men to be great slaves; it brings people into slavery. In Russia one elderly sister testified that for her whole life she had lived on the fifth floor of her apartment building. She always walked up the stairs, even carrying her groceries, because she was afraid to ride the elevator. After she got saved, she came to a meeting and said, "Now I ride the elevator because I do not fear death any longer." The slavery, the bondage, that she was under was taken away. Do you fear death? We should no longer fear death. The slavery, the bondage, under which the enemy has placed all the unbelievers, has been taken away by the death of Christ.
Resurrection
In His resurrection Christ brought forth many brothers (Heb. 2:11-12; 1 Pet 1:3; John 12:24; Rom. 8:29). In resurrection Christ became the Firstborn among the many brothers. We all were brought forth through the resurrection of Christ. We were regenerated through this resurrection, and we became the many brothers of Christ in resurrection.
In His resurrection Christ came to His brothers and declared to them the Fathers name (Heb. 2:12a). This declaration happened in the upper room on the day of His resurrection. The Fathers name is just the Father. As the Father He is the source of our life. He is even the source of our very being. God is our Father. Our life, our spiritual being, comes from Him. Praise the Lord for our Father.
In His resurrection Christ praised the Father in the church (Heb. 2:12b). The first meeting of the church was on the night of His resurrection. Christ came and praised the Father among all the brothers. Today we also should praise the Father. As we praise, the Son joins in with our praising. In our praising He praises the Father. For eternity I believe Christ will praise the Father in the church through our praising.
Glorification
In glorification Christ took the lead to enter into glory as the Pioneer to lead God's many sons into glory (Heb. 2:10; 6:20). At His resurrection, He was glorified in His divine nature. Now as the Leader and as the Pioneer He is cutting the way into glory for all of us, and we have the full assurance that one day we will follow our Leader into glory.
Exaltation
Christ has been crowned with glory and honor (Heb. 2:9; cf. John 19:2), and after He was crowned in ascension, God gave Him the dominion over all things in His exaltation (Heb. 2:7; Eph. 1:22-23; Ezek. 1:26). This One who came as the second man to restore the ordination that was given to man is now crowned with glory and honor. He also has dominion over all things in His exaltation. As the second man He recovered the divine ordination for man. In His exaltation Christ was made Head over all things (Heb. 2:8; Psa. 110:1; 1 Cor. 15:27).
AN ADDITIONAL WORD
I would like to say something that is on my heart. Brother Lee has been gone for a little over a year. Since this year has passed, the recovery has not been scattered as some had predicted. Instead, the harmony and the oneness between the saints has been built up even more than ever before, not only in this country, but across the whole earth.
Today, the churches in North America are more related to South America than ever before, they are more related to the churches in the Far East than ever before, and they are more related to the churches in Europe than ever before. There is a proverb that says, "When the tree falls, all the monkeys are scattered/5 Brother Lee was surely the "tree," but we as all the "monkeys" have not been scattered. Instead of scattering, all of the "monkeys" have been gathered together. In this past year the Lord has come in to solidify His recovery. The history of the recovery has always been to go higher and higher. When Brother Nee went to be with the Lord, the recovery continued on, mainly through the leadership of Brother Lee, and all of us were raised up in the churches. There was a continuation. Of course, the loss of Brother Nee was certainly realized, and the loss of Brother Lee has also been realized in the same way. I wish Brother Lee were still with us today, presenting all these points. But under God's sovereignty, he is not. Does this mean that the recovery should draw back or even slow down? No, the Lord's recovery should advance more and more. Do you believe the recovery can advance more and more? Brothers and sisters, I believe it can. I have the full assurance within me that the recovery is advancing and will advance further. Twenty years from now we will look back and say, "What a wonderful thing the Lord has accomplished since Brother Lee went to be with the Lord!"
There is not another people on this earth that the Lord has time to prepare for His coming. Based upon all the prophecies and all the events, the Lord is coming soon. He must come. Where is the people that the Lord is after? Before the Lord can gain that people, He must have some who will give themselves to be the overcomers living on this earth. I believe those overcomers are mainly in the local churches. But for this to be realized we have to give ourselves unreservedly to the Lord. Learn not to have any opinions. Learn not to have any concepts. Learn to submit yourselves to one another. It is in this kind of living that the Lord will gain us for the church. Therefore, we have to live Christ. We have to become the God-men, living on this earth and being built up together in all the local churches, so that all the local churches will become one Body to express Christ.一B. P.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第五篇　救恩的承受者，神所立之承受者基督的同伙
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来一2，9，14，罗八17，来二1～4。
壹　子是神所立为承受万有者─来一2：
一　神在祂的经纶里，不仅有伟大的经营，也有无穷的丰富：
１　神已立子为经营者，为承受者。
２　神已赐给子权柄，使祂按神旨意作成一切，并有权利承受祂经营中的一切。
二　子是神的后嗣，因此父神一切的所是和所有，都归祂所有─约十六15：
１　子为父所立─来一2。
２　子被父用灵所膏─来一9。
３　子在复活里为神所生，成为神的长子─徒十三33，罗一3～4，来一5。
４　子在被高举时，被立为主为基督，管理神的经营─徒二36。
５　子被标出为合法的后嗣，要在神的经纶中承受一切─地、国度、宝座和万有─诗二8，但七13～14，路一32，太十一27。
贰　我们蒙了拯救，与子同作后嗣，同享祂的产业─来一14，罗八17，加四7，弗三6，多三7：
一　我们是神的许多儿子和基督的许多弟兄，已经蒙了拯救，与子同作后嗣─来二10，约二十17，罗八29，加四7。
二　在神的救恩里，我们不仅由神而生，成为祂的众子，也成为“神的后嗣”，“和基督同作后嗣”─罗八17：
１　我们是神的儿女，并不就是后嗣；我们作后嗣是有条件的：
a　我们生为儿女后，需要在生命里长大，成为成熟的儿子─约一12～13，罗八16，14。
b　我们也需要经过受苦，才可得荣成为法定的后嗣─罗八17。
２　我们要与子同作后嗣，就必须生为神的儿女，长大成熟，并经过法定。
三　我们和子同作后嗣，不仅承受救恩，也要与祂一同承受万有─林前三21～22。
四　我们和子同作后嗣，与祂同作宇宙的拥有者。
五　同作后嗣者，就是那被立为承受者的同伙─来一9：
１　长子既是神所立的承受者，我们这些神的众子又与祂同作后嗣，所以我们是祂的同伙。
２　在神的经纶里，子是神所立，完成神计划者；我们乃是在神圣权益上的同伙─来三15。
３　子为神所膏，我们也与祂一同分受这膏，好完成神的定旨─林后一21。
４　基督是我们的约书亚，我们是祂的迦勒，祂的同伙、伙伴、同伴─民十四30。
５　我们是基督的分享者和同伙─来一9，六4：
a　当我们享受祂，我们是祂的分享者。
b　当我们跟从祂，我们是祂的同伙。
c　作分享者的目的，是为著作同伙。
叁　希伯来二章三节所说“这么大的救恩”，能拯救我们到一个地步，使我们成为子的同伙，有分于神的设立；因此，凡祂所承受的，我们都有分：
一　救恩一点不差的就是基督自己这奇妙的人位─路二30，三6，十九9：
１　基督自己这最深奥、奇妙、无限、无量的一位，乃是我们的救恩。
２　我们承受这救恩，意思是我们承受这深奥、奇妙、无限、无量的基督。
二　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在基督的所是：
１　是神的儿子─来一2上。
２　是人的儿子─来二5～9。
３　是救恩的元帅─来二10。
４　是大祭司─来二17，三1，四14，五10，六20。
三　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在基督所已经作的、正在作的、并将要作的：
１　祂成就了洗罪的事─来一3，七27。
２　祂为我们尝了死味─来二9。
３　祂废除了魔鬼，释放我们脱离奴役─来二14～15。
４　祂正在圣别我们─来二11。
５　祂能帮助我们─来二18。
四　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在救恩的广大范围：
１　使我们成为神所立承受者的同伙─来一2，9。
２　带我们进入荣耀─来二10。
３　使我们和长子一同拥有要来的居人之地─来二5。
４　拯救我们到底─来七25。
五　我们对“这么大的救恩”应当注重，而不随流漂去，也不忽略这救恩─来二1～4。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Five
Heirs of Salvation, Partners of Christ as God's Appointed Heir
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 1:2, 9, 14; Rom. 8:17; Heb. 2:1-4
I.　The Son is God's appointed Heir of all things— Heb. 1:2:
A.　In His economy God has not only a great operation but also tremendous riches:
1.　God has appointed the Son to be both the Operator and the Heir.
2.　God has given the Son the authority to do everything in His purpose and the right to inherit all things in His operation.
B.　Since the Son is the Heir of God, all that God the Father is and has is His possession—John 16:15:
1.　The Son was appointed by the Father—Heb. 1:2.
2.　The Son was anointed by the Father with the Spirit—Heb. 1:9.
3.　The Son was begotten of God in resurrection to be the Firstborn—Acts 13:33; Rom. 1:3-4; Heb. 1:5.
4.　The Son, in His exaltation, was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation—Acts 2:36.
5.　The Son was designated the legal Heir to inherit everything in God's economy—the earth, the kingdom, the throne, and all things—Psa. 2:8; Dan. 7:13-14; Luke 1:32; Matt. 11:27.
II.　We are saved to be the Son's joint heirs, sharing in His inheritance—Heb. 1:14; Rom. 8:17; Gal. 4:7; Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7:
A.　As the many sons of God and the many brothers of Christ, we have been saved to be the Son's joint heirs—Heb. 2:10; John 20:17; Rom. 8:29; Gal. 4:7.
B.　In God's salvation we are not only born of God to be His sons but also made "heirs of God" and "joint heirs with Christ"—Rom. 8:17:
1.　We are not heirs simply because we are children of God; rather, there is a condition for us to be heirs:
a.　After being born as children, we need to grow in life to become mature sons—John 1:12-13; Rom. 8:16, 14.
b.　We also need to pass through suffering that we may be glorified to become legal heirs—Rom. 8:17.
2.　In order to become the Son's joint heirs, we need to be born as children of God, grow into maturity, and be legalized.
C.　As the Son's joint heirs, we not only inherit salvation but also inherit all things with Him— 1 Cor. 3:21-22.
D.　As the Son's joint heirs, we are with Him joint owners of the universe.
E.　The joint heirs are the partners of the appointed Heir—Heb. 1:9:
1.　Since the firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint heirs, we are His partners.
2.　In God's economy the Son is the One appointed by God to accomplish God's plan, and we are the Son's partners in the divine interest—Heb. 3:14.
3.　The Son was anointed by God, and we share with Him this anointing for the fulfillment of God's purpose—2 Cor. 1:21.
4.　Christ is our Joshua, and we are His Calebs, His partners, comrades, and companions— Num. 14:30.
5.　We are Christ's partakers and partners— Heb. 1:9; 6:4:
a.　When we enjoy Him, we are His partakers.
b.　When we follow Him, we are His partners.
c.　Being a partaker is for the purpose of being a partner.
III.　The "so great a salvation" mentioned in Hebrews 2:3 is able to save us to such an extent that it brings us into partnership with the Son in His appointment; thus, we share in whatever He inherits:
A.　Salvation is nothing less than the wonderful person of Christ Himself—Luke 2:30; 3:6; 19:9:
1.　Christ Himself, as the most profound, marvelous, unlimited, immeasurable One, is our salvation.
2.　For us to be heirs of salvation means that we inherit this profound, marvelous, unlimited, and immeasurable Christ.
B.　"So great a salvation" is great in what Christ is:
1.　As the Son of God—Heb. 1:2a.
2.　As the Son of Man—Heb. 2:5-9.
3.　As the Captain of salvation—Heb. 2:10.
4.　As the High Priest—Heb. 2:17; 3:1; 4:14; 5:10; 6:20.
C.　"So great a salvation" is great in what Christ has done, is doing, and will do:
1.　He made purification of sins—Heb. 1:3; 7:27.
2.　He tasted death for us—Heb. 2:9.
3.　He destroyed the devil and released us from slavery—Heb. 2:14-15.
4.　He is sanctifying us—Heb. 2:11.
5.　He is able to help us—Heb. 2:18.
D.　"So great a salvation" is great in its extent:
1.　It makes us partners of God's appointed Heir—Heb. 1:2, 9.
2.　It brings us into glory—Heb. 2:10.
3.　It will make us to possess the coming inhabited earth with the firstborn Son—Heb. 2:5.
4.　It will save us to the uttermost—Heb. 7:25.
E.　Concerning "so great a salvation" we should give heed to it, not drift away from it, and not neglect it—Heb. 2:1-4.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第五篇　救恩的承受者，神所立之承受者基督的同伙
Heirs of Salvation, Partners of Christ as God's Appointed Heir
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来一2，9，14，罗八17，来二1～4。
壹　子是神所立为承受万有者─来一2：
I.　The Son is God's appointed Heir of all things— Heb. 1:2:
一　神在祂的经纶里，不仅有伟大的经营，也有无穷的丰富：
A.　In His economy God has not only a great operation but also tremendous riches:
１　神已立子为经营者，为承受者。
1.　God has appointed the Son to be both the Operator and the Heir.
２　神已赐给子权柄，使祂按神旨意作成一切，并有权利承受祂经营中的一切。
2.　God has given the Son the authority to do everything in His purpose and the right to inherit all things in His operation.
二　子是神的后嗣，因此父神一切的所是和所有，都归祂所有─约十六15：
B.　Since the Son is the Heir of God, all that God the Father is and has is His possession—John 16:15:
１　子为父所立─来一2。
1.　The Son was appointed by the Father—Heb. 1:2.
２　子被父用灵所膏─来一9。
2.　The Son was anointed by the Father with the Spirit—Heb. 1:9.
３　子在复活里为神所生，成为神的长子─徒十三33，罗一3～4，来一5。
3.　The Son was begotten of God in resurrection to be the Firstborn—Acts 13:33; Rom. 1:3-4; Heb. 1:5.
４　子在被高举时，被立为主为基督，管理神的经营─徒二36。
4.　The Son, in His exaltation, was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation—Acts 2:36.
５　子被标出为合法的后嗣，要在神的经纶中承受一切─地、国度、宝座和万有─诗二8，但七13～14，路一32，太十一27。
5.　The Son was designated the legal Heir to inherit everything in God's economy—the earth, the kingdom, the throne, and all things—Psa. 2:8; Dan. 7:13-14; Luke 1:32; Matt. 11:27.
贰　我们蒙了拯救，与子同作后嗣，同享祂的产业─来一14，罗八17，加四7，弗三6，多三7：
II.　We are saved to be the Son's joint heirs, sharing in His inheritance—Heb. 1:14; Rom. 8:17; Gal. 4:7; Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7:
一　我们是神的许多儿子和基督的许多弟兄，已经蒙了拯救，与子同作后嗣─来二10，约二十17，罗八29，加四7。
A.　As the many sons of God and the many brothers of Christ, we have been saved to be the Son's joint heirs—Heb. 2:10; John 20:17; Rom. 8:29; Gal. 4:7.
二　在神的救恩里，我们不仅由神而生，成为祂的众子，也成为“神的后嗣”，“和基督同作后嗣”─罗八17：
B.　In God's salvation we are not only born of God to be His sons but also made "heirs of God" and "joint heirs with Christ"—Rom. 8:17:
１　我们是神的儿女，并不就是后嗣；我们作后嗣是有条件的：
1.　We are not heirs simply because we are children of God; rather, there is a condition for us to be heirs:
a　我们生为儿女后，需要在生命里长大，成为成熟的儿子─约一12～13，罗八16，14。
a.　After being born as children, we need to grow in life to become mature sons—John 1:12-13; Rom. 8:16, 14.
b　我们也需要经过受苦，才可得荣成为法定的后嗣─罗八17。
b.　We also need to pass through suffering that we may be glorified to become legal heirs—Rom. 8:17.
２　我们要与子同作后嗣，就必须生为神的儿女，长大成熟，并经过法定。
2.　In order to become the Son's joint heirs, we need to be born as children of God, grow into maturity, and be legalized.
三　我们和子同作后嗣，不仅承受救恩，也要与祂一同承受万有─林前三21～22。
C.　As the Son's joint heirs, we not only inherit salvation but also inherit all things with Him— 1 Cor. 3:21-22.
四　我们和子同作后嗣，与祂同作宇宙的拥有者。
D.　As the Son's joint heirs, we are with Him joint owners of the universe.
五　同作后嗣者，就是那被立为承受者的同伙─来一9：
E.　The joint heirs are the partners of the appointed Heir—Heb. 1:9:
１　长子既是神所立的承受者，我们这些神的众子又与祂同作后嗣，所以我们是祂的同伙。
1.　Since the firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint heirs, we are His partners.
２　在神的经纶里，子是神所立，完成神计划者；我们乃是在神圣权益上的同伙─来三15。
2.　In God's economy the Son is the One appointed by God to accomplish God's plan, and we are the Son's partners in the divine interest—Heb. 3:14.
３　子为神所膏，我们也与祂一同分受这膏，好完成神的定旨─林后一21。
3.　The Son was anointed by God, and we share with Him this anointing for the fulfillment of God's purpose—2 Cor. 1:21.
４　基督是我们的约书亚，我们是祂的迦勒，祂的同伙、伙伴、同伴─民十四30。
