4.　Christ is our Joshua, and we are His Calebs, His partners, comrades, and companions— Num. 14:30.
５　我们是基督的分享者和同伙─来一9，六4：
5.　We are Christ's partakers and partners— Heb. 1:9; 6:4:
a　当我们享受祂，我们是祂的分享者。
a.　When we enjoy Him, we are His partakers.
b　当我们跟从祂，我们是祂的同伙。
b.　When we follow Him, we are His partners.
c　作分享者的目的，是为著作同伙。
c.　Being a partaker is for the purpose of being a partner.
叁　希伯来二章三节所说“这么大的救恩”，能拯救我们到一个地步，使我们成为子的同伙，有分于神的设立；因此，凡祂所承受的，我们都有分：
III.　The "so great a salvation" mentioned in Hebrews 2:3 is able to save us to such an extent that it brings us into partnership with the Son in His appointment; thus, we share in whatever He inherits:
一　救恩一点不差的就是基督自己这奇妙的人位─路二30，三6，十九9：
A.　Salvation is nothing less than the wonderful person of Christ Himself—Luke 2:30; 3:6; 19:9:
１　基督自己这最深奥、奇妙、无限、无量的一位，乃是我们的救恩。
1.　Christ Himself, as the most profound, marvelous, unlimited, immeasurable One, is our salvation.
２　我们承受这救恩，意思是我们承受这深奥、奇妙、无限、无量的基督。
2.　For us to be heirs of salvation means that we inherit this profound, marvelous, unlimited, and immeasurable Christ.
二　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在基督的所是：
B.　"So great a salvation" is great in what Christ is:
１　是神的儿子─来一2上。
1.　As the Son of God—Heb. 1:2a.
２　是人的儿子─来二5～9。
2.　As the Son of Man—Heb. 2:5-9.
３　是救恩的元帅─来二10。
3.　As the Captain of salvation—Heb. 2:10.
４　是大祭司─来二17，三1，四14，五10，六20。
4.　As the High Priest—Heb. 2:17; 3:1; 4:14; 5:10; 6:20.
三　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在基督所已经作的、正在作的、并将要作的：
C.　"So great a salvation" is great in what Christ has done, is doing, and will do:
１　祂成就了洗罪的事─来一3，七27。
1.　He made purification of sins—Heb. 1:3; 7:27.
２　祂为我们尝了死味─来二9。
2.　He tasted death for us—Heb. 2:9.
３　祂废除了魔鬼，释放我们脱离奴役─来二14～15。
3.　He destroyed the devil and released us from slavery—Heb. 2:14-15.
４　祂正在圣别我们─来二11。
4.　He is sanctifying us—Heb. 2:11.
５　祂能帮助我们─来二18。
5.　He is able to help us—Heb. 2:18.
四　“这么大的救恩”，乃是大在救恩的广大范围：
D.　"So great a salvation" is great in its extent:
１　使我们成为神所立承受者的同伙─来一2，9。
1.　It makes us partners of God's appointed Heir—Heb. 1:2, 9.
２　带我们进入荣耀─来二10。
2.　It brings us into glory—Heb. 2:10.
３　使我们和长子一同拥有要来的居人之地─来二5。
3.　It will make us to possess the coming inhabited earth with the firstborn Son—Heb. 2:5.
４　拯救我们到底─来七25。
4.　It will save us to the uttermost—Heb. 7:25.
五　我们对“这么大的救恩”应当注重，而不随流漂去，也不忽略这救恩─来二1～4。
E.　Concerning "so great a salvation" we should give heed to it, not drift away from it, and not neglect it—Heb. 2:1-4.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE FIVE
HEIRS OF SALVATION, PARTNERS OF CHRIST AS GOD'S APPOINTED HEIR
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The focus of the book of Hebrews is the Son. God is jealous over us, desiring us to care for, focus on, and center on the Son. God has at the last of these days spoken to us in the Son. God's speaking centers on the Son. On the Mount of Transfiguration God said, "This is My Son, the Beloved, in whom I have found My delight. Hear Him!" (Matt. 17:5). In the messages in this series we are being "sonized"； every message brings us to the Son. Message One brings us to the Son as the unique Hebrew who is now in us as the pneumatic Christ to lead us across every river and bring us with Him into the glorious expression of the Divine Being. In Message Two we see the Son as God's speaking in abundance. All the speaking among us should be in the Son. In Message Three we see the Son of God as God in His divinity, and in Message Four we see the Son of Man as man in His humanity. As God and man He accomplishes everything for God's eternal economy. God's only "language" is the Son, If we speak the Son, we have the same focus and center that God has. It pleases God that the Son would be everything to us. Footnote 4 of Hebrews 1:3 in the Recovery Version, as a marvelous, tasty condensation of the New Testament, brings us to Christ in every book. It tells us that the Christ in the book of Hebrews is "the present Christ, who is now in the heavens as our Minister (8:2) and our High Priest (4:14-15; 7:26), ministering to us the heavenly life, grace, authority, and power and sustaining us to live a heavenly life on earth. He is the Christ now, the Christ today, and the Christ on the throne in the heavens, who is our daily salvation and moment- by-moment supply/5 We appreciate the ministry that brings us such a footnote. We can never exhaust this speaking, and we will still enjoy it in eternity.
Christ is sustaining His recovery to live a life in the Holy of Holies, in the spirit, in the being of God Himself. Moreover, Christ is our salvation, not merely our long-term salvation but our daily salvation. We need to be daily saved from the flesh, self-consciousness, sin, discouragement, individualism, independence, the world, and self-likeness. He is the Christ now, the Christ today, and our daily salvation. A joyful Christian is a Christian who is daily saved. When we are saved, we are full of joy, but if we do not have much salvation, we do not have much joy. We are enjoying the Christ whom we have received under the ministry in the Lord's recovery, the Christ in the book of Hebrews, who is our daily salvation and our moment-by-moment supply. Supply implies joy. If we do not eat for several days, we will become miserable, but as soon as we are supplied, we are joyful. We love Christ as our moment-by-moment supply. What a Christ we have!
Religion is the worship of God without the enjoyment of Christ. There is no comparison between man's religion and God's New Testament economy in which Christ is everything. We are so glad to be in the Lord's recovery, because here we cross the river out of religion and into our spirit, into the Holy of Holies, to enjoy Christ as our everything. When we came into the Lord's recovery, we were brought back to our inheritance. Our inheritance is God, the all-inclusive Christ, and all things. We should never be miserable people. We should be the happy heirs of such a treasure. We are not here wondering and wandering in our soul; we are crossing the river meeting by meeting, day by day, moment by moment, to enjoy the Christ who is now, who is today, and who is everything to us.
The Life-study of Hebrews on page 52, says,
It may seem that this message on the heirs of salvation has nothing to do with Christ. Believe me, if you are going to know Christ, you must know this message, for it contains a particular aspect concerning Christ. Christ is wonderful, profound, unlimited, rich, and marvelous. As such a One, He certainly needs all of us to be heirs of salvation. Salvation is nothing less than the wonderful person of Christ Himself. Christ Himself as the most profound, marvelous, unlimited, and immeasurable One, is our salvation. To say that we are heirs of salvation means that we inherit this profound, marvelous, immeasurable, and unlimited Christ. Christ's profoundness requires our partnership. Our partnership with Christ unveils the profoundness of Christ.
The above passage twice speaks of Christ as being wonderful. In the life-studies and footnotes of the Recovery Version, Brother Lee uses the word wonderful many times in relation to Christ. Christ is a wonderful person. His name is Wonderful. Because it is hard to describe this person, no word is more fitting than wonderful. We are heirs of salvation, inheriting Christ as our salvation. We may think of salvation as an item or an action that God takes. Salvation, however, is higher than this. It is nothing less than the wonderful person of Christ Himself.
This is encouraging, exciting, and inciting. No one who inherits riches is miserable; every heir is joyful Our inheritance is so great because our inheritance is the unlimited Son.
The title of this message is "Heirs of Salvation, Partners of Christ as God's Appointed Heir." In this title there are two marvelous items of Christ. As we have seen, salvation, which is "so great a salvation"(Heb. 2:3), is the inexhaustible, precious, and unlimited Christ. The second item of Christ here is that He is God's appointed Heir. Through His salvation. He is making us not only children and sons but also joint heirs with Himself as the appointed Heir. We are not beggars in religion; we are heirs of salvation and partners of Christ. This is a very joyful and precious matter. The chorus of Hymns, #507 says, a Christ liveth in me, / Christ liveth in me; / Oh! what a salvation this, / That Christ liveth in me." It is a salvation to have Christ living in us. Even at this moment we are being saved from a host of things to become Him in a corporate way for His glorious expression.
Footnote 1 of Hebrews 1:14 in the Recovery Version says, "The Son is the appointed Heir of all things (v. 2). We, His believers, are His partners (v. 9). Thus, we are His joint heirs (Rom. 8:17), not only inheriting salvation but also inheriting all things with Him (1 Cor. 3:21-22). Hence, we with Him are joint owners of the universe."The earth is over twenty-four thousand miles in circumference, the sun is over a million times the size of the earth, and other stars are millions of times the size of the sun. Moreover, the known universe contains an immense number of stars. Nevertheless, we are joint owners of the universe because we are linked to Christ, the God-appointed Heir. The Son was appointed to be the Heir, and we are saved to be His joint heirs. The "so great a salvation," which is Christ Himself with all that He is and all that He has done, is doing, and will do, is able to save us to such an extent that it brings us into partnership with God's appointed Heir. Therefore, we share in whatever He inherits. We can have the foretaste of this even now by enjoying the Son. Every time we exercise our spirit to look away to Jesus and enjoy Him, love Him, and tell Him that He is our first love, we enjoy a foretaste of the full taste of owning the entire universe. Even if we are poor materially, the Lord is rich to all who call on Him. When we enjoy Him as the rich One, even if outwardly we have nothing, inwardly we own the universe. We have Christ as the Heir, and we inherit God's salvation.
Those who take the lead in the Lord's recovery should never try to possess or own others. Only a foolish person tries to own, possess, or control others, and eventually, he will lose everything. In every turmoil in the churches there have been brothers trying to possess others. This is a shame. Today the oneness in the Lord's recovery is greater than at any other time because there are no brothers grasping for possession of others. The Lord tells us to drop this seeking. We should be those who enjoy the wonderful Christ as our great salvation, who brings us into partnership with Himself to share and enjoy the entire universe. In this age we should not seek to own anything else. We should simply possess, gain, run after, pursue, love, and enjoy Christ as the unique Heir. We are the joyful heirs, inheriting all that God is and the entire universe. We are joint owners of the universe with Christ our Partner. Why should we foolishly try to control, possess, or own others? We simply enjoy, love, feed on, and gain this marvelous Christ. Brother Lee was faithful to focus us on Christ and feed us nothing but Christ. In The Ten Great Critical "Ones" for the Building Up of the Body of Christ, he tells us that the Lord's recovery is just the recovery of Christ (p. 33). We are the heirs, desiring nothing but to inherit Christ and enjoy Him as our daily salvation and moment-by-moment supply.
THE SON BEING GOD'S APPOINTED HEIR OF ALL THINGS
In His Economy God Having Not Only a Great Operation but Also Tremendous Riches Hebrews contains at least thirty definite items of the riches of Christ. First He is the Son, and next He is God's appointed Heir of all things (1:2). In His economy God has not only a great operation but also tremendous riches. His operation includes everything from creation to the New Jerusalem. In the previous messages we have seen the operation of Christ as the Son of God in His divinity and as the Son of Man in His humanity. This is a fine, delicate, precious operation. His riches are the riches of God Himself, which are the riches of the whole universe.
God has appointed the Son to be both the Operator and the Heir.
God has given the Son the authority to do everything in His purpose and the right to inherit all things in His operation. Every day we need to enjoy Him as the Operator and the Heir. Colossians 1:16 illustrates how Christ is the Operator and the Heir. It says that all creation was created in Him, through Him, and unto Him. In Him means that He was the intrinsic power of creation, through Him means that He was the active instrument through which the creation was accomplished in sequence, and unto Him means that He is the ultimate recipient, the entitled Heir of the universe. Verse 20 goes on to say that He reconciled all things to Himself through His death. All creation was lost and damaged through the rebellion of Satan, but He reconciled all things to Himself. Therefore, He is the Operator and the Heir.
Since the Son Is the Heir of God, All That God the Father Is and Has Being His Possession Since the Son is the Heir of God, all that God the Father is and has is His possession. John 16:15 says, "All that the Father has is Mine; for this reason I have said that He receives of Mine and will declare it to you."
Now He as the Spirit within us is disclosing and declaring all these
riches to us. The Son was appointed by the Father in eternity past to be the unique Heir (Heb. 1:2). Then in time, the Son was anointed by the Father with the Spirit (v. 9); He was anointed with the oil of exultant joy above His partners. As such, He is joy unspeakable and full of glory.
Following this, the Son was begotten of God in resurrection to be the Firstborn (Rom. 1:3-4; Heb. 1:5). Acts 13:33 quotes the second Psalm, saying, "You are My Son; this day have I begotten You." When Christ was raised from the dead, the Spirit of His divinity enlivened His humanity and uplifted His humanity into the sonship of the only begotten Son. At that point, He became the firstborn Son of God by being designated the Son of God in His humanity, and His being begotten in His humanity brought man into God. The term firstborn is precious because it indicates that many more are to follow. He is the Firstborn, and we are the many born. In His resurrection we were regenerated with Him.
The Son, in His exaltation, was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation (Acts 2:36). There is a man in the glory and in the Godhead who is the Lord of all, the Possessor of all, inheriting all things. Even as a man, He is the Administrator of God's operation. Christ is the commissioned, anointed, and appointed One to administrate God's operation.
The Son was designated the legal Heir to inherit everything in God's economy--the earth, the kingdom, the throne, and all things. Psalm 2:8 tells us that He inherits the earth; Daniel 7:1344 says that He inherits the kingdom; Luke 1:32 says that He inherits the throne; and Matthew 11:27 says that He inherits all things. This is the inheritance of Christ as the legal Heir. Matthew 1:1 speaks of "the generation of Jesus Christ, the son of David, the son of Abraham." The first mention of the Lord in the New Testament is as the son of David to be the King and the son of Abraham to be the legal Heir. As the son of Abraham, Isaac was simply a joyful heir, inheriting lands, wealth, flocks, and everything from Abraham. Now we also are the joyful ones who are fellow heirs with Christ, the legal Heir.
BEING SAVED TO BE THE SON'S JOINT HEIRS, SHARING IN HIS INHERITANCE
We are saved to be the Son's joint heirs, sharing in His inheritance (Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7). Hebrews 1:14 says that we are those who are to inherit salvation. As such, we are far superior to the angels, who are ministering servants, inferior both to Christ and to us. Romans 8:17 says that we are heirs of God and joint heirs with Christ. Galatians 4:7 says, "So then you are no longer a slave but a son; and if a son, an heir also through God." We are not here as slaves but as sons and heirs through God. A New Testament believer is no longer a slave to works under the law. Rather, he is a son in life under the abundance of grace that he has received. This grace is God as our enjoyment, supply, inheritance, and salvation.
As the Many Sons of God and the Many Brothers of Christ, Being Saved to Be the Son's Joint Heirs
As the many sons of God and the many brothers of Christ, we have been saved to be the Sons joint heirs (John 20:17; Rom, 8:29; Gal. 4:7). Before resurrection God had only one Son, but after the resurrection Christ became the Firstborn, and we became the many-born, the many sons, the many brothers of Christ. Hebrews 2:10 says that Christ is the Captain of salvation, leading many sons into glory, into the region of glory, which is the innermost being of God Himself. In John 17:24 the Lord prayed, "Father, concerning that which You have given Me, I desire that they also may be with Me where I am, that they may behold My glory, which You have given Me" Apparently, we are on the earth; actually, we are where Christ is, in the innermost being of God. He is the Captain leading us into glory. Therefore, He no longer calls us merely friends; because of the divine life He calls us His brothers.
In God's Salvation Not Only Born of God to Be His Sons but Also Made "Heirs of God" and "Joint Heirs with Christ"
In God's salvation we are not only born of God to be His sons but also made "heirs of God" and "joint heirs with Christ" (Rom. 8:17), inheriting all that God is and the entire universe. However, we are not heirs simply because we are children of God. Rather, there is a condition for us to be heirs. After being born as children, we need to grow in life to become mature sons (John 1:12-13; Rom. 8:16, 14), and we also need to pass through suffering that we may be glorified to become legal heirs (v. 17). In order to become the Sons joint heirs, we need to be born as children of God, grow into maturity, and be legalized. We are being perfected, matured, and brought on through sufferings from being children to being sons and legal heirs, joint heirs, and partners of Christ. However, we do not seek suffering; we seek Christ, God's economy, and the fulfillment of His economy in the building of His Body, consummating in the New Jerusalem. In John 21 the Lord told Peter to feed His lambs, shepherd His sheep, and feed His sheep. Then He spoke a word to Peter signifying by what kind of death he would glorify God (w. 15-19). We simply take care of this dear One and shepherd His sheep. If we take care of Christ and His hearts desire, suffering will come. After this, Peter asked the Lord concerning John, and the Lord replied, "What is that to you? You follow Me"(v. 22). We should not worry about suffering; we must simply follow the Lord. Then when we care for others, there will be suffering. It is a suffering to preach the gospel and shepherd, but we rejoice in these sufferings, and we fill up what is lacking of the afflictions of Christ in our flesh for His Body, which is the church. We should never pick up a verse like Romans 8:17 and focus on suffering. Rather, we need to enjoy Christ, and sufferings will take care of themselves. Our goal is not sufferings; our goal is Christ. However, we do desire that He would perfect us to be His genuine, legal joint heirs.
As the Son's Joint Heirs, Not Only Inheriting Salvation but Also Inheriting All Things with Him and Being Joint Owners of the Universe
As the Son's joint heirs, we not only inherit salvation but also inherit all things with Him (1 Cor. 3:21-22). As the Son's joint heirs, we also are with Him joint owners of the universe. In this age, many brothers and sisters do not even own their house, but we will be joint owners of the universe. We will possess and enjoy all things, but most of all, we will enjoy Christ.
The Joint Heirs Being the Partners of the Appointed Heir
The joint heirs are the partners of the appointed Heir (Heb. 1:9). Since the firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint heirs, we are His partners. In God's economy the Son is the One appointed by God to accomplish God's plan, and we are the Son's partners in the divine interest (3:14). Our portion in this age is not to possess or control others; it is to enjoy the senior Partner, the legal Heir, who is our salvation. We enjoy Him, and eventually we inherit the entire universe.
The Son was anointed by God, and we share with Him this anointing for the fulfillment of God's purpose. Second Corinthians 1:21 says, "But the One who firmly attaches us with you unto Christ and has anointed us is God." In His recovery and under this ministry we are enjoying this Christ as our anointing. He is the dear One who is moving and working Himself into our being to make us Him and to build us together as one for His Body and His bride.
Christ is our Joshua, and we are His Calebs, His partners, comrades, and companions (Num. 14:30). We are Christ's partakers and partners (Heb. 6:4; 1:9). When we enjoy Him, we are His partakers, and when we follow Him, we are His partners. Being a partaker is for the purpose of being a partner. Daily and moment by moment we practice to enjoy Him, and as we enjoy Him, He becomes the One living within us, whom we follow organically. After coming into the Lord's recovery, many try to follow the Lord, but they often fail.
However, we should not merely try to follow Him outwardly. Rather, we need to inwardly enjoy Him as our Leader. The more we enjoy Him, the more we become one with Him to follow Him. We become those who follow the Lamb wherever He goes. However, if we do not enjoy Him, we will not be able to follow Him.
THE "SO GREAT A SALVATION" BEING ABLE TO SAVE US TO SUCH AN EXTENT THAT IT BRINGS US INTO PARTNERSHIP WITH THE SON IN HIS APPOINTMENT
The "so great a salvation" mentioned in Hebrews 2:3 is able to save us to such an extent that it brings us into partnership with the Son in His appointment; thus, we share in whatever He inherits. Salvation is nothing less than the wonderful person of Christ Himself (Luke 2:30; 3:6; 19:9). Christ Himself, as the most profound, marvelous, unlimited, immeasurable One, is our salvation. For us to be heirs of salvation means that we inherit this profound, marvelous, unlimited, and immeasurable Christ. The entire New Testament is a story of salvation. The first mention of the name Jesus is in Matthew 1:21, which says, "And she will bear a son, and you shall call His name Jesus, for it is He who will save His people from their sins," The name Jesus means Jehovah the Savior, or the salvation of Jehovah. Our whole experience of Christ is our experience of Him as our Savior and our salvation. To experience Christ is to be saved by Christ because He is our salvation. In Luke 2:30, Simeon said, "For my eyes have seen Your salvation" and 3:6 says, "All flesh shall see the salvation of God." When Jesus came to the house of Zaccheus, He said, "Today salvation has come to this house" (19:9). When Jesus came, salvation came. This indicates that salvation is a person.
"So great a salvation" is great in what Christ is as the Son of God (Heb. 1:2a), the Son of Man (2:5-9), the Captain of salvation (v. 10), and the High Priest (v. 17; 3:1; 4:14; 5:10; 6:20). "So great a salvation" is also great in what Christ has done, is doing, and will do. He made purification of sins (1:3; 7:27), He tasted death for us (2:9), He destroyed the devil and released us from slavery (w. 14-15), He is sanctifying us (v. 11), and He is able to help us (v. 18). We should not be afraid of death because Christ tasted death on behalf of everything. Therefore, death is a lie. Christ destroyed, discarded, and annulled the devil, and He released us. For all the foregoing items we should offer up a sacrifice of praise continually to God (13:15).
"So great a salvation" is great in its extent. It makes us partners of God's appointed Heir (1:2, 9); it brings us into glory (2:10); it will make us to possess the coming inhabited earth with the firstborn Son (v. 5); and it will save us to the uttermost (7:25). Concerning "so great a salvation" we should give heed to it, not drift away from it, and not neglect it (2:1-4). We must be river crossers and not drifters. We need to enjoy Christ as the unique Hebrew, as our salvation, as the unique Heir, and as the One who is able to save us to such an extent that He brings us into partnership with Him to be joint owners of the whole universe. An earthly occupation cannot earn us very much, but if we enjoy Christ, we will inherit the whole universe. We should not be concerned with possessing other things; we should come and possess Christ, enjoy Christ, and become a partner of Christ. Eventually, the whole universe will be ours.—D. T.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第六篇　基督是救恩的元帅，要领神许多的儿子进荣耀里去
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来二10～12，16～18，弗一4～5。
壹　神永远的目标，是要领祂许多的儿子进荣耀里去：
一　荣耀是神的彰显，也就是彰显出来的神─出四十34。
二　神永远的定旨，是要借着祂所救赎的人，团体的彰显祂自己─创一26，弗三21。
三　有神荣耀的新耶路撒冷，是神团体的彰显：
１　神作为光，在作为灯的羔羊里面并借着羔羊照耀出来，至终透过整座城照耀出来，使城显出神自己的样子─启二一10～11，18，23，四3。
２　我们看见了新耶路撒冷，就看见神的彰显─在灯里的光透过碧玉照耀出来。
贰　基督是我们进入荣耀之完满救恩的元帅（创始者，起始者，开创者，首领，开拓者）─来二3，10：
一　耶稣作救恩的元帅，借着苦难得了成全─来二10：
１　成全耶稣，乃指在资格上成全祂。
２　这并不含示祂在美德或属性上，有什么不完全，只指使祂完成人生苦难的经历，能适合作我们救恩的元帅。
３　耶稣是神圣荣耀的种子，落到地里死了，并且长大，而在复活荣耀里开花─约十二23～24，路二四26，林前十五36，43上：
a　祂借着长大，祂的全人，包括祂的人性，祂属人的性情，都被带进神荣耀的彰显里。
b　祂过了死河，就是最终的河，进入了荣耀，就是神的神圣所是之彰显的实际。
c　祂是在荣耀里的人，就是在神彰显里的人，这人甚至就是神的彰显、神的荣耀。
d　身为元帅、开拓者、先锋（来六20），祂已领先进入荣耀；现今我们这些跟从祂的人，正在同一条路上，也要被带进神所命定给我们同样的荣耀里─林前二7，帖前二12：
⑴　走在我们前面，领我们经过苦难之水，进入荣耀的境地，就是神自己彰显出来。
⑵　这是过河的的真实目标，是作希伯来人真实的目标─王上八10～11。
二　救恩的元帅　，就是在荣耀里的那人，这人就是神的荣耀，乃是在我们里面荣耀的种子─西一27，约壹三9：
１　我们的救主领先争战进入了荣耀；祂的一生乃是为着荣耀争战的过程─路十二49～50。
２　在我们里面荣耀种子的长大，乃是争战的过程。
３　荣耀乃是神圣的元素从我们里面开花。
４　我们所要进入的荣耀，乃是那已经撒在我们里面作为种子之神圣元素的荣耀─帖后一9。
三　我们经过苦难的过程，得拯救进入荣耀，就是进入神的彰显─来十32～35，林后四16～18：
１　我们过了所有的河，就被带进荣耀里，而这荣耀就是神自己彰显出来。
２　一切苦难都帮助我们走在往锡安的大道上，把我们变化，从荣耀到荣耀，使我们成为祂荣耀的新妇─诗八四5～7，林后三18，罗八17～18，21，弗五27。
四　我们救恩的元帅基督，尽祂的职责，借著作我们的大祭司，将神供应到我们里面，而领我们进入荣耀─来二16～18：
１　这种供应乃是一种搭救，一种帮助的援救，在这援救中，基督进到我们里面，把我们穿在祂身上，背负我们，并带着我们─赛四十31。
２　当我们享受这搭救，我们就有分于神的元素，并被带进荣耀之神的彰显里。
叁　基督作救恩的元帅，借着圣别，生机的拯救神许多的儿子，而领他们进入荣耀，就是神团体的彰显里─来二10～11，弗一4～5，帖前五23，罗五10：
一　为着神圣儿子名分之神圣的圣别，乃是神圣经纶的中心，也是新约里启示的中心思想。
二　神圣的圣别，在完成神圣的经纶上，是主持的线，为要神圣的使我们子化，使我们成为神的儿子，在生命和性情上与神一样，但无分于祂的神格，而使我们作神的彰显：
１　寻找的圣别，也就是初步的圣别，是为着叫我们悔改，把我们带回归神─彼前一2，路十五8～10，17～21。
２　救赎的圣别，也就是地位上的圣别，是借着基督的血，把我们从亚当迁到基督里─来十三12。
３　重生的圣别，也就是在性情上圣别的开始，从我们的灵更新我们，使我们这些罪人成为神的儿子─林后五17，约一12～13。
４　更新的圣别，也就是性情上圣别的继续，从我们的心思到我们魂的各部分，更新我们的魂，使我们的魂成为神新造的一部分─罗十二2，弗四23。
５　变化的圣别，也就是日常的圣别，新陈代谢的用基督的元素把我们重新构成，使我们成为新的构成，作基督生机身体的一部分─林后三18，林前三12。
６　模成的圣别，也就是成形的圣别，将我们形成荣耀之基督的形像，使我们成为基督的彰显─罗八29。
７　得荣的圣别，也就是终极完成的圣别，借着把我们的身体改变形状，而救赎我们的的身体，使我们在荣耀里完满的成为基督的彰显─腓三21，罗八23。
肆　神圣的圣别是由圣别的那灵在我们灵里实施的─罗十五16，八4，弗五26。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Six
Christ as the Captain of Salvation Leading God's Many Sons into Glory
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 2:10-11, 16-18; Eph. 1:4-5
I.　God's eternal goal is to bring His many sons into glory:
A.　Glory is the expression of God, God expressed— Exo. 40:34.
B.　God's eternal purpose is to express Himself in a corporate way through His redeemed people— Gen. 1:26; Eph. 3:21.
C.　The New Jerusalem, having the glory of God, is the corporate expression of God:
1.　God as the light shines in and through the Lamb as the lamp, eventually shining through the entire city, causing the city to bear the appearance of God Himself—Rev. 21:10-11, 18, 23; 4:3.
2.　When we look at the New Jerusalem, we see the expression of God—the light in the lamp shining through the jasper.
II.　Christ is the Captain (Author, Originator, Inaugurator, Leader, Pioneer) of our full salvation into glory—Heb. 2:3, 10:
A.　As the Captain of salvation, Jesus was perfected through sufferings—Heb. 2:10:
1.　To make Jesus perfect was to make Him perfect in terms of qualification.
2.　It does not imply that there was any imperfection of virtue or attribute in Jesus, but only that the completing of His experience of human sufferings was needed to make Him fit to become the Captain of our salvation.
3.　Jesus was the seed of the divine glory, falling into the earth to die and growing up to blossom in glory in resurrection—John 12:23-24; Luke 24:26; 1 Cor. 15:36, 43a:
a.　By His growing up, His whole being, including His humanity and His human nature, was brought into the glorious expression of God.
b.　He crossed the death river, the ultimate river, and entered into glory, the reality of the expression of God's divine being.
c.　He is the man in the glory, the man in God's expression, even the man who is God's expression, God's glory.
d.　As the Captain, the Pioneer, the Forerunner (Heb. 6:20), He took the lead to enter into glory, and we, His followers are taking the same way to be brought into the same glory, which was ordained by God for us—1 Cor. 2:7; 1 Thes. 2:12:
1)　 He has gone ahead of us to lead us to cross through the waters of suffering and enter into the region of glory, God Himself expressed.
2)　 This is the real goal of crossing the river, the real goal of being a Hebrew— 1 Kings 8:10-11.
B.　The Captain of salvation, the man in the glory, the man who is God's glory, is the seed of glory within us—Col. 1:27; 1 John 3:9:
1.　Our Savior took the lead to fight through into glory; His whole life was a fighting process for glory—Luke 12:49-50.
2.　The growing of the seed of glory within us is a fighting process.
3.　Glory is the blossoming of the divine element from within us.
4.　The glory into which we shall enter is the glory of the divine element that has been sown into us as a seed—2 Thes. 1:10.
C.　We are saved into glory, the expression of God, through the process of suffering—Heb. 10:32-35; 2 Cor. 4:16-18:
1.　The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, the glory that is God Himself expressed.
2.　All of our sufferings help us along the highways to Zion to transform us from glory to glory so that we can become His glorious bride—Psa. 84:5-7; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rom. 8:17-18, 21; Eph. 5:27.
D.　Christ as the Captain of our salvation fulfills His duty to lead us into glory by being our High Priest to minister God into us—Heb. 2:16-18:
1.　This kind of ministry is a succor, a helpful rescue, in which Christ gets into us, puts us upon Him, bears us, and carries us—Isa. 40:31.
2.　When we enjoy this succor, we participate in God's element and are brought into the expression of the glorious God.
III.　Christ as the Captain of salvation leads God's many sons into glory, the corporate expression of God, by saving them organically through sanctification—Heb. 2:10-11; Eph. 1:4-5; 1 Thes. 5:23; Rom. 5:10:
A.　The divine sanctification for the divine sonship is the center of the divine economy and the central thought of the revelation in the New Testament.
B.　The divine sanctification is the holding line in the carrying out of the divine economy to sonize us divinely, making us sons of God that we may become the same as God in His life and in His nature (but not in His Godhead), so that we may be God's expression:
1.　The seeking sanctification, the initial sanctification, is unto repentance to bring us back to God—1 Pet. 1:2; Luke 15:8-10, 17-21.
2.　The redeeming sanctification, the positional sanctification, is by the blood of Christ, to transfer us from Adam to Christ—Heb. 13:12.
3.　The regenerating sanctification, the beginning of dispositional sanctification, renews us from our spirit to make us, the sinners, sons of God—2 Cor. 5:17; John 1:12-13.
4.　The renewing sanctification, the continuation of dispositional sanctification, renews our soul from our mind through all the parts of our soul to make our soul a part of God's new creation—Rom. 12:2b; Eph. 4:23.
5.　The transforming sanctification, the daily sanctification, reconstitutes us with the element of Christ metabolically to make us a new constitution as a part of the organic Body of Christ—2 Cor. 3:18; 1 Cor. 3:12.
6.　The conforming sanctification, the shaping sanctification, shapes us in the image of the glorious Christ to make us the expression of Christ—Rom. 8:29.
7.　The glorifying sanctification, the consummating sanctification, redeems our body by transfiguring it to make us Christ's expression in full in glory—Phil. 3:21; Rom. 8:23.
IV.　The divine sanctification is carried out by the sanctifying Spirit in our spirit—Rom. 15:16; 8:4; Eph. 5:26.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第六篇　基督是救恩的元帅，要领神许多的儿子进荣耀里去
Christ as the Captain of Salvation Leading God's Many Sons into Glory
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来二10～12，16～18，弗一4～5。
壹　神永远的目标，是要领祂许多的儿子进荣耀里去：
I.　God's eternal goal is to bring His many sons into glory:
一　荣耀是神的彰显，也就是彰显出来的神─出四十34。
A.　Glory is the expression of God, God expressed— Exo. 40:34.
二　神永远的定旨，是要借着祂所救赎的人，团体的彰显祂自己─创一26，弗三21。
B.　God's eternal purpose is to express Himself in a corporate way through His redeemed people— Gen. 1:26; Eph. 3:21.
三　有神荣耀的新耶路撒冷，是神团体的彰显：
C.　The New Jerusalem, having the glory of God, is the corporate expression of God:
１　神作为光，在作为灯的羔羊里面并借着羔羊照耀出来，至终透过整座城照耀出来，使城显出神自己的样子─启二一10～11，18，23，四3。
1.　God as the light shines in and through the Lamb as the lamp, eventually shining through the entire city, causing the city to bear the appearance of God Himself—Rev. 21:10-11, 18, 23; 4:3.
２　我们看见了新耶路撒冷，就看见神的彰显─在灯里的光透过碧玉照耀出来。
2.　When we look at the New Jerusalem, we see the expression of God—the light in the lamp shining through the jasper.
贰　基督是我们进入荣耀之完满救恩的元帅（创始者，起始者，开创者，首领，开拓者）─来二3，10：
II.　Christ is the Captain (Author, Originator, Inaugurator, Leader, Pioneer) of our full salvation into glory—Heb. 2:3, 10:
一　耶稣作救恩的元帅，借着苦难得了成全─来二10：
A.　As the Captain of salvation, Jesus was perfected through sufferings—Heb. 2:10:
１　成全耶稣，乃指在资格上成全祂。
1.　To make Jesus perfect was to make Him perfect in terms of qualification.
２　这并不含示祂在美德或属性上，有什么不完全，只指使祂完成人生苦难的经历，能适合作我们救恩的元帅。
2.　It does not imply that there was any imperfection of virtue or attribute in Jesus, but only that the completing of His experience of human sufferings was needed to make Him fit to become the Captain of our salvation.
３　耶稣是神圣荣耀的种子，落到地里死了，并且长大，而在复活荣耀里开花─约十二23～24，路二四26，林前十五36，43上：
3.　Jesus was the seed of the divine glory, falling into the earth to die and growing up to blossom in glory in resurrection—John 12:23-24; Luke 24:26; 1 Cor. 15:36, 43a:
a　祂借着长大，祂的全人，包括祂的人性，祂属人的性情，都被带进神荣耀的彰显里。
a.　By His growing up, His whole being, including His humanity and His human nature, was brought into the glorious expression of God.
b　祂过了死河，就是最终的河，进入了荣耀，就是神的神圣所是之彰显的实际。
b.　He crossed the death river, the ultimate river, and entered into glory, the reality of the expression of God's divine being.
c　祂是在荣耀里的人，就是在神彰显里的人，这人甚至就是神的彰显、神的荣耀。
c.　He is the man in the glory, the man in God's expression, even the man who is God's expression, God's glory.
d　身为元帅、开拓者、先锋（来六20），祂已领先进入荣耀；现今我们这些跟从祂的人，正在同一条路上，也要被带进神所命定给我们同样的荣耀里─林前二7，帖前二12：
d.　As the Captain, the Pioneer, the Forerunner (Heb. 6:20), He took the lead to enter into glory, and we, His followers are taking the same way to be brought into the same glory, which was ordained by God for us—1 Cor. 2:7; 1 Thes. 2:12:
⑴　走在我们前面，领我们经过苦难之水，进入荣耀的境地，就是神自己彰显出来。
1)　 He has gone ahead of us to lead us to cross through the waters of suffering and enter into the region of glory, God Himself expressed.
⑵　这是过河的的真实目标，是作希伯来人真实的目标─王上八10～11。
2)　 This is the real goal of crossing the river, the real goal of being a Hebrew— 1 Kings 8:10-11.
二　救恩的元帅　，就是在荣耀里的那人，这人就是神的荣耀，乃是在我们里面荣耀的种子─西一27，约壹三9：
B.　The Captain of salvation, the man in the glory, the man who is God's glory, is the seed of glory within us—Col. 1:27; 1 John 3:9:
１　我们的救主领先争战进入了荣耀；祂的一生乃是为着荣耀争战的过程─路十二49～50。
1.　Our Savior took the lead to fight through into glory; His whole life was a fighting process for glory—Luke 12:49-50.
２　在我们里面荣耀种子的长大，乃是争战的过程。
2.　The growing of the seed of glory within us is a fighting process.
３　荣耀乃是神圣的元素从我们里面开花。
3.　Glory is the blossoming of the divine element from within us.
４　我们所要进入的荣耀，乃是那已经撒在我们里面作为种子之神圣元素的荣耀─帖后一9。
4.　The glory into which we shall enter is the glory of the divine element that has been sown into us as a seed—2 Thes. 1:10.
三　我们经过苦难的过程，得拯救进入荣耀，就是进入神的彰显─来十32～35，林后四16～18：
C.　We are saved into glory, the expression of God, through the process of suffering—Heb. 10:32-35; 2 Cor. 4:16-18:
１　我们过了所有的河，就被带进荣耀里，而这荣耀就是神自己彰显出来。
1.　The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, the glory that is God Himself expressed.
２　一切苦难都帮助我们走在往锡安的大道上，把我们变化，从荣耀到荣耀，使我们成为祂荣耀的新妇─诗八四5～7，林后三18，罗八17～18，21，弗五27。
2.　All of our sufferings help us along the highways to Zion to transform us from glory to glory so that we can become His glorious bride—Psa. 84:5-7; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rom. 8:17-18, 21; Eph. 5:27.
四　我们救恩的元帅基督，尽祂的职责，借著作我们的大祭司，将神供应到我们里面，而领我们进入荣耀─来二16～18：
D.　Christ as the Captain of our salvation fulfills His duty to lead us into glory by being our High Priest to minister God into us—Heb. 2:16-18:
１　这种供应乃是一种搭救，一种帮助的援救，在这援救中，基督进到我们里面，把我们穿在祂身上，背负我们，并带着我们─赛四十31。
1.　This kind of ministry is a succor, a helpful rescue, in which Christ gets into us, puts us upon Him, bears us, and carries us—Isa. 40:31.
２　当我们享受这搭救，我们就有分于神的元素，并被带进荣耀之神的彰显里。
2.　When we enjoy this succor, we participate in God's element and are brought into the expression of the glorious God.
叁　基督作救恩的元帅，借着圣别，生机的拯救神许多的儿子，而领他们进入荣耀，就是神团体的彰显里─来二10～11，弗一4～5，帖前五23，罗五10：
III.　Christ as the Captain of salvation leads God's many sons into glory, the corporate expression of God, by saving them organically through sanctification—Heb. 2:10-11; Eph. 1:4-5; 1 Thes. 5:23; Rom. 5:10:
一　为着神圣儿子名分之神圣的圣别，乃是神圣经纶的中心，也是新约里启示的中心思想。
A.　The divine sanctification for the divine sonship is the center of the divine economy and the central thought of the revelation in the New Testament.
二　神圣的圣别，在完成神圣的经纶上，是主持的线，为要神圣的使我们子化，使我们成为神的儿子，在生命和性情上与神一样，但无分于祂的神格，而使我们作神的彰显：
B.　The divine sanctification is the holding line in the carrying out of the divine economy to sonize us divinely, making us sons of God that we may become the same as God in His life and in His nature (but not in His Godhead), so that we may be God's expression:
１　寻找的圣别，也就是初步的圣别，是为着叫我们悔改，把我们带回归神─彼前一2，路十五8～10，17～21。
1.　The seeking sanctification, the initial sanctification, is unto repentance to bring us back to God—1 Pet. 1:2; Luke 15:8-10, 17-21.
２　救赎的圣别，也就是地位上的圣别，是借着基督的血，把我们从亚当迁到基督里─来十三12。
2.　The redeeming sanctification, the positional sanctification, is by the blood of Christ, to transfer us from Adam to Christ—Heb. 13:12.
３　重生的圣别，也就是在性情上圣别的开始，从我们的灵更新我们，使我们这些罪人成为神的儿子─林后五17，约一12～13。
3.　The regenerating sanctification, the beginning of dispositional sanctification, renews us from our spirit to make us, the sinners, sons of God—2 Cor. 5:17; John 1:12-13.
４　更新的圣别，也就是性情上圣别的继续，从我们的心思到我们魂的各部分，更新我们的魂，使我们的魂成为神新造的一部分─罗十二2，弗四23。
4.　The renewing sanctification, the continuation of dispositional sanctification, renews our soul from our mind through all the parts of our soul to make our soul a part of God's new creation—Rom. 12:2b; Eph. 4:23.
５　变化的圣别，也就是日常的圣别，新陈代谢的用基督的元素把我们重新构成，使我们成为新的构成，作基督生机身体的一部分─林后三18，林前三12。
5.　The transforming sanctification, the daily sanctification, reconstitutes us with the element of Christ metabolically to make us a new constitution as a part of the organic Body of Christ—2 Cor. 3:18; 1 Cor. 3:12.
６　模成的圣别，也就是成形的圣别，将我们形成荣耀之基督的形像，使我们成为基督的彰显─罗八29。
6.　The conforming sanctification, the shaping sanctification, shapes us in the image of the glorious Christ to make us the expression of Christ—Rom. 8:29.
７　得荣的圣别，也就是终极完成的圣别，借着把我们的身体改变形状，而救赎我们的的身体，使我们在荣耀里完满的成为基督的彰显─腓三21，罗八23。
7.　The glorifying sanctification, the consummating sanctification, redeems our body by transfiguring it to make us Christ's expression in full in glory—Phil. 3:21; Rom. 8:23.
肆　神圣的圣别是由圣别的那灵在我们灵里实施的─罗十五16，八4，弗五26。
IV.　The divine sanctification is carried out by the sanctifying Spirit in our spirit—Rom. 15:16; 8:4; Eph. 5:26.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE SIX
CHRIST AS THE CAPTAIN OF SALVATION LEADING GOD飞 MANY SONS INTO GLORY
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The critical points of the first six messages in this series can be summarized by the following two statements: 1) The believers in Christ should be river crossers following the Lord Jesus, the Pioneer and Forerunner, into the glorious expression of the Divine Being, and 2) Christ as the Captain of salvation is leading God's many sons into glory, the corporate expression of God, by saving them organically through sanctification. This message and its outline are very critical. As we read them, we must see a wonderful revelation. The first thing we must see in a new, fresh, and living way is God's hearts desire. The desire of God's heart is the center of the divine economy and the central revelation of the New Testament. Second, we must see how God carries out His heart's desire, what is His divine enterprise to carry out His desire, and what are the contents of His economy to carry out His desire. Third, we must see how we can cooperate with Him to carry out His heart's desire.
We have seen that the center and focus of the book of Hebrews is a wonderful person, the Son. God's heart's desire is to have a corporate son. He does not want only one son; He wants many sons. He wants a firstborn Son and many sons who are His many brothers. All these many brothers become a part of the Son to be a corporate son. To carry out His desire, He comes into us as the Son to "sonize" our entire being. This is what He is doing even now as we open to Him., He is in our spirit as the Son, and He is spreading out from our being until our whole being—spirit, soul, and body—is sonized. When our entire being is sonized, we will be the corporate expression of God, which means we will be God's very glory.
As we have seen, God is the God of the Hebrews, the God of the river crossers. In order to carry out and gain His heart's desire, the God of the Hebrews became a Hebrew. Hebrews 1:8-9 says, "But of the Son, 'Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, and the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of Your kingdom . You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; therefore God, Your God, has anointed You with the oil of exultant joy above Your partners.'" The phrase Your throne, O God in verse 8 shows that the Son of God is the very God, while the phrase Your God in verse 9 indicates that the Son is a man. On the one hand, He is the God of the Hebrews; on the other hand, He became a Hebrew. As a Hebrew, He crossed all the rivers, and He entered into the region of glory, the region of the expression of God's glorious being. This was a wonderful, awesome, and tremendous achievement, but He is not happy only with this much. This is why He prayed, "Father, concerning that which You have given Me, I desire that they also may be with Me where I am, that they may behold My glory, which You have given Me" (John 17:24), Christ entered into the region of glory, into the glorious being of God, but He desires that the disciples would be with Him where He is. Therefore, this God of the Hebrews who became a Hebrew was processed and consummated to become the "pneumatic Hebrew," the life-giving Spirit who indwells us. As such, He is the indwelling, pneumatic Captain of our salvation. He is leading us to cross river after river into the glorious being of God, until we become in full the glorious expression of the Triune God. To cross the river is to wash ourselves from anything old and ruined, from anything that does not match God's glory. He leads us to cross river after river until we become His glorious expression. There is a man in the glory, who is God's glory, but God is not satisfied only with one man in the glory or with one man who is God's glory. He wants a corporate man in the glory, a corporate man who is God's glory. The New Jerusalem is the great God-man, the man in the glory. This is His divine enterprise.
GOD'S ETERNAL GOAL BEING TO BRING HIS MANY SONS INTO GLORY
Glory Being the Expression of God, God Expressed
God's eternal goal is to bring His many sons into glory. God's eternal goal is glory, and our destiny is glory. God's eternal goal is to bring all of us into His glorious being so that we become His glorious expression. Many people sing of glory, but they do not know what glory is. Glory is not a radiant shining in the air or a luxurious state into which we will enter. Glory is the expression of God, God expressed. Exodus 40:34 tells us that the glory of God filled the tabernacle. God was expressed in the tabernacle. When we speak of glory, we are in the core of God's heart. This is His eternal goal, this is where He is leading us year after year, and this is the meaning of His recovery. The Lord's recovery is to recover a people whom He can saturate, dispense into, and work Himself into until they become His full expression.
God's Eternal Purpose Being to Express Himself in a Corporate Way through His Redeemed People
God's eternal purpose is to express Himself in a corporate way- through His redeemed people. In Genesis 1:26 we see that God made man in His image and after His likeness. This shows us that from the very beginning, from God's creation of man, God wanted man to corporately express Him in His image and bear His likeness. Ephesians 3:21 says, "To Him be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus unto all the generations forever and ever." This reveals God's purpose. The church being God's glory is typified by Isaac and Rebekah. Isaac's wealth was first given to Rebekah for her beautification; then all the wealth came back to Isaac, with Rebekah, for his glorification (Gen. 24:47, 53, 61-67). This is what is happening to us. The Lord came into us with all His unsearchable riches, all His wealth, and He is dispensing this stored wealth in His being into our tripartite being until we are saturated with Him for our beautification; then we will return to Him for His glorification. This is His purpose.
The New Jerusalem, Having the Glory of God, Being the Corporate Expression of God
When we come to the end of the Bible, we see the New Jerusalem. The New Jerusalem, having the glory of God, is the corporate expression of God. God as the light shines in and through the Lamb as the lamp, eventually shining through the entire city, causing the city to bear the appearance of God Himself (Rev. 21:1041, 18, 23; 4:3). When we look at the New Jerusalem, we see the expression of God—the light in the lamp shining through the jasper. How wonderful it is that we are becoming that city! The Lamb is the lamp of that city, God is the light within the Lamb, and God in the Lamb shines through the jasper wall. Jasper is dark green in color, signifying God's communicable glory in His rich life. Revelation 4:3 tells us that the One sitting on the throne has the appearance of jasper, and 21:11 says that the light of the city has the light of a jasper stone. Therefore, the whole city bears the appearance of God, the Man-God, the God-man, on the throne. God is making us jasper so that eventually all of His redeemed, corporately, in their entire tripartite being, will shine the Triune God to the whole universe. This is God's heart's desire. To accomplish His heart's desire, God first passed through a wonderful process to become consum­mated. Then, He came into us to take us through a wonderful process.
Even now, we are in a wonderful process, and eventually, we will be consummated to be the bride of Christ as His corporate expression.
CHRIST BEING THE CAPTAIN (AUTHOR, ORIGINATOR, INAUGURATOR, LEADER, PIONEER) OF OUR FULL SALVATION INTO GLORY
As the Captain of Salvation, Jesus Being Perfected through Sufferings
Christ is the Captain (Author, Originator, Inaugurator, Leader, Pioneer) of our full salvation into glory. As the Captain of salvation, Jesus was perfected through sufferings (Heb. 2:3, 10). That Jesus was perfected means that He was completed, consummated. To make Jesus perfect was to make Him perfect in terms of qualification. It does not imply that there was any imperfection of virtue or attribute in Jesus, but only that the completing of His experience of human sufferings was needed to make Him fit to become the Captain of our salvation.
Jesus was the seed of the divine glory, falling into the earth to die and growing up to blossom in glory in resurrection (John 12:23-24; Luke 24:26; 1 Cor. 15:36, 43a). When Christ became a man, He encapsulated the divine glory as the seed of the divine glory, and as the seed He fell into the earth to die. A grain is a marvelous proof of resurrection. Brother Lee once spoke with a student in Peking who did not believe in the resurrection. Brother Lee pointed to a field of grain outside the window, telling him how a grain of wheat falls into the ground, dies, and resurrects. Moreover, when the grain resurrects, its blossom is its glory. This student was saved, and eventually he became a co-worker. Paul calls one who does not believe in resurrection a "foolish man"(1 Cor. 15:36). We should learn to preach the gospel like this.
By Christ's growing up, His whole being, including His humanity and His human nature, was brought into the glorious expression of God. He crossed the death river, the ultimate river, and entered into glory, the reality of the expression of God's divine being. He is the man in the glory, the man in God's expression, even the man who is God's expression, God's glory. As the Captain, the Pioneer, the Forerunner (Heb. 6:20), He took the lead to enter into glory, and we, His followers, are taking the same way to be brought into the same glory, which was ordained by God for us (1 Cor. 2:7; 1 Thes. 2:12). He is leading us inwardly to take the same way that He took. We were called into this glory, and glory is our destiny. Christ has gone ahead of us to lead us to cross through the waters of suffering and enter into the region of glory, God Himself expressed. This is the real goal of crossing the river, the real goal of being a Hebrew (1 Kings 8:10-11). We can see God's goal in type with Abraham. The God of glory called Abraham (Acts 7:2-3). The fact that it was the God of glory who called Abraham to be the first river crosser means that glory was His goal. Abraham crossed the river, and later his descendants also crossed the river into the good land. Moreover, the crossing of the river into the good land was for the building up of the temple, which in typology is the corporate expression of God. When Solomon prayed at the dedication of the temple, the glory of the Lord filled the house. We should pray, "Lord, may Your glory fill the church." Our goal is glory. We want God to be expressed day by day and meeting by meeting in the church. We want Him to be expressed in us in our daily life, church life, and family life until He eventually gains a full, corporate expression. First Kings 8:22 records how Solomon stood before the altar, spread his hands toward heaven, and began to pray, but verse 54 says that Solomon rose up from kneeling on his knees. If we want the glory to fill the temple, we need to spend time on our knees in prayer.
The Captain of Salvation, the Man in the Glory, the Man Who Is God's Glory, Being the Seed of Glory within Us
The Captain of salvation, the man in the glory, the man who is God's glory, is the seed of glory within us. He has sown Himself into us as the seed of glory and is in us as the hope of glory. When a seed is sown, we have the hope that it will blossom. Christ in us as the hope of glory (Col. 1:27) is Christ in us as the seed of glory with the hope that He will grow in us and blossom out of us. His divine element will blossom out of us, and that blossoming of the divine element out of our tripartite being is glory. First John 3:9 says that the seed of God abides in us; this is Christ as the seed of glory within us. There is a seed of glory in our spirit. Psalm 16:9 says, ''Therefore My heart rejoices and My glory exults," and Mary said, "My soul magnifies the Lord, and my spirit has exulted in God my Savior"(Luke 1:46-47). According to these； verses, our glory which exults is our spirit which exults. The Septuagint version of Psalm 16:9 says that the tongue exults, indicating that our spirit, which is our glory, is connected to our tongue. This shows us how to practically cooperate with God for our glorification. If we want to express God, we have to be spirit-exercising people who speak for the Lord. What kind of expression of God would we have if we all sat in a meeting silently looking at one another? If we did this, we would begin to criticize one another and become negative. God is expressed when we all exercise our spirit to speak God speaks in the Son, who is the Word, and we need to exercise our spirit to speak for the Lord so that the seed of glory can grow within us.
Our Savior took the lead to fight through into glory; His whole life was a fighting process for glory. The Lord said, "I have a baptism to be baptized with, and how I am pressed until it is accomplished!(Luke 12:50). He was baptized in death so that the divine glory in Him could burst out of His being and be dispensed into millions of beings. The growing of the seed of glory within us is also a fighting process. Therefore, we need to be fighters. We should not be passive; there is a Fighter in our spirit, who is fighting for us even now. He is our Captain, and a captain is a fighter. Our Christian life has many hills and valleys, but regardless of where we are, He is in our spirit fighting for us. When we sow a seed into the ground, it begins to fight. It fights against the shell, and it fights against the soil that surrounds it; this fighting makes it strong.
Glory is the blossoming of the divine element from within us. The glory into which we shall enter is the glory of the divine element that has been sown into us as a seed. Glory is not objective and remote. Glory has been sown into us as a seed that grows within us and blossoms out from within us. When it blossoms, we will enter fully into glory. The Lord said of the disciples, "The glory which You have given Me I have given to them "(John 17:22). We have the glory within us. Second Thessalonians 1:10 says that Christ will be glorified in His saints. We believe that the Lord will come back outwardly, but where are the people who will open their whole being to Him and give Him the ground to come from within them and to blossom out through them? We must give ourselves to be this people.
Being Saved into Glory, the Expression of God5 through the Process of Suffering
We are saved into glory, the expression of God, through the process of suffering (Heb. 10:32-35; 2 Cor. 4:1648). Paul spoke of sufferings in Hebrews 2:10 because he realized that the Hebrew believers were suffering. In 10:32 he said that they were in a great conflict of suffering. We are saved through the process of suffering. In all the things we pass through, our outer man is decaying, but our inner man is being renewed day by day. Apparently the Christian life is a life of suffering; in actuality, it is a life of being renewed day by day through the process of suffering. According to Paul, our sufferings are the momentary lightness of affliction that does not compare with the eternal weight of glory that is being dispensed into us and which will blossom out through us.
Affliction is momentary, but glory is eternal. Affliction is light, but glory is eternally weighty. We must have this view.
The crossing of all the rivers brings us into glory, the glory that is God Himself expressed. We should have the view that sufferings are our helpers. When we are in the midst of suffering, we may not have such a view, but when we look back, we can see that the sufferings we have passed through have been the greatest help to us. All of our sufferings help us along the highways to Zion to transform us from glory to glory so that we can become Christ's glorious bride (Psa. 84:5-7; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rom. 8:17-18, 21; Eph. 5:27). Zion typifies the overcomers. The Lord's recovery is to build up Zion, and in His recovery, the Lord has put highways to Zion in our heart. Psalm 84:5-7 says, ("Blessed is the man whose strength is in You; / In whose heart are the highways to Zion. / Passing through the valley of Baca [weeping], / They make it a spring; / Indeed the early rain covers it with blessings." Having highways to Zion in our heart while passing through the valley of weeping is characteristic of the Christian life. Many times in our Christian life we pass through a valley of weeping, but we must remain with the Lord, keep our hearts turned to Him, follow Him as our fighting Captain, and cooperate with Him. Even in the midst of our suffering we should pray, "Lord, I truly need Your mercy. You know where I am. Lord, in the midst of this, gain in me what You want to gain, and let me gain of You what You want me to gain. Do not let me miss You, and do not let this suffering be wasted?" Everyone suffers, peoples lives are lost, and tragedies happen. The biggest tragedy, however, is that we would pass through suffering and remain the same. If we pray in this way and receive the Lord's mercy, the valley of weeping will be made a spring. The spring is our spirit, and the tears flowing from our eyes become the fullness of living water flowing into our being. The early rain covers the valley of weeping with blessing. The early rain signifies the spirit. While we open our heart to the Lord, turning to Him and crying out to Him for mercy that He would gain us, and while we cooperate with Him to plant ourselves in the church life and keep coming to the meetings regardless of the situation, His blessing showers down upon us. In the midst of all the things we pass through, we need to say, "Lord, by Your mercy keep my heart turned to You. Lord, do not let my heart become hardened. Keep my heart soft toward You. I do not understand what I am going through, but Lord, I know that what I am going through is for Your eternal purpose. You want to gain me. You want to saturate me with Your glory. You want glory to blossom out from within me. Lord, keep my heart turned to You so that I can behold Your glory." When we behold the glory of the Lord, we are being transformed from one degree of glory to another degree of glory. This is the Captain of salvation in us, fighting for us in the midst of sufferings to lead us from glory to glory.
Christ as the Captain of Our Salvation Fulfilling His Duty to Lead Us into Glory by Being Our High Priest to Minister God into Us
Christ as the Captain of our salvation fulfills His duty to lead us into glory by being our High Priest to minister God into us (Heb. 2:16-18). The pneumatic, indwelling Captain of our salvation, who is in our spirit as the seed of glory, is our great High Priest. When we open to Him, as our hearts are turned to Him, and when we say, "O Lord Jesus, I love You," He is ministering God into us. The first mention of a priest in the Bible is that of Melchisedec in Genesis 14. Melchisedec came to Abraham, the first river crosser, to minister bread and wine to him. Bread and wine signify the processed and consum­mated Triune God for our enjoyment. God is bringing us into glory by dispensing Himself to us, and we cooperate with Him by opening to Him every day. We should say, "Lord, I want to open my entire being to You without any reservation. Minister Yourself into me right now. Minister the riches of Yourself into me for my supply, my refreshment, my energy, and my encouragement to sustain and support me." When His riches are ministered into us, they become His fullness. Moreover, His fullness is His expression, and His expression is His glory. We must go to God, open to Him, allow Him to minister His riches into us, and then cooperate with the High Priest to be one with Him to minister God into others. We allow God to minister Himself into us, and then we go to others with the riches which are ministered into us. Our career is to minister the processed and consummated God as enjoyment into people. This is what produces glory; this is how God carries out His a glory corporation."
This kind of ministry is a succor, a helpful rescue, in which Christ gets into us, puts us upon Him, bears us, and carries us (Isa. 40:31).
When we open our being to Him and He ministers the riches of God into us, this becomes a helpful rescue to us. Sometimes we are trapped in our self, just as something is set in cement. At such times we need a helpful rescue. Succor implies aid, help, or relief in times of distress or dire need. No one in dire need prays doctrinal prayers. Rather, they say, "O Lord, help me!" Such a prayer is not for us to be rescued from our outward situation; it is for God to minister Himself into us. The help He gives us is to minister God into us, and when He does this, Christ gets into us, puts us upon Him, bears us, and carries Us through all the suffering into the region of glory. When we open to the Lord in this way, we mount up with wings as eagles. When we enjoy this succor, we participate in God's element and are brought into the expression of the glorious God.
CHRIST AS THE CAPTAIN OF SALVATION LEADING GOD'S MANY SONS INTO GLORY BY SAVING THEM ORGANICALLY THROUGH SANCTIFICATION
Christ as the Captain of salvation leads God's many sons into glory, the corporate expression of God, by saving them organically through sanctification (Heb. 2:10-11; Eph. 1:4-5; 1 Thes. 5:23; Rom, 5:10). It is a great revelation to connect Hebrews 2:10 and 11. Verse 10 says that He is leading many sons into glory, and verse 11 speaks of sancti­fication. Christ as the Captain of our salvation is our inner Sanctifier. He leads us into glory by sanctifying our whole being. Ephesians 1:4-5 tells us that God chose us to be holy, predestinating us unto sonship. Our being made holy, our sanctification, is for sonship. Sanctification is God's sonizing. Sanctification is the way that God is sonizing us to bring us fully into His glorious expression as the sons of God in full.When Brother Lee saw that sanctification is for sonship, he told us that this was new light. This is the final, ultimate finding concerning the truth of sanctification. Before this, we had seen seeking sanctification before our salvation, positional sanctification to separate us unto God upon being redeemed, and dispositional sanctification by Christ coming into us, saturating, soaking, and permeating us, dispensing God's holy, divine being into our tripartite being to make us holy as He is holy. However, the ultimate finding concerning sanctification is that sanctification is God's sonizing. Sanctification is for our full sonship, and sonship is the glory of God.
First Thessalonians 5:23 says that the Lord is sanctifying us wholly. His sanctifying us wholly is actually His organic salvation; He is saving us in His life organically through sanctification to make us holy. Leviticus 19:2 says, "You shall be holy, for I, Jehovah your God, am holy." To be holy as only God is holy means that we become God in nature, but not in the Godhead. Only God is different and distinct from everything that is common, but He saturates us with His holy nature to make us the holy city, which is the city of glory. The focal point of the book of Hebrews is to bring us into the holy nature of God. That is why this book says, "Come forward to the Holy of Holies" (10:22). Our spirit is the Holy of Holies. When we pray, "Lord, strengthen me into my spirit and keep me in my spirit/' we are being sanctified; that is, we are being made as holy as God is holy.
The Divine Sanctification for the Divine Sonship Being the Center of the Divine Economy and the Central Thought of the Revelation in the New Testament
The divine sanctification for the divine sonship is the center of the divine economy and the central thought of the revelation in the New Testament. Hebrews 2:10-11 shows us that the carrying out of the eternal economy of God is entirely by the process of the Spirits sanctification. The Captain of salvation who is in us is the pneumatic One, the sanctifying Spirit in our spirit.
The Divine Sanctification Being the Holding Line in the Carrying Out of the Divine Economy to Sonize Us Divinely
The divine sanctification is the holding line in the carrying out of the divine economy to sonize us divinely, making us sons of God that we may become the same as God in His life and in His nature (but not in His Godhead) so that we may be God's expression, which is His glory. The holding line in the carrying out of the divine economy may be illustrated by a fishing line. A fishing line holds the fish and directs one's fishing. The line of the divine sanctification holds and directs the carrying out of the divine economy, and it also holds all of our experiences in God's economy, from our repentance, to our regeneration, and all the way to our glorification. Sanctification is the holding line because every step of God's economy in His work in us is to make us holy. We all were in the ocean of humanity, and the Lord "hooked" us by the line of divine sanctification. Now, even if we want to leave the church life, we cannot, because the holding line brings us back. After completing college, a brother may seek a big career, but the holding line may bring him to the full-time training.
Our being "hooked" starts with seeking sanctification, which leads to our repentance, and continues through positional sanctification by the redeeming blood, regenerating sanctification, renewing sanctification, transforming sanctification, conforming sanctification, and glorifying sanctification until we are fully "reeled" into the being of God to become the glorious expression of the Divine Being. When we are fully "reeled" into the glorious expression of the Triune God, the holding line of sanctification will be completed.
The seeking sanctification, the initial sanctification, is unto repentance to bring us back to God (1 Pet. 1:2; Luke 15:8-10, 17-21). Before we were saved, God worked in our environment to set us apart, to cause us to think about the meaning of life. We may have asked ourselves, "What am I doing here? I am eating 'hog's food.'" Then we were convicted by the Spirit, and we woke up, came to our senses, and repented. This is the seeking sanctification.
The redeeming sanctification, the positional sanctification, is by the blood of Christ, to transfer us from Adam to Christ (Heb. 13:12). This is a further "reeling in" of our being into God. The regenerating sanctification, the beginning of dispositional sanctification, renews us from our spirit to make us, the sinners, sons of God (2 Cor. 5:17; John 1:12-13). At this point, the holy God actually comes into our spirit to make our spirit a holy spirit (2 Cor. 6:6). When the Holy Spirit comes into our spirit, our spirit becomes a holy spirit. This is the regenerating sanctification. The renewing sanctification, the continuation of dispositional sanctification, renews our soul from our mind through all the parts of our soul to make our soul a part of God's new creation (Rom. 12:2b; Eph. 4:23). The transforming sanctification, the daily sanctification, reconstitutes us with the element of Christ metabolically to make us a new constitution as a part of the organic Body of Christ (2 Cor. 3:18; 1 Cor. 3:12). In this way, the Captain of salvation further leads us into glory by the holding line of divine sanctification. The conforming sanctification, the shaping sanctification, shapes us in the image of the glorious Christ to make us the expression of Christ (Rom. 8:29). The glorifying sanctification, the consummating sanctification, redeems our body by transfiguring it to make us Christ's expression in full in glory (Phil. 3:21; Rom. 8:23). This is the final part of the line, the accomplishment of God's eternal goal by the Captain of salvation leading us into glory through the divine sanctification in His organic salvation. Hymns, #948 says, a In my spirit He regenerated me, / In my soul He's now transforming me. / He will change my body like unto His own, / Wholly making me the same as He." God's heart's desire is to wholly make us the same as He is.
THE DIVINE SANCTIFICATION BEING CARRIED OUT BY THE SANCTIFYING SPIRIT IN OUR SPIRIT
The divine sanctification is carried out by the sanctifying Spirit in our spirit (Rom. 8:4; Eph. 5:26). Every part of the holding line of the divine sanctification is carried out by the sanctifying Spirit in our spirit. Romans 15:16 tells us that sanctification is by the Holy Spirit. If we are going to stay on the holding line of the divine sanctification, which brings us into the glorious expression of the Divine Being, we have to live, move, and have our being by and according to the sanctifying Spirit speaking and working in our spirit. We need to pray, "Lord, I want to care for You as the sanctifying Spirit, speaking and working in my spirit. I want to take care of the mingled spirit.n Our whole life is to care for the mingled spirit. We have to care for the sanctifying Spirit, speaking and working in our spirit in our family life and in our church life. If we open to the Lord on our knees, He may tell us that we need to exercise our glory, our spirit, to speak for Him. Many times the real sanctifying involves correcting and constituting. When we take care of the sanctifying Spirit speaking and working in our spirit. He first corrects us and then constitutes us. If we say something wrong to our family or roommate and we open to the Lord, He shines on us and corrects us, and then we repent and confess. As we are repenting and confessing, we are being constituted with the sanctifying Spirit. This means that He supplies us and transfuses us with the riches of His holy nature to make us holy dispositionally. In our daily life and our church life we always need to deal with the Spirit and be right with the Spirit in our spirit. Ephesians 4:30 says, "Do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed unto the day of redemption.To not grieve the Spirit is to not make the Spirit unhappy. Practically this means that what we love and care for the most in our Christian life is the Lord's smile. If we do not have His smile, we are miserable. Brother Lee once told us that if he did not take the way of the Lord's recovery, he would miss the Lord's smile in this age and His reward in the next. Brother Lee was a person who did not want to miss the Lord's smile; that is, he did not want to grieve the Spirit. He took care of the sanctifying Spirit speaking and working in his spirit. We need to do the same.
The way to be sanctified is first to live, walk, and move according to the Spirit in our spirit. Second, it is to be sanctified by the washing of the water in the word. We need to be people in the word all the time. We need to read, pray-read, and sing the Bible. We should pray- read the Bible by all kinds of means. We should also come to the church meetings and the ministry meetings to hear the spiritual speaking of the holy word that metabolically washes us, intrinsically sanctifies us, and makes us a part of His glorious bride for the glorious expression of the Divine Being forever and ever.一E. M.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第七篇　怜悯、忠信、尊大的大祭司
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来二17，三1，四14～15，五5，10，六20，七26～28，八1。
壹　在圣经里，祭司的基本意义不是祭司事奉神，而是祭司将神供应人：
一　在圣经里头一次题到祭司，确立了祭司的原则。
二　圣经第一次用祭司这辞，是说到麦基洗德─创十四18～20：
１　圣经中说到祭司职分基本的故事，乃是说到一个祭司从神而来，把神的一些东西供应给神的子民：
a　麦基洗德从神那里来，把神的一些东西供应给亚伯拉罕。
b　饼和酒表征神供应给我们，使我们得着滋养、复苏、维持、安慰和加强。
２　关于基督是大祭司，主要的点乃是，祂将神供应到我们里面。
贰　希伯来书是一卷论到祭司职分三方面的书─来二17，五6，七16，25：
一　第一面是亚伦的祭司职分，为我们的罪献祭给神─来十12：
１　亚伦的祭司职分，解决了罪的问题─来七27，九12，28。
２　基督为着罪，借着一次向神献上自己为祭，就把罪除掉─来九26，十10～12。
３　亚伦的祭司职分不是神原初心意的一部分，乃是因着罪的问题，后来加上的─来一3，约一29，罗八3。
二　第二面是君尊的祭司职分，为着将神供应到我们里面─来五10，七1～2：
１　基督照着麦基洗德的等到次作大祭司，乃是公义王与平安王─赛三二17，九6。
２　基督洗净了罪，是由亚伦的工作所预表的，而祂坐在高处至尊至大者的右边，乃是照着麦基洗德的等次─诗一一○1，4，来一3，八1。
３　基督是君尊的大祭司，凡我们所需要的，祂都供应我们，把经过过程并终极完成的三一神分赐到我们里面作我们的供应，以完成神永远的定旨。
三　第三面是神圣的祭司职分，为着拯救我们到底─来七25：
１　基督是君尊的，这是身分的事，而基督是神圣的，则是构成成分的事，也就是说，具有必要的元素，使祂构成为神圣的大祭司。
２　基督的神性，使祂构成为活的、满了生命的大祭司，而能长远的继续祂的祭司职任─来七17，24。
３　神圣的祭司职分乃是不能毁坏之生命的拯救大能；因此，神圣的祭职分乃是带来生命，消除死亡─来七16。
叁　基督是怜悯、忠信的大祭司─来二17：
一　希伯来一、二章启示，基督完全够资格作我们的大祭司：
１　祂是神的儿子，有神的性情─一8。
２　祂是人的儿子，有人的性情─二6，9。
３　祂成为肉体，与我们一样─二14，17。
４　祂被试诱、试验─二18。
５　祂受了死的苦─二9。
６　祂为我们的罪成就了平息─二17。
７　祂废除了魔鬼─二14。
８　祂释放我们脱离死的奴役─二15。
９　祂在复活里产生许多弟兄，形成了召会─二11～12。
１０　祂被高举，得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─二9。
１１　祂是我们救恩的元帅─二10。
１２　祂帮助我们─二16。
二　基督能成为怜悯忠信的大祭司，因为祂是神的儿子，具有神性，也是人的儿子，具有人性：
１　祂是怜悯的，与祂之是人相合。
２　祂是忠信的，与祂之是神相合。
３　基督的神性和人性，是由大祭司所穿以弗得上面的金和麻所预表─出二八6～14，三九2～7：
a　以弗得上面，金和麻交织在一起，预表在基督里神性与人性的调和。
b　“在这个宇宙中有一种金线和麻线编织而成的织物，含有金、白、蓝、紫、朱红这五种颜色。这就是主耶稣今天所穿的以弗得。祂仍然穿着用金子和麻作成的衣服，有五种美丽的颜色，表明祂的神性、人性、属天、君尊和救赎。”（出埃及记生命读经，一六○八页。）
肆　基督是尊大的大祭司─来四14～15：
一　基督作我们的大祭司，在祂的人位、工作和所达到的事上是尊大的─来一5，8，二6，一3，二9～10，14～15，17，三5～6，四8～9，六20，九24，二9。
二　基督作我们尊大的大祭司，在各方面受过试诱，与我们一样，祂经过了诸天，能同情我们的软弱─来四14～15。
三　基督作我们尊大的大祭司，在至圣所里在神前担负着我们─来九24：
１　在旧约里，大祭司预表基督是我们的大祭司。
２　大祭司每次在至圣所里进到神面前时，都要在神面前，在肩上和胸前担着以色列人的名字─出二八9～12，15～30。
３　基督是我们怜悯、忠信、尊大的大祭司，我们在祂的肩（祂担负的力量）上，也在祂的心（祂的爱）上。
４　基督在至圣所在神前担负我们，将经过过程度并终极完成的三一神供应到我们里面。
四　我们该对基督的祭司职分有响应，坦然无惧的来到施恩的宝座前─来四16。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Seven
A Merciful, Faithful, and Great High Priest
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 2:17; 3:1; 4:14-15; 5:5, 10; 6:20; 7:26-28; 8:1
I.　The basic significance of a priest in the Bible is not that a priest serves God but that a priest ministers God to man:
A.　The first mention of a priest in the Scriptures establishes the principle of a priest.
B.　The first time the word?priest?is used in the Bible is with Melchisedec—Gen. 14:18-20:
1.　The foundational story of the priesthood in the Bible is that of a priest coming from God and ministering something of God to God's people:
a.　Melchisedec came from God and ministered something of God to Abraham.
b.　The bread and wine signify God being ministered to us to nourish, refresh, sustain, comfort, and strengthen us.
2.　The main point with respect to Christ as the High Priest is that He ministers God into us.
II.　Hebrews is a book on the priesthood in its three aspects—Heb. 2:17; 5:6; 7:16, 25:
A.　The first aspect—the aspect of the Aaronic priesthood—is for offering sacrifices to God for our sins—Heb. 10:12:
1.　The Aaronic priesthood solves the problem of sin—Heb. 7:27; 9:12, 28.
2.　Christ put away sin by offering Himself to God as the one sacrifice for sins—Heb. 9:26; 10:10-12.
3.　The Aaronic priesthood was not part of God's initial intention but was added later because of the problem of sin—Heb. 1:3; John 1:29; Rom. 8:3.
B.　The second aspect—the aspect of the kingly priesthood—is for ministering God into us— Heb. 5:10; 7:1-2:
1.　As a High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec, Christ is the King of righteousness and the King of peace—Isa. 32:17; 9:6.
2.　Christ's purifying of sins is typified by the work of Aaron, whereas His sitting down on the right hand of the Majesty on high is according to the order of Melchisedec—Psa. 110:1, 4; Heb. 1:3; 8:1.
3.　As the kingly High Priest, Christ ministers to us whatever we need, dispensing the processed and consummated Triune God into us as our supply to fulfill God's eternal purpose.
C.　The third aspect—the aspect of the divine priesthood—is for saving us to the uttermost— Heb. 7:25:
1.　For Christ to be kingly is a matter of status, but for Christ to be divine is a matter of constituent, that is, of having the necessary element that constitutes Him a divine High Priest.
2.　Christ's divinity constitutes Him a High Priest who is living, full of life, and able to continue His priesthood perpetually—Heb. 7:17, 24.
3.　The divine priesthood is the saving power of the indestructible life; thus, the divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death—Heb. 7:16.
III.　Christ is a merciful and faithful High Priest— Heb. 2:17:
A.　Hebrews 1 and 2 reveal that Christ is fully qualified to be our High Priest:
1.　He is the Son of God with the divine nature—1:8.
2.　He is the Son of man with the human nature—2:6, 9.
3.　He was incarnated to be like us—2:14, 17.
4.　He was tempted, tried—2:18.
5.　He suffered death—2:9.
6.　He made propitiation for our sins—2:17.
7.　He destroyed the devil—2:14.
8.　He released us from the slavery of death— 2:15.
9.　He brought forth many brothers in resurrection to form the church—2:11-12.
10.　He was crowed with glory and honor in His exaltation—2:9.
11.　He is the Captain of our salvation—2:10.
12.　He helps us—2:16.
B.　Christ is able to be a merciful and faithful High Priest because He is both the Son of God with divinity and the Son of man with humanity:
1.　His being merciful corresponds to His being a man.
2.　His being faithful corresponds to His being God.
3.　Christ's divinity and humanity are typified by the gold and the linen in the ephod worn by the high priest—Exo. 28:6-14; 39:2-7:
a.　The weaving together of the gold and the linen in the ephod typifies the mingling of divinity and humanity in Christ.
b.　"There is a fabric in this universe woven with golden and linen thread and containing the five colors of golden yellow, pure white, blue, purple, and scarlet. This is the ephod that the Lord Jesus is wearing today. He is still clad in a garment made of gold and linen and with five beautiful colors expressing His divinity, humanity, heavenliness, kingliness, and redemption" (Witness Lee,?Life-study of Exodus,?p. 1372).
IV.　Christ is a great High Priest—Heb. 4:14-15:
A.　As our High Priest Christ is great in His person, in His work, and in His attainment—Heb. 1:5, 8; 2:6; 1:3; 2:9-10, 14-15, 17; 3:5-6; 4:8-9; 6:20; 9:24; 2:9.
B.　As our great High Priest Christ was tempted in all respects like us, He has passed through the heavens, and He sympathizes with our weaknesses—Heb. 4:14-15.
C.　As our great High Priest Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies—Heb. 9:24:
1.　In the Old Testament the high priest typifies Christ as our High Priest.
2.　Whenever the high priest went into the presence of God in the Holy of Holies, he bore upon his shoulders and upon his breast the names of the children of Israel before God— Exo. 28:9-12, 15-30.
3.　Christ is our merciful, faithful, and great High Priest, and we are on His shoulders (His bearing strength) and on His heart (His love).
4.　As Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies, He ministers the processed and consummated Triune God into our being.
D.　We should respond to Christ's priesthood by coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace—Heb. 4:16.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第七篇　怜悯、忠信、尊大的大祭司
A Merciful, Faithful, and Great High Priest
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来二17，三1，四14～15，五5，10，六20，七26～28，八1。
壹　在圣经里，祭司的基本意义不是祭司事奉神，而是祭司将神供应人：
I.　The basic significance of a priest in the Bible is not that a priest serves God but that a priest ministers God to man:
一　在圣经里头一次题到祭司，确立了祭司的原则。
A.　The first mention of a priest in the Scriptures establishes the principle of a priest.
二　圣经第一次用祭司这辞，是说到麦基洗德─创十四18～20：
B.　The first time the word?priest?is used in the Bible is with Melchisedec—Gen. 14:18-20:
１　圣经中说到祭司职分基本的故事，乃是说到一个祭司从神而来，把神的一些东西供应给神的子民：
1.　The foundational story of the priesthood in the Bible is that of a priest coming from God and ministering something of God to God's people:
a　麦基洗德从神那里来，把神的一些东西供应给亚伯拉罕。
a.　Melchisedec came from God and ministered something of God to Abraham.
b　饼和酒表征神供应给我们，使我们得着滋养、复苏、维持、安慰和加强。
b.　The bread and wine signify God being ministered to us to nourish, refresh, sustain, comfort, and strengthen us.
