２　关于基督是大祭司，主要的点乃是，祂将神供应到我们里面。
2.　The main point with respect to Christ as the High Priest is that He ministers God into us.
贰　希伯来书是一卷论到祭司职分三方面的书─来二17，五6，七16，25：
II.　Hebrews is a book on the priesthood in its three aspects—Heb. 2:17; 5:6; 7:16, 25:
一　第一面是亚伦的祭司职分，为我们的罪献祭给神─来十12：
A.　The first aspect—the aspect of the Aaronic priesthood—is for offering sacrifices to God for our sins—Heb. 10:12:
１　亚伦的祭司职分，解决了罪的问题─来七27，九12，28。
1.　The Aaronic priesthood solves the problem of sin—Heb. 7:27; 9:12, 28.
２　基督为着罪，借着一次向神献上自己为祭，就把罪除掉─来九26，十10～12。
2.　Christ put away sin by offering Himself to God as the one sacrifice for sins—Heb. 9:26; 10:10-12.
３　亚伦的祭司职分不是神原初心意的一部分，乃是因着罪的问题，后来加上的─来一3，约一29，罗八3。
3.　The Aaronic priesthood was not part of God's initial intention but was added later because of the problem of sin—Heb. 1:3; John 1:29; Rom. 8:3.
二　第二面是君尊的祭司职分，为着将神供应到我们里面─来五10，七1～2：
B.　The second aspect—the aspect of the kingly priesthood—is for ministering God into us— Heb. 5:10; 7:1-2:
１　基督照着麦基洗德的等到次作大祭司，乃是公义王与平安王─赛三二17，九6。
1.　As a High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec, Christ is the King of righteousness and the King of peace—Isa. 32:17; 9:6.
２　基督洗净了罪，是由亚伦的工作所预表的，而祂坐在高处至尊至大者的右边，乃是照着麦基洗德的等次─诗一一○1，4，来一3，八1。
2.　Christ's purifying of sins is typified by the work of Aaron, whereas His sitting down on the right hand of the Majesty on high is according to the order of Melchisedec—Psa. 110:1, 4; Heb. 1:3; 8:1.
３　基督是君尊的大祭司，凡我们所需要的，祂都供应我们，把经过过程并终极完成的三一神分赐到我们里面作我们的供应，以完成神永远的定旨。
3.　As the kingly High Priest, Christ ministers to us whatever we need, dispensing the processed and consummated Triune God into us as our supply to fulfill God's eternal purpose.
三　第三面是神圣的祭司职分，为着拯救我们到底─来七25：
C.　The third aspect—the aspect of the divine priesthood—is for saving us to the uttermost— Heb. 7:25:
１　基督是君尊的，这是身分的事，而基督是神圣的，则是构成成分的事，也就是说，具有必要的元素，使祂构成为神圣的大祭司。
1.　For Christ to be kingly is a matter of status, but for Christ to be divine is a matter of constituent, that is, of having the necessary element that constitutes Him a divine High Priest.
２　基督的神性，使祂构成为活的、满了生命的大祭司，而能长远的继续祂的祭司职任─来七17，24。
2.　Christ's divinity constitutes Him a High Priest who is living, full of life, and able to continue His priesthood perpetually—Heb. 7:17, 24.
３　神圣的祭司职分乃是不能毁坏之生命的拯救大能；因此，神圣的祭职分乃是带来生命，消除死亡─来七16。
3.　The divine priesthood is the saving power of the indestructible life; thus, the divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death—Heb. 7:16.
叁　基督是怜悯、忠信的大祭司─来二17：
III.　Christ is a merciful and faithful High Priest— Heb. 2:17:
一　希伯来一、二章启示，基督完全够资格作我们的大祭司：
A.　Hebrews 1 and 2 reveal that Christ is fully qualified to be our High Priest:
１　祂是神的儿子，有神的性情─一8。
1.　He is the Son of God with the divine nature—1:8.
２　祂是人的儿子，有人的性情─二6，9。
2.　He is the Son of man with the human nature—2:6, 9.
３　祂成为肉体，与我们一样─二14，17。
3.　He was incarnated to be like us—2:14, 17.
４　祂被试诱、试验─二18。
4.　He was tempted, tried—2:18.
５　祂受了死的苦─二9。
5.　He suffered death—2:9.
６　祂为我们的罪成就了平息─二17。
6.　He made propitiation for our sins—2:17.
７　祂废除了魔鬼─二14。
7.　He destroyed the devil—2:14.
８　祂释放我们脱离死的奴役─二15。
8.　He released us from the slavery of death— 2:15.
９　祂在复活里产生许多弟兄，形成了召会─二11～12。
9.　He brought forth many brothers in resurrection to form the church—2:11-12.
１０　祂被高举，得了荣耀尊贵为冠冕─二9。
10.　He was crowed with glory and honor in His exaltation—2:9.
１１　祂是我们救恩的元帅─二10。
11.　He is the Captain of our salvation—2:10.
１２　祂帮助我们─二16。
12.　He helps us—2:16.
二　基督能成为怜悯忠信的大祭司，因为祂是神的儿子，具有神性，也是人的儿子，具有人性：
B.　Christ is able to be a merciful and faithful High Priest because He is both the Son of God with divinity and the Son of man with humanity:
１　祂是怜悯的，与祂之是人相合。
1.　His being merciful corresponds to His being a man.
２　祂是忠信的，与祂之是神相合。
2.　His being faithful corresponds to His being God.
３　基督的神性和人性，是由大祭司所穿以弗得上面的金和麻所预表─出二八6～14，三九2～7：
3.　Christ's divinity and humanity are typified by the gold and the linen in the ephod worn by the high priest—Exo. 28:6-14; 39:2-7:
a　以弗得上面，金和麻交织在一起，预表在基督里神性与人性的调和。
a.　The weaving together of the gold and the linen in the ephod typifies the mingling of divinity and humanity in Christ.
b　“在这个宇宙中有一种金线和麻线编织而成的织物，含有金、白、蓝、紫、朱红这五种颜色。这就是主耶稣今天所穿的以弗得。祂仍然穿着用金子和麻作成的衣服，有五种美丽的颜色，表明祂的神性、人性、属天、君尊和救赎。”（出埃及记生命读经，一六○八页。）
b.　"There is a fabric in this universe woven with golden and linen thread and containing the five colors of golden yellow, pure white, blue, purple, and scarlet. This is the ephod that the Lord Jesus is wearing today. He is still clad in a garment made of gold and linen and with five beautiful colors expressing His divinity, humanity, heavenliness, kingliness, and redemption" (Witness Lee,?Life-study of Exodus,?p. 1372).
肆　基督是尊大的大祭司─来四14～15：
IV.　Christ is a great High Priest—Heb. 4:14-15:
一　基督作我们的大祭司，在祂的人位、工作和所达到的事上是尊大的─来一5，8，二6，一3，二9～10，14～15，17，三5～6，四8～9，六20，九24，二9。
A.　As our High Priest Christ is great in His person, in His work, and in His attainment—Heb. 1:5, 8; 2:6; 1:3; 2:9-10, 14-15, 17; 3:5-6; 4:8-9; 6:20; 9:24; 2:9.
二　基督作我们尊大的大祭司，在各方面受过试诱，与我们一样，祂经过了诸天，能同情我们的软弱─来四14～15。
B.　As our great High Priest Christ was tempted in all respects like us, He has passed through the heavens, and He sympathizes with our weaknesses—Heb. 4:14-15.
三　基督作我们尊大的大祭司，在至圣所里在神前担负着我们─来九24：
C.　As our great High Priest Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies—Heb. 9:24:
１　在旧约里，大祭司预表基督是我们的大祭司。
1.　In the Old Testament the high priest typifies Christ as our High Priest.
２　大祭司每次在至圣所里进到神面前时，都要在神面前，在肩上和胸前担着以色列人的名字─出二八9～12，15～30。
2.　Whenever the high priest went into the presence of God in the Holy of Holies, he bore upon his shoulders and upon his breast the names of the children of Israel before God— Exo. 28:9-12, 15-30.
３　基督是我们怜悯、忠信、尊大的大祭司，我们在祂的肩（祂担负的力量）上，也在祂的心（祂的爱）上。
3.　Christ is our merciful, faithful, and great High Priest, and we are on His shoulders (His bearing strength) and on His heart (His love).
４　基督在至圣所在神前担负我们，将经过过程度并终极完成的三一神供应到我们里面。
4.　As Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies, He ministers the processed and consummated Triune God into our being.
四　我们该对基督的祭司职分有响应，坦然无惧的来到施恩的宝座前─来四16。
D.　We should respond to Christ's priesthood by coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace—Heb. 4:16.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE SEVEN
A MERCIFUL, FAITHFUL, AND GREAT HIGH PRIEST
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Christ as the Captain of our salvation is leading God's many sons into glory. In order to carry out His function as the Captain of our salvation, He needs another qualification. He must be the High Priest. In God's economy the kingship, or leadership, and the priestly ministry go together. There can be no Joshua without the ministering priest.
In Exodus 17 Joshua fought against Amalek in the valley, but Moses, signifying the priesthood, prayed while Aaron and Hur held up his hands. When Moses1 hands came down, Joshua suffered a setback, but when his hands remained up, signifying prevailing prayer in the heavens, Joshua became victorious (vv. 10-13).
CHRIST AS OUR HIGH PRIEST MINISTERING TO US WHATEVER WE NEED
We have Christ as our High Priest. He makes no promise to give us what we want, but He is committed to God and to us to minister to us whatever we need. In my prayer I ask the Lord to minister Himself to me according to His understanding of what I need. I do not know what I need. Do you really know what you need? We do not know ourselves well enough to adequately tell God in full what we need. Our High Priest ministers to us whatever we need by dispensing Himself into us. We must have the realization that we are vessels. If we open our vessels to the High Priest, He will minister to us whatever we need by dispensing Himself into us as our supply for the sake of God's eternal purpose.
Dear brothers and sisters, our contact with the Lord may not be adequate. We all need to have personal contact with the Lord. The kind of contact that we have with the Lord depends very much on our realization concerning Him. If we only know Him in a limited way, our contact will also be limited. Many believers contact the Lord in an inadequate way because they have a limited realization concerning what He is and who He is. The Lord would like to increase our appreciation of Him and as a result enrich our experience of contacting Him.
Consider for an example that you are applying for a job at a large company. As you are walking down the hallway, you meet a gentleman that you think is the janitor. Spontaneously, you relate to him in a certain manner. But as you are speaking with him, you begin to realize that he is the CEO of the company. Would this not change the way you contact this gentleman? If we realized that the Lord is the King and the lovely Bridegroom, would this not affect our contact with Him? If we realized that He is the great Physician, able to heal us of all manner of diseases, would this not affect our contact with Him? The more we realize concerning the Lord, the more we appreciate Him, and the richer our contact with Him will be. My time with the Lord in the morning has changed in recent years because I am beginning to realize Him in His heavenly ministry. I have found that I cannot live without His interceding. I need Him as the Minister of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, not man (Heb. 8:2). Knowing Christ as the High Priest will change our life with Him.
THE ALL-INCLUSIVE CHRIST BEING THE CENTER OF GOD'S ECONOMY
In God's economy the all-inclusive Christ is the center. Christ is the center, the centrality, of God's economy. In stanzas 1, 2, 3, and 9 of Hymns, #495, Brother Lee wrote:
1 Christ is God's centrality
And His universality;
He is God's delight and joy
Throughout all eternity.
2 He's th' embodiment of God,
In Him all God's fulness dwells;
His unique supremacy
And His Godhead none excels.
3 All God's purpose is for Him,
That He might be all in all;
All the things in heav'n and earth
With Himself are made withal.
9 God intends in everything
Christ should have preeminence,
And that such a Christ of all
We should now experience.
In God's economy Christ is the unique center. Christ the Son is the center and the focal point of the book of Hebrews. Christ is the center in God's economy and Christ is the center of the book of Hebrews, but what is our center in actuality? Toward the end of his life, Brother Lee said that we must take Christ as the center, the Body as the line, and the New Jerusalem as the goal. We may enjoy Christ and we may contact Him, but in actuality the self may be our center. We may not have considered that God's economy is versus our natural being with the self as the center. There needs to be a great upheaval in our life so that we will begin to seek the Lord in this way, saying, a Lord, make Yourself the unique center of my life and my being in every way." Christ must be the center of our marriage. Christ must be the center of our family. To some people, the center of their life is their health. Whenever they get a chance, they will talk to you about their health. To others, it is their children. Some of the older saints will talk about their children much more than about Christ. This may be a river that separates them in the land of self-centeredness from the region of Christ-centeredness.
We must cross over and begin to realize that there is another realm. In this realm I am only one of millions of members of the Body of Christ; I am not the center. God's Son is the unique center. It is possible to read this message entitled "Merciful, Faithful, and Great High Priest" in a subjective, self-centered way. We may say, "How good it is that I have a High Priest who understands my need. He knows what it is like to get up early in the morning. He knows what it is like to be in traffic. He knows what it is like to hold a crying child for hours early in the morning. He knows what it is like to be misunderstood, to be betrayed, to be alone, to be ill, and to be poor. How good that there is Someone who understands me and cares for me and feels what I feel!" I do not devalue any of our enjoyment of Christ's sympathy; but Christ sympathizes with us and ministers to us for God's eternal purpose. Christ as the merciful and faithful High Priest is able to link our personal situation with the whole of God's operation in the universe. Yes, He cares for us. He suffers with us. But He takes care of us in God's way, not ours. He takes care of us for God's interest and God's purpose, not ours. We are not the center. The wonderful Son is the center. Everything He does from this center is to carry out God's economy so that God may have rest.
THE HEAVENLY CHRIST
The book of Hebrews is focused on the heavenly Christ. The main point of the heavenly Christ is His priesthood. The revelation of Christ in the earlier sections of the book of Hebrews is a preparation for the central point. Because Christ is the Son of God, the Son of Man, the God-appointed Heir, the Captain of salvation, the Apostle, and the real Joshua, He is able to be the High Priest. If we do not know Him as the High Priest, we miss the central point of the book of Hebrews because this book is centered in particular on Christ as our High Priest.
THE BASIC SIGNIFICANCE OF A PRIEST IN THE BIBLE BEING TO MINISTER GOD TO MAN
The basic significance of a priest in the Bible is not that a priest serves God but that a priest ministers God to man. Our appreciation of the word priest may not be very great; but priest is a significant word in God's economy. Our natural concept of a priest may be that he is one who dresses in unusual clothes, attends to an altar and sacrifices, burns incense, and does certain things in a prescribed way. By doing all of these things, he serves God. Yet all these things are secondary. They are not the basic significance of a priest in the Bible. The basic significance of a priest in the Bible is that a priest ministers God to man. Our basis for saying this is the principle of first mention.
The first mention of a priest in the Scriptures establishes the principle of a priest. The first mention of the word priest is with Melchisedec (Gen. 14:18-20). The foundational story of the priesthood in the Bible is that a priest comes from God and ministers something of God to God's people. This first mention is in relation to Abraham and to his fighting for his nephew Lot (w. 14-16). Do not think that river crossers are only occupied with their own river crossing and look down with disdain on those who do not do so well. Lot was not that successful. He made an unwise choice to separate from Abraham. Lot chose the well-watered plain of the Jordan and ended up in Sodom (13:1042). When Abram the Hebrew heard that Sodom was invaded by a coalition of kings, he gathered an army together and fought for his nephew. If we care for the Body, if we honor the Body, if we have the feeling for the Body, we will do at least two things. First, on our part we will endeavor to cross every river. Second, we will fight for our brothers. Those who are backslidden, those who are weak, and those who do not cross the rivers do not need our criticism; they do not need our merciless discernment; they need our fighting.
Abraham fought for Lot, and while he was fighting, Melchisedec was praying. After the fight Melchisedec came to this fighting river Grosser with bread and wine. Melchisedec came from God and ministered something of God to Abraham. This is what a priest does. He bears you before God. You may not even know it. None of us has the capacity to realize how much and how long we have been borne by our High Priest day and night. We did not come this way because of ourselves, and we have not been preserved here because of ourselves. We are not better than those who fell or those who left. We are here because of God's mercy. One has been bearing us before God, especially when we fight. After we fight He comes to serve us with bread and wine, the processed God for our enjoyment. In the Gospel of Luke we are told that in the kingdom the Lord will come to serve us (12:37). What do you think Melchisedec who has an eternal priesthood will do when all the victorious fighters are gathered together? He may come and say, ''Victors, My valiant victors. Did I not say in the Gospels that I would not partake of this feast until I partake of it new with you in My Fathers kingdom? Now here I am. I will preside over this Lord's table meeting. I will minister the bread and wine to you. May I come and offer you the bread and wine so that you may enjoy Me?" The bread and wine signify God being ministered to us to nourish, refresh, sustain, comfort, and strengthen us. How can we pass through so many trials? It is because Someone is sustaining us with bread and wine.
CHRIST BEING THE HIGH PRIEST MINISTERING GOD INTO US
The main point with respect to Christ as the High Priest is that He ministers God into us. The Captain of salvation is our Leader, our Fighter, and we follow Him. Actually, we cannot follow Him without having God ministered into our being by the same One whose status is that of a High Priest. Do you realize why so many of us are willing to follow this ministry? We are not blind followers, but when someone ministers God to us, we will follow that person anywhere. The real leadership emerges out of the God-ministering service of the priesthood. Others have tried to lead people, to be a captain, to give orders, and to organize believers without ministering God into them. Some will follow such people, and some will not. The result is division. Our Brother Lee was in another realm. For more than three decades in the United States, From this ministry the real leadership organically emerged, and we followed the leadership of this ministry, which is the apostles' teaching.
THE BOOK OF HEBREWS BEING ON THE PRlESTHOOD IN THREE ASPECTS
Hebrews is a book on the priesthood in its three aspects: the Aaronic aspect, the kingly aspect, and the divine aspect (Heb. 2:17; 5:6; 7:16, 25), With each of these three aspects of the priesthood, the key word is offering.
The First Aspect—the Aaronic Priesthood
The first aspect- the aspect of the Aaronic priesthood- is for offering sacrifices to God for our sins (Heb. 10:12). This need came in because of the fall. If there had not been the fall, there would be no need for this aspect of the priesthood. The Aaronic priesthood solves the problem of sin (7:27; 9:12, 28). Christ put away sin by offering Himself to God as the one sacrifice for sins (9:26; 10:1,042). Satan the devil has been destroyed and sin has been put away (2:14). Hallelujah! God says, "And their sins and their lawlessnesses I shall by no means remember anymore" (10:17). God is a God of forgetfulness when it comes to forgetting sins that have been put away by the Aaronic priesthood.
The Aaronic priesthood was not part of God's initial intention but was added later because of the problem of sin (Heb. 1:3; John 1:29; Rom. 8:3). God's original intention was not to redeem us; it was to impart Himself into us and to work Himself into us.
The Second Aspect—the Kingly Priesthood
The second aspect—the aspect of the kingly priesthood—is for ministering God into us (Heb. 5:10; 7:1-2). As a High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec, Christ is the King of righteousness and the King of peace (Isa. 32:17; 9:6). This world cries out for peace, but they will not take God's righteousness. God is not a politician, and He is not a diplomat. If you allow Him to be the King in righteousness, there will be peace. This Priest is a King in righteousness, and righteousness brings in peace. He exercises His righteousness to bring in peace so that there is a pleasant environment for Him to minister God into our being.
Christs purifying of sins is typified by the work of Aaron, whereas His sitting down on the right hand of the Majesty on high is according to the order of Melchisedec (Psa. 110:1, 4; Heb. 1:3; 8:1). As the kingly High Priest, Christ ministers to us whatever we need, dispensing the processed and consummated Triune God into us as our supply to fulfill God's eternal purpose.
As an illustration of how the Lord takes care of our need for His purpose, let us consider what is commonly called the "Lord's prayer" in Matthew 6:9-13. There is wisdom in the order of the items in this example of prayer. The first part of the prayer is for God's interests. "Our Father who is in the heavens, Your name be sanctified; Your kingdom come; Your will be done, as in heaven, so also on earth" (vv. 9-10). This is to care for God's economy, God's interests. But when we do this, we become vulnerable in our humanity to the enemy's assault. So we also need to pray protective prayers and prayers in which we acknowledge our need. We need to humble ourselves and pray in the following manner, saying, "Father, please give me my daily bread. Do not lead me into trial. I cannot bear any trial. Deliver me from the evil one."
We may be extremists. Some saints may be focused on their need for bread, their need for health, their need for money, and other such things. Others, the natural heroic type, may consider that this is too low. They may think to themselves, "I will just care for God's kingdom, God's glory, and God's name." This may seem very good, but actually, it can be, in part, an expression of pride. We often must be brought down to realize that we are but weak, fragile human beings. We need the Lord to protect us; we need the Lord to provide for us. If you need a job, you may ask in a simple way, "Lord, I need a job. Provide me with the best job for Your Body." If you need a wife, simply ask, saying, "Lord, I need a wife. Give me a wife for Your economy." God will meet your need for His need. He is ministering Himself to us to meet our need. He really cares for our need, not just so that we can be happy, have a nice life, and be secure, but so that we can live on the earth for His economy.
The Third Aspect—the , Divine Priesthood The third aspect- the aspect of the divine priesthood- is for saving us to the uttermost (Heb. 7:25). This is not the saving of a Savior; it is the saving of the divine priesthood. For Christ to be kingly is a matter of status, but for Christ to be divine is a matter of constituent, that is, of having the necessary element that constitutes Him a divine High Priest. Christ's divinity constitutes Him a High Priest who is living, full of life, and able to continue His priesthood perpetually (w. 17, 24). The divine priesthood is the saving power, of the indestructible life; thus, the divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death (v. 16).
The saving here is the saving from death and all of the side effects of death, like depression, decay； corruption, sighing, groaning, moaning, despair, vanity, and meaninglessness. If you are sighing, groaning, and moaning and if you feel your life is vain and meaningless, you are under the side effects of death. You need the divine High Priest who has the indestructible life. No form of death is a. match for Him. He passed through death itself and conquered it. Your death is no match for His indestructible life. Give Him a chance to save you by the power of an indestructible life. While He is saving you, He is interceding for you, praying for you all the time to be saved.
CHRIST BEING A MERCIFUL AND FAITHFUL HIGH PRIEST
Christ Being Fully Qualified to Be Our High Priest
Hebrews 1 and 2 reveal that Christ is fully qualified to be our High Priest. He is the Son of God with the divine nature (1:8). He is the Son of Man with the human nature (2:6, 9). He was incarnated to be like us (2:14, 17). The first line of Hymns, #1017 says, "Christ has put on human nature and become like a man like me." He was tempted, tried (v. 18). He was tried by things that we do not even know exist. He was tempted in a way that we cannot imagine. He experienced the full scope of trial and temptation, and He conquered it as a man. He is fully qualified. There is no test, trial, or situation that He cannot face in us. He suffered death (v. 9). He made propitiation for our sins (v. 17). He destroyed the devil (v. 14). He released us from the slavery of death (v. 15). He brought forth many brothers in resurrection to form the church (w. 11-12). He was crowned with glory and honor in His exaltation (v. 9). He is the Captain of our salvation (v. 10). He helps us as a merciful and faithful High Priest (v. 16).
Christ Being Able to Be a Merciful and Faithful High Priest Because He Is Both the Son of God with Divinity and the Son of Man with Humanity
Merciful Corresponding to His Being a Man
Christ is able to be a merciful and faithful High Priest because He is both the Son of God with divinity and the Son of Man with humanity.His being merciful corresponds to His being a man. Although there are exceptions, typically younger brothers and sisters are not very good at being merciful to others. This is because they have not experienced that much failure yet. The more real human experience you have and the more human you become, the more genuinely merciful you can be. Sometimes people may rebel against the Lord's recovery. They may go out, and some may feel happy that they are gone. Would it not be better if people repented, rather than be destroyed? Is it not better that people live, instead of die under God's judgment? Is it not better that brothers or sisters be recovered, rather than consumed? The Lord does not deal with us according to strict righteousness. If He dealt with me by strict righteousness, I would not be alive today; I would be in Forest Lawn Cemetery. I am alive because our High Priest had mercy on me. It is not a natural mercy; it is not a political mercy, but a genuine, heartfelt mercy in His humanity.
Faithful Corresponding to His Being God
Christ being faithful corresponds to His being God. He is merciful to us but simultaneously He is faithful to God. He is faithful to us, and He is faithful to God. His way of caring for us is often very different from our opinion as to how He should take care of us. He feels what we feel, but because He is faithful to God He may do something that we do not expect, or He may not do something that we did expect.
Christs divinity and humanity are typified by the gold and the linen in the ephod worn by the high priest (Exo. 28:6-14; 39:2-7). An ephod was a sacred upper garment worn by the high priest. The high priest had a robe that went down almost to the ground and a tunic. He also had an upper garment. The ephod had a hole for the neck and holes for the arms; it was sleeveless and had a girdle. The fabric of the ephod was marvelous. It was formed basically of golden thread woven with linen. Gold signifies divinity; white linen signifies humanity. This weaving signifies the mingling of Christ's divinity and humanity in His person as our High Priest. His faithfulness in His divinity is interwoven with His mercy in His humanity.
Three additional wonderful colors were contained in this fabric: blue, purple, and scarlet. Blue signifies heavenliness. He is a heavenly person in His divine-human being. Purple signifies kingliness. Scarlet, red in color, signifies redemption. So in the ephod, the primary colors of the rainbow were shown, including yellow (in the gold), blue, and red. This indicates that our High Priest is a God-man who has faithfulness woven into His constitution. He stands before God, bearing us as the God-man and the Man-God, as the faithful and merciful High Priest. Today He is still wearing this marvelous ephod in its divine and human reality.
In the Life-study of Exodus, Brother Lee said, "There is a fabric in this universe woven with golden and linen thread and containing the five colors of golden yellow, pure white, blue, purple, and scarlet. This is the ephod that the Lord Jesus is wearing today. He is still dad in a garment made of gold and linen and with five beautiful colors expressing His divinity, humanity, heavenliness, kingliness, and redemption" (p. 1372).
CHRIST BEING A GREAT HIGH PRIEST
Christ Being Great in His Person, in His Work, and in His Attainment
Christ is a great High Priest (Heb. 4:14-15). As our High Priest Christ is great in His person, in His work, and in His attainment (1:5, 8; 2:6; 1:3; 2:9-30, 14-15, 17; 3:5-6; 4:8-9; 6:20; 9:24)。I am a little concerned that some of those who have been raised in the church life from birth may not know the Lord in His greatness, in His majesty, in His awesomeness, in His sovereignty, in His kingliness, and in His lordliness. As we realize more of who He is, our appreciation of Him will increase, and we also will treasure such a One who has become crumbs under the table for us Gentile dogs to eat (Matt. 15:27), As we eat Him we need to realize that we are eating the Lord of the universe. He is the very God, the mighty God, yet He became One that we could take in as our life supply.
Christ Being Tempted in All Respects Like Us
As our great High Priest Christ was tempted in all respects like us, He has passed through the heavens, and He sympathizes with our weaknesses (Heb. 4:14-15). When Stephen was being stoned, this One stood up (Acts 7:55). He also asked Saul, "Why are you persecuting Me?" (9:4). He feels persecution.
Christ as the Great High Priest Being the One Who Bears Us before God in the Holy of Holies
As our great High Priest Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies. Hebrews 9:24 says that Christ appears before the face of God for us. The high priest in the Old Testament had a breastplate over his heart with twelve stones; on these stones the names of the twelve tribes of Israel were engraved. On the shoulder pieces of the ephod were two onyx stones that also had the names of God's people (Exo. 28:9-12, 15-30). Christ as our High Priest is now standing before the face of God. God sees this God-man, the merciful, faithful, and great High Priest, standing before Him with our names on His heart (His love) and on His shoulders (His bearing strength). What a sight this is! All the churches, all the saints, all the brothers, and all the sisters are borne before the face of God by this great High Priest as He intercedes to God for each one of them in every particular detail concerning their life on earth. This is marvelous!
Responding to Christ's Priesthood by Coming Forward with Boldness to the Throne of Grace
As the Lord is bearing us before God in this way, He is interceding, He is ministering, and He is caring for us. But often His way of caring for us is very different from what we want. Perhaps you are a brother and sister in the church, and you have a number of sons and daughters.
Your desire may have been for a long period of time that a particular one would go to the full-time training. Yet, instead of doing this, he or she may go another way. In your disappointment can you say, "Lord, Your care is the best. Your way is the best"? Isaiah 55 says, "For as the heavens are higher than the earth, / So are My ways higher than your ways" (v. 9). You may be ill, and you pray for the Lord to heal you, yet He leaves you in that situation. Someone you love may be desperately ill, and you desperately pray for the Lord to heal that one, yet the Lord takes that one. There are many situations in which the Lord as God cares for us in a way beyond what we would ever expect. He needs a number of His river crossers to experience Him in such a way that they will vindicate Him, offer sacrifices of praise to Him, and say, a Lord, I don't understand why this is happening. I don't understand Your way, but Your way to take care of me is the best.n You may buy a new car, and two days later it is totaled in an accident. You may say, "Did God not know that it would be totaled?" Yes, doctrinally, He is omniscient; He knows all. Yes, He is omnipotent. He could have prevented it, but He did not. He must have concluded that you received the best. This is the way it really is. Many things will happen to many of the saints in the coming months. We will need a lot of care, and we also need a word that can address our actual situation. What happens when the heavens are silent? What should we do when there is no word and no miracle? What happens when the prayer does not seem to be answered? We need a vision into the inner being of God to realize that if He does not answer us in the way that we think He should and if He does nothing for us, we still love Him, we still trust in Him, and we still come forward to Him. The Lord asks one thing of us in response to this revelation of His high-priestly ministry-come forward (Heb. 4:16). As Christ bears us before God in the Holy of Holies, He ministers the processed and consummated Triune God into our being. We should respond to Christ's priesthood by coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace.
In Hebrews 4:12-13, we are exposed, our soul and spirit are divided, and we are laid bare before the Lord. It is at this juncture that the writer of the book of Hebrews says, "Come forward with boldness" (v. 16). To come forward is to cross the river, to come out of ourselves, out of our mind, out of our mood, out of our feeling, out of our disposition, and out of our situation. We must come forward to our mingled spirit where we will meet Melchisedec, the great High Priest.
A CONCLUDING PRAYER
Lord, we pray to You right now. Lord, unveil Yourself. Father, reveal Your Son in us as the merciful, faithful, and great High Priest. Speak into our spirit what human lips cannot say. Oh, thank You, Jesus the Son of God. We have such a great High Priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son of God. Thank You, Lord, right now we are on Your heart, and we are on Your shoulders. You are bearing us as You stand before the face of God. You are interceding for us all the time, and You have come to minister the wealth of the being of the processed and consummated God into our regenerated spirit. O Melchisedec, we welcome You. We love You. We respect You. We appreciate You. We need You so much. Thank You for the deep assurance that the whole recovery is on Your heart and on Your shoulders. Lord, take care of us according to Your way. Lord, do things Your way. Take us on Your way.
You are merciful to us and faithful to God. Lord, thank You, for being what You are. Thank You for being who You are. May we know You more and more that our daily contact will be uplifted, our experience will be enriched, and our enjoyment will be deepened, that the churches as the Body may be built up, and that the bride may be prepared for Your coming back. Thank You, Lord Jesus.一R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第八篇　基督是使徒和神家的建设者
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来三1～6，太十六18，创一26，二7～12，22。
壹　耶稣是我们的使徒，就是受差遣，从神并同神到我们这里来的人，也是我们的大祭司，就是从我们并同我们回到神那里去的人─来三1：
一　摩西是使徒耶稣的预表（来三5，徒三22～23），是神所差遣的使徒，要去把以色列人领出埃及，带他们经过旷野，目标是要进入美地，目的为使他们能形成并构成为神的家─出三8，16～18，四十33～34。
二　耶稣是使徒，同着神到我们这里，与我们分享神，使我们有分于祂神圣的生命、性情和丰满，为使我们能形成并构成为祂的召会，就是活神的家─约六46，八16，29，十10下，太十六18，提前三15。
贰　基督是神家的建设者─来三2～6：
一　基督在祂的人性里是神建设的好材料，在祂的神性里又是建设者：
１　基督在祂的人性里是基石、（赛二八16、）房角石、（太二一42，徒四11、）顶石（亚四7）和活石（彼前二4），以产生我们为活石，作神属灵的殿（5）。─引用经文
２　基督不仅在祂的人性里是这建设的一部分，也在祂的神性里是建设者：
a　神在基督里，乃是在我们里面，将祂自己建造到我们全人里面，并将我们建造到祂里面─弗三17，约十四23。
b　祂将祂的神性建造到我们的人性里，并将我们的人性建造到祂的神性里，好将祂的神性与我们的人性，调和成一个实体。
二　我们是神活的、属灵的、生机的家（提前三15，彼前二5，弗二21～22），而基督乃是生机的建造神的家，就是祂的召会：─引用经文
１　主耶稣在马太十六章十八节预言，祂必建造祂的召会，而在祂的恢复中这预言的应验，要将祂带回来─启十九7。
２　神生机建造之建筑计划的蓝图（来十一10），见于创世记一、二章：─引用经文
a　创世记一章启示，神的心意是要得着一个团体的人，作为有祂形像的建筑，以彰显祂自己，并且这人要受托付，有祂的权柄，用祂的管治权征服并恢复地─创一26，来二5～6。
b　创世记二章启示，神完成祂定旨的路：
（一）　神造人作器皿，使人有人的灵，好叫人能盛装神，接触神，接受神，并将神吸收到人的全人里面─创二7，徒九15，林后四7，约四24，弗二22。
（二）　神完成祂心意的第二步，乃是将人这个空的器皿放在生命树前，使人有分于、并充满祂这生命─创二9，约十四6，十10，六35，57，63。
（三）　生命树旁有一道河（创二10），这启示出我们一旦接受基督作生命，祂就在我们里面成为生命的水流─约七37～39，诗三六8～9，四六4。
（四）　在河流那里有三种宝贵的材料（创二11～12），表征神圣的生命在我们里面涌流，将我们变化为宝贵的材料，为着神的建造─林前三12，林后三18，启二一18～21。
（五）　神用亚当的肋骨建造夏娃，这乃是一幅图画，说出神在基督里、并用基督这独一的元素建造召会，以终极完成新耶路撒冷，就是终极的夏娃，作神团体、得了建造的彰显─创二22，约十九34，启二一2。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Eight
Christ as the Apostle and Builder of God's House
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 3:1-6; Matt. 16:18; Gen. 1:26; 2:7-12, 22
I.　Jesus is our Apostle, the One who was sent to us from God and with God, and our High Priest, the One who went back to God from us and with us—Heb. 3:1:
A.　Moses, as a type of the Apostle Jesus (Heb. 3:5; Acts 3:22-23), was God's sent one, the apostle who was to take Israel out of Egypt and lead them through the wilderness with the goal of entering the good land for the purpose that they might be formed and constituted into the house of God—Exo. 3:8, 16-18; 40:33-34.
B.　Jesus as the Apostle came to us with God to share God with us that we might partake of His divine life, nature, and fullness so that we might be formed and constituted into His church, the house of the living God—John 6:46; 8:16, 29; 10:10b; Matt. 16:18; 1 Tim. 3:15.
II.　Christ is the Builder of God's house—Heb. 3:2-6:
A.　Christ in His humanity is the good material for God's building, and in His divinity He is the Builder:
1.　Christ in His humanity is the foundation stone (Isa. 28:16), the cornerstone (Matt. 21:42; Acts 4:11), the topstone (Zech. 4:7), and the living stone (1 Pet. 2:4) to produce us as living stones for God's spiritual house (v. 5).
2.　Christ is not only a part of the building in His humanity but also the Builder in His divinity:
a.　God in Christ is within us to build Himself into our being and to build us into His being—Eph. 3:17; John 14:23.
b.　He builds His divinity into our humanity and builds our humanity into His divinity in order to mingle and blend His divinity with our humanity into one entity.
B.　We are God's living, spiritual, organic house (1 Tim. 3:15; 1 Pet. 2:5; Eph. 2:21-22), and Christ builds God's house, His church, in an organic way:
1.　In Matthew 16:18 the Lord Jesus prophesied that He would build His church, and the fulfillment of this prophecy in His recovery will bring Him back—Rev. 19:7.
2.　The blueprint of God's architectural plan for His organic building (Heb. 11:10) is seen in Genesis 1 and 2:
a.　Genesis 1 reveals that God's intention is to have a corporate man as a building in His image to express Himself, and this man is to be committed with His authority, using His dominion to subdue and recover the earth—Gen. 1:26; Heb. 2:5-6.
b.　Genesis 2 reveals the way that God fulfills His purpose:
1)　 God created man as a vessel with a human spirit that man might contain God, contact God, receive God, and assimilate God into his whole being— Gen. 2:7; Acts 9:15; 2 Cor. 4:7; John 4:24; Eph. 2:22.
2)　 God's second step in fulfilling His intention was to put man, His empty vessel, in front of the tree of life that man might partake of and be filled with Him as life—Gen. 2:9; John 14:6; 10:10b; 6:35, 57, 63.
3)　 Along with the tree there was a river (Gen. 2:10), revealing that once we receive Christ as life, He becomes the flow of life within us—John 7:37-39; Psa. 36:8-9; 46:4.
4)　 At the flow of the river were three kinds of precious materials (Gen. 2:11-12), signifying that the divine life flowing within us transforms us into precious materials for God's building—1 Cor. 3:12; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rev. 21:18-21.
5)　 God's building of Eve with Adam's rib is a picture of His building the church in and with Christ as the unique element to consummate the New Jerusalem, which will be the ultimate Eve as God's corporate builded expression— Gen. 2:22; John 19:34; Rev. 21:2.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第八篇　基督是使徒和神家的建设者
Christ as the Apostle and Builder of God's House
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来三1～6，太十六18，创一26，二7～12，22。
壹　耶稣是我们的使徒，就是受差遣，从神并同神到我们这里来的人，也是我们的大祭司，就是从我们并同我们回到神那里去的人─来三1：
I.　Jesus is our Apostle, the One who was sent to us from God and with God, and our High Priest, the One who went back to God from us and with us—Heb. 3:1:
一　摩西是使徒耶稣的预表（来三5，徒三22～23），是神所差遣的使徒，要去把以色列人领出埃及，带他们经过旷野，目标是要进入美地，目的为使他们能形成并构成为神的家─出三8，16～18，四十33～34。
A.　Moses, as a type of the Apostle Jesus (Heb. 3:5; Acts 3:22-23), was God's sent one, the apostle who was to take Israel out of Egypt and lead them through the wilderness with the goal of entering the good land for the purpose that they might be formed and constituted into the house of God—Exo. 3:8, 16-18; 40:33-34.
二　耶稣是使徒，同着神到我们这里，与我们分享神，使我们有分于祂神圣的生命、性情和丰满，为使我们能形成并构成为祂的召会，就是活神的家─约六46，八16，29，十10下，太十六18，提前三15。
B.　Jesus as the Apostle came to us with God to share God with us that we might partake of His divine life, nature, and fullness so that we might be formed and constituted into His church, the house of the living God—John 6:46; 8:16, 29; 10:10b; Matt. 16:18; 1 Tim. 3:15.
贰　基督是神家的建设者─来三2～6：
II.　Christ is the Builder of God's house—Heb. 3:2-6:
一　基督在祂的人性里是神建设的好材料，在祂的神性里又是建设者：
A.　Christ in His humanity is the good material for God's building, and in His divinity He is the Builder:
１　基督在祂的人性里是基石、（赛二八16、）房角石、（太二一42，徒四11、）顶石（亚四7）和活石（彼前二4），以产生我们为活石，作神属灵的殿（5）。─引用经文
1.　Christ in His humanity is the foundation stone (Isa. 28:16), the cornerstone (Matt. 21:42; Acts 4:11), the topstone (Zech. 4:7), and the living stone (1 Pet. 2:4) to produce us as living stones for God's spiritual house (v. 5).
２　基督不仅在祂的人性里是这建设的一部分，也在祂的神性里是建设者：
2.　Christ is not only a part of the building in His humanity but also the Builder in His divinity:
a　神在基督里，乃是在我们里面，将祂自己建造到我们全人里面，并将我们建造到祂里面─弗三17，约十四23。
a.　God in Christ is within us to build Himself into our being and to build us into His being—Eph. 3:17; John 14:23.
b　祂将祂的神性建造到我们的人性里，并将我们的人性建造到祂的神性里，好将祂的神性与我们的人性，调和成一个实体。
b.　He builds His divinity into our humanity and builds our humanity into His divinity in order to mingle and blend His divinity with our humanity into one entity.
二　我们是神活的、属灵的、生机的家（提前三15，彼前二5，弗二21～22），而基督乃是生机的建造神的家，就是祂的召会：─引用经文
B.　We are God's living, spiritual, organic house (1 Tim. 3:15; 1 Pet. 2:5; Eph. 2:21-22), and Christ builds God's house, His church, in an organic way:
１　主耶稣在马太十六章十八节预言，祂必建造祂的召会，而在祂的恢复中这预言的应验，要将祂带回来─启十九7。
1.　In Matthew 16:18 the Lord Jesus prophesied that He would build His church, and the fulfillment of this prophecy in His recovery will bring Him back—Rev. 19:7.
２　神生机建造之建筑计划的蓝图（来十一10），见于创世记一、二章：─引用经文
2.　The blueprint of God's architectural plan for His organic building (Heb. 11:10) is seen in Genesis 1 and 2:
a　创世记一章启示，神的心意是要得着一个团体的人，作为有祂形像的建筑，以彰显祂自己，并且这人要受托付，有祂的权柄，用祂的管治权征服并恢复地─创一26，来二5～6。
a.　Genesis 1 reveals that God's intention is to have a corporate man as a building in His image to express Himself, and this man is to be committed with His authority, using His dominion to subdue and recover the earth—Gen. 1:26; Heb. 2:5-6.
b　创世记二章启示，神完成祂定旨的路：
b.　Genesis 2 reveals the way that God fulfills His purpose:
（一）　神造人作器皿，使人有人的灵，好叫人能盛装神，接触神，接受神，并将神吸收到人的全人里面─创二7，徒九15，林后四7，约四24，弗二22。
1)　 God created man as a vessel with a human spirit that man might contain God, contact God, receive God, and assimilate God into his whole being— Gen. 2:7; Acts 9:15; 2 Cor. 4:7; John 4:24; Eph. 2:22.
（二）　神完成祂心意的第二步，乃是将人这个空的器皿放在生命树前，使人有分于、并充满祂这生命─创二9，约十四6，十10，六35，57，63。
2)　 God's second step in fulfilling His intention was to put man, His empty vessel, in front of the tree of life that man might partake of and be filled with Him as life—Gen. 2:9; John 14:6; 10:10b; 6:35, 57, 63.
（三）　生命树旁有一道河（创二10），这启示出我们一旦接受基督作生命，祂就在我们里面成为生命的水流─约七37～39，诗三六8～9，四六4。
3)　 Along with the tree there was a river (Gen. 2:10), revealing that once we receive Christ as life, He becomes the flow of life within us—John 7:37-39; Psa. 36:8-9; 46:4.
（四）　在河流那里有三种宝贵的材料（创二11～12），表征神圣的生命在我们里面涌流，将我们变化为宝贵的材料，为着神的建造─林前三12，林后三18，启二一18～21。
4)　 At the flow of the river were three kinds of precious materials (Gen. 2:11-12), signifying that the divine life flowing within us transforms us into precious materials for God's building—1 Cor. 3:12; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rev. 21:18-21.
（五）　神用亚当的肋骨建造夏娃，这乃是一幅图画，说出神在基督里、并用基督这独一的元素建造召会，以终极完成新耶路撒冷，就是终极的夏娃，作神团体、得了建造的彰显─创二22，约十九34，启二一2。
5)　 God's building of Eve with Adam's rib is a picture of His building the church in and with Christ as the unique element to consummate the New Jerusalem, which will be the ultimate Eve as God's corporate builded expression— Gen. 2:22; John 19:34; Rev. 21:2.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE EIGHT
CHRIST AS THE APOSTLE AND BUILDER OF GOD'S HOUSE THE SUBJECT OF THE EPISTLE TO THE HEBREWS一CHRIST BEING SUPERIOR TO JUDAISM AND EVERYTHING RELATED TO IT
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The book of Hebrews was written with a particular background. At the time of its writing, there were many Jewish people in Judaism who believed in the Lord Jesus Christ and became Christians. However, they still loved their old religion of Judaism. Judaism may be considered the best among all the religions of the earth. The Jews believe and worship the true God, the Creator of the universe. They also have the Word of God, the Old Testament, and their religion has many wonderful matters. Therefore, the Jewish people who had believed in the Lord Jesus Christ and had become Christians had a difficult time letting go of the things of that old religion. So God raised up the writer of the book of Hebrews, whom we believe to be Paul, to write to these Jewish believers in order to help them leave the bondage of the old religion of Judaism.
In writing the book of Hebrews, Paul took the way of comparing Christ with everything in Judaism. There were five particular things that the Jews treasured and loved. First, they had God, the real God, the Creator of the universe. Second, they had angels. Third, they had Moses; fourth, Aaron; and fifth, the old covenant. First, Paul compared Christ to the God of the Jews in Judaism. Then he compared Christ to the angels, to Moses, and to Aaron. Finally, he compared the new covenant to the old covenant.
The God in Judaism was a God who spoke through the prophets (Heb. 1:1). He was a hidden God. The Jewish people worshipped the real God, but they could not really know Him. They could not know with certainty the way that God did things. They could hear the com- mandments God gave them, but they could not know the real, living God. In the New Testament, however, we have Christ, Christ is the only begotten Son of God. John 1:18 says, "No one has ever seen God; the only begotten Son, who is in the bosom of the Father, He has declared Him." The only begotten Son of God is the declared God. He is God manifested, God explained, and God defined. He is the Word of God.
He is life, light, grace, and reality. The only begotten Son of God is the Word. As the Word He is God expressed. As life He is God imparted; as light He is God shining; as grace He is God enjoyed; and as reality He is God realized. We have such a wonderful Christ. We have such a wonderful God. In the Old Testament the Jewish people could observe God's temperament and then guess about His ways and principles of doing things. They could not know the living God directly. Today, however, not only can we know God; we can actually experience Him. We can enjoy Him, and we can be one with Him. We have such a wonderful Christ.
JESUS BEING OUR APOSTLE, THE ONE SENT TO US FROM GOD AND WITH GOD, AND OUR HIGH PRIEST, THE ONE WHO WENT BACK TO GOD FROM US AND WITH US
Hebrews 1 and 2 reveal that Christ is superior to the angels because Christ is both the Son of God and the Son of Man. As the Son of God He is superior to the angels. As the Son of Man He is also superior to the angels. Christ as the expressed God is superior to the God in Judaism, and Christ as the Son of God and the Son of Man is superior to the angels. These are the first two comparisons in the book of Hebrews. The third comparison is Christ being superior to Moses. Moses was God's apostle and therefore he typified the real Apostle, Jesus Christ. The word apostle means "a sent one J God sent Moses; therefore, Moses was an apostle. Jesus is our Apostle, the One who was sent to us from God and with God, and our High Priest, the One who went back to God from us and with us (3:1). Christ is not only the High Priest; He is the Apostle, God's sent One.
Moses, as a Type of the Apostle Jesus, Being God's Sent One
Moses, as a type of the Apostle Jesus (Heb. 3:5; Acts 3:22-23), was God's sent one, the apostle who was to take Israel out of Egypt and lead them through the wilderness with the goal of entering the good land for the purpose that they might be formed and constituted into the house of God (Exo. 3:8, 16-18; 40:33-34). In the Old Testament Moses was sent by God; therefore, he was the apostle. He was sent by God to bring the people of Israel out of Egypt into the good land. He led the people of Israel into the good land with the purpose of building God's habitation. In the wilderness they built the tabernacle, which was a symbol of God's dwelling place. Moses brought the people of Israel out of Egypt to form them and constitute them into the house of God. These were types and shadows but not the reality.
Jesus as the Apostle Coming to the Believers with God to Share God with Them
Jesus as the Apostle came to us with God to share God with us that we might partake of His divine life, nature, and fullness so that we might be formed and constituted into His church, the house of the living God (John 6:46; 8:16, 29; 10:10b; Matt. 16:18), Jesus is the real Apostle. He came with God to share God with us. When God sent Moses, Moses came alone. Moses could tell the people of Israel that he saw God and that God had spoken to him and had given him the Ten Commandments to pass on to them. However, when the Father sent Jesus, He came with God the Father. God did not send Jesus by Himself alone while He remained in the heavens. When Jesus came, He came with God. When Jesus came, God came. When He came with God, He could dispense God into us so that we could receive God. When we receive the Apostle Jesus, we receive God. When we receive God, a real building is built, and this building is the house of God, the church (1 Tim. 3:15). Jesus as the Apostle came to us with God to share God with us.
Moses could not share God with the children of Israel; he could only share the Ten Commandments with them. But now Christ can share God with us. We can now partake of God's divine life, nature, and fullness that we might be formed and constituted into His church. To Christ, the church is the Body of Christ; to the Father, the church is the house of God. The house of God is not a physical building. The house of God is God's family and household (Eph. 2:19). When the real Apostle of God came to us, He brought God to us and brought God into us that we could partake of God's life and divine nature to be formed and constituted into the church, the house of God.
Christ as the Apostle, the Sent One, Breathing Him self into Us That We May Be His Sent Ones
The book of Hebrews is the best book concerning migration because it speaks of Christ as the Apostle, the sent One. On the evening of the Lord's resurrection He appeared to the disciples, breathed Himself into them, and said, "Receive the Holy Spirit" (John 20:22). Christ breathed Himself into us, and today we receive the wonderful Holy Spirit. He is everything to us, including our life and life supply. At the same time, Jesus also said, "Peace be to you; as the Father has sent Me, I also send you" (v. 21). He did not breathe Himself into us to give us a good feeling, a little power, and some supply. He wants to send us. He wants to make all of us apostles, sent ones. He is the Apostle. We have received Him into us; therefore, we have an Apostle inside of us. By this One we can migrate.
The Gospel of Grace Being to Come and Go
In the New Testament, there are two critical words concerning the gospel of grace. The first word is come, and the second word is go. John 7:37 says, "Now on the last day, the great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, If anyone thirsts, let him come to Me and drink." We need to come to Him. If we are thirsty, empty within, or feel that there is nothing in the world that can satisfy us, we can come to the Lord Jesus and drink, and we will be satisfied. Matthew 11:28 says, "Come to Me all who toil and are burdened, and I will give you rest." If you feel that in this world you are under such pressure or labor or burden, you should come to the Lord. We all need to come. Coming to the Lord is wonderful, but the gospel of grace does not stop here. After we come, we should go. In Matthew 11, the Lord asked us to come to Him, but in Matthew 28 He said, "Go therefore" (v. 19).
Going in Migration Being to Disciple All the Nations
Migration is simply to go. Since we have come to the Lord and we have received Him and have been filled with Him, we all need to go. Migration is part of our going. We all need to go. Going sometimes means that we may need to move to a new place to start the church life in a new city. However, moving to a new city is not the only way to go. We need to go out every day. We all need to go out to preach the gospel, to visit and take care of people. We need to preach the gospel in our schools, offices, and neighborhoods. We need to go out to disciple the nations. This is the burden of migration. Our burden is that all of the saints in the Lord's recovery need to wake up, rise up, and go out to disciple the nations. Sometimes the Lord does not even ask us to go and visit new people by knocking on their doors. He may simply ask us to go to the new ones who come to the meetings.
When we consider migrating to a new place, we need to be dear that our going is to disciple the nations. Thus, we are entering a battlefield. There is a battle because God wants to build up His Body and Satan wants to frustrate God's building. Migration is a move of the Body, not a personal move; therefore, we have to pray first. We do not go in the way of a movement or in an organizational way. Christ, the Head of the Body, is the only One who can send us. Our prayer is not to be clear about where to go, but it is to contact and fellowship with the wonderful Christ. The more we pray, the more we contact the living Christ, who is the Apostle. Do not forget that this Apostle is living in you. You may not want to move, or you may only want to move to an easy and nice environment. However, the Apostle living in you wants to send you. The more you pray, the more you will contact this Apostle, and the more you will become an apostle, a sent one.
This move in migration is not an individual move, but a move of the Body. We not only need to pray, but we also need to fellowship. We need to fellowship with the leading ones in our locality. This fellowship is not simply a talk. Some may not really fellowship, but would instead inform the leading ones that they are clear about the Lord's leading. Fellowship is the flow of life. First we need to pray; then we need to fellowship. We all were born in the flesh, grew up in the flesh, and do not habitually walk according to the Spirit. Therefore, we should not have that much confidence that our feeling is so accurate. We need to have fellowship.
The leading ones or elders must genuinely fellowship with the saints who are burdened to migrate. They should not speak to them in a political way or merely give them their opinion. Sometimes, after a number of saints have come to the leading ones and said, "Brothers, I really feel that the Lord is leading me to migrate," the leading ones may begin to wonder what will happen in their locality if so many move away. This concern may cause them to discourage the saints from going. This is not fellowship. In other cases, the leading ones may hesitate to fellowship frankly with certain saints for fear that they may be criticized for not being for the burden of the Body or for not yet seeing the Body. This also is not fellowship. In still other cases, the leading ones may be glad that some troublesome saints have expressed a desire to migrate, and they may encourage them to go. We should not do this, since the new churches need extra care and support. If we really see the Body, we should have a broad view of the entire situation.
The burden of migration is that we all need to wake up from our sleep, rise up, come to the Lord, and go to disciple the nations to bring in the remaining fruit, the good material, for God's building. Many of the saints may be passive. If we have not gone out to preach the gospel or we have not taken care of others in our present locality, there is not much expectation or confidence that we will suddenly begin to do these things by moving to a new locality such as Charlotte, Jacksonville, or Memphis. We need to pray to the Lord and enter into His burden. Our Lord Jesus Christ is the Apostle who is in us. As God the Father sent Him, the Lord also wants to send us to disciple the nations. This should be our living wherever the Lord has put us. We need a life of migration. Every day, wherever the Lord has put us, we need to make this a part of our living, that is, to go and disciple the nations.
CHRIST BEING THE BUILDER OF GOD'S HOUSE
In His Humanity, Christ Being the Good Material for God's Building, and in His Divinity Being the Builder
Christ is the Builder of God's house (Heb. 3:2-6). In His humanity, He is the good material for God's building, and in His divinity He is the Builder. Moses was only the type; Jesus is the real Apostle. Hence, as the first Apostle in the New Testament, He is superior to Moses. In addition, Moses was only a part of the building. God sent him, and he brought the people of Israel out of Egypt to build the tabernacle. That tabernacle was not the real dwelling place of God; it was merely a symbol. In the Old Testament time God's actual dwelling place was the people of Israel, and Moses was a part of that building. In the New Testament, our Lord Jesus Christ is the Apostle. He is building God into people and building the house of God. He is the Head of the Body, yet He is within all the members of the Body. He is part of the Body because He is within us. Moses was part of God's habitation in the Old Testament. Moses could only be part of the house; he was not the builder of the house because he only had humanity. Our Lord Jesus Christ, however, is a God-man; He has both divinity and humanity. With His humanity He is part of God's building, part of the house of God. With His divinity He is the Builder of God's house. The Bible shows us that Jesus Christ is part of the house. Christ in His humanity is the foundation stone (Isa. 28:16), the cornerstone (Matt. 21:42; Acts 4:11), the topstone (Zech, 4:7), and the living stone (1 Pet. 2:4) to produce us as living stones for God's spiritual house (v. 5). All of these aspects are for God's building.
Christ as the Builder of God's house is clearly superior to Moses. In this matter, Moses cannot compare to Christ. Christ is not only part of the house of God; He is also the Builder. He carries out this building by mingling divinity with humanity. Christ is not only part of the building in His humanity but also the Builder in His divinity. God in Christ is within us to build Himself into our being and to build us into His being (Eph. 3:17; John 14:23). He is building God into our being and building us into God's being. He builds His divinity into our humanity and builds our humanity into His divinity in order to mingle and blend His divinity with our humanity into one entity. This is what Christ is doing. He is building God into us and building us into God so that divinity and humanity can be mingled into one entity. This is illustrated by the meal offering in which the oil was mingled with the fine flour into one entity (Lev. 2:4-5). This one mingled entity is a sacrifice, an offering, which satisfied both God and man.
Today our Christ, the Apostle, is building God's house by mingling divinity with humanity into one entity, and this one entity is the mutual abode for both God and man (John 14:23). We abide in God and God abides in us (v. 20). We are God's habitation (Eph. 2:22), and God is our habitation. Furthermore, we are mutually satisfied. We are satisfied in God and God is satisfied in us. This is what Christ is doing. Moses could not do this. Yet we are enjoying this today. Why should the Jewish believers go back to Judaism? Today Christians may not be tempted to go back to Judaism, but many still try to go back to the Old Testament principles and ways. They may not care for what Christ is doing today, which is to build the house of God, to mingle God with us, and to mingle us with God, thereby producing one entity.
The Believers Being God's Living, Spiritual, Organic House, and Christ Building God's House, His Church, in an Organic Way
We are God's living, spiritual, organic house (1 Tim. 3:15; 1 Pet. 2:5; Eph. 2:21-22), and Christ builds God's house, His church, in an organic way. In Matthew 16:18 the Lord Jesus prophesied that He would build His church, and the fulfillment of this prophecy in His recovery will bring Him back (Rev. 19:7). The blueprint of God's architectural plan for His organic building (Heb. 11:10) is seen in Genesis 1 and 2. Verse 26 of chapter one shows us God's eternal purpose: 'And God said, Let us make man in our image, after our likeness: and let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, and over the fowl of the air, and over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that creepeth upon the earth." When God created man He did so according to His eternal purpose. His purpose has two aspects: image and dominion. Image means expression. God wants man to express Him. Man is the expression of God in the same way that a photo of someone is that person's image. When you see the photo, you see that person's image. God also wants man to exercise His authority over every creeping thing that creeps upon the earth.
I did not come from a Christian family, nor did I have a Christian background. However, from my youth I had an inward sense of "vanity of vanities" and of all things being vanity (Eccl. 1:2). Even before I knew this word vanity, I had the feeling of emptiness within. Later, by the Lord's mercy, I was saved and baptized, and I began to live in the church life. In the beginning of my Christian life I was very happy, but after a period of time, the sense of emptiness returned. I wondered about the meaning of human life. I thought to myself, "Is the meaning of human life merely to go to heaven and not to go to the lake of fire?" I even told a serving one who helped with the young people, "How boring it will be when we go to heaven and live there for eternity!" At that time, as a young person of fourteen or fifteen years of age, I did not have a clear vision of the New Jerusalem, and therefore I considered that it referred to a physical city. I imagined that the first few days of walking on the golden street would be very exciting. But after a week- not to say a million y e a rs-I imagined that I would be bored. One day I heard Brother Lee speaking on Genesis 1:26. He said that we human beings did not evolve from monkeys, but that we were created by God. If we had evolved from monkeys, our life would be meaningless. But God created us with a definite purpose in two aspects: image and dominion. This is why we exist in the universe, and this is the meaning of human life. Regardless of what other goal we might pursue, if we do not live according to God's eternal purpose, sooner or later, we discover that all things are empty. We human beings were not created for other things. Only when we live according to God's purpose and in His purpose are we satisfied so that we enjoy the Sabbath rest. To illustrate, he pointed out that a glass was created for the purpose of containing water. We could admire the glass for its beauty, but if the glass had feelings it might say, "Vanity of vanities- all is vanity. I was not created for this. I am：not fulfilling my purpose." This vision revolutionized my whole life.
God's Intention(Being to Have a Corporate Man in His Image and with His Authority
Genesis 1 reveals that God's intention is to have a corporate man as a building in His image to express Himself, and this man is to be committed with His authority, using His dominion to subdue and recover the earth (v. 26; Heb. 2:5-6).
The Steps in the Fulfillment of God's Purpose Revealed in Genesis 1 and 2
Genesis 2 reveals the way that God fulfills His purpose. In the first step God created man as a vessel with a human spirit that man might contain God, contact God, receive God, and assimilate God into his whole being (v. 7; Acts 9:15; 2 Cor. 4:7; John 4:24; Eph. 2:22). God's second step in fulfilling His intention was to put man, His empty vessel, in front of the tree of life that man might partake of and be filled with Him as life (Gen. 2:9; John 14:6; 10:10b; 6:35, 57, 63). Along with the tree, there was a river (Gen. 2:10), revealing that once we receive Christ as life, He becomes the flow of life within us (John 7:37-39; Psa. 36:8-9; 46:4). At the flow of the river were three kinds of precious materials (Gen. 2:11-12), signifying that the divine life flowing within us transforms us into precious materials for God's building (1 Cor. 3:12; 2 Cor. 3:18; Rev. 21:18-21). God's building of Eve with Adams rib is a picture of His building the church in and with Christ as the unique element to consummate the New Jerusalem, which will be the ultimate Eve as God's corporate, built expression (Gen. 2:22; John 19:34; Rev. 21:2).
Genesis 1 reveals God's eternal purpose, and Genesis 2 reveals God's economy. The word economy is not explicitly mentioned, but God's economy is actually His plan to fulfill His purpose and the way for Him to fulfill His purpose. In Genesis 2 we see that first God created man as a vessel. Second, God put this man in front of the tree of life. Third, we see a flowing river. Fourth, we see that many precious materials were produced. Fifth, we see the picture of the New Jerusalem through the building of Eve from Adam's rib, This is a clear picture. For God to accomplish His purpose, He created man as a vessel, not as an instrument or as a tool. Many Christians have the concept that we are God's instruments to be used by God. God did not create us as instruments, but as vessels to contain Him. If we do not contain God, we cannot do anything for Him. Genesis 2 reveals that this container has a human spirit within him. With the human spirit man can contact God. We can receive God into this container. Then God put this man in front of the tree of life. The picture is quite clear. God's intention is for this man, the container God created, to receive the tree of life into him. This is how God can fulfill His purpose. But the tragedy started when Adam did not receive the tree of life into him. Instead, Adam and Eve received the tree of the knowledge of good and evil into them. When they received the wrong thing into them, God's purpose was spoiled. We need to remember that we are containers. This is the reason that we always have the desire to receive something into us. But if we do not receive the right thing into us, we will damage the container. For example, many young ones desire to go to the movies and insist that there is no verse in the Bible that says they cannot watch movies. But when they watch movies, the wrong element gets into their containers. Later they may be sitting in a meeting, listening to a word concerning God's eternal purpose, when what they viewed some years ago flashes into their mind, becoming a frustration to them.
We are containers; therefore, we should receive the tree of life into us. The tree of life is the Lord Jesus Himself. He is the tree and He is life; therefore, He is the tree of life (John 15:1; 11:25). As the tree of life He is our food. He is the bread of life for us to eat and take in. When we eat the right food——the tree of life and the bread of life——the river flows. This is our experience. When we eat the right thing- Christ as the bread of life and as the tree of life- spontaneously we sense something flowing inside of us. When we refuse to eat the right food, the flow stops. We may argue that there is nothing wrong with a certain television program or a certain movie. But if we receive these things into us, the flow within us stops. We also may become dry, and eventually we become deadened.
The picture in Genesis 2 is so clear. We were created as vessels, and God wants us to receive the tree of life into us so that the river will flow.
When the river flows we not only have a comfortable sense within, but some precious materials are also produced. When the river is flowing, we eventually see gold, bdellium, and precious stones. Man was originally made of dust and clay. As men made of clay, there is nothing precious within us. But when we eat the right food, when we eat Christ, and when we enjoy the flow bf life, some precious things which are good for God's building will be produced within us. God will then build these precious materials together into one entity, the New Jerusalem.
The last point in Genesis 2 is not only the precious materials but also the New Jerusalem. The first two chapters of Genesis are a blueprint of the whole Bible. We see God's purpose and God's way, God's economy, to fulfill and accomplish His purpose. He wants to build a corporate expression for Himself. God does not want to express Himself through many scattered individuals. He wants to express Himself through one corporate man, one building.
The New Jerusalem Being the Corporate Expression of God—— the Fulfillment of God's Architectural Plan for His Organic Building
In Revelation 21 and 22, the last two chapters of the Bible, we see the New Jerusalem. The New Jerusalem is a picture of the final product. By comparing the blueprint with the final product, we see that God works according to His blueprint. The New Jerusalem is a corporate expression of God. The One who sits on the throne has the appearance of jasper (4:3). The New Jerusalem also has the appearance of jasper (21:11). The New Jerusalem is therefore a corporate expression of God who sits on the throne. By the Lord s mercy, He has revealed to us the blueprint, the final product, and the steps He takes to accomplish and reach His goal. The New Jerusalem is not only God's corporate expression but also the bride of Christ (w. 9-10). Today the church is not only the Body of Christ and the house of God but also God's corporate expression and the bride of Christ. There is a loving relationship between Christ and His bride. When someone is in love, nothing is boring, and time is not significant. For eternity we will be in a divine romance. Our loving relationship with Christ will never grow old or fade away. It will always be fresh and become sweeter and sweeter for eternity. Eternity will never be boring because we will be in a divine, universal romance. We will enjoy such a loving relationship with our lovely Christ. The more we know Him, the more we love Him. The more we love Him, the more we feel that there is not enough time. We will never be bored; instead, we will feel that even eternity is too short. This is how we will spend eternity. We do not have the kind of heaven that religious people have. That kind of heaven would sooner or later be quite boring. In the New Jerusalem, we will enjoy this loving relationship which will be sweeter and sweeter as the days go by. Praise the Lord, we love Christ, and His love toward us is sweet and rich. The more we know Him, the more we love and enjoy Him. What a wonderful eternity we will have!
A CONCLUDING WORD
The Lord revealed this vision to us through our Brother Lee. It is now up to us to spread this vision, this ministry, to the entire world. May the Lord be merciful to us. May we pray in order to contact the Lord Jesus as the Apostle, and may we let the Apostle, who has been sent by God, send us. When the Apostle Jesus Christ sends us, we can then say, "Amen."一B. C.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第九篇　安息日的安息
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来三5～四11。
壹　我们若要对希伯来书中安息日的安息有正确的领会，就必须认识圣经中第一次题到安息日之安息的意义─创二2～3：
一　神在第七日安息了，因为祂达到了祂所愿望的。
二　神的心愿是要得着人，在地上彰显祂并代表祂─创一26～28，来二6～8上：
１　整本圣经管制的原则，乃是在已过的永远里神制定了一个定旨，一个计划，要带着祂的权柄彰显祂自己。
２　神照着祂永远的计划而有的愿望，乃是要借着人使祂自己得着彰显和代表。
３　当人在地上彰显并代表神的时候，神的愿望就满足了。
三　当地上有一种光景，就是人彰显神并代表神时，那种光景就是神的安息日之安息。
四　新耶路撒冷是神终极并永远的安息日之安息，因为神在那里要完满的得着彰显和代表─启二一10～11，二二1，4上，5下。
五　安息日的安息就是神在祂的心愿上得了满足。
贰　在旧约里，美地是安息，因为圣殿能建造在那里─申十二9，王上八1～11：
一　神将美地、祂正确的子民和基督，看作是一个：
１　在圣经里，地表征神正确的子民；因此，按照神的观念，人总是与地有关联的。
２　在圣经里，地是复活基督的预表；基督乃是美地─创一9，申八7～10。
３　人的身体是用地上的尘土造的，但人自己是照着神的形像造的，目的是要彰显神，并且受托有神的权柄，可以施行神的管治权，并在地上形成神的国─创二7，一26。
４　地、基督和人合在一起，乃是神的彰显，也是神的国，有神的荣耀和权柄。
二　圣殿是以色列人进入美地终极的完成─王上八1～11：
１　神有了圣殿，就能为着祂的国度、管理和行政，得着祂的彰显和代表。
２　美地是地和神子民以及建造起来的神居所的组合，以彰显神并运用祂的权柄。
３　当神得着彰显和代表，神与人就得着满足；这乃是真安息。
４　今天召会生活乃是我们的美地，因为在这里我们有神的居所、彰显、权柄、国度的安息。
叁　在希伯来四章九节里，安息日的安息乃是基督作我们的安息，由迦南地所预表─申十二9，来四8：
一　基督作众圣徒的安息，分为三个阶段：
１　在召会时代，属天的基督，就是那彰显、代表、并满足了神的一位，也是那歇了一切的工，坐在诸天之上神右边的一位，祂现今在我们灵里是我们的安息─太十一28～29。
２　在千年国里，撒但从地上除去之后（启二十1～3），神要因基督和得胜的的圣徒，而得着彰显、代表并满足；然后基督连同国度，将是得胜圣徒更完满的安息，他们要与基督一同作王（4，6），有分于并享受祂的安息。─引用经文
３　在新天新地里，所有的仇敌，包括最后的仇敌，死，都被基督征服之后（林前十五24～27），祂这位全胜者，将是神所有赎民最完满的安息，直到永远。─引用经文
二　希伯来四章九节里安息日的安息，就是美地的安息所预表的，只包括基督作我们安息的头两个阶段：
１　头两个阶段的安息乃是奖赏，要给那些竭力追求主，丰满的享受了祂，并且成为得胜者的人。
２　第三阶段的安息，不是奖赏，乃是给所有赎民完满的分。
三　四章九节所说安息日的安息，是指基督在头两个阶段，特别在第二阶段，作我们的安息，就是为我们存留，要我们竭力寻求并进入的：
１　在基督作我们安息的第二阶段，祂要得着全地为业，作祂的国度一千年─诗二8，来二5～6。
２　在基督作我们安息的第二阶段，所有得胜的信徒，就是在第一阶段寻求并享受祂作安息的人，要在千年国里与祂一同作王─启二十4，6，提后二12：
a　他们要承受地土─太五5，诗三七11，路十九17，19。
b　他们要同享他们主人的快乐─太二五21，23。
c　这国度的安息，由进入迦南美地所预表，是新约信徒的目标。
肆　一共有三个安息─召会、国度、和新耶路撒冷：
一　现今的召会，乃是享受的安息日，要来的国度是赏赐的安息日─来十35，十一26，启二二12：
１　神是智慧的，用要来的安息日作为赏赐，鼓励我们享受现今的安息日。
２　神在祂的智慧里，命定来世和千年国是赏赐的时代，激励我们在今世寻求祂，并对祂的工作有回应。
二　召会生活是人在地上被神得着，而作神彰显和代表的生活─来二10～12，三6～8，四9。
三　写希伯来书的目的，是要鼓励得救的人不可丢弃召会生活，反而努力、奋斗，并竭力进入其中，因为召会生活乃是今天的安息日之安息─来十25，十三1，9，13～15。
四　我们该受鼓励，留在今天的召会生活里，就是在今天的安息日里，因为召会生活喂养、造就、建造我们，使我们有资格、并引我们进入国度时代的安息日。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Nine
The Sabbath Rest
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 3:5—4:11
I.　If we would have the proper understanding of the Sabbath rest in Hebrews, we need to know the significance of the first mention of the Sabbath rest in the Bible—Gen. 2:2-3:
A.　God rested on the seventh day because He had attained what He desired.
B.　The desire of God's heart is to have man on earth expressing Him and representing Him— Gen. 1:26-28; Heb. 2:6-8a:
1.　The governing principle of the Bible as a whole is that in eternity past God made a purpose, a plan, to express Himself with His authority.
2.　God's desire according to His eternal plan is to have Himself expressed and represented by man.
3.　When man is on earth expressing and representing God, God's desire is satisfied.
C.　When there is a situation on earth in which man expresses God and represents Him, that situation is a Sabbath rest to God.
D.　The New Jerusalem will be God's ultimate and eternal Sabbath rest because there God will be fully expressed and represented—Rev. 21:10-11; 22:1, 4a, 5b.
E.　The Sabbath rest is simply God's satisfaction in His heart's desire.
II.　In the Old Testament, the good land was a rest because the temple could be built there— Deut. 12:9; 1 Kings 8:1-11:
A.　God regards the good land, His proper people, and Christ as one:
1.　In the Bible the land, or earth, signifies the proper people of God; therefore, according to God's concept man is always related to the land.
2.　In the Bible the land is a type of the resurrected Christ; Christ is the good land—Gen. 1:9; Deut. 8:7-10.
3.　Man's body was made with the dust of the land, but man himself was made in the image of God for the purpose of expressing God and was committed with God's authority to exercise God's dominion and to form God's kingdom on earth—Gen. 2:7; 1:26.
4.　The combination of land, Christ, and humanity is the expression of God and the kingdom of God with God's glory and authority.
B.　The temple was the ultimate consummation of the entering into the good land by the children of Israel—1 Kings 8:1-11:
1.　With the temple God could have His expression and His representation for His kingdom, government, and administration.
2.　The good land is a combination of the earth and the people of God with God's dwelling place built up to express God and to exercise His authority.
3.　When God is expressed and represented, there is satisfaction for both God and man; this is true rest.
4.　The church life today is our good land, for here we have God's dwelling place, expression, authority, kingdom, and rest.
III.　The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9 is Christ as our rest, typified by the land of Canaan— Deut. 12:9; Heb. 4:8:
A.　Christ is rest to the saints in three stages:
1.　In the church age the heavenly Christ, the One who has expressed, represented, and satisfied God and who rests from His work and sits at the right hand of God in the heavens, is the rest to us in our spirit—Matt. 11:28-29.
2.　In the millennial kingdom, after Satan has been removed from the earth (Rev. 20:1-3), God will be expressed, represented, and satisfied by Christ and the overcoming saints; then Christ with the kingdom will be the rest in a fuller way to the overcoming saints, who will be co-kings with Him (vv. 4, 6) and share and enjoy His rest.
3.　In the new heaven and new earth, after all the enemies, including death, the last enemy, have been made subject to Him (1 Cor. 15:24-27), Christ, as the all-conquering One, will be the rest in the fullest way to all God's redeemed for eternity.
B.　The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9, typified by the rest of the good land, covers only the first two stages of Christ being our rest:
1.　The rest in the first two stages is a prize to the Lord's diligent seekers, who enjoy Him in a full way and become the overcomers.
2.　The rest in the third stage is not a prize but the full portion allotted to all the redeemed ones.
C.　The Sabbath rest mentioned in 4:9 refers to Christ as our rest in the first two stages, and especially in the second—the rest that remains for us to seek after and enter into diligently:
1.　In the second stage of His being our rest, Christ will take possession of the whole earth as His inheritance, making it His kingdom for a thousand years—Psa. 2:8; Heb. 2:5-6.
2.　In the second stage of Christ's being our rest, all His overcoming believers who seek Him and enjoy Him as their rest in the first stage will participate in His reign in the millennium—Rev. 20:4, 6; 2 Tim. 2:12:
a.　They will inherit the earth—Matt. 5:5; Psa. 37:11; Luke 19:17, 19.
b.　They will partake of the joy of their Lord—Matt. 25:21, 23.
c.　This kingdom rest, typified by the entering into the good land of Canaan, is the goal of the New Testament believers.
IV.　There will be a total of three Sabbaths—the church, the kingdom, and the New Jerusalem:
A.　The present church is the Sabbath of enjoyment, and the coming kingdom is the Sabbath of reward—Heb. 10:35; 11:26; Rev. 22:12:
1.　God is wise to use the coming Sabbath as a reward to encourage us to enjoy the present Sabbath.
2.　In His wisdom God has ordained the next age with the millennial kingdom to be an age of reward as an incentive for us to seek Him and respond to His work in this age
B.　The church life is a life in which man is gained by God on earth for His expression and representation—Heb. 2:10-12; 3:6-8; 4:9.
C.　The book of Hebrews was written for the purpose of encouraging the saved ones not to forsake the church life but to endeavor, strive, and be diligent to enter into it because it is today's Sabbath rest—Heb. 10:25; 13:1, 9, 13-15.
