D.　We should be encouraged to be in today's church life, today's Sabbath, for it will feed us, edify us, build us up, and qualify us for and usher us into the Sabbath in the kingdom age.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第九篇　安息日的安息
The Sabbath Rest
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来三5～四11。
壹　我们若要对希伯来书中安息日的安息有正确的领会，就必须认识圣经中第一次题到安息日之安息的意义─创二2～3：
I.　If we would have the proper understanding of the Sabbath rest in Hebrews, we need to know the significance of the first mention of the Sabbath rest in the Bible—Gen. 2:2-3:
一　神在第七日安息了，因为祂达到了祂所愿望的。
A.　God rested on the seventh day because He had attained what He desired.
二　神的心愿是要得着人，在地上彰显祂并代表祂─创一26～28，来二6～8上：
B.　The desire of God's heart is to have man on earth expressing Him and representing Him— Gen. 1:26-28; Heb. 2:6-8a:
１　整本圣经管制的原则，乃是在已过的永远里神制定了一个定旨，一个计划，要带着祂的权柄彰显祂自己。
1.　The governing principle of the Bible as a whole is that in eternity past God made a purpose, a plan, to express Himself with His authority.
２　神照着祂永远的计划而有的愿望，乃是要借着人使祂自己得着彰显和代表。
2.　God's desire according to His eternal plan is to have Himself expressed and represented by man.
３　当人在地上彰显并代表神的时候，神的愿望就满足了。
3.　When man is on earth expressing and representing God, God's desire is satisfied.
三　当地上有一种光景，就是人彰显神并代表神时，那种光景就是神的安息日之安息。
C.　When there is a situation on earth in which man expresses God and represents Him, that situation is a Sabbath rest to God.
四　新耶路撒冷是神终极并永远的安息日之安息，因为神在那里要完满的得着彰显和代表─启二一10～11，二二1，4上，5下。
D.　The New Jerusalem will be God's ultimate and eternal Sabbath rest because there God will be fully expressed and represented—Rev. 21:10-11; 22:1, 4a, 5b.
五　安息日的安息就是神在祂的心愿上得了满足。
E.　The Sabbath rest is simply God's satisfaction in His heart's desire.
贰　在旧约里，美地是安息，因为圣殿能建造在那里─申十二9，王上八1～11：
II.　In the Old Testament, the good land was a rest because the temple could be built there— Deut. 12:9; 1 Kings 8:1-11:
一　神将美地、祂正确的子民和基督，看作是一个：
A.　God regards the good land, His proper people, and Christ as one:
１　在圣经里，地表征神正确的子民；因此，按照神的观念，人总是与地有关联的。
1.　In the Bible the land, or earth, signifies the proper people of God; therefore, according to God's concept man is always related to the land.
２　在圣经里，地是复活基督的预表；基督乃是美地─创一9，申八7～10。
2.　In the Bible the land is a type of the resurrected Christ; Christ is the good land—Gen. 1:9; Deut. 8:7-10.
３　人的身体是用地上的尘土造的，但人自己是照着神的形像造的，目的是要彰显神，并且受托有神的权柄，可以施行神的管治权，并在地上形成神的国─创二7，一26。
3.　Man's body was made with the dust of the land, but man himself was made in the image of God for the purpose of expressing God and was committed with God's authority to exercise God's dominion and to form God's kingdom on earth—Gen. 2:7; 1:26.
４　地、基督和人合在一起，乃是神的彰显，也是神的国，有神的荣耀和权柄。
4.　The combination of land, Christ, and humanity is the expression of God and the kingdom of God with God's glory and authority.
二　圣殿是以色列人进入美地终极的完成─王上八1～11：
B.　The temple was the ultimate consummation of the entering into the good land by the children of Israel—1 Kings 8:1-11:
１　神有了圣殿，就能为着祂的国度、管理和行政，得着祂的彰显和代表。
1.　With the temple God could have His expression and His representation for His kingdom, government, and administration.
２　美地是地和神子民以及建造起来的神居所的组合，以彰显神并运用祂的权柄。
2.　The good land is a combination of the earth and the people of God with God's dwelling place built up to express God and to exercise His authority.
３　当神得着彰显和代表，神与人就得着满足；这乃是真安息。
3.　When God is expressed and represented, there is satisfaction for both God and man; this is true rest.
４　今天召会生活乃是我们的美地，因为在这里我们有神的居所、彰显、权柄、国度的安息。
4.　The church life today is our good land, for here we have God's dwelling place, expression, authority, kingdom, and rest.
叁　在希伯来四章九节里，安息日的安息乃是基督作我们的安息，由迦南地所预表─申十二9，来四8：
III.　The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9 is Christ as our rest, typified by the land of Canaan— Deut. 12:9; Heb. 4:8:
一　基督作众圣徒的安息，分为三个阶段：
A.　Christ is rest to the saints in three stages:
１　在召会时代，属天的基督，就是那彰显、代表、并满足了神的一位，也是那歇了一切的工，坐在诸天之上神右边的一位，祂现今在我们灵里是我们的安息─太十一28～29。
1.　In the church age the heavenly Christ, the One who has expressed, represented, and satisfied God and who rests from His work and sits at the right hand of God in the heavens, is the rest to us in our spirit—Matt. 11:28-29.
２　在千年国里，撒但从地上除去之后（启二十1～3），神要因基督和得胜的的圣徒，而得着彰显、代表并满足；然后基督连同国度，将是得胜圣徒更完满的安息，他们要与基督一同作王（4，6），有分于并享受祂的安息。─引用经文
2.　In the millennial kingdom, after Satan has been removed from the earth (Rev. 20:1-3), God will be expressed, represented, and satisfied by Christ and the overcoming saints; then Christ with the kingdom will be the rest in a fuller way to the overcoming saints, who will be co-kings with Him (vv. 4, 6) and share and enjoy His rest.
３　在新天新地里，所有的仇敌，包括最后的仇敌，死，都被基督征服之后（林前十五24～27），祂这位全胜者，将是神所有赎民最完满的安息，直到永远。─引用经文
3.　In the new heaven and new earth, after all the enemies, including death, the last enemy, have been made subject to Him (1 Cor. 15:24-27), Christ, as the all-conquering One, will be the rest in the fullest way to all God's redeemed for eternity.
二　希伯来四章九节里安息日的安息，就是美地的安息所预表的，只包括基督作我们安息的头两个阶段：
B.　The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9, typified by the rest of the good land, covers only the first two stages of Christ being our rest:
１　头两个阶段的安息乃是奖赏，要给那些竭力追求主，丰满的享受了祂，并且成为得胜者的人。
1.　The rest in the first two stages is a prize to the Lord's diligent seekers, who enjoy Him in a full way and become the overcomers.
２　第三阶段的安息，不是奖赏，乃是给所有赎民完满的分。
2.　The rest in the third stage is not a prize but the full portion allotted to all the redeemed ones.
三　四章九节所说安息日的安息，是指基督在头两个阶段，特别在第二阶段，作我们的安息，就是为我们存留，要我们竭力寻求并进入的：
C.　The Sabbath rest mentioned in 4:9 refers to Christ as our rest in the first two stages, and especially in the second—the rest that remains for us to seek after and enter into diligently:
１　在基督作我们安息的第二阶段，祂要得着全地为业，作祂的国度一千年─诗二8，来二5～6。
1.　In the second stage of His being our rest, Christ will take possession of the whole earth as His inheritance, making it His kingdom for a thousand years—Psa. 2:8; Heb. 2:5-6.
２　在基督作我们安息的第二阶段，所有得胜的信徒，就是在第一阶段寻求并享受祂作安息的人，要在千年国里与祂一同作王─启二十4，6，提后二12：
2.　In the second stage of Christ's being our rest, all His overcoming believers who seek Him and enjoy Him as their rest in the first stage will participate in His reign in the millennium—Rev. 20:4, 6; 2 Tim. 2:12:
a　他们要承受地土─太五5，诗三七11，路十九17，19。
a.　They will inherit the earth—Matt. 5:5; Psa. 37:11; Luke 19:17, 19.
b　他们要同享他们主人的快乐─太二五21，23。
b.　They will partake of the joy of their Lord—Matt. 25:21, 23.
c　这国度的安息，由进入迦南美地所预表，是新约信徒的目标。
c.　This kingdom rest, typified by the entering into the good land of Canaan, is the goal of the New Testament believers.
肆　一共有三个安息─召会、国度、和新耶路撒冷：
IV.　There will be a total of three Sabbaths—the church, the kingdom, and the New Jerusalem:
一　现今的召会，乃是享受的安息日，要来的国度是赏赐的安息日─来十35，十一26，启二二12：
A.　The present church is the Sabbath of enjoyment, and the coming kingdom is the Sabbath of reward—Heb. 10:35; 11:26; Rev. 22:12:
１　神是智慧的，用要来的安息日作为赏赐，鼓励我们享受现今的安息日。
1.　God is wise to use the coming Sabbath as a reward to encourage us to enjoy the present Sabbath.
２　神在祂的智慧里，命定来世和千年国是赏赐的时代，激励我们在今世寻求祂，并对祂的工作有回应。
2.　In His wisdom God has ordained the next age with the millennial kingdom to be an age of reward as an incentive for us to seek Him and respond to His work in this age
二　召会生活是人在地上被神得着，而作神彰显和代表的生活─来二10～12，三6～8，四9。
B.　The church life is a life in which man is gained by God on earth for His expression and representation—Heb. 2:10-12; 3:6-8; 4:9.
三　写希伯来书的目的，是要鼓励得救的人不可丢弃召会生活，反而努力、奋斗，并竭力进入其中，因为召会生活乃是今天的安息日之安息─来十25，十三1，9，13～15。
C.　The book of Hebrews was written for the purpose of encouraging the saved ones not to forsake the church life but to endeavor, strive, and be diligent to enter into it because it is today's Sabbath rest—Heb. 10:25; 13:1, 9, 13-15.
四　我们该受鼓励，留在今天的召会生活里，就是在今天的安息日里，因为召会生活喂养、造就、建造我们，使我们有资格、并引我们进入国度时代的安息日。
D.　We should be encouraged to be in today's church life, today's Sabbath, for it will feed us, edify us, build us up, and qualify us for and usher us into the Sabbath in the kingdom age.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE NINE
THE SABBATH REST
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The Sabbath rest is a weighty matter in the Scriptures. It is important that we first see the truth concerning the Sabbath rest in the way of vision and revelation. This truth should have a practical issue in our Christian life and in the church life. The way we handle this truth will determine whether or not we receive a reward from the Lord at His coining and whether or not we reign with Him in the coming kingdom of a thousand years.
THE SABBATH REST BEING GOD'S SATISFACTION IN HIS HEARTS DESIRE
In Hebrews 3:11— 4:11 the word rest is used eleven times, indicating that the word rest is crucial. God cares about something that He refers to as "My rest" (3:11; 4:3, 5), and the writer of Hebrews is one with Him to speak of His rest. The writer warns us of those who because of hardened hearts, unbelief, disobedience, obstinacy, and stubbornness died in the wilderness and did not enter into His rest. The writer also exhorts us to be diligent to enter into this rest. So the rest which matters to God must also be of concern to us. This rest is the Sabbath rest.
This Sabbath rest is simply God's satisfaction in His hearts desire. If we would know the Sabbath rest and enter into it, we must understand how the rest is related to the heart's desire of God, The phrase the heart's desire of God is not literally found in the Bible. However, in Ephesians Paul speaks of God's good pleasure and the good pleasure of His will (1:9, 5). God has a good pleasure. Here we need to see a principle. Every living being needs pleasure. The more living you are, the more pleasure you need. Since you are living and you need pleasure, happiness, joy, and delight, when that need is not met, you will not have rest because you do not have satisfaction. If your need for the proper pleasure is met, you will be satisfied and you will rest.
All human beings need enjoyment. Religion frustrates and kills this, and the world exploits it and uses it to corrupt mankind. But God created us in His image with a need for enjoyment, happiness, and pleasure. Since we have such a need and we were made in God's image, it is easy to see that God, as the most living One, has the greatest need for joy, happiness, and pleasure. So He inspired Paul to write of God's good pleasure. What is it that will satisfy God's need for happiness, delight, and pleasure? The fulfillment of God's need is the impulse and motive of all of His work in creation, in redemption, in His organic salvation, and in His building.
God works in order to fulfill His need, His hearts desire. Only when God's heart's desire has been fulfilled will He be satisfied. Only when God is satisfied will He rest. Having been created by God and appointed to be here on the earth, we were saved and gained at the proper time according to His ordination beforehand. What will it take to bring us into rest? What will it take to satisfy the need in our own being? We cannot rest until God rests. We cannot be satisfied until God is satisfied. We are here for God, for His economy, for His perfect will, for His good pleasure, for His heart's desire, and for His rest.
UNDERSTANDING THE SABBATH REST BY KNOWING THE SIGNIFICANCE OF THE FIRST MENTION OF THE SABBATH REST IN THE BIBLE
If we would have the proper understanding of the Sabbath rest in Hebrews, we need to know the significance of the first mention of the Sabbath rest in the Bible (Gen. 2:2-3). The principle of first mention concerning any matter in the Scriptures establishes the governing principle for that matter throughout the Scriptures. We cannot know or enter into the Sabbath rest in Hebrews if we do not have the proper knowledge and understanding of it. Yet we should not only know but also care enough that we would pray, saying, "'Father, may the desire of Your heart become the desire of my heart. Whatever is in Your heart, put into my heart. Whatever there is in me that is incompatible with Your heart's desire, terminate it." We must touch the motive that is in God. We must touch the fire burning in the being of God to have satisfaction and rest. Then we will live in this and live for this, because what motivates God will motivate us as God-men. As a result, we will be in the church life not merely in an outward way but in an intrinsic way with our whole being.
God Resting on the Seventh Day Because He Had Attained What He Desired— Man on Earth Expressing Him and Representing Him
God rested on the seventh day because He had attained what He desired. The desire of God's heart is to have man on earth expressing Him and representing Him (Gen. 1:26-28; Heb. 2:6-8a). The governing principle of the Bible as a whole is that in eternity past God made a purpose, a plan, to express Himself with His authority. God's desire according to His eternal plan is to have Himself expressed and represented by man. When man is on earth expressing and representing God, God's desire is satisfied.
Man Expressing God and Representing Him Being a Sabbath Rest to God
When there is a situation on earth in which man expresses God and represents Him, that situation is a Sabbath rest to God. Genesis 1:1 says, "In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth." That was God's original creation. Then there was an interval caused by God's judgment upon the rebellion of Lucifer and the beings that followed him. So beginning in Genesis 1:3 God began a work of restoration and further creation in a series of steps. God has a twofold situation: His original purpose that needs to be fulfilled, and a rebellion. His original purpose is to have a corporate expression of Himself in Christ. But a problem developed. Rebellion broke out in God's universe. A creature, a leading creature, touched God's government in the way of rebellion, becoming lawless, pernicious, evil, and perverse (Isa. 14:12-15; Ezek. 28:12b48a). So he must be dealt with. But God as God would not deal with a creature. He must have a creature in His image with His authority deal with this rebellious, one. Therefore, God began to restore, re-create, and further create.
We know from Genesis 2 that He created a special being from the dust of the ground on the sixth day (v. 7). This special being was man, Adam, created in His image and with His likeness (1:26-28), Before He did this there was a council among the Triune Godhead, and the decision was made to make man in God's image and to give man dominion. When He saw in His creation the existence of this man on the earth, God was happy (v. 31). He beheld His creation and re-creation with man as the center. He saw His image when He saw Adam, and at least in type, He saw a king. God could say, "I am happy because My desire is being fulfilled on the earth. I am expressed in this human being. Man is here in My image with My authority." As a result, God rested (2:2). This was a type, a picture, of rest.
God did not rest because He was tired. God did not rest simply because His work was done, and since there was nothing else to do, He took a break. No, God rested because He was satisfied. God was satisfied because man was there made from the dust of the ground with His image and with His authority. When God sees this, He rests because He is satisfied. May the Spirit anoint us and cause us to have an impression of how much this means to God. With man in God's image, ruling with His authority, God's original purpose can be fulfilled. God's delight and good pleasure are in His Son, and He wants to reproduce Him, enlarge Him, and increase Him on the earth to have a corporate expression of His Son.
God's delight in His Son is shown at the Sons baptism and transfiguration in Matthew 3 and 17, respectively. In Matthew 17 the voice out of the cloud said, "This is My Son, the Beloved, in whom I have found My delight. Hear Him!" (v. 5). We believe that the day is coming, at the beginning of the millennial kingdom, when the Father will say, a Behold, My beloved sons, the reproduction of the Firstborn.
I will now disperse them over the earth, and they will reign and subdue My enemy71 How much this means to God that we express Him! This is the primary need. But we must also pay attention to the secondary need of dealing with God's enemy. We know from our experience in our personal life, in the church life, and in our family life that if we do not deal with the enemy, there is no rest. We were created by God to deal with the serpent. God has a need for us to express Him and deal with His enemy. God impressed this revelation upon our Brother Watchman Nee. In the first chapter of The Glorious Church (The Collected Works of Watchman Nee, Vol. 34) entitled, a God's Plan and God's Rest," pages 1041, Brother Nee said the following:
The work of God requires that man exercise authority to have dominion over all things created by Him. God needs an authority in His creation, and He has chosen man to be that authority. If we are here just for ourselves as mere men, then all our seeking and longing would be to love the Lord more and to be more holy, more zealous, and save more souls. All of these pursuits are good indeed, but they are too man-centered. These things are concerned simply with the benefit of man; God's work and God's need are entirely neglected. We must see that God has His need. We are on this earth not merely for mans need but even more for God's need....When God created man, He spoke of what He needed. He revealed His need to have man rule and reign over all His creation and proclaim His triumph. Ruling for God is not a small thing; it is a great matter. God needs men whom He can trust and who will not fail Him. This is God's work, and this is what God desires to obtain....
If man has not restored the earth from the hand of Satan, he has not yet achieved God's purpose in creating him. Saving souls is often only for the welfare of man, but dealing with Satan is for the benefit of God. Saving souls solves man's need, but dealing with Satan satisfies God's need.
Such words have the capacity to change our life and to give us further resolve to be more one with God than we have been in the past. Under the Lord's blessing these words also have the capacity to energize us to pay the price to deal with God's enemy.
If we are to be men whom God will use to overthrow all of Satan's work and authority, we must obey the Lord completely and absolutely. The price is extremely great. In doing other work, it matters less if we preserve ourselves a little. But when dealing with Satan, we cannot leave one bit of ground for ourselves. We may hold on to something of ourselves in our study of the Scriptures, in preaching the gospel, or in helping the church or the brothers, but when we are dealing with Satan, self must be utterly abandoned. Satan will never be moved by us if our self is preserved. May God open our eyes to see that His purpose demands that we be wholly and absolutely for Him. A double-minded person can never deal with Satan. May God speak this word into our hearts.
Paying the Price to Deal with God's Enemy
How can God rest if there is no one on the earth willing to pay the price to deal with Satan? Thank the Lord that there was a man from China, our Brother Nee, who, after being enlightened, was willing to pay such a price and not spare himself at all unto the end. Yet he had joy and rest. Our Brother Lee was the same. We may be satisfied with the Lord's recovery. We may be practicing the God-ordained way, and people may be getting saved. We may know the Bible, we may be studying the truth, we may be growing in life, and we may be shepherding people. Many of the co-workers may be happy with the outward, apparent blessing on their work. I do not diminish that. But God still may have a question: 'Are you doing My work? Are you really a co-worker with Me? Can you dislodge Satan? Do you terrify him? Are you as "terrible as an army with banners5? Is My recovery a hobby or a place for you to do your own work?" You may be addicted to your work, but the Lord may ask, "Who will do My work even at the ultimate cost?"
Our High Priest is praying that we would pay the price. He is supplying us with God to such an extent that we who once were slaves of Satan will become an overcoming army to defeat him. God will then have rest. At the Lord's coming back, the Firstborn with His many brothers will defeat Antichrist and his army. Satan will then be bound for a thousand years. We will then enjoy the powers of the coming age and all the diseases will be healed. All the demons will also be cast out. The shining of the sun will be seven times brighter and the shining of the moon will be like the sun. We will be glorified God-men shining out the resplendent firstborn Son of God, and God will rest.
But many of God's people will miss that day because they abuse God's provisions in the age of grace and are not willing to pay the price. They save and coddle their souls. They want someone to hold their hand all the time, day by day. The characteristic of such a life is no cross, no throne, no warfare, and no combat. Such believers like to come to the Lord's Day meeting and maybe another meeting during the week, but otherwise have a nice living, drive luxury cars, and have secure financial futures. Today God needs radical believers who mean business with Him. We need houses, food, and transportation, but we are not on the earth for these things. In 1968 when I read this chapter from The Glorious Church, I made a decision and I prayed, "Lord, make me a person who can do Your work" Oh, that God would genuinely have a recovery on the earth! God needs a church filled with the image of God, a church with people learning to reign in life and learning to pray the prayers of spiritual warfare. To have such a church is a rest to God. This is a foretaste of the coming kingdom rest and also a foretaste of the eternal rest in the New Jerusalem. Sooner or later we all will be the New Jerusalem. But who will bring in the kingdom rest? Who will turn this age? Where are the radical followers of Joshua, the real "Calebs"？ Our enemies may be giants like the Nephilim, and they may have chariots of iron, but so what? We have Jesus as our High Priest. Let us go at once and possess the kingdom!
The New Jerusalem Being God's Ultimate and Eternal Sabbath Rest
The New Jerusalem will be God's ultimate and eternal Sabbath rest because there God will be fully expressed and represented (Rev. 21:10-11; 22:1, 4a, 5b). Satan, however, will be dealt with in the lake of fire. During the millennium the devil will be bound in the abyss (20:1-3), so there will be a significant rest. But he will come out and lead one last rebellion (vv. 7-9). Afterwards he will be cast into the lake of fire (v. 10). The enemy will be gone, the sea-the abode of the demons--will be no more (21:1), death and every negative thing will be cast into the lake of fire (20:14; 21:8), and all the millions upon millions of God-men will be perfected. In the New Jerusalem there will be the complete, consummate, ultimate, eternal, and corporate expression of the processed and consummated Triune God in the chosen, redeemed, regenerated, renewed, sanctified, transformed, glorified, and built-up tripartite man. This is God's rest, and we will rest with Him for eternity. There we will never be bored, for out of the being of our God will flow fresh and unprecedented delights of the exhaustless fountain that He is.
THE GOOD LAND IN THE OLD TESTAMENT BEING GOD'S REST
In the Old Testament, the good land was a rest because the temple could be built there (Deut. 12:9; 1 Kings 8:1-11). We need to understand the connection between God's rest and the good land. Why was the good land called the rest? The land had much to do with God's heart's desire. That land had to do with man in God's image and with His authority.
God Regarding the Good Land, His Proper People, and Christ as One
God regards the good land, His proper people, and Christ as one. In the eyes of God, man is always related to the earth. The whole thought of leaving the earth for heaven nullifies this. It is a ruse of the enemy to distract us from God's intention. We were made from the earth out of red clay. We belong to the earth. We were made out of dust but we were made in the image of God. The earth from which we were made is the land that came out of the death water on the third day (Gen, 1:9-10). That land emerging from the death water on the third day is a type of Christ in resurrection. So we have our source in the resurrected Christ.
On the one hand, we are related to the land, to the earth, to the dust, to the soil. Even we are the soil into which the Sower sows the seed of the kingdom (Matt. 13:3, 23). Yet we were made from the earth, which signifies Christ, and we were created in God's image. According to Colossians 1:15 and 2 Corinthians 4:4, the image of God is Christ. In God's eyes, we human beings are a splendid combination of land, humanity, and Christ. In the Bible the land, or earth, signifies the proper people of God; therefore, according to God's concept man is always related to the land. In the Bible the land is a type of the resurrected Christ; Christ is the good land (Gen. 1:9; Deut, 8:7-10), Man's body was made with the dust of the land, but man himself was made in the image of God for the purpose of expressing God and was committed with God's authority to exercise God's dominion and to form God's kingdom on earth (Gen. 2:7; 1:26). God's expression and God's kingdom are related to the earth. The earth is where the kingdom and God's expression must be with man. The combination of land, Christ, and humanity is the expression of God and the kingdom of God with God's glory and authority.
The good land was precious to God. On the one hand, it typifies Christ, and on the other hand, it is representative of the proper people of God. On that land God could have the proper people as His expression and as His kingdom. This is the reason that the land became the rest. In the good land, God achieved what He did not yet have over the entire earth. He had rest because He had His people in the land expressing Him and representing Him. The issue of their entering into the land was the temple, and the temple was filled with God's glory (2 Chron. 7:1). That brought Solomon to his knees, and the priests could not enter in because of the glory. With that glory was God's administration, God's kingdom. That was a moment of rest to God and to the proper people of God in the land.
The Temple Being the Ultimate Consummation of the Entering into the Good Land
The temple was the ultimate consummation of the entering into the good land by the children of Israel (1 Kings 8:141). With the temple God could have His expression and His representation for His kingdom, government, and administration. The good land is a combination of the earth and the people of God with God's dwelling place built up to express God and to exercise His authority. When God is expressed and represented, there is satisfaction for both God and man; this is true rest.
At the end of a proper Lord's table meeting we should have rest because God the Father has rest. If we neglect the Father or if we do not have a proper table there is no rest in God, and those who are sensitive to the feeling of the Spirit realize a kind of dissatisfaction.God's satisfaction is an intrinsic measure of the health of the church; He must be satisfied by seeing His expression and representation.
The Church Life Being Our Good Land
The church life today is our good land, for here we have God's dwelling place, expression, authority, kingdom, and rest. In Hebrews 3:7, the word therefore connects the house of God in verse 6 with the Sabbath rest in 4:9, indicating that these two are the same. The house of God is our church life today, our Sabbath rest. If we leave the church life, we leave today's Sabbath rest. If we do not remain in this rest, we leave the very thing that God intends to use to bring us into the kingdom rest. In the proper church life we should have two things: God's expression and God's authority.
THE SABBATH REST BEING CHRIST AS OUR REST, TYPIFIED BY THE LAND OF CANAAN
Christ Being Rest to the Saints in Three Stages
The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9 is Christ as our rest, typified by the land of Canaan (Deut. 12:9; Heb. 4:8). Christ is rest to the saints in three stages: in the church age, in the millennial kingdom, and in the new heaven and new earth. In the church age, the heavenly Christ, the One who has expressed, represented, and satisfied God and who rests from His work and sits at the right hand of God in the heavens, is the rest to us in our spirit (Matt. 11:28-29). We need to enter into this rest today. For this we need to know our spirit and discern our spirit from our soul (Heb. 4:12). In the millennial kingdom, after Satan has been removed from the earth (Rev. 20:1-3), God will be expressed, represented, and satisfied by Christ and the overcoming saints; then Christ with the kingdom will be the rest in a fuller way to the overcoming saints, who will be co-kings with Him (vv. 4, 6) and share and enjoy His rest.
First, we have a Sabbath in the church age in which we rest in the complete redemptive work of Christ and live in the church life as today's Sabbath rest, today's good land. Second, if we are faithful and diligent in this age, we will receive as a reward another stage of the Sabbath rest, not in the church but in the millennial kingdom. The kingdom of this world will become the kingdom of our Lord and of His Christ, and He will reign forever and ever. We will enter into the joy of the Lord, and the whole earth will have a Sabbath rest. We will rest with our Lord for a thousand years. If we want to be in that rest, we must know today's rest and be diligent to remain in it. Some who have left the church like to boast about how much they enjoy the Lord.
They may talk about enjoying the Lord, but what I sense in my spirit is a deep unrest in their being. Deep within they know that they have left today s Sabbath rest. We do not threaten anyone; but God Himself warns us in Hebrews 3:7- 4:13. To forsake God's Sabbath rest is to violate His governmental administration for His children. God ordains and God desires that we live the church life and enjoy the church life as the first stage of the Sabbath rest. Our enjoyment of the church life today will usher us into the second stage of the Sabbath rest, the kingdom rest. If we are not faithful in the first stage- either by not going on or by leaving it - and if we are only in the church outwardly, not intrinsically, we run the risk of not crossing the Jordan River and of dying in the wilderness of the soul. This is Hebrews.
Third, in the new heaven and new earth, after all the enemies, including death, the last enemy, have been made subject to Him (1. Cor. 15:24-27), Christ, as the all-conquering One, will be the rest in the fullest way to all God's redeemed for eternity. What will happen to those who are not in the kingdom rest? Because they are not matured, have not been perfected, and did not apply the proper heavenly ministry during the church age, they will receive a particular kind of perfecting during the thousand years of the kingdom. In this time they will be gained and trained to express Christ and not themselves. All the rebellion, all the lawlessness, all the disobedience, all the murmuring, and all the complaining will be dealt with, and they will be trained to be kings. After that thousand-year period, these believers, the vast majority of the saints, will join the overcomers as the perfected God-men to express our Lord and reign forever and ever. It is not a matter of whether or not we will enter this rest; it is a matter of when we shall enter.
Some may want to argue with us. They may say that this means our salvation is not by grace. However, we must realize that in addition to the gospel of grace, there is another food that we need to eat, and that is the word of righteousness. God is fair. If we are loose, if we will not pay the price now, and if we do not care for His heart's desire, He may let us go for our whole life in this age; but He will not let us go forever. We have been appointed by Him to be His image and His representation. There is no way to avoid our destiny. Jesus said to Saul, "Why are you persecuting Me? It is hard for you to kick against the goads." (Acts 26:14). Dear brothers and sisters, why do you kick against God's ordination for you? You may have no rest. You may be in a place finding pleasure for your soul, not caring for God's pleasure; therefore, at the end of your day, you have no rest in the bottom of your being. But in the church life, today's Sabbath rest, I can testify that I am at rest. The church life is the most restful place.
It matters to God that there are people on the earth who care for His heart. Jesus said, "You shall love the Lord your God from your whole heart and from your whole soul and from your whole mind and from your whole strength" (Mark 12:30). If you love God in this way, how can you not care for His heart? We need to love the Lord in such a way that we allow ourselves to be touched with the desire of His heart.
We need to allow Him to bring our inner being into alignment with His heart. Oh, that we would just hand ourselves' over to Him and say, "Lord, I am here on the earth to meet Your need and to care for the desire of Your heart. Thank You for enlightening me. Thank You for giving me the opportunity to be in the church as today's Sabbath rest. Lord, grant me the mercy and grace to remain here until You come." God will have a Sabbath rest. He must have it in the church adequately before He can have it in the kingdom.
The Sabbath Rest, Typified by the Rest of the Good Land, Covering the First Two Stages of Christ Being Our Rest
The Sabbath rest in Hebrews 4:9, typified by the rest of the good land, covers only the first two stages of Christ being our rest. The rest in the first two stages is a prize to the Lord's diligent seekers, who enjoy Him in a full way and become the overcomers. In the first stage, the prize is the enjoyment of Christ as our rest in the church life. But in the second stage, the prize is the reward of the kingdom. The rest in Hebrews 4:9 refers to the rest in the church life and the rest in the kingdom. The writer is not talking about the New Jerusalem here but about today's church life and the coming kingdom. Will you be diligent to enter into that rest? The rest in the third stage is not a prize but the full portion allotted to all the redeemed ones.
The Second Stage of the Sabbath Rest Being the Rest for Us to Seek After and to Enter into Diligently
The Sabbath rest mentioned in 4:9 refers to Christ as our rest in the first two stages, and especially in the second— the rest that remains for us to seek after and enter into diligently. This is because God needs the kingdom. The kingdom satisfies God's need to deal with His enemy.
In the second stage of His being our rest, Christ will take possession of the whole earth as His inheritance, making it His kingdom for a thousand years (Psa. 2:8; Heb. 2:5-6). In the second stage of Christ's being our rest, all His overcoming believers who seek Him and enjoy Him as their rest in the first stage will participate in His reign in the millennium (Rev. 20:4, 6; 2 Tim. 2:12). They will inherit the earth (Matt. 5:5; Psa. 37:11; Luke 19:17, 19). They will partake of the joy of their Lord (Matt. 25:21, 23). This kingdom rest, typified by the entering into the good land of Canaan, is the goal of the New Testament believers.
THE CHURCH, THE KINGDOM, AND THE NEW JERUSALEM BEING THE THREE SABBATHS
The Present Church Life Being the Sabbath of Enjoyment, and the Coming Kingdom Being the Sabbath of Reward
There will be a total of three Sabbaths— the church life, the kingdom, and the New Jerusalem. The present church life is the Sabbath of enjoyment, and the coming kingdom is the Sabbath of reward (Heb. 10:35; 11:26; Rev. 22:12). God is wise to use the coming Sabbath as a reward to encourage us to enjoy the present Sabbath.
God knows that many of us are naughty. He knows that many of us procrastinate. He knows that some of us are lazy. So He has a system of reward and punishment. It is as if He is saying, t£I would like for you to reign with Me for a thousand years and to be with Me on My wedding day. This will be a reward to you if you enjoy Me in the church life today. If you do not, then I will discipline you for a thousand years?" In order to get us to seek Him, He makes the kingdom Sabbath a reward. The Father has set the rules. In His wisdom God has ordained the next age with the millennial kingdom to be an age of reward as an incentive for us to seek Him and respond to His work in this age.
The Church Life Being a Life in Which Man Is Gained by God
The church life is a life in which man is gained by God on earth for His expression and representation (Heb. 2:1042; 3:6-8; 4:9). Will you let God gain you? Will you pray, even as you are reading this message, "Lord, gain me. Gain me for Your economy. Lord, gain me during my days on the earth. Gain my whole being"?
The Book of Hebrews Encouraging the Saved Ones to Enter into Today's Sabbath Rest——the Church Life
The book of Hebrews was written for the purpose of encouraging the saved ones not to forsake the church life but to endeavor, strive, and be diligent to enter into it because it is today's Sabbath rest (10:25; 13:1, 9, 13-15). There are different ways to forsake the church life. Some move from one locality to the other, sometimes seeking to be an elder. If you cannot be an elder, will you forsake the church life? How sad to be motivated by ambition! Some just withdraw. You may not rebel; you may not be negative; you may simply withdraw into yourself. But if you stay in yourself, it is risky. If you stay in yourself too long, it may be very difficult for you to come out. Do not forsake todays church life.
The book of Hebrews ends on a very practical note. Do not forsake the meetings (10:25). Be in the meetings, exhorting one another. Do not leave the home meetings, and do not leave the larger gatherings. The church is divine, mystical, and profound, but it is also practical. It is in the gatherings of the saints in the homes and in the halls. Do not forsake the meetings. You may be in a miserable condition. Sometimes you may feel that you cannot exercise your spirit very much. In such a condition, exercise your feet to get up and walk to your car. Exercise your hands to put them on the steering wheel. Drive yourself to the meeting hall and sit in the back. Many of us have had situations like this. Once we arrived at the meeting, after a period of time, we were able to touch the divine Spirit in our spirit. Do not forsake the assembling together, but rather endeavor, strive, and be diligent to enter into today s Sabbath rest, the church life. The church is the only place on earth where there is the corporate expression of Christ. The church is the only place on earth that is the kingdom of God today. If you forsake the church, you forsake "the corporate expression of God, the authority of God, the kingdom of God, and the dominion of God.
Today's Church Life, Today's Sabbath, Ushering Us into the Sabbath in the Kingdom Age
We should be encouraged to be in today's church life, today's Sabbath, for it will feed us, edify us, build us up, qualify us for, and usher us into the Sabbath in the kingdom age. In love let us usher one another into the coming Sabbath of reward.一R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十篇　魂与灵的分开
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来三12～13，四1～2，12～13。
壹　我们必须谨慎，免得有不信的恶心，将活神离弃了：
一　不信是极其邪恶的，因为侮辱了这位活的、信实的、全能的神，使我们不得进入基督作我们安息日之安息─来三10，19，诗一○三7。
二　虽然神是活的、信实的，但恶心向着神是刚硬的，并且它的不信使我们转离神─来三7～8，12。
三　没有什么比我们的信更尊重神，也没有什么比我们的不信更不尊重神、更侮辱神─民十三31～33，十四7～9，24，30。
四　我们要作有信心而得神喜悦的人，就必须运用我们相信的灵，并使我们的心一直转向主，望断以及于耶稣，就是我们信心的创始者与成终者─来十一6，十二2，林后四13。
贰　“神的话是活的，是有功效的，比一切两刃的剑更锋利，能以刺入、甚至剖开魂与灵，骨节与骨髓，连心中的思念和主意都能辨明”─来四12：
一　以色列人预表我们新约信徒（林前十6上，11）有分于神完全的救恩：─引用经文
１　第一阶段，我们接受基督，得救赎并蒙拯救脱离世界，正如以色列人被救出埃及一样。
２　第二阶段，我们在跟随主时，成了飘流的人，正如以色列人飘流在旷野一样；我们这种飘流，总是发生在我们的魂里。
３　第三阶段，　我们完满的有分于并享受基督，正如以色列人有分于并享受美地的丰富；这是我们在灵里所经历的。
４　希伯来的信徒在他们的心思里犹疑，不知如何对待他们的希伯来宗教，这种在心思里的犹疑，就是在魂里游荡，并不是在灵里经历基督。
二　希伯来书的作者劝勉希伯来的信徒，不要留在魂里游荡，而要竭力进入灵里，有分于并享受属天的基督：
１　那在天上坐在宝座上的基督（罗八34），现今也在我们里面（罗八10），就是在我们的灵里（提后四22），这灵就是神居所的所在（弗二22）。─引用经文
２　伯特利是神的家，神的居所，也是天的门；在那里基督是梯子，把地联于天，并把天带到地─创二八12～17，约一51。
３　我们的灵今天既是神居所的所在，这灵就是天的门，在这里基督是梯子，把我们在地上的人联于天，并把天带给我们。
４　因此，每当我们转到灵里，就能借着基督作天梯，进入天的门，摸着天上施恩的宝座─来四16。
５　犹豫的希伯来信徒，在他们的魂里游荡，而忽略他们的灵，但新遗命完全是在我们灵里的事，而不是在我们魂里的事─罗八16，提后四22，加六18。
三　希伯来信徒的魂及其犹疑的心思，怀疑神救恩的法则，考虑到自己的利益，必须被神活的、有功效的、能刺入的话破碎，好使他们的灵与魂分开：
１　骨髓如何深藏在骨节里，灵也照样深藏在魂里─诗五一6，彼前三4。
２　要使骨髓与骨节剖开，主要的是骨节必须破碎；照样，要使灵与魂分开，魂也必须破碎。
四　每当我们读经时，圣经必须是活的、有动力的、锋利到能把我们的魂与灵分开，并且能辨明我们的思念和主意，启示什么是出于己并为着己的，什么是出于神并为着神的；
１　我们必须借着在灵里各样的祷告，用信心调和主的话，使这话成为活的、有功效的─来四2，弗六17～18。
２　神活的话必须刺入我们里面，把我们从犹疑的心思和游荡的魂，拯救到那在我们灵里作安息日之安息的基督里面。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Ten
The Dividing of Soul and Spirit
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 3:12-13; 4:1-2, 12-13
I.　We need to beware of having an evil heart of unbelief in falling away from the living God:
A.　Unbelief is so evil because it insults the living, faithful, and almighty God, preventing us from entering into Christ as our Sabbath rest—Heb. 3:10, 19; Psa. 103:7.
B.　Although God is living and faithful, the evil heart is hardened against Him, and its unbelief causes us to turn away from Him—Heb. 3:7-8, 12.
C.　Nothing honors God more than our belief, and nothing dishonors and insults Him more than our unbelief—Num. 13:31-33; 14:7-9, 24, 30.
D.　In order to be people of faith who are well pleasing to God, we need to exercise our spirit of faith and keep our hearts turned to the Lord, looking away to Jesus, the Author and Perfecter of our faith—Heb. 11:6; 12:2; 2 Cor. 4:13.
II.　"The word of God is living and operative and sharper than any two-edged sword, and piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit and of joints and marrow, and able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart"—Heb. 4:12:
A.　The children of Israel are a type of us, the New Testament believers (1 Cor. 10:6a, 11), in our participation in the full salvation of God:
1.　In the first stage we receive Christ and are redeemed and delivered from the world just as the children of Israel were delivered from Egypt.
2.　In the second stage we become wanderers in following the Lord, just as the children of Israel wandered in the wilderness; our wandering always takes place in our soul.
3.　In the third stage we partake of and enjoy Christ in a full way, just as the children of Israel partook of and enjoyed the riches of the good land; this is experienced in our spirit.
4.　The Hebrew believers were wondering in their mind what to do with their Hebrew religion, and this wondering in their mind was a wandering in their soul, not an experience of Christ in their spirit.
B.　The writer of the book of Hebrews advised the Hebrew believers not to stagger in the wandering of their soul but to press on into their spirit to partake of and to enjoy the heavenly Christ:
1.　The very Christ who is sitting on the throne in heaven (Rom. 8:34) is also now in us (Rom. 8:10), that is, in our spirit (2 Tim. 4:22), where the habitation of God is (Eph. 2:22).
2.　At Bethel, the house of God, the habitation of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth—Gen. 28:12-17; John 1:51.
3.　Since today our spirit is the place of God's habitation, it is now the gate of heaven, where Christ is the ladder that joins us, the people on earth, to heaven, and brings heaven to us.
4.　Hence, whenever we turn to our spirit, we enter through the gate of heaven and touch the throne of grace in heaven through Christ as the heavenly ladder—Heb. 4:16.
5.　The staggering Hebrew believers were wandering in their soul and had neglected their spirit, but the new testament is absolutely a matter in our spirit, not in our soul—Rom. 8:16; 2 Tim. 4:22; Gal. 6:18.
C.　The Hebrew believers' soul, with its wondering mind, its doubting concerning God's way of salvation, and its considering of its own interests, had to be broken by the living, operative, and piercing word of God that their spirit might be divided from their soul:
1.　As the marrow is concealed deep in the joints, so the spirit is deep in the soul—Psa. 51:6; 1 Pet. 3:4.
2.　Just as the dividing of the marrow from the joints requires mainly the breaking of the joints, the dividing of the spirit from the soul requires the breaking of the soul.
D.　Whenever we read the Bible, it must be living, energizing, and sharp enough to divide our soul from our spirit and discern our thoughts and intentions, revealing which are of and for the self and which are of and for God:
1.　We must mix the word with faith by means of all prayer in spirit in order for it to be living and operative—Heb. 4:2; Eph. 6:17-18.
2.　The living word of God must pierce into our being and deliver us from our wondering mind and wandering soul into Christ as the Sabbath rest in our spirit.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十篇　魂与灵的分开
The Dividing of Soul and Spirit
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来三12～13，四1～2，12～13。
壹　我们必须谨慎，免得有不信的恶心，将活神离弃了：
I.　We need to beware of having an evil heart of unbelief in falling away from the living God:
一　不信是极其邪恶的，因为侮辱了这位活的、信实的、全能的神，使我们不得进入基督作我们安息日之安息─来三10，19，诗一○三7。
A.　Unbelief is so evil because it insults the living, faithful, and almighty God, preventing us from entering into Christ as our Sabbath rest—Heb. 3:10, 19; Psa. 103:7.
二　虽然神是活的、信实的，但恶心向着神是刚硬的，并且它的不信使我们转离神─来三7～8，12。
B.　Although God is living and faithful, the evil heart is hardened against Him, and its unbelief causes us to turn away from Him—Heb. 3:7-8, 12.
三　没有什么比我们的信更尊重神，也没有什么比我们的不信更不尊重神、更侮辱神─民十三31～33，十四7～9，24，30。
C.　Nothing honors God more than our belief, and nothing dishonors and insults Him more than our unbelief—Num. 13:31-33; 14:7-9, 24, 30.
四　我们要作有信心而得神喜悦的人，就必须运用我们相信的灵，并使我们的心一直转向主，望断以及于耶稣，就是我们信心的创始者与成终者─来十一6，十二2，林后四13。
D.　In order to be people of faith who are well pleasing to God, we need to exercise our spirit of faith and keep our hearts turned to the Lord, looking away to Jesus, the Author and Perfecter of our faith—Heb. 11:6; 12:2; 2 Cor. 4:13.
贰　“神的话是活的，是有功效的，比一切两刃的剑更锋利，能以刺入、甚至剖开魂与灵，骨节与骨髓，连心中的思念和主意都能辨明”─来四12：
II.　"The word of God is living and operative and sharper than any two-edged sword, and piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit and of joints and marrow, and able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart"—Heb. 4:12:
一　以色列人预表我们新约信徒（林前十6上，11）有分于神完全的救恩：─引用经文
A.　The children of Israel are a type of us, the New Testament believers (1 Cor. 10:6a, 11), in our participation in the full salvation of God:
１　第一阶段，我们接受基督，得救赎并蒙拯救脱离世界，正如以色列人被救出埃及一样。
1.　In the first stage we receive Christ and are redeemed and delivered from the world just as the children of Israel were delivered from Egypt.
２　第二阶段，我们在跟随主时，成了飘流的人，正如以色列人飘流在旷野一样；我们这种飘流，总是发生在我们的魂里。
2.　In the second stage we become wanderers in following the Lord, just as the children of Israel wandered in the wilderness; our wandering always takes place in our soul.
３　第三阶段，　我们完满的有分于并享受基督，正如以色列人有分于并享受美地的丰富；这是我们在灵里所经历的。
3.　In the third stage we partake of and enjoy Christ in a full way, just as the children of Israel partook of and enjoyed the riches of the good land; this is experienced in our spirit.
４　希伯来的信徒在他们的心思里犹疑，不知如何对待他们的希伯来宗教，这种在心思里的犹疑，就是在魂里游荡，并不是在灵里经历基督。
4.　The Hebrew believers were wondering in their mind what to do with their Hebrew religion, and this wondering in their mind was a wandering in their soul, not an experience of Christ in their spirit.
二　希伯来书的作者劝勉希伯来的信徒，不要留在魂里游荡，而要竭力进入灵里，有分于并享受属天的基督：
B.　The writer of the book of Hebrews advised the Hebrew believers not to stagger in the wandering of their soul but to press on into their spirit to partake of and to enjoy the heavenly Christ:
１　那在天上坐在宝座上的基督（罗八34），现今也在我们里面（罗八10），就是在我们的灵里（提后四22），这灵就是神居所的所在（弗二22）。─引用经文
1.　The very Christ who is sitting on the throne in heaven (Rom. 8:34) is also now in us (Rom. 8:10), that is, in our spirit (2 Tim. 4:22), where the habitation of God is (Eph. 2:22).
２　伯特利是神的家，神的居所，也是天的门；在那里基督是梯子，把地联于天，并把天带到地─创二八12～17，约一51。
2.　At Bethel, the house of God, the habitation of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth—Gen. 28:12-17; John 1:51.
３　我们的灵今天既是神居所的所在，这灵就是天的门，在这里基督是梯子，把我们在地上的人联于天，并把天带给我们。
3.　Since today our spirit is the place of God's habitation, it is now the gate of heaven, where Christ is the ladder that joins us, the people on earth, to heaven, and brings heaven to us.
４　因此，每当我们转到灵里，就能借着基督作天梯，进入天的门，摸着天上施恩的宝座─来四16。
4.　Hence, whenever we turn to our spirit, we enter through the gate of heaven and touch the throne of grace in heaven through Christ as the heavenly ladder—Heb. 4:16.
５　犹豫的希伯来信徒，在他们的魂里游荡，而忽略他们的灵，但新遗命完全是在我们灵里的事，而不是在我们魂里的事─罗八16，提后四22，加六18。
5.　The staggering Hebrew believers were wandering in their soul and had neglected their spirit, but the new testament is absolutely a matter in our spirit, not in our soul—Rom. 8:16; 2 Tim. 4:22; Gal. 6:18.
三　希伯来信徒的魂及其犹疑的心思，怀疑神救恩的法则，考虑到自己的利益，必须被神活的、有功效的、能刺入的话破碎，好使他们的灵与魂分开：
C.　The Hebrew believers' soul, with its wondering mind, its doubting concerning God's way of salvation, and its considering of its own interests, had to be broken by the living, operative, and piercing word of God that their spirit might be divided from their soul:
１　骨髓如何深藏在骨节里，灵也照样深藏在魂里─诗五一6，彼前三4。
1.　As the marrow is concealed deep in the joints, so the spirit is deep in the soul—Psa. 51:6; 1 Pet. 3:4.
２　要使骨髓与骨节剖开，主要的是骨节必须破碎；照样，要使灵与魂分开，魂也必须破碎。
2.　Just as the dividing of the marrow from the joints requires mainly the breaking of the joints, the dividing of the spirit from the soul requires the breaking of the soul.
四　每当我们读经时，圣经必须是活的、有动力的、锋利到能把我们的魂与灵分开，并且能辨明我们的思念和主意，启示什么是出于己并为着己的，什么是出于神并为着神的；
D.　Whenever we read the Bible, it must be living, energizing, and sharp enough to divide our soul from our spirit and discern our thoughts and intentions, revealing which are of and for the self and which are of and for God:
１　我们必须借着在灵里各样的祷告，用信心调和主的话，使这话成为活的、有功效的─来四2，弗六17～18。
1.　We must mix the word with faith by means of all prayer in spirit in order for it to be living and operative—Heb. 4:2; Eph. 6:17-18.
２　神活的话必须刺入我们里面，把我们从犹疑的心思和游荡的魂，拯救到那在我们灵里作安息日之安息的基督里面。
2.　The living word of God must pierce into our being and deliver us from our wondering mind and wandering soul into Christ as the Sabbath rest in our spirit.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TEN
THE DIVIDING OF SOUL AND SPIRIT
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Hebrews 4:12 says, "For the word of God is living and operative and sharper than any two-edged sword, and piercing even to the dividing of soul and spirit and of joints and marrow, and able to discern the thoughts and intentions of the heart." The dividing of soul and spirit is a critical matter in order for a New Testament believer to truly experience the reality of the Sabbath rest. This verse is placed near the end of the section of warning in 3:7~~~4:13 for a particular reason. The book of Hebrews was written to those who had a long history as the people of God in the flesh. The writer uses their history and background as a setting to present this Epistle. He also makes several comparisons to help the Hebrew believers fulfill God's purpose in His New Testament economy. The Hebrews, the children of Israel, were the people of God in the Old Testament age, but they were also a type of the people of God in the New Testament age. Their history prefigures the church today. To know the church today, we have to understand the type in the Old Testament. We need to see the history of the children of Israel in order to know what we should experience today.
THE THREE STAGES OF THE HISTORY OF THE CHILDREN OF ISRAEL
The history of the children of Israel can be divided into three stages based on three places: Egypt, the wilderness, and the good land of Canaan. In the first stage of their history, they were in Egypt under the tyranny of Pharaoh for over four hundred years. God came in to fulfill His promise to the forefathers and raised up Moses to lead the children of Israel out of slavery. Egypt is a type of the Satan-infested and Satan-usurped world. We also, as today s people of God, fell into the world due to the Satan-induced fall. God delivered the children of Israel from the judgment of God in Egypt by redeeming them through the Passover, including the Passover lamb (Exo. 12:3-11). In order to be delivered from Egypt, they not only had to be redeemed and passed over from judgment, but they also had to cross a river. God's initial salvation includes deliverance as well as redemption. His salvation not only delivers us from God's judgment; it also causes us to leave the world entirely.
Moses led the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt through the Red Sea into the wilderness, where they spent forty years. According to geography, a direct trip across the wilderness would not have taken forty years, even by foot. The forty years in the wilderness were not due to slow transportation; that was a time of wandering. In the second stage of their history, the children of Israel spent forty years wandering in the wilderness. At the end of their wandering they were brought to the edge of the good land. By that time, nearly every one of the generation who left Egypt had died, and their carcasses had fallen in the wilderness. The new generation raised up by God crossed the river Jordan to enter into the land God had promised to them, a land flowing with milk and honey, the good land of Canaan.
The wilderness signifies the wandering Christian life or wandering church life. The land of Canaan is a full type of the all-inclusive Christ. Some have understood that the good land is heaven, which is reached after passing through death, signified by the cold waves of the river Jordan. This is a wrong concept. The good land is the all-inclusive Christ in which God's people enjoy and partake of Him to the fullest degree. In Christ God's people become His expression and the place where God's kingdom and dominion are established. Eventually, the temple and the city are built in the good land. God's testimony is with those who labor on and enjoy this good land. This is the third stage of the children of Israel's history.
This history of the children of Israel is a picture that applies to the saints today. Today we participate in God's full salvation in three stages. In the initial stage, we receive Christ and experience His redemption and deliverance from the world, typified by Egypt. We then cross the first sea, the water of baptism. It is not sufficient to merely be redeemed to go to heaven or to be saved from hell; we need to be practically saved from the world through baptism. In the second stage of God's full salvation, we may experience some wandering in our soul, until we enter the third stage. In this stage, we enter all the way into the enjoyment of the all-inclusive Christ. The all-inclusive Christ is both in the heavens and in our spirit (Rom. 8:10, 34).
THE THREE PARTS OF MAN
The three stages of the history of the children of Israel also correspond to the three parts of man—spirit, soul, and body (1 Thes. 5:23). Egypt corresponds to the body, the wilderness corresponds to the soul, and the good land of Canaan corresponds to our spirit. In our experience, Christ first came into our spirit (Rom. 8:16; 2 Tim 4:22), He is now transforming our soul, especially our mind (Rom. 12:2), and ultimately He will glorify our body (Phil. 3:21). The New Testament experience of God's full salvation regeneration, transformation, and glorification— is from the inside out.
Our daily experiences are very much like the picture of the children of Israel as portrayed in the book of Hebrews. We daily need to come forward- out of the flesh and out of the soul- into our spirit (Heb. 4:16; 7:25; 10:22). The more we come forward from our flesh and soul to our spirit, the more the life of the Lord can spread outwardly from our spirit through our soul to our body. Our experience is one of coming forward and pressing on to enter into the Sabbath rest. Our daily experience of God's salvation is a matter of leaving our flesh and soul and entering into our spirit. We especially need to leave our soul because we are prone to wander in the soul.
The Hebrew believers were wandering in their soul. The many aspects of Judaism tempted them and caused them to doubt. They were reasoning, considering, wondering, and wandering in their soul. The writer therefore charged them to come forward out of their staggering and hesitating soul into their spirit. To be constantly wandering is a curse. We need to get out of the wilderness of our soul and enter into the good land, the all-inclusive Christ. He is in our spirit and joined to our spirit as one (1 Cor. 6:17). The heavenly Christ is in our spirit today.
We need to get out of the wandering soul and get into the enjoyment of Christ in our spirit. Christ, the church, and the Sabbath rest are in our spirit.
THE THREE SECTIONS OF THE TABERNACLE
The three parts of man also correspond to the picture of the tabernacle, which had an outer court, a holy place, and the Holy of Holies. We may be in the outer court, but in the Christian journey we must press forward into the Holy of Holies. We praise the Lord for the outer court because there we have an altar for our redemption and a laver, a sea, for us to cross. The book of Hebrews has a heavenly calling to come forward with boldness to the throne of God and of grace (4:16). The throne of grace is with God in the Holy of Holies. Our journey is from the outer court through the holy place to the Holy of Holies. This is the way to experience God's salvation.
Most of our problems today are not with the flesh but with our wandering, considering, worrying, doubting, and debating soul. If we want to enjoy the Sabbath rest we must come forward into our spirit. Our spirit and our soul are quite close, and it is possible to confuse them as the Hebrew believers did. They could not clearly discern between Judaism and the things of Christ. They needed a clear division between the soul and the spirit. They needed to be dear that the soul was the place of wandering, unbelief, and falling away, but the spirit was the place of rest, faith, and the living God.
NOT HAVING AN EVIL HEART OF UNBELIEF IN FALLING AWAY FROM THE LIVING GOD
We need to beware of having an evil heart of unbelief in falling away from the living God (3:12-13). This point is related to the dividing of the soul from the spirit. In this passage, which speaks of our entrance into the Sabbath rest, there is a repeated warning, quoted from Psalm 95:7: "Today if you hear His voice, do not harden your hearts as in the provocation, in the day of trial in the wilderness, where your fathers tried Me by testing Me and saw My works for forty years" (Heb. 3:7-9). "Beware, brothers, lest perhaps there be in any one of you an evil heart of unbelief in falling away from the living God" (v. 12). The heart includes our soul, composed of the mind (thoughts), emotion, and will (intents) plus the conscience in our spirit (4:12; Acts 11:23; John 16:6, 22; Heb. 10:22). The condition of the heart has much to do with our experience of Christ and His full salvation.
The Hebrew believers were warned to beware of having an evil heart of unbelief. An evil heart is one that goes astray (Heb. 3:10), one that errs, one that is deceived, and one that turns away from God. Dear brothers and sisters, we must beware in our Christian life and church life of a heart that is turned away from God. We treasure 2 Corinthians 3:16 which says, ''Whenever their heart turns to the Lord, the veil is taken away," Turn your heart to the Lord. Do not have a heart that turns away from the living God. The falling away by the children of Israel was a turning away, deserting, and departing from the living God. We need to always turn our heart back to the Lord. Regardless of our position or condition, we should turn our heart to the Lord. We should not have an evil heart that goes astray.
An evil heart is also a hardened heart (Heb. 3:8), a heart that is stubborn, obstinate, stony, and not persuadable. This is another dangerous sign of an evil heart. We need our heart softened, pliable, turning to the Lord, and never hardened. The children of Israel saw God's signs, His glory, and His miracles for forty years. They ate "miracle food/5 the manna from heaven, that never failed to come to them every day for forty years. Nevertheless, though they witnessed such a daily miracle, they were still capable of building a golden calf (Exo. 32:2-4). They drank water from the cleft rock, yet they still rebelled against God. They had a hard heart. We may also be the same as they were. We witness and experience the Lord's saving grace time and again, yet we may still harden our heart. This is a terrible thing that displeases, insults, and provokes the Lord. When I first came to the church, I was helped by Brother Lee and other brothers to pray, "Lord, strengthen my spirit and soften my heart." One part of our being needs tremendous strengthening, and another part needs tremendous softening. Our spirit must be strong but our heart must be soft. We may have a most difficult, even terrible, circumstance that we may not understand, but we should keep our heart soft towards the Lord. May this word be with us, especially the young people, for the rest of our lives. Be an overcomer to fulfill God's purpose in the church life by having a strong spirit and a soft heart.
An evil heart is also a heart of unbelief (Heb. 3:12), It is terrible to have a heart that does not believe or have faith. God intended to immediately bring the children of Israel out of Egypt into the good land as He had promised. The children of Israel were near the good land when God spoke to Moses to send twelve spies to go into the land to search it and bring back its produce (Num. 13:1-20). Twelve men saw the same good land; they brought back grapes so heavy that two people had to carry them, and they reported that the land was indeed flowing with milk and honey (vv. 23, 27). However, they came back in two groups. One group of ten reported that the land was good but that it was full of giants who devoured everything up in sight and made them appear in their own sight as grasshoppers. They were sure that the children of Israel would be eaten up because the inhabitants of the land were stronger than they were (vv. 31-33). This report stirred up the people and caused the children of Israel to question God's intent in bringing them out of Egypt (14:1-3). The people were basically saying that God had lied to them. Praise the Lord there was another group of two Joshua and Caleb—who were on a different side. They stilled the people and said, "Do not fear the people of the land, for they will be our bread" (v. 9). The ten said that the people were the giants, bread, but the two said the giants were the children of Israels bread. Who is the bread and who is the one eating? They all saw the same thing, but some were going to be eaten and others were going to eat. To which group do you belong? It depends on whether your heart is one of faith and belief or one of unbelief. This is an important principle for our experience of God's salvation. If we look at the environment and situation, we may say that we are going to be swallowed up. However, to the overcomers, to Christ and His partners, the situation, difficulty, obstacle, enemy, devil, evil spirit, or demon is their food. The overcomers say, "We are going to eat them up because God's word is faithful and God is with us. 'If God is for us, who can be against us?' (Rom. 8:31). Let us therefore go up at once and take the land!"
What kind of heart do you have? To have the heart of the ten is to have an evil heart of unbelief. To have the heart of the two is to have a heart and spirit of faith. Going on in the Lord's recovery is not a "bed of roses." There is fighting involved. The seed of glory came into us, and from that point we have experienced the fighting. Every seed is fighting as it grows. It must fight through the shell, through the soil, and sometimes even through concrete to grow. To enter into the good land and to experience God's full salvation requires fighting. For this fight we need faith based upon God's word.
Unbelief Being So Evil Because It Insults the Living, Faithful, and Almighty God, Preventing Us from Entering into Christ as Our Sabbath Rest
Unbelief is so evil because it insults the living, faithful, and almighty God, preventing us from entering into Christ as our Sabbath rest (Heb. 3:10, 19; Psa. 103:7). Our not entering into the Sabbath rest and not experiencing Christ today is not because something is wrong with the land but because we have an evil heart of unbelief. Unbelief insulted the living God who then prevented the children of Israel from entering into the good land. The Lord said that they tempted, provoked, proved, and tried Him so that He swore that they would not enter into the land. Were it not for the petition and supplication of Moses, God would have wiped out the entire nation (Num. 14:11-20). Nevertheless, God did not allow any of the first generation to go in, except for Joshua and Caleb.
God Being Living and Faithful, but the Evil Heart Being Hardened against Him, and Its Unbelief Causing Us to Turn Away from Him
Although God is living and faithful, the evil heart is hardened against Him, and its unbelief causes us to turn away from Him (Heb. 3:7-8, 12), Verse 8 speaks of a hardened heart, and verse 12 speaks of an evil heart of unbelief in falling away from the living God.
Honoring God by Our Belief and Dishonoring Him by Our Unbelief
Nothing honors God more than our belief, and nothing dishonors and insults Him more than our unbelief (Num. 13:31-33; 14:7-9, 24, 30). Nothing honors God like faith. When we have faith we believe in God and in God's heart, word, being, ways, and acts. The more we believe Him, the more we honor Him. We do not need to do that much. We just need to believe, and God will do the rest.
Exercising Our Spirit of Faith and Keeping our Hearts Turned to the Lord, Looking Away to Jesus, the Author and Perfecter of Our Faith
In order to be the people of faith who are well pleasing to God, we need to exercise our spirit of faith and keep our hearts turned to the Lord, looking away to Jesus, the Author and Perfecter of our faith (Heb. 11:6; 12:2; 2 Cor. 4:13). To have a heart of belief, we need to exercise a spirit of faith. Faith is in our spirit. When we exercise our spirit of faith, our heart will believe. We do not have faith from ourselves; our faith is from the Lord who is the Author and the Perfecter of it. We need to look away to Him, gaze upon Him, and let Him infuse Himself as faith into our being.
Hebrews 3:18 speaks of "the disobedient." Not believing and not obeying go together. People are disobedient because of unbelief. When you believe, your mouth will say, 'Amen, amen." We have to mix the word with faith (4:2). We take the word and believe in the word by saying amen to it. When we say, "Lord, Amen, to Your word!" we become obedient persons. We say "No!" to the devil and 'Amen!" to the Father. We say "No" to the devil's evil whisperings but "Yes" to God's word. We mix the word with faith and we become an obedient generation.
THE WORD OF GOD BEING LIVING AND OPERATIVE AND SHARPER THAN ANY TWO-EDGED SWORD
The Children of Israel Being a Type of the New Testament Believers The children of Israel are a type of us, the New Testament believers (1 Cor. 10:6a, 11), in our participation in the full salvation of God. In the first stage we receive Christ and are redeemed and delivered from the world just as the children of Israel were delivered from Egypt. In the second stage we become wanderers in following the Lord, just as the children of Israel wandered in the wilderness; our wandering always takes place in our soul. In the third stage we partake of and enjoy Christ in a full way, just as the children of Israel partook of and enjoyed the riches of the good land; this is experienced in our spirit. The Hebrew believers were wondering in their mind what to do with their Hebrew religion, and this wondering in their mind was a wandering in their soul, not an experience of Christ in their spirit.
Day by day, even minute by minute, we are faced with the decision either to press on into our spirit or to stay and wander in our soul. Will we remain wondering in our mind, being stubborn in our will, and fluctuating in our emotions, or will we leave the wilderness and turn to our spirit? In our spirit we enter into the good land, into the Sabbath rest, to enjoy the riches of Christ and be in the real church life.
The Hebrew Believers Not Staggering but Pressing On into Their Spirit to Partake Of and to Enjoy the Heavenly Christ
The writer of the book of Hebrews advised the Hebrew believers not to stagger in the wandering of their soul but to press on into their spirit to partake of and to enjoy the heavenly Christ. The very Christ who is sitting on the throne in heaven (Rom. 8:34) is also now in us (v. 10), that is, in our spirit, where the habitation of God is (Eph. 2:22). The heavenly Christ, the wonderful Son of God who is superior to everything in Judaism, is today both sitting in the heavens on the throne and also living in our spirit. This is His residence and habitation. Jesus Christ is in our spirit. "The Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit" (2 Tim. 4:22). You may not sense, see, or feel Him, but He is there in your spirit. You have to substantiate Him by faith. You have to exercise your spirit of faith and turn your heart to Him to enjoy His riches.
At Bethel, the house of God, the habitation of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth (Gen. 28:12-17; John 1:51), The habitation of God, Bethel, is simply the gate of heaven where there is a ladder, Christ, joining heaven to the earth. By this way the heavenly life is ministered to us, and we are brought into the heavenly realm across the river. Our spirit is the place where God and man are mingled together for the fulfillment of God's purpose. Since today our spirit is the place of God's habitation, it is now the gate of heaven, where Christ is the ladder that joins us, the people on earth, to heaven, and brings heaven to us. Hence, whenever we turn to our spirit, we enter through the gate of heaven and touch the throne of grace in heaven through Christ as the heavenly ladder (Heb. 4:16).
The staggering Hebrew believers were wandering in their soul and had neglected their spirit, but the new testament is absolutely a matter in our spirit, not in our soul (Rom. 8:16; 2 Tim. 4:22; Gal, 6:18). Our need is to get into our spirit. The New Testament apostles emphasized walking in the spirit, living according to the spirit, and staying in the spirit. Experientially and practically we need to turn to the spirit and come into the spirit, where Christ is. The solution to all our problems and the Sabbath rest are in our spirit. If we have a problem, are in a difficult situation, or are in need of wisdom or power, we must turn to our spirit. Outside of our spirit we are wandering and staggering. In our spirit we become partakers of all the riches of the new testament.
The Believers' Soul Being Broken by the Living, Operative, and Piercing Word of God to Divide the Spirit from the Soul
The Hebrew believers' soul, with its wondering mind, its doubting concerning God's way of salvation, and its considering of its own interests, had to be broken by the living, operative, and piercing word of God that their spirit might be divided from their soul. Hebrews 4:12 provides a picture of the joints and marrow in our bones. The marrow is hidden, covered, and enveloped by the joints. The marrow is a picture of our spirit, and the joints are a picture of our soul. There is a need to discern between the two. How can we get to the marrow, that is, our spirit? The only way is to break and pierce through the joints.
In our experience we need the breaking of the soul, the breaking of the outer man, to reach the spirit, the inner man, the hidden man of the heart (Eph. 3:16; 1 Pet. 3:4). Paul spoke this word to the Hebrew believers because they were wandering and staggering in their soul and were not getting into the good land, Christ, in their spirit. We need our soul and our spirit to be divided.
We must be able to discern our spirit from our soul; otherwise, we cannot be a truly spiritual person, a person in the spirit. Without discerning our spirit from our soul, we will be a kind of confused person, sometimes in the soul and sometimes even in the flesh. We need to be persons in the spirit and have a clear discernment of the spirit from the soul. We have to leave the soul and come into our spirit. We may be clear that we should be in the spirit. However, to discern the difference between the soul and the spirit, our soul and spirit need to be divided by the breaking of the joints, the soul. The breaking of the soul is equivalent to denying the soul. We break the soul by denying it. We find our spirit by denying our mind, emotion, and will. That which is left after denying our soul is the spirit. We may think that after denying all these things we will be a "zero." This is the case for an unbeliever. But we have Christ in our spirit. If we deny all these things, denying ourselves, that which is left is our spirit. For example, our emotion might be stirred up to watch television. The emotion might take the lead, and the mind might reason, saying, "This is not a bad idea. I need some education and an extracurricular activity." The will might then say, "Let's go!" So all three, the mind, the emotion, and the will follow each other to watch television. To be saved from this kind of wandering we must deny our emotion, deny our mind with its reasonings, and deny our will. If an unbeliever denies these three, he will have nothing left. But after we believers deny our souls, there is still something remaining. This remaining part is very peaceful and may say, "Go to the meeting. Go to enjoy Christ." When we follow this remaining part, we enter into the Sabbath rest and we find our spirit, the heavenly Christ, and the throne of God. We especially need to do this kind of denying before the prayer meeting.
Such denying is the crossing of the river. We cross the river by denying our soul, our self, our soulish desires, our considerations, our reasonings, and our feelings. At the same time that we deny them, we also must turn to our spirit where the Lord is. There we find Christ. As the marrow is concealed deep in the joints, so the spirit is deep in the soul (Psa. 51:6; 1 Pet. 3:4). Just as the dividing of the marrow from the joints requires mainly the breaking of the joints, the dividing of the spirit from the soul requires the breaking of the soul.
The Word Being Living, Energizing, and Sharp Enough to Divide Our Soul from Our Spirit and Discern Our Thoughts and Intentions
Whenever we read the Bible, it must be living, energizing, and sharp enough to divide our soul from our spirit and discern our thoughts and intentions, revealing which are of and for the self and which are of and for God. Praise the Lord, there is a ''dividing tool." It is a sharp two-edged sword. This is the living and energetic word of God. This is why we need to pray-read the Word. The word in Hebrews 4:12 is the constant word. If you merely read it in your mind, you will wander even more. Many who have read the Bible merely in their mind have turned it into letters and knowledge. We have to come to the Word and touch it as the living and energetic word of God by pray-reading it and mixing it with faith. We do this by using our spirit to contact the living word. I cannot imagine the Lord's recovery without pray-reading. We need to pray-read the Word in a strong way, mixing it with faith. We need to use our spirit to contact the living word of God. The word will then become energetic and operative in our being. It will begin to shine, pierce, and expose us. It might tell us, "This is something of the soul, of the mind, of the reasoning." It may also tell us, "This is your spirit, Christ, the anointing, the divine life." This is often our experience after having a good session of pray-reading in the morning. After pray-reading, we are clear and even exposed with the slightest misbehaving in our soul. But if we do not have the living word, we may be fully in the flesh and consider that we are okay.
We must mix the word with faith by means of all prayer in spirit in order for it to be living and operative (Heb. 4:2; Eph, 6:17-18). The only way to divide the soul from the spirit is by the living and operative word of God which has been mixed with our faith. The living word of God must pierce into our being and deliver us from our wondering mind and wandering soul into Christ as the Sabbath rest in our spirit. When we pray-read the Word of God, it pierces and divides the soul from the spirit and supplies us with the enjoyment of the Sabbath rest. If we will pray-read, day by day the word will separate, divide, and pierce us. By having much experience of receiving the Word in this way, our soul and our spirit will be discerned and divided. We will then be clear that we should not be in our wandering soul but in our restful spirit, where the heavenly Christ is today. This is the way we enter into the Sabbath rest today.一M. C.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十一篇　基督开端的话和公义的话
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来五11～六5。
壹　神的话主要不是为着知识，乃是为着滋养─来五12：
一　圣经虽是一本教训的书，但它的教训不仅是传授头脑的知识给我们，更是供应食物给我们─耶十五16，彼前二2～3。
二　主耶稣取用圣经上神的话作祂的食物，并靠此而活─太四4。
三　希伯来五章十二节中，“奶”和“干粮”这些辞，指明神的话是为着滋养人的：
１　在基督里的信徒借着重生成为婴孩，就能靠着灵奶，就是话奶的滋养，在生命里长大─彼前二2～3。
a　话奶是为着魂，就是里面的人。
b　奶是在神的话里传输出来，借着我们理性心思的的领会，且由我们心思机能的吸收，滋养我们里面的人。
２　奶主要的是为着婴孩，而干粮是为着成熟的人─林前三2，来五13。
贰　保罗在希伯来书中的负担，乃是要把信徒从基督徒生活的幼稚园阶段带出来，把他们带到成熟─带到公义的话，就是关于基督天上的职事，以及关于神经纶的法则的话，这话就是使他们达到成熟的干粮─来五12，14，六1：
一　希伯来信徒留在救恩的起初阶段，而不往前到基督天上的职事那里─来四14～16，五11～12。
二　希伯来信徒曾经蒙了光照，尝过属天的恩赐，得着并有分于圣灵，并尝过神美善的话，但他们仍停留在享受新约祝福的起初阶段─来六4～5。
三　希伯来信徒接受福音到某一个程度，但他们不知道，基督有一个在诸天之上的职事─来八1～2。
四　写希伯来书是要把信徒从关于基督在地上职事之美善的话，带到关于基督在天上职事之公义的话，而把幼稚的信徒带到成熟─来六1。
叁　基督开端的话，是基督徒生活的根基─来六1：
一　子讲说神，或神在子里说话，有两类的话─基督开端的话，和公义的话─来一1～2，五12～14，六1：
１　基督开端的话是奶，为着婴孩。
２　公义的话是干粮，为着成熟的人。
二　在六章五节里，神美善的话，是指六章一节所说基督基开端的话：─引用经文
１　神美善的话，是在幼稚园阶段的基督开端的话。
２　关于基督在地上职事的话，是美善的话，因为是喜信，佳音；然而，这是在幼稚园阶段的话。
三　基督开端的话，是指构成基督徒生活根基的六项：悔改脱开死行，信靠神，浸洗的教训，按手，死人的复活，和永远的审判─六1～2。
四　基督开端的话，不只包括信徒对基督经历的开端，也包括基督早期的职事。
肆　公义的话，具体的说出神在祂的经纶并行政上，对待祂的子民所有公正公义的思想─来五13：
一　义是照着神公义并严格的要求，在神前与人、事、物都是对的─太五20，林前十五34。
二　义是神国的事─太六33：
１　义是从神为着祂的行政而来的─诗八九14，九七2，赛三二1。
２　义与神的管理、行政和管治有关。
三　义是在我们里面与神是对的：
１　在我们里面与神是对的，乃是里面的人是透明并明亮如水晶的─里面的人是照着神的心思和意愿的。
２　有这样的义，就是在基督里成为神的义─林后五21。
四　希伯来书基本上是以神公义的行政结构而成的─十30～31，十二29：
１　希伯来书是神在经纶并行政上对待祂子民的启示。
２　本书的五个警告，都是基于神是公义的神这个事实，在行政上对付祂的子民时，祂乃是烈火─十31：
a　第一个警告─要注重论到子的话─二1～4。
b　第二个警告─不要赶不上那应许的安息─三7～四13。
c　第三个警告─要竭力前进，达到成熟─五11～六20。
d　第四个警告─要向前进入至圣所，不可退缩回到犹太教─十19～39。
e　第五个警告─要奔跑赛程，不可堕落离开恩典─十二1～29。
五　我们若要完全进入公义的话，就必须经历并操练─五13～14：
１　“没有经验的”（13），指明我们必须经历公义的话。─引用经文
２　“操练”（14），与操练我们的官能有关，好辨明、分辨神的话，正如我们辨别食物一样，考量一句特别的话是像奶，还是像干粮。─引用经文

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
