The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Eleven
The Word of the Beginning of Christ and the Word of Righteousness
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 5:11—6:5
I.　The word of God is not mainly for knowledge but for nourishment—Heb. 5:12:
A.　Although the Bible is a book of teaching, its teaching is not merely to impart mental knowledge but to minister food to us—Jer. 15:16; 1 Pet. 2:2-3.
B.　The Lord Jesus took the word of God in the Scriptures as His bread and lived on it—Matt. 4:4.
C.　The words?milk?and?solid food?in Hebrews 5:12 indicate that the word of God is for nourishment:
1.　Through regeneration the believers in Christ become babes who can grow in life by being nourished with spiritual milk, the milk of the word—1 Pet. 2:2-3:
a.　The milk of the word is for the soul, the inner being.
b.　Milk is conveyed in the word of God to nourish our inner man through the understanding of our rational mind and is assimilated by our mental faculties.
2.　Whereas milk is mainly for infants, solid food is for the mature—1 Cor. 3:2; Heb. 5:13.
II.　Paul's burden in the book of Hebrews was to bring the believers out of the kindergarten stage of the Christian life and to bring them on to maturity—to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and concerning the way of God's economy, the word which is solid food for them to reach maturity—Heb. 5:12, 14; 6:1:
A.　The Hebrew believers remained in the initial stage of salvation and were not going on to the heavenly ministry of Christ—Heb. 4:14-16; 5:11-12.
B.　The Hebrew believers had been enlightened, they had tasted of the heavenly gift, they had received and were partaking of the Holy Spirit, and they had tasted the good word of God, but they remained in the initial stage of the enjoyment of the New Testament blessings—Heb. 6:4-5.
C.　The Hebrew believers had received the gospel to a certain extent, but they did not realize that Christ has a ministry in the heavens—Heb. 8:1-2.
D.　The book of Hebrews was written to bring the believers on from the good word concerning Christ's earthly ministry to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and thereby bring the childish believers on to maturity—Heb. 6:1.
III.　The word of the beginning of Christ is the foundation of the Christian life—Heb. 6:1:
A.　In the Son's speaking God, or in God speaking in the Son, there are two categories of the word— the word of the beginning of Christ and the word of righteousness—Heb. 1:1-2; 5:12-14; 6:1:
1.　The word of the beginning of Christ is milk for infants.
2.　The word of righteousness is solid food for the mature.
B.　The good word of God in 6:5 refers to the word of the beginning of Christ in 6:1:
1.　The good word of God is the word of the beginning of Christ in the kindergarten stage.
2.　The word concerning Christ's earthly ministry is a good word because it is the glad tidings, the good news; however, it is a word in the kindergarten stage.
C.　The word of the beginning of Christ refers to six items that constitute the foundation of the Christian life: repentance from dead works, faith in God, the teaching of baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment—6:1-2.
D.　The beginning of Christ includes not only the beginning of the believers' experience of Christ but also the early ministry of Christ.
IV.　The word of righteousness embodies the thought of God's justice and righteousness in His dispensational and governmental dealings with His people—Heb. 5:13:
A.　Righteousness is being right with persons, things, and matters before God according to His righteous and strict requirements—Matt. 5:20; 1 Cor. 15:34.
B.　Righteousness is a matter of God's kingdom— Matt. 6:33:
1.　Righteousness issues from God for His administration—Psa. 89:14; 97:2; Isa. 32:1.
2.　Righteousness is related to God's government, administration, and rule.
C.　Righteousness is a matter of being right with God in our being:
1.　To be right with God in our being is to have an inner being that is transparent and crystal clear—an inner being in the mind and will of God.
2.　To be righteous in this way is to become the righteousness of God in Christ—2 Cor. 5:21.
D.　The book of Hebrews is constructed basically with God's righteous government—10:30-31; 12:29:
1.　Hebrews is a revelation of God's dispensational and governmental dealings with His people.
2.　All five warnings in this book are based on the fact that God is the God of righteousness, a consuming fire in His governmental dealings with His people—10:31:
a.　The first warning: Give heed to what is spoken concerning the Son—2:1-4.
b.　The second warning: Do not come short of the promised rest—3:7—4:13.
c.　The third warning: Be brought on to maturity—5:11—6:20.
d.　The fourth warning: Come forward to the Holy of Holies and do not shrink back to Judaism—10:19-39.
e.　The fifth warning: Run the race and do not fall away from grace—12:1-29.
E.　If we would fully enter into the word of righteousness, we need experience and exercise— 5:13-14:
1.　Inexperienced?(v. 13) indicates that we need to experience the word of righteousness.
2.　Exercised?(v. 14) is related to exercising our faculties to discern, to discriminate, God's word as we do food, considering whether a particular word is like milk or solid food.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十一篇　基督开端的话和公义的话
The Word of the Beginning of Christ and the Word of Righteousness
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来五11～六5。
壹　神的话主要不是为着知识，乃是为着滋养─来五12：
I.　The word of God is not mainly for knowledge but for nourishment—Heb. 5:12:
一　圣经虽是一本教训的书，但它的教训不仅是传授头脑的知识给我们，更是供应食物给我们─耶十五16，彼前二2～3。
A.　Although the Bible is a book of teaching, its teaching is not merely to impart mental knowledge but to minister food to us—Jer. 15:16; 1 Pet. 2:2-3.
二　主耶稣取用圣经上神的话作祂的食物，并靠此而活─太四4。
B.　The Lord Jesus took the word of God in the Scriptures as His bread and lived on it—Matt. 4:4.
三　希伯来五章十二节中，“奶”和“干粮”这些辞，指明神的话是为着滋养人的：
C.　The words?milk?and?solid food?in Hebrews 5:12 indicate that the word of God is for nourishment:
１　在基督里的信徒借着重生成为婴孩，就能靠着灵奶，就是话奶的滋养，在生命里长大─彼前二2～3。
1.　Through regeneration the believers in Christ become babes who can grow in life by being nourished with spiritual milk, the milk of the word—1 Pet. 2:2-3:
a　话奶是为着魂，就是里面的人。
a.　The milk of the word is for the soul, the inner being.
b　奶是在神的话里传输出来，借着我们理性心思的的领会，且由我们心思机能的吸收，滋养我们里面的人。
b.　Milk is conveyed in the word of God to nourish our inner man through the understanding of our rational mind and is assimilated by our mental faculties.
２　奶主要的是为着婴孩，而干粮是为着成熟的人─林前三2，来五13。
2.　Whereas milk is mainly for infants, solid food is for the mature—1 Cor. 3:2; Heb. 5:13.
贰　保罗在希伯来书中的负担，乃是要把信徒从基督徒生活的幼稚园阶段带出来，把他们带到成熟─带到公义的话，就是关于基督天上的职事，以及关于神经纶的法则的话，这话就是使他们达到成熟的干粮─来五12，14，六1：
II.　Paul's burden in the book of Hebrews was to bring the believers out of the kindergarten stage of the Christian life and to bring them on to maturity—to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and concerning the way of God's economy, the word which is solid food for them to reach maturity—Heb. 5:12, 14; 6:1:
一　希伯来信徒留在救恩的起初阶段，而不往前到基督天上的职事那里─来四14～16，五11～12。
A.　The Hebrew believers remained in the initial stage of salvation and were not going on to the heavenly ministry of Christ—Heb. 4:14-16; 5:11-12.
二　希伯来信徒曾经蒙了光照，尝过属天的恩赐，得着并有分于圣灵，并尝过神美善的话，但他们仍停留在享受新约祝福的起初阶段─来六4～5。
B.　The Hebrew believers had been enlightened, they had tasted of the heavenly gift, they had received and were partaking of the Holy Spirit, and they had tasted the good word of God, but they remained in the initial stage of the enjoyment of the New Testament blessings—Heb. 6:4-5.
三　希伯来信徒接受福音到某一个程度，但他们不知道，基督有一个在诸天之上的职事─来八1～2。
C.　The Hebrew believers had received the gospel to a certain extent, but they did not realize that Christ has a ministry in the heavens—Heb. 8:1-2.
四　写希伯来书是要把信徒从关于基督在地上职事之美善的话，带到关于基督在天上职事之公义的话，而把幼稚的信徒带到成熟─来六1。
D.　The book of Hebrews was written to bring the believers on from the good word concerning Christ's earthly ministry to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and thereby bring the childish believers on to maturity—Heb. 6:1.
叁　基督开端的话，是基督徒生活的根基─来六1：
III.　The word of the beginning of Christ is the foundation of the Christian life—Heb. 6:1:
一　子讲说神，或神在子里说话，有两类的话─基督开端的话，和公义的话─来一1～2，五12～14，六1：
A.　In the Son's speaking God, or in God speaking in the Son, there are two categories of the word— the word of the beginning of Christ and the word of righteousness—Heb. 1:1-2; 5:12-14; 6:1:
１　基督开端的话是奶，为着婴孩。
1.　The word of the beginning of Christ is milk for infants.
２　公义的话是干粮，为着成熟的人。
2.　The word of righteousness is solid food for the mature.
二　在六章五节里，神美善的话，是指六章一节所说基督基开端的话：─引用经文
B.　The good word of God in 6:5 refers to the word of the beginning of Christ in 6:1:
１　神美善的话，是在幼稚园阶段的基督开端的话。
1.　The good word of God is the word of the beginning of Christ in the kindergarten stage.
２　关于基督在地上职事的话，是美善的话，因为是喜信，佳音；然而，这是在幼稚园阶段的话。
2.　The word concerning Christ's earthly ministry is a good word because it is the glad tidings, the good news; however, it is a word in the kindergarten stage.
三　基督开端的话，是指构成基督徒生活根基的六项：悔改脱开死行，信靠神，浸洗的教训，按手，死人的复活，和永远的审判─六1～2。
C.　The word of the beginning of Christ refers to six items that constitute the foundation of the Christian life: repentance from dead works, faith in God, the teaching of baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment—6:1-2.
四　基督开端的话，不只包括信徒对基督经历的开端，也包括基督早期的职事。
D.　The beginning of Christ includes not only the beginning of the believers' experience of Christ but also the early ministry of Christ.
肆　公义的话，具体的说出神在祂的经纶并行政上，对待祂的子民所有公正公义的思想─来五13：
IV.　The word of righteousness embodies the thought of God's justice and righteousness in His dispensational and governmental dealings with His people—Heb. 5:13:
一　义是照着神公义并严格的要求，在神前与人、事、物都是对的─太五20，林前十五34。
A.　Righteousness is being right with persons, things, and matters before God according to His righteous and strict requirements—Matt. 5:20; 1 Cor. 15:34.
二　义是神国的事─太六33：
B.　Righteousness is a matter of God's kingdom— Matt. 6:33:
１　义是从神为着祂的行政而来的─诗八九14，九七2，赛三二1。
1.　Righteousness issues from God for His administration—Psa. 89:14; 97:2; Isa. 32:1.
２　义与神的管理、行政和管治有关。
2.　Righteousness is related to God's government, administration, and rule.
三　义是在我们里面与神是对的：
C.　Righteousness is a matter of being right with God in our being:
１　在我们里面与神是对的，乃是里面的人是透明并明亮如水晶的─里面的人是照着神的心思和意愿的。
1.　To be right with God in our being is to have an inner being that is transparent and crystal clear—an inner being in the mind and will of God.
２　有这样的义，就是在基督里成为神的义─林后五21。
2.　To be righteous in this way is to become the righteousness of God in Christ—2 Cor. 5:21.
四　希伯来书基本上是以神公义的行政结构而成的─十30～31，十二29：
D.　The book of Hebrews is constructed basically with God's righteous government—10:30-31; 12:29:
１　希伯来书是神在经纶并行政上对待祂子民的启示。
1.　Hebrews is a revelation of God's dispensational and governmental dealings with His people.
２　本书的五个警告，都是基于神是公义的神这个事实，在行政上对付祂的子民时，祂乃是烈火─十31：
2.　All five warnings in this book are based on the fact that God is the God of righteousness, a consuming fire in His governmental dealings with His people—10:31:
a　第一个警告─要注重论到子的话─二1～4。
a.　The first warning: Give heed to what is spoken concerning the Son—2:1-4.
b　第二个警告─不要赶不上那应许的安息─三7～四13。
b.　The second warning: Do not come short of the promised rest—3:7—4:13.
c　第三个警告─要竭力前进，达到成熟─五11～六20。
c.　The third warning: Be brought on to maturity—5:11—6:20.
d　第四个警告─要向前进入至圣所，不可退缩回到犹太教─十19～39。
d.　The fourth warning: Come forward to the Holy of Holies and do not shrink back to Judaism—10:19-39.
e　第五个警告─要奔跑赛程，不可堕落离开恩典─十二1～29。
e.　The fifth warning: Run the race and do not fall away from grace—12:1-29.
五　我们若要完全进入公义的话，就必须经历并操练─五13～14：
E.　If we would fully enter into the word of righteousness, we need experience and exercise— 5:13-14:
１　“没有经验的”（13），指明我们必须经历公义的话。─引用经文
1.　Inexperienced?(v. 13) indicates that we need to experience the word of righteousness.
２　“操练”（14），与操练我们的官能有关，好辨明、分辨神的话，正如我们辨别食物一样，考量一句特别的话是像奶，还是像干粮。─引用经文
2.　Exercised?(v. 14) is related to exercising our faculties to discern, to discriminate, God's word as we do food, considering whether a particular word is like milk or solid food.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE ELEVEN
THE WORD OF THE BEGINNING OF CHRIST AND THE WORD OF RIGHTEOUSNESS
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The purpose of the third warning in the book of Hebrews is to encourage us to be brought on to maturity (Heb. 5:11— 6:20), We should aspire to mature in the divine life that the Lord would wholly gain us. Hebrews 5:14 says, "Solid food is for the full-grown." God's intention is that we all would become full-grown. Verses 1 and 3 of Hebrews 6 continue this thought by saying, ''Therefore leaving the word of the beginning of Christ, let us be brought on to maturity.... And this we will do if God permits." May the Lord bring us onward to maturity.
THE WORD OF GOD BEING FOR NOURISHMENT
The Bible Ministering Food to Us The word of God is not mainly for knowledge but for nourishment (Heb. 5:12). Although the Bible is a book of teaching, its teaching is not merely to impart mental knowledge but to minister food to us. Jeremiah said, "Your words were found and I ate them" (Jer. 15:16). Jeremiah did not come to the word merely for knowledge. First Peter 2:2 says, "As newborn babes, long for the guileless milk of the word in order that by it you may grow unto salvation." These verses reveal that the word of God is for nourishment.
The Lord Jesus Taking the Word of God as His Bread and Living by It
The Lord Jesus took the word of God in the Scriptures as His bread and lived on it. In Matthew 4, when the Lord Jesus was tempted by Satan He said, "Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that proceeds out through the mouth of God" (v. 4).
Milk and Solid Food Indicating That the Word of God Is for Nourishment
In Hebrews 5:12 the words milk and solid food indicate that the word of God is for nourishment. Through regeneration the believers in Christ become babes who can grow in life by being nourished with spiritual milk, the milk of the word (1 Pet. 2:2-3). The milk of the word is for the soul, the inner being. Milk is conveyed in the word of God to nourish our inner man through the understanding of our rational mind and is assimilated by our mental faculties. Milk is mainly for infants; solid food is for the mature (1 Cor. 3:2; Heb. 5:13). The expression solid food (v. 14) is used in the context of the word of righteousness; hence, solid food refers to the word of righteousness.
In the Lord's recovery we consider that the word of God is mainly for nourishment rather than for knowledge. However, many Christians consider that the word of God is for knowledge and that the more knowledge one gains, the more spiritual one becomes. Yet there are many knowledgeable Christians who lack true spirituality. The word of God is mainly for nourishment, and nourishment is for growth. Nourishment is not for knowledge; nourishment is always for growth.
PAUl'S BURDEN BEING TO BRING THE BELIEVERS ON TO MATURITY
Paul's burden in the book of Hebrews was to bring the believers out of the kindergarten stage of the Christian life and to bring them on to maturity- to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and concerning the way of God's economy, the word which is solid food for them to reach maturity (Heb. 5:12, 14; 6:1). The book of Hebrews conveys to us the word of righteousness (5:13). This word of righteousness says that the resurrected Christ has ascended to the heavens and is now serving as the Minister in the better tabernacle in the heavens. He is our great High Priest, the Mediator of the new covenant. He is carrying out His heavenly ministry and infusing us with the fullness of the heavenlies, including His ascension, resurrection, and life, making us a heavenly people on earth. This heavenly ministry will bring us on to maturity. If we are willing to continue in this way receiving the heavenly ministry of Christ, we will certainly reach maturity. When the Lord comes He will give us the reward, and we will enter into the kingdom. But if we are unwilling to receive His heavenly ministry and do not reach maturity, we must realize that our God is a consuming fire (12:29) and that our work may be burned by His righteous judgment (6:8; 1 Cor. 3:9, 13).
The Hebrew Believers Remaining in the Initial Stage of Salvation
The Hebrew believers remained in the initial stage of salvation and were not going on to the heavenly ministry of Christ (Heb. 4:14-16). It is possible, like the Hebrew believers, to remain in the initial stage of salvation. Many believers have never progressed from the initial stage of salvation. This is regrettable. We must progress from the initial stage of salvation and go on to the heavenly ministry of Christ. We must be those who touch, taste, and enter into the experience of the heavenly ministry of Christ.
Hebrews 5:11-12 says, "Concerning whom we have much to say that is also difficult to interpret since you have become dull of hearing. For when because of the time you ought to be teachers, you have need again for someone to teach you what the rudiments of the beginning of the oracles of God are and have become those who have need of milk and not of solid food." According to this chapter, Christ is the High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec (v. 6). This refers to Christ's heavenly ministry, and this is the way God's economy is unveiled. God's economy is not unveiled very much through the milk of the word. It is the solid food that unveils God's economy and the heavenly ministry of Christ.
The word of righteousness is the word that brings us on to maturity. Without the word of righteousness, we can never go forward unto maturity. Without the word of righteousness we may not even have the desire to go forward unto maturity. Today in Christianity not many believers are pressing on to maturity. This is because the word of righteousness is hardly spoken among them. But in the Lord's recovery the word of righteousness is spoken again and again. It is this word of righteousness that causes us to go on to maturity.
Brother Lee once said, "I live under the light of the judgment seat of Christ." This is a word of righteousness. Brother Lee realized that one day he would stand before the judgment seat of Christ and have to give an account of everything. Do not think that because you have been saved everything will be all right. When I was in the denominations, the main thing I knew to do after being saved was to read my Bible, love the Lord, preach the gospel, and bring as many people with me into heaven as I could. Some in the denominations love the Lord, but there is no incentive to live a holy life. There is also no incentive to grow unto maturity. The word of righteousness is a word in the Bible that presses us to maturity. Without this pressing word, none of us would continue. This is God's way to cause us to arrive at maturity.
The Hebrew Believers Remaining in the Initial Stage of the New Testament Blessings
The Hebrew believers had been enlightened, they had tasted of the heavenly gift, they had received and were partaking of the Holy Spirit, and they had tasted the good word of God, but they remained in the initial stage of the enjoyment of the New Testament blessings (Heb. 6:4-5). They would not go on. I hope that all the saints in the Lord's recovery would go on and on until they reach maturity. We cannot be satisfied with the initial stage of the enjoyment of the New Testament blessings. The Hebrew believers had been enlightened and had tasted of the heavenly gift. The heavenly gift is the divine nature, the divine life, the divine function, and the law of life that we gained at the moment we were regenerated and that operates in us today. The Hebrew believers were also partaking of the Holy Spirit. According to the divine revelation in the Word, we received the Spirit when we were regenerated (Rom. 8:16; John 3:6, 34; 1 Cor. 6:17). The Spirit came into us to dwell in us for eternity. These are the initial blessings that we received when we received Christ.
We have also tasted the good word of God. When we first heard the gospel and were saved, we touched the good word of God. We loved John 3:16: "For God so loved the world that He gave His only begotten Son, that every one who believes into Him would not perish, but would have eternal life? This wonderful verse is the good word of God. There are many other precious verses that are the good word of God. All the words that were spoken concerning Jesus7 incarnation, His crucifixion, His resurrection, and all the aspects of His earthly ministry are the good word of God. The good word of God is embodied in the Lord's earthly ministry. What a glorious beginning this word brings us into! The Hebrew believers had partaken of all of that; however, they continued to remain in the initial stage of the enjoyment of the New Testament blessings.
The Powers of the Age to Come Being among the Initial Blessings
Hebrews 6:4-5 says, "For it is impossible for those who have once been enlightened and have tasted of the heavenly gift and have become partakers of the Holy Spirit and have tasted the good word of God and the powers of the age to come." All of these items are the initial blessings. The powers of the age to come are the supernatural things. These things are for the age to come. However, God allows some of these things to be enjoyed in this age. But the full enjoyment of these powers of the age to come is for the millennial kingdom, not for the New Testament dispensation. The things mentioned in Hebrews 6:4-5 are the good word of God, not the word of righteousness. They are the rudiments of the beginning of the oracles of God, the word of the beginning of Christ, the good word of God (5:12). However, some in Christianity may consider that if they can help other believers to have the experience of the supernatural things, then they have brought them on to the road of maturity. Actually, regardless of how much you experience the powers of the age to come, you may never progress to maturity.
God is operating in us to make us a new creation, to remake our whole being—inwardly and outwardly——spirit, soul, and body. God's way is to remake us, to re-create us, to make us a new creation (2 Cor. 5:17). The supernatural things do not help us very much to reach this goal. Today in Christianity people love miracles, healings, speaking in tongues, and the interpretation of tongues. We do not condemn any brother or sister for the practice of these things. Yet we realize that these practices are at most the good word of God. They do little to remake us into God's new creation. Pursuing these things will not bring us on to maturity. It is all the things of the divine life and divine nature that will bring us on to maturity and remake us to be a new creation.
I would like to share an example of what the inner working of the divine life and nature is able to do in the saints. In 1972 a brother who had very long hair and a very full beard came into the church life. After being in the church life for a couple of years he had to renew his drivers license. On this brothers driver's license was a picture of himself with long hair and a full beard. At the time he went to renew his license, his long hair was cut short and his face was clean-shaven. When he presented his old driver's license to the lady at the desk, she looked intently at his picture with the head full of long hair and the face fully bearded, and then she looked with amazement at his bright shining face. Before she could say anything, the brother said, "Jesus did it." When the Lord remakes us, our outward appearance may change, but that is not the primary issue. This brother was inwardly changed by the Lord's life.
We expect that the Lord's life will transform us, renew us, and conform us to His image. This is the Lord's work in all of His children, and we must pay attention to this today. Do not be concerned about the powers of the age to come. All the powers of the age to come will not transform us or glorify us. We need to take the remaking way. The word of righteousness is the remaking way. This word will make us a new creation. This is the Lord's desire.
The Hebrew Believers Not Realizing That Christ Has a Ministry in the Heavens
The Hebrew believers had received the gospel to a certain extent, but they did not realize that Christ has a ministry in the heavens (Heb. 8:1-2). The book of Hebrews was written to bring the believers on from the good word concerning Christ's earthly ministry to the word of righteousness concerning Christ's heavenly ministry and thereby bring the childish believers on to maturity (6:1).
THE WORD OF THE BEGINNING OF CHRIST BEING THE FOUNDATION OF THE CHRISTIAN LIFE
The Word of the Beginning of Christ and the Word of Righteousness The word of the beginning of Christ is the foundation of the Christian life (Heb. 6:1). In the Sons speaking God, or in God speaking in the Son, there are two categories of the word- the word of the beginning of Christ and the word of righteousness (1:1-2; 5:12-14). The word of the beginning of Christ is milk for infants. The word of righteousness is solid food for the mature. The good word of God in 6:5 refers to the word of the beginning of Christ in 6:1. It is the word in the kindergarten stage. The word concerning Christ's earthly ministry is a good word because it is the glad tidings, the good news; however, it is a word in the kindergarten stage.
The Good Word of God Being the Word of the Beginning of Christ
The good word of God is referred to in two ways. First, in Hebrews 5:12 it is called the rudiments of the beginning of the oracles of God. Rudiments of the beginning of the oracles of God are the primary elements of the beginning of the oracles of God. Second, it is referred to in Hebrews 6:1 as the word of the beginning of Christ. Although this word is very good and very kind, it is considered milk for infants. It is not solid food. In contrast to the good word of God, the word of righteousness is very strict and very just.
The good word of God is the word of grace, the word of life, the word of truth, and the word of the gospel (Acts 20:32; Phil, 2:16; James 1:18; Acts 15:7). All of these are very good to us. When we heard the gospel, this word brought us to repentance. We opened to Him, received Him, and were regenerated. This was the good word of God and also the word of grace. Grace is the Triune God for our enjoyment. This word of grace is also the word of life. It is the God-breathed word, the word that is spirit and life. This is the word that we enjoy every day. We also enjoy the word of truth, which conveys the divine realities to us. Every morning when we pray-read we enjoy the good word of God. It is very soothing, kind, nourishing, and a supply to us.
This good word of God refers to the milk of the word (1 Pet. 2:2). It is not the word of righteousness. As milk it supplies and nourishes us. Paul said to the Corinthians, "I gave you milk to drink, not solid food, for you were not yet able to receive it" (1 Cor. 3:2). The Corinthians were nourished by this word, and they enjoyed this word which was the beginning of Christ. It was the rudiments, or the primary elements, of the beginning of the oracles of God. The good word of God is the beginning stage, the kindergarten stage. It is the word of the glad tidings, the word of the good news, but it is only the kindergarten stage. This is the word that we enjoy initially and that produces our rebirth. It is the word by which we begin to grow at the beginning of our Christian life.
We are the same as the Hebrew believers. We did not go immediately to the word of righteousness; instead, we first enjoyed the good word. We were supplied by the good word. We were even brought to Christ by this good word. Praise the Lord for the word of grace, the word of life, the word of truth, and the word of the gospel. We all enjoy this good word of God. This word is effectual in us, and it causes us to grow unto salvation.
Six Items Constituting the Foundation of the Christian Life The word of the beginning of Christ refers to six items that constitute the foundation of the Christian life: repentance from dead works, faith in God, the teaching of baptisms, the laying on of hands, the resurrection of the dead, and eternal judgment (Heb. 6:1-2). In footnote 1 on Hebrews 6:1 in the Recovery Version of the New Testament, Brother Lee said,
These six items form three pairs. The first item of each pair refers to our emergence from a negative situation, and the second speaks of our entering into the positive things. Repentance is a turning away from dead works; faith is the entry into God. Baptisms are separations from and terminations of the negative things; the laying on of hands is the identification and fellowship with the divine things. The resurrection of the dead is the emergence from death; eternal judgment is the entry into eternity and the eternal destiny.
The beginning of Christ includes not only the beginning of the believers' experience of Christ but also the earthly ministry of Christ.
THE WORD OF RIGHTEOUSNESS EMBODYING THE THOUGHT OF GOD'S JUSTICE AND RIGHTEOUSNESS
The word of righteousness embodies the thought of God's justice and righteousness in His dispensational and governmental dealings with His people (Heb, 5:13). The word of righteousness comprises the entire book of Hebrews. Hebrews 2 mentions the matter of the just recompense (v. 2). If we do not take care of Christ in this age, there is a just recompense coming. The just recompense is related to the coming inhabited earth. If we do not take care of Christ in this age, then we are in danger of not being among those who will reign over the coming inhabited earth (v. 5).
Righteousness— Being Right with Persons, Things, and Matters before God
Righteousness is being right with persons, things, and matters before God according to His righteous and strict requirements (Matt. 5:20; 1 Cor. 15:34). Righteousness is a matter of God's kingdom (Matt. 6:33).
Righteousness issues from God for His administration (Psa. 89:14; 97:2; Isa. 32:1). Righteousness is related to God's government, administration, and rule.
Righteousness—a Matter of Being Right with God in Our Being
Righteousness is a matter of being right with God in our being. To be right with God in our being is to have an inner being that is transparent and crystal clear- an inner being in the mind and will of God. To be righteous in this way is to become the righteousness of God in Christ (2 Cor. 5:21). Righteousness always refers to God's government and governmental dealings. God's divine government among His people and God's justice are unveiled in the word of righteousness. This is what the word of righteousness is.
The Word of Life and the Word of Righteousness in the Book of Revelation Revelation 22 contains both the word of life and the word of righteousness. Verse 14 says, "Blessed are those who wash their robes that they may have right to the tree of life and may enter by the gates into the city." This is a wonderful promise! Verse 17 continues with a wonderful call: 'And the Spirit and the bride say, Come! And let him who hears say, Come! And let him who is thirsty come; let him who wills take the water of life freely." Both of these verses are words of life and grace. But there is another kind of word in Revelation 22, which is neither a word of grace nor a word of life. It is a word of righteousness: "Behold, I come quickly, and My reward is with Me to render to each one as his work is" (v. 12). Brothers and sisters, the day is coming when God will deal with us according to His word of righteousness. Will we be those who receive the reward or will we be those who undergo discipline for a certain period of time? At the judgment seat of Christ there will be both reward and discipline. It all depends on how we live according to the word of righteousness.
The Word of Righteousness in Matthew 24 and 25
Matthew 24 and 25 also contain a word of righteousness. When Brother Lee shared with people more than sixty-five years ago that the believers could be cast into outer darkness, many rose up to oppose him. As long as you speak the good word of God, everything is fine.
But when you rise up and say that Christians who are unfaithful to the Lord are in danger of being cast into outer darkness, where there is weeping and gnashing of teeth, many believers do not like to hear this.
However, if we do not hear this word and do not let it govern us, and if we are not under God's disciplinary dealings today in this dispensation to prepare us for the next dispensation, then our destiny will be outer darkness (24:51; 25:30).
Some may read these verses in Matthew 24 and 25 and consider that they do not apply to them. But through this speaking of Brothers Witness Lee and Watchman Nee, we have received the word of righteousness, and we understand what the word of righteousness is today. It is not just the good word of God. Paul was very concerned that after preaching to others he might be disapproved (1 Cor. 9:27). There was a possibility that even Paul could be disapproved. However, near the end of his life, Paul said, "Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, with which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will recompense me in that day, and not only me but also all those who have loved His appearing" (2 Tim. 4:8). The righteous Judge, the One who spoke the word of righteousness, the One who would judge Paul according to righteousness, will give him such a crown, even the crown of righteousness.
God's Righteous Government in the Book of Hebrews
The book of Hebrews is constructed basically with God's righteous government (10:30-31; 12:29). Hebrews is a revelation of God's dispensational and governmental dealings with His people. All five warnings in this book are based on the fact that God is the God of righteousness, a consuming fire in His governmental dealings with His people. The first warning charges us to give heed to what is spoken concerning the Son (2:1-4). The second warning exhorts us to not come short of the promised rest (3:7一 4:13). The third warning encourages us to be brought on to maturity (5:11- 6:20). The fourth warning calls us to come forward to the Holy of Holies and not to shrink back to Judaism (10:19-39), The fifth warning urges us to run the race and not fall away from grace (12:1-29). In the book of Hebrews these five warnings are related to God's dispensational dealings with His people in a righteous way. Unless we heed these warnings, which are words of righteousness to His people, our destiny will not be so positive. These words may not be so easy to digest, but we all have to be willing to digest them and to assimilate them.
Entering into the Word of Righteousness through Experience and Exercise
If we would fully enter into the word of righteousness, we need experience and exercise (5:13-14). The word inexperienced (v. 13) indicates that we need to experience the word of righteousness. The word of God is not only for teaching us; it is also to be experienced. We have to pray, "Lord, do not let the words of the Bible or the words of this ministry become mere teachings or doctrine to me. Let them become my experience." The Lord's intention in giving us the Bible and in giving us the word of righteousness is that we might be brought into the experience of all these things.
The word exercised (v. 14) is related to exercising our faculties to discern, to discriminate, God's word as we do food, considering whether a particular word is like milk or solid food. The phrase faculties exercised for discriminating does not mean to discriminate or judge right from wrong. Rather, it means to discriminate or to judge the superior from the inferior. What Paul did in Hebrews was to present a wonderful portrait of the Christ who is superior to the God in the Old Testament, superior to angels, superior to Moses, and superior to Aaron, the high priest. He also showed us that the New Testament, the new covenant, is far superior to the Old Testament or the old covenant. We need the proper discernment. We need our faculties, our senses, to be keen so that we can discern what is superior and what is inferior.
I believe that the Lord's recovery, having been raised up through the leading of Brother Nee and Brother Lee in the ministry over the past seventy years, has had the proper discernment. I believe that the discernment, the judgment, is right. We must discern and judge all things in the same way. Do not pursue the inferior things. Before I came into the Lord's recovery, the only word I heard was the good word of God. But there was something in me that wanted to hear more. The first set of audio tapes that I heard of Brother Lee in 1964 were the messages that now comprise the book, A Brief Definition of the Kingdom of the Heavens. As I listened to those messages I was captured. It was the word of righteousness, and I knew it was the word that I wanted and needed. The word of righteousness will bring us onward unto maturity. Becoming mature is not something that happens overnight. It happens gradually, little by little, as we run the race to arrive at maturity. In the Lord's recovery we should always be advancing. There should be definite progress every year. If next year we are in the same state as we are this year, something is wrong. Our contact with the word of God should be in a higher realm and on a higher level. May the word of righteousness become a beacon to us, shining upon us for the rest of our lives. We should enjoy the good word of God, but we must also be brought into the word of righteousness. I love the good word of God, but I also love the word of righteousness. May the Lord gain us for His recovery and for His kingdom.—B. P.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十二篇　我们当竭力前进达到成熟
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来六1～8，19～20，腓三12～15。
壹　变化是在我们天然生命中新陈代谢的改变，而成熟乃是被那改变我们的神圣生命所充满─来六1：
一　变化的最后阶段是成熟，就是生命的丰满：
１　神永远的定旨，惟有借着我们的变化与成熟，才能成就。
２　成熟乃是一再得着神圣的生命分赐到我们里面，直到我们得着生命的丰满─约十10下，林后五4下。
二　生命的丰满是祝福，也就是生命满溢到别人里面─创四七7，10，四九28，约壹五16：
１　神要按祂主宰的权柄，使用人、事、物，将一切充满我们里面的，全都倒空，并除去一切的霸占，好使我们的度量加增，而被神充满─路一53，太五6。
２　一切发生在我们身上的事，都在神主宰的权柄下，为要使我们变化和成熟；没有什么事是偶然的：
a　成熟的信徒知道，神是怜悯的，是全丰全足的，能应付他在各种情形中的需要─创四三14，十七1，腓一19～21上，四11～12。
b　信徒的信靠和安息，全在于他全丰全足的神，而不再在于他自己，或他的能力─罗九16。
贰　希伯来书是一本讲成熟的书，我们若要达到成熟，就必须过河：
一　以色列人过了红海和约但河，　我们也照样必须过一道又一道河：
１　红海的水，是基督的死结束了世界的权势这一面的预表，而约但河的水则代表基督的死将我们旧人了结的一面─书四8～9，20。
２　我们一切的难处，都要在约但河的那一边，并在约但河里被对付。
３　当我们过了河，从我们魂的旷野出来，而进到我们的美地基督，就是在我们灵里安息日之安息里面时，我们就与祂是一，以解决祂的难处，并顾到祂的权益，打那属灵的仗，好使我们在召会生活中完满的彰显祂，并与祂一同作王，以建造基督的身体，终极完成新耶路撒冷：
a　我们若是在自己里，或是单独行事，或是试着要圣别、属灵、或得胜，我们就是不成熟的。
b　真正的成熟不仅是在我们灵里，也是在召会生活中─弗四11～13。
c　成熟的信徒认识并顾到基督的身体，有身体的感觉，也以身体为中心─林前十二16，18～19，21，24。
二　我们必须极力逃往（参徒十四6）避难所，进到幔内，进入至圣所，在此作先锋的主已在荣耀里登宝座─来六18～20：
１　这表征我们必须在我们的灵里，在经历上这灵乃是今天实际至圣所的所在。
２　作先锋的主耶稣，逃离一切的事，进入幔内，进到神面前。
３　我们必须跟从祂，逃离一切基督和召会以外的事物，而进入我们灵里。
４　逃离乃是真正的过河。
叁　我们必须往前，竭力前进，达到成熟，忘记背后，努力面前的，追求对基督最完满的享受并赢得基督，好在千年国里对基督有极点的享受─腓三12～15：
一　信徒若偏离了，又回转过来，就不需再立基督徒生活的根基（来六1）；只要继续往前，竭力前进，达到完全、成熟就好了。─引用经文
二　我们基督徒的生活，是一个建筑的工程，也是一个赛程；我们开始之后若跌倒了，不需回到起点再开始；我们只需从跌倒的地方再往前跑。
三　信徒就像田地，吸收了屡次下在其上的雨水，并且生产合式的菜蔬，而不是像田地长出荆棘和蒺藜，就被废弃，近于咒诅，结局就是焚烧─来六7～8：
１　雨水是指希伯来六章四至五节所说五类美好的事物；生产菜蔬是竭力前进，达到成熟的例证。
２　信徒就如同田地，为着神被耕种，好生产基督，如同田地生产菜蔬，以达到完全、成熟，而从神得享祝福─参歌四12～15。
３　荆棘和蒺藜，是指希伯来信徒老宗教传统的事物。
４　田地是绝不会被焚烧的，但其上所长的却会被焚烧；信徒绝不会被焚烧，但他们不按着神的经纶所生长的，却要被焚烧─林前三9，12～13。
肆　主要带领我们往前，达到成熟，但我们必须与祂恩典的工作甘心乐意的合作：
一　我们需要与基督同享祂所达到的─来一9，三15。
二　我们需要竭力进入那存留之安息日的安息─来四9，11。
三　我们需要来到施恩的宝座前，受怜悯，得恩典─来四16。
四　我们需要吃干粮，以享受基督照着麦基洗德的等次作我们的大祭司─来五9～10，14。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Twelve
Let Us Be Brought On to Maturity
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 6:1-8, 19-20; Phil. 3:12-15
I.　To be transformed is to be metabolically changed in our natural life, whereas to be matured is to be filled with the divine life that changes us—Heb. 6:1:
A.　The last stage of transformation is maturity, the fullness of life:
1.　God's eternal purpose can only be accomplished through our transformation and maturity.
2.　Maturity is a matter of having the divine life imparted into us again and again until we have the fullness of life—John 10:10b; 2 Cor. 5:4b.
B.　The fullness of life is blessing, which is the overflow of life into others—Gen. 47:7, 10; 49:28; 1 John 5:16:
1.　God will sovereignly use persons, things, and events to empty us of everything that has filled us and to take away every preoccupation so that we may have an increased capacity to be filled with God—Luke 1:53; Matt. 5:6.
2.　Everything that happens to us is under God's sovereignty for our transformation and maturity; nothing is accidental:
a.　A mature believer has learned that God is merciful and all-sufficient to meet his needs in every kind of situation— Gen. 43:14; 17:1; Phil. 1:19-21a; 4:11-12.
b.　His trust and rest are altogether in the mercy of his all-sufficient God, no longer in himself or in his ability—Rom. 9:16.
II.　The book of Hebrews is a book on maturity, and in order to be brought on to maturity we need to cross the river:
A.　Just as the children of Israel crossed the Red Sea and the Jordan River, we must cross river after river:
1.　The water of the Red Sea is a type of the aspect of Christ's death which ends the power of the world, whereas the water of the Jordan represents the aspect of Christ's death which brings our old man to an end—Josh. 4:8-9, 20.
2.　All of our difficulties are dealt with on the other side of and in the Jordan River.
3.　When we cross the river out of the wilderness of our soul into Christ as our good land, our Sabbath rest in our spirit, we are one with Him to deal with His difficulty and care for His interests, fighting the spiritual warfare so that we may fully express Him and reign with Him in the church life for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem:
a.　If we are in ourselves or are individualistic, or if we are trying to be holy, spiritual, or victorious, we are immature.
b.　Real maturity is not only in our spirit but also in the church life—Eph. 4:11-13.
c.　A mature believer knows and cares for the Body of Christ, being Body-conscious and Body-centered—1 Cor. 12:16, 18-19, 21, 24.
B.　We need to intensively flee (cf. Acts 14:6) for refuge to enter within the veil, to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord as the Forerunner is enthroned in glory—Heb. 6:18-20:
1.　This signifies that we must be in our spirit, where experientially the practical Holy of Holies is today.
2.　The Lord Jesus as the Forerunner fled everything to enter into the presence of God within the veil.
3.　We need to follow Him to flee from everything other than Christ and the church life into our spirit.
4.　This fleeing is a real river crossing.
III.　We need to go on, to be brought on, to maturity by forgetting the things which are behind and stretching forward to the things which are before, pursuing toward the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ for the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom—Phil. 3:12-15:
A.　If believers fall away and turn back, there is no need for them to lay the foundation of their Christian life again (Heb. 6:1); there is only the need to go on, to be brought on, to perfection, to maturity.
B.　Our Christian life is a building project and a race; if we fall away after we have begun, we do not need to go back to the beginning and start again; we only need to go on from where we fell.
C.　The believers should be like the earth which drinks the rain which often comes upon it and brings forth proper vegetation, instead of like the earth bringing forth thorns and thistles, which is disapproved and near a curse, whose end is to be burned—Heb. 6:7-8:
1.　The rain refers to the five categories of good things mentioned in Hebrews 6:4-5, and bringing forth vegetation is an illustration of being brought on to maturity.
2.　The believers, as the earth, are tilled for God's sake that they might bring forth Christ, as the vegetation, to maturity to partake of blessing from God—cf. S. S. 4:12-15.
3.　The thorns and thistles refer to the traditional things of the Hebrew believers' old religion.
4.　The earth could never be burned, but what it brings forth could be burned; the believers could never be burned, but all that they bring forth that is not according to God's economy will be burned—1 Cor. 3:9, 12-13.
IV.　The Lord wants to bring us on to maturity, but we must willingly cooperate with His gracious work:
A.　We need to share with Christ in His attainments—Heb. 1:9; 3:14.
B.　We need to be diligent to enter into the remaining Sabbath rest—Heb. 4:9, 11.
C.　We need to come forward to the throne of grace to receive mercy and find grace—Heb. 4:16.
D.　We need to feed on the solid food to enjoy Christ as our High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec—Heb. 5:9-10, 14.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十二篇　我们当竭力前进达到成熟
Let Us Be Brought On to Maturity
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来六1～8，19～20，腓三12～15。
壹　变化是在我们天然生命中新陈代谢的改变，而成熟乃是被那改变我们的神圣生命所充满─来六1：
I.　To be transformed is to be metabolically changed in our natural life, whereas to be matured is to be filled with the divine life that changes us—Heb. 6:1:
一　变化的最后阶段是成熟，就是生命的丰满：
A.　The last stage of transformation is maturity, the fullness of life:
１　神永远的定旨，惟有借着我们的变化与成熟，才能成就。
1.　God's eternal purpose can only be accomplished through our transformation and maturity.
２　成熟乃是一再得着神圣的生命分赐到我们里面，直到我们得着生命的丰满─约十10下，林后五4下。
2.　Maturity is a matter of having the divine life imparted into us again and again until we have the fullness of life—John 10:10b; 2 Cor. 5:4b.
二　生命的丰满是祝福，也就是生命满溢到别人里面─创四七7，10，四九28，约壹五16：
B.　The fullness of life is blessing, which is the overflow of life into others—Gen. 47:7, 10; 49:28; 1 John 5:16:
１　神要按祂主宰的权柄，使用人、事、物，将一切充满我们里面的，全都倒空，并除去一切的霸占，好使我们的度量加增，而被神充满─路一53，太五6。
1.　God will sovereignly use persons, things, and events to empty us of everything that has filled us and to take away every preoccupation so that we may have an increased capacity to be filled with God—Luke 1:53; Matt. 5:6.
２　一切发生在我们身上的事，都在神主宰的权柄下，为要使我们变化和成熟；没有什么事是偶然的：
2.　Everything that happens to us is under God's sovereignty for our transformation and maturity; nothing is accidental:
a　成熟的信徒知道，神是怜悯的，是全丰全足的，能应付他在各种情形中的需要─创四三14，十七1，腓一19～21上，四11～12。
a.　A mature believer has learned that God is merciful and all-sufficient to meet his needs in every kind of situation— Gen. 43:14; 17:1; Phil. 1:19-21a; 4:11-12.
b　信徒的信靠和安息，全在于他全丰全足的神，而不再在于他自己，或他的能力─罗九16。
b.　His trust and rest are altogether in the mercy of his all-sufficient God, no longer in himself or in his ability—Rom. 9:16.
贰　希伯来书是一本讲成熟的书，我们若要达到成熟，就必须过河：
II.　The book of Hebrews is a book on maturity, and in order to be brought on to maturity we need to cross the river:
一　以色列人过了红海和约但河，　我们也照样必须过一道又一道河：
A.　Just as the children of Israel crossed the Red Sea and the Jordan River, we must cross river after river:
１　红海的水，是基督的死结束了世界的权势这一面的预表，而约但河的水则代表基督的死将我们旧人了结的一面─书四8～9，20。
1.　The water of the Red Sea is a type of the aspect of Christ's death which ends the power of the world, whereas the water of the Jordan represents the aspect of Christ's death which brings our old man to an end—Josh. 4:8-9, 20.
２　我们一切的难处，都要在约但河的那一边，并在约但河里被对付。
2.　All of our difficulties are dealt with on the other side of and in the Jordan River.
３　当我们过了河，从我们魂的旷野出来，而进到我们的美地基督，就是在我们灵里安息日之安息里面时，我们就与祂是一，以解决祂的难处，并顾到祂的权益，打那属灵的仗，好使我们在召会生活中完满的彰显祂，并与祂一同作王，以建造基督的身体，终极完成新耶路撒冷：
3.　When we cross the river out of the wilderness of our soul into Christ as our good land, our Sabbath rest in our spirit, we are one with Him to deal with His difficulty and care for His interests, fighting the spiritual warfare so that we may fully express Him and reign with Him in the church life for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem:
a　我们若是在自己里，或是单独行事，或是试着要圣别、属灵、或得胜，我们就是不成熟的。
a.　If we are in ourselves or are individualistic, or if we are trying to be holy, spiritual, or victorious, we are immature.
b　真正的成熟不仅是在我们灵里，也是在召会生活中─弗四11～13。
b.　Real maturity is not only in our spirit but also in the church life—Eph. 4:11-13.
c　成熟的信徒认识并顾到基督的身体，有身体的感觉，也以身体为中心─林前十二16，18～19，21，24。
c.　A mature believer knows and cares for the Body of Christ, being Body-conscious and Body-centered—1 Cor. 12:16, 18-19, 21, 24.
二　我们必须极力逃往（参徒十四6）避难所，进到幔内，进入至圣所，在此作先锋的主已在荣耀里登宝座─来六18～20：
B.　We need to intensively flee (cf. Acts 14:6) for refuge to enter within the veil, to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord as the Forerunner is enthroned in glory—Heb. 6:18-20:
１　这表征我们必须在我们的灵里，在经历上这灵乃是今天实际至圣所的所在。
1.　This signifies that we must be in our spirit, where experientially the practical Holy of Holies is today.
２　作先锋的主耶稣，逃离一切的事，进入幔内，进到神面前。
2.　The Lord Jesus as the Forerunner fled everything to enter into the presence of God within the veil.
３　我们必须跟从祂，逃离一切基督和召会以外的事物，而进入我们灵里。
3.　We need to follow Him to flee from everything other than Christ and the church life into our spirit.
４　逃离乃是真正的过河。
4.　This fleeing is a real river crossing.
叁　我们必须往前，竭力前进，达到成熟，忘记背后，努力面前的，追求对基督最完满的享受并赢得基督，好在千年国里对基督有极点的享受─腓三12～15：
III.　We need to go on, to be brought on, to maturity by forgetting the things which are behind and stretching forward to the things which are before, pursuing toward the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ for the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom—Phil. 3:12-15:
一　信徒若偏离了，又回转过来，就不需再立基督徒生活的根基（来六1）；只要继续往前，竭力前进，达到完全、成熟就好了。─引用经文
A.　If believers fall away and turn back, there is no need for them to lay the foundation of their Christian life again (Heb. 6:1); there is only the need to go on, to be brought on, to perfection, to maturity.
二　我们基督徒的生活，是一个建筑的工程，也是一个赛程；我们开始之后若跌倒了，不需回到起点再开始；我们只需从跌倒的地方再往前跑。
B.　Our Christian life is a building project and a race; if we fall away after we have begun, we do not need to go back to the beginning and start again; we only need to go on from where we fell.
三　信徒就像田地，吸收了屡次下在其上的雨水，并且生产合式的菜蔬，而不是像田地长出荆棘和蒺藜，就被废弃，近于咒诅，结局就是焚烧─来六7～8：
C.　The believers should be like the earth which drinks the rain which often comes upon it and brings forth proper vegetation, instead of like the earth bringing forth thorns and thistles, which is disapproved and near a curse, whose end is to be burned—Heb. 6:7-8:
１　雨水是指希伯来六章四至五节所说五类美好的事物；生产菜蔬是竭力前进，达到成熟的例证。
1.　The rain refers to the five categories of good things mentioned in Hebrews 6:4-5, and bringing forth vegetation is an illustration of being brought on to maturity.
２　信徒就如同田地，为着神被耕种，好生产基督，如同田地生产菜蔬，以达到完全、成熟，而从神得享祝福─参歌四12～15。
2.　The believers, as the earth, are tilled for God's sake that they might bring forth Christ, as the vegetation, to maturity to partake of blessing from God—cf. S. S. 4:12-15.
３　荆棘和蒺藜，是指希伯来信徒老宗教传统的事物。
3.　The thorns and thistles refer to the traditional things of the Hebrew believers' old religion.
４　田地是绝不会被焚烧的，但其上所长的却会被焚烧；信徒绝不会被焚烧，但他们不按着神的经纶所生长的，却要被焚烧─林前三9，12～13。
4.　The earth could never be burned, but what it brings forth could be burned; the believers could never be burned, but all that they bring forth that is not according to God's economy will be burned—1 Cor. 3:9, 12-13.
肆　主要带领我们往前，达到成熟，但我们必须与祂恩典的工作甘心乐意的合作：
IV.　The Lord wants to bring us on to maturity, but we must willingly cooperate with His gracious work:
一　我们需要与基督同享祂所达到的─来一9，三15。
A.　We need to share with Christ in His attainments—Heb. 1:9; 3:14.
二　我们需要竭力进入那存留之安息日的安息─来四9，11。
B.　We need to be diligent to enter into the remaining Sabbath rest—Heb. 4:9, 11.
三　我们需要来到施恩的宝座前，受怜悯，得恩典─来四16。
C.　We need to come forward to the throne of grace to receive mercy and find grace—Heb. 4:16.
四　我们需要吃干粮，以享受基督照着麦基洗德的等次作我们的大祭司─来五9～10，14。
D.　We need to feed on the solid food to enjoy Christ as our High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec—Heb. 5:9-10, 14.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TWELVE
LET US BE BROUGHT ON TO MATURITY THE WORD OF RIGHTEOUSNESS BRINGING US TO MATURITY
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
In the previous message we covered a marvelous word concerning the beginning of Christ and the word of righteousness. We saw that the writer of the book of Hebrews was burdened that the believers would leave the "kindergarten" stage of the Christian life and be brought on to maturity. In the natural and human realm parents would be quite concerned if their child remained in kindergarten for ten or twenty years. Regrettably, this is the condition today among many Christians. In a certain sense, we should never be content before the Lord with our spiritual attainment; we should always pursue the Lord.
The word of righteousness will bring us to maturity. This word concerns Christ's heavenly ministry and His economy. This is the kind of word being ministered to us in His recovery. The Lord's recovery should always be advancing. As members of the Body of Christ, we should always be advancing, both corporately and personally. Today is a wasted day if we do not gain this day's worth of growth in life. Day by day, we want to grow in life until we reach maturity, not merely to meet our need but also to meet the Lord's need in His economy.
First Timothy 4:15 says, "Practice these things; be in them, that your progress may be manifest to all." We need to practice the things that we have read in these messages. We need to pray them into our being and into our practice. We need to be in the things of this ministry. We need to be immersed in the revelation, truth, and experience of these things. I pray that we will make much progress in life, that our "progress may be manifest to all." It would be a real tragedy to remain the same, year after year. We want to go on each day, each month, and each year until we are fully transformed and arrive at the state of maturity to be the glorious, mature bride of Christ that will bring Him back.
REDEEMING THE TIME BECAUSE THE DAYS ARE EVIL
Ephesians 5:16 speaks of "redeeming the time, because the days are evil." None of us knows how much time he has. Our lives are so short that we have to redeem each moment. In the Recovery Version of the New Testament, Brother Lee points out in footnote 2 of this verse that "in this evil age (Gal. 1:4) every day is an evil day full of pernicious things that cause our time to be used ineffectively, to be reduced, and to be taken away. Therefore, we must walk wisely that we may redeem the time, seizing every available opportunity," The evil, harmful, and pernicious things that damage and destroy our time may not be sinful or may not appear harmful in our eyes. But they destroy the time that God has given us to gain Him. We only have so much time on this earth. Thank the Lord that we are here and that we have today. "Today" is a big word in the book of Hebrews. "Today if you hear His voice, do not harden your hearts." (3:7-8, 15; 4:7). We have today, so do not wait for tomorrow to go on. Do not procrastinate and do not think about the past. Brother Lee told the saints who were migrating to Russia that they had only today and that they did not have tomorrow.
We thank the Lord that we have today and that we can gain Him to the uttermost today. Time passes so quickly. Today you may be in your mid-thirties, but before you realize it, thirty years will have passed and you will be sixty-five. We need to remind one another that we do not have much time, so we must endeavor to redeem the time.
Giving Food at the Proper Time to Receive the Lord's Reward
We do not want to meet the Lord without that extra portion of oil in our soul (Matt. 25:1-13). When we meet the Lord we want to hear Him say, "Well done, good and faithful slave....Enter into the joy of your master" (vv. 21, 23). Matthew 24 and 25 are a word of righteousness. Matthew 24:45-46 says, "Who then is the faithful and prudent slave, whom the master has set over his household to give them food at the proper time? Blessed is that slave whom his master, when he comes, will find so doing." When the Lord comes back, may He find us giving food to God's household. To give others food means that we ourselves must be filled with food. We need to be filled with the milk and we need to be filled with the solid food of the word of righteousness. Then we need to give this food to one another and to the young and new ones among us. Our career is to give food to the Lord's children. If the Lord finds us engaged in this career, then He will reward us when He comes back. There is also a warning given in verse 48, which says, "But if that evil slave says in his heart, My master delays...." We should reject any such consideration in our heart. Whenever there is something in our heart that says, "I have a lot of time; the Lord is not coming or He is coming much later,w e should say, "No! Lord, I want to live in the light of Your appearing." Our career in the Lord's recovery is to give the Lord's people food and to bring Him back. We should never say, "My Master delays.", Instead, we would love His appearing (2 Tim. 4:8).
Matthew 2 describes the magi from the east who had been following the star and took a detour to Jerusalem to ask where the King of the Jews had been born (vv. 1-2). The reaction of Herod and the Jews was quite interesting. "When Herod the king heard this, he was troubled and all Jerusalem with him" (v. 3). Brother Nee once asked, "Why would all of Jerusalem be troubled at the news that the Messiah had been born somewhere in Israel?" They should have been rejoicing; instead, they were troubled. This indicates that many of the believers will be troubled at the Lord's coming. This is very serious. Many will be troubled because they have spent and wasted their lives on things other than Christ and the church. They will miss the reward in the kingdom.
The evil slave in Matthew 24 said in his heart that his master delayed, so he began to beat his fellow slaves (v. 49). This beating of the fellow slaves is to mistreat fellow believers. We should not mistreat, abuse, control, or criticize any of the saints. We are here to give them food. The slave also ate and drank with the drunken. This is to keep company with worldly people and to be drunk with the wine of this world. We should instead be filled with the new, heavenly wine and with the solid food that we could give food to others for their nourishment. The wise virgins in Matthew 25 were those who bought the oil (w. 3-4). We need to be those who pay the price to gain the extra portion of the Spirit in our soul. We also need to be faithful in service, investing the spiritual gift that the Lord has given us for the sake of the kingdom, rather than burying it in the earth (w. 15-18).
Examples of Brothers Who Redeemed Their Time
Once Brother Lee recounted the experience of D. L. Moody on his deathbed, surrounded by a number who had been saved through his preaching. As he was departing, he said, "Good-by, good-by. See you in the kingdom." He was happy and joyful, having seen the fruit of his labor. D. L. Moody realized that he had finished his course. As Brother Lee related this story, he asked, "What about us?" We do not have much time. Recall the tragedy of the space shuttle Challenger in which all the astronauts aboard lost their lives instantaneously due to an explosion. At the time of that tragedy Brother Lee said, "This should be a warning to us and to everyone. We do not know how much time we have, so we must redeem the time" (see The Way to Practice the Lord's Present Move, p. 127).
May the Lord have mercy on us that we would not waste our time. Consider the apostle Paul. According to The Life and Epistles of Saint Paul by Coneybeare and Howsen, Paul was converted around A.D. 36 and was martyred around A.D. 68. This implies that Paul labored approximately thirty-two years. He was an example of a brother who did not waste any time; instead, he spent his time to pursue the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ for the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom. Thirty-two years is not a very long period of time; but he did not waste any time.
Our Brother Abraham Chang was another example to us of how to pursue the Lord. Brother Chang went to be with the Lord during the Living Stream Ministry s Summer Training of 1986 in Irving, Texas. He came into the church when he was more than forty years of age, and the Lord took him after he reached his seventies. Again, the time was not very long, but he made every day count. I never saw Brother Lee weep as he did at Brother Chang's funeral. Brother Lee said,
Throughout the past thirty-three or more years, my appreciation of Brother Chang has been according to the following main items: His absoluteness in seeking and following the Lord and His recovery; number two, his usefulness and faithfulness in the ministry for the Lord's recovery; number three, his constant, unchanging oneness with the ministry; and lastly, his not caring for his own life to the uttermost.
We should pray over each of the above points, especially concerning the first point of seeking and following the Lord and His recovery. When we stand before the judgment seat there will be no excuses.
Surely we are not absolute, but we have an absolute One within us. We can enjoy Him as our absoluteness every day. His drawing power is our pursuing power. We can pray every day, saying, "Lord draw me to the uttermost. Draw me today like You have never drawn me before in my entire life. Your drawing power is my pursuing power." We should also pray concerning our usefulness and faithfulness for the Lord's recovery.
We should pray, "Lord, maximize my usefulness. Make me a useful member in my portion of the Body of Christ. I want to be as useful as You want me to be. By Your mercy constitute Your faithfulness into me in the work of the ministry for Your recovery? We should also pray concerning our constant, unchanging oneness with the ministry. It is our joy to speak the same thing. We do not want to be different. We want to speak the same thing and pursue the one th in g Christ. Just as Brother Chang did not care for his own life to the uttermost, we should be the same. The overcomers do not love their soul-life even unto death (Rev. 12:11). We have such an Overcomer in our spirit. We can take Him as the overcoming One in us.
BEING TRANSFORMED AND BEING MATURED
The Last Stage of Transformation Being Maturity, the Fullness of Life
To be transformed is to be metabolically changed in our natural life, whereas to be matured is to be filled with the divine life that changes us (Heb. 6:1). The last stage of transformation is maturity, the fullness of life. God's eternal purpose can only be accomplished through our transformation and maturity. God's eternal purpose has been greatly frustrated because few Christians have paid attention to transformation and maturity. This is being recovered among us. We want to be transformed, and we want the fullness of life. We want His divine life to fill us until we overflow the divine life into others for the building up of the Body of Christ.
Maturity is a matter of having the divine life imparted into us again and again until we have the fullness of life. The Lord said, "I have come that they may have life and may have it abundantly,5 (John 10:10b).
Second Corinthians 5:4 says, "For also, we who are in this tabernacle groan, being burdened, in that we do not desire to be unclothed, but clothed upon, that what is mortal may be swallowed up by life." In these verses the Greek word for life is zoe. Our prayer day by day should be, "Lord, I want to be swallowed up and filled with zoe. Fill me to the brim. Fill me by Your mercy to overflowing with zoe so that I can be swallowed up by zoe."
Blessing Being the Fullness of Life, the Overflow of Life into Others
The fullness of life is blessing, which is the overflow of life into others (Gen. 47:7, 10; 49:28; 1 John 5:16). The book of Genesis presents a complete portrait of how Jacob passed through a process of transformation and maturity. Genesis begins with God's desire for a man in His image with His dominion to express and represent Him. This is actually the Sabbath rest. God is satisfied in His hearts desire whenever He has a man expressing Him, representing Him, and reigning with Him. Jacob with Joseph was one person to express God with His image and to represent God with His dominion. At the end of Genesis Jacob expressed God and prophesied, speaking for God. His speaking for God was the expression of God. Likewise, Joseph, as the completion of Jacob, was reigning for God over the whole earth. Consequently, we see image and dominion. God's need was being met by a corporate person who had passed through the process of transformation and maturity. This was a Sabbath rest to God, the satisfaction of God's hearts desire. We are here for God's satisfaction in His hearts desire. We are not on this earth for our happiness. Our happiness is God's happiness. If He is not happy, I am not happy. Many Christians only consider what God can do for them. They make themselves the center of the universe, with everything revolving around them. But we are here for God's need to be met.
The Three Stages of Jacob's History Being Our Biography
Jacobs history has to be our biography. We can all identify with Jacob. Chapters twenty-seven, thirty-seven, and forty-seven of Genesis show the three stages of Jacob's history. In Genesis 27 Jacob was a sup- planter, always trying to be first. Even his birth was unique because as a twin he held the heel of his brother Esau while coming out of his mother Rebekah's womb. Jacob, whose name means, "heel holder," tried to pull Esau back. He seemed to be saying, "Get back here! I want to be the first one out." This is the way we were, always trying to be "number one." However, the Lord predestined us to be number two. If we are the first one, we lose the blessing. The blessing goes to the lesser, not the greater. We tried to be first, but the Lord knocked us down.
In chapter thirty-seven Jacob was relatively transformed but not yet mature. It was not until chapter forty-seven that Jacob reached the stage of a mature God-man. He was filled to the brim and overflowing with life. He came to Pharaoh, the ruler of the known world at that time, and blessed him. We know from Hebrews 7:7 that "the lesser is blessed by the greater." If you bless someone, you are greater than that person. Jacob was greater than the ruler of the world in God's sight. He was a mature God-man.
Jacob became a mature God-man through a process that spanned more than forty years. He spent approximately twenty years with Laban, during which time he was transformed through many hardships and sufferings. Then there was another period of approximately twenty years, from the time he was transformed to the time in chapter forty-seven when he became filled to the brim with life. During this time, Jacob left Laban and dwelt in Hebron, which means fellowship. Jacob may have thought that he would "retire" there. He had lost Rachel and had passed though much suffering. However, God came in to upset his "retirement." In chapter thirty-seven, under God's sovereignty, Jacob lost Joseph, the treasure of his heart. Joseph's brothers sold him, and he ended up in Egypt. However, Jacob only knew that wild beasts probably attacked him, based upon the lies told by Jacob's other sons. This was a deep and personal dealing to Jacob. He had already lost Rachel and now he lost the treasure of his heart. How deep and personal this was! This touched his heart so profoundly in order that he might be mature. Though he lost Joseph, the treasure of his heart, he still had the Lord's presence, which no one could take away from him. From this point forward Jacob was like an open jar. Many times the Lord uses things in our environment to open us. We are occupied and preoccupied, and then he touches us in some way to empty us and unload us. Jacob's whole being was open to the Lord without reservation. Nothing preoccupied him or filled him. He remained there as an empty vessel, receiving the heavenly rain until the rain of the divine life filled him to overflowing. He had become a mature God-man.
The fullness of life is blessing, the overflow of life into others. At the end of his life Jacob had a career of blessing people. He blessed Pharaoh, his own children, and his grandchildren. We need to be filled with the divine life to bless others with the divine life for the building up of the Body of Christ. First John 5:16 says, "If anyone sees his brother sinning a sin not unto death, he shall ask and he will give life to him." When we see someone sinning, we need to ask concerning that person and then give life to him. We should always bless people by giving them life. Life is the best medication in the church life. When we see things, we should pray, we should ask, and then we should go and be a zoe dispenser, a life-dispenser. This is blessing. We should do this all the time until we become fully mature and this life is constantly flowing out of us to bless others.
God Using Persons, Things, and Events to Empty Us That We May Be Filled with God
God will sovereignly use persons, things, and events to empty us of everything that has filled us and to take away every preoccupation so that we may have an increased capacity to be filled with God (Luke 1:53; Matt. 5:6). This is in our environment. God will use persons, things, and events just to empty us. He wants to get us to say, "O Lord, I need You. I cannot make it without You. I need You to fill me. Lord, I need You desperately to fill me; otherwise, I have no way to go on." The Lord does this continually in our Christian life. Our whole Christian life is one of repeatedly being emptied and filled. This is how we make progress in life. We exercise to keep ourselves emptied and filled. Second Kings 4:1-7 tells of Elisha and a widow who had nothing left but a jar of oil. Elisha told her to borrow vessels from the neighbors, to shut the door behind her, and to pour her remaining small amount of oil into those empty vessels. The oil kept flowing as long as the empty vessels continued to come. Vessel after vessel was filled with oil until they did not have any more vessels. Then the oil stopped. After reading such a passage, there was a prayer in me, "Lord, may the oil never stop flowing into us." If we are not emptied, the oil has no place to go. If we are full, preoccupied, and filled with our past knowledge, our past history in the church life, or our past successes, the Lord will sovereignly use things to empty us so that we can be filled. We need to exercise to be poor in spirit, to be emptied (Matt. 5:3). If we do not exercise in this way, many times the Lord comes in the environment to empty us. One way or another He has a way to empty us. But we have to cooperate with Him. It is a tragedy if we would not cooperate with Him and say, Lord, I want to open my entire being to You." We need to open without reservation. What the Lord wants is an open vessel. Brother Lee said there are two words that summarize Paul's fourteen Epistles: open vessel. This is what the Lord wants.
Everything Happening to Us for Our Transformation and Maturity
Everything that happens to us is under God's sovereignty for our transformation and maturity; nothing is accidental. Many times things happen to us and we think that this is a big mistake or an accident that should never have happened. We may also consider that what happened to us is wrong or unfair. However, nothing is accidental. When the Lord went to the cross, He could have said that it was unfair. We need to have the view that nothing is accidental. Everything is for us that we may open to Him. We may say to Him, "Lord, I like opening to You and being filled with You. I want to let You be everything to me, to do everything in me, through me, and for me."
A mature believer is one who has learned that God is merciful and all-sufficient to meet his needs in every kind of situation (Gen. 43:14; 17:1; Phil. l:19-21a; 4:11-12). When Jacob sent his sons to get food from Joseph (even though at that time he did not know that it was his son), he said, "And God Almighty give you mercy..." (Gen. 43:14). The Hebrew word for God Almighty, or the all-sufficient God, is El-Shaddai. El means the Almighty One, and Shad means breast or udder. Our God is the Almighty One with a divine udder. The more mature in life a person becomes, the more dependent he is on the Lord for everything. Jacob came to the point of enjoying El-Shaddai, the Nourisher, the Strength-giver, and the Satisfies He was dependent on this One to be his all-sufficient supply.
A mature believers trust and rest are altogether in the mercy of his all-sufficient God, no longer in himself or in his ability. Romans 9:16 says, "It is not of him who wills, nor of him who runs, but of God who shows mercy." Jacob had this kind of declaration at the end of his life. We are here in the Lord's recovery by His mercy. We depend on His mercy day by day.
NEEDING TO CROSS THE RIVER TO BE BROUGHT ON TO MATURITY
The book of Hebrews is a book on maturity, and in order to be brought on to maturity, we need to cross the river. To cross the river is to wash ourselves from anything old or ruined and from anything that does not match His economy.
Crossing River after River
Just as the children of Israel crossed the Red Sea and the Jordan River, we must cross river after river. The water of the Red Sea is a type of the aspect of Christ's death which ends the power of the world, whereas the water of the Jordan represents the aspect of Christ's death which brings our old man to an end (Josh. 4:8-9, 20). All of our difficulties are dealt with in the Jordan River.
When we cross the river out of the wilderness of our soul into Christ as our good land, our Sabbath rest in our spirit, we are one with Him to deal with His difficulty and care for His interests, fighting the spiritual warfare so that we may fully express Him and reign with Him in the church life for the building up of the Body of Christ to consummate the New Jerusalem. There are difficulties on the other side of the Jordan. The flesh is dealt with exhaustively in the wilderness, but after we cross the Jordan, after our old man is dealt with, we can begin to deal with God's difficulty. We should be dealing with His difficulty and caring for His interests by fighting the spiritual warfare. We are not here merely to deal with our difficulties. We want to open to the Lord, allowing Him to get through all the obstacles and difficulties in our being, so that we can cross the river. We cross river after river to eventually deal with His difficulty, that is, to fight the spiritual warfare一those seven tribes in the land. We also desire to build up His temple for His expression in this universe, which is the church.
If we are in ourselves or are individualistic, or if we are trying to be holy, spiritual, or victorious, we are immature. The Lord has to save us from individualism. Suppose you fellowship with some brothers and they all feel one way, yet you feel a different way. If you say, "Well, even though all of you disagree with me, I feel I should still do this," this is to be individualistic. Individualism is a sin that damages the Body. Real maturity is not only in our spirit but also in the church life (Eph. 4:11-13). It is in the fellowship that we learn the lessons of life and get crossed out. There is a practical application of the cross in fellowship with the brothers and sisters. You may want to call a certain hymn in the meeting, and another brother may want to call another hymn. It is beautiful if you would say, "Amen, brother, you call your hymn." Real maturity, the cross, and the Spirit are in the church life. A mature believer knows and cares for the Body of Christ, being Body-conscious and Body-centered (1 Cor. 12:16, 18-19, 21, 24).
Intensively Fleeing for Refuge to Enter within the Veil, to Enter into the Holy of Holies We need to intensively flee (cf. Acts 14:6) for refuge to enter within the veil, to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord as the Forerunner is enthroned in glory (Heb. 6:18-20). The same word for flee is used when Paul and Barnabas fled persecution. The people were about to stone and kill them, so they fled. They did not casually stroll out of town, but they fled intensively. We need to flee in the same way to enter within the veil, to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord as the Forerunner is enthroned in glory.
The significance of this is that we must be in our spirit, where experientially the practical Holy of Holies is today. We have to flee into our spirit. The Lord Jesus as the Forerunner fled everything to enter into the presence of God within the veil. The Lord as the Forerunner was the "Forefleer." To flee is to pursue. In the divine logic fleeing is synonymous with pursuing. The Lord fled His relatives and everything of Judaism into the glorious being of God to become a man in the glory, a man who is God's glory. We need to follow Him to flee from everything other than Christ and the church life into our spirit. Our spirit is the practical Holy of Holies. In 2 Timothy 2:22 Paul charged Timothy to flee youthful lusts. Then he said, "Pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart," While we are fleeing, we are pursuing. We need to flee youthful lusts and pursue in the church life "with those." There are those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart. Thank the Lord that we have "those." Without "those" there is no way. We have to practically be in the Body. We need one another in order to flee every distracting thing in this world and to enter into our spirit where we can enjoy the heavenly Christ in the church life. This fleeing is a real river crossing.
BEING BROUGHT ON TO MATURITY BY PURSUING TOWARD THE FULLEST ENJOYMENT AND GAINING OF CHRIST FOR THE UTTERMOST ENJOYMENT OF CHRIST IN THE MILLENNIAL KINGDOM
We need to go on, to be brought on, to maturity by forgetting the things which are behind and stretching forward to the things which are before, pursuing toward the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ for the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom (Phil. 3:12-15). Paul was one who pursued toward the goal, which is the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ. Our goal for this age is the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ. Our pursuit toward the goal is for the prize, which is the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom. We pursue by forgetting and stretching forward. We need to forget the things which are behind. This is a continual exercise. Even our past experiences of Christ can be a frustration to our present pursuit if we linger in them. Forget your failures and forget your successes. Just go on. Forget and stretch forward to gain Christ.
Believers Who Fall Away and Turn Back Not Needing to Lay the Foundation of Their Christian Life Again, but Only Needing to Go On
If believers fall away and turn back, there is no need for them to lay the foundation of their Christian life again (Heb. 6:1); there is only the need to go on, to be brought on, to perfection, to maturity.
Our Christian Life Being a Building Project and a Race
Our Christian life is a building project and a race; if we fall away after we have begun, we do not need to go back to the beginning and start again; we only need to go on from where we fell.
