If you are running a race and you fall, do you turn around and go back to the beginning of the race? No runner does this. In the Olympics, if a runner falls, he will immediately get up and keep running. When you fall, get up and keep running. We do not have time to waste. We need to confess, be cleansed, and go on! Many Bible readers have misinterpreted this passage to say that it is impossible for someone who falls away to repent. They say that if one falls, he is finished, because he cannot repent again. This is not a logical interpretation. The meaning here is that it is unnecessary for you to repeat again your original repentance at the time you first received the Lord. You have already laid that foundation. Many Christians preach the gospel again and again in their meetings. Often the attendants feel that they must be saved every Lord's Day. These so- called revivals may attempt to lay the same foundation again and again. In the Christian life we do not need to go back to the beginning. We can just pick up from where we are and go on.
The Believers Being like the Earth Which Drinks the Rain Which Falls upon It and Brings Forth Proper Vegetation
The believers should be like the earth which drinks the rain which falls upon it and brings forth proper vegetation, instead of like the earth bringing forth thorns and thistles, which is disapproved and near a curse, whose end is to be burned (Heb. 6:7-8). The rain refers back to the five categories of good things mentioned in Hebrews 6:4-5, and bringing forth vegetation is an illustration of being brought on to maturity. We want to be those who Bring forth the proper vegetation. We can open our being saying, "Lord, I would like to open up my being without reservation. I want to drink You in as the heavenly rain." The believers, as the earth, are tilled for God's sake that they might bring forth Christ, as the vegetation, to maturity to partake of blessing from God (cf. S. S. 4:12-15). The thorns and thistles refer to the traditional things of the Hebrew believers, old religion. The earth could never be burned, but what it brings forth could be burned; the believers could never be burned, but all that they bring forth that is not according to God's economy will be burned (1 Cor. 3:9, 12-13). This burning through fire is mentioned in verse 15, which says, "He himself will be saved, yet so as through fire." Once we receive Christ into us as our life and Savior, we will never suffer eternal perdition. But it is possible to lose the reward in the next age, if we are not faithful to pursue the Lord in this age. We can never lose our eternal salvation, but we can lose our reward and suffer some punishment. We therefore need to cooperate with the Lord. We need to build with gold, silver, and precious stones, not with the things of our natural man. We want to be filled with the Triune God and want Him to flow out of us as the building element to build up the Body of Christ.
COOPERATING WILLINGLY WITH THE LORD'S GRACIOUS WORK THAT HIS DESIRE TO BRING US ON TO MATURITY MAY BE REALIZED
Sharing with Christ in His Attainments The Lord wants to bring us on to maturity, but we must willingly cooperate with His gracious work. We need to share with Christ in His attainments (Heb. 1:9; 3:14). We can do this by having the anointing Spirit in our spirit as the oil of exultant joy.
Being Diligent to Enter into the Remaining Sabbath Rest
We need to be diligent to enter into the remaining Sabbath rest (Heb. 4:9, 11). To do this we need the living and operative word of God to divide our soul from our spirit (v. 12). By this way we can enter the reality of the church life in this age, God's Sabbath rest in this age, so that we could enjoy the Sabbath rest in the millennial kingdom as our reward.
Coming Forward to the Throne of Grace
We need to come forward to the throne of grace to receive mercy and find grace (Heb. 4:16). Our spirit is the house of God, the gate of heaven. Christ as the heavenly ladder (John 1:51) brings heaven to earth and joins earth to heaven. He is the One who joins our spirit to the third heavens. We come forward to the throne of grace by entering into our spirit. In this way we enter through the gate of heaven to touch the throne of grace.
Feeding On the Solid Food to Enjoy Christ as Our High Priest
We need to feed on the solid food to enjoy Christ as our High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec (Heb. 5:9-10, 14), This is to feed on the word of Christ's heavenly ministry. By these points we can realize that we have everything we need to possess Christ as the good land for the building up of His habitation for His expression and for the destruction of His enemy that we might reign with Him on this earth. Sharing Christ's attainments, entering the Sabbath rest, coming forward to the throne of grace, and feeding on the solid food involve the Spirit as the anointing oil in our spirit. These points involve the Spirit, our mingled spirit, the Word, and the church life. The church life is today s Sabbath rest. We therefore need to enter into the church life and give ourselves to the church life.
Possessing Christ as Our All-inclusive Land to Satisfy God's Heart's Desire
Today in the church life, we have every provision that we need. We have the blood, the Word, the Spirit, and the church. The way to maintain our enjoyment of God's organic salvation in His heavenly ministry is to constantly apply the reality of His judicial redemption by applying the precious blood of Christ. Revelation 12:11 says the overcomers overcame the accuser by the blood of the Lamb. None of us should be discouraged. God has given us every provision that we might take and possess Christ as our all-inclusive land to satisfy His hearts desire. We have the blood, the Word, the Spirit, and the church.一E. M.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十三篇　天上的基督─君尊和神圣的大祭司
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来七1～八2
壹　希伯来书是以天上的基督为中心，而关于这位天上的基督，主要的点是说祂作祭司，不是照着亚伦的等次，乃是照着麦基洗德的等次─一3，四14，八1，九24，十二2，五6，10，六20：
一　希伯来书主要是论到基督的祭司职分，一至六章所说到基督的各方面，都是必要的资格，使祂成为君尊和神圣的大祭司，能供应我们一切的需要，并且拯救我们到底─八1，七11，25。
二　希伯来书所启示天上的基督，乃是现时的基督─现在的基督、今日的基督，也是在诸天上之上、宝座上的基督，作我们每日的救恩并时刻的供应─八2，四14～15，七26。
贰　照基督的工作和职事，希伯来书在七章一节有一个大的转，就是从地上的外院转到天上的至圣所─八2，九11～12，24：
一　这个转是从外院的赎罪祭坛转到至圣所里施恩的宝座，就是神与我们相会，与我们说话的地方，也是我们与神在祂经纶里成为一的地方─十三10，十12，四16。
二　这个转是从地上受苦的耶稣转到诸天之上神宝座上得荣耀的基督─七26，十二2，八1。
叁　我们必须经过基督地上职事物质的范围，进入基督天上职事奥秘的范围：
一　“我有负担告诉你们，你们需要进入一个范围、领域、国度，是远高于你们现今所在的范围。这高得多的范围，就是基督天上职事奥秘的范围。”(神圣奥秘的范围，二一页。)
二　这两个范围有一个鲜明的对比：属地的和属天的相对，物质的和奥秘的相对，法理的和生机的相对，以及客观的和主观的相对：
１　基督在祂的肉体里尽祂地上的职事，在物质的范围里，在客观一面完成了神法理的救赎─西一22，来九12～15。
２　基督这赐生命的灵，(林前十五45下，林后三17，)在祂天上的职事里，在奥秘的范围里，在主观一面正在完成神生机的救恩─罗五10。
３　今天我们不是借着在肉体里、在物质范围里之基督地上的职事，而在法理、客观一面蒙拯救；我们乃是借着那是赐生命的灵、在奥秘范围里之基督天上的职事，而在生机、主观一面蒙拯救。
肆　我们若要享受基督天上的职事，在我们的经历中这位天上的基督就必须对我们是客观而主观的─来一3，十三21：
一　基督是客观的，但我们对这位客观的基督可以有主观的经历─罗八34，10，西三1，一27。
二　基督现今是在诸天之上的主，也是在我们里面的那灵─徒　二33上，五31，弗一19～21，林后三17：
１　在诸天之上的主，与在我们灵里的那灵乃是一─林前六17。
２　凡是主在诸天之上执行的，都由祂作为那灵在我们灵里应用到我们身上。
三　伯特利是神的家，也就是天的门，在那里基督是天梯，把地联于天，并把天带到地上─创二八10～17，约一51，弗二22，来四12，16。
伍　得了成全的神的儿子，以不能毁坏之生命的大能构成为君尊和神圣的大祭司，一直在为我们代求，使我们能蒙拯救到底─来七15～17，24～26，28：
一　基督照祂君王的身分是君尊的，照祂神圣的性情是神圣的；这神圣的性情，是把祂构成为神圣大祭司的基本元素─七1，28。
二　基督构成为大祭司，乃是照着不能毁坏之生命大能的元素，这生命是神圣、永远、非受造的生命，也是经过死亡和阴间之试验的复活生命；因此，祂的神圣祭司职分带来生命，并消除死亡─七16，23～25，徒二24，启一18。
三　神圣的大祭司乃是得了成全的神的儿子─来七28：
１　作为神的独生子（约一18，三16），基督不需要成全，因为祂是永远完全的；但作为神的长子（来一6，罗八29），基督已经借着成为肉体、人性生活、钉死十架和复活，得了成全。─引用经文
２　神的长子已经全然得着成全，受到装备，并且够资格作我们神圣的大祭司。
四　基督作我们的大祭司，为我们代求，照顾我们─来七25：
１　祂为我们显在神前，为我们祷告，使我们可以蒙拯救到底，并完全被带进神永远的定旨─来九24，罗八34。
２　主的代求会促使我们里面生命的种子发展、长大并浸透我们的全人，使我们得以变化并模成为祂的形像，至终被带进祂的得荣里─可四26～29，太十三3～8，罗十二2，八29～30，来二10。
五　作我们大祭司的基督乃是天上的执事，把天　(指生命的情形)　供应到我们灵里，使我们有属天的生命、恩典、权柄和能力，在地上过属天的生活─八2。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Thirteen
The Heavenly Christ— a Kingly and Divine High Priest
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 7:1—8:2
I.　The book of Hebrews is focused on the heavenly Christ, and the chief point concerning the heavenly Christ is that He is a Priest not according to the order of Aaron but according to the order of Melchisedec—1:3; 4:14; 8:1; 9:24; 12:2; 5:6, 10; 6:20:
A.　Hebrews is primarily concerned with the priesthood of Christ, and all the aspects of Christ in chapters one through six are the necessary qualifications for Him to be a kingly and divine High Priest, who can minister to us whatever we need and save us to the uttermost—8:1; 7:11, 25.
B.　The heavenly Christ revealed in Hebrews is the present Christ—the Christ now, the Christ today, and the Christ on the throne in the heavens, who is our daily salvation and our moment-by-moment supply—8:2; 4:14-15; 7:26.
II.　According to the work and ministry of Christ, the book of Hebrews makes a great turn in 7:1, a turn from the outer court on earth to the Holy of Holies in heaven—8:2; 9:11-12, 24:
A.　This turn is from the altar for sin in the outer court to the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies, where God meets with us and speaks with us and where we are one with God in His economy—13:10; 10:12; 4:16.
B.　This turn is from the suffering Jesus on earth to the glorified Christ on the throne of God in the heavens—7:26; 12:2; 8:1.
III.　We need to pass through the physical realm of Christ's earthly ministry and enter into the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry:
A.　"I am burdened to tell you that you need to enter into a realm, a sphere, a kingdom, which is much higher than the realm you are in now. This higher realm is the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry" (The Divine and Mystical Realm,?p. 25).
B.　Regarding these two realms, there is a sharp contrast: earthly versus heavenly, physical versus mystical, judicial versus organic, and objective versus subjective:
1.　In His flesh Christ carried out His earthly ministry by accomplishing God's judicial redemption objectively in the physical realm— Col. 1:22; Heb. 9:12-15.
2.　As the life-giving Spirit (1 Cor. 15:45b; 2 Cor. 3:17) Christ in His heavenly ministry is accomplishing God's organic salvation subjectively in the mystical realm— Rom. 5:10.
3.　Today we are not being saved judicially and objectively by Christ in the flesh in the physical realm of His earthly ministry; rather, we are being saved organically and subjectively by Christ as the life-giving Spirit in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry.
IV.　If we would enjoy the heavenly ministry of Christ, the heavenly Christ must become subjectively objective to us in our experience—Heb. 1:3; 13:21:
A.　Christ is objective, but we may have the subjective experience of the objective Christ—Rom. 8:34, 10; Col. 3:1; 1:27.
B.　Christ is now the Lord in the heavens and also the Spirit within us—Acts 2:33a; 5:31; Eph. 1:19-21; 2 Cor. 3:17:
1.　The Lord in the heavens and the Spirit in our spirit are one—1 Cor. 6:17.
2.　Whatever the Lord carries out in the heavens, He applies to us as the Spirit in our spirit.
C.　At Bethel, the house of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the heavenly ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth—Gen. 28:10-17; John 1:51; Eph. 2:22; Heb. 4:12, 16.
V.　As a kingly and divine High Priest constituted with the power of an indestructible life, the perfected Son of God is interceding for us that we may be saved to the uttermost—Heb. 7:15-17, 24-26, 28:
A.　Christ is kingly according to His royal status and divine according to His divine nature, the basic element that constitutes Him to be a divine High Priest—7:1, 28.
B.　Christ has been constituted the High Priest according to the powerful element of an indestructible life, the divine, eternal, uncreated, resurrection life that has passed through death and Hades; therefore, His divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death— 7:16, 23-25; Acts 2:24; Rev. 1:18.
C.　The divine High Priest is the perfected Son of God—Heb. 7:28:
1.　As the only begotten Son of God (John 1:18; 3:16) Christ needed no perfecting, because He is eternally perfect; however, as the firstborn Son of God (Heb. 1:6; Rom. 8:29) He has been perfected through His incarnation, human living, crucifixion, and resurrection.
2.　The firstborn Son of God is completely perfected, equipped, and qualified to be our divine High Priest.
D.　As our High Priest Christ cares for us by interceding for us—Heb. 7:25:
1.　He appears before God on our behalf and prays for us that we may be saved to the uttermost and brought fully into God's eternal purpose—Heb. 9:24; Rom. 8:34.
2.　The Lord's interceding will motivate our inner life-seed to develop, grow, and permeate our whole being that we may be transformed and conformed to His image and ultimately be brought into His glorification—Mark 4:26-29; Matt. 13:3-8; Rom. 12:2; 8:29-30; Heb. 2:10.
E.　As our High Priest Christ is the heavenly Minister, ministering heaven as a condition of life into our spirit that we may have the heavenly life, grace, authority, and power to live a heavenly life on earth—8:2.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十三篇　天上的基督─君尊和神圣的大祭司
The Heavenly Christ— a Kingly and Divine High Priest
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来七1～八2
壹　希伯来书是以天上的基督为中心，而关于这位天上的基督，主要的点是说祂作祭司，不是照着亚伦的等次，乃是照着麦基洗德的等次─一3，四14，八1，九24，十二2，五6，10，六20：
I.　The book of Hebrews is focused on the heavenly Christ, and the chief point concerning the heavenly Christ is that He is a Priest not according to the order of Aaron but according to the order of Melchisedec—1:3; 4:14; 8:1; 9:24; 12:2; 5:6, 10; 6:20:
一　希伯来书主要是论到基督的祭司职分，一至六章所说到基督的各方面，都是必要的资格，使祂成为君尊和神圣的大祭司，能供应我们一切的需要，并且拯救我们到底─八1，七11，25。
A.　Hebrews is primarily concerned with the priesthood of Christ, and all the aspects of Christ in chapters one through six are the necessary qualifications for Him to be a kingly and divine High Priest, who can minister to us whatever we need and save us to the uttermost—8:1; 7:11, 25.
二　希伯来书所启示天上的基督，乃是现时的基督─现在的基督、今日的基督，也是在诸天上之上、宝座上的基督，作我们每日的救恩并时刻的供应─八2，四14～15，七26。
B.　The heavenly Christ revealed in Hebrews is the present Christ—the Christ now, the Christ today, and the Christ on the throne in the heavens, who is our daily salvation and our moment-by-moment supply—8:2; 4:14-15; 7:26.
贰　照基督的工作和职事，希伯来书在七章一节有一个大的转，就是从地上的外院转到天上的至圣所─八2，九11～12，24：
II.　According to the work and ministry of Christ, the book of Hebrews makes a great turn in 7:1, a turn from the outer court on earth to the Holy of Holies in heaven—8:2; 9:11-12, 24:
一　这个转是从外院的赎罪祭坛转到至圣所里施恩的宝座，就是神与我们相会，与我们说话的地方，也是我们与神在祂经纶里成为一的地方─十三10，十12，四16。
A.　This turn is from the altar for sin in the outer court to the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies, where God meets with us and speaks with us and where we are one with God in His economy—13:10; 10:12; 4:16.
二　这个转是从地上受苦的耶稣转到诸天之上神宝座上得荣耀的基督─七26，十二2，八1。
B.　This turn is from the suffering Jesus on earth to the glorified Christ on the throne of God in the heavens—7:26; 12:2; 8:1.
叁　我们必须经过基督地上职事物质的范围，进入基督天上职事奥秘的范围：
III.　We need to pass through the physical realm of Christ's earthly ministry and enter into the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry:
一　“我有负担告诉你们，你们需要进入一个范围、领域、国度，是远高于你们现今所在的范围。这高得多的范围，就是基督天上职事奥秘的范围。”(神圣奥秘的范围，二一页。)
A.　"I am burdened to tell you that you need to enter into a realm, a sphere, a kingdom, which is much higher than the realm you are in now. This higher realm is the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry" (The Divine and Mystical Realm,?p. 25).
二　这两个范围有一个鲜明的对比：属地的和属天的相对，物质的和奥秘的相对，法理的和生机的相对，以及客观的和主观的相对：
B.　Regarding these two realms, there is a sharp contrast: earthly versus heavenly, physical versus mystical, judicial versus organic, and objective versus subjective:
１　基督在祂的肉体里尽祂地上的职事，在物质的范围里，在客观一面完成了神法理的救赎─西一22，来九12～15。
1.　In His flesh Christ carried out His earthly ministry by accomplishing God's judicial redemption objectively in the physical realm— Col. 1:22; Heb. 9:12-15.
２　基督这赐生命的灵，(林前十五45下，林后三17，)在祂天上的职事里，在奥秘的范围里，在主观一面正在完成神生机的救恩─罗五10。
2.　As the life-giving Spirit (1 Cor. 15:45b; 2 Cor. 3:17) Christ in His heavenly ministry is accomplishing God's organic salvation subjectively in the mystical realm— Rom. 5:10.
３　今天我们不是借着在肉体里、在物质范围里之基督地上的职事，而在法理、客观一面蒙拯救；我们乃是借着那是赐生命的灵、在奥秘范围里之基督天上的职事，而在生机、主观一面蒙拯救。
3.　Today we are not being saved judicially and objectively by Christ in the flesh in the physical realm of His earthly ministry; rather, we are being saved organically and subjectively by Christ as the life-giving Spirit in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry.
肆　我们若要享受基督天上的职事，在我们的经历中这位天上的基督就必须对我们是客观而主观的─来一3，十三21：
IV.　If we would enjoy the heavenly ministry of Christ, the heavenly Christ must become subjectively objective to us in our experience—Heb. 1:3; 13:21:
一　基督是客观的，但我们对这位客观的基督可以有主观的经历─罗八34，10，西三1，一27。
A.　Christ is objective, but we may have the subjective experience of the objective Christ—Rom. 8:34, 10; Col. 3:1; 1:27.
二　基督现今是在诸天之上的主，也是在我们里面的那灵─徒　二33上，五31，弗一19～21，林后三17：
B.　Christ is now the Lord in the heavens and also the Spirit within us—Acts 2:33a; 5:31; Eph. 1:19-21; 2 Cor. 3:17:
１　在诸天之上的主，与在我们灵里的那灵乃是一─林前六17。
1.　The Lord in the heavens and the Spirit in our spirit are one—1 Cor. 6:17.
２　凡是主在诸天之上执行的，都由祂作为那灵在我们灵里应用到我们身上。
2.　Whatever the Lord carries out in the heavens, He applies to us as the Spirit in our spirit.
三　伯特利是神的家，也就是天的门，在那里基督是天梯，把地联于天，并把天带到地上─创二八10～17，约一51，弗二22，来四12，16。
C.　At Bethel, the house of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the heavenly ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth—Gen. 28:10-17; John 1:51; Eph. 2:22; Heb. 4:12, 16.
伍　得了成全的神的儿子，以不能毁坏之生命的大能构成为君尊和神圣的大祭司，一直在为我们代求，使我们能蒙拯救到底─来七15～17，24～26，28：
V.　As a kingly and divine High Priest constituted with the power of an indestructible life, the perfected Son of God is interceding for us that we may be saved to the uttermost—Heb. 7:15-17, 24-26, 28:
一　基督照祂君王的身分是君尊的，照祂神圣的性情是神圣的；这神圣的性情，是把祂构成为神圣大祭司的基本元素─七1，28。
A.　Christ is kingly according to His royal status and divine according to His divine nature, the basic element that constitutes Him to be a divine High Priest—7:1, 28.
二　基督构成为大祭司，乃是照着不能毁坏之生命大能的元素，这生命是神圣、永远、非受造的生命，也是经过死亡和阴间之试验的复活生命；因此，祂的神圣祭司职分带来生命，并消除死亡─七16，23～25，徒二24，启一18。
B.　Christ has been constituted the High Priest according to the powerful element of an indestructible life, the divine, eternal, uncreated, resurrection life that has passed through death and Hades; therefore, His divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death— 7:16, 23-25; Acts 2:24; Rev. 1:18.
三　神圣的大祭司乃是得了成全的神的儿子─来七28：
C.　The divine High Priest is the perfected Son of God—Heb. 7:28:
１　作为神的独生子（约一18，三16），基督不需要成全，因为祂是永远完全的；但作为神的长子（来一6，罗八29），基督已经借着成为肉体、人性生活、钉死十架和复活，得了成全。─引用经文
1.　As the only begotten Son of God (John 1:18; 3:16) Christ needed no perfecting, because He is eternally perfect; however, as the firstborn Son of God (Heb. 1:6; Rom. 8:29) He has been perfected through His incarnation, human living, crucifixion, and resurrection.
２　神的长子已经全然得着成全，受到装备，并且够资格作我们神圣的大祭司。
2.　The firstborn Son of God is completely perfected, equipped, and qualified to be our divine High Priest.
四　基督作我们的大祭司，为我们代求，照顾我们─来七25：
D.　As our High Priest Christ cares for us by interceding for us—Heb. 7:25:
１　祂为我们显在神前，为我们祷告，使我们可以蒙拯救到底，并完全被带进神永远的定旨─来九24，罗八34。
1.　He appears before God on our behalf and prays for us that we may be saved to the uttermost and brought fully into God's eternal purpose—Heb. 9:24; Rom. 8:34.
２　主的代求会促使我们里面生命的种子发展、长大并浸透我们的全人，使我们得以变化并模成为祂的形像，至终被带进祂的得荣里─可四26～29，太十三3～8，罗十二2，八29～30，来二10。
2.　The Lord's interceding will motivate our inner life-seed to develop, grow, and permeate our whole being that we may be transformed and conformed to His image and ultimately be brought into His glorification—Mark 4:26-29; Matt. 13:3-8; Rom. 12:2; 8:29-30; Heb. 2:10.
五　作我们大祭司的基督乃是天上的执事，把天　(指生命的情形)　供应到我们灵里，使我们有属天的生命、恩典、权柄和能力，在地上过属天的生活─八2。
E.　As our High Priest Christ is the heavenly Minister, ministering heaven as a condition of life into our spirit that we may have the heavenly life, grace, authority, and power to live a heavenly life on earth—8:2.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE THIRTEEN
THE HEAVENLY CHRIST— A KINGLY AND DIVINE HIGH PRIEST
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Prayer: Lord Jesus, we love You, and we need You every moment.Under Your precious, prevailing, cleansing, covenant-enacting, Satan- conquering blood, we open our whole being to You. We believe that You are the heavenly ladder joining our spirit to the Holy of Holies in the highest heaven. May our prayers ascend and may the heavenly transmission flow down. Lord, we offer this training to You as a burnt offering and as a living sacrifice. Because of Your glorious redemption, we are not here for ourselves, but for You. We turn from every distraction to behold Your giorious face. Lord, we are seeking You, calling on You, and receiving You. Open the heavens and pour down Your blessing, not for our success but for Your Body, Your bride, and the New Jerusalem.
In this message we come to the second section of the crystallization-study of the book of Hebrews. Such a crystallization-study is based on the essence of the Word; it is an attempt to draw forth from the Word as crystals the intrinsic significance of the Word of God.
Hebrews 7 -13 is a profound, deep, and somewhat complex section of the Word; it was written by someone in the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry. The thought is divine, the feeling is heavenly, and the burden is intense. Yet it is matched by an abundant supply of grace from the heavenly High Priest in His present heavenly ministry.
The following four statements comprise the burden for this training: 1) In His heavenly ministry, Christ, the kingly and divine High Priest, is executing the new testament by interceding for us, shepherding us, and ministering Himself into us. 2) The firstborn Son of God works in us as the law of life to "sonize" us, to conform us to His image for His corporate expression. 3) Faith is the unique way to enjoy Christ in His heavenly ministry and to experience all the bequests in the new testament. 4) We need to exercise our spirit that we may enter within the veil to have direct contact with the heavenly Christ, beholding Him to be infused with Him that we may become His corporate reproduction.
CHRIST BEING ROYAL AND KINGLY IN STATUS AND DIVINE IN CONSTITUTION
We have a heavenly Christ who is a kingly and divine High Priest.
He is royal and kingly in status and divine in constitution. He is the King of righteousness and the King of peace. His priesthood will never end. Death will never stop Him. He is a Priest forever in the power of an indestructible life. As the kingly High Priest, He ministers God as life and the life supply into our being. As the divine High Priest, He intercedes for us and. ministers life to us. The presence of the divine High Priest means the absence of death. We can be one with Him that life would prevail and that death would be swallowed up under the impact of His heavenly ministry as the divine High Priest.
The book of Hebrews presents many objective matters concerning Christ who is in the heavens—the emphasis in this book. On one hand, we have a Christ who appears before the face of God for us and who bears us and cares for our situation. On the other hand, He is related to us in a very subjective way as the life-giving Spirit. The book of Hebrews shows us what Christ is doing now and brings us into the realization, experience, and enjoyment of the present Christ, the Christ today, the One who is our daily salvation and moment-by-moment supply.
THE HEAVENLY CHRIST BEING A PRIEST ACCORDING TO THE ORDER OF MELCHISEDEC
The book of Hebrews is focused on the heavenly Christ, and the chief point concerning the heavenly Christ is that He is a Priest not according to the order of Aaron but according to the order of Melchisedec (1:3; 4:14; 9:24; 12:2; 5:6, 10; 6:20). The writer of this book helps us to be clear that the focus of his writing is the heavenly Christ. Hebrews 8:1 says, "Now in the things which are being said the chief point is this: We have such a High Priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens."
In Hebrews 7:11, the two expressions order of Aaron and order of Melchisedec are mentioned in contrast and require some explanation. In the Old Testament with the people of Israel there was a priestly service presided over by a high priest. The first high priest was Aaron; hence, the expression ''priesthood according to Aaron" is used to describe that priesthood. This priesthood deal with the fundamental problem, barrier, and frustration to our fellowship with God— our sin and sins. God provided the sacrifices with the shedding of blood to deal with these problems that we could have fellowship with Him. The Aaronic priesthood, therefore, was a priesthood that offered sacrifices一all of which typified Christ— to deal with sin. Aaron as a high priest was a type of Christ in His earthly ministry, particularly in Christ's ministry and work on the cross. As a priest, typified by Aaron, Christ offered Himself as a sacrifice, typified by the sacrifices, pouring out His blood and obtaining an eternal redemption, dying once for all (9:12, 28a).
Christ is also another kind of priest in His resurrection and ascension一a priest according to the order of Melchisedec (Heb. 5:6, 10). In Genesis 14 a king named Melchisedec, a priest of the most high God, appeared to Abram after he fought for the recovery of Lot (w. 14-16, 18-20). This priest ministered bread and wine to Abram. Melchisedec is a type of Christ in His heavenly ministry (Heb. 7:1-3). Christ is now in the heavens, and He is very concerned with our situation, very aware of our condition and need, and fully conscious of where we are and what we need to experience in order to grow in life. He knows the hunger and thirst in our being. He knows what is in the Fathers heart concerning us, and He is ministering God as life and the life supply into every open spirit among His people. This is the chief and main point of the book of Hebrews. We have a High Priest praying for us, ministering to us, and supplying us. Furthermore, He not only helps us in our suffering but also prays to fully incorporate us into God that He may fully accomplish in us what He has appointed for us. This is Christ as the High Priest according to the order of Melchisedec.
Christ's Qualifications to Be a Kingly and Divine High Priest
Hebrews is primarily concerned with the priesthood of Christ. All the aspects of Christ in chapters one through six are the necessary qualifications for Him to be a kingly and divine High Priest, who can minister to us whatever we need and save us to the uttermost (8:1; 7:11, 25). Christ is the Son of God with divinity (1:8), the Son of Man with humanity (2:6-9), the Author and Captain of our salvation fighting to bring us into glory (v. 10), the Apostle (3:1), and the real Joshua (4:8). Because of these qualifications, He can fulfill His central office in His heavenly ministry to be a High Priest. He is now ministering to us with a dual objective. His first objective is to minister to us whatever we need. He loves to do this, welcomes every opportunity to do this, and is an expert in doing so. We may not know what we need, but He knows and He will minister to us whatever we need. It may not be whatever we want, but it will be whatever we need. As we reflect on our experience, we may offer a simple prayer to the Lord, saying, "Lord, I thank You.
You always gave me what I needed. You are so faithful that when what I wanted conflicted with what I needed, You gave me what I needed.
Lord, thank You for not always giving me what I have wanted. Thank You for giving me what You knew that I needed."
His second objective is to care for God's economy. He knows our need but He also knows God's economy. He wants to save us to the uttermost for God, for His economy, for the Body of Christ, for the bride of Christ, and for the New Jerusalem. His heavenly ministry is more for God than it is for us and our need. We should not be ashamed to talk to Him about our need and to open to Him and converse with Him, making our requests known to Him. Eventually, however, we need a turn, not only once for all but day by day, from our need, problem, and situation to God and His economy. We should pray, "Lord, grow in me for the Body. Gain me for the Body. Save me to the uttermost for the Body. Do it for Yourself and Your economy. Do it for the kingdom of God and for the shame of Your enemy." Thus, we cooperate with Him to connect our present situation with His heavenly ministry that He may minister to our need and save us to meet His need.
Christ on the Throne Being Our Daily Salvation and Moment-by-moment Supply
The heavenly Christ revealed in Hebrews is the present Christ— the Christ now, the Christ today, and the Christ on the throne in the heavens, who is our daily salvation and our moment-by-moment supply (8:2; 4:14-15; 7:26). Many dear brothers and sisters in the Lord have a historical Christ and a future Christ but not a present Christ. All fundamental believers testify that God became a man named Jesus. He was born of a virgin, in fulfillment of prophecy, and lived the life of a God-man mainly in Nazareth. He came forth to minister, He died an all-inclusive redemptive death, He was buried, He resurrected. from the dead physically and spiritually, and He ascended to the heavens. This is our historic faith. This is the historic Christ. Fundamental believers also testify of a future Christ. They believe the word of the angel in Acts 1 when he said, "This Jesus, who has been taken up from you into heaven, will come in the same way as you beheld Him going into heaven" (v. 11). They believe in a future Christ. However, it is quite sad to have a historical Christ and a future Christ but not know the present Christ. What is Christ doing now? How can we see into the heavenly Holy of Holies? How can we go there? There is a practical gate of heaven-our spirit. 'We can enter through this gate and approach the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies.
In this way, we can touch the Christ who is now, the Christ on the throne, the present Christ who is our daily salvation and our moment-by- moment supply. Our destiny is to be one spirit with the Lord all the time. To be detached for any period of time is death. We would not want any organic function of our physical body to "go on vacation.n We would not want our lungs to say, "Give us a break. Just give us ten minutes off. Just give us ten minutes of passivity;" We would protest. Our whole body would rise up to say, "No, don't stop, because we need a constant supply.,, The Lord is interceding for us that we would realize how desperately we need a supply moment by moment. We have spent enough time living in the past and dreaming about the future. Let us live moment by moment. Let us enjoy Christ right now. He is the Christ now, the Christ today, and the present Christ.
It is wonderful to have a Christ in the heavens who is fully aware of every one of us, aware of our need, aware of God's thought concerning us, and wanting to be our moment-by-moment supply. The Lord knows how- open we are. He knows how much we still trust in ourselves, so He intercedes for us and ministers to us. He brings us into particular experiences in order to convince us that we need a moment-by-moment supply. He is not a supply that we cannot bear. We do not eat one months worth of food in one meal. God's way is little by little and day by day. Our destiny is to spend ordinary days in the divine dispensing. He is wonderful in His moment-by-moment supply.
THE WORK AND MINISTRY OF CHRIST TURNING FROM THE OUTER COURT ON EARTH TO THE HOLY OF HOLIES IN HEAVEN
According to the work and ministry of Christ, the book of Hebrews makes a great turn in 7:1, a turn from the outer court on earth to the Holy of Holies in heaven (8:2; 9:11-12, 24). This turn is not merely a matter of doctrine or of truth. The first six chapters of Hebrews unveil the judicial aspect of God's salvation- the redemption of Christ. But at this juncture — Hebrews 7 :1 - there is a great turn from the realm of Christ's earthly ministry to His heavenly ministry. Brother Lee expressed his burden concerning this matter in the Crystallization-study of the Gospel of John, page 137: .
The critical need of the Lord's recovery today is not to be held back by the satisfaction of its judicial section but to press forward to carry out its organic section so that there may be the possibility of accomplishing the eternal economy of God.
The Lord is burdened that His people would have a great turn, from one phase of Christ's ministry on the earth, to another phase of His ministry in the heavens. The churches need this, the Lord's recovery- needs this, and we need this personally, day by day.
In our experience, we begin by applying the precious blood of Christ, the blood of the covenant, taking Christ as the offerings. But we should not remain at the altar in the outer court; our goal should be the ark in the Holy of Holies. Our High Priest is burdened that we would have a deep, intrinsic, experiential turn in our being so that we would be. brought into another realm to know Him in another stage. We need to see that we have a High Priest in the heavens, in the mystical realm, ministering God into our being. He is intensified sevenfold (Rev. 4:5; 5:6), and He wants us to pick up the pace. He wants the growth in life to accelerate. The target of His intercession is the life-seed within us. He is aiming to activate the law of life within us that our growth would match the burden in His being. So we need a turn. We need to pray that we would see the turn and make the turn. We need to pray for one another that we would make this great turn together. Paul made the turn. Brother Nee and Brother Lee also made the turn. Now we are making it together. Those who have made this turn are aware that God's people need to cross the river again, and are beckoning: "Come forward!"
Christ is the Author and Perfecter of our faith (Heb. 12:2). We need our faith perfected. We need to have faith in the blood of Jesus. His blood has opened the way for all of us to enter into the Holy of Holies. We need to put religious humility and self-condemnation under our feet and believe the precious blood of Christ. We have boldness to enter the Holy of Holies by the blood of Jesus at any time. This is our birthright and a new testament bequest. Brother Nee said that we should pray, "O Lord, apply the blood on my behalf according to Your evaluation of it" (The Collected Works of Watchman Nee, Vol. 34, p. 90). We may pray, "Lord, apply the blood to me. Apply the blood to my conscience and to my situation according to Your understanding of the power, worth, value, and effectiveness of Your blood." If we would believe more in the blood and less in our failures, we would be able to make a turn.
God wants us to make a great turn. This turn is from the altar for sin in the outer court to the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies, where God meets with us and speaks with us, and where we are one with God in His economy (13:10; 10:12; 4:16). God's economy cannot be fulfilled at the altar in the outer court. God's economy is fulfilled at the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies. The blood of Jesus has opened the way for us to come to the throne of grace to receive mercy and grace in time of need. This turn is from the suffering Jesus on earth to the glorified Christ on the throne of God in the heavens (7:26; 12:2; 8:1).
PASSING THROUGH THE PHYSICAL REALM OF CHRIST'S EARTHLY MINISTRY AND ENTERING INTO THE MYSTICAL REALM OF CHRIST'S HEAVENLY MINISTRY
Entering into the Higher Realm of Christ's Heavenly Ministry
We need to pass through the physical realm of Christ's earthly ministry and enter into the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry. Brother Lee told us that we need a clear view concerning these two realms, and we need to enter into the higher realm of Christ's heavenly ministry (The Divine and Mystical Realm, pp. 24-25):
All the saints in the Lord's recovery, especially the co-workers and the elders, need to have a dear view concerning the physical realm and the mystical realm. The co-workers and the elders, who take the lead in the Lord's recovery, must realize that the Lord's recovery is resting upon their shoulders. What the recovery will be depends upon what they will be. I am heavily burdened about this. Since this is my burden, I cannot give you ordinary teachings. Instead, I must present something particular. You need to know this age and to realize that it is an age of ignorance, a time when Christians are being blinded and held back by traditional theology. Therefore, I am burdened to tell you that you need to enter into a realm, a sphere, a kingdom, which is much higher than the realm you are in now. This higher realm is the mystical realm of Christ's heavenly ministry.
Do not wait until you understand fully. Just respond and say, "Lord, I want to enter in and know this mystical- realm of Your heavenly ministry."
Two Realms— One Earthly, Physical, Judicial, and Objective and the Other Heavenly, Mystical, Organic, and Subjective Regarding these two realms, there is a sharp contrast: earthly versus heavenly, physical versus mystical, judicial versus organic, and objective versus subjective.
Accomplishing God's Judicial Redemption, Objectively in the Physical Realm
In His flesh Christ carried out His earthly ministry by accomplishing God's judicial redemption objectively in the physical realm (Col. 1:22; Heb, 9:12-15), We value and love the judicial redemption that Christ accomplished for us in the physical realm of His earthly ministry.
We love what 1 Peter 2:24 tells us: "Who Himself bore up our sins in His body on the tree." Christ personally bore on the cross in His physical body all our transgressions, iniquities, sins, and mistakes. In the sight of God He was dying on the cross as the unique sinner. He was made in the likeness of the flesh of sin (Rom. 8:3). While He was there on the cross, God made Him sin for us; He who knew no sin became sin that we who were sin might become the righteousness of God in Him (2 Cor. 5:21). He died on the cross as a bronze serpent, as if He were the serpent itself (John 3:14). God gave Him up on the cross for us, and only the Father knows what He suffered there for us, pouring out His blood for our redemption. We praise the Lord for the precious blood and His precious judicial redemption. He will always be the Lamb (John 1:29; Rev. 22:1, 3). He bears on His glorified, resurrected body the imprints of the nails and the marks of the spear (John 20:25-27).
We are not here by our merit, but by the blood of Jesus. We are covered with Him as our righteousness, and God sees us in Christ. Our sins have been forgiven and washed away. We have been justified, reconciled, and sanctified objectively, having been set apart for God. We should never minimize this truth. But when God Himself wants us to go higher, we can minimize this truth by remaining in the stage of God's judicial redemption.
Accomplishing God's Organic Salvation Subjectively as the Life-giving Spirit in the Mystical Realm
As the life-giving Spirit (1 Cor, 15:45b; 2 Cor. 3:17) Christ in His heavenly ministry is accomplishing God's organic salvation subjectively in the mystical realm. (Rom. 5:10), If we remain bound in the physical things and controlled by the physical view, we will hinder the outworking of God's organic salvation. The Lord Himself is in the mystical realm, the realm of the processed and consummated Triune God. He is .ministering in this realm that we would. be saved organically. The more we enter this realm, the more we discover that the Christian life is effortless and that victory is spontaneous. On. one hand, the Christian life is impossible for the natural man. On the other hand, we have the highest life with the highest law installed in our spirit. When we are in the spirit, we are in another realm where everything is ours automatically. In this realm we experience much of God's organic salvation without being aware of it. If we are striving, this means we are in the physical realm, in the self, and under the law. But if we would be where He is, in the reality of the divine things, we will discover that there is a basic law that this life is spontaneous.
We have the divine life in us, and it is going to make us God in life and nature but not in the Godhead. It will make us the Body of Christ in reality, the kingdom of God in manifestation, and the bride of Christ in glory for His satisfaction. This life is going to make us the New Jerusalem. We will be saved to the uttermost. This is in a divine and mystical realm, so we need to help one another by our prayers and through fellowship to see and enter into this realm.
Being Saved Today Organically and Subjectively in the Mystical Realm of Christ's Heavenly Ministry
Today Christ is not saving us judicially and objectively in the flesh in the physical realm of His earthly ministry; rather, we are being saved organically and subjectively by Christ as the life-giving Spirit in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry. If we want to be saved more completely, we can cooperate by praying a little, especially at the start of the day. We may want to pray, "Lord, I thank You for another day to be saved in Your life. You are the High Priest in the mystical realm, and I need Your organic salvation. Lord, I pray that today You would save me organically. Give me a whole day's worth of organic salvation. Lord, I pray today that life would renew, sanctify, transform, conform, glorify, and. build me up in the functioning organic Body of Christ.w If we would pray in this way while taking- a shower, taking a walk, or driving to work, our High Priest would be very happy. This kind of prayer is actually an echo of His intercession for us. He has been trying to get us to talk to Him in such a way for a long time. He has endeavored, to turn us away from our self to His saving indestructible life. When we echo His intercession, even in a small way with very little faith. He happily obliges us with one day, worth of solid, permanent organic salvation.
We, have a heavenly Christ who knows our situation, who knows the Father's heart, and who knows the power of His indestructible life. He knows what this life will do in us. He knows that it will make every one of us the same as God in life and nature but not in the Godhead. We should not believe our temporary condition. We should believe in our eternal destiny, which is to be saved to the uttermost by God's organic salvation administered personally and directly to us by our Melchisedec in His heavenly ministry in the divine and mystical realm.
THE HEAVENLY CHRIST BECOMING SUBJECTIVELY OBJECTIVE TO US IN OUR EXPERIENCE
If we would enjoy the heavenly ministry of Christ, the heavenly Christ must become subjectively objective to us in our experience (Heb. 1:3; 13:21). As more light dawns and we respond more to the heavenly intercession, we will have a fuller appreciation that our Christ is objectively on the throne of God and subjectively the life-giving Spirit in. our spirit. Some may take the objective side to an extreme, only stressing truth and doctrine. We need to know the truth to protect the interest of the riches of God's divinity. We need to speak the high truth for God so that His name would be hallowed and sanctified on the earth. Religion has caused His name to be blasphemed among the unbelievers. We therefore need to know the high truths and be constituted with them for God's interest. We need to know the truths objectively; however, if we stay merely in the objective realm, there is no application and no subjective reality.
We need a subjective application of the objective reality. We need to have a subjective experience of Christ. However, some may go to the extreme of emphasizing their subjective experiences, without the proper emphasis on the subjective experience of Christ. Everyone has subjective experiences, but these subjective experiences may be of themselves, of their own feelings, of their own views, of their own outlooks, of their own opinions, and of their own biases. If we are naturally subjective, we may become like "frogs in the bottom of a well," spontaneously making ourselves the center of everything. This is not what we mean by the subjective experience of Christ. We are speaking of Christ who is in glory transmitting Himself as the Spirit into our spirit to make Himself personally and subjectively real in us and to us. We can illustrate this by a glass of water. While the water is in the glass, it exists objectively. When I drink the water, it becomes subjective to me and even becomes a part of me.
We should be careful not to limit the revelation by our present personal reality. The revelation and vision will always surpass our personal reality, in the same way that air and water exceed our appropriation of them. If we are subjective and pragmatic, always trying to immediately apply what we see, we may compromise and dilute the revelation. We may not immediately know how a particular revelation will work in our marriage, health, or family, but we know that it will help in God's economy. We should therefore not criticize the release of the divine revelation in the name of personal reality. Brother Lee spoke of this in The Practice of the Church Life according to the God- ordained Way, page 46:
One brother in the past pointed out that if a speaker cannot speak something higher than himself, he is a poor speaker. We must learn to teach and preach things which are higher than what we are. Our reality can never match our revelation. Did Paul's reality match his revelation? Did Paul's experiences come up to his revelation? The Lord Jesus Himself is the only one whose reality fully matched His teaching. Apart from Him, no one on this earth can have the real experience to fully match his own teaching.
We are not here preaching ourselves, "but Christ Jesus as Lord, and ourselves as your slaves for Jesus' sake" (2 Cor. 4:5). We are teaching something high, heavenly, glorious, and beyond our experience, yet we bear witness before the Lord that this is not a mere theory or doctrine. This is a reality that is subjectively objective. However, there is much more to be experienced. We should praise the Lord for what we have not yet experienced. For eternity in the New Jerusalem God will forever flow His being into us, and we will always have fresh experiences of Him.
Having the Subjective Experience of the Objective Christ Christ is objective, but we may have the subjective experience of the objective Christ (Col. 3:1; 1:27). According to Romans 8, Christ is at the right hand of God interceding for us (v. 34), yet He is also in us (v. 10). He is both at the right hand of God and in us. He is both objective and subjective. He is there in heaven interceding, and He is also in our spirit.
Christ Being the Lord in the Heavens and the Spirit within Us
Christ is now the Lord in the heavens and also the Spirit within us (Acts 2:33a; 5:31; Eph. 1:19-21; 2 Cor. 3:17). He has the compound title, "Lord Spirit" (2 Cor. 3:18). When we say, "Lord," we contact the One in the heavens. As the Lord, He is in the heavens, and as the Spirit, He indwells us. He is the Lord Spirit.
The Lord in the heavens and the Spirit in our spirit are one (1 Cor. 6:17). Christ is actually in our spirit; at the same time He is in the heavenly Holy of Holies as the High Priest ministering Himself into our spirit. Whatever the Lord carries out in the heavens, He applies to us as the Spirit in our spirit. There is much on His heart in the heavens. He prays, ministers, and then governs and administers the environment to work out His burden and desire.
Christ Being the Heavenly Ladder to Join Earth to Heaven and to Bring Heaven to Earth
At Bethel, the house of God, which is the gate of heaven, Christ is the heavenly ladder that joins earth to heaven and brings heaven to earth (Gen. 28:10-17; John 1:51; Eph. 2:22; Heb. 4:12,16). When Jacob was fleeing from his brother Esau, he came to a certain place at night and found a stone, which he took for a pillow. While he slept, he had a dream in which he saw heaven opened with a ladder that was on the earth and whose top reached up into the heavens, with the angels of God ascending and descending upon it. Then he woke up in amazement and said, Know dreadful is this place! this is none other but the house of God, and this is the gate of heaven" (Gen. 28:17). The New Testament Bethel, which in Hebrew means the house of God, is the church (1 Tim. 3:15). Bethel, the dwelling place of God, is also our spirit (Eph. 2:22). Our spirit is Bethel, connected by Christ as the heavenly ladder to the Holy of Holies in the heavens. Our spirit is also the gate of heaven today (see note 1 of Heb. 4:16). When we turn to our spirit, we pass through the gate and enter the heavenly realm. I believe the Lord will shepherd us into a realization that we can go into the heavens where He is by going to our spirit. He will bring us into the realization that our spirit is Bethel, the house of God, the gate of heaven, and that there is a ladder-the Son of Man- who connects us personally to Himself in the heavens. The dividing of soul and spirit is so crucial because it enables us to be in the spirit to experience all the reality of what Christ is doing in His heavenly ministry. This is the moment-by-moment supply, the Christ now, the present Christ, the Christ as the kingly and divine High Priest, meeting our need and saving us to the uttermost. He Himself as the Spirit applies what He ministers because He is the heavenly ladder.
Christ is our heavenly ladder. We cannot see Him or feel Him, but He really is a heavenly ladder. The more we experience Him as a heavenly ladder, the more we become the practical embodiment in miniature of a heavenly ladder. When we go for fellowship and open in our spirit to another brother who is in spirit, that brother is a "little Bethel" and in our fellowship we climb up into God. When we come together to meet in a small group, we realize that there is a heavenly ladder in our midst. When we call "O Lord Jesus,n we climb to the third heavens.
THE PERFECTED SON OF GOD INTERCEDING FOR US THAT WE MAY SAVED TO THE UTTERMOST
As a kingly and divine High Priest constituted with the power of an indestructible life, the perfected Son of God is interceding for us that we may be saved to the uttermost (Heb. 7:15-17, 24-26, 28). Regardless of our feeling, we affirm by faith that our High Priest is interceding for each one of us, for every church, and for every brother and sister, regardless of our condition. He will continue to intercede until every one of His brothers is saved to the uttermost.
Christ Being Kingly according to His Royal Status and Divine according to His Divine Nature
Christ is kingly according to His royal status and divine according to His divine nature, the basic element that constitutes Him to be a divine High Priest (Heb. 7:1, 28). The more we realize concerning Christ, the more our contact with Him will be enriched. Our appreciation of Christ as Melchisedec may be limited temporarily, but the truth will prevail. As the Lord intercedes for us, this word will gradually dawn on us, and we will remember more frequently during the day that we have a High Priest. We will also experience interruptions. A brother may begin to exchange words with his wife when something flows in from somewhere to subdue him, causing him to stop. This happens because our High Priest has been watching us, praying for us, and supplying us that the law of life may be activated in each one of us.
His Divine Priesthood Being the Presence of Life and the Absence of Death
Christ has been constituted the High Priest according to the powerful element of an indestructible life, the divine, eternal, uncreated, resurrection life that has passed through death and Hades; therefore, His divine priesthood is the presence of life and the absence of death (Heb. 7:16, 23-25; Acts 2:24; Rev. 1:18). We have a High Priest who conquered death, who swallowed up death, and who has passed through death. We can therefore by faith declare that death will not prevail in us, in our marriage, in our children, in our family, in the church, or in the Lord's recovery. The Lord said, "I will build My church, and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it" (Matt. 16:18). He is ministering His death-defeating life, and where He is as the High Priest, death cannot be. We should not try to deal with death directly; instead, we should just let life come in. If we open to our High Priest, life will flow into our being.
The Divine High Priest Being the Perfected Son of God
The divine High Priest is the perfected Son of God (Heb. 7:28). As the only begotten Son of God (John 1:18; 3:16) Christ needed no perfecting, because He is eternally perfect; however, as the firstborn Son of God (Heb. 1:6; Rom. 8:29), He has been perfected through His incarnation, human living, crucifixion, and resurrection. The firstborn Son of God is completely perfected, equipped, and qualified to be our divine High Priest.
Christ the High Priest Caring for Us by Interceding for Us
As our High Priest, Christ cares for us by interceding for us (Heb. 7:25). He appears before God on our behalf and prays for us that we may be saved to the uttermost and brought fully into God's eternal purpose (9:24; Rom. 8:34). We are still in the race because Someone is interceding for us. Christ's intercession activates the life-seed in us, giving us hope when we are hopeless, and giving us faith when we do not have the faith to take the next step. Despite what the enemy has tried to do to us personally and corporately, we are going on in the race until the crown is won. We do not trust in our ability or resolve, but we trust in His indestructible life that is able to save each one of us to the uttermost.
The Lord's interceding will motivate our inner life-seed to develop, grow, and permeate our whole being that we may be transformed and conformed to His image and ultimately be brought into His glorification (Mark 4:26-29; Matt. 13:3-8; Rom. 12:2; 8:29-30; Heb, 2:10).
Christ the Heavenly Minister, Ministering Heaven as a Condition of Life into Our Spirit
As our High Priest, Christ is the heavenly Minister, ministering heaven as a condition of life into our spirit that we may have the heavenly life, grace, authority, and power to live a heavenly life on earth (Heb. 8:2). As a Minister of the true (heavenly) tabernacle, Christ ministers heaven into us that we may have the heavenly life and power to live a heavenly life on earth, as He did while He was here.—R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十四篇　新约（一）其定义与成立
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来八7～13，九1，15～23，十19，太二六27～28，路二二20
壹　基督的血所完成的新约，不仅是约，更是遗命，将基督的死所成就的一切，遗赠给我们─来九15～17：
一　约和遗命相同；只是立约者还活着的时候，那就是约；他若死了，那就是遗命。
二　约是合同，带着一些应许，要为受约的人成就一些事；遗命是遗书，带着一些已成就的事实，遗赠给承受的人。
三　我们若要明白新约，就必须知道应许、起誓、约和遗命的不同：
１　神的约是凭神的应许立定为法的，而应许是尚未经过确立的普通、平常的话─八6。
２　神应许之后，还以起誓为印证；祂凭祂的神格起誓，必成就祂的应许─六13，17。
３　神的应许一经起誓确立，就立即成为神所印证的约─八7～8。
４　立约的那一位死了，使这约得以成为遗命、遗书─九16～17。
四　新约的内容就是整本新约圣经的内容：
１　主在这新遗命里所遗赠给我们的，乃是取用不尽的，这都要借着那灵给我们经历并享受，直到永远─九15。
２　在新约中，神赐给我们赦罪、救恩、生命，和一切属灵、属天、神圣的福分─弗一3。
贰　主耶稣所立的新约，比那借着摩西所立的旧约(第一约)更美─来七22：
一　新约在性质、品质和样式上都是新的─八8～13，路二二20：
１　旧约有瑕疵，是软弱无益的，所以废掉了─来七18，八7。
２　新约是永远的，是永远有功效的─十三20。
二　新约是更美之约─七22，八6：
１　新约是凭更美的应许而立的，也是借着基督更美的祭物和基督更美的血所完成的─耶三一31～34，来八10～12，九23，12，14。
２　这更美之约的大祭司，在更大、更全备的帐幕里，尽更超特的职任─八6，九11。
３　旧约是无生命的，是凭外面字句的律法立的，但新约是由不能毁坏之生命所构成的─七16。
三　旧约是律法的约；新约是恩典的约─七19，十29，十二15，28，十三9，加四22～26。
四　在旧约里，一切都是影儿；在新约里，一切都是实际─来十1。
叁　按神圣的原则，神的约需要供物和血；因此，新约是凭更美的祭物，并凭那说更美之言的血所立定的─九23，十二24：
一　基督是供物，祂也是流出更美的血，以立定新约，并使这约成为满了神圣遗赠之遗书的那一位。
二　在新约里，基督这独一的祭物是一切供物的实际：
１　基督原是活神永远的儿子，成为肉体，作了人子，并且借着永远的灵，将自己献给神，所以祂这祭物比那些祭牲更美─九23，十11。
２　基督把自己当作一个祭献上，祂这独一的祭物，一次永远的对付了罪─九14，十12，一3，二17，七27，九26。
三　新约的成立需要流出神人的血，就是神儿子耶稣的血，使罪得赦─太二六28，路二二20，约壹一7，来九12：
１　没有赦罪，就无法满足神公义的要求，无法使约借此成立。
２　基督的血已经流出，使罪得赦，新约也凭祂的血得以成立─太二六28。
３　这宝血为我们说话，使新约借这血得以成立；因此，这血称为“永约之血”─来十二24，十三20。
四　“这杯是用我血所立的新约，这血是为你们流出来的”─路二二20：
１　主流了血，神立了约，而我们享受杯；在这杯里，神和一切属神的都是我们的分。
２　血是基督为我们所付的代价，约是神为我们所立的契据，杯是我们从神所领受的分。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Fourteen
The New Covenant (1) Its Definition and Enactment
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 8:7-13; 9:1, 15-23; 10:19; Matt. 26:27-28; Luke 22:20
I.　The new covenant consummated with the blood of Christ is not merely a covenant but also a testament with all the things which have been accomplished by the death of Christ bequeathed to us—Heb. 9:15-17:
A.　A covenant and a testament are the same, but when the maker of the covenant is living, it is a covenant, and when he has died, it is a testament.
B.　A covenant is an agreement containing some promises to accomplish certain things for the covenanted people; a testament is a will containing certain accomplished things bequeathed to the inheritor.
C.　If we would understand the new covenant, we need to know the difference between a promise, an oath, a covenant, and a testament:
1.　God's covenant is enacted upon God's promise, which is a common, ordinary word without confirmation—8:6.
2.　After God made His promise, He sealed it with an oath, swearing by His Godhead that His promise would be fulfilled—6:13, 17.
3.　After God's promise had been confirmed by an oath, it immediately became the covenant sealed by God—8:7-8.
4.　The One who made the covenant died so that the covenant might become a testament, a will—9:16-17.
D.　The contents of the new covenant are the contents of the entire New Testament:
1.　The bequests bequeathed to us by the Lord in the new testament are inexhaustible, and they are for us to experience and enjoy through the Spirit for eternity—9:15.
2.　In the new covenant God gives us forgiveness, salvation, life, and all spiritual, heavenly, and divine blessings—Eph. 1:3.
II.　The new covenant, which the Lord Jesus enacted, is better than the old covenant, the first covenant, made through Moses—Heb. 7:22:
A.　The new covenant is new in nature, quality, and form—8:8-13; Luke 22:20:
1.　The old covenant was faulty, weak, and unprofitable; hence, it was annulled—Heb. 7:18; 8:7.
2.　The new covenant is eternal and is eternally effective—13:20.
B.　The new covenant is a better covenant—7:22; 8:6:
1.　The new covenant was enacted upon better promises and consummated with Christ's better sacrifices and the better blood of Christ—Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:10-12; 9:23, 12, 14.
2.　The High Priest of this better covenant ministers with a more excellent ministry in the greater and more perfect tabernacle—8:6; 9:11.
3.　Whereas the old covenant was lifeless, made with the outward law of letters, the new covenant is constituted with the indestructible life—7:16.
C.　The old covenant was a covenant of law; the new covenant is a covenant of grace—7:19; 10:29; 12:15, 28; 13:9; Gal. 4:22-26.
D.　In the old covenant all things were shadows; in the new covenant everything is reality— Heb. 10:1.
III.　According to the divine principle, God's covenant required the offerings and the blood; hence, the new covenant was enacted with the better sacrifices and with the blood that speaks something better—9:23; 12:24:
A.　Christ is the offerings and He is also the One who shed the better blood to enact the new covenant and to make this covenant a will full of divine bequests.
B.　In the new covenant Christ, the unique sacrifice, is the reality of all the offerings:
1.　As Christ is the eternal Son of the living God incarnated to be the Son of Man who offered Himself to God through the eternal Spirit, so His sacrifices are better than those of animals—9:23; 10:11.
2.　Christ offered Himself as one sacrifice, and as the unique sacrifice He has dealt with sins once for all—9:14; 10:12; 1:3; 2:17; 7:27; 9:26.
C.　The enactment of the new covenant required the shedding of the blood of the God-man, the blood of Jesus, the Son of God, for the forgiveness of sins—Matt. 26:28; Luke 22:20; 1 John 1:7; Heb. 9:12:
1.　Without forgiveness of sin there is no way to fulfill the requirement of God's righteousness that by it the covenant may be enacted.
2.　Christ's blood has been shed for the forgiveness of sins, and the new covenant has been enacted with His blood—Matt. 26:28.
3.　This precious blood speaks for us that by it the new covenant may be enacted; hence, it is called "the blood of an eternal covenant"—Heb. 12:24; 13:20.
D.　"This cup is the new covenant established in My blood"—Luke 22:20:
1.　The Lord shed His blood, God established the covenant, and we enjoy the cup, in which God and all that is of Him are our portion.
2.　The blood is the price that Christ paid for us, the covenant is the title deed that God made for us, and the cup is the portion that we receive from God.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十四篇　新约(一)其定义与成立
The New Covenant (1) Its Definition and Enactment
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来八7～13，九1，15～23，十19，太二六27～28，路二二20
壹　基督的血所完成的新约，不仅是约，更是遗命，将基督的死所成就的一切，遗赠给我们─来九15～17：
I.　The new covenant consummated with the blood of Christ is not merely a covenant but also a testament with all the things which have been accomplished by the death of Christ bequeathed to us—Heb. 9:15-17:
一　约和遗命相同；只是立约者还活着的时候，那就是约；他若死了，那就是遗命。
A.　A covenant and a testament are the same, but when the maker of the covenant is living, it is a covenant, and when he has died, it is a testament.
二　约是合同，带着一些应许，要为受约的人成就一些事；遗命是遗书，带着一些已成就的事实，遗赠给承受的人。
B.　A covenant is an agreement containing some promises to accomplish certain things for the covenanted people; a testament is a will containing certain accomplished things bequeathed to the inheritor.
三　我们若要明白新约，就必须知道应许、起誓、约和遗命的不同：
C.　If we would understand the new covenant, we need to know the difference between a promise, an oath, a covenant, and a testament:
１　神的约是凭神的应许立定为法的，而应许是尚未经过确立的普通、平常的话─八6。
1.　God's covenant is enacted upon God's promise, which is a common, ordinary word without confirmation—8:6.
２　神应许之后，还以起誓为印证；祂凭祂的神格起誓，必成就祂的应许─六13，17。
2.　After God made His promise, He sealed it with an oath, swearing by His Godhead that His promise would be fulfilled—6:13, 17.
３　神的应许一经起誓确立，就立即成为神所印证的约─八7～8。
3.　After God's promise had been confirmed by an oath, it immediately became the covenant sealed by God—8:7-8.
４　立约的那一位死了，使这约得以成为遗命、遗书─九16～17。
4.　The One who made the covenant died so that the covenant might become a testament, a will—9:16-17.
四　新约的内容就是整本新约圣经的内容：
D.　The contents of the new covenant are the contents of the entire New Testament:
１　主在这新遗命里所遗赠给我们的，乃是取用不尽的，这都要借着那灵给我们经历并享受，直到永远─九15。
1.　The bequests bequeathed to us by the Lord in the new testament are inexhaustible, and they are for us to experience and enjoy through the Spirit for eternity—9:15.
２　在新约中，神赐给我们赦罪、救恩、生命，和一切属灵、属天、神圣的福分─弗一3。
2.　In the new covenant God gives us forgiveness, salvation, life, and all spiritual, heavenly, and divine blessings—Eph. 1:3.
贰　主耶稣所立的新约，比那借着摩西所立的旧约(第一约)更美─来七22：
II.　The new covenant, which the Lord Jesus enacted, is better than the old covenant, the first covenant, made through Moses—Heb. 7:22:
一　新约在性质、品质和样式上都是新的─八8～13，路二二20：
A.　The new covenant is new in nature, quality, and form—8:8-13; Luke 22:20:
１　旧约有瑕疵，是软弱无益的，所以废掉了─来七18，八7。
1.　The old covenant was faulty, weak, and unprofitable; hence, it was annulled—Heb. 7:18; 8:7.
２　新约是永远的，是永远有功效的─十三20。
2.　The new covenant is eternal and is eternally effective—13:20.
二　新约是更美之约─七22，八6：
B.　The new covenant is a better covenant—7:22; 8:6:
１　新约是凭更美的应许而立的，也是借着基督更美的祭物和基督更美的血所完成的─耶三一31～34，来八10～12，九23，12，14。
1.　The new covenant was enacted upon better promises and consummated with Christ's better sacrifices and the better blood of Christ—Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:10-12; 9:23, 12, 14.
２　这更美之约的大祭司，在更大、更全备的帐幕里，尽更超特的职任─八6，九11。
2.　The High Priest of this better covenant ministers with a more excellent ministry in the greater and more perfect tabernacle—8:6; 9:11.
３　旧约是无生命的，是凭外面字句的律法立的，但新约是由不能毁坏之生命所构成的─七16。
3.　Whereas the old covenant was lifeless, made with the outward law of letters, the new covenant is constituted with the indestructible life—7:16.
三　旧约是律法的约；新约是恩典的约─七19，十29，十二15，28，十三9，加四22～26。
C.　The old covenant was a covenant of law; the new covenant is a covenant of grace—7:19; 10:29; 12:15, 28; 13:9; Gal. 4:22-26.
四　在旧约里，一切都是影儿；在新约里，一切都是实际─来十1。
D.　In the old covenant all things were shadows; in the new covenant everything is reality— Heb. 10:1.
叁　按神圣的原则，神的约需要供物和血；因此，新约是凭更美的祭物，并凭那说更美之言的血所立定的─九23，十二24：
III.　According to the divine principle, God's covenant required the offerings and the blood; hence, the new covenant was enacted with the better sacrifices and with the blood that speaks something better—9:23; 12:24:
一　基督是供物，祂也是流出更美的血，以立定新约，并使这约成为满了神圣遗赠之遗书的那一位。
A.　Christ is the offerings and He is also the One who shed the better blood to enact the new covenant and to make this covenant a will full of divine bequests.
二　在新约里，基督这独一的祭物是一切供物的实际：
B.　In the new covenant Christ, the unique sacrifice, is the reality of all the offerings:
１　基督原是活神永远的儿子，成为肉体，作了人子，并且借着永远的灵，将自己献给神，所以祂这祭物比那些祭牲更美─九23，十11。
1.　As Christ is the eternal Son of the living God incarnated to be the Son of Man who offered Himself to God through the eternal Spirit, so His sacrifices are better than those of animals—9:23; 10:11.
２　基督把自己当作一个祭献上，祂这独一的祭物，一次永远的对付了罪─九14，十12，一3，二17，七27，九26。
2.　Christ offered Himself as one sacrifice, and as the unique sacrifice He has dealt with sins once for all—9:14; 10:12; 1:3; 2:17; 7:27; 9:26.
三　新约的成立需要流出神人的血，就是神儿子耶稣的血，使罪得赦─太二六28，路二二20，约壹一7，来九12：
C.　The enactment of the new covenant required the shedding of the blood of the God-man, the blood of Jesus, the Son of God, for the forgiveness of sins—Matt. 26:28; Luke 22:20; 1 John 1:7; Heb. 9:12:
１　没有赦罪，就无法满足神公义的要求，无法使约借此成立。
1.　Without forgiveness of sin there is no way to fulfill the requirement of God's righteousness that by it the covenant may be enacted.
２　基督的血已经流出，使罪得赦，新约也凭祂的血得以成立─太二六28。
2.　Christ's blood has been shed for the forgiveness of sins, and the new covenant has been enacted with His blood—Matt. 26:28.
３　这宝血为我们说话，使新约借这血得以成立；因此，这血称为“永约之血”─来十二24，十三20。
3.　This precious blood speaks for us that by it the new covenant may be enacted; hence, it is called "the blood of an eternal covenant"—Heb. 12:24; 13:20.
四　“这杯是用我血所立的新约，这血是为你们流出来的”─路二二20：
D.　"This cup is the new covenant established in My blood"—Luke 22:20:
１　主流了血，神立了约，而我们享受杯；在这杯里，神和一切属神的都是我们的分。
1.　The Lord shed His blood, God established the covenant, and we enjoy the cup, in which God and all that is of Him are our portion.
２　血是基督为我们所付的代价，约是神为我们所立的契据，杯是我们从神所领受的分。
2.　The blood is the price that Christ paid for us, the covenant is the title deed that God made for us, and the cup is the portion that we receive from God.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE FOURTEEN
THE NEW COVENANT (1) ITS DEFINITION AND ENACTMENT
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The Lord Jesus enacted the new covenant in His earthly ministry through His judicial redemption in the physical realm. But the enforcement, the execution, the application, of this new covenant is in the mystical realm; it is the main activity of Christ as our High Priest in His heavenly ministry. We must open to the Lord that we may see this new covenant. In Luke 24 the word open is used three times, each in a very significant way. The first mention is in verse 31 where two of the Lord's disciples had their eyes opened to recognize Him as they ate a meal with Him. They did not open their own eyes, but the Lord opened their eyes. Then in verse 32, they spoke of their hearts burning within them while He was opening to them the Scriptures. The last mention of the word open is in verse 45 where, speaking of the disciples, Luke says that the Lord "opened their mind to understand the Scriptures." These three verses indicate that we need our eyes opened, the Scriptures opened, and our minds opened to understand the Scriptures. Thus, as we touch the matter of the new covenant- a great crystal in the book of Hebrews- we also need the Lord to open us in these three ways. If we allow the Lord, He will open our eyes to see Him, open the Scriptures, and open our minds to the Scriptures that we may gradually have a proper, solid, stable, steadfast, and continuing spiritual understanding of this matter.
As an illustration of this new covenant, we may consider what would take place if a distant relative died and left all of his riches to you as bequests in his last will and testament. Perhaps he owned houses, a business, stocks, and many other things. If you were unaware that he left such things to you, then you would be unaffected. But if you knew about these riches and you had the way to apply them, your life would be radically different. You did not work or labor to get anything, but someone loved you and before he died, being mindful of you, he put you into his will. His will has bequests and all of these bequests will become yours. It is a legal matter, a matter of righteousness.
In a similar manner, the Lord has enacted a new covenant. In His resurrection, the new covenant has become the new testament with tremendous riches. But God's enemy with his use of religion has blinded the children of God from the fact of this covenant, from the blessing of the testament. He has distracted them from the present ministry of Christ that is largely devoted to opening our eyes, opening the Scriptures, - and opening our minds that we may see and know the new covenant. If we saw all the bequests in the new covenant, our life would be radically revolutionized. May we see the covenant, know the covenant, and know the covenant-enforcing and covenant-executing Christ so that gradually we can begin to relate to Him as the God who has established a covenant for us.
THE NEW COVENANT CONSUMMATED WITH THE BLOOD OF CHRIST BEING A TESTAMENT
The new covenant consummated with the blood of Christ is not merely a covenant but also a testament with all the things which have been accomplished by the death of Christ bequeathed to us (Heb. 9:15-17).
A Testament Being a Covenant
A covenant and a testament are the same, but when the maker of the covenant is living, it is a covenant, and when he has died, it is a testament. When the Lord was alive on earth, He established the table, saying, "This cup is the new covenant established in My blood" (Luke 22:20). He enacted the covenant. This involved promises and pledges, and it was serious. It was accompanied by an oath. When the Lord died, He poured out His blood, which the Bible calls "the blood of the covenant," to officially and permanently enact the covenant and thereby replace the old covenant and change the dispensation. This was a radical change, a great turn. The Lord Himself changed the dispensation; He replaced the old covenant with a new covenant through His redemptive death. After His death and now in His resurrection and ascension, this covenant is a will, a testament.
A Covenant Being an Agreement Containing Promises and a Testament Being a Will Containing Accomplished Things
A covenant is an agreement containing some promises to accomplish certain things for the covenanted people; a testament is a will containing certain accomplished things bequeathed to the inheritors. When God makes a promise, this should be sufficient. For God it is sufficient. However, we are weak and fallen, and it is not easy for us to be confident to stand on promises. So God goes further to make the promises a covenant and the covenant a testament.
The new testament is applied by faith not by hope. Hope is related to the future. Many of our prayers are prayers of hope. But faith substantiates hope and makes it an accomplished fact. Mark 11:24 says, "All things that you pray and ask, believe that you have received them, and you will have them." In the testament everything is accomplished. John saw the New Jerusalem— a bequest in the New Testament—because in God's eyes it is accomplished (Rev. 21:2). In Romans 8 Paul speaks of our having been glorified, because in God's eyes regeneration, sanctification, renewing, transformation, conformation, and glorification are accomplished facts. These accomplished facts are bequeathed to us, the inheritors. Our being heirs does not depend on us. A certain transaction has made us heirs. It is a legal matter. If we are heirs then whatever is in the will is ours. Our eyes, the Scriptures, and our minds need to be opened so that we may realize that we are joint-heirs with Christ. We are inheritors of the new testament.
Understanding the New Covenant
God's Covenant Being Enacted upon God's Promise
If we would understand the new covenant, we need to know the difference between a promise, an oath, a covenant, and a testament. God's covenant is enacted upon God's promise, which is a common, ordinary word without confirmation (Heb. 8:6). First, there is a word. Then something stronger than a word is a promise. But this promise is given without confirmation; it is simply something declared.
God Making His Promise, Sealing It with an Oath, and Swearing by His Godhead
After God made His promise, He sealed it with an oath, swearing by His Godhead that His promise would be fulfilled (Heb. 6:13, 17). The Lord has sworn and He will not repent. The immutable God has made an unchanging oath, and He has sealed this oath by Himself. God has bound Himself with an oath in order to show how strong His commitment is, how absolute He is, how much He is for us, and how much He understands our weakness and the littleness of our faith. He would not only promise but also seal that promise with an oath, swearing by His Godhead (v. 17). It is impossible for God to lie (v. 18). In this matter He cannot righteously change His mind. He is bound by His righteousness, and He loves for us to remind Him of this. We may pray, "Lord, I am not so sure how I am doing, and I don't know how You feel about me. But I know one thing: You cannot change Your mind. You have to give Yourself to me for my enjoyment right now," God might say, "Amen! I am glad to do this. I want to do this. I have been waiting for quite a long time for you to ask Me to do what I have sworn to do by My eternal Godhead." Thus, by God's promise and oath, we have a solid standing.
God's Promise Having Been Confirmed by an Oath Becoming the Covenant Sealed by God and the Covenant Becoming a Testament
After God's promise had been confirmed by an oath, it immediately became the covenant sealed by God (Heb, 8:7-8). The One who made the covenant died so that the covenant might become a testament, a will (9:16-17).
The Contents of the New Covenant Being the Contents of the Entire New Testament The contents of the new covenant are the contents of the entire New Testament. How we need our minds opened and our thinking changed to regard the New Testament that we read every day as a testament, as a will, given to us by the Triune God, ratified and enacted by the blood of Christ. All the positive things in the New Testament are bequests to us.
The Bequests Bequeathed to Us by the Lord in the New Testament Being Inexhaustible
The bequests bequeathed to us by the Lord in the new testament are inexhaustible, and they are for us to experience and enjoy through the Spirit for eternity (Heb. 9:15). If our experience and enjoyment are based upon a God-given understanding of the testament, and if we realize that our daily experience is activated by the heavenly High Priest, we will spend less time looking at ourselves and worrying about ourselves. We will spend less time begging God to give us what He has already bequeathed to us, and we will spend more time opening to the Lord that each bequest would be brought into our experience and enjoyment. The Lord knows our inexperience. He also knows that there are many things that distract us from experiencing our bequests, so He does not condemn us. Instead, He is interceding for us and ministering Himself to us. He wants us to see what He has bequeathed to us in this wonderful testament. Let us use our days and hours to explore all of our bequests.
God Giving Us All Spiritual, Heavenly, and Divine Blessings in the New Covenant
In the new covenant God gives us forgiveness, salvation, life, and all spiritual, heavenly, and divine blessings (Eph. 1:3).
THE NEW COVENANT BEING BETTER THAN THE OLD COVENANT
The new covenant, which the Lord Jesus enacted, is better than the old covenant, the first covenant, made through Moses (Heb, 7:22). In various ways, many Christians are "Old Testament Christians." They may emphasize a way of prophesying that is like the Old Testament prophets by saying, a Hear ye, My people. Thus saith the Lord… They may consider this kind of speaking as genuine spirituality; but it is "Old Testament spirituality." Others may appreciate the Psalms in a natural way, not discerning what is of the human concept and what is of the divine concept. Some are "Old Testament Christians" in regard to the law. In doctrine they know about grace, but their daily living is full of self-effort and striving. The principle of the law is man trying to fulfill a standard by his own effort. Still others are fond of the beautiful religious symbolism of the high priests, the garments, and the robes. Their religious souls like all these outward things. Today it is really rare and out of the ordinary to find a genuine New Testament Christian. May we endeavor to be new covenant, even New Testament, believers in actuality and practicality.
The New Covenant Being New in Nature, Quality, and Form
The Old Covenant Being Faulty, Weak, and Unprofitable
The new covenant is new in nature, quality, and form (Heb. 8:8-13; Luke 22:20), The old covenant was faulty, weak, and unprofitable; hence, it was annulled (Heb. 7:18; 8:7). We have to be willing for God to annul things in us. The Lord has to annul anything and everything that is not up-to-date and that is not of the new covenant. The Jews had a hard time realizing that the temple, the priests, the sacrifices, the law, and the ordinances were over. They may have said, "We do not understand why we should not go to the temple anymore but meet in houses, in groups, and in vital groups. All our relatives are coming to town for the feasts, and they will have a great time. How can you say that all of those things are over?" It should come as no surprise that the Jews in the book of Acts tried to do away with Paul. We need a thorough annulment of the old covenant in our being so that we can be genuine New Testament believers.
The New Covenant Being Eternal and Being Eternally Effective
The new covenant is eternal and is eternally effective (Heb. 13:20). No matter what our environment is, no matter what is coming, no matter what we are facing, this covenant is always effective. It is eternally effective.
The New Covenant Being a Better Covenant
The New Covenant Being Enacted upon Better Promises
The new covenant is a better covenant (Heb. 7:22; 8:6). It was enacted upon better promises and consummated with Christ's better sacrifices and the better blood of Christ (Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:10-12; 9:23, 12, 14).
The High Priest Ministering with a More Excellent Ministry in the Greater and More Perfect Tabernacle
The High Priest of this better covenant ministers with a more excellent ministry in the greater and more perfect tabernacle (Heb. 8:6; 9:11). In the first century, a strong vision was needed in order to look at the temple and say, "Christ is not here.
