He is in a greater and more perfect tabernacle not made with hands, eternal in the heavens. He is now in the heavenly Holy of Holies."Today we do not need a physical temple that we can feel. This is to be in the physical realm. We must open to the Lord that He may perfect our faith so that we can say, "I do not live in the realm of feeling. I do not live in the realm of physical senses. I live in another realm by the faith infused into me by God Himself." We do not need the outward trappings of religion. We have a better priest in a better tabernacle fulfilling better promises to carry out a better covenant with the better blood of Jesus. Hallelujah!
The Old Covenant Being Lifeless, and the New Covenant Being Constituted with the Indestructible Life
Whereas the old covenant was lifeless, made with the outward law of letters, the new covenant is constituted with the indestructible life (Heb. 7:16). The letter kills, but the Spirit gives life (2 Cor. 3:6). Any time we put others or ourselves under the old covenant of letters, even if the outward behavior improves, there is death. Perhaps you are caring for a few new ones and you cannot wait for the real experience of life to come in. So you may adjust them in order to make them better. After your adjustment, they may not wear certain things or go to certain places. They may be better, but their joy is gone; their spontaneity is gone. The life is gone because the letters have come in. We must learn not to minister the letters to others or to ourselves. We are new covenant Christians with a covenant full of the indestructible life.
The Old Covenant Being a Covenant of Law, and the New Covenant Being a Covenant of Grace
The old covenant was a covenant of law; the new covenant is a covenant of grace (Heb. 7:19; 10:29; 12:15, 28; 13:9; Gal. 4:22-26), Grace means that I cannot do it but the Triune God can do it. Grace means that I cannot be it but the Triune God as my enjoyment can be it. Grace means that I cannot make it but the Triune God can make it. Grace means that I cannot take it but the Triune God, consummated and processed to be the life-giving Spirit dispensed into my spirit, can take it. He can do it, He can be it, He can make it, and He can take it because He is the all- sufficient grace that is never powerless (Hymns, #312). I am not encouraging anyone to be loose with the Lord; but what kind of prayer would you prefer to pray: "Lord if You forgive me this time, I will never do it again. Just show me mercy this one time, and I will never do it again"? Or would you rather pray a more honest prayer, saying, "Lord, I did it. I am without excuse. Forgive me. Unless You live in me, I cannot do otherwise, and the likelihood of my doing it again is very high. I cannot promise You, Lord, that I will be better"? To promise in the way of the first prayer would be the la.w. It is better to be a new covenant Christian, enjoying grace.
All Things in the Old Covenant Being Shadows, and Everything Being Reality in the New Covenant
In the old covenant all things were shadows; in the new covenant everything is reality (Heb. 10:1). Many believers have a conscious hunger and thirst for reality. They crave reality and want the real thing in their daily experience. The nature of the new covenant is to supply us with reality. The Spirit in the new covenant is called the Spirit of reality, and the Spirit of reality guides us into all the reality (John 16:13). We may pray, "Lord, today cause the Spirit of reality to guide me into the reality of the all-inclusive Christ and be this reality to me." After praying such a prayer, we should just go about our day as we normally do. I believe that the Lord will pay much attention to such prayers offered by us, and the Spirit will guide us into the reality, not the shadows. However, we need to come out of the old covenant and let it be annulled.
THE NEW COVENANT BEING ENACTED WITH THE BETTER SACRIFICES AND WITH THE BLOOD THAT SPEAKS BETTER THINGS
According to the divine principle, God's covenant required the offerings and the blood; hence, the new covenant was enacted with the better sacrifices and with the blood that speaks better things (Heb. 9:23; 12:24). The natural man, especially the mind of the educated natural man, does not like this. I once heard a theologian and a scholar of some reputation say, "Any talk about the shedding of blood and one dying for another's sins is immoral." The natural man does not like to hear a verse like Hebrews 9:22, which says, "Without shedding of blood there is no forgiveness." However, the natural man does not set the rules for how God relates to humanity. If we want to be forgiven, if we want the new covenant to be the new testament applied to us, we have to accept the terms by which it was enacted. God requires the sacrifices, the offerings, and the blood. So when a covenant was made with Abraham in Genesis 15:7-18, and when a covenant was made with Israel in Exodus 24:6-8, there were offerings and blood. Therefore, we need to have a heavenly, divine view and appreciation of Christ as the offerings, and we need to have a fresh faith in the blood of Christ.
Romans 3:25 says that Christ was set forth as a propitiation place through faith in His blood. If you have faith in His blood, you will realize that the blood satisfies the righteous requirements of the law symbolized by the tables in the ark. The blood satisfies the requirements of God's holiness indicated by the Holy of Holies. The blood also satisfies the requirements of God's glory, signified by the overshadowing cherubim, who observe and examine to ensure that everything passes the test of God's glory. The blood shed to enact this covenant fulfills all the requirements of God's righteousness, holiness, and glory, and it gives us access to God as the tree of life in the Holy of Holies. We need the Lord to perfect our faith in His blood so that we will have the boldness to come forward to the Holy of Holies. Hebrews 10:19 says, "Having therefore, brothers, boldness for entering the Holy of Holies in the blood of Jesus."
Christ Being the One Who Shed the Better Blood to Enact the New Covenant and Making the New Covenant a Will Full of Divine Bequests
Christ is the offerings and He is also the One who shed the better blood to enact the new covenant and to make this covenant a will full of divine bequests. Christ executes this. He just wants us to believe this. He knows we are not very good at believing, so He is the Author of our faith to give us a start. He also realizes that we are rather feeble, so He wants to perfect our faith in order that the pace of our running the race could be increased.
Christ, the Unique Sacrifice, Being the Reality of All the Offerings
Christ's Sacrifices Being Better Than Those of Animals
In the new covenant Christ, the unique sacrifice, is the reality of all the offerings. As Christ is the eternal Son of the living God incarnated to be the Son of Man who offered Himself to God through the eternal Spirit, so His sacrifices are better than those of animals (Heb. 9:23; 10:11). He offered Himself through the eternal Spirit. The Spirit applies to us the reality, the power, and the efficacy of His precious blood.
Christ Offering Himself as the Unique Sacrifice to Deal with Sins Once for All
Christ offered Himself as one sacrifice, and as the unique sacrifice He has dealt with sins once for all (Heb. 9:14; 10:12; 1:3; 2:17; 7:27; 9:26).
The Enactment of the New Covenant Requiring the Shedding of the Blood of the God-man for the Forgiveness of Sin
The enactment of the new covenant required the shedding of the blood of the God-man, the blood of Jesus, the Son of God, for the forgiveness of sins (Matt. 26:28; Luke 22:20; 1 John 1:7; Heb. 9:12). It had to be the blood of a man or it would not be real blood. It had to be the blood of a God-man so that it would be eternal and unlimited in its efficacy. This is the precious blood of Jesus, the Son of God, shed for the forgiveness of sins. The good news concerning the forgiveness of sins is very precious.
The Requirement of God's Righteousness Being Fulfilled by the Forgiveness of Sins
Without forgiveness of sins there is no way to fulfill the requirement of God's righteousness that by it the covenant may be enacted. The covenant has to be enacted according to righteousness, which requires the forgiveness of sins. But the forgiveness of sins requires that someone die in order to fulfill the demands of the law. The wages of sin is death (Rom. 6:23). The law requires that someone die; the blood must be shed. The God-man died and shed His blood that our sins could be forgiven. The shedding of His blood was not an end in itself, but the way for the covenant to be enacted. We may be shortsighted and see only that our sins are forgiven. But God sees far beyond the forgiveness of sins. Perhaps, He may say, "I have solved the problem of sin, the way is open for My people to contact Me, and the way is open for Me to contact them. Now I have established My covenant with them, and I can do what is on My heart. I can dispense Myself into them; I can have My expression with them. I can make them the same as My firstborn Son."
Christ's Blood Being Shed for the Forgiveness of Sins, and the New Covenant Being Enacted with His Blood
Christ's blood has been shed for the forgiveness of sins, and the new covenant has been enacted with His blood. Matthew 26:28 says, "This is My blood of the covenant, which is being poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins."
The Precious Blood Speaking for Us That by It the New covenant May Be Enacted
This precious blood speaks for us that by it the new covenant may be enacted; hence, it is called "the blood of an eternal covenant" (Heb. 12:24; 13:20), The enemy is talkative. He accuses the brothers day and night (Rev. 12:10). Sometimes, we may wake up late at night or early in the morning under some kind of accusation. This is because of the enemy s accusation day and night. The enemy is speaking. But there is something else speaking; the blood is speaking. The blood of the covenant is speaking, the Spirit responds to this better speaking, and that speaking shuts the mouth of the accuser. "They overcame him because of the blood of the Lamb" (v. 11). This precious blood speaks for us in a particular way that by it the new covenant may be enacted. If our eyes would be opened, our minds opened, and the Scriptures opened to understand the meaning of the blood of the covenant, our daily enjoyment of Christ would be greatly enriched.
The New Covenant Established in the Blood of Christ
Luke 22:20 says, "This cup is the new covenant established in My blood, which is being poured out for you." According to the Gospel of Luke, after the Passover the Lord had another feast in order to establish His table. He spoke of the bread, and then He spoke of the cup (w. 19-20). In the Scriptures a cup means a portion (Psa. 16:5) and a blessing (1 Cor. 10:16), The New Testament reveals two main cups- the cup of wrath and the cup of salvation. Revelation 14:10 speaks of the .cup of God's wrath. The unbelievers, especially during the tribulation, will drink of the cup of God's wrath. All sinners deserve this cup as their portion. When the Lord prayed in Gethsemane, He said, "My Father, if it is possible, let this cup pass from Me; yet not as I will, but as You will" (Matt. 26:39). He struggled in agony until He was certain that the cup, the cross, and the will of God all converged into one. In His prayer, He prepared Himself to carry out the will of God, which was to go to the cross to drink the cup of God's wrath to the bottom. That cup was the cup of the wrath of a holy and righteous God, poured out upon sin and rebellion. In John 18:11, when Peter tried to interfere with the Lord's arrest, the Lord Jesus said，"The cup which the Father has given Me, shall I not drink it?" He tasted death on behalf of everything (Heb. 2:9).
Before going to the cross, however, He spoke of another cup. Matthew 26:27-29, "And He took a cup and gave thanks, and He gave it to them, saying, Drink of it, all of you, for this is My blood of the covenant, which is being poured out for many for the forgiveness of sins. But I say to you, I shall by no means drink of this product of the vine from now on until that day when I drink it new with you in the kingdom of My Father .This product of the vine in the cup signifies the Lord's blood, the blood of the covenant. This cup, which we enjoy at the Lord's table, signifies a blessing-God as our portion for our enjoyment. In a very real sense the story of God's economy is a story of these two cups- the cup of wrath and the cup of salvation. We no longer have the cup of wrath; we have the cup of salvation. This cup is the new covenant, and the content of this cup is God Himself as the all-inclusive blessing given to us for our enjoyment.
The Lord Jesus wanted us to enjoy God to such an extent that He drank the cup of wrath to the bottom. When He was on the cross, He did not take anything to lessen His pain. They offered Him wine mingled with gall to dull His senses, but He would not take it (Matt. 27:34), The wrath of God against sin was directed at Him as our sin offering. The phrase poured out in both Matthew 26:28 and Luke 22:20 indicates the tremendous work He accomplished on the cross. When He was nailed to the cross, He shed blood because He was persecuted, but when His side was pierced, He poured out His lifeblood (John 19:34). He poured out His blood so that there could be a covenant; this covenant is a cup, which signifies God as our portion for our enjoyment. When we come to the Lord's table, we have the bread, which signifies the physical and mystical Body of Christ, and we also have the cup. The liquid in the cup symbolizes the blood of the covenant. The cup itself symbolizes a portion—— God and all that He is for our enjoyment. We, who once were under God's righteous judgment, can now sit around the table of the Lord and drink the cup! What a gospel this is! We have the cup!
The Blood, the Covenant, and the Cup
The Lord shed His blood, God established the covenant, and we enjoy the cup, in which God and all that is of Him are our portion. Whenever we come to the Lord's table, we see a big cup on the table. We cannot replace that cup. When we drink of that cup, we testify that we are drinking in God who is covenanted to us to be our portion based upon the shed blood of the God-man, Jesus Christ. He shed the blood, God enacted the covenant, and we enjoy the cup. This is the highest worship we can give to Him, that is, to enjoy the cup, to bless the cup. No wonder Paul called it the cup of blessing which we bless (1 Cor. 10:16). God is our portion in the new covenant.
The Blood Being the Price That Christ Paid for Us, the Covenant Being the Title Deed That God Made for Us, and the Cup Being the Portion That We Receive front God
The blood is the price that Christ paid for us, the covenant is the title deed that God made for us, and the cup is the portion that we receive from God. Psalm 116:12-13 says, "What shall I return to Jehovah / For all His benefits toward me? 1 1 will take up the cup of salvation / And call upon the name of Jehovah."—R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十五篇　新约（二）其执行与应用
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来七25，九15，七22，八2，十三20，十二1～2，九14，十29来7:25　所以，那借着祂来到神面前的人，祂都能拯救到底；因为祂是长远活着，为他们代求。
壹　基督是在诸天之上坐宝座的升天者，如今在执行新约，就是祂遗赠给我们作遗命的，并在为我们代求，且供应我们，使我们能认识、经历并享受包含在新遗命中的一切遗赠─来十二2，七25，八1～2：
一　新遗命，遗书，因基督的死已经得以生效，且由复活并升天的基督执行并实施。
二　新约已经作为新遗命遗赠给我们，现今基督在祂天上职事奥秘的范围里，正在执行祂所遗赠的。
三　基督现今在诸天之上是活的、神圣的、也是有能力的；祂能在每一细节上执行新遗命，使其中所包含的每项遗赠对我们成为便利、实际的：
１　作为神圣的大祭司，基督借着为我们代求执行新约，为我们祷告，好使我们被带进新约的实际里─七25。
２　作为新约的中保、执行者，基督在祂天上的职事里正在执行新遗命，并在我们里面实施其中的每一项遗赠─八6，九15，十二24。
３　作为新约的保证，基督是其中一切都必成就的凭质；祂担保并保证新遗命的功效─七22。
４　作为真帐幕(天上帐幕)的执事，基督用新遗命的遗赠、福分，服事我们，使新约的事实在我们的经历中有效─八2。
５　作为群羊的大牧人，基督借着祂的牧养，按照神的永约，终极完成新耶路撒冷─十三20。
贰　我们若要接受新约中一切福分的应用，就必须是对基督天上职事有回应的人─来十二1～2，西三1：
一　基督在诸天之上的职事执行新约，需要我们的回应─来七25，四16，十19：
１　历世纪以来，基督一直想要得着一班人，来回应祂在天上的职事，却没有完全成功。
２　靠着主的怜悯和恩典，今天在地上有一班在主恢复里的人，回应基督天上的职事。
３　当元首在诸天之上为我们代求，并供应我们时，我们作为基督的身体，就在地上回应基督天上的职事，配合并反映祂执行新约时所作的─弗一22～23，四15～16。
二　我们的眼必须被开启，看见新遗命，遗书，及其一切遗赠的属天异象─弗一17～18：
１　父应许了一切，主耶稣成就了这一切；如今这一切已成的事实，都列在遗书里，成为给我们的遗赠─路二二20，来九16～17。
２　我们若有属天的异象，看见神一切的福分都是遗书里的遗赠，我们祷告时就不会像可怜的乞丐，乃是荣耀的承受人，凭信领受这些遗赠─罗八17，弗三6，来六17，一14。
３　我们对新遗命若有属天的看见，我们的观念就会改变，我们会有彻底的大改变，并且会癫狂的赞美主─林后五13，启五6～13。
三　我们必须看见，基督的血就是立约的血─来九14，18～22，十29，十三20：
１　在希伯来书里，基督的血特别显著且重要：
a　借着这血，基督一次永远的进入至圣所，就为我们得到了永远的救赎─九12。
b　基督也用这血洁净了诸天和诸天上一切之物─九22～24。
c　这血圣别我们，洁净我们的良心，叫我们事奉活神，并且为我们所说的，比亚伯的血所说的更美─十三12，十29，九14，十二24。
d　我们因这血，得以坦然进入至圣所─十19。
２　基督的血乃是立约的血─十29，十三20。
a　这血是永约之血，更美的新约是用这血立的─十29，十三20，路二二20。
b　立约的血主要的不是为着赦免，乃是为着神要成为我们的分─启二二14。
c　立约的血带我们进入至圣所，也就是说，带我们进到神里面，并完满的享受神作我们的分，从今时直到永远─来十19。
d　立约的血为我们说话，使新约借着这血得以立定；并且在这新约里，神必须将祂自己和祂一切的福分，赐给在基督里本于信接受这约的信徒─十二24。
四　我们必须凭信来到神面前，坦然无惧的来到施恩的宝座前，为要受怜悯，得恩典，并进前来到至圣所，这乃是“因耶稣的血，得以坦然进入至圣所”─七25，十一6，四16，十19～20。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Fifteen
The New Covenant (2) Its Execution and Application
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 7:25; 9:15; 7:22; 8:2; 13:20; 12:1-2; 9:14; 10:29
I.　As the ascended One sitting on the throne in the heavens, Christ is now executing the new covenant, which He has bequeathed to us as a testament, interceding for us and ministering to us that we may realize, experience, and enjoy all the bequests contained in the new testament—Heb. 12:2; 7:25; 8:1-2:
A.　The new testament, the will, has been validated by Christ's death and is being executed and enforced by Christ in His resurrection and ascension.
B.　The new covenant has been bequeathed to us as the new testament, and now, in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry, Christ is executing what He has bequeathed.
C.　Christ is now in the heavens, living, divine, and capable; He is able to execute the new testament in every detail, making every bequest in it available and real to us:
1.　As the divine High Priest, Christ is executing the new covenant by interceding for us, praying that we would be brought into the reality of the new covenant—7:25.
2.　As the Mediator, the Executor, of the new covenant, Christ in His heavenly ministry is executing the new testament and carrying out in us every item of its bequests—8:6; 9:15; 12:24.
3.　As the surety of the new covenant, Christ is the pledge that everything in the new covenant will be fulfilled; He guarantees and ensures the effectiveness of the new testament—7:22.
4.　As the Minister of the true (heavenly) tabernacle, Christ is serving us with the bequests, the blessings, of the new testament, making the facts of the new covenant effective in our experience—8:2.
5.　As the great Shepherd of the sheep, Christ, by His shepherding, is consummating the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant—13:20.
II.　If we would receive the application of all the blessings in the new covenant, we need to be those who respond to Christ's heavenly ministry—Heb. 12:1-2; Col. 3:1:
A.　Christ's ministry in the heavens to execute the new covenant requires our response—Heb. 7:25; 4:16; 10:19:
1.　For centuries Christ has tried without adequate success to gain a group of people to respond to His ministry in the heavens.
2.　By His mercy and grace, there is on earth today a group of people in the recovery responding to the heavenly ministry of Christ.
3.　As the Head is in the heavens interceding for us and ministering to us, we, the Body, are on earth responding to Christ's heavenly ministry, corresponding to and reflecting what He is doing to execute the new covenant—Eph. 1:22-23; 4:15-16.
B.　Our eyes must be opened to see the heavenly vision of the new testament, the will, with all its bequests—Eph. 1:17-18:
1.　The Father promised everything, and the Lord Jesus accomplished everything; now all the accomplished facts have been itemized in the will as our bequests—Luke 22:20; Heb. 9:16-17.
2.　If we have the heavenly vision to see that all God's blessings are bequests in the will, we will pray not as poor beggars but as glorious inheritors, receiving the bequests by faith— Rom. 8:17; Eph. 3:6; Heb. 6:17; 1:14.
3.　If we have the heavenly view of the new testament, our concept will be changed, we will be radically revolutionized, and we will be beside ourselves with praise to the Lord— 2 Cor. 5:13; Rev. 5:6-13.
C.　We need to realize that the blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant—Heb. 9:14, 18-22; 10:29; 13:20:
1.　In the book of Hebrews the blood of Christ is particularly prominent and crucial:
a.　Through this blood Christ entered once for all into the Holy of Holies and obtained an eternal redemption for us— 9:12.
b.　By this blood Christ cleansed the heavens and all things in the heavens—9:22-24.
c.　This blood sanctifies us, purifies our conscience to serve the living God, and speaks something better for us than the blood of Abel—13:12; 10:29; 9:14; 12:24.
d.　In this blood we have boldness for entering the Holy of Holies—10:19.
2.　The blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant—10:29; 13:20:
a.　It is the blood of the eternal covenant, with which the new and better covenant was enacted—10:29; 13:20; Luke 22:20.
b.　The blood of the covenant is not mainly for forgiveness; it is primarily for God to be our portion—Rev. 22:14.
c.　The blood of the covenant brings us into the Holy of Holies; that is, it brings us into God and into the full enjoyment of God as our portion both now and forever—Heb. 10:19.
d.　The blood of the covenant speaks for us, saying that by the blood the new covenant has been enacted and that in this new covenant God must give Himself and all His blessings to the believers in Christ who receive this covenant out of faith— 12:24.
D.　We need to come forward to God in faith, coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace to receive mercy and grace and coming forward to the Holy of Holies, having "boldness for entering the Holy of Holies in the blood of Jesus"—7:25; 11:6; 4:16; 10:19-20.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十五篇　新约(二)其执行与应用
The New Covenant (2) Its Execution and Application
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来七25，九15，七22，八2，十三20，十二1～2，九14，十29来7:25　所以，那借着祂来到神面前的人，祂都能拯救到底；因为祂是长远活着，为他们代求。
壹　基督是在诸天之上坐宝座的升天者，如今在执行新约，就是祂遗赠给我们作遗命的，并在为我们代求，且供应我们，使我们能认识、经历并享受包含在新遗命中的一切遗赠─来十二2，七25，八1～2：
I.　As the ascended One sitting on the throne in the heavens, Christ is now executing the new covenant, which He has bequeathed to us as a testament, interceding for us and ministering to us that we may realize, experience, and enjoy all the bequests contained in the new testament—Heb. 12:2; 7:25; 8:1-2:
一　新遗命，遗书，因基督的死已经得以生效，且由复活并升天的基督执行并实施。
A.　The new testament, the will, has been validated by Christ's death and is being executed and enforced by Christ in His resurrection and ascension.
二　新约已经作为新遗命遗赠给我们，现今基督在祂天上职事奥秘的范围里，正在执行祂所遗赠的。
B.　The new covenant has been bequeathed to us as the new testament, and now, in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry, Christ is executing what He has bequeathed.
三　基督现今在诸天之上是活的、神圣的、也是有能力的；祂能在每一细节上执行新遗命，使其中所包含的每项遗赠对我们成为便利、实际的：
C.　Christ is now in the heavens, living, divine, and capable; He is able to execute the new testament in every detail, making every bequest in it available and real to us:
１　作为神圣的大祭司，基督借着为我们代求执行新约，为我们祷告，好使我们被带进新约的实际里─七25。
1.　As the divine High Priest, Christ is executing the new covenant by interceding for us, praying that we would be brought into the reality of the new covenant—7:25.
２　作为新约的中保、执行者，基督在祂天上的职事里正在执行新遗命，并在我们里面实施其中的每一项遗赠─八6，九15，十二24。
2.　As the Mediator, the Executor, of the new covenant, Christ in His heavenly ministry is executing the new testament and carrying out in us every item of its bequests—8:6; 9:15; 12:24.
３　作为新约的保证，基督是其中一切都必成就的凭质；祂担保并保证新遗命的功效─七22。
3.　As the surety of the new covenant, Christ is the pledge that everything in the new covenant will be fulfilled; He guarantees and ensures the effectiveness of the new testament—7:22.
４　作为真帐幕(天上帐幕)的执事，基督用新遗命的遗赠、福分，服事我们，使新约的事实在我们的经历中有效─八2。
4.　As the Minister of the true (heavenly) tabernacle, Christ is serving us with the bequests, the blessings, of the new testament, making the facts of the new covenant effective in our experience—8:2.
５　作为群羊的大牧人，基督借着祂的牧养，按照神的永约，终极完成新耶路撒冷─十三20。
5.　As the great Shepherd of the sheep, Christ, by His shepherding, is consummating the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant—13:20.
贰　我们若要接受新约中一切福分的应用，就必须是对基督天上职事有回应的人─来十二1～2，西三1：
II.　If we would receive the application of all the blessings in the new covenant, we need to be those who respond to Christ's heavenly ministry—Heb. 12:1-2; Col. 3:1:
一　基督在诸天之上的职事执行新约，需要我们的回应─来七25，四16，十19：
A.　Christ's ministry in the heavens to execute the new covenant requires our response—Heb. 7:25; 4:16; 10:19:
１　历世纪以来，基督一直想要得着一班人，来回应祂在天上的职事，却没有完全成功。
1.　For centuries Christ has tried without adequate success to gain a group of people to respond to His ministry in the heavens.
２　靠着主的怜悯和恩典，今天在地上有一班在主恢复里的人，回应基督天上的职事。
2.　By His mercy and grace, there is on earth today a group of people in the recovery responding to the heavenly ministry of Christ.
３　当元首在诸天之上为我们代求，并供应我们时，我们作为基督的身体，就在地上回应基督天上的职事，配合并反映祂执行新约时所作的─弗一22～23，四15～16。
3.　As the Head is in the heavens interceding for us and ministering to us, we, the Body, are on earth responding to Christ's heavenly ministry, corresponding to and reflecting what He is doing to execute the new covenant—Eph. 1:22-23; 4:15-16.
二　我们的眼必须被开启，看见新遗命，遗书，及其一切遗赠的属天异象─弗一17～18：
B.　Our eyes must be opened to see the heavenly vision of the new testament, the will, with all its bequests—Eph. 1:17-18:
１　父应许了一切，主耶稣成就了这一切；如今这一切已成的事实，都列在遗书里，成为给我们的遗赠─路二二20，来九16～17。
1.　The Father promised everything, and the Lord Jesus accomplished everything; now all the accomplished facts have been itemized in the will as our bequests—Luke 22:20; Heb. 9:16-17.
２　我们若有属天的异象，看见神一切的福分都是遗书里的遗赠，我们祷告时就不会像可怜的乞丐，乃是荣耀的承受人，凭信领受这些遗赠─罗八17，弗三6，来六17，一14。
2.　If we have the heavenly vision to see that all God's blessings are bequests in the will, we will pray not as poor beggars but as glorious inheritors, receiving the bequests by faith— Rom. 8:17; Eph. 3:6; Heb. 6:17; 1:14.
３　我们对新遗命若有属天的看见，我们的观念就会改变，我们会有彻底的大改变，并且会癫狂的赞美主─林后五13，启五6～13。
3.　If we have the heavenly view of the new testament, our concept will be changed, we will be radically revolutionized, and we will be beside ourselves with praise to the Lord— 2 Cor. 5:13; Rev. 5:6-13.
三　我们必须看见，基督的血就是立约的血─来九14，18～22，十29，十三20：
C.　We need to realize that the blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant—Heb. 9:14, 18-22; 10:29; 13:20:
１　在希伯来书里，基督的血特别显著且重要：
1.　In the book of Hebrews the blood of Christ is particularly prominent and crucial:
a　借着这血，基督一次永远的进入至圣所，就为我们得到了永远的救赎─九12。
a.　Through this blood Christ entered once for all into the Holy of Holies and obtained an eternal redemption for us— 9:12.
b　基督也用这血洁净了诸天和诸天上一切之物─九22～24。
b.　By this blood Christ cleansed the heavens and all things in the heavens—9:22-24.
c　这血圣别我们，洁净我们的良心，叫我们事奉活神，并且为我们所说的，比亚伯的血所说的更美─十三12，十29，九14，十二24。
c.　This blood sanctifies us, purifies our conscience to serve the living God, and speaks something better for us than the blood of Abel—13:12; 10:29; 9:14; 12:24.
d　我们因这血，得以坦然进入至圣所─十19。
d.　In this blood we have boldness for entering the Holy of Holies—10:19.
２　基督的血乃是立约的血─十29，十三20。
2.　The blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant—10:29; 13:20:
a　这血是永约之血，更美的新约是用这血立的─十29，十三20，路二二20。
a.　It is the blood of the eternal covenant, with which the new and better covenant was enacted—10:29; 13:20; Luke 22:20.
b　立约的血主要的不是为着赦免，乃是为着神要成为我们的分─启二二14。
b.　The blood of the covenant is not mainly for forgiveness; it is primarily for God to be our portion—Rev. 22:14.
c　立约的血带我们进入至圣所，也就是说，带我们进到神里面，并完满的享受神作我们的分，从今时直到永远─来十19。
c.　The blood of the covenant brings us into the Holy of Holies; that is, it brings us into God and into the full enjoyment of God as our portion both now and forever—Heb. 10:19.
d　立约的血为我们说话，使新约借着这血得以立定；并且在这新约里，神必须将祂自己和祂一切的福分，赐给在基督里本于信接受这约的信徒─十二24。
d.　The blood of the covenant speaks for us, saying that by the blood the new covenant has been enacted and that in this new covenant God must give Himself and all His blessings to the believers in Christ who receive this covenant out of faith— 12:24.
四　我们必须凭信来到神面前，坦然无惧的来到施恩的宝座前，为要受怜悯，得恩典，并进前来到至圣所，这乃是“因耶稣的血，得以坦然进入至圣所”─七25，十一6，四16，十19～20。
D.　We need to come forward to God in faith, coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace to receive mercy and grace and coming forward to the Holy of Holies, having "boldness for entering the Holy of Holies in the blood of Jesus"—7:25; 11:6; 4:16; 10:19-20.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE FIFTEEN
THE NEW COVENANT (2) ITS EXECUTION AND APPLICATION
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Acceding to the divine principle, a covenant requires sacrifices. When those sacrifices are offered, blood is shed; the shedding of blood enacts the covenant. The old covenant, according to Exodus 24, was enacted with the sacrifices and the blood (vv. 6-8). Christ is the reality of the Old testament sacrifices. His blood— the blood of the covenant— is the better blood. He could declare, when He established the table, that the cup is the new covenant in His blood. Our Lord drank the cup of wrath, pouring out His precious blood in His work on the cross. In that outpoured blood, He enacted a covenant. For us, this covenant has become a cup, which signifies a portion. The content of that cup and our eternal portion is God Himself. So the cup is the cup we bless (1 Cor. 10:16), This is the reason that Paul say in Ephesians 1:3, "Blessed be the God and Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has blessed us with every spiritual blessing in the heavenlies in Christ." God has blessed us with Himself as our portion. When we bless the cup，we actually bless God who is our portion.
In brief this is the definition and the enactment of the covenant. However, for centuries, largely due to ignorance and distraction, there has been a great lack in the appropriation of this new covenant the new testament. Therefore, we must realize that the Lord is actively ministering to execute the covenant which has become the testament. The fulfillment of God's economy depends on the execution of the new testament by the heavenly Christ in His heavenly ministry. But there must be a people responding and applying everything that the Lord has accomplished and bequeathed to us. The execution by Christ in His heavenly ministry must be matched by application. Without the execution and the application, the new testament with all of its bequests remains true; but in practicality, nothing happens. That is an intolerable situation. What the Lord has accomplished for us and what He has bequeathed to us must now be applied to us. There is One interceding for all of us at every moment. In His intercession, He is endeavoring to execute and to bring us into every bequest of the new testament for our experience.
CHRIST NOW EXECUTING THE NEW COVENANT
As the ascended One sitting on the throne in the heavens, Christ is now executing the new covenant, which He has bequeathed to us as a testament, interceding for us and ministering to us that we may realize, experience, and enjoy all the bequests contained in the new testament (Heb. 12:2; 7:25; 8:1-2). I appreciate the word interceding. When you intercede for someone, you speak something that the other person cannot speak for himself. He may not be able to speak for himself, or he may not know that he needs to speak for himself. We have Someone not only praying for us but also interceding for us, uttering those things which need to be uttered on our behalf to bring into our experience everything that God desires. He is not only interceding, but He is also ministering to answer His own prayers. As the High Priest, He is interceding; as the heavenly Minister, He is dispensing Himself and all the bequests into us. Such a wonderful process is going on so that we may realize, experience, and enjoy all the bequests.
The New Testament Having Been Validated by Christ's Death and Being Executed and Enforced by Christ in His Resurrection and Ascension
The new testament, the will, has been validated by Christ's death and is being executed and enforced by Christ in His resurrection and ascension.
The New Covenant Having Been Bequeathed to Us as the New Testament, and Christ Executing What He Has Bequeathed
The new covenant has been bequeathed to us as the new testament, and now, in the mystical realm of His heavenly ministry, Christ is executing what He has bequeathed. Many Christians focus too much attention on the physical realm. Although we do not demean the actual, practical, outward, visible, and physical church life, all these physical things are for something mystical, divine, and heavenly. The realm of the new covenant is the divine and mystical realm.
Christ Being Able to Execute the New Testament in Every Detail, Making Every Bequest in It Available and Real to Us
Christ is now in the heavens, living, divine, and capable; He is able to execute the new testament in every detail, making every bequest in it available and real to us. We must have a clear view that Christ is active today. He is active, able, living, divine, and capable to execute the new testament in detail and to make every bequest available and real to us. We should pray, "Lord, make every bequest available and real to me." We should not let the enemy rob us by telling us, "Oh, you should never pray for yourself." We are not here for ourselves; we are here for the Body of Christ. If there is to be a Body of Christ, there must also be a number of brothers and sisters who personally experience the execution of the new testament. We must spend time alone with the Lord, one on one, to pray, "Lord, execute the new testament for me.
Do it in me for the Body. Make all the things I am hearing about the new testament real and experiential to me." Surely He will answer such a prayer.
Christ Executing the New Covenant by Interceding for Us
As the divine High Priest, Christ is executing the new covenant by interceding for us, praying that we would be brought into the reality of the new covenant (Heb. 7:25).
Christ Executing the New Testament and Carrying Out in Us Every Item of Its Bequests
As the Mediator, the Executor, of the new covenant, Christ in His heavenly ministry is executing the new testament and carrying out in us every item of its bequests (Heb. 8:6; 9:15; 12:24), Whenever a will is read, there must be an executor. There has to be someone empowered by the law to carry out the will. It is a shameful thing in human society that family members come together in a greedy way to the reading of a will. Sometimes if they feel that they did not receive enough, they might challenge it or try to negate it. But in the Body of Christ, there is no jealousy. In the Body of Christ, there is no envy. In the Body of Christ, there is no competition. None of the members of my body are competing. My hands are not competing with my feet. My ears are not competing with my eyes. All the members of my body are content to be what they are in the body according to the arrangement of the head of my body. When we are in the divine life, we realize that there is an eternal abundance for everyone. We do not have to envy any- one's portion, function, or measure. If we open up to be flooded with all the bequests and inundated with God as our portion, we will no longer care about so many trivial things. We will only care that God is the cup that we drink. We will be satisfied with our portion. We will never fear that there will be nothing left. This is an eternal portion. The new covenant will be enforced forever, and the bequests are inexhaustible. In the new heaven and the new earth, after a trillion years, the river of water of life will still be flowing out of the throne (Rev. 22:1). God is infinite and inexhaustible and He will ceaselessly flow out His being into us for our exquisite and delightful enjoyment. However, we do need an executor because of a criminal element in the universe. He is an accuser, a thief, a deceiver, and one who veils and blinds God's people. He lies to God's children and says, "You are not worthy; you are not spiritual enough or able enough to receive what God intends for you." For this reason, we need a proper mediator, an executor, one who has the authority to enforce the testament in the face of the enemy. The executor guarantees that we get our portion. This Mediator realizes we need a lot of education, enlightenment, and supply. He realizes that we need our minds and eyes opened so that we can understand what we have in our will. He understands us. The Lord knows how weak we are and how slow we are to understand. So He is ministering what we need that we would see and understand that we have a testament and that He is the Mediator of this testament, carrying out in us every one of its bequests.
Christ as the Surety of the New Covenant Being the Pledge That Everything in the New Covenant Will Be Fulfilled
As the surety of the new covenant, Christ is the pledge that everything in the new covenant will be fulfilled; He guarantees and ensures the effectiveness of the new testament (Heb. 7:22), It is not up to you to make it work. Just pray a little and open up a little. It is His responsibility to ensure the effectiveness of the covenant. He is the guarantee. He has the power, the standing, and the ability to make it work in detail.
Christ Serving Us as the Minister of the True Tabernacle
As the Minister of the true (heavenly) tabernacle, Christ is serving us with the bequests, the blessings, of the new testament, making the facts of the new covenant effective in our experience (Heb. 8:2). According to Luke, in the kingdom the Lord Himself will come and gird Himself and serve His overcomers (12:37), Our serving can only be a response to how He has served us. He is the One who girded Himself, took a towel, and washed the disciples5 feet. Then He said, "If I then, the Lord and the Teacher, have washed your feet, you also ought to wash one another's feet" (John 13:14), What a wonderful person He is! He is interceding, He is executing, He is guaranteeing, He is serving, and He is also shepherding.
Christ Consummating the New Jerusalem by His Shepherding
As the great Shepherd of the sheep, Christ, by His shepherding, is consummating the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant (Heb. 13:20).
RECEIVING THE APPLICATION OF ALL THE BLESSINGS IN THE NEW COVENANT BY RESPONDING TO CHRIST'S HEAVENLY MINISTRY
If we would receive the application of all the blessings in the new covenant, we need to be those who respond to Christ's heavenly ministry (Heb. 12:1; Col. 3:1). The key word is respond. We need a recovery of our ability to respond. We were dead in trespasses and sins, and God took the initiative to enliven us (Eph. 2:4-5). We were totally indifferent and unresponsive. He took the initiative by His life to enable us to respond. Some of us have been trapped in ourselves and trapped in our history, afraid to respond. He knows this, so He intercedes and ministers for this. He will supply us concerning this until we begin to respond in a way that we have never responded before. In one sense, the whole book of Hebrews asks for a response. We should not stay where we are. We should not wander anymore; rather, we must come forward (Heb. 4:16; 10:22). Response is crucial. This does not mean that we do things by our willpower alone. It means that we are not passive. Passivity equals death; it brings in the practical nullification of many things. We have to respond. The Lord needs our response. He will not carry out His economy without His Body. The Head cannot and will not do it without the Body. The Head and the Body must correspond with one another. The Body responding to the Head will carry out God's New Testament economy. This is the principle.
Therefore, we need to be those who respond. We should not only have an occasional action of responding; we should be the kind of people who respond. We should be responding brothers or sisters. I am so glad that all the members of my body are responding members. Any member that does not respond is a problem. The Lord is ministering so that we can respond.
Christ's Ministry in the Heavens to Execute the New Covenant Requiring Our Response
Christ Trying to Gain a Group of People to Respond to His Ministry in the Heavens without Adequate Success
Christ's ministry in the heavens to execute the new covenant requires our response (Heb. 7:25; 4:16; 10:19), For centuries Christ has tried without adequate success to gain a group of people to respond to His ministry in the heavens. During the past few years in the Lord's recovery, have we given Christ the adequate response? Surely we all would say that we have not given Him an adequate response. Yet, He is here for us, and He is ministering to us. He is faithful to us, and He is concerned for us. Even though our response has been less than adequate, He is still here to minister until we respond.
A Group of People in the Recovery Responding to the Heavenly Ministry of Christ
By His mercy and grace, there is on earth today a group of people in the recovery responding to the heavenly ministry of Christ. We know that the saints are responding because many continue to attend the semiannual trainings, and many of the young people are giving themselves to enter the full-time training for two years. Both of these things are matters of response. It is marvelous that in this country (blessed by God but in many ways still very corrupt) there is a small number in the Lord's recovery responding to Him. It is a mercy to be in that number of those who are responding in the Lord's recovery to the present heavenly ministry of Christ.
The Head Interceding for Us and Ministering to Us, and the Body on Earth Responding to Christ's Heavenly Ministry
As the Head is in the heavens interceding for us and ministering to us, we, the Body, are on earth responding to Christ's heavenly ministry, corresponding to and reflecting what He is doing to execute the new covenant (Eph. 1:22-23; 4:15-16). Recently, in a meeting, as we were fellowshipping about Christ as the heavenly High Priest, a pager sounded off. As we considered for a moment, we realized that we all have a spiritual pager,w and that God is trying to "page" us all the Christ in His heavenly ministry is "paging" the members of His Body. We are connected to the heavens and the Body twenty-four hours a day. The Body life is twenty-four hours a day. You may be "paged" at four o'clock in the morning; that is, you may receive a kind of feeling or impression that you need to pray for a brother. Though it may be 3:00 in the afternoon where the brother is and 4:00 in the morning where you are, the Head knows that there is the need for the Body to be a channel of supply to this dear member. So the Head "pages" you. It will be wonderful when together we are responding to "pages" day and night. It may be three o'clock in the afternoon and you just have this feeling that you should call a particular person. You have no reason, just a feeling that will not go away. You may not be aware of what is happening, or you. may be aware and you just wonder whether it is you or the Spirit.
Consider what reality of the Body-life we would have if we all lived in the Spirit, one with the heavenly Christ, and received impressions, burdens, and speaking from Him in our spirit. What would happen if we all responded? How many would be saved? How many people would be vital? How many backsliders would be visited? How many letters would be written? So much would take place if we ordinary members of the Body recognized the heavenly "beeps," Have you not been "paged" by the High Priest? We all are the same, and we all are learners. Many times we have not responded to His "pages." Sometimes we have responded, and the outcome has been amazing. You may call a sister, one whom you do not normally call. Because you do not have any reason to call, you may not know what to say. So you simply say truthfully, a I just had the feeling to call you. That is all." By making the call, you take the small risk of being misunderstood. But when you discover that your call could not have come at a better time, you realize that you became a channel of supply to that saint. This is because the Body exists twenty-four hours a day. No matter how good, the meetings are, they all have to be dismissed. The trainings are wonderful, but they also have to end. The Body, however, is never dismissed. The Body exists all the time.
We must live in the transmission from the Head more and more. Eventually, we need to live in the transmission all the time. Then we are the Body in reality and practicality. The members of my body have the flow of blood all the time, not the majority of the time but all of the time. The Lord wants an organic Body that is always responding to the Head in the heavens. So we are learning to recognize His "pages." We should keep our "pager" on. Whenever we have some activity in our spirit, we must realize that it could be the Head in the heavens desiring to activate our function for the sake of His Body.
The Lord is not ministering in a general way. Our names are on His heart, and He bears us on His shoulders. He cares for and cherishes each one of us, because each one of us is an irreplaceable member of His Body. He wants us to respond to His transmission. He is patient to train us, to disciple us, to teach us, and to divide our soul from our spirit so that we know the difference between an impulse in the soul and the anointing in our spirit.
We are on the physical earth living a practical church life, but we are also in the divine and mystical realm of the organic Body of Christ. We are in contact with the heavenly Christ who is executing the new covenant and transmitting its bequests into us. He is not only transmitting into us, but also through us into others who desperately need Him. Many times the only way that others will receive God is if someone brings God to them. This can be by a phone call, a visit, an e-mail letter, or a fax. Often, we may be that someone. We are members of one another in the Body of Christ, which is in the divine and mystical realm.
Our Eyes Needing to Be Opened to See the Heavenly Vision of the New Testament with All of Its Bequests
Our eyes must be opened to see the heavenly vision of the new testament, the will, with all its bequests (Eph. 1:17-18).
All the Accomplished Facts Having Been Itemized in the Will as Our Bequests
The Father promised everything, and the Lord Jesus accomplished everything; now all the accomplished facts have been itemized in the will as our bequests (Luke 22:20; Heb. 9:16-17). The Bible is a book of bequests. Since the Bible says, "Husbands, love your wives" (Eph. 5:25), we could pray, "Lord, I say amen to Your speaking. I take it as my bequest. You are the wife-loving Christ, and I regard a husband's love for his wife and Christ's love for the church as my bequest. I open to receive a transmission of this bequest into me and through me to my dear wife."
Having the heavenly Vision to See All God's Blessings as Bequests in the Will and Not Praying as Poor Beggars but as Glorious Inheritors, Receiving the Bequests by Faith
If we have the heavenly vision to see that all God's blessings are bequests in the will, we will pray not as poor beggars but as glorious inheritors, receiving the bequests by faith (Rom. 8:17; Eph. 3:6; Heb. 6:17; 1:14). This is Brother Lee's word to us based on his own experience. We are not beggars. My daughter and sons never had to beg for their necessities. Yet, how many begging, moaning, self-loathing, religious prayers have we offered? We may have prayed, "Please God, have mercy on me. Please do this thing for me." In His mercy, He may initially answer such prayers; but also in His mercy, He stops answering such prayers so that eventually we will stop begging. The Father is saying, "My people, stop begging! Claim your inheritance. My Son is the Executor. All that I am and all that I have is all in Him. It is made available to you as countless bequests." Do you need forgiveness? It is a bequest. Do you need cleansing? It is a bequest. Do you need encouragement, strength, energy, power, faith, love, a burden for the gospel, a love for the church, overcoming, the Body, the kingdom, the New Jerusalem? These all are bequests! This is the new testament. The Lord is interceding so that we would realize this, and He is ministering to us so that we would see this and respond.
Our Concept Being Changed, Being Radically Revolutionized, and Being Beside Ourselves with Praise to the Lord by Having the Heavenly View of the New Testament
If we have the heavenly view of the new testament, our concept will be changed, we will be radically revolutionized, and we will be beside ourselves with praise to the Lord (2 Cor. 5:13; Rev. 5:6-13). We should not try to be revolutionized or act revolutionized. We should simply let this reality dawn on us. Let it change our concept, and we will be radically revolutionized. At the root, the core, and the center of our being, there will be a great turn. The turn is not merely outward; it is intrinsic. Gradually, the light will begin to dawn on us that we are heirs.
Realizing That the Blood of Christ Is the Blood of the Covenant
We need to realize that the blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant (Heb. 9:14, 18-22; 10:29; 13:20). It is not that hard to understand that we are defiled and need to be cleansed. We have also done wrong and need to be forgiven. We realize that the blood of Jesus is the means by which we are cleansed. But there is another aspect of the blood that can only be grasped by divine revelation. It is not in the natural understanding. This aspect of the blood is the blood of the covenant. Our concept is that the blood is mainly for us to be forgiven, but God has a different concept, a different thought, and a different view.
The Blood of Christ Being Particularly Prominent and Crucial in the Book of Hebrews
Through His Blood Christ Entering Once for All into the Holy of Holies, Obtaining an Eternal Redemption for Us
In the book of Hebrews the blood of Christ is particularly prominent and crucial. Through this blood Christ entered once for all into the Holy of Holies and obtained an eternal redemption for us (Heb. 9:12). This is the blood for our redemption. We were lost, but through the blood of Christ we have been purchased and returned to God.
Christ Cleansing the Heavens and All Things in the Heavens by His Blood
By this blood Christ cleansed the heavens and all things in the heavens (Heb. 9:22-24). Satan's rebellion caused the defilement of the heavens, so the heavens needed the sprinkling of the blood.
The Blood Sanctifying Us, Purifying Our Conscience, Speaking Something Better Than Abel's Blood, and Our Entrance into the Holy of Holies Being with Boldness by His Blood
This blood sanctifies us, purifies our conscience to serve the living God, and speaks something better for us than the blood of Abel (Heb. 13:12; 10:29; 9:14; 12:24). Hence, in this blood we have boldness for entering the Holy of Holies (10:19).
The Blood of Christ Being the Blood of the Covenant
The blood of Christ is the blood of the covenant (Heb. 10:29; 13:20). This far surpasses our natural understanding.
The Blood Being the Blood of the Eternal Covenant, with Which the New and Better Covenant Was Enacted
It is the blood of the eternal covenant, with which the new and better covenant was enacted (Luke 22:20).
The Blood of the Covenant Not Being Mainly for Forgiveness, but Being Primarily for God to Be Our Portion
The blood of the covenant is not mainly for forgiveness; it is primarily for God to be our portion (Rev. 22:14). We think of the blood mainly for forgiveness, and we surely need to be forgiven. In our experience we will need forgiveness again and again until we are transfigured. But we need to see the blood from God's point of view. It is as if God would say, "I am the highest enjoyment in the universe. It is My desire to open up the depths of My being to you so that you would enjoy Me supremely. I have foreordained and predestinated you to enjoy Me and have bequeathed to you this enjoyment in the New Testament. I want you to enter into Me, to live in Me, to be infused by Me and with Me, to eat and drink of Me, and to enjoy Me forever."
The blood of the covenant is the blood that opens the way for us to go into God and to enjoy Him as our eternal supply. This redeeming blood brings sinful, corrupted people into the enjoyment of God. Because of the covenant, the testament, God is bound to give Himself to us for our enjoyment. When we by faith believe the blood of the covenant, God is bound by this covenant to be our enjoyment. But many times we settle for mere forgiveness, being unwilling to ask for anything more, because we may consider such asking as being too zealous. Oh, if we only knew our Fathers heart! He would say, "I spared you so that I could be everything to you. I forgave you so that you could enjoy Me. Do not stop at the altar. Follow the blood all the way into the Holy of Holies, stay there on the basis of the blood of the covenant, and let Me infuse Myself into you.w The blood of the covenant gives us the ground and the standing to enter into God and to enjoy Him. The blood of the covenant brings us into the presence of God, into the infusion of God, and into the eternal enjoyment of God.
The Blood of the Covenant Bringing Us into God and the Full Enjoyment of God as Our Portion Both Now and Forever
The blood of the covenant brings us into the Holy of Holies; that is, it brings us into God and into the full enjoyment of God as our portion both now and forever. The blood of the covenant does bring us into God's presence, but we should not stop merely with being in the presence of God. God does not want us in His presence only. He wants us to be in Him, in His being, where the shekinah glory of God is permeating everything. Hence, from every angle we are infused with God's radiance until we glow and shine. This is the experience in the Holy of Holies. Hebrews 10:19 says, "Having therefore, brothers, boldness for entering the Holy of Holies in the blood of Jesus." The blood of the covenant brings us into God and into the full enjoyment of God as our portion both now and forever.
The blood in Leviticus 16 typifies the blood of the covenant, which brings us into God. In Exodus 12 the blood was for redemption (w. 7,13) and in Exodus 24 the blood was to enact the covenant (w. 6-8). But in Leviticus 16 the high priest took the blood of the sin offering and brought this blood within the veil and sprinkled it on the propitiation cover (w. 14-15), At that time, the Holy of Holies was a reality, because the shekinah glory was actually there. The high priest entered into the glory and sprinkled the blood; that blood gave him the basis to stay within the Holy of Holies to receive God's speaking and become one with God to carry out His economy. That was the type. The reality is that our High Priest offered Himself on the cross, pouring out His blood (Luke 22:20). As the High Priest He has taken His blood as the sin offering and has entered into the Holy of Holies in the heavens, into the being of God Himself with His blood. Thus, He is saying, "Have faith in My blood of the covenant, take the way that I have opened by being slain for you, and come in with Me into the glorious being of God." This is our right; this is our bequest. This is the new testament. The blood of the covenant is not simply to cleanse us so that we have peace at the altar; it is not simply to cleanse us so that we may enjoy the bread of the presence in the Holy Place; it is to enable us to enter into God and to enjoy God.
The Blood of the Covenant Speaking for Us
The blood of the covenant speaks for us, saying that by the blood the new covenant has been enacted and that in this new covenant God must give Himself and all His blessings to the believers in Christ who receive this covenant out of faith (Heb. 12:24). This blood is speaking.
The covenant has been enacted, and this enacted covenant is the testament executed by our High Priest. Now in this covenant God is bound to give Himself to us .When we come by the blood into the Holy of Holies, God cannot judge us. Judgment was at the altar in the outer court; hence, the matter of judgment has been settled. God can only give Himself to us. The only limitation is what we can presently bear. For example, we cannot take all the electricity generated from the power plant into our individual home. But in our being, God will give us more than we ever dreamed.
Needing to Come Forward to God in Faith
We need to come forward to God in faith, coming forward with boldness to the throne of grace to receive mercy and grace, and in coming forward to the Holy of Holies, we have "boldness for entering the Holy of Holies in the blood of Jesus" (Heb. 7:25; 11:6; 4:16; 10:19-20)—R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十六篇　新约（三）其内容
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：赛四二6，四九8；耶三一31～34；来八8～12
壹　因着耶利米预言到新约，基于这事实，耶利米书这卷旧约的书，也可视为新约的书─耶二13，十七9，十三23，二三5～6，三一31～34。
贰　基督自己就是神所赐给我们的新约，新遗命─赛四二6，四九8：
一　神的救恩，神的祝福，以及神一切的丰富都已经立约给了我们；这约就是基督：
１　新约中千百项遗赠的实际乃是基督─创二二18上，加三14，林前一30，十五45下，弗一3。
２　基督是新遗命的实际，也就是神一切所是的实际，并神一切所赐的实际；所以，基督就是新遗命。
二　接受并享受基督作我们这约的路，乃是借着呼求我们主基督的名，而操练我们的灵，照着我们的灵而活，住留在我们灵里；而与我们的灵同在的，就是这位基督─赛四二5～6，亚十二1，罗八4下，启一10上，提后四22，赛十二3～4。
叁　新遗命的内容（耶三一31～34，来八8～12）包括以下四项福分，作为神给我们的遗赠：─引用经文
一　“我要将我的律法赐在他们心思里，并且将这些律法写在他们心上”─来八10：
１　生命的律，生命之灵的律，乃是经过过程之三一神作赐生命的灵住在我们的灵里─罗八2～3，11，34。
２　生命的律乃是生命自发的能力；这律是生命所具自然的特性，和自有、自动的功能。
３　生命之律的功用乃是：
a　使我们在生命、性情和彰显上，但不在神格上成为神，使我们成形为神长子的形像，而成为祂团体的彰显─罗八2，29。
b　将我们构成为基督身体的肢体，有各种的功用─弗四11～12，16。
４　我们享受生命分赐到我们里面，好借着生命之灵的律的工作，完成神的经纶─耶三一33，来八10，罗八10，6，11。
５　生命之灵的律已经装置好，且正在运行，我们必须借着把这律的“开关打开”而与这律合作─帖前五16～18，路八15。
二　“我要作他们的神，他们要作我的子民”─来八10：
１　神作我们的神，意即神是我们的产业；而我们作神的子民，意即我们乃是神的产业─弗一11，14，18，三21。
２　在新约里，我们有特权得着神作我们的神，我们作祂的子民─乃是生命使我们能在与神的交通中享受神，好叫祂被我们所认识、领会，且由我们活出─约壹一3，7。
三　“他们各人绝不用教导自己同国之民，各人也绝不用教导自己的弟兄，说，你该认识主；因为他们从最小的到至大的，都必认识我”─来八11：
１　生命的功能，使我们能以生命内里的方式认识神。
２　我们能凭生命的感觉，就是我们里面神圣生命的感觉、知觉，而从里面主观的认识神─罗八6，弗四18～19，腓三10上：
a　生命的感觉，与死的感觉和生命平安的感觉都有关联─罗八6，赛二六3。
b　生命的感觉使我们知道我们是活在天然的生命里，或活在神圣的生命里；也使我们知道我们是活在肉体里，或活在灵里。
c　我们应当照生命的感觉，按生命的原则而活，而不是照对错的原则，就是死的原则而活。
四　“我要宽恕他们的不义，绝不再记念他们的罪”─来八12：
１　基督为我们的罪成就了平息，以平息神的公义，也就是满足了神公义的要求，使我们得以与神和好─来二17。
２　基督那又宝贵又有功效的血，解决了我们一切的难处，使我们得以维持在与神不断的交通中，不断的享受祂生机的救恩─约壹一7～9，二1～2：
a　在神面前，主救赎的血已经一次永远的洗净了我们，(来九12，14，)这洗净的功效无需重复。
b　然而每当我们与神交通，良心蒙了神圣之光的光照，我们就必须在我们的良心里，一再的即时应用主的宝血常时的洗净。
３　神一赦免我们，就从祂的记忆里涂抹我们的罪，不再记念：
a　赦罪的意义，就是消除人在神面前的罪案，使我们免去神公义的刑罚─约三18，五24。
b　神赦免了我们的罪，就使我们所犯的罪离开我们─诗一○三12，利十六7～10，15～22。
４　神赦免我们的罪，结果使我们恢复与祂的交通，而敬畏祂，并且爱祂─诗一三○4，路七47。
肆　至终，新约要产生新耶路撒冷，就是神的新约的具体化身，以团体的方式，彰显神到极致，直到永远─加四26～28，31。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Sixteen
The New Covenant (3) Its Contents
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Isa. 42:6; 49:8; Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:8-12
I.　Based on the fact that Jeremiah prophesies concerning the new covenant, the book of Jeremiah may be considered an Old Testament book that is also a New Testament book—Jer. 2:13; 17:9; 13:23; 23:5-6; 31:31-34.
II.　Christ Himself is the new covenant, the new testament, given to us by God—Isa. 42:6; 49:8:
A.　God's salvation, God's blessings, and all of God's riches have been covenanted to us, and this covenant is Christ:
1.　The reality of all the hundreds of bequests in the New Testament is Christ—Gen. 22:18a; Gal. 3:14; 1 Cor. 1:30; 15:45b; Eph. 1:3.
2.　Christ is the reality of the new testament, the reality of all that God is and of all that God has given to us; therefore, Christ is the new testament.
B.　The way to receive and enjoy Christ as the covenant to us is to exercise our spirit, to live according to our spirit, and to remain in our spirit, with which is the very Christ, by calling on Christ our Lord's name—Isa. 42:5-6; Zech. 12:1; Rom. 8:4b; Rev. 1:10a; 2 Tim. 4:22; Isa. 12:3-4.
III.　The contents of the new covenant (Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:8-12) include the following four blessings as God's bequests to us:
A.　"I will impart My laws into their mind, and on their hearts I will inscribe them"—Heb. 8:10:
1.　The law of life, the law of the Spirit of life, is the processed Triune God as the life-giving Spirit dwelling in our spirit—Rom. 8:2-3, 11, 34.
2.　The law of life is the spontaneous power of life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of life.
3.　The function of the law of life is:
a.　To make us God in life, nature, and expression but not in the Godhead, shaping us into the image of the firstborn Son of God so that we may become His corporate expression—Rom. 8:2, 29.
b.　To constitute us the members of the Body of Christ with all kinds of functions—Eph. 4:11-12, 16.
4.　We enjoy the dispensing of life into our being for the accomplishment of God's economy by the working of the law of the Spirit of life—Jer. 31:33; Heb. 8:10; Rom. 8:10, 6, 11.
5.　We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life by "switching on" this law—1 Thes. 5:16-18; Luke 8:15.
B.　"I will be God to them, and they will be a people to Me"—Heb. 8:10:
1.　For God to be our God means that He is our inheritance, and for us to be God's people means that He is our inheritance—Eph. 1:11, 14, 18; 3:21.
2.　In the new covenant we have the privilege of having God as our God and of being His people—the enabling of life for us to participate in the enjoyment of God in fellowship with Him so that He can be known by us, apprehended by us, and lived by us—1 John 1:3, 7.
C.　"They shall by no means each teach his fellow citizen and each his brother, saying, Know the Lord; for all will know Me from the little one to the great one among them"—Heb. 8:11a:
1.　The function of life enables us to know God in the inward way of life.
2.　We can know God subjectively from within by the sense of life, which is the feeling, the consciousness, of the divine life within us— Rom. 8:6; Eph. 4:18-19; Phil. 3:10a:
a.　The sense of life involves both the feeling of death and the feeling of life and peace—Rom. 8:6; Isa. 26:3.
b.　The sense of life makes us know whether we are living in the natural life or in the divine life and whether we are living in the flesh or in the spirit.
c.　We should live according to the sense of life in the principle of life, not according to the principle of right and wrong, the principle of death.
D.　"I will be propitious to their unrighteousnesses, and their sins I shall by no means remember anymore"—Heb. 8:12:
1.　Christ made propitiation for our sins to appease God's righteousness, to reconcile us by satisfying the demands of God's righteousness—Heb. 2:17.
2.　The precious and all-efficacious blood of Christ resolves all our problems so that we can remain constantly in fellowship with God to continually enjoy His organic salvation—1 John 1:7-9; 2:1-2:
a.　Before God, the redeeming blood of the Lord has cleansed us once for all eternally (Heb. 9:12, 14), and the efficacy of that cleansing need not be repeated.
b.　However, in our conscience we need the instant application of the constant cleansing of the Lord's precious blood again and again whenever our conscience is enlightened by the divine light in our fellowship with God.
3.　Once God forgives us, He erases our sins from His memory and remembers them no longer:
a.　Forgiveness of sins means the removal of the charges of sin against us before God that we may be delivered from the penalty of God's righteousness—John 3:18; 5:24.
b.　When God forgives us of our sins, He causes the sins which we have committed to depart from us—Psa. 103:12; Lev. 16:7-10, 15-22.
4.　God's forgiveness of our sins results in our fearing Him and loving Him in our restored fellowship with Him—Psa. 130:4; Luke 7:47.
IV.　The new covenant will ultimately issue in the New Jerusalem, the embodiment of God's new covenant to express Him in a corporate way to the uttermost for eternity—Gal. 4:26-28, 31.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十六篇　新约（三）其内容
The New Covenant (3) Its Contents
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：赛四二6，四九8；耶三一31～34；来八8～12
壹　因着耶利米预言到新约，基于这事实，耶利米书这卷旧约的书，也可视为新约的书─耶二13，十七9，十三23，二三5～6，三一31～34。
I.　Based on the fact that Jeremiah prophesies concerning the new covenant, the book of Jeremiah may be considered an Old Testament book that is also a New Testament book—Jer. 2:13; 17:9; 13:23; 23:5-6; 31:31-34.
贰　基督自己就是神所赐给我们的新约，新遗命─赛四二6，四九8：
II.　Christ Himself is the new covenant, the new testament, given to us by God—Isa. 42:6; 49:8:
一　神的救恩，神的祝福，以及神一切的丰富都已经立约给了我们；这约就是基督：
A.　God's salvation, God's blessings, and all of God's riches have been covenanted to us, and this covenant is Christ:
１　新约中千百项遗赠的实际乃是基督─创二二18上，加三14，林前一30，十五45下，弗一3。
1.　The reality of all the hundreds of bequests in the New Testament is Christ—Gen. 22:18a; Gal. 3:14; 1 Cor. 1:30; 15:45b; Eph. 1:3.
２　基督是新遗命的实际，也就是神一切所是的实际，并神一切所赐的实际；所以，基督就是新遗命。
2.　Christ is the reality of the new testament, the reality of all that God is and of all that God has given to us; therefore, Christ is the new testament.
二　接受并享受基督作我们这约的路，乃是借着呼求我们主基督的名，而操练我们的灵，照着我们的灵而活，住留在我们灵里；而与我们的灵同在的，就是这位基督─赛四二5～6，亚十二1，罗八4下，启一10上，提后四22，赛十二3～4。
B.　The way to receive and enjoy Christ as the covenant to us is to exercise our spirit, to live according to our spirit, and to remain in our spirit, with which is the very Christ, by calling on Christ our Lord's name—Isa. 42:5-6; Zech. 12:1; Rom. 8:4b; Rev. 1:10a; 2 Tim. 4:22; Isa. 12:3-4.
叁　新遗命的内容（耶三一31～34，来八8～12）包括以下四项福分，作为神给我们的遗赠：─引用经文
III.　The contents of the new covenant (Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:8-12) include the following four blessings as God's bequests to us:
一　“我要将我的律法赐在他们心思里，并且将这些律法写在他们心上”─来八10：
A.　"I will impart My laws into their mind, and on their hearts I will inscribe them"—Heb. 8:10:
１　生命的律，生命之灵的律，乃是经过过程之三一神作赐生命的灵住在我们的灵里─罗八2～3，11，34。
1.　The law of life, the law of the Spirit of life, is the processed Triune God as the life-giving Spirit dwelling in our spirit—Rom. 8:2-3, 11, 34.
２　生命的律乃是生命自发的能力；这律是生命所具自然的特性，和自有、自动的功能。
2.　The law of life is the spontaneous power of life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of life.
３　生命之律的功用乃是：
3.　The function of the law of life is:
a　使我们在生命、性情和彰显上，但不在神格上成为神，使我们成形为神长子的形像，而成为祂团体的彰显─罗八2，29。
a.　To make us God in life, nature, and expression but not in the Godhead, shaping us into the image of the firstborn Son of God so that we may become His corporate expression—Rom. 8:2, 29.
b　将我们构成为基督身体的肢体，有各种的功用─弗四11～12，16。
b.　To constitute us the members of the Body of Christ with all kinds of functions—Eph. 4:11-12, 16.
４　我们享受生命分赐到我们里面，好借着生命之灵的律的工作，完成神的经纶─耶三一33，来八10，罗八10，6，11。
4.　We enjoy the dispensing of life into our being for the accomplishment of God's economy by the working of the law of the Spirit of life—Jer. 31:33; Heb. 8:10; Rom. 8:10, 6, 11.
５　生命之灵的律已经装置好，且正在运行，我们必须借着把这律的“开关打开”而与这律合作─帖前五16～18，路八15。
5.　We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life by "switching on" this law—1 Thes. 5:16-18; Luke 8:15.
二　“我要作他们的神，他们要作我的子民”─来八10：
B.　"I will be God to them, and they will be a people to Me"—Heb. 8:10:
１　神作我们的神，意即神是我们的产业；而我们作神的子民，意即我们乃是神的产业─弗一11，14，18，三21。
1.　For God to be our God means that He is our inheritance, and for us to be God's people means that He is our inheritance—Eph. 1:11, 14, 18; 3:21.
２　在新约里，我们有特权得着神作我们的神，我们作祂的子民─乃是生命使我们能在与神的交通中享受神，好叫祂被我们所认识、领会，且由我们活出─约壹一3，7。
2.　In the new covenant we have the privilege of having God as our God and of being His people—the enabling of life for us to participate in the enjoyment of God in fellowship with Him so that He can be known by us, apprehended by us, and lived by us—1 John 1:3, 7.
三　“他们各人绝不用教导自己同国之民，各人也绝不用教导自己的弟兄，说，你该认识主；因为他们从最小的到至大的，都必认识我”─来八11：
C.　"They shall by no means each teach his fellow citizen and each his brother, saying, Know the Lord; for all will know Me from the little one to the great one among them"—Heb. 8:11a:
１　生命的功能，使我们能以生命内里的方式认识神。
1.　The function of life enables us to know God in the inward way of life.
２　我们能凭生命的感觉，就是我们里面神圣生命的感觉、知觉，而从里面主观的认识神─罗八6，弗四18～19，腓三10上：
2.　We can know God subjectively from within by the sense of life, which is the feeling, the consciousness, of the divine life within us— Rom. 8:6; Eph. 4:18-19; Phil. 3:10a:
a　生命的感觉，与死的感觉和生命平安的感觉都有关联─罗八6，赛二六3。
a.　The sense of life involves both the feeling of death and the feeling of life and peace—Rom. 8:6; Isa. 26:3.
b　生命的感觉使我们知道我们是活在天然的生命里，或活在神圣的生命里；也使我们知道我们是活在肉体里，或活在灵里。
b.　The sense of life makes us know whether we are living in the natural life or in the divine life and whether we are living in the flesh or in the spirit.
c　我们应当照生命的感觉，按生命的原则而活，而不是照对错的原则，就是死的原则而活。
c.　We should live according to the sense of life in the principle of life, not according to the principle of right and wrong, the principle of death.
四　“我要宽恕他们的不义，绝不再记念他们的罪”─来八12：
D.　"I will be propitious to their unrighteousnesses, and their sins I shall by no means remember anymore"—Heb. 8:12:
１　基督为我们的罪成就了平息，以平息神的公义，也就是满足了神公义的要求，使我们得以与神和好─来二17。
1.　Christ made propitiation for our sins to appease God's righteousness, to reconcile us by satisfying the demands of God's righteousness—Heb. 2:17.
２　基督那又宝贵又有功效的血，解决了我们一切的难处，使我们得以维持在与神不断的交通中，不断的享受祂生机的救恩─约壹一7～9，二1～2：
2.　The precious and all-efficacious blood of Christ resolves all our problems so that we can remain constantly in fellowship with God to continually enjoy His organic salvation—1 John 1:7-9; 2:1-2:
a　在神面前，主救赎的血已经一次永远的洗净了我们，(来九12，14，)这洗净的功效无需重复。
a.　Before God, the redeeming blood of the Lord has cleansed us once for all eternally (Heb. 9:12, 14), and the efficacy of that cleansing need not be repeated.
b　然而每当我们与神交通，良心蒙了神圣之光的光照，我们就必须在我们的良心里，一再的即时应用主的宝血常时的洗净。
b.　However, in our conscience we need the instant application of the constant cleansing of the Lord's precious blood again and again whenever our conscience is enlightened by the divine light in our fellowship with God.
３　神一赦免我们，就从祂的记忆里涂抹我们的罪，不再记念：
3.　Once God forgives us, He erases our sins from His memory and remembers them no longer:
a　赦罪的意义，就是消除人在神面前的罪案，使我们免去神公义的刑罚─约三18，五24。
a.　Forgiveness of sins means the removal of the charges of sin against us before God that we may be delivered from the penalty of God's righteousness—John 3:18; 5:24.
b　神赦免了我们的罪，就使我们所犯的罪离开我们─诗一○三12，利十六7～10，15～22。
b.　When God forgives us of our sins, He causes the sins which we have committed to depart from us—Psa. 103:12; Lev. 16:7-10, 15-22.
４　神赦免我们的罪，结果使我们恢复与祂的交通，而敬畏祂，并且爱祂─诗一三○4，路七47。
4.　God's forgiveness of our sins results in our fearing Him and loving Him in our restored fellowship with Him—Psa. 130:4; Luke 7:47.
肆　至终，新约要产生新耶路撒冷，就是神的新约的具体化身，以团体的方式，彰显神到极致，直到永远─加四26～28，31。
IV.　The new covenant will ultimately issue in the New Jerusalem, the embodiment of God's new covenant to express Him in a corporate way to the uttermost for eternity—Gal. 4:26-28, 31.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE SIXTEEN
THE NEW COVENANT (3) ITS CONTENTS
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
THE BOOK OF JEREMIAH BEING AN OLD TESTAMENT BOOK AND A NEW TESTAMENT BOOK
Based on the fact that Jeremiah prophesies concerning the new covenant, the book of Jeremiah may be considered an Old Testament book that is also a New Testament book (Jer. 2:13; 17:9; 13:23; 23:5-6; 31:31-34), The prophecy of Jeremiah concerning the new covenant takes all twenty-seven books of the New Testament to unfold it. At the conclusion of the Life-study of Jeremiah, Brother Lee shared that the book of Jeremiah is like a walnut. In order to see the kernel of this Old Testament book that can be considered a New Testament book, we need to crack the shell, open it up, and get into the intrinsic significance of the book. The book of Jeremiah unveils three things to us: what God wants from us, what we are in our fallen condition, and what Christ is to us. These three matters are related to the new covenant.
According to the book of Jeremiah, what God wants from us is that we would drink of Him as the fountain of living waters. In Jeremiah 2:13, the Lord said, "My people have committed two evils: / They have forsaken Me, / The fountain of living waters, / To hew out for themselves cisterns, / Broken cisterns, / Which hold no water." God wants us to drink of Him as the fountain of living waters so that we can be saturated, soaked, and permeated with Him to become His corporate Body, expressing Him in this universe.
Jeremiah 17:9 says, "The heart is deceitful above all things / And it is incurable; / Who can know it?" This shows us what we are in our fallen condition. No one can cure our psychological heart. Jeremiah 13:23 says, "Can the Cushite change his skin, / Or the leopard his spots? / Then you also may be able to do good, / Who are accustomed to do evil." These verses show us that our nature is unchangeable. In our fallen condition, we are leopards with spots that cannot be removed. Self-improvement classes cannot remove our spots; it is impossible. Our heart is incurable, and our nature is, unchangeable. The book of Jeremiah does not stop here; it goes on to show us what Christ is to us. Jeremiah 23:5-6 shows us that Christ is our righteousness, and Jeremiah 31 shows us that Christ is also our law of life (v. 33). These are bequests of the new testament. These three things are the intrinsic significance of the book of Jeremiah. When we come to God and drink of Him as the fountain of living waters, the law of life is activated to dispense God as life into us to cure our incurable heart and to change our unchangeable nature. As a result, we become God in life, nature, expression, and function (but not in the Godhead) for the satisfaction of His heart's desire. This is a wonderful prophecy of the new covenant.
CHRIST HIMSELF BEING THE NEW COVENANT, THE NEW TESTAMENT, GIVEN TO US BY GOD
God's Salvation, God's Blessings, and All of God's Riches Being Covenanted to Us
Christ Himself is the new covenant, the new testament, given to us by God. The book of Isaiah tells us clearly that Christ will be a covenant for His people (42:6; 49:8). Apparently, God is giving us many bequests; actually, He is giving us one thing一 Christ. Through the shedding of His precious blood, Christ enacted the new covenant. Now in resurrection, He has become the new covenant. God's salvation, His blessings, and all of His riches have been covenanted to us, and this covenant is Christ.
Christ Being the Reality of All the Hundreds of Bequests in the New Testament
The reality of all the hundreds of bequests in the New Testament is Christ (Gen. 22:18a; Gal. 3:14; 1 Cor. 1:30; 15:45b; Eph. 1:3). This Christ is the pneumatic Christ, the life-giving Spirit. In resurrection, He became the life-giving Spirit as the reality of all the bequests of the new covenant. So if there were no life-giving Spirit, there would be no reality of the new covenant. This shows us how crucial it is that Christ became the life-giving Spirit.
We need to pass through the judicial realm of Christ's earthly ministry and enter into the divine and mystical realm of His heavenly ministry. This divine and mystical realm is a person- the pneumatic Christ who is the consummated Spirit. This pneumatic Christ is the new covenant. Therefore, the divine and mystical realm is the new covenant. To enter into the new covenant is to enter into the divine and mystical realm of the pneumatic Christ as the consummated Spirit.
Christ Being the Reality of the New Testament
Christ is the reality of the new testament, the reality of all that God is and of all that God has given to us; therefore, Christ is the new testament. Galatians 3:14 shows us the unique blessing in God's New Testament economy— the Spirit. The blessing of Abraham is the promise of the Spirit. Christ as the Spirit is the unique bequest of the new testament. The pneumatic Christ as the Spirit is the new testament. Without the pneumatic Christ, the new testament would be empty. Today God is blessing us with the pneumatic Christ as the life-giving Spirit. All the blessings, all the bequests in the new covenant, are spiritual blessings. In reality, all the blessings are the Spirit Himself, who is the pneumatic Christ, and this pneumatic Christ is the new testament.
Receiving and Enjoying Christ's as the Covenant to Us by Exercising Our Spirit, Living according to Our Spirit, and Remaining in Our Spirit
The way to receive and enjoy Christ as the covenant to us is to exercise our spirit, to live according to our spirit, and to remain in our spirit, with which is the very Christ, by calling on Christ our Lord's name (Isa. 42:5-6; Zech. 12:1; Rom. 8:4b; Rev. 1:10a; 2 Tim. 4:22; Isa. 12:3-4), We can enjoy Christ as the covenant to us in a very simple way by exercising our spirit, living according to our spirit, and remaining in our spirit. Christ is with our spirit. This means that Christ as the reality of the new covenant is in our spirit. All of the bequests, all of the riches of Christ, are within our spirit. Christ as the covenant is the life-giving Spirit in our spirit.
We also enjoy this covenant by calling on Christ our Lord's name. This is not difficult. We do not have to climb Mount Kilimanjaro. We can simply say, "Lord Jesus!M This is the simplicity that is toward Christ (2 Cor. 11:3). All the vast riches embodied in Christ as the Spirit in our spirit can be enjoyed by calling on the Lord's name. This calling needs to increase in the Lord's recovery. We need to call on the Lord's name. Psalm 116:12-13 says, "What shall I return to Jehovah / For all His benefits toward me? / I will take up the cup of salvation / And call upon the name of Jehovah." This is God as our portion, full of Christ and full of riches. We can enjoy this by calling, "Lord Jesus!" During the day when we are working, we can stop, look up into the heavens, and say, "O Lord Jesus.Isaiah 12:3 says, ''Therefore you will draw water with rejoicing / From the springs of salvation." The /water we draw is the "liquid new covenant. This is the "liquid" Triune God, the processed and consummated Triune God, who is the pneumatic Christ, the consummated Spirit, whom we drink of by calling on His name. With rejoicing, we draw water from the springs of salvation and say in that day, "Praise Jehovah" (v. 4). Calling upon the name of the Lord is the best way to drink Christ as the new covenant. This needs to be our practice so that we can receive and enjoy Christ as the covenant.
THE CONTENTS OF THE NEW COVENANT
The contents of the new covenant (Jer. 31:31-34; Heb. 8:8-12) include four blessings as God's bequests to us: the imparting of God's laws into our minds and on our hearts inscribing them, God being God to us and we being a people to Him, knowing God according to the inner subjective consciousness, and God being propitious to our unrighteousnesses and our sins not being remembered anymore.
Imparting His Laws into Our Minds and on Our Hearts Inscribing Them
"I will impart My laws into their mind, and on their hearts I will inscribe them" (Heb. 8:10). This is a bequest. Today God is imparting Himself as laws into us, and He is inscribing these laws on our hearts. Because the law can be imparted into us, the law spoken of here must be the law of life.
The Law of Life, the Law of the Spirit of Life, Being the Processed Triune God as the Life-giving Spirit Dwelling in Our Spirit
