The law of life, the law of the Spirit of life, is the processed Triune God as the life-giving Spirit dwelling in our spirit (Rom. 8:2). Romans 8 reveals the processes that the Triune God passed through so that He may be installed in us as the law of the Spirit of life in our spirit. Verse 3 says, "God, sending His own. Son in the likeness of the flesh of sin and concerning sin, condemned sin in the flesh." This verse shows His incarnation (with His human living implied) and His crucifixion by which He condemned sin in the flesh. Verse 11 says, "And if the Spirit of the One who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in you… This verse shows His resurrection, and verse 34 His ascension, revealing that Christ is at the right hand of God interceding for us. Thus, the Triune God passed through the processes of incarnation, human living, crucifixion, resurrection, and ascension that He as the life-giving Spirit may be dispensed into us as the law of the Spirit of life.
The Law of Life Being the Spontaneous Power of Life
The law of life is the spontaneous power of life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of life. The Triune God was processed and consummated to come into us as a living law, the law of life. This law is a spontaneous power of life. The Triune God is in us as a spontaneous power. He is in us as a natural characteristic and an innate, automatic function. He functions automatically. When you flip the "switch," He functions. He is an automatic function within us. A peach tree has a law of its life. As the life within the peach tree grows, the law of that life, the natural characteristic and innate function of that life manifests itself by producing peaches. It is automatic, natural, spontaneous, and effortless. There is no need to teach the peach tree to bring forth peaches. There is no need to rebuke it. Pears will not come forth from its branches. All the peach tree has to do is grow. In the same manner, the law of the divine life has been dispensed into us. Now the Triune God is within us as the law of the divine life.
The Function of the Law of Life
Making Us God in Life, Nature, and Expression but Not in the Godhead
The function of the law of life is to make us God in life, nature, and expression but not in the Godhead, shaping us into the image of the firstborn Son of God so that we may become His corporate expression (Rom. 8:2, 29). This is a bequest to us. The Triune God has been processed and consummated to become the Spirit of life within us. This Spirit of life is a law. As we enjoy Him, call on Him, praise Him, and exercise our spirit to contact Him, this law is activated within us, and this law grows within us. The law of the peach tree life produces peaches. The law of the divine life produces the life of God. And the function of this life is to make us God. Consider a bird. The bird has a life, and the bird life has a law. As the bird grows, the law of its life, the spontaneous power of its life, the innate, automatic function of its life, builds it up into the shape of a bird with the innate ability to fly. The bird has no concern or worry because to fly is the spontaneous, automatic, and natural function of its life. The bird has a power within it to simply fly.
As the Triune God functions within us, the law of the divine life, the life of Christ, the law of the life of Christ, builds us up into the shape of Christ. The function of the law of life is to make us God in life, in nature, and in expression but not in the Godhead. As the law of life innately functions and spontaneously operates within us, it shapes us into the image of the firstborn Son of God to make us the mass reproduction of Christ-the standard model of a God-man— that we may become a part of the great corporate God-man in this universe.
The Function of the Law of Life Constituting Us the Members of the Body
The function of the law of life is to constitute us the members of the Body of Christ with all kinds of functions (Eph. 4:11-12, 16), The law of life makes us God not only in life, nature, and appearance but also in function.
Enjoying the Dispensing of Life into Our Being by the Working of the Law of the Spirit of Life
We enjoy the dispensing of life into our being for the accomplishment of God's economy by the working of the law of the Spirit of life (Jer. 31:33; Heb, 8:10; Rom. 8:10, 65 11). Life is dispensed into our tripartite being by the working of this natural characteristic and innate, automatic function of life.
Cooperating with the In stalled and Operating Law of the Spirit of Life by "Switching On" This Law We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life by "switching on" this law (1 Thes. 5:1648; Luke 8:15). This is like electricity that has been installed in a building. If the lights in a building are switched off, we do not need to contact the power plant to turn the lights on. Once electricity has been installed in a building, all one needs to do is flip the switch and the lights will be switched on. The divine electricity— the processed and consummated Triune God— has been installed in our spirit. We simply need to switch on. For the divine electricity to exercise its spontaneous power and innate, automatic function, we simply need to switch on. If we switch on, the divine electricity is appropriated. We switch on by exercising our spirit. First Thessalonians 5:16 says, "Always rejoice." This is a command. We switch on by exercising our spirit to "always rejoice." We need to keep the switch on so that the law of life might operate in us and the divine life can grow in us. In this way God's eternal purpose can be fulfilled in all the saints corporately to shape them into the image of the firstborn Son of God. If we always rejoice, unceasingly pray, and give thanks in everything (vv. 16…18a) we switch on. We also switch on— regardless of how we may feel— by saying, "Thank You, Lord Jesus." Likewise, if we say, "Praise the Lord," we switch on. By thanking the Lord, praising Him, or by calling upon the name of the Lord, we remain in uninterrupted fellowship.
Also, according to Luke 8:15, if we are going to keep the switch on5 we have to deal with our heart so that the law of life can operate in us in a free way. We need to deal with all the things in our heart by allowing the Lord to remove the stones and the thorns in the soil of our heart, in order to make our heart soft toward Him。This gives Him a way to grow in our being and operate fully as the law of life. This law of life is spontaneous and effortless. If we switch on this law, we do not have to worry about anything. We simply need to call on the Lord and praise the Lord. When this law is switched on, spontaneously we are being shaped into the image of the firstborn Son of God.
God Being God to Us, and the Believers Being a People to Him
God Being Our Inheritance and the Believers Being God's Inheritance
"I will be God to them, and they will be a people to Me " (Heb. 8:10). For God to be our God means that He is our inheritance, and for us to be God's people means that we are God's inheritance (Eph. 1:11, 14, 18; 3:21). This means that we inherit all that God is as the reality of the new covenant. We inherit all that He is, and this makes us His inheritance. We inherit Him and He inherits us. We inherit all that He is; then when we inherit all of His riches and are filled with Him as the reality of all the bequests of the new testament. He inherits us with Himself as all the riches constituted into our being for His glorious expression in the universe.
The Enabling of Life for Us to Participate in the Enjoyment of God in Fellowship with Him So That He Can Be Known by Us, Apprehended by Us, and Lived by Us
In the new covenant we have the privilege of having God as our God and of being His people— the enabling of life for us to participate in the enjoyment of God in fellowship with Him so that He can be known by us, apprehended by us, and lived by us (1 John 1:3, 7). Within us is the enabling of life for us to participate in the enjoyment and fellowship of God. We can know Him, apprehend Him, and live Him. It is our bequest that the life within us has enabled us to enjoy God.
Knowing the Lord
"They shall by no means each teach his fellow citizen and each his brother, saying, Know the Lord; for all will know Me from the little one to the great one among them" (Heb. 8:11a). To know God is a tremendous bequest! The first mention of the word know in Hebrews 8:11 refers to the outward objective knowledge, but the second mention of the word know refers to the inward subjective consciousness. We know God according to our inward subjective consciousness.
Knowing God in the Inward Way of Life and Knowing God Subjectively from within by the Sense of Life
The function of life enables us to know God in the inward way of life. We can know God subjectively from within by the sense of life, which is the feeling, the consciousness, of the divine life within us (Rom. 8:6; Eph, 4:18-19; Phil 3:10a). We have the Triune God as life to us, and we have the sense of life, the feeling, the consciousness, of the divine life within us.
The Sense of Life Involving Both the Feeling of Death and the Feeling of Life and Peace
The sense of life involves both the feeling of death and the feeling of life and peace (Rom. 8:6; Isa. 26:3). We have the sense of life within us. By this sense we know the Lord inwardly in our subjective, inward consciousness. No one has to teach us outwardly. We know Him inwardly and subjectively according to the sense of life. This sense involves the feeling of death. If we feel deadened, in a sense, it is good news. It would be lamentable if we were deadened and. did not know it. Unfortunately, there are thousands of Christians who are deadened, and they have no consciousness of it.
The sense of life involves the feeling of death. What is this feeling of death? If we feel weakness within or if we feel empty, uneasy, restless, depressed, dry, dark, or pain, such feelings are the sense of death. This sense tells us that we are in the wrong place. It is a wonderful indicator This sense also involves the feeling of life and peace. This is a bequest. When we set our mind on the spirit, our mind becomes zoe (the divine life). Our mind also becomes peace. This means that we have the inner sense of strength, satisfaction, peace, rest, release, liveliness, watering, brightness, and comfort. These are the feelings of the sense of life and peace.
The Sense of Life Making Us Know Whether We Are Living in the Natural Life or in the Divine Life and Whether We Are Living in the Flesh or in the Spirit
The sense of life makes us know whether we are living in the natural life or in the divine life and whether we are living in the flesh or in the spirit. We know whether we are living in the flesh or in the spirit by the sense of life. If we have the feeling of death, this indicates that our mind is set on the flesh. If we set our mind on the spirit, we will sense watering, satisfaction, brightness, comfort, and peace.
Living according to the Sense of Life in the Principle of Life and Not according to the Principle of Right and Wrong, the Principle of Death
We should live according to the sense of life in the principle of life, not according to the principle of right and wrong, the principle of death. We should not live according to right and wrong. Our conscience should not merely be governed by right and wrong. Our conscience should be governed mainly by life and death. We should be a miniature tree of life, living according to the principle of life and by the sense of life. This is also a bequest that we need to claim.
God Being Propitious to Our Unrighteousnesses, and Our Sins Not Being Remembered Anymore
"I will be propitious to their unrighteousnesses, and their sins I shall by no means remember anymore" (Heb. 8:12). God is propitious to our unrighteousnesses. Christ has satisfied the demands of God's righteousness, and He has appeased God on our behalf. Christ met the demands of God's righteousness so that we could enter into God, enjoy God, and have intimate fellowship with God. God also forgives our sins and erases our sins from His memory. When it comes to our sins, the Triune God has amnesia. When we enjoy God as the forgiving One, we forgive others and we no longer remember the sins of the brothers and sisters.
Christ Making Propitiation for Our Sins to Appease God's Righteousness
Christ made propitiation for our sins to appease God's righteousness, to reconcile us by satisfying the demands of God's righteousness (Heb. 2:17). Leviticus 16, which describes the day of atonement, presents a marvelous type of what the Lord did for us on the cross to accomplish the propitiation for our sins in the new testament. The meaning of atonement is reconciliation or "at-one-ment." On the day of atonement, the high priest brought two goats (v. 5). One of the goats was for a sin offering for Jehovah, and the second goat was for Azazel (v. 8), which signifies Satan. The word Azazel means the goat of departure or, as the King James Version translates it, scapegoat. The high priest took the first goat and offered it for a sin offering (vv. 15-20) to satisfy God's righteous requirements. According to Isaiah.53 when Christ died on the cross, God laid, on Him the iniquity of us all (v. 6). He bore up our sins in His body on the tree (1 Pet. 2:24). In the last three hours of His crucifixion on the cross, He was made sin for us (2 Cor. 5:21), satisfying the demands of God's righteousness.
After the first goat was offered, the high priest took the second goat, laid his hands on it, and confessed all the sins and iniquities of the children of Israel. Then he sent that goat away into the wilderness (Lev. 16:21-22). The scapegoat, Azazel, signifies God's giving of our sins back to Satan. First, God laid our sins on Christ, and Christ took away our sins. Then God took those sins, laid them on Satan— the author of sin and the source of sin (John 8:44; Heb. 2:14)-- and sent those sins with Satan into the lake of fire (Rev. 20:10). Hallelujah! Now our sins and Satan are in the lake of fire!
The blood of the eternal covenant is seen in typology on the day of atonement. In Leviticus 16, a bullock was offered for a sin offering (w. 6, 11), The blood of that sin offering was sprinkled in the Holy of Holies by the high priest (v. 14). As a fulfillment of the type, the blood of the covenant is to bring us into God to make us one with God. The shedding of the Lord's blood, God laying our sins on Christ, and God placing those sins on Satan and sending them with him into the lake of fire, is so that we would be brought into God to enjoy Him. Through Christ's propitiation for our sins, we are brought into the Holy of Holies into the ark to enjoy God as the law of life. This law of life operates spontaneously in us to dispense the Triune God as life into our being to make us one entity with God.
The Precious and All-efficacious Blood of Christ Resolving All Our Problems That We May Remain Constantly in Fellowship with God to Continually Enjoy His Organic Salvation
The precious and all-efficacious blood of Christ resolves all our problems so that we can remain constantly in fellowship with God to continually enjoy His organic salvation (1 John 1:7-9; 2:1-2). Our organic salvation is maintained by our constantly applying the accomplishment of Christ's judicial redemption.
The Redeeming Blood of the Lord Cleansing Us Once for All Eternally
Before God, the redeeming blood of the Lord has cleansed us once for all eternally (Heb. 9:12, 14), and the efficacy of that cleansing need not be repeated.
Needing the Instant Application in Our Conscience of the Constant Cleansing of the Lord's Precious Blood
In our conscience we need the instant application of the constant cleansing of the Lord's precious blood again and again whenever our conscience is enlightened by the divine light in our fellowship with God. Sin has been taken away. This is a bequest. But whenever sin comes up in our being, we need to confess it. We need to agree with God that it is sin. To confess is to agree with God and acknowledge our sin. Whenever we do this, the blood of Jesus cleanses us from every sin (1 John 1:7-9). He is faithful in His word and righteous in His blood to cleanse us from all unrighteousness. In other words, God has to forgive us; otherwise, He would not be righteous. All of our sins were laid on Christ. He paid the debt for our sins. He took away our sins. Now all we need to do is confess our sins, and God is bound by His righteousness to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.
God Erasing Our Sins from His Memory and Remembering Them No Longer
Forgiveness of Sins— the Removal of the Charges of Sin against Us before God
Once God forgives us, He erases our sins from His memory and remembers them no longer. Forgiveness of sins means the removal of the charges of sin against us before God that we may be delivered from the penalty of God's righteousness (John 3:18; 5:24).
God Causing the Sins Which We Have Committed to Depart from Us
When God forgives us of our sins, He causes the sins which we have committed to depart from us (Lev. 16:7-10, 15-22). Psalm 103:12 says, 'As far as the east is from the west, / So far has He removed our transgressions from us." Our sins have been removed infinitely far from us. This is a bequest of the new covenant. All this is for the purpose of bringing us into God that we might enjoy God as everything to us.
God's Forgiveness of Our Sins Resulting in Our Fearing Him and Loving Him
God's forgiveness of our sins results in our fearing Him and loving Him in our restored fellowship with Him (Luke 7:47). Our fearing Him and loving Him are the issue of the forgiveness of sins. When we are forgiven of our sins, this does not make us reckless. Quite the contrary, the forgiveness of sins issues in our fearing God. Psalm 130:4 says, "But with You there is forgiveness, / That You would be feared." When we are forgiven and even as we confess our sins throughout the day to be washed in the Lord's blood, this causes us to revere God. To fear God means that we revere Him, honor Him, and respect Him. We do not want to offend Him. We fear Him in the sense that we do not want to lose His indwelling smile. Hence, His forgiveness causes us to fear Him and to love Him.
In Luke 7, Simon, a Pharisee, invited the Lord for dinner, and a woman who was a sinner was there (w. 36-37). She wet the Lord's feet with her tears and wiped them with the hair of her head. She also anointed His feet with ointment (v. 38). Simon, thinking within himself, reasoned, "This man, if He were a prophet, would know who and what sort of woman this is who is touching Him, because she is a sinner" (v. 39). He did not say this aloud; he was only thinking within himself. Then the Lord said, "Simon, I have something to say to you" (v. 40). He knew what Simon was thinking, and. He said, "A certain moneylender had two debtors: one owed five hundred denarii and the other fifty. But since they had nothing with which to repay, he graciously forgave them both. Which of them therefore will love him more? Simon answered and said, I suppose the one whom he graciously forgave the more. And He said to him, You have judged correctly,, (vv. 41-43). Then turning to the woman, the Lord said to Simon, "Her sins which are many are forgiven, because she loved much; but to whom little is forgiven, he loves little" (v. 47). If we see how much we have been forgiven, we will love much. I have to testify for Brother Howard Higashi that he was one who loved much. He was a crazy lover of Jesus because he saw that he was forgiven much. This is the issue of our forgiveness of sins.
THE NEW COVENANT ULTIMATELY ISSUING IN THE NEW JERUSALEM
The new covenant will ultimately issue in the New Jerusalem, the embodiment of God's new covenant to express Him in a corporate way to the uttermost for eternity (Gal. 4:26-28, 31). On the one hand, the new covenant produces the New Jerusalem, and on the other hand, the New Jerusalem is the new covenant. Christ is the new covenant and the New Jerusalem is the new covenant. Christ as the new covenant produces the New Jerusalem as the new covenant, which is the corporate, enlarged Christ. Christ produces the enlarged Christ, the corporate Christ, the universal Christ, the great God-man. We say this based on what is revealed in Galatians 4. This chapter shows us that Sarah signifies the new covenant of grace and the Jerusalem above, the New Jerusalem (v. 26). Grace is the new covenant, and the new covenant is the New Jerusalem. The New Jerusalem is the embodiment of God's new covenant. Christ as the new covenant is dispensed into us as all the bequests. All of His unsearchable riches as bequests are dispensed into us to produce the New Jerusalem as the ultimate consummation of the new covenant for God's expression forever and ever. This is wonderful!——E. M.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十七篇　生命之律
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：罗八2，29；来八10
壹　神圣生命的律就是神圣生命的自然能力，乃是神圣生命自然的特性，并自有、自动的功能：
一　每一种生命都有一个律，甚至都是一个律─箴三十19上。
二　神的生命是最高的生命，这生命的律是最高的律─赛四十30～31。
三　三一神已经经过成为肉体、钉死、复活并升天的过程，成了生命之灵的律，装置在我们灵里─罗八3，11，34，16。
四　在我们这人三个不同的部分里，有三个不同的律：
１　罪与死的律就是撒但的人位，住在我们的肉体里─罗七18，23。
２　我们心思中，就是我们魂里善的律，源自我们人天然的生命─罗七23。
３　生命之灵的律就是三一神的人位，装置在我们的灵里─罗八2，16：
a　神圣的出生已将我们迁移到一个新的范围，就是神圣的生命连同这生命之律的范围里；在这范围里没有罪、世界或肉体。
b　在这范围里，一切的得胜都是不知不觉、不花力气的，因为托住我们的是生命之灵的律，不是我们自己的意志。
贰　生命之灵的律已经装置好，且正在运行，我们必须凭下列的路把这律的“开关打开”，而与这律合作，好使我们得着生机的救恩─腓二12～13，罗八2：
一　照着灵而行─罗八4。
二　思念那灵的事，将心思置于灵─5～6节。
三　靠着那灵治死身体的行为─13节。
四　被神的灵引导，作神的儿子─14节。
五　在儿子名分的灵里呼叫“阿爸，父”─15节。
六　那灵同我们的灵见证我们是神的儿女─16节。
七　为着完满的儿子名分，就是我们的身体得赎而叹息─23节。
叁　神借着将祂自己作为生命的律分赐到我们里面，而完成祂的经纶：
一　神的经纶是要将祂自己分赐到我们这人里面，使我们这人被祂的所是构成，而与祂的所是成为一个构成；这只有借着神将祂自己作为神圣的生命，分赐到我们里面，才能完成。
二　神圣生命的律“子化”我们，使基督的身体得着建造：
１　当那在我们灵里的生命之律扩展到我们内里的各部分─我们的心思、情感和意志时，这律就成了几个律─耶三一33，来八10：
a　这扩展就是分赐，这分赐就是“写”。
b　借着生命之律在我们里面的运行、扩展，神就使我们在生命、性情和彰显上与祂一样。
c　借着生命之律的运行，我们就被模成神长子的形像。
２　长子是原型，是标准的模型，为着大量复制出神许多的儿子，就是长子许多的弟兄，构成祂的身体，作神团体的彰显─罗八29：
a　神大量复制这原型的作法，乃是将祂这活的原型，就是祂的长子，作到我们全人里面。
b　我们若与这奇妙的原型合作，向祂敞开，祂就要从我们的灵向外扩展到我们的魂里。
c　这内住的原型，就是神的长子，作为生命的律，在我们里面自动的作工，将我们模成祂的形像，就是“子化”我们。
d　在祂的恢复里，神正在我们中间竭力作工，要使我们每一个人与长子一模一样。
e　基督的身体作为新人，就是神的长子这标准模型的团体复制品。
三　生命的律，就是生命的神圣性能，将我们构成基督身体的肢体，包括各种功用─丰富供应的节，以及身体上依其度量而尽功用的各部分─弗四11，16。
肆　生命之律的功能需要生命的长大，因为生命之律只在生命长大时才发挥功能：
一　一种生命若不长大，那种生命的律就无法发挥功能。
二　生命之律只在生命长大时运行─可四3，14，26～29：
１　基督在宝座上的代求，推动祂在复活时所种在我们里面的生命种子─来七25，罗八34。
２　长子正在为我们代求，为要叫祂所种在我们灵里的生命被推动，得以生长、发展、并浸润我们里面的各部分，直到我们完全被祂那得着荣耀和拔高的所是浸透：
a　许多时候，我们在祷告中所发表的话，乃是引述天上的代求；在我们属灵生命上所发生的一切事，都是引述天上的代求，或是对天上代求的反应。
b　一面，祂是在我们灵里，作为生命在长大；另一面，　祂是在诸天之上的宝座上，为着祂撒在我们里面种子的长大和发展代求。
三　当神圣的生命在我们里面长大时，生命的律就发挥功能，使我们成形，将我们模成神长子基督的形像：
１　生命的律不是规律我们不作错事，乃是规律出生命的形状。
２　生命的律发挥功能，主要的不是在消极方面告诉我们不该作什么；反之，当生命长大时，生命的律就在积极方面发挥功能，使我们成形，也就是把我们模成基督的形像。
３　借着生命之律的功能，我们都要成为神成熟的儿子，神也就要得着祂宇宙、团体的彰显。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Seventeen
The Law of Life
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Rom. 8:2, 29; Heb. 8:10
I.　The law of the divine life is the spontaneous power of the divine life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of divine life:
A.　Every life has a law and even is a law—Prov. 30:19a.
B.　God's life is the highest life, and the law of this life is the highest law—Isa. 40:30-31.
C.　The Triune God has been processed through incarnation, crucifixion, resurrection, and ascension to become the law of the Spirit of life installed in our spirit—Rom. 8:3, 11, 34, 16.
D.　There are three different laws in the three different parts of our being:
1.　The law of sin and death is the person of Satan residing in our flesh—Rom. 7:18, 23.
2.　The law of good in our mind, that is, in our soul, derives from our natural human life— Rom. 7:23.
3.　The law of the Spirit of life is the person of the Triune God installed in our spirit—Rom. 8:2, 16:
a.　The divine birth has transferred us into a new realm, the realm of the divine life with its law, a realm in which there is no sin, world, or flesh.
b.　In this realm, all victories are unconscious and effortless because the law of the Spirit of life is upholding us, not our own will.
II.　We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life for our organic salvation by "switching on" this law in the following ways—Phil. 2:12-13; Rom. 8:2:
A.　By walking according to the spirit—Rom. 8:4.
B.　By minding the things of the Spirit, setting our mind on the spirit—vv. 5-6.
C.　By putting to death by the Spirit the practices of our body—v. 13.
D.　By being led by the Spirit of God to be sons of God—v. 14.
E.　By crying "Abba, Father" in the spirit of sonship—v. 15.
F.　By the Spirit witnessing with our spirit that we are God's children—v. 16.
G.　By groaning for the full sonship, the redemption of our body—v. 23.
III.　God accomplishes His economy by dispensing Himself into us as the law of life:
A.　God's economy is to dispense Himself into our being that our being might be constituted with His being to be one constitution with His being; this can be accomplished only by God imparting Himself into us as the divine life.
B.　The law of the divine life "sonizes" us for the building up of the Body of Christ:
1.　When the law of life, which is in our spirit, spreads into our inward parts—our mind, emotion, and will—it becomes several laws— Jer. 31:33; Heb. 8:10:
a.　This spreading is the imparting, and the imparting is the inscribing.
b.　By the working, the spreading, of the law of life within us, God makes us the same as He is in life, nature, and expression.
c.　We are conformed to the image of the firstborn Son of God by the working of the law of life.
2.　The firstborn Son is the prototype, the standard model, for the mass reproduction of the many sons of God, who are His many brothers to constitute His Body for God's corporate expression—Rom. 8:29:
a.　God's way to mass reproduce this prototype is to work His living prototype, the firstborn Son, into our entire being.
b.　If we cooperate with and open up to this wonderful prototype, He will spread outward from our spirit into our soul.
c.　The indwelling prototype, the firstborn Son of God, automatically works in us as the law of life to conform us to His image, to "sonize" us.
d.　In His recovery, God is working desperately among us to make every one of us the same as the firstborn Son.
e.　The Body of Christ as the new man is the corporate reproduction of the standard model, the firstborn Son of God.
C.　The law, the divine capacity, of life constitutes us to be the members of the Body of Christ, including all kinds of functions—the joints of the rich supply and every part of the Body that functions in its measure—Eph. 4:11, 16.
IV.　The function of the law of life requires the growth in life, for the law of life functions only as it grows:
A.　If a certain life does not grow, the law of that life cannot function.
B.　The law of life operates only as life grows— Mark 4:3, 14, 26-29:
1.　Christ's intercession on the throne motivates the life-seed that He sowed into us at the time of resurrection—Heb. 7:25; Rom. 8:34.
2.　The firstborn Son is interceding for us so that the life which He has sown into our spirit may be motivated to grow, develop, and saturate all our inward parts until we are completely permeated with His glorified and uplifted being:
a.　Many times our utterances in prayer are quotations of the heavenly intercession; whatever happens to us in our spiritual life is either a quotation of the heavenly intercession or a reaction to it.
b.　On the one hand, He is in our spirit as life growing; on the other hand, He is on the throne in the heavens interceding for the growth and development of the seed He has sown within us.
C.　While the divine life grows in us, the law of life functions to shape us, to conform us, to the image of Christ as the firstborn Son of God:
1.　The law of life does not regulate us from doing wrong; it regulates the shape of life.
2.　The law of life does not primarily function in the negative sense of telling us what not to do; rather, while life grows, the law of life functions in the positive sense of shaping us, that is, conforming us to the image of Christ.
3.　Through the function of the law of life, we all shall become the mature sons of God, and God will have His universal, corporate expression.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十七篇　生命之律
The Law of Life
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：罗八2，29；来八10
壹　神圣生命的律就是神圣生命的自然能力，乃是神圣生命自然的特性，并自有、自动的功能：
I.　The law of the divine life is the spontaneous power of the divine life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of divine life:
一　每一种生命都有一个律，甚至都是一个律─箴三十19上。
A.　Every life has a law and even is a law—Prov. 30:19a.
二　神的生命是最高的生命，这生命的律是最高的律─赛四十30～31。
B.　God's life is the highest life, and the law of this life is the highest law—Isa. 40:30-31.
三　三一神已经经过成为肉体、钉死、复活并升天的过程，成了生命之灵的律，装置在我们灵里─罗八3，11，34，16。
C.　The Triune God has been processed through incarnation, crucifixion, resurrection, and ascension to become the law of the Spirit of life installed in our spirit—Rom. 8:3, 11, 34, 16.
四　在我们这人三个不同的部分里，有三个不同的律：
D.　There are three different laws in the three different parts of our being:
１　罪与死的律就是撒但的人位，住在我们的肉体里─罗七18，23。
1.　The law of sin and death is the person of Satan residing in our flesh—Rom. 7:18, 23.
２　我们心思中，就是我们魂里善的律，源自我们人天然的生命─罗七23。
2.　The law of good in our mind, that is, in our soul, derives from our natural human life— Rom. 7:23.
３　生命之灵的律就是三一神的人位，装置在我们的灵里─罗八2，16：
3.　The law of the Spirit of life is the person of the Triune God installed in our spirit—Rom. 8:2, 16:
a　神圣的出生已将我们迁移到一个新的范围，就是神圣的生命连同这生命之律的范围里；在这范围里没有罪、世界或肉体。
a.　The divine birth has transferred us into a new realm, the realm of the divine life with its law, a realm in which there is no sin, world, or flesh.
b　在这范围里，一切的得胜都是不知不觉、不花力气的，因为托住我们的是生命之灵的律，不是我们自己的意志。
b.　In this realm, all victories are unconscious and effortless because the law of the Spirit of life is upholding us, not our own will.
贰　生命之灵的律已经装置好，且正在运行，我们必须凭下列的路把这律的“开关打开”，而与这律合作，好使我们得着生机的救恩─腓二12～13，罗八2：
II.　We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life for our organic salvation by "switching on" this law in the following ways—Phil. 2:12-13; Rom. 8:2:
一　照着灵而行─罗八4。
A.　By walking according to the spirit—Rom. 8:4.
二　思念那灵的事，将心思置于灵─5～6节。
B.　By minding the things of the Spirit, setting our mind on the spirit—vv. 5-6.
三　靠着那灵治死身体的行为─13节。
C.　By putting to death by the Spirit the practices of our body—v. 13.
四　被神的灵引导，作神的儿子─14节。
D.　By being led by the Spirit of God to be sons of God—v. 14.
五　在儿子名分的灵里呼叫“阿爸，父”─15节。
E.　By crying "Abba, Father" in the spirit of sonship—v. 15.
六　那灵同我们的灵见证我们是神的儿女─16节。
F.　By the Spirit witnessing with our spirit that we are God's children—v. 16.
七　为着完满的儿子名分，就是我们的身体得赎而叹息─23节。
G.　By groaning for the full sonship, the redemption of our body—v. 23.
叁　神借着将祂自己作为生命的律分赐到我们里面，而完成祂的经纶：
III.　God accomplishes His economy by dispensing Himself into us as the law of life:
一　神的经纶是要将祂自己分赐到我们这人里面，使我们这人被祂的所是构成，而与祂的所是成为一个构成；这只有借着神将祂自己作为神圣的生命，分赐到我们里面，才能完成。
A.　God's economy is to dispense Himself into our being that our being might be constituted with His being to be one constitution with His being; this can be accomplished only by God imparting Himself into us as the divine life.
二　神圣生命的律“子化”我们，使基督的身体得着建造：
B.　The law of the divine life "sonizes" us for the building up of the Body of Christ:
１　当那在我们灵里的生命之律扩展到我们内里的各部分─我们的心思、情感和意志时，这律就成了几个律─耶三一33，来八10：
1.　When the law of life, which is in our spirit, spreads into our inward parts—our mind, emotion, and will—it becomes several laws— Jer. 31:33; Heb. 8:10:
a　这扩展就是分赐，这分赐就是“写”。
a.　This spreading is the imparting, and the imparting is the inscribing.
b　借着生命之律在我们里面的运行、扩展，神就使我们在生命、性情和彰显上与祂一样。
b.　By the working, the spreading, of the law of life within us, God makes us the same as He is in life, nature, and expression.
c　借着生命之律的运行，我们就被模成神长子的形像。
c.　We are conformed to the image of the firstborn Son of God by the working of the law of life.
２　长子是原型，是标准的模型，为着大量复制出神许多的儿子，就是长子许多的弟兄，构成祂的身体，作神团体的彰显─罗八29：
2.　The firstborn Son is the prototype, the standard model, for the mass reproduction of the many sons of God, who are His many brothers to constitute His Body for God's corporate expression—Rom. 8:29:
a　神大量复制这原型的作法，乃是将祂这活的原型，就是祂的长子，作到我们全人里面。
a.　God's way to mass reproduce this prototype is to work His living prototype, the firstborn Son, into our entire being.
b　我们若与这奇妙的原型合作，向祂敞开，祂就要从我们的灵向外扩展到我们的魂里。
b.　If we cooperate with and open up to this wonderful prototype, He will spread outward from our spirit into our soul.
c　这内住的原型，就是神的长子，作为生命的律，在我们里面自动的作工，将我们模成祂的形像，就是“子化”我们。
c.　The indwelling prototype, the firstborn Son of God, automatically works in us as the law of life to conform us to His image, to "sonize" us.
d　在祂的恢复里，神正在我们中间竭力作工，要使我们每一个人与长子一模一样。
d.　In His recovery, God is working desperately among us to make every one of us the same as the firstborn Son.
e　基督的身体作为新人，就是神的长子这标准模型的团体复制品。
e.　The Body of Christ as the new man is the corporate reproduction of the standard model, the firstborn Son of God.
三　生命的律，就是生命的神圣性能，将我们构成基督身体的肢体，包括各种功用─丰富供应的节，以及身体上依其度量而尽功用的各部分─弗四11，16。
C.　The law, the divine capacity, of life constitutes us to be the members of the Body of Christ, including all kinds of functions—the joints of the rich supply and every part of the Body that functions in its measure—Eph. 4:11, 16.
肆　生命之律的功能需要生命的长大，因为生命之律只在生命长大时才发挥功能：
IV.　The function of the law of life requires the growth in life, for the law of life functions only as it grows:
一　一种生命若不长大，那种生命的律就无法发挥功能。
A.　If a certain life does not grow, the law of that life cannot function.
二　生命之律只在生命长大时运行─可四3，14，26～29：
B.　The law of life operates only as life grows— Mark 4:3, 14, 26-29:
１　基督在宝座上的代求，推动祂在复活时所种在我们里面的生命种子─来七25，罗八34。
1.　Christ's intercession on the throne motivates the life-seed that He sowed into us at the time of resurrection—Heb. 7:25; Rom. 8:34.
２　长子正在为我们代求，为要叫祂所种在我们灵里的生命被推动，得以生长、发展、并浸润我们里面的各部分，直到我们完全被祂那得着荣耀和拔高的所是浸透：
2.　The firstborn Son is interceding for us so that the life which He has sown into our spirit may be motivated to grow, develop, and saturate all our inward parts until we are completely permeated with His glorified and uplifted being:
a　许多时候，我们在祷告中所发表的话，乃是引述天上的代求；在我们属灵生命上所发生的一切事，都是引述天上的代求，或是对天上代求的反应。
a.　Many times our utterances in prayer are quotations of the heavenly intercession; whatever happens to us in our spiritual life is either a quotation of the heavenly intercession or a reaction to it.
b　一面，祂是在我们灵里，作为生命在长大；另一面，　祂是在诸天之上的宝座上，为着祂撒在我们里面种子的长大和发展代求。
b.　On the one hand, He is in our spirit as life growing; on the other hand, He is on the throne in the heavens interceding for the growth and development of the seed He has sown within us.
三　当神圣的生命在我们里面长大时，生命的律就发挥功能，使我们成形，将我们模成神长子基督的形像：
C.　While the divine life grows in us, the law of life functions to shape us, to conform us, to the image of Christ as the firstborn Son of God:
１　生命的律不是规律我们不作错事，乃是规律出生命的形状。
1.　The law of life does not regulate us from doing wrong; it regulates the shape of life.
２　生命的律发挥功能，主要的不是在消极方面告诉我们不该作什么；反之，当生命长大时，生命的律就在积极方面发挥功能，使我们成形，也就是把我们模成基督的形像。
2.　The law of life does not primarily function in the negative sense of telling us what not to do; rather, while life grows, the law of life functions in the positive sense of shaping us, that is, conforming us to the image of Christ.
３　借着生命之律的功能，我们都要成为神成熟的儿子，神也就要得着祂宇宙、团体的彰显。
3.　Through the function of the law of life, we all shall become the mature sons of God, and God will have His universal, corporate expression.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE SEVENTEEN
THE LAW OF LIFE
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
THE ONE ACCORD BEING THE MASTER KEY TO ENJOY THE BLESSINGS OF THE NEW COVENANT
To enjoy Christ as all of the hundreds of bequests in the new covenant, we need a master key. The one accord is the master key to every blessing and bequest in the New Testament. We have to practice the one accord to enjoy Christ as the myriad of rich bequests in the new covenant. In the book of Jeremiah, the new covenant was spoken to the entire house of Israel, not merely to individuals (31:31, 33). In the New Testament, the church as the Body of Christ is the Israel of God (Gal. 6:16), If we are to enjoy all the bequests of the new covenant, we need to be in one accord in His house.
Psalm 133 shows how good and pleasant it is to practice the one accord and to dwell together in oneness (v. 1). This oneness is likened to the precious ointment (v. 2) and to the dew of Hermon (v. 3). The ointment signifies the Spirit of life, and the dew signifies the grace of life. The new covenant is a covenant of life and of grace. When we are diligent to keep the oneness of the Spirit (Eph. 4:3), we enjoy the ointment. The new covenant is a covenant of the Spirit (2 Cor. 3:6). Paul said that he was a minister of the new covenant, ministering the Spirit. To enjoy the Spirit we need to dwell together in oneness, that is, to practice the one accord. When we practice the one accord, we enjoy the blessing of life forever (Psa. 133:3). It is crucial that we practice the one accord. We cannot enjoy Christ as the hundreds of bequests in the new covenant if we are not one with the elders, the co-workers, and the other brothers and sisters.
THE LAW OF LIFE BEING THE SPONTANEOUS POWER AND AUTOMATIC FUNCTION OF THE DIVINE LIFE
The law of the divine life is the spontaneous power of the divine life; it is the natural characteristic and the innate, automatic function of the divine life. The processed and consummated Triune God is installed in our spirit as a spontaneous power. This life has a natural characteristic, which comes out all the time. The Triune God is an innate, automatic function in us.
We can illustrate this with the law of a birds life. When a bird is hatched，it has a life and that life has a law. As the life within the small bird grows and increases, there is a spontaneous power in its life. There is an innate, automatic function in its life that builds up and. shapes the bird. By the growth and dispensing of its life, the law of its life builds and conforms the bird, into a bird shape. In like manner, we have the law of the divine life i.n us. As the divine life grows in us, the law of this life not only builds up Christ within us but also builds up the Body of Christ. Christ is the Head (CoL 2:19), and He is also the Body (1 Cor. 12:12). As the divine life increases within us, the law of life— a spontaneous power and innate, automatic function— causes Christ to "be built up within us. Corporately, when people see us, they should see Christ. As the divine life increases within us, we are conformed to the image of Christ innately, automatically, and spontaneously. It is crucial that we see this.
Every Life Having a Law and Being a Law
Every life has a. law and even is a law. For example, a dog has a life that makes its actions predictable; when it sees a cat, it spontaneously chases it. In the book of Proverbs, Agur says, "There are three things which are too wonderful for me.." (30:18), meaning that these things are beyond his comprehension. One of these wonderful things is "the way of an eagle in the sky" (v. 19a). God wants us to be as an eagle in the air. These verses illustrate the laws of certain lives. An eagle on the edge of a roof is not concerned about the law of gravity. In fact, it has never heard of the law of gravity, nor of Isaac Newton. All it knows is that it has the life of an eagle, and based on the law of that life, it can fly. Flying is innate and spontaneous to the eagle. The eagle is in another realm, which overcomes the law of gravity.
The Highest Life Having the Highest Law
God's life is the highest life, and the law of this life is the highest law (Isa. 40:30-31), In Exodus, the Lord said, "I bare you on eagles' wings" (19:4). In Deuteronomy 32, God the Father is likened, to an eagle: "As an eagle rouses his nest, / Hovers over his young, / Spreads his wings, takes them, / And. bears them up upon his pinions; / So Jehovah alone led him …" (vv. 11-12a ). In this universe God is the real "eagle," and today this "eagle" is in our spirit. In Matthew 23 God the Son is likened to a hen: "Jerusalem, Jerusalem, who kills the prophets and stones those who are sent to her! How often I desired to gather your children together, the way a hen gathers her brood under her wings, and you would not!" (v. 37), and in Matthew 3 the Spirit is likened to a dove (v. 16). Although the law of sin and death, which can be likened to gravity, tries to drag us down, we have an "eagle" life, a "bird" life, that can spontaneously, innately defy the law of sin and death. This ""eagle" life signifies God as our soaring life. The Triune God as an "eagle" is in our spirit, and such a One defies the law of gravity-the law of sin and death-because He cannot be held down by it.
Isaiah 40:31 says, "Yet those who wait on Jehovah will renew their strength; / They will mount up with wings like eagles; / They will run and will not faint; / They will walk and will not become weary." To wait on the Lord is to bring every question and problem to Him. We consult with Him and eagerly look to Him in everything. To wait on the Lord is to look away unto Jesus (Heb. 12:2). When we look away unto Jesus, He is not merely in the third heavens. We have a ladder in our spirit that connects our spirit to the third heavens (Gen, 28:12; John 1:51; 2 Tim 4:22). Therefore, we can look away to the indwelling "eagle" within us. As we gaze on Him, He infuses Himself as the divine and mystical "eagle" into our being. We then begin to sprout wings 0 The New English Bible translates the phrase They will mount up with wings like eagles in Isaiah 40:31 in the following manner: "They will grow wings like eagles." This translation is based on the Septuagint, which says, "They shall put forth new feathers as eagles." This is consistent with our experience. When we are under suffering, trial, or tremendous pressure, we may spend some time with the Lord to gaze on Him. During such a time we may tell Him, "Lord, I do not care about anything. I want to spend this whole time looking at You, I do not want to see anything else." As we gaze on Him, He infuses Himself into us, and anew feathers" begin to come out of us. We a sprout wings" and soar above the problems on this earth. In the physical realm. the problems do not necessarily go away, but in the divine and mystical realm of the law of the divine life, we realize that there are no problems.
The Triune God Having Been Processed through Incarnation, Crucifixion, Resurrection, and Ascension to Become the Law of the Spirit of Life in Our Spirit
The Triune God has been processed through incarnation, crucifixion, resurrection, and ascension to become the law of the Spirit of life installed in our spirit (Rom. 8:3, 11, 16), God sent His own Son in the likeness of the flesh of sin, condemning sin in the flesh; this is the process of incarnation. According to verse 34 ,Christ died, rose, and ascended to the right hand of God; thus, the processes of crucifixion, resurrection, and ascension are indicated. Then verse 16 reveals that Christ is now the Spirit in our spirit witnessing that we are the sons of God; this indicates that the law of the Spirit of life has been installed in our spirit.
Three Laws in the Three Parts of Our Being
The Law of Sin and Death in Our Flesh
There are three different laws in the three different parts of our being. The law of sin and death is the person of Satan residing in our flesh (Rom. 7:18, 23). Our flesh is the transmuted and corrupted body. Our flesh is a "meeting hall" of Satan, sin, and death, and the meeting among these three will not be dismissed until we meet the Lord. We should not think that one day we will grow in life to the point that the flesh will get better. The flesh will never get better.
According to Romans 7 sin is personified. Sin can deceive and kill us (v. 11) and dwells in us (vv. 9 17, 20). This shows us that sin is actually Satan entering into our being to deceive us, to kill us, and to lord it over us. Verses 18-20 say)"For I know that in me, that is, in my flesh, nothing good dwells; for to will is present with me5 but to work out the good is not. For I do not do the good which I will; but the evil which I do not will, this I practice. But if what I do not will, this I do5 it is no longer I that work it out but sin that dwells in me." In verse 20 Paul says, "No longer I …but sin." This is the opposite of Galatians 2:20, where Paul says, "No longer I …but …Christ." What a contrast!
The Law of Good in Our Mind The law of good in our mind that is in our soul derives from our natural human life (Rom. 7:23). In our mind, which is the leading part of our soul, there is a law of good. In our created human life, man wants to do good because he was created in God's image. Outside of us we have the law of God, which is a portrait and testimony of God (w. 12, 22). The law of God shows us who and what God is. He is love, light, holiness, and righteousness. God wants us to express Him in His image and be His portrait, The law of good in our mind wants us to conform to the law of God.
However, whenever we endeavor to keep the law of God, we discover another law. Paul says)"I find then the law with me who wills to do the good, that is, the evil is present with me" (v. 21), When we will to do good, we flip the wrong "switch." We need to ask the Lord to save us from turning this "switch" on. Whenever we exercise our will to do good, we switch on" another law, the law of defeat. Whenever we say, "Lord,help me not to lose my temper ever again," we are "switching on" the law of defeat. As soon as we try to do good, evil is present with us.
In Romans 7 we see a man who says,"To will is present with me, but to work out the good is not" (v. 18). This shows us that our will and our soul are powerless against the law of sin and of death. Sin is a law within us. We observe that young children lie even though no one teaches them to lie. They have the law of sin and of death within them. Whenever we try to conquer the law of sin and of death, we are defeated. Sin is a natural, innate, and powerful force within us. The newspapers today give daily reports of what the law of sin and of death has worked out in the world. Our will is powerless against this law. It is a great revelation to see that sin is a law in our flesh and that the person of Satan resides in our flesh as a law within us. It is also a great revelation to see that our will is powerless against this law.
The Law of the Spirit of Life in Our Spirit
The law of the Spirit of life is the person of the Triune God installed in our spirit (Rom. 8:2) 16). The law of the Spirit of zoe (the divine life) is in our spirit. To live as a human being without this law is terrible. We preach the gospel to people in order to install this law inside of them. The gospel, which is the good news, is that there is another law in our spirit. This law can overcome the law of sin and of death every time. Each time we "switch on" the law of life in our spirit, it spontaneously and automatically, without any struggle or strain, overcomes the law of sin and of death. This is the divine and mystical realm of the consummated Spirit of the new covenant.
We may ask, "Lord , why do I still have this flesh, which is so pitiful, dark, and terrible?" The Lord uses this flesh to force us to turn to the spirit. We have to set our mind on the spirit (Rom. 8:6). The flesh makes us desperate to turn to Him. Thus, the Lord uses our failures and defeats. The failures cause us to apply the blood of the covenant. This ushers us into the Triune God in our spirit so that we can dwell in God and enjoy God as the law of the Spirit of life, the "eagle" life. He dispenses His "eagle" life into our being so that we sprout wings and become God as the "eagle," in life, nature, and function, but not in the Godhead. This is wonderful!
Thus, we have two meetings going on simultaneously in our being. One is the meeting of Satan, sin, and death, and the other is the meeting of the Father, the Son, and the Spirit. Our spirit is the meeting hall of the Father, the Son, and the Spirit. This meeting will never dismiss. In the same way, we can see that we are a miniature garden of Eden. The tree of life and the tree of the knowledge of good and evil were both in the garden (Gen. 2:9). When we struggle to do good, we are partaking of the wrong tree. Thus, we have a choice. We can set our mind on the flesh, which is death and which activates the terrible law of sin, or we can set our mind on the spirit, which activates the Triune God who has been installed in our being as the law of life. We can activate this law of life by calling, "Lord Jesus, I need You."
Being Transferred by the Divine Birth into the Realm of the Divine Life with Its Law
The divine birth has transferred us into a new realm, the realm of the divine life with its law, a realm in which there is no sin, world, or flesh. When we are in our spirit, we see the processed and consummated Triune God only. Brother Lee encouraged us to pray a simple short prayer: "Lord, keep me in my spirit." When we find that we have drifted out of our spirit, that we are setting our mind on the flesh, we can turn back by saying, "Lord, keep me in my spirit." For this reason we need to start our day praying, "Father, strengthen me with power through Your Spirit into my spirit, into the inner man, so that I can be in the realm where there is no sin, flesh, or world, but the Triune God."
Being Upheld by the Law of the Spirit of Life to Have Unconscious and Effortless Victories
In this realm, all victories are unconscious and effortless because the law of the Spirit of life is upholding us, not our own will. We are typically not aware of real victories, in the same way that we are typically not conscious of the normal functioning of our stomach and its digestive process. When we are enjoying the Lord, we spontaneously and unconsciously enjoy the pneumatic Christ as our victory. Brother Nee once said that only that which requires no exertion is true victory.
COOPERATING WITH THE INSTALLED AND OPERATING LAW OF THE SPIRIT OF LIFE
We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life for our organic salvation by ''switching on" this law (Phil. 2:12-13; Rom. 8:2). Praise the Lord that we can "switch on" this law. The switch is our spirit. It is the Lord's mercy that we know that we have a human spirit. The divine Spirit dwells in our spirit and is mingled with our spirit; hence, we are one spirit with the Triune God. We just need to "switch on." The year I met the Lord I fell in love with the Lord, but I did not know very much. Sometimes, I would accidentally bump into the "light switch" of my spirit. Later, under this ministry, I began to see how to operate the switch of my spirit. If we need light, air, or any other spiritual need, we can simply exercise our spirit to switch on." It is God who operates in us (Phil 2:13). Romans 8:2 says, "For the law of the Spirit of life has freed me." The me in this verse is not a passive me, but a ''switching on" me. We need to "switch on" the law of the Spirit of life. When this law is "switched on/, life is dispensed into our being. "The mind set on the spirit is life" (v. 6). As we turn the switch on, He gives life even to our mortal body (v. 11). Life swallows up death in our entire being so that we become a tripartite being of zoe (the divine life).
Walking according to the Spirit
We can cooperate with the law of life by walking according to the spirit (Rom. 8:4). We can pray, "Lord, today constitute me to be a person who is one with You. I would like to switch You on by exercising my spirit to be a person who lives, walks, moves, and has his being according to the spirit." The Lord loves this kind of prayer. He will say "amen" to this prayer. If we do not have such a walk, sooner or later we will be a trouble to the church.
Setting Our Mind on the Spirit and Putting to Death by the Spirit the Practices of the Body
We also "switch on" the law of life by minding the things of the Spirit, setting our mind on the spirit (Rom. 8:5-6), and by putting to death by the Spirit the practices of our body. The phrase by the Spirit you in Romans 8:13 is quite meaningful. It is the Spirit and you, even the "Spirit-you." If it is you only, that is asceticism; if it is the Spirit only, that is mysticism. By the Spirit, we put to death the practices of the body. We certainly do not put to death the body; rather, we put to death the practices of the body.
Being Led by the Spirit of God to Be the Sons of God
We cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life for our organic salvation by being led by the Spirit of God to be sons of God (Rom. 8:14). We need to be led by the Spirit. Paul was such a person. He was a captive of Christ. He said, "I had no rest in my spirit....But thanks be to God, who always leads us in triumph in the Christ35 (2 Cor. 2:13-14), We need to pray,"Lord, make me Your captive; never let me win. Defeat me all the time! I want to be led by the Spirit. I want to respond to Your "paging" me in my spirit. If You want me to go to Memphis or Jacksonville, I want to respond?5 If we respond and move according to His leading, we will be enlivened because we will have escaped our set, settled, and occupied situation. By responding to the leading of the One who has been installed in our spirit, we will be "zoe-fied."
Crying "Abba, Father" in the Spirit of Sonship
We switch on the installed and. operating law of the Spirit of life by crying 'Abba, Father." in the spirit of sonship (Rom, 8:15), If we are low or depressed we can cry, 'Abba, Father." Abba means daddy. If my youngest son cries out, "Daddy!" I respond immediately. In like manner, our Father responds whenever we cry out to Him, 'Abba!" We should not be silent. It is so profound, yet in our experience it is so simple. This is the simplicity toward Christ (2 Cor. 11:3).
The Spirit Witnessing with Our Spirit That We Are the Children of God
We need to cooperate with the installed and operating law of the Spirit of life for our organic salvation by "switching on" this law by the Spirit witnessing with our spirit that we are God's children (Rom. 8:16). This means that our spirit takes the initiative.
Groaning for the Full Sonship, the Redemption of Our Body
Another way to "switch on" the law of life is by groaning for the full sonship, the redemption of our body (Rom. 8:23). Sometimes we may not know what to pray. Romans 8:26 says, ''Moreover, in like manner the Spirit also joins in to help us in our weakness, for we do not know for what we should pray as is fitting, but the Spirit Himself intercedes for us with groanings which cannot be uttered?" We need to groan. Sometimes we can say,"O Lord,I need You. O Lord, I do not know what to pray. Lord, I do not know what to do. O Lord, the church. O Lord, the saints." By such a prayer we "switch on."
GOD ACCOMPLISHING HIS ECONOMY BY DISPENSING HIMSELF INTO US AS THE LAW OF LIFE
God's Economy Being to Dispense Himself into Our Being That Our Being Might Be Constituted with His Being to Be One Constitution with His Being
God accomplishes His economy by dispensing Himself into us as the law of life. God's economy is to dispense Himself into our being that our being might be constituted with His being to be one constitution with His being; this can be accomplished only by God imparting Himself into us as the divine life.
The Law of the Divine Life "sonizing" Us for the Building Up of the Body of Christ
The law of the divine life "sonizes" us for the building up of the Body of Christ. When the Son who is in us as the law of life is activated, He spreads within our being, "sonizing" us.
The Law of Life Spreading within Our Inward Parts to Become Several Laws
When the law of life, which is in our spirit, spreads into our inward parts— our mind, emotion, and will— it becomes several laws (Jer 31:33; Heb. 8:10). Jeremiah uses the singular word law, whereas the writer of Hebrews, when quoting from Jeremiah, uses the word laws. The one law in our spirit spreads into our soul to become the law of our mind, the law of our emotion, and the law of our will. This spreading is the imparting, and the imparting is the inscribing. By the working, the spreading, of the law of life within us, God makes us the same as He is in life, nature, and expression. We are conformed to the image of the firstborn Son of God by the working of the law of life. Romans 8:29 says, "Those whom He foreknew, He also predestinated to be conformed to the image of His Son, that He might be the Firstborn among many brothers/5 He is the Firstborn, and when we are filled with the riches of Christ, we are conformed to His image. He then can be the firstborn Son of God among many brothers who are like Him, in His image.
The Firstborn Son Being the Prototype, the Standard Model, for the Mass Reproduction of the Many Sons of God
The firstborn Son is the prototype, the standard model, for the mass reproduction of the many sons of God, who are His many brothers to constitute His Body for God's corporate expression (Rom. 8:29). God's way to m.ass reproduce this prototype is to work His living prototype, the firstborn Son, into our entire being. If we cooperate with and open up to this wonderful prototype. He will spread outward from our spirit into our soul We can open our mouth, our heart, and our spirit to Him by saying)"Lord I want to open up my entire being to You. Work Yourself into me for the building up of Your Body. 'Sonize' me as much as possible today."
The indwelling prototype, the firstborn Son of God, automatically works in us as the law of life to conform us to His image, to "sonize" use This is the most secret mystery in the entire universe and the most vital and. crucial point in the book of Hebrews. We need to enter into our spirit, which is the practical Holy of Holies, to contact- God in Christ as the law of the Spirit of life. In this way He can be activated within us to spread Himself as the prototype into our tripartite being so that we become His mass reproduction for His universal expression. This is the mystery of godliness (1 Tim 3:16). In His recovery, God is working desperately among us to make every one of us the same as the firstborn Son. The Body of Christ as the new man is the corporate reproduction of the standard model, the firstborn Son of God.
The Law, the Divine Capacity, of Life Constituting Us to Be the Members of the Body of Christ The law, the divine capacity, of life constitutes us to be the members of the Body of Christ, including all kinds of functions— the joints of the rich supply and every part of the Body that functions in its measure (Eph. 4:115 16). Exercising our spirit makes us God in life, nature, expression, and function. Of course, this does not make us God in the Godhead, We will never become an object of worship, and we will never participate in His Godhead. Nevertheless, we do become God in life, nature, and even function. Corporately, we become the "acting God." There is a book in the New Testament called the Acts of the Apostles. The book of Acts is a record of the "acting God" operating through persons who have activated the law of life within their being.
They are being "sonized" and are functioning as sons of God to spread God's kingdom by "gospelizing," "churchizing," and "truthizing" the earth for Christ's coming back.
THE FUNCTION OF THE LAW OF LIFE REQUIRING THE GROWTH IN LIFE
The Law of Life Operating Only as Life Grows
The function of the law of life requires the growth in life, for the law of life functions only as it grows. If a certain life does not grow,the law of that life cannot function. The law of life operates only as life grows (Mark 4:3, 14, 26-29). A simple prayer we can pray every day is, "Lord, grow in me. Grant me this day's growth in life."
Christs intercession on the throne motivates the life-seed that He sowed into us at the time of resurrection (Heb, 7:25; Rom„ 8:34), The firstborn Son is interceding for us so that the life which He has sown into our spirit may be motivated to grow, develop, and saturate all our inward parts until we are completely permeated with His glorified and uplifted being. Many times our utterances in prayer are quotations of the heavenly intercession; whatever happens to us in our spiritual life is either a quotation of the heavenly intercession or a reaction to it. He is interceding for us. We may pray, "O Lord, I hate my flesh. I want to turn to You in my spirit." That is a quotation, a response, or a reaction to His heavenly intercession. You may pray, "Lord, I love You. I consecrate myself to You." That is a. response to His praying that you would give yourself to Him so the seed can grow. On the one hand, He is in our spirit as life growing; on the other hand, He is on the throne in the heavens interceding for the growth and development of the seed He has sown within us.
The Law of Life Functioning to Shape Us, to Conform Us to the Image of Christ
While the divine life grows in us, the law of life functions to shape us, to conform us, to the image of Christ as the firstborn Son of God. The law of life does not regulate us from doing wrong; it regulates the shape of life. On pages 769-770 of the Life-study of Hebrews Brother Lee says:
In understanding the Bible, we have been influenced too much by our natural concepts… The law of life does not regulate us from doing wrong; it regulates the shape of life.... The law of life does not primarily function in the negative sense of telling us what not to do. No, rather, while life grows the law of life functions in the positive sense of shaping us, that is, conforming us to the image of Christ. This is the function of the law of life.
For example, the law of the peach tree life does not regulate it from doing wrong; it regulates it to the shape of that life. The law of life does not primarily function in the negative sense of telling us what not to do; rather, while life grows, the law of life functions in the positive sense of shaping us, that is of conforming us to the image of Christ. Through the function of the law of life, we all shall become the mature sons of God, and God will have His universal, corporate expression.—E. M.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第十八篇　在至圣所里经历经过过程并终极完成的三一神以及有分于基督那作神圣行政中心的代求生活
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来九2～4；启二17，八3
壹　帐幕的图样乃是基督一幅详细的图画，也是我们对基督之经历完满的说明─来八5，2，九24。
贰　希伯来书启示，我们经历基督，可以是外面的，在外院子的祭坛那里和洗濯盆那里；可以是里面的，在圣所的陈设饼那里、灯台那里和香坛那里；也可以是最深入的，在至圣所的见证柜那里、并见证柜里面─来九2～4。
叁　在至圣所的约柜里面，我们经历经过过程并终极完成的三一神─父在祂神圣的性情里作一切供应的源头，基督作隐藏的吗哪和复活的生命，以及生命的灵作内里生命的律：
一　金罐表征神圣的性情，就是父神的性情，父神乃是独一的源头─来九4，彼后一4，太六4，6，18，林前八6上，弗三14～15。
二　金罐里隐藏的吗哪，表征我们最深的经历基督作我们生命的供应─来九4，出十六33～34，启二17：
１　隐藏的吗哪就是隐藏的基督在祂隐藏的生命供应里，就是隐藏在神圣性情里拔尖的一分基督─西三3～4。
２　我们若住在至圣所里，就会享受隐藏的基督作为那保留给祂得胜之寻求者特别的一分属天食物─启二17。
３　借着吃基督作隐藏的吗哪，我们就合并到神人二性的宇宙合并里─约十四20：
a　旧约里的帐幕乃是这宇宙合并的表号。
b　我们越吃基督作隐藏的吗哪，就越合并到这宇宙合并里。
三　发芽的杖表征我们经历复活的基督，使我们蒙神悦纳，在神所赐的职事上有权柄─来九4，民十七1～10：
１　发芽、开花、结果的杖表征基督复活的生命及其权柄─民十七8，约十一25。
２　发芽的杖乃是享受隐藏吗哪的结果；因此，我们越享受隐藏的基督，就越经历复活基督的发芽、开花和结果。
四　约版表征我们经历基督作内里生命的律，将神圣的性情和属性灌注到我们这人里面，把我们模成神长子的形像，使我们成为基督这标准模型的团体复制品─来九4，八10，罗八2，29：
１　基督经过了过程，进入祂所得着的成全和荣耀里，如今祂作为生命之律，正在我们里面重复这过程，把我们带进祂所得着的成全和荣耀里─来二10：
２　当生命之律将基督作到我们这人的每一部分里，使基督成形在我们里面时，神就作到人里面，人就与神调和，神与人也就成为一个实体─这是全宇宙中最隐藏的奥秘─加四19，约十四20，十七21～24。
肆　在金香坛那里，我们有分于基督那作神圣行政中心的代求生活─来九3～4，出三十1～10，启八3：
一　希伯来书的作者把那与祷告有关的香坛算作属至圣所─来九3～4，王上六22，路一10～11。
二　香坛表征基督作代求者，以维持神和祂子民之间的关系─罗八34，来七25：
１　香坛是基督之人位的预表。
２　香坛表征基督在祷告，基督在代求─约十七1～26。
三　我们必须享受基督，不仅作陈设饼桌子、灯台和约柜，更作香坛：
１　香坛是神圣的马达，帐幕和外院子其余各处的活动，都是从这地方推动的。
２　我们若没有香坛，帐幕和外院子的各方面，就无一能在我们的经历中发挥功效。
四　基督的代求生活，就是祂的祷告生活，乃是神圣行政的中心─启八3：
１　神的行政执行的中心是香坛，不是约柜。
２　基督的祷告生活乃是神在地上执行祂的管治的中心─属天的白宫。
３　在香坛所献上的祷告管治全宇宙。
五　基督复活升天以后，个人的基督成了团体的基督；因此，今天在神面前，不仅有个人的基督在代求，也有团体的基督，身体基督，就是头同着身体，在那里代求，以执行祂的代求─林前十二12，徒十二5，12，罗八26～27：
１　我们对香坛若有这样的观点，我们的祷告生活就会彻底翻转过来，我们就会为着神定旨的执行，为着神圣行政的实施，并为着神供应之恩典的分赐而祷告。
２　这种祷告成就神的定旨，满足神的饥渴，并使神的心喜悦。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Eighteen
Experiencing the Processed and Consummated Triune God in the Holy of Holies and Participating in Christ's Interceding Life as the Center of the Divine Administration
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 9:2-4; Rev. 2:17; 8:3
I.　The diagram of the tabernacle (p. 51) is a detailed picture of Christ and a full definition of our experiences of Christ—Heb. 8:5, 2; 9:24.
II.　The book of Hebrews reveals that we may experience Christ at the altar and at the laver in the outer court in an outward way, at the showbread table, at the lampstand, and at the incense altar in the Holy Place in an inner way, and at the ark of the testimony and in the ark of the testimony in the Holy of Holies in the innermost way—Heb. 9:2-4.
III.　In the ark in the Holy of Holies, we experience the processed and consummated Triune God— the Father in His divine nature as the source of all supply, Christ as the hidden manna and the resurrection life, and the Spirit of life as the inner law of life:
A.　The golden pot signifies the divine nature, the nature of God the Father, who is the unique source—Heb. 9:4b; 2 Pet. 1:4; Matt. 6:4, 6, 18; 1 Cor. 8:6a; Eph. 3:14-15.
B.　The hidden manna in the golden pot signifies our experience of Christ as our life supply in the deepest way—Heb. 9:4c; Exo. 16:33-34; Rev. 2:17:
1.　The hidden manna is the hidden Christ in His hidden life supply, the topmost portion of Christ hidden in the divine nature—Col. 3:3-4.
2.　If we abide in the Holy of Holies, we will enjoy the hidden Christ as a special portion of heavenly food reserved for His overcoming seekers—Rev. 2:17.
3.　By eating Christ as the hidden manna, we are incorporated into the universal, divine-human incorporation—John 14:20:
a.　The tabernacle in the Old Testament is a sign of the universal incorporation.
b.　The more we eat of Christ as the hidden manna, the more we are incorporated into this universal incorporation.
C.　The budding rod signifies our experience of Christ in His resurrection as our acceptance by God for authority in the God-given ministry— Heb. 9:4d; Num. 17:1-10:
1.　The budding, blossoming, fruit-yielding rod signifies the resurrection life of Christ with its authority—Num. 17:8; John 11:25.
2.　The budding rod is the issue of the enjoyment of the hidden manna; thus, the more we enjoy the hidden Christ, the more we will experience the budding, blossoming, and fruit-bearing of the resurrected Christ.
D.　The tablets of the covenant signify our experience of Christ as the inner law of life, which infuses the divine nature and attributes into our being, conforms us to the image of the firstborn Son of God, and makes us the corporate reproduction of Christ, the standard model— Heb. 9:4e; 8:10; Rom. 8:2, 29:
1.　Christ has passed through a process to enter into His perfection and glorification, and now, as the law of life, He is repeating this process within us, bringing us into His perfection and glorification—Heb. 2:10.
2.　As the law of life works Christ into every part of our being, causing Christ to be formed in us, God is wrought into man, man is mingled with God, and God and man become one entity—this is the most secret mystery in the whole universe—Gal. 4:19; John 14:20; 17:21-24.
IV.　At the golden incense altar, we participate in the interceding life of Christ as the center of the divine administration—Heb. 9:4a; Exo. 30:1-10; Rev. 8:3:
A.　The writer of Hebrews regarded the incense altar, which is related to prayer, as belonging to the Holy of Holies—Heb. 9:3-4; 1 Kings 6:22; Luke 1:10-11.
B.　The incense altar signifies Christ as the Intercessor to maintain the relationship between God and His people—Rom. 8:34; Heb. 7:25:
1.　The incense altar is a type of the person of Christ.
2.　It signifies Christ praying, Christ interceding—John 17:1-26.
C.　We need to enjoy Christ not only as the table, the lampstand, and the ark but also as the incense altar:
1.　As a divine motor, the incense altar is the place from which the activities in all other places in the tabernacle and the outer court are motivated.
2.　If we do not have the incense altar, none of the aspects of the tabernacle and the outer court will be effective in our experience.
D.　Christ's interceding life, His prayer life, is the center of the divine administration—Rev. 8:3:
1.　The executing center of Gods' administration is the incense altar, not the ark.
2.　The prayer life of Christ is the center—the heavenly White House—of God's execution of His government on earth.
3.　The prayer offered at the incense altar governs the whole universe.
E.　After His resurrection and ascension, the individual Christ became the corporate Christ; thus, before God today not only is the individual Christ interceding, but the corporate Christ, the Body-Christ, the Head with the Body, is also interceding to carry out His intercession—1 Cor. 12:12; Acts 12:5, 12; Rom. 8:26-27:
1.　If we have this view of the incense altar, our prayer life will be revolutionized, and we will pray for the executing of God's purpose, for the carrying out of the divine administration, and for the dispensing of God's supplying grace.
2.　This kind of prayer fulfills God's purpose, satisfies His hunger, and delights His heart.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第十八篇　在至圣所里经历经过过程并终极完成的三一神以及有分于基督那作神圣行政中心的代求生活
Experiencing the Processed and Consummated Triune God in the Holy of Holies and Participating in Christ's Interceding Life as the Center of the Divine Administration
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来九2～4；启二17，八3
壹　帐幕的图样乃是基督一幅详细的图画，也是我们对基督之经历完满的说明─来八5，2，九24。
I.　The diagram of the tabernacle (p. 51) is a detailed picture of Christ and a full definition of our experiences of Christ—Heb. 8:5, 2; 9:24.
贰　希伯来书启示，我们经历基督，可以是外面的，在外院子的祭坛那里和洗濯盆那里；可以是里面的，在圣所的陈设饼那里、灯台那里和香坛那里；也可以是最深入的，在至圣所的见证柜那里、并见证柜里面─来九2～4。
II.　The book of Hebrews reveals that we may experience Christ at the altar and at the laver in the outer court in an outward way, at the showbread table, at the lampstand, and at the incense altar in the Holy Place in an inner way, and at the ark of the testimony and in the ark of the testimony in the Holy of Holies in the innermost way—Heb. 9:2-4.
叁　在至圣所的约柜里面，我们经历经过过程并终极完成的三一神─父在祂神圣的性情里作一切供应的源头，基督作隐藏的吗哪和复活的生命，以及生命的灵作内里生命的律：
III.　In the ark in the Holy of Holies, we experience the processed and consummated Triune God— the Father in His divine nature as the source of all supply, Christ as the hidden manna and the resurrection life, and the Spirit of life as the inner law of life:
一　金罐表征神圣的性情，就是父神的性情，父神乃是独一的源头─来九4，彼后一4，太六4，6，18，林前八6上，弗三14～15。
A.　The golden pot signifies the divine nature, the nature of God the Father, who is the unique source—Heb. 9:4b; 2 Pet. 1:4; Matt. 6:4, 6, 18; 1 Cor. 8:6a; Eph. 3:14-15.
二　金罐里隐藏的吗哪，表征我们最深的经历基督作我们生命的供应─来九4，出十六33～34，启二17：
B.　The hidden manna in the golden pot signifies our experience of Christ as our life supply in the deepest way—Heb. 9:4c; Exo. 16:33-34; Rev. 2:17:
１　隐藏的吗哪就是隐藏的基督在祂隐藏的生命供应里，就是隐藏在神圣性情里拔尖的一分基督─西三3～4。
1.　The hidden manna is the hidden Christ in His hidden life supply, the topmost portion of Christ hidden in the divine nature—Col. 3:3-4.
２　我们若住在至圣所里，就会享受隐藏的基督作为那保留给祂得胜之寻求者特别的一分属天食物─启二17。
2.　If we abide in the Holy of Holies, we will enjoy the hidden Christ as a special portion of heavenly food reserved for His overcoming seekers—Rev. 2:17.
３　借着吃基督作隐藏的吗哪，我们就合并到神人二性的宇宙合并里─约十四20：
3.　By eating Christ as the hidden manna, we are incorporated into the universal, divine-human incorporation—John 14:20:
a　旧约里的帐幕乃是这宇宙合并的表号。
a.　The tabernacle in the Old Testament is a sign of the universal incorporation.
b　我们越吃基督作隐藏的吗哪，就越合并到这宇宙合并里。
b.　The more we eat of Christ as the hidden manna, the more we are incorporated into this universal incorporation.
三　发芽的杖表征我们经历复活的基督，使我们蒙神悦纳，在神所赐的职事上有权柄─来九4，民十七1～10：
C.　The budding rod signifies our experience of Christ in His resurrection as our acceptance by God for authority in the God-given ministry— Heb. 9:4d; Num. 17:1-10:
１　发芽、开花、结果的杖表征基督复活的生命及其权柄─民十七8，约十一25。
1.　The budding, blossoming, fruit-yielding rod signifies the resurrection life of Christ with its authority—Num. 17:8; John 11:25.
２　发芽的杖乃是享受隐藏吗哪的结果；因此，我们越享受隐藏的基督，就越经历复活基督的发芽、开花和结果。
2.　The budding rod is the issue of the enjoyment of the hidden manna; thus, the more we enjoy the hidden Christ, the more we will experience the budding, blossoming, and fruit-bearing of the resurrected Christ.
四　约版表征我们经历基督作内里生命的律，将神圣的性情和属性灌注到我们这人里面，把我们模成神长子的形像，使我们成为基督这标准模型的团体复制品─来九4，八10，罗八2，29：
D.　The tablets of the covenant signify our experience of Christ as the inner law of life, which infuses the divine nature and attributes into our being, conforms us to the image of the firstborn Son of God, and makes us the corporate reproduction of Christ, the standard model— Heb. 9:4e; 8:10; Rom. 8:2, 29:
１　基督经过了过程，进入祂所得着的成全和荣耀里，如今祂作为生命之律，正在我们里面重复这过程，把我们带进祂所得着的成全和荣耀里─来二10：
1.　Christ has passed through a process to enter into His perfection and glorification, and now, as the law of life, He is repeating this process within us, bringing us into His perfection and glorification—Heb. 2:10.
２　当生命之律将基督作到我们这人的每一部分里，使基督成形在我们里面时，神就作到人里面，人就与神调和，神与人也就成为一个实体─这是全宇宙中最隐藏的奥秘─加四19，约十四20，十七21～24。
2.　As the law of life works Christ into every part of our being, causing Christ to be formed in us, God is wrought into man, man is mingled with God, and God and man become one entity—this is the most secret mystery in the whole universe—Gal. 4:19; John 14:20; 17:21-24.
肆　在金香坛那里，我们有分于基督那作神圣行政中心的代求生活─来九3～4，出三十1～10，启八3：
IV.　At the golden incense altar, we participate in the interceding life of Christ as the center of the divine administration—Heb. 9:4a; Exo. 30:1-10; Rev. 8:3:
一　希伯来书的作者把那与祷告有关的香坛算作属至圣所─来九3～4，王上六22，路一10～11。
A.　The writer of Hebrews regarded the incense altar, which is related to prayer, as belonging to the Holy of Holies—Heb. 9:3-4; 1 Kings 6:22; Luke 1:10-11.
二　香坛表征基督作代求者，以维持神和祂子民之间的关系─罗八34，来七25：
B.　The incense altar signifies Christ as the Intercessor to maintain the relationship between God and His people—Rom. 8:34; Heb. 7:25:
１　香坛是基督之人位的预表。
1.　The incense altar is a type of the person of Christ.
２　香坛表征基督在祷告，基督在代求─约十七1～26。
2.　It signifies Christ praying, Christ interceding—John 17:1-26.
三　我们必须享受基督，不仅作陈设饼桌子、灯台和约柜，更作香坛：
C.　We need to enjoy Christ not only as the table, the lampstand, and the ark but also as the incense altar:
１　香坛是神圣的马达，帐幕和外院子其余各处的活动，都是从这地方推动的。
1.　As a divine motor, the incense altar is the place from which the activities in all other places in the tabernacle and the outer court are motivated.
２　我们若没有香坛，帐幕和外院子的各方面，就无一能在我们的经历中发挥功效。
2.　If we do not have the incense altar, none of the aspects of the tabernacle and the outer court will be effective in our experience.
四　基督的代求生活，就是祂的祷告生活，乃是神圣行政的中心─启八3：
D.　Christ's interceding life, His prayer life, is the center of the divine administration—Rev. 8:3:
１　神的行政执行的中心是香坛，不是约柜。
1.　The executing center of Gods' administration is the incense altar, not the ark.
２　基督的祷告生活乃是神在地上执行祂的管治的中心─属天的白宫。
2.　The prayer life of Christ is the center—the heavenly White House—of God's execution of His government on earth.
３　在香坛所献上的祷告管治全宇宙。
3.　The prayer offered at the incense altar governs the whole universe.
五　基督复活升天以后，个人的基督成了团体的基督；因此，今天在神面前，不仅有个人的基督在代求，也有团体的基督，身体基督，就是头同着身体，在那里代求，以执行祂的代求─林前十二12，徒十二5，12，罗八26～27：
E.　After His resurrection and ascension, the individual Christ became the corporate Christ; thus, before God today not only is the individual Christ interceding, but the corporate Christ, the Body-Christ, the Head with the Body, is also interceding to carry out His intercession—1 Cor. 12:12; Acts 12:5, 12; Rom. 8:26-27:
１　我们对香坛若有这样的观点，我们的祷告生活就会彻底翻转过来，我们就会为着神定旨的执行，为着神圣行政的实施，并为着神供应之恩典的分赐而祷告。
1.　If we have this view of the incense altar, our prayer life will be revolutionized, and we will pray for the executing of God's purpose, for the carrying out of the divine administration, and for the dispensing of God's supplying grace.
２　这种祷告成就神的定旨，满足神的饥渴，并使神的心喜悦。
2.　This kind of prayer fulfills God's purpose, satisfies His hunger, and delights His heart.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE EIGHTEEN
EXPERIENCING THE PROCESSED AND CONSUMMATED TRIUNE GOD IN THE HOLY OF HOLIES AND PARTICIPATING IN CHRIST'S INTERCEDING LIFE AS THE CENTER OF THE DIVINE ADMINISTRATION
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
In this message, we come to a matter that is absolutely crucial and vital to God for the carrying out of His administration through His recovery on the earth today. What has been covered thus far in the Crystallization-study of the Epistle to the Hebrews helps to prepare us to respond to and receive the benefit of Christ intercession; Furthermore, we can now enter into His intercession and become those who carry out the prayer ministry of the corporate Christ to execute God's administration to bring the will of the heavens to the earth.
Christ's interceding life is the center of the divine administration. We should not be limited by our present experience. All of the items in this message are for our experience. We have to spend more time at the first altar, at the laver, at the showbread table, at the lampstand, at the incense altar, and at the ark. In the ark we touch the hidden manna, the budding rod, and the tables of the covenant which signify the law of life. Then in oneness with Christ as the ark we go back to the golden incense altar, whose function is for the Holy of Holies, There we merge our praying being with Christ's praying being to learn to govern the universe by intercessory prayer. God has a need for us not only to respond to His intercession but also to join Him in a particular kind of prayer. This prayer binds the enemy, activates God's administration, releases rivers of grace, and causes every part of the outer court and the tabernacle to .operate for our own experience and for the experience of those for whom we are concerned.
The heavens are waiting for the earth to respond. There has been much binding and loosing in the heavens concerning the world situation, the United States, the world economy, the opening up of countries, the salvation of thousands of souls, and the binding of the pernicious and evil work of the enemy against the Lord's recovery. The heavens are waiting for a corporate response from the earth, not to beg and not only to receive bequests but also to exercise the divine government through intercessory prayer.
THE DIAGRAM OF THE TABERNACLE BEING A PICTURE OF CHRIST AND A DEFINITION OF OUR EXPERIENCES OF CHRIST
The diagram of the tabernacle is a detailed picture of Christ and a full definition of our experiences of Christ (Heb, 8:5, 2; 9:24). In addition, the diagram of the tabernacle is a detailed picture of God's economy. God's economy is Christ experienced by us. Christ is a mystery, and our experience is a mystery; therefore, a diagram helps us to know the experience of Christ and to accurately interpret our experiences. Before the tabernacle was constructed, God told Moses, "See… that you make all things according to the pattern that was shown to you in the mountain5" (Heb. 8:5; Exo. 25:40). The book of Revelation shows us that there is a diving heavenly, and mystical reality expressed in the type of the tabernacle (11:19; 21:3).
