Faith creates an organic union, a life union, in which we and Christ are one (1 Cor. 6:17). In this organic union we experience Christ and live Christ (John 14:17; Gal. 2:20; 2 Cor. 5:7). The real experience of Christ is invisible; it is altogether a matter of faith (Heb. 11:1; Eph. 3:17). We should not put our trust in anything extraordinary or spectacular; instead, we should take the way of faith, which is to read the Word and believe it (Rom. 10:8, 17). The devil can do miracles, so we should not seek for them. We should read the Bible, study the ministry that opens up the Bible, and say amen to the Lord's word.
God Apportioning a Measure of Faith to Each Member in the Body In the Body "God has apportioned to each a measure of faith" (Rom. 12:3). God first allotted faith to us in quality (2 Pet. 1:1), and then He apportioned it in quantity. How much faith we have depends upon God's apportioning. We should esteem ourselves according to the faith that God has apportioned to us, that is, according to the measure of God's element that has been infused into us. We all have the same faith in quality. On our side by growth, faith can be increased. On God's side, He measures it. If we have the faith to speak for five minutes, we should not presume to speak for fifty minutes. We should stay within the measure of faith. Romans 12:3 indicates that we may think more highly of ourselves than we ought to think. If we act not out of faith but out of high-mindedness, God is not committed to act or endorse our presumptuousness. He will measure our faith. He will train us to sense what is the measure of faith and to function within that measure. The law of the Body is that we function according to the God-allotted faith. If we stay within our measure, the Body will be organically in order and everyone will feel comfortable. Do not be falsely humble and deny your measure, and do not be proud and overreach it.
Faith Being the Divine Requirement for the Overcomers to Meet Christ in His Triumphant Return and to Receive the Reward of the Kingdom
Faith is the divine requirement for the overcomers to meet Christ in His triumphant return and to receive the reward of the kingdom (Heb. 10:35, 39). Luke 18 contains the parable of the unrighteous judge who will not pay attention to the widow, though her adversary is mistreating her. God is likened to an unjust judge who seems not to care or answer, and we are the widow. We are temporarily without our Husband because the Lord is not visibly here. We are widows on the earth and we have an adversary, a persecutor. We have a slanderer attacking us, so we pray. Initially we may get an instant response, but eventually, we may get no response, and the situation may even get worse. During this time God seems to be silent and indifferent. Yet this is a parable about praying persistently in faith. The Lord says, "I tell you that He will carry out their avenging quickly. Nevertheless, when the Son of Man comes, will He find faith on the earth?" (v. 8). The overcomers have this kind of faith. They are not stumbled by the environment and they are not stumbled by the apparent silence and inactivity of God. They believe Him though He seems to do nothing. They trust in Him though He seems to be silent. They shame the enemy and conquer the enticements of the physical realm by continuous prayer. Eventually, the prayer of the living overcomers will join the prayer of the departed overcomers under the altar who are crying out for vindication (Rev. 6:9-10), Then the Son of Man will return and reward these precious overcomers with the reward of the kingdom.
The believers who live an overcoming life by faith will be found by Christ at His return as the treasures ready to receive the salvation of their souls as the end (result) of their faith (1 Pet. 1:7-9; Heb. 10:39). The overcomers will be rewarded by Christ with the co-kingship and the top enjoyment of divine life with Him in the millennium (Rev. 20:4, 6). To the overcomers the Lord will say, "Well done, good and faithful slave.... Enter into the joy of your master" (Matt. 25:21, 23).
We all want to be an overcomer, but we should not proclaim ourselves as such. Paul is our pattern. He did not have the assurance that he was an overcomer until the very end. We are running the race. We can fail at any time; therefore, we do not trust ourselves. This is altogether a matter of faith. God is for us, so who can be against us? (Rom. 8:31). He is infusing faith into us that whatever betide, there is something in our spirit of faith that rises up to say, "Lord, I praise You. I worship You. I adore You. I love You. I do not feel anything, or see anything, or know anything, but I believe You. I will run this race to the end." When the Lord meets such a one, He will say, "Well done, good and faithful slave. You were faithful over a few things; I will set you over many things. Enter into the joy of your master"(Matt. 25:21). Then we will reign with Him for a thousand years. In this age faith is the unique way to enjoy Christ in His heavenly ministry and to experience all the bequests in the new testament. May the Lord bless us and grace us to take the unique way of faith day by day and step by step until our beloved Lord Jesus returns. "Amen. Come, Lord Jesus!" (Rev. 22:20).—R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第二十二篇　希伯来书中所启示的召会
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来二10～12，三14，一9，三6，四9，十二22～24，28，十三20
壹　召会乃是神许多儿子活的组合，他们是在基督复活里所生出来的许多弟兄─来二10～12，罗八29，约二十17，来一5，五5，徒十三33，彼前一3：
一　神是我们神圣的父，我们是由祂神圣生命所生的神圣儿子，有祂神圣的性情─约二十17，弗一5，加三26，约一12～13，彼后一4。
二　我们是基督许多的弟兄，和长子一样一式；祂是神而人者，我们是人而神者─来二11，一6，罗八29。
三　召会是一个有两种生命和两种性情结合并调和在一起的生机体─利二1～16：
１　召会完全是生命的事─神圣的生命以及被拔高、复活的人性生命─罗一3～4。
２　召会有两种性情─人的性情和神的性情；因此召会是神人二性的─来二14，11。
四　在召会里，神的长子向祂的弟兄宣告父的名；在召会中，神的长子歌颂父─来二12，诗二二22，参太二六30。
贰　召会是基督团体的同伙─来三15，一9：
一　神在宇宙中经营的目标，乃是要完成祂自己荣耀的彰显─来二10，约十七1，5，，22，24，启二一10～11。
二　主耶稣在祂的高举里，就任为主为基督，以管理神的经营，祂也被立为神经纶中承受万有的合法后嗣─徒二36，来一2。
三　长子是神所立的后嗣，我们众子已蒙拯救，和祂同作后嗣，不仅承受救恩，也要与祂一同承受万有─来一14，罗八17，加四7，弗三6，多三7。
四　神的长子基督是神所立的后嗣，而我们这些神的众子是与祂同作后嗣，因此，我们是基督的同伙─来一9，三15：
１　基督和我们是在一个合伙的经营里，在“基督与召会”这神圣的企业里有分于同样的权益─弗五32。
２　作为基督的同伙，我们有分于祂的受膏，并在祂的经营里与祂合作，以达到神经纶的目标，就是神圣者荣耀的彰显─来一9，林后一21，启二一10～11。
叁　召会是神的家─来三4～6,提前三15～16:
一　神的家是神的家庭，是神的儿女在父的生命和实际里活的组合─约一12～13，约壹三1，罗八16。
二　作为神的家，召会是真伯特利，天的门，有神的长子在那里作天梯─创二八10～22，约一51。
三　父的家是经过过程并终极完成之三一神与祂所救赎、重生、变化之选民神人二性的合并─约十四2，20。
四　内住的基督正在将祂自己建造到我们里面，并将我们建造到祂里面，以产生相互的住处─弗三17，约十四2～3，23，撒下七12～14上。
肆　召会，基督的扩大，乃是安息日的安息─约二19，21，三29上，30，林前十二12，来三6，四9：
一　个人的基督是神的安息日（太十一28～29），而召会是基督的扩大；因此，召会也是神安息日的安息。─引用经文
二　召会是神的满足与安息，因为神在召会中得着祂的居所，使祂得着彰显与代表─弗二22。
三　只有一种情形能使神满足─这种情形就是神得着一班人作祂的彰显和代表─来二12，三6，15。
伍　来到召会就是来到新约，来到属天的新耶路撒冷─来十二22～24，八7～13，十一10，16，加四24～26：
一　新约、属天的耶路撒冷、和召会乃是一。
二　接受新约就是进入新耶路撒冷，并来到召会：
１　希伯来八章指明律法的旧约已经被新约顶替。
２　希伯来十二章说，我们乃是来到锡安山，来到活神的城，来到召会，并来到新约的中保这里─22～24节。
陆　召会是不能震动的神的国─来十二28：
一　我们所要领受那不能震动的国，就是基督和祂的扩大：
１　国度其实就是主自己在我们里面作国度─路十七20～21，可四3，26。
２　召会是基督在生命上的扩增，而国度是基督在行政上的扩增。
３　在来世，团体的基督，就是基督同祂的得胜者，要成为大山充满地，使全地成为神的国─但二35，44。
二　我们在召会中，就是生活在今天神的国里─约三3，5，罗十四17，启一9。
三　神的国作为今世的召会生活，乃是使我们借着忍受患难，而在神圣的权柄上有操练和装备，并使我们学习如何在生命中作王─启一9，徒十四22，太十八18，罗五17。
柒　召会是神的群羊，在基督这群羊之大牧人周全、柔细的照顾之下─来十三20～21，彼前二25，五2：
一　神使主耶稣从死人中复活，成为大牧人，照着神的永约，终极完成新耶路撒冷─来十三20。
二　基督天上的职事，主要的是牧养神的召会，就是祂的群羊，结果带进祂的身体，而使徒的职事与基督天上的职事合作，牧养神的群羊─约二一15～17。
三　“众召会若都接受这教训，有分于基督奇妙的牧养，在主的恢复里就会有一次大的复兴。”（活力排，四九页。）

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Twenty-Two
The Church as Revealed in Hebrews
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 2:10-12; 3:14; 1:9; 3:6; 4:9; 12:22-24, 28; 13:20
I.　The church is a living composition of the many sons of God, who are the many brothers of Christ brought forth in His resurrection— Heb. 2:10-12; Rom. 8:29; John 20:17; Heb. 1:5; 5:5; Acts 13:33; 1 Pet. 1:3:
A.　God is our divine Father, and we are His divine sons born of His divine life with His divine nature—John 20:17; Eph. 1:5; Gal. 3:26; John 1:12-13; 2 Pet. 1:4:
B.　As the many brothers of Christ, we are the same as the firstborn Son; He is divine and human, and we are human and divine—Heb. 2:11; 1:6; Rom. 8:29.
C.　The church is an organism with two lives and two natures combined and mingled together— Lev. 2:1-16:
1.　The church is altogether a matter of life— the divine life and the uplifted, resurrected human life—Rom. 1:3-4.
2.　The church has two natures—the human nature and the divine nature; therefore, the church is both human and divine—Heb. 2:14, 11.
D.　In the church the firstborn Son of God declares the Father's name to His brothers, and in the midst of the church the firstborn Son of God sings hymns of praise unto the Father—Heb. 2:12; Psa. 22:22; cf. Matt. 26:30.
II.　The church is a corporate partnership with Christ—Heb. 3:14; 1:9:
A.　The goal of God's operation in the universe is to accomplish a glorious expression of Himself— Heb. 2:10; John 17:1, 5, 22, 24; Rev. 21:10-11.
B.　In His exaltation the Lord Jesus was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation, and He was also designated as the legal Heir to inherit all things in God's economy—Acts 2:36; Heb. 1:2.
C.　The firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir, and we, the many sons, have been saved to be His joint-heirs, inheriting not only salvation but all things with Him—Heb. 1:14; Rom. 8:17; Gal. 4:7; Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7.
D.　Since Christ as the firstborn Son of God is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint-heirs, we are Christ's partners— Heb. 1:9; 3:14:
1.　Christ and we are in one partnership, sharing the same interests in the divine corporation—"Christ and the church"—Eph. 5:32.
2.　As Christ's partners we share in His anointing and cooperate with Him in His operation to reach the goal of God's economy—the glorious expression of the Divine Being— Heb. 1:9; 2 Cor. 1:21; Rev. 21:10-11.
III.　The church is the house of God—Heb. 3:4-6; 1 Tim. 3:15-16:
A.　The house of God is the family of God, a living composition of the children of God in the Father's life and reality—John 1:12-13; 1 John 3:1; Rom. 8:16.
B.　As the house of God, the church is the real Bethel, the gate of heaven, where the firstborn Son of God is the heavenly ladder—Gen. 28:10-22; John 1:51.
C.　The Father's house is a divine and human incorporation of the processed and consummated Triune God with His redeemed, regenerated, and transformed elect—John 14:2, 20.
D.　The indwelling Christ is building Himself into us and building us into Him to produce a mutual abode—Eph. 3:17; John 14:2-3, 23; 2 Sam. 7:12-14a.
IV.　The church, the enlargement of Christ, is the Sabbath rest—John 2:19, 21; 3:29a, 30; 1 Cor. 12:12; Heb. 3:6; 4:9:
A.　The individual Christ is God's Sabbath (Matt. 11:28-29), and the church is the enlargement of Christ; therefore, the church is also God's Sabbath rest.
B.　The church is God's satisfaction and rest because in the church God has His habitation for His expression and representation—Eph. 2:22.
C.　There is only one situation that can satisfy God—a situation in which God has gained a group of people to be His expression and representation—Heb. 2:12; 3:6, 14.
V.　To come to the church is to come to the new covenant and to the heavenly New Jerusalem—Heb. 12:22-24; 8:7-13; 11:10, 16; Gal. 4:24-26:
A.　The new covenant, the heavenly Jerusalem, and the church are one.
B.　To receive the new covenant is to enter the New Jerusalem and come to the church:
1.　Hebrews 8 indicates that the old covenant of law has been replaced by the new covenant.
2.　Hebrews 12 says that we have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, to the church, and to the Mediator of a new covenant—vv. 22-24.
VI.　The church is the unshakable kingdom of God—Heb. 12:28:
A.　The unshakable kingdom which we are receiving is Christ with His enlargement:
1.　The kingdom is actually the Lord Himself as the kingdom within us—Luke 17:20-21; Mark 4:3, 26.
2.　Whereas the church is Christ's increase in life, the kingdom is Christ's increase in administration.
3.　In the coming age the corporate Christ, Christ with His overcomers, will become a great mountain to fill the earth, making the whole earth God's kingdom—Dan. 2:35, 44.
B.　In the church we are living in the kingdom of God today—John 3:3, 5; Rom. 14:17; Rev. 1:9.
C.　The kingdom of God as the church life in this age is for our exercise and equipping in the divine authority through the endurance of sufferings and for our learning how to reign in life—Rev. 1:9; Acts 14:22; Matt. 18:18; Rom. 5:17.
VII.　The church is God's flock under the all-inclusive, tender care of Christ as the great Shepherd of the sheep—Heb. 13:20-21; 1 Pet. 2:25; 5:2:
A.　God raised up the Lord Jesus from the dead to be the great Shepherd to consummate the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant—Heb. 13:20.
B.　Christ's heavenly ministry is mainly to shepherd the church of God as His flock, which issues in His Body, and the apostolic ministry cooperates with Christ's heavenly ministry to shepherd God's flock—John 21:15-17.
C.　"If all the churches receive this teaching to participate in Christ's wonderful shepherding, there will be a big revival in the recovery" (The Vital Groups,?p. 40).

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第二十二篇　希伯来书中所启示的召会
The Church as Revealed in Hebrews
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来二10～12，三14，一9，三6，四9，十二22～24，28，十三20
壹　召会乃是神许多儿子活的组合，他们是在基督复活里所生出来的许多弟兄─来二10～12，罗八29，约二十17，来一5，五5，徒十三33，彼前一3：
I.　The church is a living composition of the many sons of God, who are the many brothers of Christ brought forth in His resurrection— Heb. 2:10-12; Rom. 8:29; John 20:17; Heb. 1:5; 5:5; Acts 13:33; 1 Pet. 1:3:
一　神是我们神圣的父，我们是由祂神圣生命所生的神圣儿子，有祂神圣的性情─约二十17，弗一5，加三26，约一12～13，彼后一4。
A.　God is our divine Father, and we are His divine sons born of His divine life with His divine nature—John 20:17; Eph. 1:5; Gal. 3:26; John 1:12-13; 2 Pet. 1:4:
二　我们是基督许多的弟兄，和长子一样一式；祂是神而人者，我们是人而神者─来二11，一6，罗八29。
B.　As the many brothers of Christ, we are the same as the firstborn Son; He is divine and human, and we are human and divine—Heb. 2:11; 1:6; Rom. 8:29.
三　召会是一个有两种生命和两种性情结合并调和在一起的生机体─利二1～16：
C.　The church is an organism with two lives and two natures combined and mingled together— Lev. 2:1-16:
１　召会完全是生命的事─神圣的生命以及被拔高、复活的人性生命─罗一3～4。
1.　The church is altogether a matter of life— the divine life and the uplifted, resurrected human life—Rom. 1:3-4.
２　召会有两种性情─人的性情和神的性情；因此召会是神人二性的─来二14，11。
2.　The church has two natures—the human nature and the divine nature; therefore, the church is both human and divine—Heb. 2:14, 11.
四　在召会里，神的长子向祂的弟兄宣告父的名；在召会中，神的长子歌颂父─来二12，诗二二22，参太二六30。
D.　In the church the firstborn Son of God declares the Father's name to His brothers, and in the midst of the church the firstborn Son of God sings hymns of praise unto the Father—Heb. 2:12; Psa. 22:22; cf. Matt. 26:30.
贰　召会是基督团体的同伙─来三15，一9：
II.　The church is a corporate partnership with Christ—Heb. 3:14; 1:9:
一　神在宇宙中经营的目标，乃是要完成祂自己荣耀的彰显─来二10，约十七1，5，，22，24，启二一10～11。
A.　The goal of God's operation in the universe is to accomplish a glorious expression of Himself— Heb. 2:10; John 17:1, 5, 22, 24; Rev. 21:10-11.
二　主耶稣在祂的高举里，就任为主为基督，以管理神的经营，祂也被立为神经纶中承受万有的合法后嗣─徒二36，来一2。
B.　In His exaltation the Lord Jesus was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation, and He was also designated as the legal Heir to inherit all things in God's economy—Acts 2:36; Heb. 1:2.
三　长子是神所立的后嗣，我们众子已蒙拯救，和祂同作后嗣，不仅承受救恩，也要与祂一同承受万有─来一14，罗八17，加四7，弗三6，多三7。
C.　The firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir, and we, the many sons, have been saved to be His joint-heirs, inheriting not only salvation but all things with Him—Heb. 1:14; Rom. 8:17; Gal. 4:7; Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7.
四　神的长子基督是神所立的后嗣，而我们这些神的众子是与祂同作后嗣，因此，我们是基督的同伙─来一9，三15：
D.　Since Christ as the firstborn Son of God is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint-heirs, we are Christ's partners— Heb. 1:9; 3:14:
１　基督和我们是在一个合伙的经营里，在“基督与召会”这神圣的企业里有分于同样的权益─弗五32。
1.　Christ and we are in one partnership, sharing the same interests in the divine corporation—"Christ and the church"—Eph. 5:32.
２　作为基督的同伙，我们有分于祂的受膏，并在祂的经营里与祂合作，以达到神经纶的目标，就是神圣者荣耀的彰显─来一9，林后一21，启二一10～11。
2.　As Christ's partners we share in His anointing and cooperate with Him in His operation to reach the goal of God's economy—the glorious expression of the Divine Being— Heb. 1:9; 2 Cor. 1:21; Rev. 21:10-11.
叁　召会是神的家─来三4～6,提前三15～16:
III.　The church is the house of God—Heb. 3:4-6; 1 Tim. 3:15-16:
一　神的家是神的家庭，是神的儿女在父的生命和实际里活的组合─约一12～13，约壹三1，罗八16。
A.　The house of God is the family of God, a living composition of the children of God in the Father's life and reality—John 1:12-13; 1 John 3:1; Rom. 8:16.
二　作为神的家，召会是真伯特利，天的门，有神的长子在那里作天梯─创二八10～22，约一51。
B.　As the house of God, the church is the real Bethel, the gate of heaven, where the firstborn Son of God is the heavenly ladder—Gen. 28:10-22; John 1:51.
三　父的家是经过过程并终极完成之三一神与祂所救赎、重生、变化之选民神人二性的合并─约十四2，20。
C.　The Father's house is a divine and human incorporation of the processed and consummated Triune God with His redeemed, regenerated, and transformed elect—John 14:2, 20.
四　内住的基督正在将祂自己建造到我们里面，并将我们建造到祂里面，以产生相互的住处─弗三17，约十四2～3，23，撒下七12～14上。
D.　The indwelling Christ is building Himself into us and building us into Him to produce a mutual abode—Eph. 3:17; John 14:2-3, 23; 2 Sam. 7:12-14a.
肆　召会，基督的扩大，乃是安息日的安息─约二19，21，三29上，30，林前十二12，来三6，四9：
IV.　The church, the enlargement of Christ, is the Sabbath rest—John 2:19, 21; 3:29a, 30; 1 Cor. 12:12; Heb. 3:6; 4:9:
一　个人的基督是神的安息日（太十一28～29），而召会是基督的扩大；因此，召会也是神安息日的安息。─引用经文
A.　The individual Christ is God's Sabbath (Matt. 11:28-29), and the church is the enlargement of Christ; therefore, the church is also God's Sabbath rest.
二　召会是神的满足与安息，因为神在召会中得着祂的居所，使祂得着彰显与代表─弗二22。
B.　The church is God's satisfaction and rest because in the church God has His habitation for His expression and representation—Eph. 2:22.
三　只有一种情形能使神满足─这种情形就是神得着一班人作祂的彰显和代表─来二12，三6，15。
C.　There is only one situation that can satisfy God—a situation in which God has gained a group of people to be His expression and representation—Heb. 2:12; 3:6, 14.
伍　来到召会就是来到新约，来到属天的新耶路撒冷─来十二22～24，八7～13，十一10，16，加四24～26：
V.　To come to the church is to come to the new covenant and to the heavenly New Jerusalem—Heb. 12:22-24; 8:7-13; 11:10, 16; Gal. 4:24-26:
一　新约、属天的耶路撒冷、和召会乃是一。
A.　The new covenant, the heavenly Jerusalem, and the church are one.
二　接受新约就是进入新耶路撒冷，并来到召会：
B.　To receive the new covenant is to enter the New Jerusalem and come to the church:
１　希伯来八章指明律法的旧约已经被新约顶替。
1.　Hebrews 8 indicates that the old covenant of law has been replaced by the new covenant.
２　希伯来十二章说，我们乃是来到锡安山，来到活神的城，来到召会，并来到新约的中保这里─22～24节。
2.　Hebrews 12 says that we have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, to the church, and to the Mediator of a new covenant—vv. 22-24.
陆　召会是不能震动的神的国─来十二28：
VI.　The church is the unshakable kingdom of God—Heb. 12:28:
一　我们所要领受那不能震动的国，就是基督和祂的扩大：
A.　The unshakable kingdom which we are receiving is Christ with His enlargement:
１　国度其实就是主自己在我们里面作国度─路十七20～21，可四3，26。
1.　The kingdom is actually the Lord Himself as the kingdom within us—Luke 17:20-21; Mark 4:3, 26.
２　召会是基督在生命上的扩增，而国度是基督在行政上的扩增。
2.　Whereas the church is Christ's increase in life, the kingdom is Christ's increase in administration.
３　在来世，团体的基督，就是基督同祂的得胜者，要成为大山充满地，使全地成为神的国─但二35，44。
3.　In the coming age the corporate Christ, Christ with His overcomers, will become a great mountain to fill the earth, making the whole earth God's kingdom—Dan. 2:35, 44.
二　我们在召会中，就是生活在今天神的国里─约三3，5，罗十四17，启一9。
B.　In the church we are living in the kingdom of God today—John 3:3, 5; Rom. 14:17; Rev. 1:9.
三　神的国作为今世的召会生活，乃是使我们借着忍受患难，而在神圣的权柄上有操练和装备，并使我们学习如何在生命中作王─启一9，徒十四22，太十八18，罗五17。
C.　The kingdom of God as the church life in this age is for our exercise and equipping in the divine authority through the endurance of sufferings and for our learning how to reign in life—Rev. 1:9; Acts 14:22; Matt. 18:18; Rom. 5:17.
柒　召会是神的群羊，在基督这群羊之大牧人周全、柔细的照顾之下─来十三20～21，彼前二25，五2：
VII.　The church is God's flock under the all-inclusive, tender care of Christ as the great Shepherd of the sheep—Heb. 13:20-21; 1 Pet. 2:25; 5:2:
一　神使主耶稣从死人中复活，成为大牧人，照着神的永约，终极完成新耶路撒冷─来十三20。
A.　God raised up the Lord Jesus from the dead to be the great Shepherd to consummate the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant—Heb. 13:20.
二　基督天上的职事，主要的是牧养神的召会，就是祂的群羊，结果带进祂的身体，而使徒的职事与基督天上的职事合作，牧养神的群羊─约二一15～17。
B.　Christ's heavenly ministry is mainly to shepherd the church of God as His flock, which issues in His Body, and the apostolic ministry cooperates with Christ's heavenly ministry to shepherd God's flock—John 21:15-17.
三　“众召会若都接受这教训，有分于基督奇妙的牧养，在主的恢复里就会有一次大的复兴。”（活力排，四九页。）
C.　"If all the churches receive this teaching to participate in Christ's wonderful shepherding, there will be a big revival in the recovery" (The Vital Groups,?p. 40).

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TWENTY-TWO
THE CHURCH AS REVEALED IN HEBREWS
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
In Matthew 16 Simon Peter received from the Father the revelation that Jesus was the Christ, the Son of the living God. The Lord told Peter that he was blessed for receiving such a revelation. The Lord then went on to say, "Upon this rock I will build My church, and the gates of Hades shall not prevail against it" (w. 16-18), The Lord's response to Peter's declaration concerning Him shows us how much the church is on His heart and how closely connected Christ is to the church. Christ is a church-loving Christ. He loved the church and gave Himself up for her (Eph. 5:25). Christ is the mystery of God (Col. 2:2), and the church is the mystery of Christ (Eph. 3:4), but the great mystery is Christ and the church (5:32), We do not have a churchless Christ, nor do we have a Christless church. We have a Christ who is for the church and a church who is for Christ. We should therefore not be surprised that in a profound and heavenly book such as Hebrews, which reveals some marvelous things concerning Christ, there would be some precious points concerning the church.
THE LOCAL CHURCHES BEING THE PROCEDURE TO REACH THE GOAL OF GOD'S ECONOMY—THE BODY OF CHRIST
In the book The Practical Points concerning Blending, on page 18, Brother Lee gives a very balanced word concerning the local churches and the Body of Christ:
The highest peak of the Lord's recovery that can really, practically, and actually carry out God's economy is for God to produce not many local churches in a physical way but an organic Body to be His organism. We all have a physical body, but our body actually is not the reality of our being. My body is me, but it is not the reality of my being. Likewise, the churches set up around the globe are a physical frame, but among the churches there may be no reality of the Body of Christ. If we are honest with ourselves, we must admit that this is our situation today. Where is the reality of the Body of Christ? We must love and treasure the actual, practical, and visible local churches. At the same time, we must be clear that God's goal is the organic and mystical Body of Christ. The Body of Christ is the goal of His economy because the Body will consummate the New Jerusalem. The Body is the goal, and the local churches are the procedure to reach this goal.
We need to be fair and balanced concerning the truth of the Body being God's goal and the local churches being the procedure to reach this goal. Some who may be at odds with others in their local church may misuse this word in order to withdraw from the church life. They may say, "The church is only a procedure. We are not here for a procedure; we are here for the Body of Christ as the goal." In saying this they may want to put aside the truth concerning the local churches. This is to mishandle and misuse this word. Without the procedure we cannot reach the goal. The current migrations, for example, are for increasing, enriching, intensifying, and spreading the procedure. Yet these migrations are for the goal of building up the Body of Christ. We love the local churches, and we care for them day and night in practicality.
At the same time, those bearing the responsibility in the churches should be careful that they do not make the local churches a goal rather than a procedure. God's goal is the Body with many churches as the procedure. Paul said to the local church in Corinth, will most gladly spend and be utterly spent on behalf of your souls" (2 Cor. 12:15), Paul gave himself to establish and build up the churches, but he was one with God in His goal. God's goal is something invisible, intrinsic, organic, divine, mystical, and in resurrection.
We need to be faithful to the New Testament revelation by being faithful to its proper interpretation according to what Brother Lee taught us. We must take the word in a pure way. The local churches are mainly physical. While we are practicing the church life we need to touch something intrinsic, organic, divine, and mystical. Some of the saints who have migrated have shared that they are practicing the God-ordained way in a fresh church life, and by practicing that way they are touching something real in the church. That reality is the Body.
In the book The Practical Points concerning Blending, on pages 33-34, Brother Lee speaks of how some saints are unaware of mistakes that have crept in. He says:
They may consider that the recovery is for the recovery of the local churches. I say "yes" with a "but." "Yes." Brother Nee did minister on the local churches, "but" he went on from the local churches to the Body of Christ. The local churches are too much in the expression of the physical things. You have to arrange the elders and set up the deacons to take care of so many outward affairs. But with the Body of Christ, there are no physical things. The local churches are nearly ninety percent physical, but they should be for something spiritual, and this spiritual thing is the Body of Christ.
The book of Hebrews was sent to and read by actual believers in Jerusalem, who were an actual and practical church. They met in physical locations, mainly in homes. They were the real ekklesia, the assembly of the called-out ones. Today we need many more such churches. At the same time, we must admit that the local churches are mainly an expression of the physical things. If we do not see beyond the physicality of the church into the intrinsic essence of the church, we cannot be used by God to build up the Body of Christ within the church. We may be very content with what we are doing in the physical realm of the church, but God may not yet be content. As Brother Lee said, "Where is the reality of the Body of Christ?"
THE CHURCH IN HEBREWS
The focus of this message is the crystals in Hebrews concerning the church. Hebrews 12 speaks of coming to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, to the universal festal gathering (v. 22). Chapter twelve goes on to speak of the heavens and the earth being shaken (v. 26). Then this chapter concludes by saying, ''Therefore receiving an unshakable kingdom, let us have grace, through which we may serve God well- pleasingly with piety and fear; for our God is also a consuming fire" (w. 28-29).
Then in chapter thirteen of Hebrews we are brought down to earth. It says, "Let brotherly love continue. Do not forget hospitality, for through this some, without knowing it, have entertained angels. Remember those who are imprisoned as bound with them, those who are ill treated as being yourselves also likewise treated in body" (w. 1-3). Thus, we need to have a Body-consciousness. Today we need to remember the brothers in mainland China who have been imprisoned for the testimony of Jesus. In the following verses, the exhortation continues by saying, "Let marriage be held in honor among all.... Let your way of life be without the love of money, being satisfied with the things which are at hand" (w. 4-5). This is very practical. Formerly, in chapter twelve we were on Mount Zion, but in Hebrews 13 the writer tells us not to love money, to be satisfied with what we have, instead of dreaming about the future.
Hebrew 13 continues by saying, "But do not forget doing good and sharing with others, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased. Obey the ones leading you and submit to them" (w. 16-17). In these verses the writer speaks of sharing with others in a material and practical way and of obeying the leading ones. Some may say, "I like to hear about the divine and mystical realm and about the great mystery, but please don't speak to me about obeying the leading ones." However, the fact is that if one cannot obey the leading ones by receiving the supply of grace from the heavenly Christ, neither will he have the capacity for the higher truths. We need to obey the leading ones and submit to them because they watch over our souls. It is precious to have brothers who have been dealt with by the Lord in their soul. Christ is the Shepherd and Overseer of our souls (1 Pet. 2:25). In the same way, the proper leading ones are watchful to understand, to know, and to shepherd the souls of the saints. The word here is balanced because it says, "They watch over your souls as those who will render an account" (Heb. 13:17). The leading ones do not need our criticism. The faithful brothers are aware of the weight of responsibility they bear; they know that they will give an account. The vast majority of our dear leading brothers are faithful slaves. We should respect, honor, follow, and submit to the leading ones. Verse 7 says that we should consider the issue of their manner of life and imitate their faith. How much faith the church has depends in large measure on how much faith the leading brothers have. The flock of God cannot go farther than the leading sheep have gone. Brother Lee gave a lengthy testimony of how he learned to live and walk by faith (see The Crystallization of the Epistle to the Romans, pp. 93-98). Reading his testimony may awaken an organic desire to imitate that kind of faith.
In Hebrews 13:18, the writer says, "Pray for us." We might consider that a brother with such a vision would receive his supply directly from God. Surely he received much from God, but he was one who lived in the Body, so he could say, "Pray for us."
The aforementioned references show that the church in Hebrews is quite practical. The writer says that we need to have a married life for the church and manage our finances for the church. We also need to love the brothers, show hospitality, suffer with the suffering saints, and obey the leading ones who watch over our souls. These aspects are very lovely, but they are not the crystals concerning the church in the book of Hebrews. There are seven facets of the church in its intrinsic significance in this book.
THE CHURCH BEING A LIVING COMPOSITION OF THE MANY SONS OF GOD WHO ARE THE MANY BROTHERS OF CHRIST BROUGHT FORTH IN HIS RESURRECTION
The church is a living composition of the many sons of God, who are the many brothers of Christ brought forth in His resurrection (Heb. 2:10-12; Rom. 8:29; John 20:17; Heb. 1:5; 5:5; Acts 13:33; 1 Pet. 1:3). In Christ's resurrection, many brothers, many sons, were brought forth. We need to be one with Him in the life-releasing aspect of His death that the church-building and propagating life would be released. The church is a living composition. This is intrinsic. We can see the people, but we need the eyes of faith to see the composition. It is easy to look at someone in the church and form an outward impression of them, but we need the eyes of faith to realize that this person is a brother. To address one another as "brother" or "sister" is not a term we use as a substitute when we have forgotten another saint's name. We really are brothers having the same Father. Our ages, personalities, cultures, nationalities, colors, and dispositions may be different, but God is really our Father. In Revelation John did not say, "I John, the last and sole-remaining, surviving apostle." He said, "I John, your brother" (1:9).
God Being Our Divine Father and His Believers Being His Divine Sons Born of His Divine Life with His Divine Nature
God is our divine Father, and we are His divine sons born of His divine life with His divine nature (John 20:17; Eph. 1:5; Gal, 3:26; John 1:12-13; 2 Pet. 1:4). In the eyes of God, all the believers, both brothers and sisters, are brothers and sons of God. At the same time, we all are virgins and part of the bride. The brothers in particular need to learn to be as a female in their relationship with the Lord. We need to pray, "Let him kiss me with the kisses of his mouth!" (S. S. 1:2). I am a man, but when I am with my Lord Jesus, my lovely Bridegroom, I love being kissed by Him. With Him, my head is covered and I am veiled, and I submit to Him as my Head. The church is wonderful. All the sisters are brothers and sons, and all the brothers are virgins part of the bride. Our destiny is to be part of the wife, to be "Mrs. Christ." At the same time, God is our divine Father, and we are His divine sons born of His life with His divine nature.
The Many Brothers of Christ Being the Same as the Firstborn Son
As the many brothers of Christ, we are the same as the firstborn Son; He is divine and human, and we are human and divine (Heb. 2:11; 1:6; Rom, 8:29). In His position as the only begotten Son of God, we are not the same as He. But in His status as the firstborn Son, His many brothers are the same as He. He is divine and human, and we are human and divine. He was divine and became human, and we are human becoming divine.
The Church Being an Organism with Two Lives and Two Natures Combined and Mingled Together
The church is an organism with two lives and two natures combined and mingled together (Lev. 2:1-16). The church is a dual-natured entity. The church is a hybrid, consisting of two lives joined and two natures mingled and combined together. The church is altogether a matter of life—the divine life and the uplifted, resurrected human life (Rom, 1:3-4). There is a filter in each of our spirits, and nothing natural can get through. As a line from Hymns, #1226 says, "Nothing natural does the Body life allow.n The Body allows only the resurrected, uplifted humanity that is in the process of being developed in us. The church has two natures—the human nature and the divine nature; therefore, the church is both human and divine (Heb. 2:14, 11). The brothers and sisters are as acacia wood overlaid with gold (Exo. 25:10-11), which signifies the uplifted humanity of Christ with His divinity. We should see and appreciate the gold and bear one another in love while the gold increases within us.
The Firstborn Declaring the Father's Name to His Brothers in the Church and Singing Hymns of Praise unto the Father in the Midst of the Church
In the church the firstborn Son of God declares the Father's name to His brothers, and in the midst of the church the firstborn Son of God sings hymns of praise unto the Father (Heb. 2:12; Psa. 22:22). On the night before He was betrayed, the Lord observed the Passover with the disciples, and in so doing fulfilled the Old Testament dispensation. Then He established His table with the bread and with the cup (Matt 26:26-28). Afterwards, they sang a hymn (v. 30). Surely that hymn was sung to the Father. The Son took the lead in singing a hymn to the Father. Before He was crucified He was among and with the disciples. However, in resurrection He became the pneumatic Christ to breathe Himself as the Spirit into the disciples (John 20:22), who had become His brothers (v. 17). Now He sings from within our mingled spirit.
When we have the proper worship to the Father, there is a certain point in the Lord's table when the firstborn Son, who has been the focus of our remembrance, takes a turn in our being. At this point, which is very organic, He takes the lead within us as the Spirit to sing to the Father. He sings in our singing. If we have a proper spiritual ear, we will hear Christ singing in the brothers. He is not among us in a physical way; He is in us and among us in a divine, mystical, pneumatic way as the singing Son of God. In the Lord's table we enter into the coinhering relationship of the Father and the Son. When it is a time of remembrance, the Father delights that we focus on the beloved Son in whom He is well pleased. We remember Him and declare His death. Then at a certain point in the table meeting, there is a turn and the Son wants to direct us to the Father as the unique, yet hidden, source. He leads us to worship the Father that the Father?s thirst may be quenched and that His hunger may be satisfied in the church which is a living composition of the many sons of God and the many brothers of Christ. How glad we are to be in the church!
Some of the young people were foreordained by God to be born into a family that had come into such a church life. These young saints may not have a dramatic story to tell; yet it is a mercy that they were born into such a church family and were preserved by the Lord's grace. As they grow up, they can make a decision on their own concerning the church life. They may say, "I was born here, but I am not here only because my parents are here. I choose to be in this wonderful place." It is wonderful for the second, third, and fourth generation saints in the church life to honor the God of their fathers and to take a stand in their generation for the divine-human, mingled, incorporated, organic, living composition, which is the church.
THE CHURCH BEING A CORPORATE PARTNERSHIP WITH CHRIST
The Goal of God's Operation in the Universe Being to Accomplish a Glorious Expression of Himself
The church is a corporate partnership with Christ (Heb. 3:14; 1:9). The goal of God's operation in the universe is to accomplish a glorious expression of Himself (Heb. 2:10; John 17:1, 5, 22, 24; Rev. 21:10-11).
The Lord Jesus Being Inaugurated as Lord and Christ to Administrate God's Operation, and Christ Being Designated the Legal Heir to Inherit All Things in God's Economy
In His exaltation the Lord Jesus was inaugurated as Lord and Christ to administrate God's operation, and He was also designated as the legal Heir to inherit all things in God's economy (Acts 2:36; Heb. 1:2). The firstborn Son is the unique designated Heir to inherit all things. However, He is a wonderful person who wants to share the inheritance and enlarge the corporation by adding more and more partners. In today's law firms it is a big thing to become a partner. We have been born into a marvelous corporate partnership.
The Many Sons Having Been Saved to Be Joint-heirs, Inheriting Salvation and All Things with the Firstborn Son
The firstborn Son is God's appointed Heir, and we, the many sons, have been saved to be His joint-heirs, inheriting not only salvation but all things with Him (Heb. 1:14; Rom. 8:17; Gal. 4:7; Eph. 3:6; Titus 3:7).
The Many Sons Being Christ's Partners
Since Christ as the firstborn Son of God is God's appointed Heir and we as the many sons of God are His joint-heirs, we are Christ's partners (Heb. 1:9; 3:14), We might say that the name of this corporation is "Jehovah and Sons, Glory Incorporated." God has enlarged His Son by- duplicating Him in the many sons, the many brothers, and has attached them to His Son, the Anointed, to make them co-anointed joint-heirs and joint-partners. If we see this we will have at least a moment of ecstasy. We can tell the devil, "I am a partner of the firstborn Son of God. I am a partner in the 'Glory Corporation.' I am becoming part of the glory of God. Satan, you are a liar and an accuser, and you will roast forever in the lake of fire prepared specifically for you. While you are in torment there day and night, I will join all of the conformed and glorified sons of God as the New Jerusalem; we will enjoy our inheritance and execute our partnership as co-owners of the new heaven and new earth." While we are meeting in a practical church life, we are learning how to experience our partnership.
Christ and the Many Brothers Being in One Partnership, Sharing the Same Interests in the Divine Corporation— "Christ and the Church"
Christ and we are in one partnership, sharing the same interests in the divine corporation— "Christ and the church" (Eph. 5:32). First John 1:3 speaks of the fellowship of the apostles and the Triune God. Note 3 on this verse in the Recovery Version says, "Fellowship here indicates a putting away of private interests and a joining with others for a certain common purpose. We must lay aside our private interests and join with the apostles and the Triune God to carry out His economy. Paul could send Timothy to Philippi and say, "For I have no one like-souled who will genuinely care for what concerns you; for all seek their own things, not the things of Christ Jesus" (Phil. 2:20-21). Presumably some of the co-workers within the domain of the work related to Paul were not fully and absolutely seeking the Father's interests. In contrast, it is a real blessing to be with brothers who have no private interests in the Lord's recovery. How good it is to be with those who only care for what is in our Father's heart and for Christ in His all-inclusiveness! In such a situation, all the brothers are not striving or competing with one another but sharing a portion in this corporation and enterprise.
Christ's Partners Sharing in His Anointing and Cooperating with Him in His Operation to Reach the Goal of God's Economy—— the Glorious Expression of the Divine Being
As Christ's partners we share in His anointing and cooperate with Him in His operation to reach the goal of God's economy一the glorious expression of the Divine Being (Heb. 1:9; 2 Cor. 1:21; Rev. 21:10-11). He is Joshua and we are Caleb. He has chosen to go into the good land of the kingdom rest with His partners. The Father wants this, and the Firstborn says, "Amen."
THE CHURCH BEING THE HOUSE OF GOD
The House of God Being the Family of God, a Living Composition of the Children of God in the Father's Life and Reality
The church is the house of God (Heb. 3:4-6; 1 Tim. 3:15-16). The house of God is the family of God, a living composition of the children of God in the Father's life and reality (John 1:12-13; 1 John 3:1; Rom. 8:16).
The Church Being the Real Bethel, the Gate of Heaven, and the Firstborn Son of God Being the Heavenly Ladder
As the house of God, the church is the real Bethel, the gate of heaven, where the firstborn Son of God is the heavenly ladder (Gen. 28:10-22; John 1:51). There is much traffic on this ladder. Our spirit is the gate of heaven. Let us go to heaven by going to our spirit and through our spirit into another realm. This is the intrinsic significance of the church in the midst of the practical and seemingly ordinary and many times imperfect church life. We do not want to withdraw from the church to pursue a utopia. We will be stumbled if we are looking for an ideal or perfect local church. One cannot be found in the New Testament. There were opposers, Judaizers, and other kinds of problems in many of the churches.
Yet we need to see that the house of God is in the midst of the church where we are. Such a place is Bethel and the gate of heaven.
When I met the church I had the feeling of being home. John 14:2 says, "In My Father's house are many abodes....! go to prepare a place for you." There is a place reserved for each of us in the Father's house; no one else can fill our place. Christ is interceding, and we also will spend time at the incense altar to pray for all our brothers and sisters to come home. We do not criticize or judge them; instead, we have a robe to cover them and a fattened calf to feed them. We rejoice with God the Father. A family is not a law court, but a loving, intimate unit full of life. This is the church. Eventually, we all will be full-grown and fully matured as the Father's house.
The Father's House Being a Divine and Human Incorporation of the Processed and Consummated Triune God with His Redeemed, Regenerated, and Transformed Elect
The Father's house is a divine and human incorporation of the processed and consummated Triune God with His redeemed, regenerated, and transformed elect (John 14:2, 20). The indwelling Christ is building Himself into us and building us into Him to produce a mutual abode (Eph. 3:17; John 14:2-3, 23; 2 Sam. 7:12-14a). Ephesians 3:16-17, a crucial prayer, says, "To be strengthened with power through His Spirit into the inner man, that Christ may make His home in your hearts through faith."Christ builds the church by building Himself into our being and by building us into the being of the Triune God.
THE CHURCH AS THE ENLARGEMENT OF CHRIST BEING THE SABBATH REST
The Individual Christ Being God's Sabbath, and the Church as the Enlargement of Christ Also Being God's Sabbath Rest
The church, the enlargement of Christ, is the Sabbath rest (John 2:19, 21; 3:29a, 30; 1 Cor. 12:12; Heb. 3:6; 4:9). The individual Christ is God's Sabbath (Matt. 11:28-29), and the church is the enlargement of Christ; therefore, the church is also God's Sabbath rest. Christ personally is the Sabbath rest, and as the enlarged corporate Christ, He is still the Sabbath rest. The corporate Christ is the church; hence, the church is God's Sabbath rest.
God Having His Habitation for His Expression and Representation in the Church as His Satisfaction and Rest
God is satisfied in the church because in the church He sees something of His image and dominion. In the church God has His habitation for His expression and representation (Eph. 2:22). If God is not at rest we cannot be at rest. Our rest is a by-product of the Father's rest. When God is satisfied, He is at rest. There is only one situation that can satisfy God— a situation in which God has gained a group of people to be His expression and representation (Heb, 2:12; 3:6,14). It is a universal building expressed locally. God is at rest only when Christ is expressed.
TO COME TO THE CHURCH BEING TO COME THE NEW COVENANT AND TO THE HEAVENLY NEW JERUSALEM
The New Covenant, the Heavenly Jerusalem, and the Church Being One
To come to the church is to come to the new covenant and to the heavenly New Jerusalem (Heb. 12:22-24; 8:7-13; 11:10, 16). The new covenant, the heavenly Jerusalem, and the church are one. Hebrews 12 says that we have come to the heavenly Jerusalem and to the church. According to Galatians 4, when we come to the New Jerusalem we come to the new covenant (w. 24-26), The new covenant and the New Jerusalem are our mother. The mother is composed of all the sons produced by the Father. If we take the way of the new covenant, we are brought to the church. When we come into the church, we come to the new covenant. When we come to the new covenant by coming to the church, we come to today's developing expression of the New Jerusalem. Hence, we have come to Mount Zion and to the heavenly Jerusalem.
To Receive the New Covenant Being to Enter the New Jerusalem and to Come to the Church
To receive the new covenant is to enter the New Jerusalem and come to the church. When we come to the proper church life we are brought not to the old covenant but to the new covenant. The more we receive the bequests of the new covenant, the more we will be ushered into another realm. The New Jerusalem is in the divine and mystical realm. The Body of Christ, which is the New Jerusalem in its process of being consummated, is also in this realm.
The Old Covenant of Law Having Been Replaced by the New Covenant
Hebrews 8 indicates that the old covenant of law has been replaced by the new covenant. We should not violate the church by bringing any old covenant things into our church practice. We should be watchful, faithful shepherds to ensure that nothing of the law or of the old covenant is laid as a weight upon the lovely brothers and sisters. They need the Spirit of grace, the life supply, our prayers, the ministry of life, and all the things of the new covenant.
The Believers Coming to Mount Zion, to the City of the Living God, to the Church, and to the Mediator of a New Covenant
Hebrews 12 says that we have come to Mount Zion, to the city of the living God, to the church, and to the Mediator of a new covenant (w. 22-24).
THE CHURCH BEING THE UNSHAKABLE KINGDOM OF GOD
The church is the unshakable kingdom of God (Heb. 12:28). We should not put our security in the stock market, in any earthly government, or in any leader. The heavens and the earth will be shaken. However, we are in the most secure place in the universe. The proper church life, as an unshakable kingdom, is our security and refuge. We are in the process of receiving the unshakable kingdom.
The unshakable kingdom which we are receiving is Christ with His enlargement. The kingdom is actually the Lord Himself as the kingdom within us (Luke 17:20-21; Mark 4:3, 26). Whereas the church is Christ's increase in life, the kingdom is Christ's increase in administration. In the coming age the corporate Christ, Christ with His overcomers, will become a great mountain to fill the earth, making the whole earth God's kingdom (Dan. 2:35, 44). In the church we are living in the kingdom of God today (John 3:3, 5; Rom. 14:17; Rev. 1:9). The kingdom of God as the church life in this age is for our exercise and equipping in the divine authority through the endurance of sufferings and for our learning how to reign in life (Rev. 1:9; Acts 14:22; Matt. 18:18; Rom. 5:17).
THE CHURCH BEING GOD'S FLOCK UNDER THE CARE OF THE GREAT SHEPHERD
The church is God's flock under the all-inclusive, tender care of Christ as the great Shepherd of the sheep (Heb. 13:20-21; 1 Pet. 2:25; 5:2). The matter of shepherding was very much on Brother Lee's heart at the very end of his life. He was concerned that we would have the loving and forgiving heart of our Father God and the shepherding and seeking spirit of our Savior Christ. We can only have this by the law of life bringing this kind of heart and spirit into us. It also helps to pray, "Father, make my heart a duplication of Your loving and forgiving heart."
God raised up the Lord Jesus from the dead to be the great Shepherd to consummate the New Jerusalem according to God's eternal covenant (Heb. 13:20). The New Jerusalem is a bequest that will unfold organically as we enjoy the bequests in this new testament.
Christ's heavenly ministry is mainly to shepherd the church of God as His flock, which issues in His Body, and the apostolic ministry cooperates with Christ's heavenly ministry to shepherd God's flock (John 21:15-17). He is interceding and shepherding in the heavens, and we are learning to shepherd and to be shepherded in mutuality in the church life. In The Vital Groups, page 40, Brother Lee says, "If all the churches receive this teaching to participate in Christ's wonderful shepherding, there will be a big revival in the recovery."—R. K.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第二十三篇　取用恩典以奔跑赛程，好得着国度的赏赐
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来四16，十二1，15，28～29；林前九24～27；腓三12～14；提后四7～8
壹　基督徒的一生就是一个赛程，我们必须奔跑这赛程，好得着奖赏，就是不能坏的华冠，这是主所要赏给赢得赛程之得胜圣徒的─来十二1，林前九24～25：
一　所有得救的基督徒都必须奔跑这赛程，好赢得奖赏（林前九24）；这奖赏不是指一般的救恩（弗二8，林前三15），乃是指特别的赏赐（来十35，林前三14）。─引用经文
二　使徒保罗奔跑赛程，跑尽了他当跑的赛程，并且赢得了奖赏，就是公义的冠冕，这也是主要赏赐凡爱祂显现之人的─林前九26～27，腓三12～14，徒二十24，提后四7～8。
三　我们这些主的信徒，都已借着信基督接受了救恩和永远的生命，我们不会永远灭亡，（约三16，36，十28～29）但我们能否得着祂的赏赐，逃避时代的惩罚，乃在于我们怎样奔跑赛程─太二五30：
１　我们需要治服我们的身体，使其成为被征服的俘虏，像奴隶一样服事我们，好成就我们神圣的目的─林前九27，西三5，罗八13。
２　我们必须极其儆醒的奔跑我们的赛程，使我们在基督的审判台前，不致不蒙称许，并被弃绝（林后五10），不配得着要来国度的赏赐─太二四42～46，可十三33～37，路十二37，太七21～23，二五11～12。
３　我们必须以最完满的享受基督、赢得基督为标竿，而向这标竿竭力追求，为要得着奖赏，就是在千年国里对基督极点的享受，作为奔跑新约赛程之得胜者的赏赐─腓三12～14：
a　为要赢得基督到最完满的地步，我们需要忘记背后，甚至不停留在我们已往对基督的经历中。
b　不论已往的经历多真实，我们若停留在其中，就会受阻挠，不能进一步追求基督。
c　我们必须天天努力面前的，更多得着在我们前面之基督那广阔、未经探寻的范围。
４　我们必须作精明的童女，赎回光阴，被那灵充满，也必须作忠信的奴仆，完全使用主的恩赐，使我们能在要来的国度里有分于主的快乐─太二五2～4，22～23。
５　留在至圣所，我们的灵里，就是继续不断的奔跑赛程─徒十七16，十九21，二十22，罗一9，林后二13：
a　我们越奔跑，至圣所就越扩大，从帐幕扩大到圣殿，最终扩大到新耶路撒冷─出二六8，16，王上六20。
b　至终，整个新耶路撒冷将是扩大的至圣所─启二一16。
６　我们今天必须活在国度的实际里（太五～七），好使我们在主回来时，能得着国度的实现为赏赐（太二四～二五）：
a　诸天的国乃是最高的要求，父神圣的生命也是最高的供应，以达到这要求─太五48，路六35。
b　我们要活在国度的实际里，就必须操练灵里贫穷，清心，并有超过经学家和法利赛人之义的义─太五3，8，20。
c　我们要活在国度的实际里，就必须赦免别人（太十八21～22，35，弗四32，五2），并寻求得着别人的赦免（太五23～24）。─引用经文
d　我们必须活出公义、和平、并圣灵中的喜乐，这些乃是神国的实际─罗十四17。
贰　我们必须取用恩典，跑完我们的赛程，好得着国度的赏赐─来十二28，15，十三25：
一　恩典乃是神在基督里作为那灵分赐到我们里面，作我们在经历中的享受─约一17，来十29下，腓四23，提后四22，林前五10，加二20。
二　我们要领略并有分于希伯书所揭示的一切，就需要恩典：
１　我们要取用恩典，接受恩典，就需要来到施恩的宝座前，使我们得恩典，作应时的帮助─来四16。
２　我们借着操练我们的灵，摸着至圣所里施恩的宝座，就得以享受恩典的灵（十29），我们的心也就靠恩典得坚固（十三9）：─引用经文
a　神在祂经纶中的恩典是丰富的、是繁增的、又是洋溢的─弗二7，彼前一2下，彼后一2，弗一6～8。
b　在以西结书里，鹰翅预表神在基督里的恩典，应用到我们身上作力量和能力，为着我们的行动，并为著作我们的保护，作我们的藏身之处─结一6下，9上，出十九4，赛四十31，林后十二9，林前十五10，诗十七8，五七1，六三7，九一4。
c　我们能借着在不朽坏之中爱主，而接受恩典─弗六24。
d　我们能借着与主的谦卑是一，而接受恩典─彼前五5～6。
e　我们能享受祂恩典的话─徒二十32，耶十五16。
f　我们在一的真正立场上，在召会生活中能享受生命的恩典，就是降在锡安山上黑门的甘露所预表的─诗一三三。
g　我们经历经过过程之三一神作恩典，使我们能成为神诸般恩典的好管家，就是与我们尊大的大祭司是一的人，在祂天上的职事里，将经过过程的神供应到人里面，作人的享受─彼前四10，来七1，创十四18～20。
３　我们如此享受恩典，就能奔那摆在前头的赛程（来十二1），以达到神经纶的目标。─引用经文

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Thirty
Taking Grace to Run the Race for the Kingdom Reward
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 4:16; 12:1, 15, 28-29; 1 Cor. 9:24-27; Phil. 3:12-14; 2 Tim. 4:7-8
I.　The Christian life is a race, and we must run this race to receive the prize, the incorruptible crown, which the Lord will award to His overcoming saints who win the race—Heb. 12:1; 1 Cor. 9:24-25:
A.　All the saved Christians must run the race to win the prize (1 Cor. 9:24), which is not salvation in the common sense (Eph. 2:8; 1 Cor. 3:15) but a reward in a special sense (Heb. 10:35; 1 Cor. 3:14).
B.　The apostle Paul ran the race, finished his course, and won the prize, the crown of righteousness, with which the Lord will also reward all those who have loved His appearing—1 Cor. 9:26-27; Phil. 3:12-14; Acts 20:24; 2 Tim. 4:7-8.
C.　As His believers, we have all received salvation and eternal life through faith in Christ, and we will not suffer eternal perdition (John 3:16, 36; 10:28-29), but our being rewarded by Him and escaping dispensational punishment depends on how we run the race—Matt. 25:30:
1.　We need to subdue our body and make it a conquered captive to serve us as a slave for fulfilling our holy purpose—1 Cor. 9:27; Col. 3:5; Rom. 8:13.
2.　We must be very much on the alert to run our course so that we might not be disapproved and rejected at the judgment seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:10) and be found unworthy of the reward of the coming kingdom—Matt. 24:42-46; Mark 13:33-37; Luke 12:37; Matt. 7:21-23; 25:11-12.
3.　We must pursue toward the goal, the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ, for the prize, the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom as a reward to the victorious runners of the New Testament race—Phil. 3:12-14:
a.　In order to gain Christ to the fullest extent, we need to forget the past, not even lingering in our past experiences of Christ.
b.　Lingering in our past experiences, however genuine they are, frustrates our further pursuing of Christ.
c.　We must stretch forward day by day to gain more of the vast unexplored territory of the Christ before us.
4.　We must be the wise virgins, redeeming the time to be filled with the Spirit, and the faithful slaves, using the Lord's gift to the fullest extent, so that we can participate in the Lord's joy in the coming kingdom—Matt. 25:2-4, 22-23.
5.　To remain in the Holy of Holies, our spirit, is to continue the running of the race—Acts 17:16; 19:21; 20:22; Rom. 1:9; 2 Cor. 2:13:
a.　The more we run, the larger the Holy of Holies becomes, expanding from the tabernacle to the temple and ultimately to the New Jerusalem—Exo. 26:8, 16; 1 Kings 6:20.
b.　Eventually, the entire New Jerusalem will be the enlarged Holy of Holies—Rev. 21:16.
6.　We must live in the reality of the kingdom today (Matt. 5—7) so that we can be rewarded with manifestation of the kingdom at the Lord's coming back (Matt. 24—25):
a.　The kingdom of the heavens is the highest demand, and the divine life of the Father is the highest supply to meet that demand—Matt. 5:48; Luke 6:35.
b.　To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must exercise to be poor in spirit, to be pure in heart, and to have the righteousness which exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees—Matt. 5:3, 8, 20.
c.　To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must forgive others (Matt. 18:21-22, 35; Eph. 4:32; 5:2) and seek forgiveness from others (Matt. 5:23-24).
d.　We need to live out righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit, which are the reality of the kingdom of God—Rom. 14:17.
II.　We must take grace to finish our course for the kingdom reward—Heb. 12:28, 15; 13:25:
A.　Grace is God in Christ as the Spirit dispensed into our being for our experiential enjoyment— John 1:17; Heb. 10:29b; Phil. 4:23; 2 Tim. 4:22; 1 Cor. 15:10; Gal. 2:20.
B.　To realize and participate in all the things unveiled in Hebrews, we need grace:
1.　To take grace, to have grace, we must come forward to the throne of grace that we may find grace for timely help—Heb. 4:16.
2.　When we touch the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies through the exercise of our spirit, we enjoy the Spirit of grace (Heb. 10:29) and our heart is confirmed by grace (13:9):
a.　The grace of God in His economy is rich, multiplying, and abounding—Eph. 2:7; 1 Pet. 1:2b; 2 Pet. 1:2; Eph. 1:6-8.
b.　In Ezekiel the eagle's wings typify the grace of God in Christ applied to us as the strength and power for our move and for our protection, our hiding place—Ezek. 1:6b, 9a; Exo. 19:4; Isa. 40:31; 2 Cor. 12:9; 1 Cor. 15:10; Psa. 17:8; 57:1; 63:7; 91:4.
c.　We can receive grace by loving the Lord in incorruptibility—Eph. 6:24.
d.　We can receive grace by being one with the Lord in His humility—1 Pet. 5:5-6.
e.　We can enjoy the word of His grace—Acts 20:32; Jer. 15:16.
f.　We can enjoy the grace of life, typified by the descending dew of Hermon on the mountains of Zion, in the church life on the genuine ground of oneness—Psa. 133.
g.　Our experience of the processed Triune God as grace enables us to be good stewards of the varied grace of God, those who are one with our great High Priest in His heavenly ministering of the processed God into people for their enjoyment— 1 Pet. 4:10; Heb. 7:1; Gen. 14:18-20.
3.　By such an enjoyment of grace, we run the race set before us (Heb. 12:1) that we may reach the goal of God's economy.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第二十三篇　取用恩典以奔跑赛程，好得着国度的赏赐
Taking Grace to Run the Race for the Kingdom Reward
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来四16，十二1，15，28～29；林前九24～27；腓三12～14；提后四7～8
壹　基督徒的一生就是一个赛程，我们必须奔跑这赛程，好得着奖赏，就是不能坏的华冠，这是主所要赏给赢得赛程之得胜圣徒的─来十二1，林前九24～25：
I.　The Christian life is a race, and we must run this race to receive the prize, the incorruptible crown, which the Lord will award to His overcoming saints who win the race—Heb. 12:1; 1 Cor. 9:24-25:
一　所有得救的基督徒都必须奔跑这赛程，好赢得奖赏（林前九24）；这奖赏不是指一般的救恩（弗二8，林前三15），乃是指特别的赏赐（来十35，林前三14）。─引用经文
A.　All the saved Christians must run the race to win the prize (1 Cor. 9:24), which is not salvation in the common sense (Eph. 2:8; 1 Cor. 3:15) but a reward in a special sense (Heb. 10:35; 1 Cor. 3:14).
二　使徒保罗奔跑赛程，跑尽了他当跑的赛程，并且赢得了奖赏，就是公义的冠冕，这也是主要赏赐凡爱祂显现之人的─林前九26～27，腓三12～14，徒二十24，提后四7～8。
B.　The apostle Paul ran the race, finished his course, and won the prize, the crown of righteousness, with which the Lord will also reward all those who have loved His appearing—1 Cor. 9:26-27; Phil. 3:12-14; Acts 20:24; 2 Tim. 4:7-8.
三　我们这些主的信徒，都已借着信基督接受了救恩和永远的生命，我们不会永远灭亡，（约三16，36，十28～29）但我们能否得着祂的赏赐，逃避时代的惩罚，乃在于我们怎样奔跑赛程─太二五30：
C.　As His believers, we have all received salvation and eternal life through faith in Christ, and we will not suffer eternal perdition (John 3:16, 36; 10:28-29), but our being rewarded by Him and escaping dispensational punishment depends on how we run the race—Matt. 25:30:
１　我们需要治服我们的身体，使其成为被征服的俘虏，像奴隶一样服事我们，好成就我们神圣的目的─林前九27，西三5，罗八13。
1.　We need to subdue our body and make it a conquered captive to serve us as a slave for fulfilling our holy purpose—1 Cor. 9:27; Col. 3:5; Rom. 8:13.
２　我们必须极其儆醒的奔跑我们的赛程，使我们在基督的审判台前，不致不蒙称许，并被弃绝（林后五10），不配得着要来国度的赏赐─太二四42～46，可十三33～37，路十二37，太七21～23，二五11～12。
2.　We must be very much on the alert to run our course so that we might not be disapproved and rejected at the judgment seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:10) and be found unworthy of the reward of the coming kingdom—Matt. 24:42-46; Mark 13:33-37; Luke 12:37; Matt. 7:21-23; 25:11-12.
３　我们必须以最完满的享受基督、赢得基督为标竿，而向这标竿竭力追求，为要得着奖赏，就是在千年国里对基督极点的享受，作为奔跑新约赛程之得胜者的赏赐─腓三12～14：
3.　We must pursue toward the goal, the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ, for the prize, the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom as a reward to the victorious runners of the New Testament race—Phil. 3:12-14:
a　为要赢得基督到最完满的地步，我们需要忘记背后，甚至不停留在我们已往对基督的经历中。
a.　In order to gain Christ to the fullest extent, we need to forget the past, not even lingering in our past experiences of Christ.
b　不论已往的经历多真实，我们若停留在其中，就会受阻挠，不能进一步追求基督。
b.　Lingering in our past experiences, however genuine they are, frustrates our further pursuing of Christ.
c　我们必须天天努力面前的，更多得着在我们前面之基督那广阔、未经探寻的范围。
c.　We must stretch forward day by day to gain more of the vast unexplored territory of the Christ before us.
４　我们必须作精明的童女，赎回光阴，被那灵充满，也必须作忠信的奴仆，完全使用主的恩赐，使我们能在要来的国度里有分于主的快乐─太二五2～4，22～23。
4.　We must be the wise virgins, redeeming the time to be filled with the Spirit, and the faithful slaves, using the Lord's gift to the fullest extent, so that we can participate in the Lord's joy in the coming kingdom—Matt. 25:2-4, 22-23.
５　留在至圣所，我们的灵里，就是继续不断的奔跑赛程─徒十七16，十九21，二十22，罗一9，林后二13：
5.　To remain in the Holy of Holies, our spirit, is to continue the running of the race—Acts 17:16; 19:21; 20:22; Rom. 1:9; 2 Cor. 2:13:
a　我们越奔跑，至圣所就越扩大，从帐幕扩大到圣殿，最终扩大到新耶路撒冷─出二六8，16，王上六20。
a.　The more we run, the larger the Holy of Holies becomes, expanding from the tabernacle to the temple and ultimately to the New Jerusalem—Exo. 26:8, 16; 1 Kings 6:20.
b　至终，整个新耶路撒冷将是扩大的至圣所─启二一16。
b.　Eventually, the entire New Jerusalem will be the enlarged Holy of Holies—Rev. 21:16.
６　我们今天必须活在国度的实际里（太五～七），好使我们在主回来时，能得着国度的实现为赏赐（太二四～二五）：
6.　We must live in the reality of the kingdom today (Matt. 5—7) so that we can be rewarded with manifestation of the kingdom at the Lord's coming back (Matt. 24—25):
a　诸天的国乃是最高的要求，父神圣的生命也是最高的供应，以达到这要求─太五48，路六35。
a.　The kingdom of the heavens is the highest demand, and the divine life of the Father is the highest supply to meet that demand—Matt. 5:48; Luke 6:35.
b　我们要活在国度的实际里，就必须操练灵里贫穷，清心，并有超过经学家和法利赛人之义的义─太五3，8，20。
b.　To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must exercise to be poor in spirit, to be pure in heart, and to have the righteousness which exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees—Matt. 5:3, 8, 20.
c　我们要活在国度的实际里，就必须赦免别人（太十八21～22，35，弗四32，五2），并寻求得着别人的赦免（太五23～24）。─引用经文
c.　To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must forgive others (Matt. 18:21-22, 35; Eph. 4:32; 5:2) and seek forgiveness from others (Matt. 5:23-24).
d　我们必须活出公义、和平、并圣灵中的喜乐，这些乃是神国的实际─罗十四17。
d.　We need to live out righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit, which are the reality of the kingdom of God—Rom. 14:17.
贰　我们必须取用恩典，跑完我们的赛程，好得着国度的赏赐─来十二28，15，十三25：
II.　We must take grace to finish our course for the kingdom reward—Heb. 12:28, 15; 13:25:
一　恩典乃是神在基督里作为那灵分赐到我们里面，作我们在经历中的享受─约一17，来十29下，腓四23，提后四22，林前五10，加二20。
A.　Grace is God in Christ as the Spirit dispensed into our being for our experiential enjoyment— John 1:17; Heb. 10:29b; Phil. 4:23; 2 Tim. 4:22; 1 Cor. 15:10; Gal. 2:20.
二　我们要领略并有分于希伯书所揭示的一切，就需要恩典：
B.　To realize and participate in all the things unveiled in Hebrews, we need grace:
１　我们要取用恩典，接受恩典，就需要来到施恩的宝座前，使我们得恩典，作应时的帮助─来四16。
1.　To take grace, to have grace, we must come forward to the throne of grace that we may find grace for timely help—Heb. 4:16.
２　我们借着操练我们的灵，摸着至圣所里施恩的宝座，就得以享受恩典的灵（十29），我们的心也就靠恩典得坚固（十三9）：─引用经文
2.　When we touch the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies through the exercise of our spirit, we enjoy the Spirit of grace (Heb. 10:29) and our heart is confirmed by grace (13:9):
a　神在祂经纶中的恩典是丰富的、是繁增的、又是洋溢的─弗二7，彼前一2下，彼后一2，弗一6～8。
a.　The grace of God in His economy is rich, multiplying, and abounding—Eph. 2:7; 1 Pet. 1:2b; 2 Pet. 1:2; Eph. 1:6-8.
b　在以西结书里，鹰翅预表神在基督里的恩典，应用到我们身上作力量和能力，为着我们的行动，并为著作我们的保护，作我们的藏身之处─结一6下，9上，出十九4，赛四十31，林后十二9，林前十五10，诗十七8，五七1，六三7，九一4。
b.　In Ezekiel the eagle's wings typify the grace of God in Christ applied to us as the strength and power for our move and for our protection, our hiding place—Ezek. 1:6b, 9a; Exo. 19:4; Isa. 40:31; 2 Cor. 12:9; 1 Cor. 15:10; Psa. 17:8; 57:1; 63:7; 91:4.
c　我们能借着在不朽坏之中爱主，而接受恩典─弗六24。
c.　We can receive grace by loving the Lord in incorruptibility—Eph. 6:24.
d　我们能借着与主的谦卑是一，而接受恩典─彼前五5～6。
d.　We can receive grace by being one with the Lord in His humility—1 Pet. 5:5-6.
e　我们能享受祂恩典的话─徒二十32，耶十五16。
e.　We can enjoy the word of His grace—Acts 20:32; Jer. 15:16.
f　我们在一的真正立场上，在召会生活中能享受生命的恩典，就是降在锡安山上黑门的甘露所预表的─诗一三三。
f.　We can enjoy the grace of life, typified by the descending dew of Hermon on the mountains of Zion, in the church life on the genuine ground of oneness—Psa. 133.
g　我们经历经过过程之三一神作恩典，使我们能成为神诸般恩典的好管家，就是与我们尊大的大祭司是一的人，在祂天上的职事里，将经过过程的神供应到人里面，作人的享受─彼前四10，来七1，创十四18～20。
g.　Our experience of the processed Triune God as grace enables us to be good stewards of the varied grace of God, those who are one with our great High Priest in His heavenly ministering of the processed God into people for their enjoyment— 1 Pet. 4:10; Heb. 7:1; Gen. 14:18-20.
３　我们如此享受恩典，就能奔那摆在前头的赛程（来十二1），以达到神经纶的目标。─引用经文
3.　By such an enjoyment of grace, we run the race set before us (Heb. 12:1) that we may reach the goal of God's economy.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TWENTY-THREE
TAKING GRACE TO RUN THE RACE FOR THE KINGDOM REWARD
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
Prayer: Lord, we consecrate ourselves to You. Lord Jesus, we love You. We open our entire being to You. We look to You as the source of blessing. Infuse Yourself into us as the unique blessing in the entire New Testament. Lord, we look to You as the One out from whom are all things and through whom are all things. Lord Jesus, we trust in You for the release of this word. We like for You to be the speaking One. Be with us in our listening. Give us an ear to hear what You are speaking. In that day we want to hear You say, "Well done, good and faithful slave? We pray this for one another. We pray that we all would enter into Your joy in the millennium.
THE CHRISTIAN LIFE BEING A RACE THAT WE MUST RUN TO RECEIVE THE PRIZE, THE INCORRUPTIBLE CROWN, WHICH THE LORD WILL AWARD TO HIS OVERCOMING SAINTS WHO WIN THE RACE
The Christian life is a race, and we must run this race to receive the prize, the incorruptible crown, which the Lord will award to His overcoming saints who win the race (Heb. 12:1; 1 Cor. 9:24-25). In Genesis 15 the Lord told Abraham, "Do not be afraid, Abram; I am your shield and your exceedingly great reward" (v. 1). Christ Himself is our reward. We all want to be those who receive a reward. Our prayer should be that we would be in the coming "award ceremony."We are here to bring in the manifestation of the kingdom of the heavens by being prepared by the Lord and by cooperating with this preparation to make ourselves ready to be His overcoming bride. The Lord's recovery is to produce Christ's overcoming bride. For this, all of us have to run the Christian race.
When a woman gets married, she is a bride for one day. After the wedding day, she is a wife. In the same way, we need to be prepared to be Christ's bride for the wedding day of a thousand years (2 Pet. 3:8). All of God's redeemed will become His wife for eternity. But only the overcomers will receive the reward of being His bride on His wedding day of a thousand years to feast with Him and enjoy Him to the uttermost.
The Need of All the Saved Christians to Run the Race to Win the Prize, Which Is Not Salvation in the Common Sense but a Reward in a Special Sense
All the saved Christians must run the race to win the prize (1 Cor. 9:24), which is not salvation in the common sense (Eph. 2:8; 1 Cor. 3:15) but a reward in a special sense (Heb. 10:35; 1 Cor. 3:14). Since we have received the Lord, we have eternal life (John 10:28). No one can take this eternal life away from us. However, whether or not we will receive a reward depends on how we live our lives in this age. The coming reward is the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom of one thousand years. We all certainly want to be there.
Hebrews 12:1 tells us that we need to run the Christian race with endurance. This race is not a one-hundred-meter dash. It is a long distance race, which lasts the entire course of our Christian life. For this reason, we need endurance. Revelation 1:9 says, "I John, your brother and fellow partaker in the tribulation and kingdom and endurance in Jesus." The two words in Jesus are significant. We must run the race with endurance by looking away unto Jesus. We gaze on Him so that He can infuse us with all of His riches. He infuses us with Himself as our enduring element. Second Corinthians 6:4 tells us that the first qualification of a minister of the new covenant is endurance. Therefore, if we are going to be one with the Lord to be His co-workers to carry out His divine enterprise on this earth, we need endurance. Endurance is in Jesus- the wonderful person who is in our spirit (2 Tim, 4:22).
Brother Watchman Nee once said that the most powerful person is the one who endures. We need Christ as our endurance. First Corinthians 9:24 says, "Do you not know that those who run on a racecourse all run, but one receives the prize? Run in this way, that you may lay hold." We need to run "not as though without a clear aim" (v. 26) in order that we might obtain Christ as our prize in the next age. Second Timothy 2:5 says, "If anyone contends in the games, he is not crowned unless he contends lawfully", In this verse Paul compares the believers to athletes. We all are athletes in the real "games." The "games" or contest that we are involved in is a matter of life or death. Paul says in 1 Corinthians 9 that as athletes we must exercise self-control in all things (v. 25). When the Olympic athletes are training for their events, everything about them, their whole living, from morning until evening, is strictly controlled so that they can do their best to obtain the gold medal. They are very disciplined and strict with themselves. Otherwise, they cannot win the gold medal. We need to be the same way in our Christian life. We need to pray, "Lord, Hike to be limited and restricted to my spirit where You live as my self-control." Brother Nee once said concerning Brother Lee that one of the reasons Brother Lee was so blessed by the Lord was because he was so strict with himself. Those of us who had the privilege of serving with him can testify that this was the case with him. We also must be strict with ourselves in this age that we may receive the reward in the next age.
In the parable concerning the sower in Matthew 13, four different kinds of soil are mentioned; each soil signifies a different condition of the heart (w. 3-8, 18-23), One kind of soil is that which is full of rocks (v. 5 and note 1). These rocks signify hidden sins, personal desires, self-seeking, and self-pity. Self-pity is a rock which occupies the heart, limiting the growth of the kingdom-seed. If an athlete contending in the games is knocked down or if he passes through some kind of suffering, there is no self-pity. In athletics, when one is contending, there is no time for self-pity. Similarly, in the Christian life there is no time for us to pity ourselves. We need to reject and deny ourselves.
As an illustration of self-pity, I would like to share an experience I had during the first few years of coming into the church life. Everyone loves the new ones in the church life. When they say something in the meetings, everyone says a hearty amen. However, as they go on in the church life, the strength of the saints' amen to their speaking may grow less and less. Also, when new ones are coming into the church life, the saints often invite them into their homes for meals. When I first came into the church life, I was invited by many of the saints into their homes. So I enjoyed many wonderful meals with the saints. But after my wife and I were married, I began to realize that the saints did not invite me to their homes as often as they had before. At first I was puzzled and I said to myself "What's going on? Don't the saints love me anymore? No one is caring for me anymore. Woe is me!" Then I began pray, "Lord, what is wrong?" I began to pity myself. But as I continued to fellowship with the Lord, He rebuked me, saying, ££Why don't you start caring for others? You are not a baby anymore. Why don't you invite people into your home, instead of waiting for people to invite you into their homes?" Eventually, I said amen to the Lord. Though my wife and I were just newlyweds and we had only a small amount of furniture in our apartment, we began to invite the saints into our home. My wife who is a nurse cooked meals for the saints nearly every night during those first years of our church life. That was a wonderful experience.
We need to get out of self-pity. We should not be in the church life to be served. The Lord said, "The Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve" (Mark 10:45). The Son of Man in us is in the church life to serve others. We are happiest when we are serving others, and this is the way to run the race.
The Apostle Paul Running the Race, Finishing His Course, and Winning the Prize, the Crown of Righteousness, with Which the Lord Will Also Reward All Those Who Have Loved His Appearing
The apostle Paul ran the race, finished his course, and won the prize, the crown of righteousness, with which the Lord will also reward all those who have loved His appearing (1 Cor. 9:26-27; Phil. 3:12-14; 2 Tim. 4:7-8). Paul said, a I have finished the course" (2 Tim. 4:7). We should pray this for one another, that we would finish our course. In the Christian race there is a general course that we have to run corporately for the building up of the Body of Christ. However, as particular members of the Body of Christ- each with its own function—we also have our own personal course to run. When Paul was running the race in Acts 20, he said, "That I may finish my course." (v. 24). In the synagogue in Acts 13, he spoke about John the Baptist and said, "Now as John was completing his course."(v. 25). John had a particular course, and each of us has a particular course. It is a great tragedy if we do not finish our course. Therefore, we have to make every day count for the Lord.
Brother Watchman Nee said that if any of the Lord's chosen ones desires the freedom to walk in his own way, he will experience nothing but failure and pain. If we walk in our own way and do not walk in the course that has been preordained for us to build up the Body of Christ, our experience will be nothing but failure and pain. If, however, we do walk in the course the Lord has chosen for us, we will experience Christ as our success and our eternal joy.
In 2 Timothy 4:8 Paul said, "Henceforth there is laid up for me the crown of righteousness, with which the Lord, the righteous Judge, will recompense me in that day, and not only me but also all those who have loved His appearing.We need to be those people who love His appearing. We should pray, "Lord, make me a person who loves Your appearing. Fill me with Yourself as love so that I may love Your appearing." To love the Lord's appearing means that we love His person to the uttermost. We love Him in His precious indwelling presence, and we are expecting and hastening His coming (2 Pet. 3:12).
If we look at the world situation and if we see what the Lord is doing in His recovery, we have to affirm that the Lord's coming is very near. We should not say as the evil slave in Matthew 24, "My master delays"; neither should we beat our fellow slaves (w. 48-49), which is to mistreat them by criticizing them. We also should not eat and drink with the drunken, which is to keep company with worldly people, drinking what they drink and eating what they eat. Instead, we should be in a place where we feast on the Lord with one another.
Believers Receiving Salvation and Eternal Life through Faith in Christ, Not Suffering Eternal Perdition, but Our Being Rewarded by Christ and Escaping Dispensational Punishment Being Dependent on How We Run the Race
As His believers, we have all received salvation and eternal life through faith in Christ, and we will not suffer eternal perdition (John 3:16, 36; 10:28-29), but our being rewarded by Him and escaping dispensational punishment depends on how we run the race (Matt. 25:30). This is good news. We have eternal life, and no one can take it away from us. We have been born of God; we are His sons (John 1:12-13). We cannot be "unborn." If we are "naughty boys," we may have to be disciplined, but we will forever remain as sons of the Father. Of course, we want to be well pleasing to the Father by exercising our spirit of faith every day.
In Hebrews all the warnings are related to reward and punishment. In note 1 on Hebrews 12:28 in the Recovery Version, Brother Lee says:
To miss the coming kingdom and to forfeit our birthright does not mean that we will perish. It means that we will lose the reward but not our salvation. (See note 351 in ch. 10.) We will suffer loss but still will be saved, yet so as through fire (1 Cor. 3:14-15), This is the basic concept on which all five warnings in this book are given and with which they are all pervaded. All the negative points of these warnings are related to the suffering of the loss of the reward in the coming kingdom, whereas all the positive points are related to the reward and enjoyment of the kingdom. All seven epistles in Rev. 2 and 3 conclude with this same concept- the reward of the kingdom or the loss of it. Only in light of this concept can we rightly understand and properly apply the word in Matt. 5:20; 7:21-23; 16:24-27; 19:23-30; 24:46-51; 25:11-13, 21, 23, 26-30; Luke 12:42-48; 19:17, 19, 22-27; Rom. 14:10, 12; 1 Cor. 3:8, 13-15; 4:5; 9:24-27; 2 Cor. 5:10; 2 Tim. 4:7-8; Heb. 2:3; 4:1, 9, 11; 6:4-8; 10:26-31, 35-39; 12:16-17, 28-29; and Rev. 2:7, 10-11,17, 26-27; 3:4-5, 11-12, 20; 22:12. If we do not have this ： concept, the interpretation of these verses falls into either the extreme objectiveness of the Calvinist school or the extreme subjectiveness of the Arminian school. Neither of these schools recognizes the reward of the kingdom; even more, they do not see the suffering of the loss of the kingdom reward. Hence, both consider all the negative points in these verses as referring to perdition. The Calvinist school, believing in eternal salvation (i.e., that once a person is saved, he will never perish), regards all these negative points as applying to the perdition of false believers; while the Arminian school, believing that a saved person will perish if he falls, regards these points as applying to the perdition of believers who have fallen. But the complete revelation of the Bible shows that these negative points refer to the suffering of the loss of the kingdom reward. God's salvation is eternal; once we obtain it, we will never lose it (John 10:28-29). But we may suffer the loss of the kingdom reward, even though we still will be saved (1 Cor. 3:8, 14-15), The warnings in Hebrews do not refer to the loss of eternal salvation but to the loss of the kingdom reward.
No genuine believer will suffer perdition. However, it is possible for the believers to be rewarded by the Lord in the next age either with unimaginable enjoyment or "outer darkness" (Matt. 8:12; 22:13; 25:30).
