We need to pray, "Lord, by Your mercy and grace, save me from outer darkness where there is weeping and gnashing of teeth." Weeping indicates regret, and gnashing of teeth indicates self blame. Those who are in outer darkness will regret and blame themselves for a thousand years. May the Lord save us from this.
On the one hand, we need to receive the warning, and on the other hand, we must realize that there is a wonderful incentive. First Corinthians 3:15 says, "If anyones work is consumed, he will suffer loss, but he himself will be saved, yet so as through fire." As regenerated ones we will be saved, but if the content of our work is not the Triune God as gold, silver, and precious stones, we will be saved through fire. This is to "be hurt of the second death" (Rev. 2:11); the second death is the lake of fire (20:14). The believers who do not take grace in this age to pursue Christ, enjoy Christ, and be filled with Christ, turning away from the Lord, will be hurt by the lake of fire in the next age. They will not be cast into the lake of fire to suffer perdition, but they will be hurt by the lake of fire.
The Need to Subdue Our Body and Make It a Conquered Captive to Serve Us as a Slave for Fulfilling Our Holy Purpose
We need to subdue our body and make it a conquered captive to serve us as a slave for fulfilling our holy purpose (Col. 3:5; Rom. 8:13), Paul said, KI buffet my body and make it my slave, lest perhaps having preached to others, I myself may become disapproved" (1 Cor. 9:27), The word buffet means to strike; however, it is not asceticism. Rather, it is to subdue our body and make it our conquered captive to serve our most holy purpose. To present our body in the meetings is to subdue our body. Many times our body does not want to be in the meetings. We need to exercise our spirit to subdue our body and get ourselves to the meetings. To do this is to bring our whole being into God's holy purpose so that our mind can be renewed, our spirit can be set on fire, and our body can be "zoe-ified" (enlivened) for God's purpose (Rom. 12:1-2, 11; 8:11).
Being Very Much on the Alert to Run Our Course So That We Might Not Be Disapproved and Rejected at the Judgment Seat of Christ and Be Found Unworthy of the Reward of the Coming Kingdom
We must be very much on the alert to run our course so that we might not be disapproved and rejected at the judgment seat of Christ (2 Cor. 5:10) and be found unworthy of the reward of the coming kingdom (Matt. 24:42-46; Mark 13:33-37; Luke 12:37; Matt. 25:11-12). This is a very sobering matter. In Matthew 7 the Lord said, "Not every one who says to Me, Lord, Lord, will enter into the kingdom of the heavens, but he who does the will of My Father who is in the heavens.5, Then He said, "Many will say to Me in that day, Lord, Lord, was it not in Your name that we prophcsied, and in Your name cast out demons, and in Your name did many works of power?" (vv. 21-22). The Lord's response to them was not that they did not do those things. Fie said, "I never knew you. Depart from Me, you workers of lawlessness," (v. 23). The words I never knew you indicate that He never approved of what they did. Hence, He called them "workers of lawlessness."
Numbers 18:1 uses the phrase the iniquity of the sanctuary. This iniquity, or sin, is related to the work. In other words, as we are serving the Lord, as we are cooperating with Him to fulfill His purpose, it is possible that we can sin in our work for Him. This is the meaning of the Lord's word in Matthew 7 concerning "workers of lawlessness" (v. 23). There is a way to avoid the iniquity of the sanctuary. First, the initiation of God's work has to be according to His will. In other words, everything has to come out from Him as the source. In whatever way we are serving, we must look to the Lord that He would be the source of everything. If He is not the source of our labor, it does not count. Second, the advance of God's work needs to be according to His power. In other words, whatever we do has to be by the Spirit, through the Spirit, and in the Spirit. Third, the result of God's work has to be for God's glory, for God's expression. If we are going to avoid the iniquity of the sanctuary and have these three characteristics with us, we have to be people of prayer.
In Matthew 14 before the Lord Jesus fed. the five thousand, He looked up to heaven (v. 19). When He looked up to heaven, He acknowledged the Father as the source of blessing and that as God's sent One, He was not the source of blessing. In our work for the Lord, we should not initiate anything without prayer. Our work for the Lord should not and must not be prayerless. Our work for the Lord should flow out of prayer. We have to look to the Father to be the source of blessing so that whatever we do and whatever we are would flow out of Him as the source. We must do everything through prayer, by prayer, and in prayer.
Pursuing toward the Goal, the Fullest Enjoyment and Gaining of Christ, for the Prize, the Uttermost Enjoyment of Christ in the Millennial Kingdom as a Reward to the Victorious Runners of the New Testament Race
We must pursue toward the goal, the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ, for the prize, the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom as a reward to the victorious runners of the New Testament race (Phil. 3:12-14). Our goal is the fullest enjoyment and gaining of Christ. This is for the prize, which is the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom as a reward to the victorious runners of the New Testament race.
Forgetting the Past, Not Even Lingering in Our Past Experiences of Christ, in Order to Gain Christ to the Fullest Extent
In order to gain Christ to the fullest extent, we need to forget the past, not even lingering in our past experiences of Christ. Paul said, "But one thing I do: Forgetting the things which are behind and stretching forward to the things which are before, I pursue toward the goal for the prize" (Phil. 3:13-14). We need to be the same. We must exercise our spirit to forget yesterday. We should not even linger in our past experiences of Christ. The Christ we enjoy in the book of Hebrews is the present Christ, the Christ today, and the Christ now. The word today is a big word in the book of Hebrews (3:7, 13, 15; 4:7). In sending the co-workers to Russia from the United States, Brother Lee said to them, "You only have today; you do not have tomorrow.n Christ is our today.
Lingering in Our Past Experiences Frustrating Our Further Pursuing of Christ, and Needing Therefore to Stretch Forward Day by Day to Gain More of the Vast Unexplored Territory of the Christ before Us
Lingering in our experiences, however genuine they are, frustrates our further pursuit of Christ. We must stretch forward day by day to gain more of the vast unexplored territory of the Christ before us.
Becoming the Wise Virgins, Redeeming the Time to Be Filled with the Spirit, and the Faithful Slaves, Using the Lord's Gift to the Fullest Extent, So That We Can Participate in the Lord's Joy in the Coming Kingdom
We must be the wise virgins, redeeming the time to be filled with the Spirit, and the faithful slaves, using the Lord's gift to the fullest extent, so that we can participate in the Lord's joy in the coming kingdom (Matt. 25:2-4, 22-23). We need to redeem our time because we do not know how much time we have. Time passes by so quickly. One day we are thirty-five, and it seems like the next day we are fifty-five. Twenty years seems to pass by in an instant. We do not want to look back with any regret; therefore, we must redeem our time today.
The enemy will send things to take away our time, to damage our time, or to cause us to use our time ineffectively. Like the wise virgins, we are buying the oil (Matt. 25:4, 9). To buy the oil is to pay the price to gain the Spirit. It means that we rise early in the morning and we pay the price to contact the Lord. There are many ways to buy the oil. We need to spend our days paying the price to receive the infilling of the Holy Spirit and investing the spiritual gift or ability that the Lord has given each one of us as members of His Body.
To the church in Philadelphia in Revelation 3 the Lord said, a You have a little power" (v. 8). In one of the notes on this verse Brother Lee says, "What pleases the Lord is not our doing much for Him but our doing our best for Him with what we have." Consider the apostle Paul who spent a number of years in prison. Perhaps, while he was in prison, Paul could have said, "My service is over. How can I function now? In the past I raised up churches, but what can I do now?" Instead of lamenting over his situation, he wrote letters. He might have said, "Though I cannot visit the churches, I can still write letters to them." Paul's Epistles may have been the most useful part of his service. By his Epistles we can see that no matter what our environment is, no matter what our limitation is, we should do our best for the Lord with what we have of Him.
To Remain in the Holy of Holies, Our Spirit, Being to Continue the Running of the Race
To remain in the Holy of Holies, our spirit, is to continue the running of the race (Acts 17:16; 19:21; 20:22; Rom. 1:9; 2 Cor. 2:13). Paul was a person who lived in his spirit. A simple prayer that we could pray throughout the day is: "Lord, keep me in my spirit." If we find that we have drifted away, we can pray in a simple way, "Lord, I turn my heart back to You. Keep me in my spirit. Lord, preserve me in my spirit."
The Holy of Holies Becoming Larger the More We Run, Expanding from the Tabernacle to the Temple and Ultimately to the New Jerusalem
The more we run, the larger the Holy of Holies becomes, expanding from the tabernacle to the temple and ultimately to the New Jerusalem (Exo. 26:8, 16; 1 Kings 6:20). In the tabernacle, the Holy of Holies was ten cubits square, but in the temple, it was twenty cubits square. Ultimately, the New Jerusalem which is also the Holy of Holies is twelve thousand stadia square, or fifteen hundred miles square. This expansion of the Holy of Holies indicates that the more we run the race, the larger the Holy of Holies becomes in our experience.
The Entire New Jerusalem Being the Enlarged Holy of Holies
Our spirit today is the Holy of Holies because that is where God dwells (Eph. 2:22), The more we stay in our spirit, the larger the Holy of Holies becomes in us, until eventually we are consummated as the New Jerusalem, becoming the enlarged Holy of Holies in this universe (Rev. 21:16).
Living in the Reality of the Kingdom Today So That We Can Be Rewarded with the Manifestation of the Kingdom a t the Lord's Coming Back
The Kingdom of the Heavens Being the Highest Demand, and the Divine Life of the Father Being the Highest Supply to Meet That Demand
We must live in the reality of the kingdom today (Matt. 5- 7) so that we can be rewarded with the manifestation of the kingdom at the Lord's coming back (Matt. 24- 25). The kingdom of the heavens is the highest demand, and the divine life of the Father is the highest supply to meet that demand (Luke 6:35). The constitution of the kingdom of the heavens revealed in Matthew 5- 7 is impossible for anyone to do in himself. As an unbeliever, I took a course in college that was called "The Life and Teachings of Jesus." In the class discussion over Matthew 5- 7, I said to the professor, a No one can do this. Who can do this?" There were many believers in the class, and as an unbeliever who was always contending in this way, they would get upset with me. But no one had an answer to my question. Today the answer to my question is in the footnotes on Matthew 5 in the Recovery Version. Verse 48 says, "You therefore shall be perfect as your heavenly Father is perfect." In note 1 on this verse, Brother Lee says:
For the kingdom people to be perfect as their heavenly Father is perfect means that they are perfect in His love. They are the Father's children, having the Fathers divine life and divine nature. Hence, they can be perfect as their Father is. The demand of the new law of the kingdom is much higher than the requirement of the law of the old dispensation. This higher demand can be met only by the Father's divine life, not by the natural life. The kingdom of the heavens is the highest demand, and the divine life of the Father is the highest supply to meet that demand.
How can we be perfect? We have the divine life of the Father as the highest supply to meet that demand. We have His life; we are sons of the Most High God (cf. Heb. 7:1). This means that we also have the "most high life." When this life is lived out of us, its expression is Matthew 5- 7.
Brother Lee told the following story. Brother Watchman Nee and some of the brothers preached the gospel to some farmers in a rural part of mainland China, and a number of them got saved. Two of farmers who had become brothers had a plot of tiered land at the top of a small mountain. They brought water to their fields by carrying it, and when they finished, they left to come back the next day. When they returned the next day, they discovered that the farmer below them had drained all the water off their fields into his own field. They were very upset but they said to one another, "We are Christians. We have to be patient. Let's forget about it." The next day, they brought the water up, and when they returned the following day, the man had drained their field again. When it happened the second time, they went to Brother Nee and asked him what they should do. Brother Nee, pointing at them, said, "You have the divine life in you, and this divine life is the highest supply to meet the demand in Matthew 5- 7 ." Then he showed them Matthew 5:41, which says, 'And whoever compels you to go one mile, go with him two." As a result of his fellowship, they turned to the Lord and opened to Him. The next day, instead of watering their own fields immediately, they watered the other man's field first. Then they went above his field and watered their own fields. When the man came to steal the water from their fields again, he saw the water on his field and realized what had happened. He was so touched that he got saved. Praise the Lord!
Living in the Reality of the Kingdom by Exercising to Be Poor in Spirit, to Be Pure in Heart, and to Have the Righteousness Which Exceeds That of the Scribes and Pharisees
To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must exercise to be poor in spirit, to be pure in heart, and to have the righteousness which exceeds that of the scribes and Pharisees (Matt. 5:3, 8, 20). It is not a small thing to pray, "Lord, I like to be poor in spirit. I want to be unloaded of all my experiences, of all my oldness. Lord, I hate to be stale. I want to be fresh. I want to enjoy Your freshness infused into me today. I do not like to linger in the past. I want to enjoy You in Your newness. I open my entire being to You, Lord. Make me pure in heart. I want to have You as my unique goal. I want to see only You. I do not want to look at two things at the same time. I just like to look at You." By praying such prayers, the righteousness that surpasses that of the scribes and Pharisees becomes Christ as our lived-out righteousness.
Living in the Reality of the Kingdom by Forgiving Others and Seeking Forgiveness from Others
To live in the reality of the kingdom, we must forgive others (Matt. 18:21-22, 35; Eph. 4:32; 5:2) and seek forgiveness from others (Matt. 5:23-24). This seems to be a simple matter, but it is a great matter. Brother Lee pointed out that the two roots of the rebellion that took place among us were unfulfilled ambition and unforgiven offenses. Unforgiven offenses can eventually propel us out of the church life and out of the kingdom. This is very serious. In Matthew 16, the Lord speaks about the universal church (v. 18), but in Matthew 18, He speaks of the local church for the first time in the New Testament (v. 17). In this chapter forgiveness is a very significant matter (w. 21-35). In practicing the church life, there is the need to forgive one another many times. Peter asked the Lord, "How often shall my brother sin against me and I forgive him? Up to seven times?" Perhaps Peter asked this question because a brother had offended him seven times, and he had reached his limit. Many times we also do this. A brother may repent and ask for forgiveness, and we say, "I forgive you, brother." Then the brother offends us again, and we say, "That is the third time." Our counting indicates that we never forgave him for the previous two times. But the Lord said to Peter, "I do not say to you, Up to seven times, but, Up to seventy times seven" (v. 22). This is four hundred ninety times, which indicates unlimited forgiveness. After a number of times, we lose count of the number of offenses.
At the end of Matthew 18, the Lord tells a parable about a man who owed his master ten thousand talents. He was forgiven of his debt. But when another person owed him only one ten thousandth of ten thousand talents, he choked the indebted one and said, "Repay me what you owe" (v. 28). This is what we are like when we do not take the Lord as our forgiving life to forgive one another. The Lord warns us concerning forgiveness in this parable by saying, !(And his master became angry and delivered him to the torturers until he would repay all that was owed. So also will My heavenly Father do to you if each of you does not forgive his brother from your hearts (w. 34-35). In note 1 of verse 34, Brother Lee says, "This is forgiveness in the kingdom. This implies that if we do not forgive a brother from our heart today, we will not be allowed to enter into the kingdom in the coming age." In Matthew 6 the Lord tells us that if we do not forgive our brother, neither will our Father forgive us (v. 15). We not only need to forgive others; we also need to seek forgiveness from one another. This is very serious. We do have a forgiving life in our spirit. It is not in our natural man. Brother Nee had a proverb for a healthy marriage life: "Always say to your spouse, 'I am sorry. Forgive me.'" If we will do this, our marriages will be healthy. We also need to practice this in the church life, saying to one another, "I'm sorry, brother. Forgive me."
Living Out Righteousness, Peace, and Joy in the Holy Spirit, Which Are the Reality of the Kingdom of God
We need to live out righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy Spirit, which are the reality of the kingdom of God (Rom. 14:17). We need to be righteous toward ourselves, we should have peace toward others, and we should have joy toward God. If we are not full of joy, this is an indicator that we are not living in the reality of the kingdom. We need to go to the Lord and say, "Restore to me the gladness of Your salvation" (Psa. 51:12).
TAKING GRACE TO FINISH OUR COURSE FOR THE KINGDOM REWARD
We must take grace to finish our course for the kingdom reward (Heb. 12:15; 13:25). Hebrews 12:28 says, "Let us have grace." This can also be translated, "Let us take grace." We need to take grace. Grace is with our spirit (2 Tim. 4:22). We can take grace by exercising our spirit.
Grace Being God in Christ as the Spirit Dispensed into Our Being for Our Experiential Enjoyment
Grace is God in Christ as the Spirit dispensed into our being for our experiential enjoyment (John 1:17; Heb. 10:29b; Phil. 4:23; 2 Tim. 4:22; 1 Cor. 15:10; Gal. 2:20). Grace is a person——the Triune God dispensed into our being.
Needing Grace to Realize and Participate in All the Things Unveiled in Hebrews
Taking Grace by Coming Forward to the Throne of Grace That We May Find Grace for Timely Help
To realize and participate in all the things unveiled in Hebrews, we need grace. To take grace, to have grace, we must come forward to the throne of grace that we may find grace for timely help (Heb. 4:16).
Enjoying the Spirit of Grace and Having Our Hearts Confirmed by Grace through the Exercise of Our Spirit
When we touch the throne of grace in the Holy of Holies through the exercise of our spirit, we enjoy the Spirit of grace (10:29) and our heart is confirmed by grace (13:9). The way we enjoy grace in the practical Holy of Holies, which is our spirit, is by exercising our spirit.
The Grace of God in His Economy Being Rich, Multiplying, and Abounding
The grace of God in His economy is rich, multiplying, and abounding (Eph. 1:6-8). Grace is everywhere in God's economy. There is a rich, multiplying, and abounding supply of grace. Ephesians 2:7 speaks of the surpassing riches of His grace. His grace has surpassing riches. Peter tells us about multiplied grace (1 Pet. 1:2b; 2 Pet. 1:2). We should pray, "Lord, multiply Your grace in me."
In Ezekiel, the Eagle's Wings Typifying the Grace of God in Christ Applied to Us as the Strength and Power for Our Move and for Our Protection, Our Hiding Place
In Ezekiel the eagle's wings typify the grace of God in Christ applied to us as the strength and power for our move and for our protection, our hiding place (Exo. 19:4; Isa. 40:31; 1 Cor, 15:10; Psa. 17:8; 57:1; 63:7; 91:4). Each of the four living creatures had eagle's wings (Ezek. l ：6b, 9a). Two were for moving, and two were for protection. These wings signify strength and power. Our strength is our enjoyment of the Triune God. The joy of the Lord is our strength (Neh. 8:10), When we are enjoying the Lord, we have the strength to be one with Him for His move. That enjoyment of Christ is our strength and our power. This strength and power are not only for our move but also for our protection, our hiding place. Eventually, Paul said that in his sufferings, grace as power tabernacled over him (2 Cor. 12:9). He lived in a tent of grace. We need to do the same. Our tent, our home, should be grace. In the midst of all of our circumstances and sufferings, we can enjoy the Triune God as grace tabernacling over us.
Receiving Grace by Loving the Lord in Incorruptibility
We can receive grace by loving the Lord in incorruptibility (Eph. 6:24). To love the Lord in incorruptibility is to love Him in the new creation. The old creation is corruptible. The new creation is incorruptible. We love Him in incorruptibility. Actually, to love the Lord in incorruptibility is to love Him according to all the things of the new creation revealed in the book of Ephesians. It is to love Him in the new man. In this regard, Brother Lee made the following statement: "Grace swallows up race." I still am enthralled when I look at the church meetings. There are all colors and all nations. What a wonderful rainbow this is! We all are one new man. We all are brothers. We all love one another. We do not see race; we just see grace. This is to love the Lord in incorruptibility.
Receiving Grace by Being One with the Lord in His Humility
We can receive grace by being one with the Lord in His humility (1 Pet. 5:5-6). We need to take Christ as our humility so that we can be in His humble position to receive Him as our enjoyment. Brother Lee said that we should always remember that pride makes anyone a top fool. We need to beware of pride. Of course, our whole being apart from Christ is pride. We need to take Him as our humility.
Enjoying the Word of His Grace
We can enjoy the word of His grace. Paul told the elders in Ephesus, "And now I commit you to God and to the word of His grace" (Acts 20:32). Every day we can experience the Triune God as our enjoyment by eating His words. Jeremiah 15:16 says, "Your words were found and I ate them, / And Your word became to me / The gladness and joy of my heart.n This is grace.
Enjoying the Grace of Life, Typified by the Descending Dew of Hermon on the Mountains of Zion, in the Church Life on the Genuine Ground of Oneness
We can enjoy the grace of life, typified by the descending dew of Hermon on the mountains of Zion, in the church life on the genuine ground of oneness (Psa. 133). The mountains of Zion typify the local churches. When you come to the meetings of the church or the ministry meetings, the dew descends. The dew signifies the grace of life. Regardless of our shortages, whenever we meet together, we are gradually and delicately being saturated and permeated with the divine and mystical dew; this dew is grace as the very processed and consummated Triune God. The dew of Hermon typifies this. Hermon is the place were Christ was transfigured; hence it is the Mount of Transfiguration (Matt. 16:13 and note 1; 17:1 and note 2). If we want to receive grace, we need to be in the meetings. We need to be in all kinds of meetings, such as the home meetings, the group meetings, the Lord's Day morning meetings, the prayer meetings, and the ministry meetings. In such meetings we get saturated and soaked with Christ as grace, and that grace will transfigure our being.
Our Experience of the Processed Triune God as Grace Enabling Us to Be Good Stewards of the Varied Grace of God, Those Who Are One with Our Great High Priest in His Heavenly Ministering of the Processed God into People for Their Enjoyment
Our experience of the processed Triune God as grace enables us to be good stewards of the varied grace of God, those who are one with our great High Priest in His heavenly ministering of the processed God into people for their enjoyment (1 Pet. 4:10; Heb. 7:1; Gen. 14:18-20). We enjoy God as grace so that we can be good stewards of Him as grace, dispensing Him as grace into others for their enjoyment. This is what it means to participate in Christ's heavenly ministry. To respond to Christ's heavenly ministry is to enter into His interceding life, where we intercede for others, for the church, and for His Body, so that God's economy can be carried out. This is the experience of Christ as the incense altar. We also participate in His heavenly ministry by ministering the processed God into people for their enjoyment. When we enjoy His heavenly ministry, we enjoy the dispensing of all His riches into us. The purpose of that enjoyment is that we would go to others and dispense Him as grace into them for their enjoyment. In other words, we enjoy the Lord for others. We need to be like Boaz who did not care for his own inheritance but cared for Ruth's inheritance. In typology this indicates that we should care for others' enjoyment, not just our own enjoyment. We need to care for one another's enjoyment of the Lord. We need to be good stewards of the grace of God.
Running the Race Set before Us by Enjoying Grace That We Might Reach the Goal of God's Economy
By such an enjoyment of grace, we run the race set before us (Heb. 12:1) that we may reach the goal of God's economy. This goal is the building up of the Body of Christ. The reality of the Body of Christ consummates in the New Jerusalem.
Revelation 22:21, which is the last verse of the Bible, says, "The grace of the Lord Jesus be with all the saints. Amen." This last word of the Bible shows how critical grace is. Eventually, we will enjoy the Triune God as grace forever and ever. This enjoyment will never end, and we will never be bored. We will enjoy the Father as the light of life, the Son as the tree of life, and the Spirit as the river of life forever and ever. We need to enjoy the Triune God in such a way in this age, so that we can finish our course and can be rewarded with the uttermost enjoyment of Christ in the millennial kingdom as His bride. Eventually, that enjoyment will usher us into the eternal age, where all of God's redeemed become His wife to enjoy Him forever and ever for His glorious expression in the universe.—E. M.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
　
　
第二十四篇　进入幔内与出到营外
晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
读经：来六19～20，十三13；出三三7～11
壹　希伯来书的目标和最终的结论，乃是我们要进入幔内，并出到营外─来六19～20，十三13：
一　进入幔内，意即进入主已在其中，在荣耀里登宝座的至圣所；出到营外，意即脱离主曾从其中被人弃绝、驱逐的宗教：
１　营表征属地和属人的宗教组织。
２　一切宗教都是属人的组织，也是属地的范围，使人远离神的经纶。
二　我们必须在我们的灵里，从经历说，今天实行的至圣所就是在我们的灵里；我们也必须出到宗教之外，今天实行的营就是在宗教里：
１　我们越在灵里享受天上的基督，就越出到宗教的营外，跟随受苦的耶稣。
２　我们越在灵里接触这位在荣耀里天上的基督，就越出到宗教的营外，就了卑微的耶稣去，与他一同受苦。
３　真正新约的职事，带我们进入灵里，就是进入幔内，对基督有享受，并加强我们跟随耶稣出到营外，为着祂身体的缘故，交通于祂的受苦─林后十一2～3，23～33：
a　在幔内，我们有分于天上基督的职事，使我们得着装备，能将祂供应给营外干渴的灵。
b　借着进入幔内并出到营外，我们就在各样的善事上得着装备，好实行神的旨意；祂是在我们里面，行祂看为可喜悦的事─来十三20～21。
三　进入幔内就是进到我们的灵里；当我们转向我们的灵并操练我们的灵，我们就进入幔内─提前四7～8：
１　我们必须将我们的灵如火挑旺起来，将我们的心思置于灵，并辨明我们的灵与魂，借此操练、运用、并使用我们的灵─提后一6～7，罗八5～6，来四12。
２　我们必须操练我们的灵，好进入幔内，直接接触天上的基督这位在荣耀里的人，而观看祂，被祂灌输并注入，使我们成为祂团体的复制品─林后三18。
３　在幔内就是在至圣所里，在这个范围里，我们有分于基督，并享受祂作隐藏的吗哪、发芽的杖、和生命之律，结果带进神的团体彰显，以完成神永远的定旨─来九3～4。
贰　以色列人拜了金牛犊后，摩西就迁到营外，所有寻求主的人都到那里与他同聚，因为主的同在和说话都在那里─出三三7～11：
一　我们必须看见金牛犊偶像的原则，并在此受警戒；神所救赎之人所作的偶像，使他们成了拜偶像的营─林前十5～7：
１　自我装饰导致拜偶像─出三二1～3，三三5～6，创三五2～4，参出二八2，赛六十21。
２　拜偶像乃是撒但篡夺了神所赐给我们的，要造成浪费；这是我们滥用神所赐给我们的，而不为着神的目的使用神在物质和属灵上的赐与。
３　拜偶像就是敬拜我们所享受的东西，就是崇拜享受、消遣和娱乐─出三二6，18～19，参诗三六8～9。
４　拜偶像都是假装敬拜真神─出三二4～6，王上十二26～30。
５　拜偶像是在敬拜上的搀杂─出三二4～6，21～24。
二　摩西因为晓得主的同在不再在百姓中间，就把他的帐棚迁移，支搭在远离营的地方；他的帐棚于是成了神的帐棚─出三三7：
１　营表征属宗教的人，他们按名是属主的，事实上却是拜偶像的，敬拜并寻求主自己以外的东西。
２　在神子民的历史中，至少可以看见三个时期的营：
a　这营最初是拜金牛犊以后的以色列人。
b　主生活在地上时，犹太宗教成了这营─太十五7～9。
c　后来，召会改变了性质，从帐棚变为营，就是宗教的系统，也就是宗教的巴比伦，由一班属宗教的人所组成，他们只按名是属主的，并以嘴唇尊敬主，心却定在主以外的东西上─创十一4，7，9，代下三六6～7，拉一11，启十七3～5，十八2上，4。
３　摩西迁移他的帐棚，远离拜偶像的营之后，主面对面与他说话，好象人与同伴说话一般─出三三11：
a　神与摩西是同伴、伙伴、同伙，同有一个事业，并在一个大企业中有共同的权益。
b　国为摩西与神是亲密的，他是一个懂得神心的人，是合乎神心、并能摸着神心的人。
c　我们必须进入幔内并出到拜偶像的营外，与主有最亲、最密的交通，使我们成为与神有共同权益的人，能为神所用，以完成祂在地上的事业。

TOP-晨兴-大纲|Outline|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
GENERAL SUBJECT
The Crystallization-Study of Epistle to the Hebrews
Message Thirty-Four
Enter within the Veil and Go outside the Camp
晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
Scripture Reading: Heb. 6:19-20; 13:13; Exo. 33:7-11
I.　The goal and ultimate conclusion of the book of Hebrews is that we would enter within the veil and go outside the camp—Heb. 6:19-20; 13:13:
A.　To enter within the veil means to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord is enthroned in glory, and to go outside the camp means to come out of religion, whence the Lord was cast in rejection:
1.　The camp signifies the organization of religion, which is earthly and human.
2.　Every religion is a human organization and an earthly realm which keeps people away from God's economy.
B.　We must be in our spirit, where, experientially speaking, the practical Holy of Holies is today, and outside religion, where the practical camp is today:
1.　The more we are in our spirit, enjoying the heavenly Christ, the more we will come outside the camp of religion, following the suffering Jesus.
2.　The more we remain in our spirit to contact the heavenly Christ, who is in glory, the more we will go forth outside the camp of religion unto the lowly Jesus to suffer with Him.
3.　The genuine New Testament ministry brings us into the enjoyment of Christ in our spirit, within the veil, and strengthens us to follow Jesus outside the camp in the fellowship of His sufferings for the sake of His Body— 2 Cor. 11:2-3, 23-33:
a.　Within the veil, we participate in the ministry of the heavenly Christ that we may be equipped to minister Him to the thirsty spirits outside the camp.
b.　By entering within the veil and going outside the camp, we are equipped in every good work for the doing of the will of God, who does in us that which is well pleasing in His sight—Heb. 13:20-21.
C.　To enter within the veil is to get into our spirit; when we turn to our spirit and exercise it, we enter within the veil—1 Tim. 4:7-8:
1.　We have to exercise, to use, to employ, our spirit by fanning our spirit into flame, setting our mind on the spirit, and discerning our spirit from our soul—2 Tim. 1:6-7; Rom. 8:5-6; Heb. 4:12.
2.　We must exercise our spirit that we may enter within the veil to have direct contact with the heavenly Christ, the man in the glory, beholding Him to be transfused and infused with Him so that we may become His corporate reproduction—2 Cor. 3:18.
3.　To be within the veil is to be in the Holy of Holies, in a realm where we partake of Christ and enjoy Him as the hidden manna, the budding rod, and the law of life, issuing in God's corporate expression for the fulfillment of His eternal purpose—Heb. 9:3-4.
II.　After the children of Israel worshipped the golden calf, Moses moved to a place outside the camp, where everyone who sought the Lord went to meet with him, for both the Lord's presence and speaking were there— Exo. 33:7-11:
A.　We need to see and be warned by the principle of the golden-calf idol, an idol made by God's redeemed people to make them an idolatrous camp—1 Cor. 10:5-7:
1.　Self-beautification leads to idolatry—Exo. 32:1-3; 33:5-6; Gen. 35:2-4; cf. Exo. 28:2; Isa. 60:21.
2.　Idolatry is Satan's usurping of what God has given us in order to make it a waste; it is our abusing what God has given us and not using God's gifts, both material and spiritual, for God's purpose.
3.　Idolatry is the worship of the things we enjoy, the worship of enjoyment, amusement, and entertainment—Exo. 32:6, 18-19; cf. Psa. 36:8-9.
4.　With idolatry there is the pretense of worshipping the true God—Exo. 32:4-6; 1 Kings 12:26-30.
5.　With idolatry there is mixture in worship— Exo. 32:4-6, 21-24.
B.　Because Moses realized that the Lord's presence would no longer be in the midst of the people, he removed his tent and pitched it afar off from the camp; his tent then became the tent of God—Exo. 33:7:
1.　The camp signifies a religious people, who belong to the Lord in name but who, in actuality, worship idols, worshipping something and seeking something other than the Lord Himself:
2.　In the history of God's people, the camp may be seen in at least three periods:
a.　The camp was first the children of Israel after they worshipped the golden calf.
b.　The Jewish religion became the camp at the time of the Lord's living on the earth—Matt. 15:7-9.
c.　Later, the church changed in nature from being a tent to being a camp, a religious system, religious Babylon, comprising a group of religious people belonging to the Lord in name and honoring the Lord with their mouth, but having their hearts set on something other than the Lord— Gen. 11:4, 7, 9; 2 Chron. 36:6-7; Ezra 1:11; Rev. 17:3-5; 18:2a, 4.
3.　After Moses removed his tent and separated it from the idolatrous camp, the Lord spoke to him face to face, as a man speaks to his companion—Exo. 33:11:
a.　God and Moses were companions, associates, partners, involved in the same career and having a common interest in a great enterprise.
b.　Because Moses was intimate with God, he was a person who knew God's heart, who was according to God's heart, and who could touch God's heart.
c.　We need to enter within the veil and go outside the idolatrous camp to have the closest and most intimate relationship with the Lord so that we can be persons who share a common interest with God and who can be used by Him to carry out His enterprise on earth.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|对照-听抄-目录
　
　
第二十四篇　进入幔内与出到营外
Enter within the Veil and Go outside the Camp
晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
读经：来六19～20，十三13；出三三7～11
壹　希伯来书的目标和最终的结论，乃是我们要进入幔内，并出到营外─来六19～20，十三13：
I.　The goal and ultimate conclusion of the book of Hebrews is that we would enter within the veil and go outside the camp—Heb. 6:19-20; 13:13:
一　进入幔内，意即进入主已在其中，在荣耀里登宝座的至圣所；出到营外，意即脱离主曾从其中被人弃绝、驱逐的宗教：
A.　To enter within the veil means to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord is enthroned in glory, and to go outside the camp means to come out of religion, whence the Lord was cast in rejection:
１　营表征属地和属人的宗教组织。
1.　The camp signifies the organization of religion, which is earthly and human.
２　一切宗教都是属人的组织，也是属地的范围，使人远离神的经纶。
2.　Every religion is a human organization and an earthly realm which keeps people away from God's economy.
二　我们必须在我们的灵里，从经历说，今天实行的至圣所就是在我们的灵里；我们也必须出到宗教之外，今天实行的营就是在宗教里：
B.　We must be in our spirit, where, experientially speaking, the practical Holy of Holies is today, and outside religion, where the practical camp is today:
１　我们越在灵里享受天上的基督，就越出到宗教的营外，跟随受苦的耶稣。
1.　The more we are in our spirit, enjoying the heavenly Christ, the more we will come outside the camp of religion, following the suffering Jesus.
２　我们越在灵里接触这位在荣耀里天上的基督，就越出到宗教的营外，就了卑微的耶稣去，与他一同受苦。
2.　The more we remain in our spirit to contact the heavenly Christ, who is in glory, the more we will go forth outside the camp of religion unto the lowly Jesus to suffer with Him.
３　真正新约的职事，带我们进入灵里，就是进入幔内，对基督有享受，并加强我们跟随耶稣出到营外，为着祂身体的缘故，交通于祂的受苦─林后十一2～3，23～33：
3.　The genuine New Testament ministry brings us into the enjoyment of Christ in our spirit, within the veil, and strengthens us to follow Jesus outside the camp in the fellowship of His sufferings for the sake of His Body— 2 Cor. 11:2-3, 23-33:
a　在幔内，我们有分于天上基督的职事，使我们得着装备，能将祂供应给营外干渴的灵。
a.　Within the veil, we participate in the ministry of the heavenly Christ that we may be equipped to minister Him to the thirsty spirits outside the camp.
b　借着进入幔内并出到营外，我们就在各样的善事上得着装备，好实行神的旨意；祂是在我们里面，行祂看为可喜悦的事─来十三20～21。
b.　By entering within the veil and going outside the camp, we are equipped in every good work for the doing of the will of God, who does in us that which is well pleasing in His sight—Heb. 13:20-21.
三　进入幔内就是进到我们的灵里；当我们转向我们的灵并操练我们的灵，我们就进入幔内─提前四7～8：
C.　To enter within the veil is to get into our spirit; when we turn to our spirit and exercise it, we enter within the veil—1 Tim. 4:7-8:
１　我们必须将我们的灵如火挑旺起来，将我们的心思置于灵，并辨明我们的灵与魂，借此操练、运用、并使用我们的灵─提后一6～7，罗八5～6，来四12。
1.　We have to exercise, to use, to employ, our spirit by fanning our spirit into flame, setting our mind on the spirit, and discerning our spirit from our soul—2 Tim. 1:6-7; Rom. 8:5-6; Heb. 4:12.
２　我们必须操练我们的灵，好进入幔内，直接接触天上的基督这位在荣耀里的人，而观看祂，被祂灌输并注入，使我们成为祂团体的复制品─林后三18。
2.　We must exercise our spirit that we may enter within the veil to have direct contact with the heavenly Christ, the man in the glory, beholding Him to be transfused and infused with Him so that we may become His corporate reproduction—2 Cor. 3:18.
３　在幔内就是在至圣所里，在这个范围里，我们有分于基督，并享受祂作隐藏的吗哪、发芽的杖、和生命之律，结果带进神的团体彰显，以完成神永远的定旨─来九3～4。
3.　To be within the veil is to be in the Holy of Holies, in a realm where we partake of Christ and enjoy Him as the hidden manna, the budding rod, and the law of life, issuing in God's corporate expression for the fulfillment of His eternal purpose—Heb. 9:3-4.
贰　以色列人拜了金牛犊后，摩西就迁到营外，所有寻求主的人都到那里与他同聚，因为主的同在和说话都在那里─出三三7～11：
II.　After the children of Israel worshipped the golden calf, Moses moved to a place outside the camp, where everyone who sought the Lord went to meet with him, for both the Lord's presence and speaking were there— Exo. 33:7-11:
一　我们必须看见金牛犊偶像的原则，并在此受警戒；神所救赎之人所作的偶像，使他们成了拜偶像的营─林前十5～7：
A.　We need to see and be warned by the principle of the golden-calf idol, an idol made by God's redeemed people to make them an idolatrous camp—1 Cor. 10:5-7:
１　自我装饰导致拜偶像─出三二1～3，三三5～6，创三五2～4，参出二八2，赛六十21。
1.　Self-beautification leads to idolatry—Exo. 32:1-3; 33:5-6; Gen. 35:2-4; cf. Exo. 28:2; Isa. 60:21.
２　拜偶像乃是撒但篡夺了神所赐给我们的，要造成浪费；这是我们滥用神所赐给我们的，而不为着神的目的使用神在物质和属灵上的赐与。
2.　Idolatry is Satan's usurping of what God has given us in order to make it a waste; it is our abusing what God has given us and not using God's gifts, both material and spiritual, for God's purpose.
３　拜偶像就是敬拜我们所享受的东西，就是崇拜享受、消遣和娱乐─出三二6，18～19，参诗三六8～9。
3.　Idolatry is the worship of the things we enjoy, the worship of enjoyment, amusement, and entertainment—Exo. 32:6, 18-19; cf. Psa. 36:8-9.
４　拜偶像都是假装敬拜真神─出三二4～6，王上十二26～30。
4.　With idolatry there is the pretense of worshipping the true God—Exo. 32:4-6; 1 Kings 12:26-30.
５　拜偶像是在敬拜上的搀杂─出三二4～6，21～24。
5.　With idolatry there is mixture in worship— Exo. 32:4-6, 21-24.
二　摩西因为晓得主的同在不再在百姓中间，就把他的帐棚迁移，支搭在远离营的地方；他的帐棚于是成了神的帐棚─出三三7：
B.　Because Moses realized that the Lord's presence would no longer be in the midst of the people, he removed his tent and pitched it afar off from the camp; his tent then became the tent of God—Exo. 33:7:
１　营表征属宗教的人，他们按名是属主的，事实上却是拜偶像的，敬拜并寻求主自己以外的东西。
1.　The camp signifies a religious people, who belong to the Lord in name but who, in actuality, worship idols, worshipping something and seeking something other than the Lord Himself:
２　在神子民的历史中，至少可以看见三个时期的营：
2.　In the history of God's people, the camp may be seen in at least three periods:
a　这营最初是拜金牛犊以后的以色列人。
a.　The camp was first the children of Israel after they worshipped the golden calf.
b　主生活在地上时，犹太宗教成了这营─太十五7～9。
b.　The Jewish religion became the camp at the time of the Lord's living on the earth—Matt. 15:7-9.
c　后来，召会改变了性质，从帐棚变为营，就是宗教的系统，也就是宗教的巴比伦，由一班属宗教的人所组成，他们只按名是属主的，并以嘴唇尊敬主，心却定在主以外的东西上─创十一4，7，9，代下三六6～7，拉一11，启十七3～5，十八2上，4。
c.　Later, the church changed in nature from being a tent to being a camp, a religious system, religious Babylon, comprising a group of religious people belonging to the Lord in name and honoring the Lord with their mouth, but having their hearts set on something other than the Lord— Gen. 11:4, 7, 9; 2 Chron. 36:6-7; Ezra 1:11; Rev. 17:3-5; 18:2a, 4.
３　摩西迁移他的帐棚，远离拜偶像的营之后，主面对面与他说话，好象人与同伴说话一般─出三三11：
3.　After Moses removed his tent and separated it from the idolatrous camp, the Lord spoke to him face to face, as a man speaks to his companion—Exo. 33:11:
a　神与摩西是同伴、伙伴、同伙，同有一个事业，并在一个大企业中有共同的权益。
a.　God and Moses were companions, associates, partners, involved in the same career and having a common interest in a great enterprise.
b　国为摩西与神是亲密的，他是一个懂得神心的人，是合乎神心、并能摸着神心的人。
b.　Because Moses was intimate with God, he was a person who knew God's heart, who was according to God's heart, and who could touch God's heart.
c　我们必须进入幔内并出到拜偶像的营外，与主有最亲、最密的交通，使我们成为与神有共同权益的人，能为神所用，以完成祂在地上的事业。
c.　We need to enter within the veil and go outside the idolatrous camp to have the closest and most intimate relationship with the Lord so that we can be persons who share a common interest with God and who can be used by Him to carry out His enterprise on earth.

TOP-晨兴-纲目|outline-听抄-目录
　
　
MESSAGE TWENTY-FOUR
ENTER WITHIN THE VEIL AND GO OUTSIDE THE CAMP THE GOAL AND ULTIMATE CONCLUSION OF THE BOOK OF HEBREWS BEING THAT WE WOULD ENTER WITHIN THE VEIL AND GO OUTSIDE THE CAMP
晨兴-纲目|Outline|对照-目录
文档OCR所得，仅供追求参考
The goal and ultimate conclusion of the book of Hebrews is that we would enter within the veil and go outside the camp (13:13), Hebrews 6:18-20 tells us that we must flee for refuge to lay hold of the hope set before us, and that this hope is within the veil. "Within the veil", refers to the Holy of Holies, and our spirit today is the practical Holy of Holies. Therefore, we need to flee for refuge into our spirit. The literal meaning of the word flee is to flee intensively. A compound form of the Greek word translated as flee here is used in the book of Acts when Paul and his co-workers fled persecution in an intensive way (14:6).
We need to flee in an intensive way from our mind and into our spirit. We must flee within the veil. Many things confront us in this earthly and human realm. Brother Lee said that the best way to fight the battle is to retreat. We should retreat into our spirit. That is where the victory is. In our spirit, we are within the veil beholding the heavenly Christ. Our fleeing is actually our pursuing. Second Timothy 2:22 says, "But flee youthful lusts, and pursue righteousness, faith, love, peace with those who call on the Lord out of a pure heart."
Entering within the Veil to Enter into the Holy of Holies and Going outside the Camp to Come out of Religion
To enter within the veil means to enter into the Holy of Holies, where the Lord is enthroned in glory, and to go outside the camp means to come out of religion, whence the Lord was cast in rejection. The Lord's enthronement in the heavenly Holy of Holies is His enthronement in glory. In Revelation 4 John said, ''Immediately I was in spirit; and behold, there was a throne set in heaven" (v. 2). When we get into our spirit, the first thing we touch is the throne. Christ is the preeminent One. He is the One sitting on the throne. Hence, when we get into our spirit, we are dethroned. When we are in our spirit, He is the only One who is and we are not.
As God is organically saving us, we are going up step-by-step and level-by-level. We are going from regeneration to sanctification, from sanctification to renewing, from renewing to transformation, from transformation to conformation, and from conformation to glorification, until eventually we will become the consummated Holy of Holies. As the consummated Holy of Holies, there will be no more flesh. When we become the consummated Holy of Holies, we will be in spirit forever.
To know the address of the Triune God is a real mercy. In order to find a person in another city, we must have a specific address. Without a specific address, even if we know that a person lives in a certain city, it will be difficult to find them. The specific address of the Triune God is our human spirit; hence, we can contact Him there. This good news needs to be spread to the whole earth. We need to install the Triune God into the spirit of others; then we must share with them that He lives in their spirit. By repenting, which is to turn away from all things other than God Himself we can contact Him at any time. Then we need to go outside the camp, that is, to come out of religion. In the four Gospels the ones who hated the Lord the most were the religious ones, who even wanted to kill Him. Consequently, He was rejected and cast out by religion. This rejection by religion has also been carried out toward His believers throughout church history. Religion, therefore, is versus Christ.
The Camp Signifying the Organization of Religion, and Every Religion Being a Human Organization and an Earthly Realm Keeping People Away from God's Economy
The camp signifies the organization of religion, which is earthly and human. Every religion is a human organization and an earthly realm, which keeps people away from God's economy. Therefore, we need to come out of human organization into the organism of the Triune God. We need to come out of the earthly realm into the divine and mystical realm of the pneumatic Christ and the consummated Spirit.
Being in Our Spirit, the Practical Holy of Holies Today, and Being outside of Religion, the Practical Camp Today
We must be in our spirit, where, experientially speaking, the practical Holy of Holies is today, and outside religion, where the practical camp is today.
Enjoying the Heavenly Christ in Our Spirit to Come outside the Camp of Religion, Following the Suffering Jesus
The more we are in our spirit, enjoying the heavenly Christ, the more we will come outside the camp of religion, following the suffering Jesus. According to our human understanding, we may think that we have to come outside the camp of religion first, and then we get into our spirit. However, according to God's revelation, the more we are in our spirit enjoying Christ, the more we come outside the camp of religion to follow the suffering Jesus. We should pray, "Lord, I want to spend more and more time in my spirit. Lord, I am not content to spend most of my time outside of my spirit." Our day should start with a time with the Lord. The morning time with the Lord is so crucial. We should put this time ahead of every other duty. No matter how busy we are, we must have a time with the Lord in the morning. We never graduate from such a time. Often the enemy attacks this time by bringing in many distractions. When we wake up in the morning, the first thing we should say is, "O Lord" not, "Oh, no!" The psalmist said, "I anticipated the dawn and cried out; / I hoped in Your words" (Psa. 119:147). In a recent conference, one of the brothers testified of his experience of waking up very early in the morning anticipating a time with the Lord. We also should anticipate the dawn.
We have to build up a habit of spending a time with the Lord each morning. In terms of personal hygiene, we would not consider leaving our house without brushing our teeth. In the same way, we should not leave our house without spending time with the Lord. In our time with the Lord, we can pray a simple prayer: "Father, strengthen me with power through Your Spirit into the inner man, that Christ might make His home in my heart that I might be filled unto all the fullness of God." If we pray in this way each day for thirty days, we will be different persons. When we pray in the morning, we are ushered into our spirit, into our inner man. This kind of prayer will energize our whole being and activate the "dynamite" of God in our being to bring us into our spirit at the beginning of our day. Then throughout the day, we can pray over some verses of the Bible. In such a way, we enjoy the Lord. If we fail, we simply confess and say, "Lord, forgive me. O Lord, keep me in my spirit."
We should call on the name of the Lord all the time. Our calling on the Lord should become deeper, richer, and higher to us. Many can testify that calling on the Lord is sweeter than it has ever been. During the day, we can call "O Lord," and we can also talk to the Lord about everything, saying, "Lord Jesus, what about this situation? Lord Jesus, what about that situation?" First Corinthians 12:3 says, "No one can say, Jesus is Lord! except in the Holy Spirit.w If we want to be in the Holy Spirit, we should call "Lord Jesus." To say that such calling is too simple indicates that we are shortsighted. Our calling upon the name of the Lord should increase and even become sweeter, richer, and deeper to us.
Remaining in Our Spirit to Contact the Heavenly Christ in Glory and Going Forth outside the Camp of Religion unto the Lowly Jesus to Suffer with Him
The more we remain in our spirit to contact the heavenly Christ, who is in glory, the more we will go forth outside the camp of religion unto the lowly Jesus to suffer with Him. This suffering is for the sake of Christ's Body. We participate in the fellowship of His sufferings for the building up of His Body. Throughout the history of the church, everyone who pursued Christ absolutely in an unadulterated way suffered persecution. We should not think that such persecution is strange (1 Pet. 4:12-13). Actually, this is a wonderful sign. If everyone always congratulates us and tells us how wonderful we are, we should be concerned. The New Testament is not a book about how to win friends and influence people. It is a book that tells us how to enjoy the Lord within the veil and how to follow Him as the suffering Jesus outside the camp.
The Genuine New Testament Ministry Bringing Us into the Enjoyment of Christ in Our Spirit within the Veil and Strengthening Us to Follow Jesus outside the Camp in the Fellowship of His Sufferings for the Sake of His Body
The genuine New Testament ministry brings us into the enjoyment of Christ in our spirit, within the veil, and strengthens us to follow Jesus outside the camp in the fellowship of His sufferings for the sake of His Body (2 Cor. 11:2-3, 23-33).
Participating in the Ministry of the Heavenly Christ within the Veil and Being Equipped to Minister Him to the Thirsty Spirits outside the Camp
Within the veil, we participate in the ministry of the heavenly Christ that we may be equipped to minister Him to the thirsty spirits outside the camp. When we are in our spirit, we behold Him, we are transfused with Him, and we enjoy His loveliness, preciousness, and surpassing worth. We are also enthralled with Him. When we are in our spirit, we are equipped to minister Him to the thousands of thirsty spirits on this earth. By spending more time in our spirit, we are propelled to go outside the camp to minister the Christ we have enjoyed to those around us.
Being Equipped in Every Good Work for the Doing of the Will of God by Entering within the Veil and Going outside the Camp
By entering within the veil and going outside the camp, we are equipped in every good work for the doing of the will of God, who does in us that which is well pleasing in His sight (Heb. 13:20-21). Whenever we enter within the veil, we enjoy Christ as the great Shepherd of the sheep. Entering within the veil propels us outside the camp to be one with Christ in His heavenly ministry of shepherding people. Thus, we are equipped in every good work.
Entering within the Veil Being to Turn to Our Spirit
To enter within the veil is to turn to our spirit; when we turn to our spirit and exercise it, we enter within the veil (1 Tim. 4:7-8). We need to be spirit-exercising people.
Exercising Our Spirit by Fanning Our Spirit into Flame, Setting Our Mind on the Spirit, and Discerning Our Spirit from Our Soul
We have to exercise, to use, to employ, our spirit by fanning our spirit into flame, setting our mind on the spirit, and discerning our spirit from our soul (2 Tim. 1:6; Rom. 8:5-6; Heb. 4:12). When Paul wrote 2 Timothy he was about to be martyred. Outwardly, it was a very discouraging situation because Paul was in prison and all those in Asia had turned away from him (1:15). Paul's spiritual son, Timothy, a young brother and co-worker, undoubtedly was very discouraged. Therefore, Paul wrote to Timothy, saying, "You are discouraged because you have forgotten something. There is a flame in you. I remind you to fan your spirit into flame." There is also a flame in each one of us. In gas stoves, there is a pilot light, which is a small flame for igniting the larger burners on top of the stove. This small flame remains lit all the time. In the Christian life, sometimes we are up and sometimes we are down. Sometimes we are in, and other times we are out. But no matter where we are, there is a flame within us. In 2 Timothy, Paul reminds us to "turn up the gas," that is, to fan into flame the gift of God, which is in us. Paul says, "For God has not given us a spirit of cowardice, but of power and of love and of sobermindedness" (v. 7). The gift of God is our God-given spirit. To fan into flame the gift of God is to fan our spirit into flame. Our God-given spirit is our capital. Brother Lee said that we must force ourselves to use our spirit. To exercise our spirit is a forced matter. Brother Lee also said that all the national problems, social problems, and family problems are due to the fact that people do not exercise their spirit. We should exercise our spirit and not wait for inspiration. In stressing this matter of exercise, Paul used the example of athletics (1 Tim. 4:7-8). Olympic athletes force themselves to exercise. They may not feel like getting up at five o'clock in the morning and running eight miles every day, but they force themselves to exercise. We have to force ourselves to exercise our spirit. Christ is in our spirit. The New Jerusalem is in our spirit. Everything we need is in our spirit.
Exercising Our Spirit That We May Enter within the Veil to Have Direct Contact with the Heavenly Christ, the Man in the Glory, and Beholding Him to Be Transfused and Infused with Him So That We May Become His Corporate Reproduction
We must exercise our spirit that we may enter within the veil to have direct contact with the heavenly Christ, the man in the glory, beholding Him to be transfused and infused with Him so that we may become His corporate reproduction (2 Cor. 3:18). The first line of each stanza of Hymns, #505 begins with the phrase, "There's a Man in the glory? Sometimes in singing this hymn, we like to change the first line to say, "There's a Man in my spirit." This is really true. The man in the glory is the man in our spirit. We have a wonderful person in us. We behold Him to be transfused and infused with Him so that we may- become His corporate reproduction. We need this kind of direct contact with Him every day. I implore all of us to have a personal time with the Lord every day. We all— old or young— need to build up a habit of having a personal time with the Lord each day.
The Lord Jesus practiced to have time privately with the Father. After He fed the five thousand, He did not remain in the issue of that miracle. Instead, He compelled His disciples to get into a boat and to go before Him to the other side of the lake (Matt. 14:22). He did that so that He could have more time to pray privately to the Father (v. 23). He wanted to have the Father with Him in whatever He did to carry out His ministry to build up the Body of Christ. We need to be one with the Lord in this way.
Being within the Veil, in the Holy of Holies, in a Realm Where We Partake of Christ and Enjoy Him as the Hidden Manna, the Budding Rod, and the Law of Life, Issuing in God's Corporate Expression for the Fulfillment of His Eternal Purpose
To be within the veil is to be in the Holy of Holies, in a realm where we partake of Christ and enjoy Him as the hidden manna, the budding rod, and the law of life, issuing in God's corporate expression for the fulfillment of His eternal purpose (Heb. 9:3-4). When we are in our spirit, we are in the divine and mystical realm. We should pray, "Lord, today, I want to enjoy You as the hidden manna in the most hidden, intimate, and sweet way. I want to eat You. I want to digest You. I want to assimilate You so that I can become You and be built up in the Body of Christ for Your corporate expression. Lord, I want You to be my budding rod. I want You to 'bud' out of my being. I want You to be my God-given authority, not to beat people but to feed people. I want to enjoy You as the law of life. I want to turn on that switch and keep it on all day today. 'Sonize' my whole being for Your corporate expression."
MOSES MOVING TO A PLACE OUTSIDE THE CAMP WHERE EVERYONE WHO SOUGHT THE LORD WENT TO MEET WITH HIM FOR BOTH THE LORD'S PRESENCE AND SPEAKING
After the children of Israel worshipped the golden calf, Moses moved to a place outside the camp, where everyone who sought the Lord went to meet with him, for both the Lord's presence and speaking were there (Exo. 33:7-11). Hebrews 13:13 says, "Let us therefore go forth unto Him outside the camp, bearing His reproach." Paul's whole thought here refers to the account of the worship of the golden calf in Exodus 33, Moses went up on the mountain for forty days. Probably near the end of that forty days the children of Israel, regretfully with Aaron's help, made a golden calf, an idol. Exodus 32:6 says, "The people sat down to eat and drink and rose up to play." God's people became an idolatrous camp. Because of this idolatry, Moses moved his tent outside the camp. This is the significance of the phrase outside the camp. Moses' tent became the tent of God. If anyone wanted the Lord's presence, if anyone wanted the Lord's speaking, he had to go outside the idolatrous camp of God's people to Moses' tent. There he could contact God, meet with God, hear God, and enjoy God. This is a picture of our going outside religion and into our spirit. Our spirit is the tent of God where we contact the heavenly Christ.
Seeing and Being Warned by the Principle of the Golden-calf Idol, an Idol Made by God's Redeemed People to Make Them an Idolatrous Camp
We need to see and be warned by the principle of the golden-calf idol, an idol made by God's redeemed people to make them an idolatrous camp (1 Cor. 10:5-7). God's redeemed people made an idol. This made them an idolatrous camp. This word should become divine "penicillin" and a divine "antibiotic" in us. This is a word of love to us. We have to realize that the golden-calf idol was not an idol made by pagans. It was an idol made by God's redeemed people, those who had eaten the Passover and had crossed the Red Sea. When they made that idol, they were enjoying the manna from the heavens at the same time. We need to realize that it is possible for us to have such an idol. Do not think that we are immune. We must be warned. In 1 Corinthians 10 Paul says that whatever happened to the children of Israel was a type of us, a warning to us (v. 6). In verse 7 he says, "Neither become idolaters, as some of them did; as it is written, 'The people sat down to eat and drink, and stood up to play.'" We as God-regenerated people can become idolaters.
In Ezekiel 14 the Lord told Ezekiel, "Son of man, these men have set their hearts on their idols" (v. 3). An idol is anything that is a substitute for God. Whatever we love more than the Lord is an idol. If we love our children more than we love the Lord, we make our children an idol. If we love our wives more than we love the Lord, we make our wives an idol. If we love our parents more than we love the Lord, we make our parents an idol. We should not love anything more than we love the Lord. We should love Him supremely. Whatever is more important to us than the Lord is an idol. Whatever our heart is set on other than the Lord Himself is an idol. Idolatry is the enjoyment of something in place of the Lord Himself. God wants us to enjoy Him exclusively and supremely. The name of the garden into which Adam was placed was Eden (Gen. 2:8), which means pleasure. God wants to be our pleasure, entertainment, and amusement. He wants to be everything to us, and He is.
Self-beautification Leading to Idolatry
Self-beautification leads to idolatry (Exo. 33:5-6; Gen. 35:2-4; cf. Exo. 28:2; Isa. 60:21), The children of Israel came to Aaron, and he acquiesced to their demands. He told them to give him the gold earrings of their wives, daughters, and sons (Exo. 32:1-3). That gold came from Egypt when they plundered the Egyptians (12:35-36). God arranged that the gold would be used to build the tabernacle, which typifies the Body of Christ. However, the people used their gold to make earrings to beautify themselves. Those self-beautifying earrings were formed into an idol for them to worship. The golden-calf idol had existed in another form on the ears of the children of Israel for their self-beautification. This indicates that self-beautification leads to idolatry, and even is idolatry.
The United States is a well-developed country, and we do not see many idols of wood and stone. However, today's culture in the United States promotes self-beautification. Self-beautification is a billion-dollar industry. Many people have had cosmetic surgery to get their faces lifted and their wrinkles removed. They also get their teeth and noses rearranged in an effort to beautify themselves. So in a sense, they have actually made themselves idols. Paul says that the sisters should wear proper, modest clothing (1 Tim. 2:9), and Peter says that the sisters should not adorn themselves with gold or costly raiment, but with the precious adornment of a meek and quiet spirit (1 Pet. 3:4). In the Lord's recovery, we are not for self-beautification, that is, to display ourselves or express ourselves. We desire to express Christ, because He is our beauty. Exodus 28 says that Aaron's garments were for glory and beauty (v. 2). Aaron's garments are a type of Christ as our clothing. As the glorious and beauteous Christ is dispensed into our being, we become His corporate beautification. Even in our service in the church, we should not beautify our work for people to worship it. We do not want anything to do with self-beautification. We want only to become His beautification for His glorification.
Idolatry Being Satan's Usurping of What God Has Given Us in Order to Make It a Waste, and Our Abusing of What God Has Given Us, Not Using God's Gifts, Both Material and Spiritual, for God's Purpose
Idolatry is Satan's usurping of what God has given us in order to make it a waste; it is our abusing what God has given us and not using God's gifts, both material and spiritual, for God's purpose. The children of Israel should have used their gold for the tabernacle. Instead, they used it to make an idol. This signifies the use of God's gifts, material and spiritual, for something other than God's purpose. If we do this, we become an idolatrous camp. Brother Lee said that if a person has the ability to teach the Bible, that ability should be used for God's purpose. If it is not used for God's purpose, that is idolatry.
Idolatry Being the Worship of the Things We Enjoy, the Worship of Enjoyment, Amusement, and Entertainment
Idolatry is the worship of the things we enjoy, the worship of enjoyment, amusement, and entertainment (Exo. 32:6, 18-19; cf. Psa. 36:8-9). A calf signifies enjoyment. In Luke 15, the father killed the fattened calf for enjoyment (v. 23). Idolatry is the enjoyment of things other than the Lord Himself. Along with the worship of the golden calf, the people ate and drank. In addition, they played, sang, and danced. The world is so strong in the United States; it is full of worldly enjoyment, worldly amusement, and worldly entertainment. All of these things are idols. This kind of worldly enjoyment is different from the genuine enjoyment of the Lord. We need to realize that our meetings are not for amusement, entertainment, or fun. Our meetings are for the genuine, holy, pure enjoyment of God. Whenever we speak for the Lord, we should not have any thought of displaying ourselves or of having some kind of amusement. Some in Christianity use dramas to attract crowds. Dramas attract crowds, but they also appeal to the flesh. In the Lord's recovery, our desire is not to attract crowds; rather, we desire to satisfy the heart's desire of our Father God.
Idolatry Being the Pretense of Worshipping the True God
With idolatry there is the pretense of worshipping the true God (Exo. 32:4-6; 1 Kings 12:26-30). With the golden calf, the children of Israel offered sacrifices. Aaron said, "Tomorrow shall be a feast to Jehovah" (Exo. 32:5). Actually, they were worshipping a golden calf. This was a pretense. We do not want any pretense. We need to pray, "Lord, save me from hypocrisy. Make me real. Lord, I repent that too much I live two lives. I want to be pure. Fill me with Yourself as the Spirit of reality."
Idolatry Being a Mixture in Worship
With idolatry there is mixture in worship (Exo. 32:4-6, 21-24). The celebration of Christmas is a mixture. Such a celebration uses the name of Christ for amusement and entertainment. In the word Christmas, we love Christ but we do not love mas. Christ has nothing to do with "mas." We love others, but we have to be faithful. Often, many saints in the Lord's recovery have been misunderstood and criticized for not celebrating Christmas. Some have asked why there is no celebration of Christmas among the saints in the local churches.
We can say to them that we want only what is in the Bible; we do not want any mixture. Consequently, all we want is God. We must love to be within the veil, and we must hate the idolatrous camp. Our desire is to be within the veil to the uttermost and to go outside of every camp until all the camps are gone, until God and man dwell together as one.
Moses Realizing That the Lord's Presence Would No Longer Be in the Midst of the People Removing His Tent and Pitching It Afar Off from the Camp
Because Moses realized that the Lord's presence would no longer be in the midst of the people, he removed his tent and pitched it afar off from the camp; his tent then became the tent of God (Exo. 33:7). Moses said, "Whoever is for Jehovah, come to me" (32:26). The Levites crossed over to Moses. That is how we should be; we want to be absolute. God is not wishy-washy; He is absolute. There is no gray zone with Him. His throne is not gray, but white——the great white throne (Rev. 20:11). He did not say, "Jacob, I am fond of; Esau, I do not care much about"; rather, He said, "Jacob have I loved, but Esau have I hated" (Rom. 9:13). He is absolute, and He is pure. When we are one with Him, we enjoy Him as our absoluteness and our purity.
The Camp Signifying a Religious People, Who Belong to the Lord in Name but Who, in Actuality, Worship Idols, Worshipping Something and Seeking Something Other Than the Lord Himself
The camp signifies a religious people, who belong to the Lord in name but who, in actuality, worship idols, worshipping something and seeking something other than the Lord Himself. In Matthew 4 when the Lord was tempted by the devil, He said, "You shall worship the Lord your God, and Him only shall you serve" (v. 10). Hence, the Lord told the devil clearly that He would not bow down to him. We do not seek anything other than the Lord Himself. We are not seeking excitement. If the Lord is excited, we are excited. If He says, "Hallelujah," we say, "Hallelujah." If He is calm, we also are calm. We are one with Him. Revelation 21 says that the New Jerusalem is prepared as a bride adorned for her husband (v. 2). This shows that the overcoming bride cares for one thing only, her Husband. In a certain sense, she is not for what the Lord can do for her or what the Lord can give her. She is for the Lord Himself alone.
The Camp Being Seen in the History of God's People in Three Periods
The Camp Being the Children of Israel after They Worshipped the Golden Calf
In the history of God's people, the camp may be seen in at least three periods. The first period was with the children of Israel after they worshipped the golden calf.
The Jewish Religion Becoming the Camp at the Time of the Lord's Living on the Earth
The Jewish religion became the camp at the time of the Lord's living on the earth (Matt. 15:7-9). This is the second period. The Jews had the temple, and they had the sacrifices. Then the Lord Jesus came as a little Nazarene, a little carpenter. Yet this little carpenter was the Triune God; He was the embodiment of the Triune God. At that time, to worship in the physical temple was in vain. In order to contact God, one had to come out of the shell of Judaism to contact the Lord Jesus. The Lord said, "This people honors Me with their lips, but their heart stays far away from Me" (v. 8). We never want to fall into this situation. We should pray, "Lord, I do not want to be a person who honors You with my lips only. I want my heart to be turned to You. I do not want my heart to be far away from You." We do want to pay lip service to the Lord. We want to turn our hearts to Him, to be filled with Him, and to speak Him out as reality into people.
The Church Changing in Nature from Being a Tent to Being a Camp, a Religious System, Religious Babylon, Comprising a Group of Religious People Belonging to the Lord in Name and Honoring the Lord with Their Mouth, but Having Their Hearts Set on Something Other Than the Lord
Later, the church changed in nature from being a tent to being a camp, a religious system, religious Babylon, comprising a group of religious people belonging to the Lord in name and honoring the Lord with their mouth, but having their hearts set on something other than the Lord (Gen. 11:4, 7, 9; 2 Chron. 36:6-7; Ezra 1:11; Rev. 17:3-5; 18:2a, 4). This is the third period in which God's people became a camp. We have to be careful because we also could become a camp. We need to be on the alert and apply this word to ourselves. We should not apply this word to others. We do not want anything to do with the idolatrous camp. We want to be infused with our precious, pure, and wonderful Lord within the veil.
The first characteristic of Babel was that man wanted to make a name for himself (Gen. 11:1-9). Such seeking has no place in the Lord's recovery. We should not have any desire to make a name for ourselves. Only Christ makes a name for Himself, and we exalt Him only. In Babylon, there is confusion, scattering, and mixture一all of which are indicated by a golden cup full of abominations (Rev. 17:4). Babylon is gilded with gold, but we want to be filled with gold.
The Lord Speaking to Moses Face to Face, as a Man Speaks to His Companion, after Moses Removed His Tent and Separated It from the Idolatrous Camp
After Moses removed his tent and separated it from the idolatrous camp, the Lord spoke to him face to face, as a man speaks to his companion. Let us remove our tent and separate it from all of the idolatrous camps. Let us come out of Babylon. In Revelation 18 the Lord says, "Come out of her, My people" (v. 4). We must enter within the veil and come out of Babylon. Then we will enjoy the Lord's speaking as Moses did. If we pray-read Exodus 33:11, personalizing it, we can say, "Lord, speak to me face to face, as a man speaks to his companion." The best translation of the Hebrew in this verse is companion, not friend. The Hebrew word indicates someone more than a friend. A companion is a friend, but a companion is more than a friend.
God and Moses Being Companions, Associates, Partners, Involved in the Same Career and Having a Common Interest in a Great Enterprise
God and Moses were companions, associates, partners, involved in the same career and having a common interest in a great enterprise. We need to pray, "Lord, make me Your business partner. I want to be Your business associate. I do not want any other career but Your career. I am not on this earth for anything else. Your career is my career. Your great enterprise to build up the Body of Christ, to prepare the bride, to usher in Your kingdom, and to consummate the New Jerusalem is my enterprise.
Moses Being Intimate with God, Knowing God's Heart, Being according to God's Heart, and Touching God's Heart
Because Moses was intimate with God, he was a person who knew God's heart, who was according to God's heart, and who could touch God's heart. We have to be intimate with God. We should pray, "Lord, make us persons who are intimate with You," Maybe we have lost our intimacy with the Lord. We can pray, a Lord, recover my intimacy with You. I want to be a person who lives in the closest and most intimate contact with You for Your heart's desire? If we are intimate with God, we will be like Moses who knew God's heart, who was according to God's heart, and who could touch God's heart.
Needing to Enter within the Veil and Go outside the Idolatrous Camp to Have the Closest and Most Intimate Relationship with the Lord
We need to enter within the veil and go outside the idolatrous camp to have the closest and most intimate relationship with the Lord so that we can be persons who share a common interest with God and who can be used by Him to carry out His enterprise on earth. His enterprise on this earth is to prepare His bride to consummate us as the consummate Holy of Holies, which is the New Jerusalem for His glory forever and ever. Hallelujah!—E. M.
